description:
Snowball Slide is a game which is also a Christmas card. On the front of the card is a picture, while on the back the rules are laid out neatly. Inside each is a small piece of card with eight counters to cut out and use with the game. It’s a two player game which takes around five minutes to play.

The card shows ski slopes, down which snowballs can run. Half the funds go to Camp Stripe and half to Camp Spots, which are both at the bottom of the card. The slopes are interrupted by cogs, which can send snowballs down one of two slopes, depends which way they are tilted — or can be horizontal and stop the snowball! The aim of the game is to get as many snowballs as possible into your camp. One player is aiming for Camp Stripe, the other Camp Spots.  Players take turns at rolling two dice, and then use the results to move cogs or release snowballs. The snowball rolls down the slopes until it gets to a horizontal cog — or until it arrives in one of the two camps!

This is published as an A5 greetings card and is played using cut out counters included in the envelope. All you (or the lucky people to whom you send the card) need to find is some scissors and two standard six-side dice. It is also available as a print-and-play.

-description from publisher

Firebirds: Gunship is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire/2-player co-op wargame using a tables-based game engine.

As the pilot of either a US Army OH-6A Cayuse scout helicopter or AH-1G Cobra gunship helicopter, your ultimate goal is to survive a 1-year Vietnam tour of duty during the year 1969, while at the same time trying to destroy as many enemy Viet Cong as possible.


     The following dice are required to play: One D20, one D6, and two D10s.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     Total play time to complete a full 1-year tour of duty takes approximately 3-10 hours (depending on game length chosen).
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.
     While originally designed as solitaire, the game can easily be played as 2-player co-op, with player 1 flying helo 1, and player 2 flying helo 2.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with 2 flyable helicopter types, numerous crewmen skills, mission types, detailed damage modeling, weather effects, terrain types, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Firebirds: Gunship offers high replay value.

Are you up to the challenge of surviving a 1-year tour of duty in Vietnam?

—description from the publisher

Story
The notorious Big Bad Pig and his pirate crew, the Three Little Wolves, stumbled upon a massive treasure chest on a deserted island. When celebrating at the Whisker's Bar, Big Bad Pig grabs a handful of gemstones and drops them into a mug, saying, “Whoever can guess how many gemstones I threw in gets them!” Thus begins an adventure of treasure-seeking and rivalry over the coveted mug!

Overview
You and your fellow pirates will take turns secretly tossing Treasure into the mug. Who can guess the total value correctly or catch others claiming an exceeding value will be able to split the roots! Watch closely and listen to the “clanks!” carefully, you might become the pirate hoarding the most treasure!

Unique features
The treasure is composed of glass marbles, acrylic gems, wooden coins, and metal fake coins. The clanks of these materials when they are tossed in the mug differ but you have to pay attention not to be misled by other players' bluffing moves.

This game is inspired by the popular Spanish comics series of the same name.   Capitán Trueno is a knight errant in the era of the 3rd Crusade (ca.AD 1200) who travels the world with his companions to fight bandits, tyrants and other evil characters. His companions are his young squire, the astute Crispin, his strong friend, the robust Goliath, and the Viking princess Sigrid.

The premise of the game is that Sigrid has been captured by a gang of Orcs and is now held in a Dungeon. The players have to travel along a game track through desert, jungle and into the Dungeon. The first player to reach Sigrid sets her free and wins the game.

At the beginning the players get a set of 'assistance cards'. The game track has fields with obstacles which you can pass by a die roll or by discarding a card. Other fields can give you new cards.

An unusual twist is that many of the hazards, such as orcs, snakes or skeletons, are represented by counters. Each player controls a set of these that he can move to harass the other players.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Firebirds: FAC is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine.

As the pilot of a US Army L-19 Bird Dog Forward Air Control (FAC) plane, your ultimate goal is to survive a 1-year Vietnam tour of duty during the year 1968, while at the same time trying spot and mark hidden enemy Viet Cong units in FAC zones, then later calling in air strikes to take them out.


     The following dice are required to play: One D20, one D6, and two D10s.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a full 1-year tour of duty takes approximately 3-10 hours (depending on game length chosen).
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.
     For game support, please be sure to visit the Black Cat Wargames Facebook group


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with numerous crewmen skills, detailed damage modeling, weather effects, terrain types, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Firebirds: FAC offers high replay value.

Are you up to the challenge of surviving a 1-year tour of duty in Vietnam?

—description from the publisher

Inspired by the popular book and movie, Flight of the Intruder is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine.

As the pilot of a US Navy A-6 Intruder all-weather attack aircraft, your ultimate goal is to survive a 1-year Vietnam tour of duty during the year 1972, while at the same time trying to complete night missions against North Vietnam targets.


     The following dice are required to play: One D20, one D12, one D6, and two D10s.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a full 1-year tour of duty takes approximately 3-8 hours (depending on game length chosen).
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with detailed aircraft damage modeling, fuel management, numerous crewmen skills, mission types, weather effects, terrain types, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Flight of the Intruder offers high replay value.

Are you up to the challenge of surviving a 1-year tour of duty in Vietnam?

—description from the publisher

Two dice are rolled. Everyone has 2 game pieces, which are differentiated by color for each player. The players take turns to roll and move.
The 40 city cards are shuffled at the start of the game and distributed to the players. Each player must touch the cities of their city cards with their two tokens (one or the other) and thus discard the city card in question. When a double is rolled, the following rules apply:
2x6: discards one of their city cards of their choice.
2x5: exchange one of your cards with the cards that were not dealt.
2x4: you can roll the dice again.
2x3: you have to skip the dice once.
2x2: pays one token into the cash register.
2x1: receives a city card from the player on the right.
Whoever touches all of the cities on their cards and is the first to put down their cards is the winner.

—user summary

boardgame: Struggle for hegemony in Eastern Europe
description: The game covers the years 1558-1667. There are 12 moves in the game. It is built on a card engine. And in its essence it resembles Here I Stand and Virgin Queen.Players can declare wars and enter into alliances. Alliances allow players to share both cards and troops. Throughout the map, large-scale events will take place that affect all countries. For example, the Deluge in the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, the Troubles in the Russian Kingdom, the Thirty Years' War in Germany.Also, each move gives certain advantages to both one side and weakening the other.The game is very balanced. The author tried to convey this historical period as accurately as possible. The game requires 4-5 players.But in the future the author is thinking of adding more scenarios. Many events reflect the state of a particular state in a given period of time. Winning battles can provide benefits, but the player must not forget about diplomacy. If you are not protected by your allies, then any war you have will be doomed.

Combining on-the-spot strategic decisions, along with exciting press-your-luck, competitive gameplay, Tee Masters gives golfers and non-golfers alike a set of engaging, continually evolving challenges, combined with fast-action gameplay that makes players call for “one more round!” as soon as the game ends. Just grab the Tee Masters custom dice, tee up on its 18 killer holes, and find out which friends and family rule the course!

Scoring mechanic of Tee Masters is simple to understand, but not necessarily easy: just match three of the Scoring Dice you’re rolling on a hole, and you get that score on the hole. And you’ve got three “Group Rolls” with all the dice, you set aside rolls you like as you move through those rolls, and you build up your best score for the hole.  In short: three tries to nail the lowest score possible for the hole.

But in Tee Masters, the Scoring Dice don’t just have pars, birdies and eagles. Unfortunately, they also feature more damaging scores like +1 bogeys, +2 double bogeys, and even the dreaded +3 triple bogey.

And then there’s the Shot Die, and that adds hole hazards like sand traps, wind, and water, and the dreaded DUFF, which can derail a great score in an instant. But the Shot Die also delivers “Great Shots,” which can help you improve your score on a hole, big time.

And in addition to its super-charged multiplayer gameplay, Tee Masters also plays great for one player. Because gameplay is so fast and straightforward, you’ll find yourself hitting the Tee Masters course when you’ve got ten or so minutes of spare time, just to see if you can achieve a new personal-best score.

Go for eagle, or play it safe? Avoid hazards, don’t duff, and navigate each hole like a boss. Tee Masters is a fantastic challenge for golfers and non-golfers alike. At home, at game night, with fellow golfers at the clubhouse, or on vacation, Tee Masters is a great time for 1-4 players, or even teams.

—description from the designer

Race around the board to reach your goal first, but watch out! – your opponents could change the landscape at any moment.

The modular board grows or shrinks to fit the number of players. The individual boards rotate in response to players' decisions, requiring them to adapt quickly or fall behind.

Simple rules and the modular board lead to fast turns and ever-changing strategies, making the game perfect for both children and adults.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Galahad's Ordeal: The Battle for Myitkyina, May-August, 1944
description:
“Bad news from Mitch (US code name for Myitkyina). Now they say 800 Japs got into Charpate last night. And 200 crossed the river from the east. McCammon says situation is critical”. –Except from General Stilwell’s May 22nd personal log report about offensive to take Myitkyina.

Galahad’s Ordeal portrays the Allied attack and siege of Myitkyina during WWII. The town and its nearby airfield were a constant threat to the Allied airlift operations that ferried war materials and supplies from India to Nationalist China. The Japanese occupation of the town also severed the only land access from India to China for the Allies. 
When the Japanese offensive at Imphal and Kohima was launched the Allied High Command viewed the battle as an opportunity to launch a counter offensive by the US 5307th Infantry Group (Merrill’s Marauders, also nicknamed Galahad Force by their British and Indian Allies) supported by Nationalist Chinese forces against Myitkyina, deep into the rear areas of Japanese occupied northern Burma. The Allies calculated that the Japanese had to have weakened their hold here to launch their massive offensive to the west. Unfortunately for the Allies, the US forces were badly depleted from earlier battles, and the Japanese had not weakened their grip at Myitkyina, and instead, anticipating just such an attack, were in the process of strengthening the town’s garrison. One of the most costly and prolonged battles of the Burma theatre would be waged here. Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart? Learn and enjoy!

—description from the publisher

Meals & Myths is a cooperative survival crafting expansion that adds a rich new layer of culinary discovery and storytelling to the prehistoric world of Stonesaga. From hunting and gathering new ingredients to creating recipes that strengthen your characters, cooking is now a crucial tool for survival. But beware—unpredictable results can arise when you drop your tokens into the roiling pot!

Uncover ancient myths as you journey to a new region, face a mighty behemoth, and tackle fresh challenges. With new structures, items, and a fifth character mantle, this expansion brings even more ways to leave your mark on the valley.

- Adds a 5th player to Stonesaga

Nature of the Beast brings animal companions to life in your Stonesaga adventures. From loyal birds to hulking predators, each creature offers unique abilities and personality, becoming a vital part of your tribe’s survival. Bonded to individual characters, companions can aid in challenges, carry resources, or even scout the valley, all while deepening the story of your journey. Simple yet impactful mechanics ensure they seamlessly integrate into gameplay, offering both strategic benefits and heartfelt moments.

This expansion also introduces a new region to explore and an expanded array of challenges, structures, and foraging opportunities.

- Adds animal companions to Stonesaga

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Iron Dogs
description:
Several times a year, when we feel like it, the Golden Journal brings Gold Club members some fun extras for their Avalanche Press games. Our new-model Golden Journal features a “real” booklet, just like a small magazine, and a small set of die-cut, silky-smooth playing pieces.

The first clash of modern dreadnoughts took place at the Battle of Dogger Bank in January 1915, where five British battle cruisers met and defeated three German battle cruisers (plus one large armored cruiser). The battle features heavily in our Great War at Sea: Jutland Second Edition, and we delved into the battle more deeply in the analysis book Jutland: Dogger Bank.

Iron Dogs presents these nine heavy ships (plus three more than could have been present, and are used in the variant scenarios in the Dogger Bank book) on the huge, oversized pieces we’ve used before for battleships in Golden Journal No. 31: Deluxe Bismarck. You don’t need these pieces to play Jutland; they’re the same as the pieces in the game. Just way bigger and way nicer.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements
Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
The Innsmouth Curse takes you on a journey around the ancient, haunted seaside town of Innsmouth, made famous by the writer H.P.Lovecraft. Make your way to the Devils Reef and be rid of the curse that surrounds you! The first person there is the winner, but to get there you will face various Encounters along the way. Only the sane will survive to make it to the end.

Sort out the cards into the different piles and give them all a good shuffle. Lay out the locations, and choose a character, a curse (which indicates where you begin) and take your first clue. Each character gets 6 Sanity points and 4 Health points to begin with. Should either of these gets to zero, your character needs to rest for a turn in the Hotel before continuing. You also lose any Artefacts you may have gathered, including any boat tickets.

Move your character one location at a time to get to the clue destination. Each time you move, take an Event. Resolve the Event and if you survive, you are rewarded with an Artefact. If you have reached your Clue Destination, take another Clue and continue on your way.

When you reach your third Clue Destination, take a Boat Ticket and head to the Harbour. From there, you can make it across the Open Seas and to the Devils Reef. The first person to get to the Devils Reef is the winner!

Age of Swords is an epic strategy board game that takes you back to the Middle Ages where valor, skill, and cunning will determine who will dominate the Kingdom. As the leader of your army, you will need to recruit your soldiers, equip them with weapons, and send them into battle against your opponents on a dynamic battlefield.

In Age of Swords, you will have at your disposal 28 cards for each of the two armies, each with their own unique units and special abilities. From light cavalry to archers and crossbowmen, knights and commanders, each unit plays an important role in battle. You play on a 8x12 battlefield grid.

Each card has three main characteristics that define its role in battle: movement speed on the board, attack strength, and, in the case of ranged units like archers and crossbowmen, also includes the shooting distance. In combat the defender also rolls a D20 to determine the outcome.

You will have to demonstrate your ingenuity and skill by moving them across the board to claim victory. You win by defeating all enemy units.

Battles: Napoleonic is a 1-2 player tactical skirmish wargame depicting infantry warfare in Europe between France and England during the early 1800s.

The game features 5 different soldier types, 3 types of battles, and 10 battle maps. 
Campaigns consist of either 5 or 10 random linked skirmish battles. 
For solitaire players, the game offers an advanced AI bot opponent which makes calculated decisions based on its current situation. 
Features such as day/night, weather, line of sight, 7 terrain types with movement/cover effects, and ranged/melee soldier attacks are also included.


     One D6, one D20, and two D10 dice are required to play. A dry erase pen is also required.
     Solitaire or 2 players.
     5 minute game components setup and breakdown times.
     May be played on a small size table.
     Adjustable difficulty based on player's skill level.
     Total campaign play time is approximately 2.5-5+ hours (depending on campaign length chosen). Individual battles can last between 15-45 minutes.
     For game support, please be sure to visit the Black Cat Wargames Facebook group


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with 5 soldier types, 10 battle maps, randomized enemy soldiers setup (single-player), an advanced AI bot opponent (single-player), randomized enemy soldier skill levels (single-player), small table footprint, and quick setup/breakdown times, Battles: Napoleonic offers an entertaining game for players of all skill levels, plus high replay value.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Curse of Candelabria: 6th Player Expansion
description:
The House of the Chimaera is ready to emerge from the mists of the north to unleash its fury on the lands of Candelabria! The House, given its strong bond with nature and the unknown, boasts powerful mystical powers and the ability to pass through roads and paths unknown to most. This House has its own initial Region, which is added on the main board as an expansion tile to the original game board. This also raises the player level count to 6.

The Explorations:

Inside the package you will find 18 exploration tokens, each depicting one or more different icons.  These represent the mysteries and discoveries that the Houses make as they wander through Candelabria, when they decide not to plunder the territory but to see what is in front of them.

During the Set-Up these tokens are to be placed face down, randomly, on each territory belonging to a region that is not identified as the initial Region of a House (whether or not this is present in the game).

When a model (Candle or Castle) of a House enters a territory containing a token, he can INSTEAD of deploying a Land Flame and then perform the action relating to the territory, take the Exploration token, placing it face down next to his House Board.  He may then reveal one of his exploration tokens as part of any action he takes to obtain the benefits indicated on this, or keep it covered to gain 1 VP at the end of each Year for each face-down token in his possession.

boardgame: The Curse of Candelabria: Solo Mode
The Curse of Candelabria: Missions and Decay
description:
A new deck of 40 cards that adds the Missions and Decay mechanics to the game Missions and Corruption is package containing 40 mini-cards divided into 24 Mission cards and 16 Decay cards that adds two mechanics and a twist to the game. This package and the mechanics contained are usable in every player count, with different twists and applications.

Mission cards are cards with a specific request on them, be it to kill a candle, build a temple or control a specific region. By completing the request, the players can receive Victory Points to help them gain some edge in the war for the throne of Candelabria.

Every player will receive two Mission Cards at the beginning of a game, during Set-Up. One of the two cards is taken by the player in the form of a private mission, a secret objective that he needs to accomplish, while the other card is placed near the board and create a set of mission that all the players can access to. Two types of missions are available:


    Yearly missions, which can be completed once a year.
    Ranking missions, where the reward can be taken by the first three players.


While the personal mission will award points only to the player that possess the mission, the common ones will be claimed through one of this methods. The idea behind this is to create a pool of changing milestones and objectives for the players to interact with, giving other ways to gather Victory Points and have fun while strategizing around that. This will help giving substance to the Turn Order too, since now the advantage for the first player would not only be to win draws but also the be able to complete an objective first, and thus giving some weight to the combat choices of getting initiative.

Decay cards are cards with specific effects, similar to tactics cards. Their usage, although, is different. Decay cards are received every time a player try to win influence over a Course but fails, during the combat phase. These cards are placed on the War Board and modify the effect on that until the current War Season is over, giving a new tactical asset and effects to the Houses. Decay is a mechanic with the intent to lessen the burden for a failed bet or wrong move on the Curse, by giving not an advantage but the chance to play around new effects and get a well-deserved revenge (with enough strategy and intelligence).

Smart 10 Family: Neue Fragen 2.0 contains 200 new questions with 2,000 new answers, with the questions covering a wide variety of subject areas appropriate for the whole family: Is the black Mamba poisonous? Do young people like to wear "long kicks"? Is "foyer" a type of cheese? And what color are Asterix's pants?

This expansion can be used with any of the base games in the Smart 10 series.

Speed is king in the party game Itzi Blitzi Bäm! because whoever finds the right terms the fastest wins. For example, you are looking for a carbonated drink that begins with "C" or a dog command that starts with "S".

Once you get good at that, move on to the next mode in which the colors and symbols on the letter cards also matter.

With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization.

The game features 7 types of missions and 10 battle maps. Solitaire campaigns consist of either 5 or 10 random linked skirmish battles, including missions such as Bridge Demolitions and Officer Assassinations. 
The game features an advanced enemy IJA AI bot opponent which makes calculated decisions based on its current situation. Other features such as day/night, weather, line of sight, suppression, and 7 terrain types with movement/cover effects are also included. 
Players will be able to improve their Commandos by assigning them a variety of skills, plus equip them with numerous types of weapons and equipment before each mission.

A speed-based game.

One player rolls dice and holds the cup, the others have bones-on-a-string sitting in a circle on the board. When a named number or doubles are rolled, the player with the cup tries to trap the bones in the cup before they're snatched away. If you trap a bone, you get a chip from its owner. Player with the most chips after a decided-upon amount of time, wins.

boardgame: Resident Evil: The Board Game – Director's Cut
description:
The Director's Cut box includes all the Stretch Goals from the Kickstarter campaign.

It includes additional characters to play as in the Core game complete with their cards and models, as well as new enemies complete with their cards and models to use in certain scenarios in the Core game.

It also includes a rulebook which details how to add the new enemies to the Core game and also includes some additional gameplay modes which includes Traitor mode where one character can be revealed to be a traitor sent by the enemy organization, Umbrella, to sabotage the game from the inside. Real Survival mode takes the Hard mode (also featured in this box) and cranks up the challenge even more and Battle mode is a new solo mode which sees a single character trying to kill as many enemies as possible before the tension deck runs out.

Bronze medal @the invention international contest of 1951 by Edmond Dujardin.
This is a board game with cards exhibiting road signs. The player must get ride of all the cards he gots at the start of the play by playing them each time he reach a step on the board. he is allowed to play the card if the steps allows him.

Join grampa Jones on his annual family weekend into the woods. The player who collects the most treasure points wins a spot in the family trophy room.

DIBS! is an interactive, real-time speed matching card game that plays under 30 minutes for ages 8 and up. You play as one of 5 characters and try to collect the most treasures by tactically tossing cards from your hand on a shared pile.
The cards need to correspond to a number of dice that were thrown upfront.

—description from the publisher

If you are a fan please consider getting this microbadge

A creation by Simon de Thuillières. 
The aim of the game is to illuminate stories (the idea: like an old manuscrit). 
To win the game, you have to use color pigments to build together a codex. You get points depending on the colors used. 
Monks are used to produce the necessary pigments for illumination. 
Note: the author uses jokes referring to the modern world in his codex, like "pugilat de ruelle 2" (street pugilist in the middle Ages) for street fighter 2 (modern world).

Une création de Simon de Thuillières.
Le but du jeu est d'enluminer des histoires. 
Pour remporter la partie, il faut avoir utilisé des pigments colorés pour construire le meilleur codex. Les points dépendent des couleurs utilisées. 
On utilise des moines pour produire les pigments nécessaires à l enluminure. 
Ce que les content pour un d un âge moyen un culte bas petit serre lit lumination créa tri cieux (ce qui traduit de l ancien francois donne : ce que les contemporains du moyen âge incultes baptisèrent l'illumination créatrice).

A game of Jordy Questeur, illustrated by Simon de Thuillières. 
Bare-handed fighting card game, one versus one, based on anticipation, probabilities, and risk taking. 
As it can be played for up to 777 years old, that means it's a miraculous game. 
Note : the author makes jokes in his game, like "pugilat de ruelle" (street pugilist in the middle Ages) for street fighter 2 (in the modern world).

Un jeu de Jordy Questeur, illustré par Simon de Thuillières.
Jeu de cartes de bagarre à mains nues en un contre un (ou autrement dit : une bagarre a deux avec estourbissement), basée sur l'an Ticipation, les pro Babil Lités (deux noeuds plats six fer tous chez), et la prise de risk. 
Particularité : on peut y jouer jusque 777 ans, ce qui en fait un jeu miraculeux du même coup.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Midnight in the Garden of Cuteness and Catastrophe
description:
It has similarities to Stud and Whiskey Poker. Players are dealt eight cards, some face-up, some face-down, then have three turns to draw and discard cards to try and get a good hand: five of a kind, four of a kind, three of a kind with one pair, one trio, two pairs, or one pair (in order of rank).
After three turns, everyone plays their hand to win a blue flower card. The game is played for nine rounds and whoever has the most of the nine flower cards wins.

INTRODUCTION:
Dragons abound brave adventurer!  Pull out your trusty pips of weaponry and mayhem and search the skies for sharp-toothed dragons.  Keep you quick wits about you as you roll for success!

DESCRIPTION:
Dragon’s Fury is a quick dice-based fame (VP) system where 2-6 players attempt to complete different challenge cards to end with the most fame.

GAME OBJECTIVE:
Score the highest fame points by completing variable difficulty challenge cards

GAME PLAY:
Players will take turns rolling their favorite set of polyhedral dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, and d20) to complete sets of varying difficulty.  Each turn, players will receive an additional challenge card from the draft pool, increasing their scoring potential.

GAME ELEMENTS:
Use your own favorite DND dice set
Easy to setup, learn, and play
Travel size - bring it with you anywhere

NOTE: You will need to provide a set of d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, and d20 dice for each player.  They are *not* included with the game.

—description from the designer

Battles: Western Europe is a 1-2 player tactical skirmish wargame depicting American Army operations against the German Army during the Battle of the Bulge in the Belgian Ardennes from November 1944 to January 1945.

The game features 3 types of missions and 10 battle maps. Campaigns consist of either 5 or 10 random linked skirmish battles, including missions such as Meeting Engagements and Defending Towns. 
The game features an advanced enemy German Army AI bot opponent which makes calculated decisions based on its current situation. 
Day/night, weather, line of sight, suppression, and 6 terrain types with movement/cover effects are also included. Players will be able to improve their units' skill levels by doing well on the battlefield.

Game Features:

     Solitaire or 2 players.
     One D6, one D20, and two D10 dice are required. A dry erase pen is also required.
     5 minute game components setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is required for play.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.
     Total campaign play time is approximately 2.5-5+ hours (depending on campaign length chosen). Individual battles can last between 15-45 minutes.
     For game support, please be sure to visit the Black Cat Wargames Facebook group


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. 
And with 10 enemy unit types, 10 battle maps, an advanced AI bot opponent, randomized enemy German Army setup, randomized enemy German Army skill levels, moderate table footprint, and quick setup/breakdown times, Battles: Western Europe offers an entertaining game for players of all skill levels, plus great replay value.

—description from the publisher
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

boardgame: Survival Stories: The Cottage – Through the Woods
description: In this DLC you will live a parallel story to the main plot of the game, where you will feel the fear in your adventure through the woods of Connieville and its surroundings.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 80 wins the game.

The game was published in the 1920s as promotional giveaway for "Leipziger Textil-Gesellschaft m.b.H." in Chemnitz.

—user summary

UNLEASH YOUR INNER DETECTIVE AND SOLVE THE CLAW-SOME CRIMES IN PURRANORMAL CATIVITY!

Purranormal Cativity is the mystery game for people with mischievous cats or those with a love for cheeky animals! It is now your task to uncover what happened in the scenes leading up to your arrival.
Winning Combination…

Identify the criminal cat, their mischievous crime, motive and hiding place! When you believe you have the answer, give your best Meow before revealing your guess to your opponents!

Questions about the 80s.

—user summary

En uppsättning nya frågekort till SMART10: 50 dubbelsidiga kort med 100 nya frågor och 1000 nya svar!

Temat är 80-talet med sina kändisar, musik, trender, TV-program, politik och mycket mer. Byt ut frågekorten i Smartboxen mot två förpackningar temafrågor eller blanda dessa frågor med hälften av de ursprungliga frågorna. För att ha glädje av dessa frågekort behövs också Smartboxen ur SMART10 eller SMART10 JR.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Sun, The Sky, and The Stars
description:
The Sun, The Sky, and The Stars is a 2-4 player, family-friendly, point trick-taking game for all ages. Follow Kyupi on his adventure through the stars.

The game includes 50 cards: 3x10 cards (value of 1 to 10) for each Sky, Sun and Stars suit cards and also 20 "Kyupi" cards, which function as "Event" cards, that can change the current win condition.

Each player is dealt 6 cards. Each turn one player leads by playing one card. The other players have to follow the suit (Sun, Sky, Stars) if possible. If not they can try to trump with another suit or play a "Kyupi" card to change the rules. The suits trump each other in a rock-paper-scissors style and all trump the "Kyupi" cards. After 6 turns the round is over, shuffle up and re-deal. Who has the most points after 3 rounds is the winner!

Welcome to classical Greece! 
It's 431 B.C. and your leadership is urgently requested! 
Guide the fortune of the naval power of Athens, the belligerent Sparta, the trading giant of Corinth or the seven-gated Thebes.

You decide how you want your civilization to succeed. Will Sparta become a democracy with strong maritime trade? 
Will Corinth conquer their neighbors and become an aristocratic empire? 
Will Athens manipulate the other players and surprise everyone with a hidden agenda?
Everything is possible - you decide!

The game is played on a 5 x 5 raster. The four (or three) players start in a corner. 
It's about exploration & expansion, about trading and diplomatic agreements.
Aegeapolis is an economic game at its core. 
You can establish trade routes with neutral cities and with other players. You can capture neutral cities, 
conquer player poleis (cities) and disrupt their trade.

To win the game you earn victory points. This happens in recurring scoring rounds by investing resources, 
by having the largest empire and by agenda civilization cards which surprise your opponents with your hidden goals and scoring opportunities.

The prophecy has been fulfilled. The Earth will soon become uninhabitable. The only chance is to build a space station and find our new home, the New Earth. The HELIX project is designed to allow players to build their own space station, each controlling a company. Whoever manages their resources best can build a bigger and more efficient space station. But to win, you have to be able to get the resources, and it's important to make efforts in planetary exploration.

Each turn, you roll dice as many times as you want to get resources to build your own space station. With a push your luck mechanism, if you roll too much, you fail. But if you don't take risks, you progress more slowly... Then activate the space station moduls you've already built. Activating these can create countless combinations and chain reactions, giving you access to new resources and opportunities on your own board. Whoever has the most victory points at the end of the game wins!

—description from the publisher

Pirate Hunter: 1718 is an old-school style tactical solitaire board game of action and adventure, with a bit of wargame and RPG elements mixed in. An open-ended sandbox style game where you, as a warship captain, may choose your own path.

Set in the Caribbean Islands during the year 1718, as a privateer warship captain hired by the governor of Port Royal, you will be tasked with hunting down dastardly pirates that are disrupting the shipping industry in the Caribbean, with your ultimate goal being to attack and shut down the notorious pirate town of Nassau. 
But it's not all work and no play. 
In your free time, you might decide to also gamble in a tavern, or even attempt to recover buried treasure from acquired maps. 
Whatever path you choose, no two games will ever play the same.


     Two D10 dice are required to play. However, ten D10 dice are recommended to reduce the number of rolls during ship combat. 
     10 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A large size table is required for full game setup. * Using the optional Warship sheet eliminates some of the game mats and reduces overall table footprint size.
     Total play time to the end is approximately 5-10 hours (depending on game length chosen).
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. 
And with 5 types of sailing ships, ship damage modeling, weather conditions, special items, warship captain skills, specialist crewmen, random events, a multitude of table modifiers, and an almost unlimited number of player choices, Pirate Hunter: 1718 offers high replay value.

Can you hunt down the most notorious pirates in the Caribbean and eventually close down Nassau?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Keyflower: The Glade is a new village tile for the game Keyflower.

The Glade tile is being published at Spiel in October 2023 along side the new R&D game called The Glade.  The same creatures can be found on the tile as are found on The Glade box lid.

The Glade tile is a winter tile.  Score 5 points for each adjacent tile in the player's village that displays a summer icon.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rat Trap: Je größer die Gang, desto größer das Durcheinander!
description: These are two promo dice that allow to expand the Ludolux version of the game and add even more excitement to the game.

Men of War: Central America Nicaragua-CONTRAS is Module 3
Standalone Solitaire Tactical Book Wargame designed to re-create modern low-intensity conflicts at the tactical level. Men of War is an infantry-centric, old-school hex and counter wargame system that mimics the nuances of modern small unit leadership, fire, and maneuver, primarily team and squad-based formations.
In this third installment of the series, “Nicaragua 1979-1990”-CONTRAS the player controls guerrilla forces led by Commander Zero and trained by U.S. Special Forces, backed by the United States intelligence in a controversial covert war against communist encroachment in Central America. CONTRAS are up against the disciplined, well-equipped, and well-trained Sandinista National Liberation Front or FSLN - Frente Sandinista de Liberación Nacional. 
Lead CONTRA and U.S. Special Forces teams in missions across the Nicaragua/Honduras border.
¡Contrarrevolucion!
Game Features
Unit Scale – Teams/Squads
Map – Hexagonal, one Hex=40 meters
Turns – Minutes
Playing Time – 30-60 Minutes
Players – One (Solitaire Play)
Complexity – Medium
CONTRA, U.S. Special Forces, FSLN and Advisor Cadre
Dynamic Enemy Bot system.
10 historically Inspired missions

—description from the publisher

Caïssa Britannia, originally known as British Chess, is based on an idea Fergus Duniho had in 2001 to create a British themed Chess variant with Lions, Unicorns, Dragons, and a royal Queen.

Lions, Unicorns, and Dragons are respectively the heraldic animals for England, Scotland, and Wales.

The Queen is the royal piece, because at the time of the game's creation the British monarch was a Queen.

There's been an accident in the Gear-Gum factory and all of the bots have been caught inside bubbles!

In Doublevolt, 2 players will take turns drawing and playing supercharged robot cards to connect circuits on a shared tableau and create their own secret patterns. Each card in hand can either be played OR score you points, so use your robots wisely!

First to empty their hand of supercharged robots wins!

—description from the publisher

The deck is evenly distributed to all players, any spare cards left in the box. Each player draws three cards and tries to play the most powerful weapon (bombs, firearms, knives, bludgeons) to win the trick. The deck isn't reshuffled between rounds: whatever cards you have won (plus any left over from your previous deck) become one's new deck. After five rounds, the person with the most cards wins.

A chaotic and colourful spoof of old-school roll n' move games, Roll & Groove has a constantly moving finish line, always-changing dice, and a crazily generated path system, with a horde of goofy characters that keep changing the rules!

Players place their Groove Tokens on the randomly drawn start card and start with one die, rolling along their path.

Whenever a new card is added to the map, the finish line moves!

Certain rolls of the dice will force players to steal or give dice to other players!
Landing on the whacky Dunderhead characters, or on other player's token triggers a wide variety if effects! 
Players can claim ownership of unique powers or change the community powers at any moment!
Oh, and building Groove Towers on the roundabouts can results in more points!!!

—description from the designer

An asymmetric card game, each player use their unique abilities and drink tolerances to feast, fest, and fight more than anyone else before succumbing to drink and passing out.

Play cards to serve drinks to your friends that they must imbibe while also trying to minimize how many drinks you consume. As each round progresses, it becomes harder and harder to keep your wits about you and to avoid being part of the celebration. The single player left still having space for more drinks wins the cheers and admiration of their fellow Vikings for their skill, ingenuity and robust constitution.

—description from the designer
Elephant Bouncing Game is a game of dexterity. Put your chip onto the tail of the elephant launching it into one of the four slots. Connect three in a row and win!

SUDEGO, a.k.a. スデゴー is a game about the sustainable redevelopment of a once prosperous but now run-down island.
You pioneer islands, gather resources and build bases for redevelopment.

On a turn, you choose either pioneering action or building action within your area of activity on the board.  
By pioneering action, you can pick up and cut through the terrain tile (dump,ruins,and so on) on the board that block your way and turn them into resources and get them and widen your area of activity.
By building action, you can build base chip (village, city, and so on) on the board using the resources you obtain, scoring points and even narrowing other players' area of activity.
These simple two actions are repeated, and the player with the highest final score is the winner.

There are more than 500000000000000000000000000000 initial terrain patterns on the island where the game takes place.
So you will enjoy the development of a different game each time.

Also, SUDEGO includes 17 Goals cards, each with a unique effect, and if you wish, you can play with them in a game to create a new playing environment. 
The game also includes rules for team play when you have four players.

—description from the designer

No time to lose! 
Gather the team and take on the latest crime case in Krimsta City. But hurry, because the crime scene trail is getting cold and the suspects must be released unless you produce enough evidence.
In Agenterna ("Agents"), you play a select team of agents from the KSSA - Krimsta Secret Service Agency - who are deployed to solve the most difficult crimes in Krimsta City. Go out and investigate the crime scene to collect clues, analyze what you get and interrogate suspects to build your evidence.
With varying crime scenes, crimes and suspects and random elements, there is plenty of variety for many rounds of play. The game is a cooperative game where you work together to win, and you can choose to compete against yourself by keeping track of your score from previous games.
Are you up to the task?

—description from the designer

Th.B.A.Th. Labs is a tile/ card laying, pattern building/ matching game in which you will be creating a connected network of circuits in a Quantum Computer to try and overcome the evil plans from Th.E.Y. (The Entangled You, a version of yourself in a parallel universe who is trying to be the only you alive). In the game you will be choosing and connecting a path in the computer circuitry by matching patterns of coding components required by each unit of circuit.

This game is in the same Universe of Th.B.A.Th. The Battle Against Th.E.Y. for 1 to 2 players (coop).

Bookshelf is a cozy and quick literary set collection card game for 1-5 players (in competitive mode or 2-3 players in co-op mode).

In Bookshelf, you are an avid book collector trying to curate sets of books for your personal collection. Acquire books from the library or bookstore, scour the local flea market for bargains and gain access to the rare books section to build your collection of books!

—description from the designer

Gemstones is an a-symmetrical area-control shape puzzle game where you exploit the strengths of your gemstone to grow it faster and larger than your opponents.

Each turn players take turn placing a gemstone from their available pieces on the board. Once no players choose to place a gemstone (or cannot) the game ends.

Points are counted by counting the point value of each gemstone shard (piece) a player played onto the board, minus 5 points for each separate non-connected section of gemstone. (So you loose no points if all your gemstones are touching.)

Other than that, there are also 3 placing rules when placing a gemstone shard (piece). 

     Each shard has some edges that must be covered when placed.
     The shard must be touching at least 2 different sides on the board (parallel sides are fine). 
     At least one side must be touching either the edge of the board or one of your other gemstone shards.


This causes players to place shards as if their gemstone is "growing" out of the edge of the geode and their other pieces.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Sewers & Ladders – Radical Edition
description:
Created by a team of fanatical film and video game fans who grew up in the 80's and 90's watching and reading about the adventures of Leonardo, Raphael, Donatello and Michelangelo.
Wanting to relive their memories they created 'Sewers & Ladders’ in
the hope that you enjoy playing this game as much as they did creating it!

With a board measuring 400x400mm, four Turtle counters and two dice this is a FaNaTtik homage to the classic 80's TMNT cartoon.

—description from the publisher
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description: The Grounded for Life expansion pack features 125 fresh cards and countless opportunities for hilarity.

The Battle of Khalkhin Gol is an Operational level board-wargame covering the World War II border clash between Japan and the USSR at the very dawn of this global conflict. It is a hex & counter wargame with unique battle mechanism, a lot of strategy and a number of features specific to the harsh conditions of the setting. Khalkhin Gol is medium level complexity and lasts from 2 to 4 hours. Variable setup and random cards bringing effects to the game offers great replayability.

If played in two players, one player controls Japan, while the other player controls the USSR. 3 different win conditions give the game added complexity.

—description from the designer
