description:
In Battle of Gods, the fate of the world hangs in the balance as champions of Norse mythology clash in a fast-paced arena battle game. Whether you prefer all-against-all or team-based showdowns, the choice is yours.

In Battle of Gods: Hel Expansion, take command of the legendary figure Hel, Goddess of Death, and battle your way to claim the title as the most glorious champion or the last one standing.

This is a character expansion for the base game Battle of Gods.

The goal of the two-player game (1 against 1) is to safely increase the size of one's networks.
The more robustly a player's networks are designed, the more points their networks are worth. Numerous and insidious cyber threats can be used to weaken the opponent's networks and reduce the point value. The first player to reach a score of 10 or more points at any time wins the game immediately. A player who cannot draw a card or whose score at any time has a total value of -10 or less loses the game immediately.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Battle of Gods, the fate of the world hangs in the balance as champions of Norse mythology clash in a fast-paced arena battle game for 2-4 players. Whether you prefer all-against-all or team-based showdowns, the choice is yours.

Take command of legendary figure Odin, God of War, and battle your way to claim the title as the most glorious champion or the last one standing.

This is a character expansion for Battle of Gods.

The Dungeon Within is a Print&Play Roll&Write Dungeon Crawler game for 1-3 players. Your brotherhood must fight the three Horsemen of the Night and escape The Deep Dungeon to win.

To play you choose 3 Character Cards (one as party leader), set up the 12 room tiles in a 3x4 grid and prepare the Guide, the Beastiary and The Shop documents, which generate enemies and treasures. You also need min. 5xD6 and a 54 card poker deck.

You start on Tile 1 and start to explore the rooms on the tile. Check the Guide to see the tile objective and the maximum number of turns you have to complete it. F.E. Tile 1 requires you to explore at least 1 Dark Room in 2 turns. If you succeed, you can decide to explore more rooms or go to the next tile. If you fail, you loose.

Each character draws 5 cards from the Poker deck. The cards you draw each turn are used for buffs you can use to overcome the encounters, f.E. a Heart suit card can give you +1 health. You win the game if all 3 characters manage to successfully complete the objective of the last tile. If you fail to complete the objective within the given turns, you loose the game. If all 3 characters die, you also loose the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: フィラデルフィア：拡張 (Philadelphia: Expansion)
description:
フィラデルフィア：拡張 (Philadelphia: Expansion) is an expansion set to the game Philadelphia. It adds a set of advertising cards, including six that act as multi-industry cards. These cards enable players to arrange hands as composites, but also make the player choose between making money or doing good. Playing these composites cost an extra 3 gold, but make it easier to play more cards.
user summary
publishers: Mogmog Project (モグモグプロジェクト)

description:
可惜夜 (あたらよ) (Atarayo) is a card game from Japan, with a similar feel to UNO, in that you play cards following the same number or colour as played before. But in Atarayo, you can also progress by playing a card that is the same number as the cards in your hand.

In addition, the cards have simple effects such as skip, reverse, draw 2, wild cards and so on. The cards feature Wagara (classic Japanese design patterns) such as Yagasuri, Shippo and others. Players win by shedding their hand first.
user summary
publishers: Mogmog Project (モグモグプロジェクト)

description:
Two new characters for Printing Press - Rafał Szłapa and Felix Catenberg.
One of them, at the end of the turn, lets you take the remaining frame or contract and choose which one to use.
The other one lets you choose a card that was left or lets you move the bonus marker at the end of the game.

—user summary

MILLRAT Pack! A collection of four unique board games created for the perfect game night.

Featuring:

Someone's Y - Everyone's a vowel in this deduction word game, but someone's Y. On your turn say a one word clue and see who takes a token. Use those tokens to pay for abilities and vote out Y! Don't be too obvious as to which vowel you are, because if Y is able to guess who's who, they win instead.

Scrapbook - You're scrapbooking with your friends in this cooperative party game. Manipulate old images on your smartphone and place them in the frame to try and resemble the scrapbook card shown. It's up to the guesser to figure out the correct card based on the clues you gave.

Footfalls - Race your pack of rats to the end of the bridge first to enjoy the scraps of food. Choose an ability to use on your turn and combine it with the previous one to help you move your rats across. Combine them together for an even stronger effect. However, the ability you choose will be given to the next player, so choose wisely.

Bad Eggs - It's a busy morning in the breakfast diner, and you're trying to make the biggest omelette possible. Correctly guess how the dice will roll to give you an eggvantage. Push your luck and flip over as many good eggs as you can without flipping too many bad eggs. Gain abilities, sabotage others, know when to stop, and know when to whisk it all!

A unique game for every situation & any player at any time.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wave Chase Race (ウェーブチェイスレース)
description:
Wave Chase Race (ウェーブチェイスレース) is a race prediction game from Japan. The players are trying to get their predicted sailing team to win the regatta but not the races. The gameplay has various twists. The team that actually wins the race does not score. Instead, the team in 2nd place determines the points distribution.

As teams move along the course, they can get a boost by passing through boats ahead of them. With several boats, this can create large moves. But if a leading group catches up with a lagging group, instead of being lapped, the lagging group simply joins the leaders. So falling behind is not necessarily bad if you can get the leader to catch you up.

The game is played over two races, and the final winner is determined from several factors: the rankings from each race, your predictions during the races, the cards remaining in your hand, points from your colours. The final total gives the eventual winner.

ENG rules are available online.
user summary

1チーム4隻のヨットで行うレースゲームです
カードプレイによるレースゲームですが、「次の空きマスまでヨットは進む」「先頭集団の先頭のヨットが先頭になる（周回遅れのヨット群に追いつくとそこが先頭に吸収される）」
「全員がカードを使い切るまでレースは続く」といった特徴があります。
また、「各チーム2番目のヨットで順位が決まる」「各チーム1番目のヨットはレースから抜ける」ことでレースは少し混沌となります。
前後半2回のラウンドを通じてより多くの点数を獲得すると思うチームに投票し、最終的に投票に使用したチケットの点数が最も多い人が勝者となります。
publisher summary

The Mongoose Mini-Expansion includes five-cards, each tied to the Ancients Trilogy from Mongoose Publishing. These include an escalating series of Encounters, a deadly new Caste, and even a System of hidden wonders.

boardgame: Leaf: Season of the Bear Expansion
description:
As summer turns to autumn, the large animals start to forage in earnest as they prepare for a long winter ahead. Each change of season brings new challenges and rewards, and all the animals of the forest have something to contribute.

The Season of the Bear expansion offers three advanced play modes that can be added to the game Leaf. Animal Allies give each player a unique ability they can use throughout the game. Season Events add extra scoring conditions to each Frost. The Greater Animals affect the whole forest, giving each player an effect they can use once per game.

—description from the publisher

Extend your influence in all areas of power and beat the competition. But don't let your opponents get in your way… Become the Supreme Leader.

Autocracy is a a management and strategy game played with 2 12-faced dices and tokens. 
The first player to have at least 1 Economy Token, 1 Media Token, 1 Justice Token, 1 Army Token and 1 Politics Token during their turn wins the game.

The player who starts is the last to sit at the table. Each player draws 1 Mentor Token and 1 “Dirty Trick” Token. The Mentor Token indicates the influence tokens with which the players will start the game (in addition to there Low Blow). They are immediately collected. Each player in turn rolls the dice to win and accumulate as much influence as possible.

Winning Influence

Each turn, to calculate your winnings, you add your dice roll to your tokens corresponding to the draw. Divide the total obtained for each area of influence and/or “Dirty Trick” by 2 to obtain your winnings.

Influence Peddling

At the start of each round (before rolling the dices) you can trade with the reserve or with another player. The exchange values are fixed, even between players.

There are 5 types of influence areas: Economy, Media, Justice, Army, Politics. The trading values are the following:

1 Media token = 6 Economy tokens

1 Justice token = 2 Media tokens

1 Army token = 3 Justice tokens

1 Politics token = 2 Army tokens

1 “Dirty Trick” token = 2 Justice tokens

(“Dirty Tricks” tokens can’t be converted back into influence tokens)

“Dirty Trick”s

At the start of each round (before rolling the dices) you can use 1 “Dirty Trick” token against an opponent. The attacking player cannot make exchanges, but can roll the dices once the result of his attack is known. Attacks cannot be made on the first turn.

There are 5 types of “Dirty Tricks”

Nationalisation
Make a player lose: all his Economy Tokens and you hijack half of them OR all his Media Tokens and you hijack half of them.

Revolt
Make a player lose half their Justice Tokens AND Army Tokens. You hijack 1 type of lost token of your choice (Justice OR Army).

Blackmail
Take the Influence Token of your choice from an opponent. Blackmail can be countered by blackmail.

Spying
Take a “Dirty Trick” token from an opponent, without showing it to the other players. Spying can be countered by spying (Spying cancelled).

Corruption
Redirect the effects of a Nationalisation or Revolt to another competitor. The diversion cannot be used against the player attacking you. Corruption can be countered by a Corruption (redirection cancelled).  Does not protect against Blackmail.

Defending Yourself
You can counter a “Dirty Trick”. Show your “Dirty Trick” Token which protects you when you are attacked. Only if you want to.

Losing Influence
If you cannot defend yourself against an attack, or if you decide not to, you lose the influence tokens corresponding to the attack suffered.

—description from the designer

The Ad-Austral extension opens the southern air routes to Australia, Africa and South America. This expansion features yellow dice that represent the harsh weather conditions of the desert. A new module is also added with the risk of engine failure so common at this time.
Ad Austral also contains a complete kit for a fifth player as well as a set of new planes and pilots.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Chicago Speakeasy / Rush Belt 2112
description:
This is a two map release for Age of Steam 2023. Maps are printed on high quality poster stock front and back.

Chicago Speakeasy (3 to 5 players recommended), map design by Ashley Miller

The Windy City. Speakeasies and bootleggers in the Roaring ‘20s.  Your stomping grounds are the famous haunts of the likes of Al Capone and other Chicago gangsters. This ain’t gonna be easy. You will need to build the best underground network to deliver the ‘goods’ and ‘handle’ the Feds to come out on top.   
This map is based on 1920s era Chicago speakeasies and the battle with agents from the Bureau of Prohibition to keep them running. Black cubes represent the Bureau agents and may only be delivered to the Bureau of Prohibition. Goods cannot be delivered to Cities containing black cubes. Bribes must be paid for delivering goods through Cities containing black cubes. The special action ‘Bump Off an Agent’ can be used to remove a black cube from the map.

Rush Belt 2112 (3-6 players recommended) , map design by Kevin McCurdy

The year is 2112. Everything has changed. Sea levels have risen. Some say a comet fell from the sky. Followed by tidal waves. Those that survived have built and sheltered in massive dome structures. Finally, the first signs of life above ground are reappearing. Your goal is to explore the abandoned Cities, collect resources, and bring them back to the domes. 
This map is based in the near future in the heart of the USA. Domes start with demands for specific colors of cubes providing +1 income. When the demands are met, the domes become a colored City with +1 income for deliveries to the dome. Players must build out from Domes and explore the land afresh after the great disaster and bring back the high demand goods to succeed.

—description from the publisher

Pharmacology is a dice-driven strategy game featuring dice drafting, worker allocation, engine building, and strategic chaining of actions.

Pharmacology is for two to five players (plus a solo mode), playing in about 15-20 minutes per player. Lead a pharmaceutical company to victory by developing the most lucrative drug treatments and vaccines. You’ll need to send your medical workforce to outbreak locations to secure samples, research drugs, and develop vaccines to fight the world’s diseases.

Each turn is a puzzle to solve. How do you maximize your workforce and resources with the dice available to you in the dice pool? Deftly manipulating your drafted dice can create exciting action chains that make for huge turns!

Drafted dice can be used to power locations to generate temporary workers, medical supplies, resources, and money, which can then be used to power other locations to research drugs and vaccines so you can treat diseases to earn points. Outreach Programs provide abilities that can further increase your engine’s potential output.

Features:

     Unique dice drafting, dice allocation, and action chaining mechanics.
     Simple to learn, but complex puzzles result in feelings of accomplishment every turn.
     Interesting interactions provide layers of depth.
     Build up to amazing turns with action chains.
     Solo Automa provides a challenging competitor that gives the feel of a multiplayer experience. The Automa can defeat you!


Overview:
Pharmacology uses a unique dice drafting mechanic. Everyone drafts from a common pool of Action Dice, which represent your Workforce. On your turn, you will draft dice from the Action Dice Pool equal to your Workforce level, then take your Actions. If there aren’t enough dice in the pool you’ll re-roll all the dice used by other players and then select the rest of your dice.

Using the drafted dice, you will work to collect Samples, use and upgrade various Locations, hire Medical Technicians, Doctors, and Scientists, develop Drugs and Vaccines, gain benefits through Outreach Programs, and Treat Diseases. You can also increase the size of your Workforce, boost your score by specializing in certain Disease Classifications, Quarantine diseases for easier research, and use profits to buy Medical Supplies, re-rolls, additional resources, and more. In an advanced game, you’ll also have opportunities to commit acts of Industrial Espionage and file Lawsuits to hinder your opponents.

Any dice remaining in the Action Dice Pool at the end of your turn are passed to the next player to begin drafting their Workforce. Throughout the game, your Workforce will increase and actions may allow you to draft additional dice. In this way the number of dice passed from player to player changes, resulting in a new puzzle to solve on each turn!

Pharmacology won the 2017 Manhattan Project Dice Game design contest presented by Minion Games. The late James Mathe loved the game, but it was bigger than what he was looking at publishing at the time. He suggested retheming the game (thanks to Troy Pichelman for the new theme idea) and continuing to work on getting it produced.

—description from the publisher

All’s fair in love and land in this ultimate game for Yellowstone fans! MONOPOLY: Yellowstone lets players buy, sell, and trade well-known locations anchored by the Dutton family’s coveted cattle ranch, including Kayce and Monica’s House and the Painted Horse Casino & Hotel. Gear up with collectible cowhand-related tokens and post Wranglers and Foremen to secure your grounds to be the last operator standing with custom Yellowstone currency to win!

Module 5 Economy allows you and the other players to buy and sell shares in aerospace companies whose value is tracked in a matrix called the stock market. The players themselves are the customers for these companies, and with more customers for a customer service, the more capital the company can purchase and the higher their share value. If you own the most shares in a company, you are its chair and decide if dividends are paid out to the shareholders or instead the funds are reinvested into company capital allowing them to have cheaper services. Players buy shares in companies that are likely to rise in value and sell them when their cash could be better used elsewhere. Because shares are so easily bought and dumped, and they retain value after an Aqua crash, holding shares is generally superior to holding Aqua.

—description from the publisher

This is a promotional scenario for Verplant & Zugestellt handed out to buyers of the game at Essen 2023.

Two game geeks have built a house in the same shape as the fair halls in Essen. There is a lot of furniture to place into several fairly small rooms. Unlike in the base game, there is a sixth room into which they can put excess furniture. Additional points can be scored for each of the many doors that remains accessible from both sides.

boardgame: The Dungeon Within: The Empire of Snow
description:
The Dungeon Within: The Empire of Snow is the first expansion for the game The Dungeon Within. Dive deeper in the universe of The Dungeon Within with this expansion. More levels, new characters and brand new game mechanics.

This expansion includes (needs base game to play):

     12 new tiles with Snow rooms
     3 new characters
     new guide documents with new items
     new "partner mechanic" rules update

The pirate's map is a single sheet print and play game, for 1 to 4 players (although you can play more without problems) with many dice rolls, ideal for those players who enjoy games where you have to draw. A quick game for any number of players (30min) where you can have fun snatching the best treasures from your rivals using simple drawings.

Players will take the role of ambitious pirates who explore the seas with the aim of obtaining the greatest loot. To do this, the pirates will draw a map where they will record their misdeeds and discoveries.

The pirate's map is a roll and write game with simultaneous turns. The active player will roll 3 dice. All players will use 2 of them to determine a location on their map, and the remaining die indicates the symbol to draw. Two of the dice are white, ALL players will be able to virtually modify their values by spending rum. The black die can be used ONLY by the active player, both with the visible value and its opposite, giving him more possibilities.

On the game sheet there are areas with which you can interact using the symbols that you draw on your map; these will give you different rewards to increase or make your treasure more valuable.

The game lasts an undetermined number of turns, until one player fills their treasure chest, one player gets 3 skulls (they have gone 3 turns without drawing on their map), or no one draws anything on a turn. The player with the most valuable treasure will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Introducing Greg Sovan’s newest creation, Field Day Baseball. Field Day Baseball takes the tabletop game to the next level of ease of play, tiny learning curve, and fast play results with minimal chart lookups, while still having all the depth of major league baseball. See if you can manage your favorite stars to the pennant.

Baseball simulation game with full season MLB teams. Dice are rolled and pitcher/batter cards are consulted which provide results.

Clueverge is a competitive game for 2-4 players, where players take on the role of detectives and conduct investigations into a murder case. From gradually revealed evidence and suspects, try to identify the True Culprit before anyone else!

The “Investigation Cards” and “Crime Scene Cards” are arranged in a 4x4 grid. Each card contains hidden information about “Suspects”, “Weapons”, “Motives”, and “Clues”.

However, due to “False Testimony”, some cards may contain misleading information. Additionally, some of “the Suspects” may have “Alibis”.

Who among these cards is the True Culprit? Or perhaps the True Culprit has already “Fled the country”?

This game also includes a “Cooperative Play Mode” and a “Solo Play Mode”.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 主の帰りを待ちながら (Waiting For The Master's Return)
description:
主の帰りを待ちながら (Waiting For The Master's Return) is a co-op mystery game from Japan. The game is strictly for 4 players only, who are the four android characters in the game. Through role-play and discussion, they solve the mystery together.

The publisher says...
"Somewhere in Japan, deep in the mountains that doesn't even appear on maps, there is a small research institute. There, Dr. Sakurada and the four androids he developed were conducting research every day. But now, there is no one who appears to be a doctor at the research institute, and four people appear to have lost their memories...

Players will be asked to step into the shoes of a specific character, experience the story, and uncover the truth of this story through discussion.
The first in a series of 'mystery experience games' that combine the story experience elements of a murder mystery game and the truth-finding elements of a mystery-solving game!"

日本のどこか、地図にも載っていないようなとある山奥に、小さな研究所がある。
そこでは、桜田博士と彼が開発した4体のアンドロイドが、日々研究を行っていた。
しかし今、研究所に博士らしき者の姿がなく、
記憶を失った様子の4人が……。

プレイヤーの皆様には、特定のキャラクターの立場になって、物語を体験しながら、議論を通してこの物語の真実を解き明かしていただきます。
マーダーミステリ―ゲームの物語体験要素、謎解きゲームの真相究明要素を組み合わせた、「ミステリー体験型ゲーム」の第一弾！
publishers: Mogmog Project (モグモグプロジェクト)

description:
Town Constructor is a print and play roll and write game for 1-100 players.  Players take the role of town planners to make the wealthiest and most efficient cities they can.

Players play in turns. Each round available buildings for construction and values are changing. The player is the one to decide which dice will determine the market values change, and what buildings will be built this turn. Players look at theirs own town plans and draw buildings to make the most efficient town. Furthermore there are public buildings which need to be drawn in tactical places, because each of them scores in a different way. The trick is to draw the best town possible and score in the right moment, when the market values are high in scored region of the map.

All you need to play is 2 standard 6 sided dice, writing tool and print of a game sheet.

Dead Line is a confrontation card game in which you have to make artists survive until they publish their comics and earn you a maximum of Street Cred! Colorists, scriptwriters, publishers, distributors, booksellers, festival organizers: the army of the book market will be on hand to bring these comic projects to fruition.

But fate will throw in its espelette pepper: backache, tendonitis, computer crashes or even misplaced boards... your opponents will bring life's little surprises down on your artists. Dead Line is a humorous look at the plight of cartoonists in the merciless world of books.

—description from the designer

Dragon’s Nest is an exciting board game where players hatch their own dragon eggs and compete for victory points.

Each player starts with 12 egg cards and 4 secret dragon cards, creating their nests as they play. During the game, players strategically draft eggs and unleash their dragons’ unique abilities to maximize points.

Dragon cards offer various ways to score, from balancing nests to forming number straights. The player with the most points at the end of the game emerges as the ultimate dragon keeper.

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of a gladiator in a future dystopian world and fight for honor and glory in different arenas across the universe to earn your freedom and gain universal fame!

Future Heroes is a fast-paced trick-taking board game that’s meant to be easy to pick up and with a small footprint, perfect as the in-between game or when you’re in the mood for something fun and light. Best suited for 2-4 players.

—description from the publisher

A soccer trivia game where the team advances toward the opposing goal by answering correctly, with the questions getting harder the closer to the goal you get. If your answer is wrong, the opposing team takes the ball and begins answering questions.
The game ends after an agreed-upon amount of time.

—user summary

サイクリアン (Cyclian) is a quick network building game from Japan. Players use just 12 of the 24 tiles in the game, meaning a 3P game each player plays 4 turns, a 4P game they only have 3 turns.

Whilst the graphic design is very abstract, the game is explicitly about operating a rail network. Players start from their own colour stations as connectors, and build and manipulate routes. The tiles show lines and curves, with 2 lines on each side, in one or two colours on each tile. There are stations which show two rail companies crossing.

The goal is to build the longest section on the board that connects two of your stations. But the track does not have to be all your own colour. In the manner of the private rail companies in Japan, trains run on other people's tracks. So the objective is to get the longest run of your colour from into the whole board and out of the board somewhere else, by which two of your stations connect, thus controlling that line. A section scores 1 point for each tile, and also 1 point for each opponent's station on the section.

However, on their turn, a player can turn a placed tile 90 degrees before placing their own tile from their hand. This is not rotating the tile in place. Instead, it spins 90 degrees on one corner, so moving the tile (and possibly connecting tiles) to an adjacent space. In this way, a player's high scoring section can be broken up.

When all the tiles are played, the player controlling the highest scoring section is the winner.

The designer says this is the first game in a 'Minimalian' series, the next being Serealian.
user summary

The Skirmisher #4 is the fourth magazine in the award-winning Great Campaigns of the American Civil War (GCACW) series of games.

Skirmisher #4 is centered around the reprint of Stonewall Jackson's Way II (SJW2), so players should own this game (or the original SJW) to fully enjoy all the elements of Skirmisher #4.

Skirmisher #4 contains the following scenarios and articles:

    “The Skirmish Line” which discusses upcoming GCACW plans and games.
    Lee’s Last Offensive, a mini-module on the Bristoe Station and Mine Run campaigns in the Fall of 1863. Designed by Niall Taylor there are 4 basic scenarios, 3 campaign scenarios, and a detailed “Game as History” article.  These scenarios use the SJW2 maps.
    A colorful set of playtest reports on the Lee’s Last Offensive scenarios.
    Battle for the Gaps, a cavalry scenario in June 1863, when the Confederate and Union cavalry engaged just west of Aldie (on the SJW2 north map) as the Gettysburg campaign was developing.
    A detailed strategy article on optimal ways to play the All Green Alike campaign.
    A lengthy replay of a SJW2 scenario, filled with images to be easy-to-follow.
    An article on battlefield staff rides that Joe Balkoski conducts for the army.
    A listing of all that is GCACW on-line, tournaments, etc.


  
Skirmisher #4 components list:

    56 color pages
    A half counter sheet (140 counters) for the included scenarios


-description from publisher
family: Series: Great Campaigns of the American Civil War (Avalon Hill/MMP)

boardgame: The Egg Game: Complete the challenges, don't drop the egg!
description:
Complete the challenges, don't drop the Egg!

The Egg Game is our brilliant new party game where you have to complete hilarious challenges whilst trying not to drop the egg.

Before pulling a challenge card, players can choose to use the bouncy rubber egg for one point, or up the ante and use a real egg for 3 points (optional)

Don’t be the one to crack under pressure!

Guaranteed entertainment and endless laughter for both kids and adults, this eggcellent game will surely take top spot on your game shelf!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Get Wavey: The Hilariously Weird Party Game
description:
Get Wavey is the ultimate family party game of general knowledge with a wavey twist!

You're about to get wavey...

This hilarious new party game will have you feel like you’re slurring your words as you attempt to read out wavey sounding questions that will totally throw off your opponents.

Can you guess what your wavey sounding friends are saying?

How to Play

Place the pile of question cards face down on the table. The youngest player starts by turning over a card and immediately reading the distorted question out loud (without attempting to pre-read it or looking at the answer…no cheating!).

All other players have 60 seconds to figure out the question and the answer. The first player to shout out the correct answer wins the card.

If no player can guess the answer after 60 seconds, the reader can turn the card upside down and read the question in plain English. The first player to answer correctly wins the card.

First to collect 10 cards wins the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Peanuts Charlie Brown: Trim the Tree Game
description:
Charlie Brown wanted to get a tree with heart, but it’s going to need some love! Hang ornaments everywhere you can without losing the big red ornament. Work together and use a light touch to brighten Charlie Brown’s Christmas!


     Cooperative gameplay brings your family together for a lively holiday game night!
     Charlie Brown’s iconic Christmas tree takes center stage! Play as a team to decorate it – and be careful not to bend it too much!
     Featuring 18 ornaments to hang on the tree, with art in the classic Peanuts style!
     Use special star ornaments to help in a pinch and lighten up your tree!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Nickelodeon Rugrats: The Meanie of Chanukah
description:
As the first night arrives, it’s time for the Rugrats to learn the story of Chanukah. Dreidels, gelt, and latkes await—but will the “Meanie” of Chanukah spoil the holiday? Bring old rivals Grandpa Boris and the mysterious Shlomo together, and they will shed light on the real meaning of Chanukah!


     Your favorite Rugrats characters come to life on the table, capturing the spirit of the acclaimed holiday special!
     Engaging tile-matching gameplay uses your memory to build beautiful Chanukah candles!
     Dozens of original illustrations showcase classic Chanukah imagery and tell the beloved Rugrats story.
     Build your candles higher to score more points – but make sure to light them in time, or they won’t score at all!


—description from the publisher

Object: To be the first to win 7 cards.

Preparing to play:

Shuffle the cards and place them in a pile (with their backs showing) on the table.
Just next to the pile, arrange the four totems in 4 differents colours around a space big enough to hold a card.

The youngest player turns over the top card from the deck and lays it maze side up in the middle of the four totems. The players all play at the same time.
As quickly as possible, all of the players visually follow the path indicated by the arrows, starting with the one outlined in red, and as far as the arrow indicating the exit. The first player to spot the exit grabs the corresponding totem.

Which totem should be grabbed depends on the symbol showing on the back of the top card on the deck.
If you are the first player to grab the right totem you win the maze card that you place in front of you as a point won.

—description from the publisher

アマロン (Amaron) is a simplified Shogi style game from Japan, using a 4 x 6 grid. The two players move their hieroglyph pieces as indicated, trying to capture their opponent's Eye Of Horus piece to win.

To assist them, there are black cubes called Amaron (meaning Energy) they can collect and spend to get extra turns. But if a player gets three Amaron, they explode and are sent to Hell. So another player option is to curse the opponent to give them a cube, to make them explode and lose.

The game is untended to be played rapidly and can finish in just a minute. The game has a cloth board, branded wood pieces, and five black Amaron cubes.
user summary

boardgame: Disney Winnie the Pooh: Snow Parade Game
description:
Help Winnie the Pooh and friends put together a holiday parade! Work together to match colors, moving the characters forward and backward. If you need an extra hand, Christopher Robin is always there! Get all the characters in the right order to win!


     Delightful cooperative gameplay brings players of all ages together to succeed.
     Original illustrations bring the snowy celebration to life on your table.
     Use your cards to place the characters in the right order, or pair them with other players’ for more exciting moves!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rudolph the Red-Nose Reindeer: Snowstorm Scramble Game
description:
Journey with Rudolph, Hermey, and Yukon Cornelius across the North Pole. Scamper through snowstorms to outrun Bumble the Abominable Snow Monster! Work together and help all three friends reach the end to win!


     Quick-paced cooperative play brings everyone together to remember clues and push your luck!
     Find the characters’ matching tokens to move them forward…but watch out for Bumble in the storm!
     Play solo or on a team of up to five players!


—description from the publisher

Discover the hilarious world of Unicorn Poop through its illustrated cartoon-style cards featuring animals and insects in their most intimate moments. From the philosophical gaze of a pooping unicorn to the vacant stare of a rhino, this deck is packed with heaps of laughter.

Party All You Want
Whether you have 2, 4, or 8 players, Unicorn Poop has got you covered. A single deck is perfect for 2-4 players, while two decks provide endless fun for a party of 8. It's a choose-your-own adventure kind of game, just like your unique bathroom experience. Each round lasts for 5-15 minutes, exactly the amount of time nature intended for a poo game to be played.

—description from the designer

Share a smile and pass a pair of Coca-Cola Polar Bears in this lively push-your-luck game! Each turn adds bigger rewards as you roll the bears to match and score. You can stop anytime, or keep rolling for more points...just don’t get frozen out! Enjoy at your next game night!


     Roll the wooden Polar Bears like dice to press your luck and score points!
     13 double-sided tiles feature all sorts of different ways to score * and are worth more points as the game heats up!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Waste Knights: Second Edition – Krewni Pyrkonu 2 Promo Cards
description:
This supplement contains 2 cards: KRWIOBUS and ZAPACH KRWI, which are part of the 'Talia Krewni Pyrkonu 2' received for donating blood at the Pyrkon '23 convention. Available only in Polish language.

The wilderness card titled KRWIOBUS should be shuffled during game preparation into the highway/desert wilderness deck found in the base game Waste Knights: Second Edition and used as described in the instructions.

Place the ZAPACH KRWI card into the Sea deck of the Beyond the Horizon expansion pack during game preparation and use as described in the expansion pack instructions.

boardgame: Kinfire Chronicles: Night's Fall – Upgrade Kit
description:
Upgrade your copy of Kinfire Chronicles: Night's Fall with the following items:


     50 metal fate tokens
     46 acrylic enemy standees & bases
     6 neoprene player mats
     150 custom card sleeves (6 designs of 25 each)
     1 blank quest folio
     gold-diamond treasure pack with player cards (can be added with treasure packs from base game)
     13 exclusive character skin cards
     premium audio narration by Forteller

Overtake: Race Manager is an exhilarating, competitive racing management board game. Players represent managers of corporations vying for supremacy in the high-stakes world of motorsport racing. You're tasked to build your fleet of cutting-edge cars, strategically allocate staff and resources, and outmaneuver rival corporations on the track to secure the prestigious title of Motorsport Racing Champion.

Scoring rounds resolve through races, where skilled drivers in finely-tuned machines compete for the podium. Success translates to prestige, points, and funding - assuming you've managed to entice sponsors.

Gameplay scales with game progression and is tightly crafted to reflect the tension of racing management. Features include:

An original cast featuring men and women from 31 countries.
Action prioritization of resources to recruit staff, build cars, and acquire sponsors. 
Set collection bonuses unlock through headquarter expansion.
Streamlined 10-car racing events, including variable skill level automa corporations.
Racing that factors strategic decisions with car development and team agility with track weather conditions.

Compete over a year, a series comprised of 6 races, to etch your name into the halls of racing history!

—description from the designer

A truly special collectors item for both Porsche and board game enthusiasts alike. Designed under the direction of Porsche Design, very few of these games were actually made and distributed through official Porsche channels.

“Porsche Design is a German product design studio and brand founded in 1972 by F. A. Porsche, the designer of the original Porsche 911. The current legal instantiation of the company, Porsche Lizenz- und Handelsgesellschaft mbH & Co. KG, based in Ludwigsburg, Germany, was formed in November 2003 as a majority-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG to combine Porsche AG's and Porsche Design Group's accessories and licensing business into one single company.”
-from Porsche Design Wikipedia

Welcome to one of the world most renowned test tracks.

You are one of the 3 to 6 “911 Cup driver” who are vying for the title of “best Porsche driver” on the Porsche testing grounds in Weissach in their racing cars.

The goal is simple: beat the others to it and be the first to cross the finish line in your Porsche Cup vehicle! You must try to stick to the ideal line and take the lead in as many track sections as possible.

A race take place over one lap.

You will have to fight for you position on every section of the track.

The various acceleration and braking sections give you the opportunity to display your talents.

Of course, you don’t have an unlimited supply of fuel and the brakes can also only handle so much. That’s why you can only the points on each card once. And added to that are those unpredictable events that make racing so exciting!

So it’s just like in real life: the winner will need his wits, some good fortune and, of course, the right vehicle!
New Box, familiar factions. Collecting the Witches, Ghosts and Trolls into the same size box as Vol. 1 and Vol. II, complete with room for storing a neoprene playmat.

Embark on an exhilarating journey through World War II with our captivating board game, featuring ICONIC miniatures, intricate 3D battle locations, immersive imagery, and...top-secret folders!

To secure victory, players must skillfully navigate through three pivotal phases:

Launching Campaign: Strategically identify allies and build an entry-level military deck to receive your classified route assignment.

Completing the Route: Collaborate closely with your allies to secure key strategic victories and outmaneuver the advancing enemy forces.

Final Battle: Harness all available military resources to mount a decisive assault, bringing an end to the enemy campaign and seizing ultimate victory.

—description from the designer

Looking for the spiritual journey of a lifetime? Hold onto your butts, travelers. The race to enlightenment begins. From the visionary artist behind The Wild Unknown Tarot comes a board game that requires the winner to lose it all.

In this cutthroat competition against karma itself, two to six players—with the help of the game’s “Adventure Guide”—will embrace new personas and race up the sacred mountain toward the cave of enlightenment. The problem: this ancient cave must be entered empty-handed. Players start with a bounty of material possessions and must learn to let go of each attachment, which is easier said than done.

Meanwhile, the winding path up the mountain is full of tricks, slips, backstabs, and sticky fingers. Put fate and free will to the test, avoid the karmic loop (where you could get stuck for the rest of eternity!), and make decisions carefully, considering the cosmic effect you’ll have on your allies, enemies, and all humankind.

What new attachments will you pick up along the way? Beer? A European lover? Your vinyl collection? Will the Astrologer steal a yacht from the Venture Capitalist? Will the Poet give up their rent-controlled New York City apartment and their trust fund? Will the Healer get stuck in the karmic loop? Will the Principal go to rehab?

Only you determine your destiny. Only you can lose to win. May good karma be with you—and all who travel the path of Renunciation.

—description from the publisher

Penguin's Iceberg Adventure Cooperative Game from Mudpuppy is a fun new game that builds community, encourages respect, and supports social emotional development. Players work together to flip tiles and match shapes to build an iceberg path; but be careful of revealing a snowflake tile and revealing an approaching storm. Help the baby penguin cross safely to his mama and everyone wins!

A worker placement game set in Ancient Egypt.

Perform good deeds for the people of the village and complete your secret mission to earn your place in the afterlife!

Become a fisherman, hunter, trader, explorer and more!

Try different strategies for reaching your goal: Call in favors from the Gods or try to become the richest person in the market! There are tons of possibilities!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Focus!: Last Roll for the Hangul
description:
In this turn-based push-your-luck strategy game for 1-5 players you take the role of a Wildlife Photographer, visiting the Kashmir Valley in an attempt to photograph and publish photos of the critically endangered Hangul. Set in 1970, you are using colour film to take photos, and the game begins when you are down to your last 20 exposures. So, what will you do? Track down the elusive Hangul, or settle for other wildlife? What publishing deals will you manage to obtain—and what photos do they require? Push yourself too hard, and you may end up scaring the wildlife or worse — wasting precious film!

Focus! Last Roll for the Hangul deploys multiple mechanism to recreate the thrilling challenge of wildlife photography: the unpredictability of the animals is reflected by dice, while the challenge of getting published is represented by cards that specify which animals to photograph for the publication. A modular board is used to speed up the process of updating the position of all the fast moving animals on the table.

On on your turn you spend Action Tokens to execute actions like moving to a new area, adding more Animal Tokens to the board, negotiating Contracts before they are gone, and taking photos. When an animal is successfully photographed, the targeted Animal Token is collected from the board. To take the photo however, you must be in the same area as the animal, which means there is a constant pressure to move around the board. You will additionally have to balance the need for a sharp photo with the need to conserve film. And all of this, is wrapped in a push-your-luck mechanic that allows you to repeat any successful action by increasing the chance of failure.

A new round will not start if two of multiple game ending conditions arise, allowing you to tactically end the game when it secures your victory. You win the game by having the most victory points, which are earned by the photos you take — and doubled by getting them published.

—description from the designer

boardgame: King of the Valley: The Festival
description:
The Festival is the first expansion to the game King of the Valley. This game introduces several commoners wandering around the valley seeking entertainment, a few new specialists and several interesting festivities (locations) where the players can gain gold, actions or reputation.

Players can pick up commoners for free, during their quest to form Kingdoms and to claim subjects for their goals. As soon as they pick up the commoners, they must send them to one of the five active locations to generate income, reputation or to activate an executioner or a fortune teller.

Still excellent for 2 players, but now there is more fun (flesh and bones) to the game for 3 & 4 players. Due to the mini-games created by the workerplacement system with the commoners, there will be much more dilemmas.

Players have to decide if they send a commoner to drink beer in the Bierstube to gain gold, or contribute to culture by sending them to the theatre. They can also be sent to the Jousting Field, influence the outcome and the King has more knowledge about the income. He can bet on the knight he expects to be the winner of the tournament. Plenty to choose from, but the interaction between the players is strong, since they influence the revenues for each other.

The specialists of the basic game, King of the Valley, have more up their sleeves, since the Priest can also brew and serve beer. The Wizard is able to use his magic to make the show at the theatre truly magnificent. And the Tax Collectors get a chance to compete with each other in the Treasury Room. A place where kings can show in a "majority-game" to get additional taxes.

The game play is extended with 15 minutes on average, for all number of players. 2 players: 45 minutes, 3&4: 60 minutes.

—description from the publisher

The Guinea Pigs live happily and contentedly in their beautiful Pig Palace. They will never leave their cosy place unless you entice them with their favourite treats. Maybe they'll join you in a game - try it!

Competitive game with a tactical variant for 2 - 4 players, from 3 years old, (4 and 5 with variants) for a 10 minute game.

—description from the rulebook

A promo resin miniature included with Ashes Magazine: Messen Spiel 2023 #8 from Ludus Magnus Studio. It contains rules to be used in Black Rose Wars, Black Rose Wars Rebirth, Black Rose Wars Lex, Nova Aetas Renaissance and Requiem.

Heroes, the mighty and cunning dragon Fafnir — once a mighty nobleman, now transformed into a beast by a cursed treasure — is trying to burn our village to the ground from afar by launching fireballs from his lair in the mountains. You must answer the call and travel with the champion Sigurd through Heathland both to build defenses to protect the village and to collect runes needed to defeat the beast. Fafnir will fall to either Sigurd's sword or one of the heroes' strikes...or will he? While most heroes quest for glory in The Flames of Fafnir to receive the town's honor, one might seek victory through more nefarious means by joining Fafnir in his destruction of the village!

The fireballs launched from Fafnir's mouth toward the town are represented by marbles that will collide with anything in their path, damaging heroes and destroying defenses. Each round, Fafnir acts by changing his angle of attack, spawning monsters, or building up his fiery breath. Eventually, he will release all his charged fireballs towards the town — and if all the town's walls are destroyed, everyone loses.

While walking the Heathland, you'll encounter mythological creatures and monsters; slaying them will aid you in your quest as snow trolls, giant spiders, and selkies guard magical sites where ancient runes can be found. Claim these runes and bring them to bear on Fafnir to kill the dragon. You can gather wood, stone, and gold to construct defenses to protect the town. Barricades, watch towers, and trenches will reduce the carnage, and if your structures intercept the fireballs, you'll be rewarded, so plan your defense well. Praying to Gods and raising defenses will reward you with powerful artifacts that allow you to improve your movement, gain more glory, weaken the dreaded Fafnir, and more.

If an opportunistic hero chooses to join Fafnir, they get to ride the dragon, turning on the other heroes thanks to rider cards while trying to destroy the town in an attempt to take victory for their self!

It is a dynamic game of observation and speed.

Ideal to play with family or friends.

The objective of the game is to score the most points.

The game has cards with pairs of animals, and there are 3 identical cards from each of those pairs. You have to be careful when and where the cards are repeated because we all play at the same time and whoever reacts correctly first will take the cards.

Look for the repeated card and try to identify it before someone else does! But be careful! Don't make mistakes because they are penalized with a -1 card.

1854: The Alma is a small footprint grand tactics scale solitaire minigame on the iconic battle between Russia, France, England and Turkey at the outset of the Crimean War. Allied units are mostly brigades while Russian units are regiments, each hex is around 500m, and each turn is one hour.

1854 is mechanically a streamlined combination of our Imperial Bayonets series as well as Koniggratz and the rules only clock in at around 6 pages. The game features no CRT and can be played opposed making it an excellent intro into wargaming for new gamers while providing a satisfying challenge to more experienced gamers.

—description from the publisher

Represent your family by hosting a grand event with amazing drinks, only some of which will send certain parts of the aristocracy to the infirmary. At the same time, you'll have to to find your own patrons a unsullied glass of wine amongst the impostors.

This expansion includes 2 New Magic Spells for Wandering Towers. New Spells included are Fill a Potion Bottle and Extra Die Roll.

FILL A POTION BOTTLE: If you use this magic spell, you may flip one of your own empty magic potion flask to the full side.

EXTRA DIE ROLL: If you use this magic spell, you may roll a new movement value with the die after a movement card is played, instead of moving the value indicated on the card. If the movement card shows a die or dice, you may roll the die again. In both cases you have to move the result of the roll.

- description from publisher

Trick-taking to “buy” leads: Expansion for up to 9 players
An expansion set that allows up to 9 people to play "Cryptrick", a trick-taking game where you buy leads.
A new strategy is born as blocks that were previously obtained by only one winner can now be divided among multiple people.

Add a new level of strategic play with 16 pieces to add to your main game to really mix it up.

Mix expansion pieces face down with main game pieces. Blue Wild sides can play or be played on by any colour. Heights must still match.

Expansion Rules:

- Full Wild - Play this single-coloured blue wild piece in any available space, except adjacent to blocking pieces. Once played, the player can play a second game piece.

- Blocking Wild - This piece works like a normal Blocking piece, but the blue Wild coloured side can be placed against any colour game pieces, except another Blocking piece. You must still wait untill after the third game piece has been played before playing a Blocking wild piece.

- Colour Wild - Play the blue Wild side in any available space, except adjacent to blocking pieces. You can also play a matching colour side, leaving the Wild side exposed. Another player can play any colour against the w=Wild side. If the Wild side is partially exposed, a player must match the colour that the Wild side is attatched to or used another wild.

In Lairen, two players will dive into an epic battle between good and evil for the control of Lairen, a magical and medieval land full of discord and mysteries to explore.

Players collect cards and build decks out of their collection to duel against an opponent's deck. With those cards, players will try to win the game by either reducing opponent's life from twenty to zero or by making the opponent run out of cards to draw.

An important part of the game is deck construction, which is done prior to the actual game by selecting what cards are included in a particular deck. Each player will have two decks: A "Kingdom" deck with between 45 and 60 cards (exact amount to be chosen by the player), this deck is composed of three types of cards: Action, Unit and Monument cards. The second deck, known as a "Vault", is composed of 15 Treasure cards.

description:
Soloventure is a single player RPG adventure campaign that fits within a single tuck box for play anywhere, anytime.

The game provides 4 heroes to choose from, a randomized procedurally generated dungeon, random encounters, and even a dice deck for on-the-go play. Everything you need in one portable box.

From 1943 to the end of World War Two, both the American Office of Strategic Services and the British Special Operations Executive (SOE) materially assisted the anti-Fascist, anti-Nazi Partisan movement in Italy. Teams of agents were inserted behind the Axis lines by submarine or parachute to make contact with the Partisan groups and brigades.

An example of this is Operation Canuck, an SOE mission undertaken near Turin in 1945. Captain Robert “Buck” MacDonald of Nova Scotia, seconded from the Royal Canadian Dragoons to the 2nd British Special Air Service Regiment, arrived with his team in the area of Alba, a large town about 30 km from Turin, in January 1945. They set up their main base in the village of Castino and began to work with local Partisan leaders to train and arm fighters and arrange air drops of supplies and weapons. The climax of the operation was on 26 April 1945 when their combined forces liberated Alba and forced the occupiers to surrender, several days before Allied troops arrived in the area.

Operation CANUCK is a short game that portrays the activities of this mission in an abstract way. The game is intended for solitaire play. During the game the single player (the Partisan) will draw from a deck of ordinary playing cards. The Partisan will deliberately select from the red cards drawn to execute operations representing the efforts of their forces and draw randomly from the black cards to show the Fascist, German and collaborator (collectively, “Axis”) counter-actions.

This is a free print-and-play game. Besides the material downloaded, players need 6 dice and a deck of ordinary playing cards.

—description from the designer

description:
Zpátky do lavic ("Back to School") is a trivia game that will test your knowledge from elementary school.

In the game you will find 1200 questions divided into 6 subjects - Czech language, mathematics, history, science, geography and civics.

Everyone play at the same time and the task is to answer 4 questions from the given subject within the time limit. You get a grade for each subject and at the end of the game you will receive a report card.

The game can be enhanced by action cards, which allow you to cheat(i.e. copy an answer from your classmate).

description:
From 1943 to the end of World War Two, both the American Office of Strategic Services (OSS) and the British Special Operations Executive (SOE) materially assisted the anti-Fascist, anti-Nazi Partisan movement in Italy.

This themed module for the Mastering Resistance  game system abstracts and fictionalizes the work of OSS teams in and around Turin during the final months of World War Two in Italy. The Mastering Resistance basic system consists of rules for a single player to practice and play through a series of missions and operations typical of a covert team sent in to organize and direct resistance within a country in conflict, what the US Special Forces call “unconventional warfare”. The general idea is to present this along the lines of the USMC tactical vignettes book Mastering Tactics, via the popular genre of simple solitaire interactive “book wargames”. 

This is a free, print-and-play solitaire wargame (with the possibility for 2 players). Besides the downloaded material for the game, players need 8-10 dice, 1 deck of ordinary playing cards (2 decks if playing the 2 or 3-player experimental versions) and 3 containers for markers.

—description from the designer

description:
Balance Yer Mateys or Overboard Ye Go!

Take turns trying to balance your Pirate Penguins on the ship. But if you rock the boat, some mateys may be overboard and even fall into the water.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Wyrdhollow – Voidcursed Thralls Rivals Deck
description:
A curious affliction has been observed in Wyrdhollow – The energy from loosed Realmshaper Engines is striking living creatures and altering them to some unknown purpose. More sinister still, those affected appear to lose their volition, becoming vessels for a power and will not their own. While little is known about these emotionless Voidcursed Thralls, what is certain is that they are entirely devoted to their mysterious cause, forgoing sleep, safety, and in some cases survival. Those who seek to prevent or defeat them risk falling prey to the same cosmic hex, their flesh being slowly claimed by the star-dotted blackness of the Void itself, until they are little more than silhouettes.

Get stuck into Warhammer Underworlds or mix up your game with the complete Voidcursed Thralls Rivals deck, usable by any warband – including those from past seasons. These cards can be used as a standalone deck in Rivals games, mixed with a warband's unique cards for Nemesis games, or used to build a deck from the ground-up in Relic games. This deck doesn’t include any spells, so warbands from any season can put it to good use.

Each card is brand new to the game, focused on making your fighters Voidcursed so they can unleash hidden powers. Aggressive warbands with numerous fighters such as the Grinkrak’s Loorncourt, The Headsmen’s Curse, and Ephilim’s Pandemonium will find Voidcursed Thralls particularly suited to their play style.

This deck contains a 33-card universal Rivals deck, consisting of:

     12x objective cards
     10x gambit cards
     10x upgrade cards
     1x plot card with additional rules for using the Voidcursed Thralls deck


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Wyrdhollow – Paths of Prophecy Rivals Deck
description:
In the arcane caverns of the Wyrdhollow, cosmic omens and dire portents abound. Those with the wit to interpret these signs can avert the vicissitudes of cruel fate and lead their followers to fortune untold. Such warbands follow strange auguries and subterranean constellations that point them to places of power, lost relics, and objects that can shape the very future. Will you walk the Paths of Prophecy to a glittering destiny, or be cursed to strife and obscurity?

Get stuck into Warhammer Underworlds or mix up your game with the complete Paths of Prophecy Rivals deck, usable by any warband – including those from past seasons. These cards can be used as a standalone deck in Rivals games, mixed with a warband's unique cards for Nemesis games, or used to build a deck from the ground up in Relic games. This deck doesn’t include any spells, so warbands from any season can put it to good use.

Each card is brand new to the game, focused on seizing and holding specific objectives according to their prophetic significance. Warbands with lots of models which look to control the board, such as Skabbik's Plaguepack and Grinkrak's Looncourt, will find Paths of Prophecy particularly suited to their play style.

This deck contains a 32-card universal Rivals deck consisting of:

     12x objective cards
     10x gambit cards
     10x upgrade cards
     1x background card that includes a deck list of the Paths of Prophecy cards


—description from the publisher

Get ready for an innovative, high-tech twist to game night with Pictionary vs. AI. What is known and loved about Pictionary is elevated to a whole new level when it's human sketches pitted against AI guesses. But the real points are earned by predicting which sketches the AI will guess correctly.

Pictionary vs. AI challenges players to draw a picture of an object or idea and then uses a neural network artificial intelligence to guess what the drawings represent.


     Players sketch and the AI guesses with this new way to play Pictionary.



     Will the AI guess the drawing that kind of looks like a crocodile and the one that really looks like pizza? Players place a token with their predictions and win points if they guessed correctly.



     The first player to cross the finish line wins in this fun game for kids and adults alike!



     App designed with children's privacy in mind.


—description from publisher

Enter the ring for a fast-smackin' slug-fest with Rock 'Em Sock 'Em Robots Fight Cards, a wild, team-based party game. One player on each team wears a robot-themed boxing glove, and whenever matching cards are played, the gloved players race to smack the cards with their gloves. Fastest smackdown wins the cards! Perfect for the whole family, just pass each glove to a teammate so everyone gets a chance to battle for the most cards! Colors and decorations may vary.

Each team wears a padded robot boxing glove – either red or blue. Players take turns flipping over cards until there's a match, then SMACK 'EM! Gloved players race to punch the cards and win the pile!
Teammates swap their glove each round so everyone gets a chance to bring the smackdown! For added craziness, special cards force players to toss their gloves to a teammate before making the smackdown!

—description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description:
Earth is crumbling! Piece by piece, chunks of the earth have floated off to space with only an ice flow from the Antarctic circle left. The earth’s remaining inhabitants, two colonies of penguins, fight for self-preservation. As chunks of ice fall off, penguins will maneuver to the best location of the ice to save themselves as the expense of others. Which penguin colony will have the Last Penguins on Earth.

The Last Penguins on Earth is a two player, dice drafting game where players try to maneuver their penguins away from the disappearing ice chunks onto safer parts of the ice pack. But be careful, for the very icebreak you draw could be used against you! And you could be bumped into oblivion if you get stuck between the edge and your opponent. If a player’s opponent loses all of their penguins, that player wins the game.

Each turn, players will alternate drafting dice for up to three turns and place them on their player boards. Player will decide if their dice will be used for movement or breaking ice. Players will then alternate turns activating their dice. Once all of the dice on the player boards are used. The rounds end and the dice will be rolled to start a new round. Time (and ice) is running out.  The player who has one or more of their penguins left on the board, wins.

—description from the designer

As a volunteer conservationist, it is your job to hike to the Crystal River, keep track of the health of the river and the animals, fish and insects that reside at the Crystal River. With notebook in hand, you will spend time recording what wildlife you spot around the river. This can be a little tricky, since the water moves very quickly, and you have to jump around on floating logs. You have to watch out for floating debris, and you don’t want to get swept away down river. But you love your job and the danger associated with it. Can you find all of the animals, fish and insects without getting swept down river?

Keeper of the Crystal River is a solo, tile moving game where your goal is to mark off the animals, fish and insects in your journal that you have observed at the river. Each turn you will move your keeper, after which, you will shift all of the river sticks down one. As you jump on a log with animal, fish or insect icon, you will move a cube on your player to the right to indicate another one has been spotted. Beware of floating debris, it can be quite a challenge for the most seasoned observer. If you find everything in your journal and cross the river, you win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ハイエナ勇者はサボリたい (Hyena Hero Wants to Slack Off)
description:
You are "hyena heroes" who fight in packs Their usual style is to work together to defeat monsters. But everyone thinks "I just want to be able to slack off somehow..."

All players work together to defeat enemy monsters that attack one after another. Each monster has a damage value. Players simultaneously choose an attack card from their hand. When revealed, if everyone's attacks add up to the monster's value, they win. If they fall short of the monster's damage value, they have failed that battle.

When a monster is defeated, it's the player who slacks off the most (plays the lowest value attack card) that gets points. However, if everyone slacks off too much and cannot defeat the enemy, the person who slacks off the most will receive a penalty instead! So choose wisely!

description:
Airbattle RAG is the journal of adventure and learning,
expanding your world of jet dogfighting with new aircraft, missiles, and scenarios! Airbattle RAG 6 features all the new content released at two recent Birds of Prey Conventions.

“Strike from the Sea” was the theme of the 2021 Birds of Prey Convention and featured fights where at least one side launched from the deck of an aircraft carrier. Scenarios range from the icy waters of the Baltic, to the ever-tense Taiwan Straight; between the the two warring Koreas, and over the sweltering sands of Egypt.

“The Aircraft We’ve Longed to Fly” of the 2022 Convention featured aircraft that the designers and previous convention attendees selected as those they most wanted to see that had not yet been featured in a product. This adds some very exciting new aircraft, including uncovered gems from the early cold war though ultramodern aircraft that have barely entered service!

All this, plus several aircraft and missiles not featured at the conventions, make Airbattle RAG 6 a substantial addition to your air combat collection!

Good luck and good hunting!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Birds of Prey (Ad Astra Games)

description:
The rise of industrialization during the Victorian Era enabled both the mass production of a type of glazed pottery known as majolica as well as its spread to the Far East, including Japan, China, Taiwan, and Southeast Asia. The name “Majolica” comes from the Spanish name for the island of Maio (Cape Verde), which became renowned for (and later synonymous with) its export of high quality colorful glazed pottery tiles.

In the game of Majolica, players take turns to collect majolica tiles from the outlying sides of a 4 x 4 grid and place them in one of their four workshops. When the ratio of the tile colors matches the requirement of the workshop, you may deliver the tiles to your design card and "carry-over" the remaining tiles to the next workshop.

Choose the tiles wisely, arrange your design cards in the right place, and "recycle" your remaining tiles efficiently are the keys to score the most points and win the game.

Majolica: Revised Edition is the new edition of Majolica@2018. Its mechanism was improved and all new art is inside it now!

No costume party can compare to the annual Grimm Fairytale Ball. All of the wealthy elites and influential people of Valley Falls will be there. For years, you’ve imagined what such an event would be like in person, but you have never been rich nor important enough to receive an invitation.  Yet, all of that changes when the hostess of the party, Cynthia Grimm, hires you to uncover the identity of a blackmailer who calls themselves the Pied Piper. The Pied Piper has threatened to reveal family secrets that would destroy Miss Grimm if their demands aren’t met. These secrets have the potential to harm not only Miss Grimm but all of the charities she is a part of. Your client believes the Piper will be at the ball and has given you open access to the guests in attendance. This time you have no intention of paying the Piper. Instead, you plan to beat them at their own game!  So, grab a costume, investigate all of the fairy tale characters that have shown up for the evening, and see if you can solve the Mystery at the Fairytale Ball.

For a few months now, people have gone missing while hiking alone in Easthaven Forest. Some people believe it is the work of the South Ridge Stalker. The Stalker is half urban legend, half campfire story. He is said to appear to lone hikers in the woods, and those who encounter him are never seen again. When four hikers stumble across what they believe to be The Stalker’s lair, you, a Will Street Detective, are sent out to investigate the site. Will this lead be a dead end, or will you find evidence to corroborate The Stalker's existence and solve the mystery of so many disappearances?

Difficulty Rating – 7/10


    This mystery was never a part of our monthly subscription service. It was released as a one-time purchase.

Your career as an investigative journalist has provided you a thorough education on the darkness that lives in the hearts of some men. Over the years, you have developed quite a network of informants and tipsters, and today, one of those has dropped a story into your lap about another black-hearted soul who has been mentioned in the news lately. According to the tipster, someone anonymously dropped off a roll of film. While it was being developed, the photo tech noticed that some of the images on the roll showed the grisly scene of what appeared to be a murder. This is the third in a string of recent killings. In each instance, the details of the murder have been revealed by a roll of film that has been dropped off at a different photo center. Police are at a loss on how to track down the killer the media has dubbed “The Darkroom Devil.” Thankfully, another of your informants works inside the police department and has given you access to everything they have on this killer. Can you step in and find the hidden clues and bring this devil to justice?

Difficulty Rating – 6/10

Originally released as the monthly subscription mystery for March 2023.

As the owner of an authentic Irish pub, St. Patrick's Day is one of the biggest days of the year for you. This year seems to be no different. The night's festivities go off without a hitch until a fire breaks out. After evacuating patrons, you assess the damage and find half of your pub burnt to a crisp and your father’s gold coin collection stolen from your safe. With the police tied up with other St. Patty’s Day shenanigans, you take it upon yourself to investigate both the fire and robbery. Can you catch the thief and find your Pot Of Gold?


    This mystery was never a part of our monthly subscription service. It was released as a one-time purchase. 


Difficulty Rating – 6/10

boardgame: The Case of the Missing Eyes
description:
The Queen’s Eye jewel was to remain on display at the Valley Falls Museum for a month before entering the private collection of its new owner, Charles Henderson. Despite numerous precautions taken by both the museum and the owner, however, the priceless diamond was stolen. Henderson will stop at nothing to get his diamond back and make the thief pay, which is why he hired the world’s most renowned private eye, Jasper Graves. Yet, after just a few days of taking on the case, Graves has gone missing. With few options remaining, Henderson turns to you and your detective agency. Do you have what it takes to untangle this intricate web of deception, locate the missing Queen’s Eye diamond, and find out what happened to one very famous private eye? Accept the case and find out!

Difficulty Rating – 7/10

Originally released as the monthly subscription mystery for April 2023.

The Eternal Slumber Funeral Parlor is exactly the sort of place you would expect to find a dead body. But, normally the bodies are already deceased when they arrive. That isn't true in the case of Bernard Sparks, the owner of Eternal Slumber who is found murdered in the chapel. Can you sift through the remains of Bernard's life and uncover the secrets which led to his demise? Or will the truth be buried with him?  Ashes to ashes, lies to lies.

Difficulty Rating – 6.5/10

Originally released as the monthly subscription mystery for May 2023.

Summer is about to be in full swing, and that means the Hampstead Zoo is getting ready for its busiest season. After the zoo closed on Thursday, staff members were busy at work cleaning habitats, caring for the animals, and adding the finishing touches to a new exhibit entitled, “Endangered Species.” Little did they know that the most endangered species that evening was man. Claude, the adult male lion of the Hampstead Zoo, escaped his exhibit and killed head zoo keeper Daniel Strickland in an uncharacteristic display of savagery. Claude was immediately tranquilized and caged pending further inquiry into the incident, but there are many suspicious details about this incident that warrant investigation. The Chief Director of the Hampstead Zoo doesn’t think this was a mere accident and has hired the Will Street Detective Agency to solve this mystery.  Explore this wild kingdom and determine if Strickland’s death was the result of human orchestration or the byproduct of trying to tame Mother Nature. But, remember, it’s a jungle out there!

Difficulty Rating – 6/10

Originally released as the monthly subscription mystery for June 2023.

Overcrowd is a roll and shade type of game, where players choose a grid, 14x14/17x17/20x20. The starting player rolls 3 dice and the players shade the board in their liking. Dice roles are ranging from height and the width of the cells, to the number inside the cells, which we call the anchor!
You gain a points equal to adjacent anchors, everything that isn't shaded counts as negative points. 
But be sure to be quick, as players gain rewards if someone finishes before you!

For the release of Ritual Cuts' 3rd kickstarted, Microbia, a zine about magical fermentation, we are releasing a small free solo one-page roll & write about the many active things that happen in a ferment.

Here, we are mitigating good and bad bacteria through taking actions that affect temperature, sugar consumption and volatility. Meant to introduce aspects of lacto fermentation (the game representing a vegan kim chi), your brew has to survive its early, vulnerable days to slowly de-escalate the various variables that affect the brew.

It is a war game set in brine!

Guyenne is a card game of bluff, hand management and castle conquest, with a historical flavour.

The name "Guyenne" refers both to the hereditary duchy of the English crown resulting from the Treaty of Brétigny signed in 1360 and to the battle cry of the Anglo-Guyenne armies during the Hundred Years War.

Players compete in successive rounds. They play the cards in their hand to take control of as many of the castle defences as possible that face them. But their forces are limited, and they must be used at the right moment to get the final victory.

Each game turn consists of two phases (laying cards and resolving battles) during which both players are alternately attacker and defender.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Oceans: Legends of the Deep Kickstarter Promos
description:
6 New Legend cards, Including Leviathan and Poseidon
7 New Deep cards, Including Dragon Turtle and Boobrie
9 New Scenario Cards, Including Mega Predators and Toxic Reef

Included with any pledge over $1 in the Legends of the Deep Kickstarter Campaign.

Win Big in Your Transformation Journey

Stay ahead of your competition and help your organization accelerate its digital transformation by addressing core to edge to cloud with intelligent solutions from Dell Technologies.

Discover your path to transformation by navigating new opportunities and collecting multiple technology solutions. Scale your organization faster by building Coworking Spaces and Reginonal Offices to increase overall business impact.

Adorable cats are seeking people and like felines to play with!

Cat Trois is a traditional matching game with a twist. On each turn, the player reveals a card. A toy card is immediately added to hand and the turn ends. Cats, on the other hand, present choices. Should you attract this cat to your display using the yarn and stuffed bird it desires? Or should you flip a second card to go for a match, and risk revealing toys - or cats - for your opponents to claim?

Each of the cats is present in the deck three times. Create the largest sets of as many cats as possible to win the game!

boardgame: Berlin's Gateway: The Battle of Kustrin ‘45
description:
From the creative wargaming pen of Piers Brand, we visit the apocalyptic rehearsal of the downfall of Berlin. 
The Battle of Kustrin was every inch as terrible and desperate, and for the miniature wargamer, had just as many opportunities to game out the vicious close quarters fighting that took place as in Berlin. 
Moreover, it features one of the last panzer attacks made by the German army. 
Over the course of nine scenarios, game out actions representative of the fighting in and around Kustrin. 
A variety of troop types and weapons featured in the battle, including King Tigers and everything from Wehrmacht Auxiliaries to Volksturm on the German side, and Lend lease Shermans and White Scout Cars, and assault engineers on the Soviet side.

The scenarios are rules-neutral, so feel free to adapt them for use with your favorite rules system. 
This book will have all you need to game this battle out and will be a jumping point to research even more actions set in and around Kustrin.

—description from the publisher

Get ready to dance along with Toucan Sam in Froot Loops Frenzy! Move and groove with Toucan Sam as you choose Froot Loops from his spoon. Will you be able to keep the loop, or will you have to add it to his bowl? The longer he dances the more he moves, until his signature 360 spin will send the Froot Loops flying! Can you be the first to collect all your Froot Loops to win? Get your groove on and play Froot Loops Frenzy!

Join Toucan Sam in this Fast-Paced, Dizzy, Dancin' Collect 'Em All Game. While the music plays, collect colored loops, as dizzy Sam dances and spins to send his loops flying! Be the first to collect all 6 Froot Loops game pieces to win!
