Requires 3 AA Batteries - Not Included.

What's Included: 1 Toucan Sam, 30 Loops, 4 Collection Cards, 1 Action Sticker Sheet, Complete Instructions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sunrise Lane: Double Park Promo Card
description:
Enjoy this promo for Sunrise Lane, a strategical game for 2 to 4 players where you pick the most prestigious plots of land and build beautiful houses… if you can beat your competitors to it!

Rules "Whenever you may build a House during your turn, you may discard this card to build 2 Park tiles in a row, following the regular construction rules."

This promo card was a bonus for preordering the game. It was also added to the spielbox magazine #5 2023 and could be found in Plato Magazine 163.

Magical cards is a turn-based smart card game for two players.
Use your iPhone as a game board, scan cards and perform actions on screen to cast spells.
Intuitive app supervises player's compliance to the game rules, monitors various status effects and scores points automatically.
Make use of your card counting skills, plan your moves and adapt your strategy in each turn to be victorious!

—description from the designer
This is available from the 2021 Promo Pack, it includes the Crosspire chip and Landmark Spire Instructions card.

The Legendary Edition of Oceans contains the Oceans deluxe core game (retail), previously released Oceans promo cards packs and the two expansions (Solo & Legends of the Deep) in a Legendary Edition box with custom insert.

Does not include:
- Legends of the Deep Kickstarter Promos 
- Frosthaven Piranna Pig Promo card
- 5-6 Players expansion

boardgame: El gran juego de circulación vial
description:
Board game in which children learn to move safely in their closest environment, respecting safety and coexistence rules.
Traffic signs trivia game.

The game consists of completing a four-section bullfight while avoiding the horns and hooves of the opponents. The first to arrive does not win, but rather the one with the most points. Bravery is especially valued, such as running close to the bull, being trampled by a bull, or being gored.

Game of simple mathematical operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) and crossed numbers in which chance, taking the numbers and skill in calculating the operations are combined to obtain the highest possible score in the game.
The score of each player depends on the result of the number obtained, stipulated by certain positions that double or triple the absolute value of the numbers.
The scores obtained by each player accumulate until the end of the game, with the winner being the one with the highest score.
The game is basically a horse race with obstacles where you have to roll dice to advance. The winner will be the first to cross the finish line.

The Sea of ​​Mists is capricious and changeable. In this world, even islands can change their location, and enemy ships can appear at any time. Each player will command their own fleet of powerful airships, and by fighting and expanding your economic supply, you will try to gain more victory points than your opponents or destroy three enemy harbors. There are several paths to victory, but only one commander will become the lord of Nebelmeer!

Nebelmeer is a strategy game of battling airships in a fantasy steampunk world of floating islands. The gameplay is divided into turns, which individual players take until the winner is determined. As the game progresses, players unlock subsequent acts (maximum 4), which give them access to new buildings and better airships. The game ends in one of three cases:


     The last act IV victory point token is collected - the player who has collected the most points wins (the tokens have different values, from 2 to 4).
     One player destroys 3 enemy harbors - this player automatically wins the game.
     One player is eliminated (there are no harbors or airships). In a 2-player game, the opponent immediately wins. In a game with 3 or 4 players, the game ends immediately and the player with the most victory points wins. In a 2 vs 2 game, the team with the highest total victory points wins.


—description from the publisher

The 'lobster' and 'mobster' in this game are unusual dice. If you roll them and neither the lobster or mobster are standing upright, you'll take a fish. Collect 5 fish and you're "sleeping with the fishes" and lost the game. There are no winners in this game, only a loser and everybody else.

Each turn you'll draw a card, many of which will add rules which penalise players for doing various things such as checking their phone or answering a player's question. As the game goes on, the various rules will become increasingly confusing and contradictory until someone loses the game.

—user summary

Band of Ogres is a push-your-luck, set-collection, auction card game with an ogre-musician battle-of-the-bands theme. First players assemble sets of ogre musician cards through Gin Rummy-style set-collection. Then they use a push-your-luck deck in an effort to get as many matches as possible between the push-your-luck cards and their set of ogre musician cards. Then they use the gold they gained through these matches to bid on “Win” cards that provide victory points or special powers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Going Up? An Elevator Card Game
description:
Going Up? An Elevator Card Game is a casual party game combining elements of games like "99" and "Uno" in which players takes turns playing a card from their hand to move the "Elevator" a set number of floors, based on the value of the card. If a player can’t play a card without moving the elevator below the 1st floor or above the 99th, they’re out. The last player standing wins!

To make things more interesting, there are special cards scattered throughout the deck that can shake things up, buying you a breather or putting the pressure on your friends. Will you hoard these cards to protect yourself, or will you risk everything to catch your opponents off-guard? Play your cards right, and victory will be yours!

Players take turns strategically playing cards from their hands in an attempt to keep the "Elevator" between floors 1 and 99. The Elevator starts on floor 1 going up. Each numbered card that's played adds its numbered value to the Elevator. However, players can also flip the Elevator's direction to going down, at which point numbered cards subtract their value from the Elevator.

It's every player for themselves! You can't look at your cards, but with a little luck, and a little strategy, you'll win...unless another player pulls a Doozie.

Part Rack-O, part Phase 10, with other many other ingredients thrown in, too. Some luck, some strategy. Easy for anyone to learn, including children from around 8 years old. An expansion pack adds some Uno-esque elements, ways to force other players to swap a card with you, and a few other more dastardly actions. And, you can choose which of these cards to include when you play, making the game a little different each time.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kutná Hora: The City of Silver – Land Rights Compact Promo Card
description:
A new event card that was given out to those people that pre-ordered Kutná Hora: The City of Silver, or bought the game at SPIEL '23. Its effects are as follows:

Advance the tax counter by one, advance the ore deck by one, and advance the population deck by one.
At the end of the round, lose 1 reputation for each plot marker you have in Kutná Hora.

It is the year 2215 and the world is a barren wasteland, filled mostly with raiders that will kill you the first chance they get. You and your troupe have been living deep in the mountains away from such raiders, but there is a storm heading your way, and the only way to survive is to head towards the haven you have heard tale of.

Packing up your things you head over the mountains in search of refuge. Be careful and move quickly, because whether at the hand of the storm or the hand of raiders you do not want to get stranded without supplies.


SurvioRoll is a solitaire roll and write in which you are rolling to survive. Using a timer, roll dice as often as you want trying to hit the requirements before time runs out. Along the way you will gain water that you will need to give troupe members, bullets you will need to fight off bandits, and extra troupe members that will help you complete objectives.

So grab 10 dice, a timer, a writing utensil, and print off one of the game sheets and see how you fair! Will you make it to the end, or will your name be lost to the desert?

—description from the designer

Little penguins love ice cream too. Today they’re all excited because Eddi’s ice cream truck is stopping by at the South Pole again. Which team of penguins is the quickest to hop over the icebergs from the penguin school to the ice cream truck? With a bit of luck and tactical skill, it might be you! But watch out! Sometimes a penguin plops into the water and has to start all over again.

Two new and original Varia classes battle in this exciting, accessible stand-alone dueling game.

Varia is a game of tactical card play, powerful fantasy-themed classes, and unique player-controlled RNG.

A fast-paced 2+ player combat game, Varia has you select from a variety of classes that play and feel completely distinct from one another. Attack your opponent or defend against their blows with unique action cards that represent your chosen class. This box contains two such classes - the Sunrise Samurai and the Sunset Ninja.

Sunrise Samurai - Katana in hand, you'll use stances to shift fluidly between offense and defense. If your opponent won't back down, quickly draw your blade and cut them down in a single stroke!

Sunset Ninja - Kukri in hand, you’ll use moves that enable fluid misdirection, both offensive and defensive. If your opponent is reading all your moves, wield the power of darkness to throw them off, and cut them down!

This is a stand-alone release supporting 2 players. Additional classes are required to play with more than 2 players.

—description from the publisher

Object. Buy Sell! "Pass GO and Collect $200!" Or"Go directly to Jail!" All the fun and excitement of Parker Brothers classic real estate trading game is here in this University of Nebraska Edition. Increase the value of your properties by adding houses and hotels. Amass the greatest fortune in money and property and you're the winner!

After the success of Adivina Adivinador... We released a new, pocket-sized version!

Adding dynamics for adults and children. Players must act, imitate sounds and be very creative with their explanations to earn points.

GREAT GAME FOR GROUPS AND MEETINGS!

How to explain that the phone is busy? How to imitate the sound of a spring? How to mime the full moon?

With this game you will be able to explore your expressive capacity 100%.

Players must draw cards where a picture is shown and get their partners to guess it. To do this, he must make sounds, mimic or explain what he sees, as indicated on the card taken out. Also, bring 10 action cards to give it more difficulty. The team that manages to guess the most cards is the winner.

Gather your family members and friends around and brace yourself for a fish ‘N’ chip-tastic ancient adventure as you push your luck, encounter absurdly fun surprises, and risk it all in your quests to save chips from this cone-turned-castle dish!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Kids Cookie Shop is a fun family card game themed with your favorite cookies and friends from the Kids Cookie Break Show. Players compete against each other by collecting cookies to fill customer orders. The first player to fill enough orders wins. It seems simple enough... but add some tricky action cards and the craziness begins! Kids Cookie Shop is designed to be customized, players can pick through customers of various difficulties to make game play easier for younger players or a bit harder for players that need a challenge. And with lots of options, the game can be different every time you play!

—description from the publisher

This expansions contains 118 new cards. It’s compatible with the basic game.

It contains new type of units: Witches, animals, pirates, elementals and wizards. Also a new type of card, 'Monuments' that will incorporate new moves for the generals. And a new rarity is added: 'emerald'.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Great War at Sea Jutland: Dogger Bank
description:
The 1916 Battle of Jutland was not the only action on the North Sea during the Great War. The battle cruisers of Britain’s Grand Fleet and Germany’s High Seas Fleet met in the January 1915 Battle of Dogger Bank, and both fleets continually sortied into the waters dividing them.

Jutland: Dogger Bank continues the story we began with Jutland: Battle Analysis 1914. It’s a book of history, scenarios and game/historical analysis, all woven together to use our Great War at Sea: Jutland game as the basis to tell the campaign’s story in a unique manner.

Dogger Bank picks up the story with the Cuxhaven Raid of December 1914, and continues it into the operations of early 1915 - a rarely-if-ever-discussed part of the North Sea naval campaign. There are 28 new scenarios (11 operational and 17 battle scenarios), plus historical articles and analyses all relating the game play to the actual events. We look at how the game simulates the operations, and then we flesh them out with still more scenarios: fast-playing battle scenarios using just the Tactical Map, and additional operational scenarios using the operational map as well to pick up the action at key decision points and discuss why the admirals involved made the decisions that determined the battle’s outcome.

Jutland is already a very fine game (our most popular ever, even more than Third Reich) and a good instrument through which to re-play history. Dogger Bank and the Battle Analysis make it even more so, while adding more quick fun by fleshing out the battle scenario aspect of the game.

Dogger Bank is a 64-page, large-format history/analysis book for Jutland; you’ll need a copy of the game to play these scenarios.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Tổ tôm or Tụ tam bài (聚三牌) is a draw-and-discard card game played in Vietnam, usually by men. The game is often played at festivals. It is derived from the Chinese game of Khanhoo.

The game uses a type of Chinese money-suited pack consisting of 120 cards. The head and foot of the card give the rank and suit in chữ Hán. During French colonial rule, the Marseilles firm Camoin redesigned the deck to depict people wearing traditional Japanese costumes from the Edo period.[3] There are three suits: Cash (文, Văn), Strings of Cash (索, Sách), and Myriads of Strings (萬, Vạn). Each suit is divided into ten ranks with the highest cards being the Half Cash, Zero String, and Old Man respectively. There are four copies of each card unlike its four-suited kin, Bài bất.

Cờ vòng quanh thế giới is a multi-player economics-themed board game in Vietnam. In the late 80s, chess sets were widely sold in bookstores and toy stores nationwide. The original purpose is to teach children how to count numbers and learn more about national flags and famous landmarks. Later, even adults became interested in this form, becoming a pastime for the family to gather together.
Cờ vòng quanh thế giới has quite simple gameplay, each player takes their turn in turn.

During your turn, you roll the dice. From the number of points on the dice, you check which number corresponds to the indication in the box where you stand. Then you move your piece to a new square as directed. In case you roll a number of points that is not listed in the instructions, you must stand still.
The winner of the Round the World game is the one who moves first to the center square or Vietnam square, which means they have completed one Round the World. The others can continue playing until there is only one player left who has not returned to the Center box or started a new game.

boardgame: Clash of Fleets: Naval Warfare of World War II
description:
Clash of Fleets is a miniature tabletop wargame focussing on World War II naval combat.

While we had World War II in mind, it is possible to adapt these rules to World War I scenarios easily, omitting any aircraft except spotters and only using generic torpedo rulers.

You can play any size of game ranging from skirmishes with just a few smaller ships to larger fleet actions following some simple tweaks.

—description from the publisher

We have more in common than you might think. In fact, that's kind of the point of this game. This Venn diagram-based party game by Brass Monkey is all about finding (hopefully) hilarious similarities between two random (and we mean random) things. Just draw the included red and blue discs to create unexpected pairings (like the Month of February and Tom Cruise*) and then compete with friends to try to find the funniest thing that they both have in common.


    We recommend: Shorter than average.


Includes 100 red & 100 blue 'topics' to pair together.
Not to mention 520 different pre-written 'answers.'
Psst: You also have the option to come up with your own answers (since you obviously think you're more clever than we are).
The outer box measures 5.8" wide by 3.7" tall by 3.5" deep–that's 147mm by 94mm by 89mm if you're fancy.
Suitable for 3-8+ players. Go crazy.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
The players are divided into two groups, the "Detectives" and the "Bomber," and each group aims to achieve different victory conditions.
 The detective's only condition for victory is to reveal six copies each of the "Bomb" and the corresponding "Bomb defuse key."
The bomber's victory condition is the achievement of one of three conditions.
 The only action is to guess the card held by the player to the left and declare the "keyword".

Each keyword corresponds to several kinds of cards.
If you want to reveal a particular card, the player to your left chooses it.
If he/she finds out that you are the "enemy", he/she will reveal other cards "on purpose".

Find your teammates and achieve your victory conditions.
designers: イイダ タカアキ (Iida Takaaki)

description:
Too Many Bones promo card:

A weapon for someone with a true heart and a sharp mind.

Setup:
The card will be mixed into the Loot Deck. When received it can be used up to three times.

Usage:
Roll Nexus's Tyrant Die and apply the rolled result by either using Backup Plan effects for free or gaining training Pt.

boardgame: War with the Allies to Berlin
description:
"War with the Allies to Berlin" is a wartime game and the race is on to see which of the Allies could get to Berlin first. Players choose one of the allied forces to advance on Berlin starting from different points in Europe, Players pass through various towns and cities by roll of a dice. Special spots on the way are colored black or yellow to advance or retreat. First to Berlin in the winner.

—user summary

The playing cards will be distributed equally among the players. One card will be put in the middle of the table. Every player can only see his top card. The first player who sees the matching symbol of his card and the one in the middle shouts his symbol and puts his card on the pile. The first player who has put all his cards on the pile, wins the game.

Frayed Ally is a party card game filled with deduction, strategy, and explosive misfortune for 3-6+ players.

In the untamed streets of Sewer City, a battle for survival unfolds. Don the cloak of a random crafty character, each with their own unique special ability that can be used at pivotal moments to outlast your adversaries. The villain with a thirst for power and destruction, commands an army of loyal minions, determined to take down all rival animals. Rivals on the other paw are fighting for glory, battling to deal the final blow that defeats the villain and become champion of the city. However if no villain is in play, it becomes a classic battle royale, last character standing.

Strategically wield an array of action cards to outmanoeuvre your opponents, use them to your advantage to boost your defences or enhance your attack but beware of traps hidden within the deck. Traps create an added element of danger, they will deal instant damage to disrupt your carefully crafted plans and are especially dangerous end game.

With every successful attack, characters collect skull tokens to track damage taken until they inevitably fall. When critical with only one health point remaining, a player must reveal their character by flipping it face up on the field and that's how you discover who is in play. Revealed characters are now at your most vulnerable but also at their strongest. Special ability can be used once at any time per reveal to help claw back to the top.

The path to victory lies within the paws of the craftiest Frayed Ally.

—description from the designer

Get ready for an interstellar showdown with Galaxy Fighters, the arcade-inspired co-operative strategy game!

Brace yourself as you step into the role of a valiant defender tasked with safeguarding one of the four districts of Galaxy City from an imminent alien space invasion. The fate of the city hangs in the balance as waves of relentless Exovon extraterrestrials descend from the cosmos.

Armed with a custom ship, embark on a heart-pounding mission to thwart the invaders and ensure the survival of humanity's last stronghold. Shield your ships from damage, strategize your every move, and blast through swarms of Exovon aliens with your state-of-the-art weaponry.

Only your team’s cunning tactics and unwavering determination will stem the tide of invasion and secure the future of Galaxy City. Will you and your friends rise to the challenge and become the ultimate Galaxy Fighters?

—description from the publisher

リーサルチェックメイト (Lethal Check Mate) is a Chess-style game from Japan that uses cards to move and attack. The game board is only 3x4 and each player has only a single pawn on the board. It is the specialised cards that let a player either move or attack with their pawn.

Played cards build up, so a player might play a Centaur card to attack the column ahead, and then play a Rook card to move a pawn sideways, which now combines with the attack to do damage to the opponent. Each attack does one damage to the opponent, and players start with 3 Hit Points (HP). Each loss also increases the player's hand size, from 3 cards to 4 to 5 cards. The first to zero is the loser.

However, a player can opt to lose a hit point in order to deal as much damage as their hand allows. Hence the game being lethal. A player can even be on 1 HP and still do a lethal attack to win.

The board also rotates. When a player advances a piece to the opponent's back row, the board turns 180 degrees. The cards remain in play, so by the board changing position, the opponent's attack might suddenly be useless, or the player's pawn might now have a valid attack.

The game comes with the 3x4 chequer board, 4 pawns (one to play and one to score for each player), and a deck of 11 cards each, one for scoring your HP, one player aid listing the card powers, and nine cards to play.
user summary
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Immerse yourself in a vibrant and mesmerizing adventure as you Build, Create, and Win!

In Prismatic, you strategize, plan, and compete to construct captivating creations using motes of color and prisms to earn the most stars. Bring your imagination to life while gathering resources and testing your strategy. Will you emerge as the ultimate master of light and color, illuminating your game nights? Play with friends and family, and let Prismatic shine brilliantly on your path to colorful glory and a palette of possibilities!

Prismatic is a competitive strategy card game for 2-4 players, where you draw motes of vibrant colors from your bag and use them to craft stunning creations. Fill your creation cards with motes, earn stars, and prove your mastery of light and color. The player that builds the most valuable creation cards wins.

—description from the publisher
Two games in one: Weichei & Hartei combined in one box.

boardgame: Monopoly: The Disney Theme Park Edition II
description: In this special edition of the classic Monopoly game, you'll visit some of the most popular attractions at Disney's Theme Parks from around the word! "Magic Mirror" and "Sorcerer's Hat" cards might make your dreams come true, or bring an unexpected surprise. Use your "Wishes" wisely to collect the deeds to Disney Theme Park attractions and increase your property value by adding Mickey's Toontown House and Disney castles on your way to winning the game!

boardgame: Monopoly: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Mutant Mayhem
description:
Hop into the sewers as one of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles!

Calling all mutants! Team up with the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles as they rise out of the sewers to take down the evil mutants and save New York City from their very not rad mutant powers! Your ninja abilities and Turtle Powers will help you along the way. And one last Turtle-y awesome thing-theres pizza! Cowabunga!
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

boardgame: Monopoly: Predators of the Deep Edition
description:
Get up and close and personal with your favorite sharks in Monopoly: Predators of the Deep Edition!

Take your knowledge of these fascinating fish to new depths as you buy, sell, and trade discoveries about your favorite species, including the Great White Shark, Megamouth, Tiger Shark, and more! Explore the board with deep sea-inspired tokens and build your collection Camera Tags and Satellite Tags. Outswim the competition and be the last one with Shark Teeth currency to win!

Whether you check out the Strip with friends or take a road trip through the Ozarks with your family, you are sure to fine some fun! Enjoy some live shows and attractions or take on some lakeside adventures, either way, your days will be filled with fun and excitement. Experince everything Branson's IMAX Entertainment Complex offers before taking a lesurely stroll in Historic Downtown Branson to shop for a souvenir. With the Runaway Mountain Coaster, Silver Dollar City, and taking in the sites from atop the Branson Ferris Wheel, Branson is an action-packed city that is ideal for thrill seekers. No matter how you spend the day, don't forget to end it by watching the sunset on Table Rock Lake from Branson's Nantucket Resort. You'll be able to explore all of this and more with the Branson Edition of Monopoly!
A hundred new cards for "Kampf gegen das Spiessertum" choosen from the community ideas.

Shape Cycle is a quick but thinky card-drafting game where players compete to create the most efficient chains of interconnected shapes.

Each player's cards are not only connected to themselves, but also to the cards of every other player, creating a thought provoking puzzle that requires constant adaptation to opponents' strategies.

Combining quick, engaging gameplay with deep decision making, Shape Cycle will challenge your mind and leave you eager for more!

—description from the designer

The world opens up beneath us Overseer. A wealth of new Biomes to explore, Rooms to Build, and Units to Hire as we continue our search for the fabled Void Crystal. But be warned, with new opportunity comes new danger and there are rumours among the loremasters and miners about terrible things lurking in the shadows.

This expansion to the solo map drawing game DELVE is all about Biomes which are unique areas with their own Natural Formation (♣) and Remnant (♠) tables, Artefacts, Enemies, Achievements and even Biome specific gameplay mechanics.

There are 14 Biomes in this Expansion, each of which can randomly be found by Drawing an Ace while Exploring. From the simple Ant Colony to the fabled Hall of the Gods, any Overseer willing to brave these Biomes will find great reward and terrible risk.

THIS GAME IS STILL IN DEVELOPMENT, AND SO SCREENSHOTS ARE PLACEHOLDERS

STORY
"In the heart of our once-peaceful land, a formidable menace has pitched its sinister camp, unleashing hordes of malevolent minions upon innocent towns and villages. As with all good quests, this one begins in a dimly lit tavern, where you, the intrepid leader, rally a fearless band of companions. Together, you embark on a relentless quest, charging headlong into the chaos, leaving nothing but destruction in your wake.

The unknown malevolent evil, once an ancient guardian of the land, was corrupted by a primeval and sinister artifact hidden deep within the realm. This artifact, long sealed away to prevent its dark powers from being unleashed, has now resurfaced, and the unknown evil entity has fallen under its sinister influence. Driven by the artifact's insidious whispers and promises of untold power, the corrupt guardian seeks to plunge the land into chaos and darkness, believing that only through such chaos can they fully harness the artifact's terrible might. As the land's last hope, your band of heroes must confront the corrupted evil, break the artifact's hold, and restore peace to the realm before it's too late.

There's no retreat, and there's no going back! The fate of the land rests in your hands."

OBJECTIVE
Step into the shoes of one of up to five courageous characters who have taken it upon themselves to combat the malevolent force that has gripped the land, unleashing hordes of nefarious bad guys upon the unsuspecting population. In this epic tale, you are the heroes, the champions of good, and the central figures driving the narrative. Brace yourselves as you plunge headlong into uncharted peril!

No Going Back is a cooperative adventure, where it's either victory for all or defeat for all. Your mission: survive the treacherous journey, gather potent weapons and formidable armour, and ultimately confront and vanquish the evil forces to secure victory. But it's no walk in the park; peril lurks at every turn, and arriving unprepared to face the Final Boss could prove fatal — you might encounter anything from dragons to rock monsters! The Urgency tracker will dictate the intensity of attacks from goblins, orcs, evil mages, and even the undead, while random Events may either aid your team or further complicate your quest.

For those truly seeking a challenge, lead your team on a daring campaign to conquer every Boss character in the game, retaining your hard-earned items and upgrades for the next leg of your journey.

—description from the designer

The amber light flashes, and forty-five pairs of wings snap open at the starting gate.  Steely eyes of all colours stare down their neighbours just as intensely as they stare down the vortex-laden, magical-warp-portal-strewn drag strip of floating rings that separates them from the rainbow finish line.

Welcome to Cloudsdale!  The Grand Prix has begun, the sponsors have arrived, and crowds look to the skies to catch a glimpse of their favourite superstar daredevils streaking overhead.

YOU will play one of these superstar daredevils!  In this unique racing game, the number of racers that are packed together with you determines your speed for each turn.  You must manage your pack, draft appropriate bonus abilities over five races, and end up with the most Grand Prix Points overall.

(This game is a work of MLP:FIM fan fiction.  I have no affiliation with Hasbro, Lauren Faust, the Hub, or F1 Racing.)

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gettysburg:  Will Lee Turn?  Lee Has Turned!
description:
Both (where) Will Lee Turn? strategic and Lee Has Turned! operational-tactical games at division combat unit level for the South and corps for the Union overall.
Fast and simple.  Can be solitaire at the strategic level. Different maps for different scales, including a specific one for the Battle of Gettysburg, if Lee turns to it as historically.
Hidden units - only for South robot in the solitaire strategic game - both, stand-ups for the 2-player game.
Union:  7 3-factor infantry corps and 3 0-1 cavalry divisions.
South:  9 2-factor infantry divisions and 1 1-3 Stuart cavalry corps and Lee himself with the supply train.
Simple 25%-50%-25% combat odds/resolution system.
Point-to-point movement.
Southern objectives:  Philadelphia and/or Baltimore and/or even Washington DC which would end game immediately.'

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Monopoly, but the properties are famous National Parks, the Railroads are Hiking, Cycling, Rock Climbing and Rafting, the community chest and chance cards are now Battlefield Parks and Historic Sites, the money even has national monuments on it. The tokens are also appropriately themed.

The game is a martial arts duel between 2 combatants.

Each player control a peep(fighter) and their objective is to reduce your opponents life to 0.

The Life is defined by dice. Which are rolled once when placed on the board and then they keep the values.

The game is devided into Rounds which are devided into Turns.

At the beginning of each Round you gain an amount of Energy(dice), that maybe used as Power, Speed or Reserved for the next Round.

You can move your Peep and perform techniques to damage your oponent or defend against your opponent. And always take into consideration your and your oponent's energy.

The techniques are represented by cards, with each peep having its own unique deck.

The cards are dealt among the players and each player pay 10 coins for each of his cards. Then the players take turns to roll the five symbol dice and one number die.

If symbols are rolled, the owners of the corresponding cards get coins from the toll, accordingly to the rolled number.

If a player rolls his own symbol, he has to pay coins into the toll.

If no symbol is rolled, the owner of the frog gets the coins from the player who rolled die dice.

Whomever owns the most coins after the toll is cleared an agreed on number of times wins the game.

—user summary

Release-Promo for Pirates of Maracaibo.
Available during Spiel23 at the DLP both. Its for free if you get the game at Spiel23.


Two additional Figure Heads for your ship.

Dolphin 
Each time the player gains a pearl, they may bury it immediately.

Hydra
Pay 1 fewer doubloon for every gained Improvement or Residence card.
During each Explore action, the player may move 1 extra space
During each Raiding Action, gain 1 extra Raiding Power


Zwei neue Galionsfiguren für euer Schiff.

Delphin
Du kannst nun jede erhaltene Perle sofort vergraben.

Hydra
Erhalte einen Rabatt von 1 Dublone auf den Erwerb von Ausbaukarten und das Errichten von Residenzen.
Du kannst bei jeder Aktion Entdecken, die du nutzt, 1 Feld zusätzlich vorrücken.
Beim Kapern erhältst du immer 1 zusätzliches Schwert.

Each player receives 5 bumblebees (figures) of the same color, which he places on the four-leaf clover in the circles of his color. The task of the game is to bring all the bumblebees onto the inner disk with the picture of the bumblebees. The game is over as soon as this happens the player succeeds and that player wins the game.

The players take turns to roll and move. As long as the player does not have a bumblebee in circulation on the rings, he rolls the die and tries to throw the number 1 or 6 to bring a bumblebee into the game. As often as the bumblebee comes to stand on a sun after the move has been made, the player rolls the die again and moves on with the same bumblebee in such a way that when the bumblebee comes to stand on the sun on a black surface, he moves on in the same ring. When the bumblebee comes to stand on the sun on a white surface, in the following move it passes over to the sun one ring further inwards and continues on this new ring.

If comes to a ring occupied by a foreign bumblebee, the player lets this foreign bumblebee return to its cloverleaf. He then makes another move with this bumblebee of his, as if He had stopped on the sun. If, after completing the move, the bumblebee comes to a ring that is occupied by its own bumblebee, both return to their cloverleaf.

If the bumblebee stops on a flower, the player transfers it to the inner white disk with the image of the bumblebees. After such a transfer, however, the player places a new bumblebee from the cloverleaf into the game.

—user summary

First, each player has to pay 4 coins into the game cash register, after which the dice roll begins. Whoever rolls a 6 first gets the dwarf Pfiffikus figure and begins the game.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 160 wins the game.

—user summary

The Players take turns to roll and move. Placement is on Space 2 to 6 (space 1 is not in play). The outer, unboxed keys: shift key, lock key, backspace key, etc. are not taken into account in the game, but the intermediate spaces filled with fingers or pictures count. All throws are made when moving from the left to the right half of the game and vice versa using the space key. 
At the goal, both space 2 and 1 are considered to have reached the goal. Throws beyond the target do not count. The first payer to reach his goal space wins the game. There are 7 game variants which differ in the use of the special dice.

The game was made as a promotional for "Olympia" typewriters ("Modell 8" was produced from 1930 to 1939).

—user summary

A game played whilst in a car journey.
A card is drawn. 
Players try to be the first to see the item shown on the card or give clues and let their fellow players guess the item on the card. The player who solves the observation first takes the card. Game ends when the deck is used up and the winner is the player who has the most cards.

In this card game you have to collect event cards for all four disciplines and have at least a record in one of them and no fail to win. Be aware that the other players could steal your cards. The gameplay is as follows: Every player starts with 3 cards and can do one of the following before drawing a new card:

     play a card on his own or (if it has negative impact) on another player's clan
     play an action card or
     discard a card.


—user summary

Immerse yourself in the world of the Scottish Highland Games and compete in the Kilt'N'Strong against the best from across the country. Under the motto "Heavier - Higher - Further", you must be the first to achieve a record and a valid attempt in all events.

The traditional challenges of the Caber Toss, Stone Press, Farmers Walk and Weight over Bar await you.

But be careful when chasing records - the strongest in the land will do anything to beat you. What seems like a sure victory can also be snatched from you at the last second.

—description from the publisher

1st game:
2 people play against each other on 2 number areas each with 12 figures of one color. The numbers from 1-16 in the area are arranged in such a way that 4 specific spaces result in the number 34. One can form the number 34 in over 70 different ways. The players take turns placing a figure in a number space with the aim of reaching 34 with 4 figures as quickly as possible and preventing each other from doing so by cleverly placing them in between. Once a player has reached 34, he calls out “is” and robs his opponent of a piece of his own choice, just not from a closed “is”. The winner is whoever robs the opponent of all but 3 figures. 2 or 4 people can play this way, on 2 areas each. If there are 6 or 8 participants, two players each play against each other, but only on one area with 6 pieces of one color each. Here, for an “Is” no figure is stolen, but 1 point is added; The number of points to be achieved is determined in advance.

2nd game:
In each area, 2 people can play with 5 figures of one color each. Whoever rolls one puts his figure on space one, rolls the dice again and advances the figure by the number he rolls. The path crisscrosses the 16 spaces. If a player rolls onto an occupied field, the throw is invalid, but if he already has several figures in play, he can carry out this forward movement with another one, as long as the space reached is free. The path goes over the 16 spaces to the 8 white spaces , always starting with the smallest space. From there it goes through the 5 spaces in the pyramid into the SOS-space, where only the O is occupied. But the O must be touched with every figure. From here go straight to the middle via the 31st, 32nd, 33rd and 34th to the 35th red space. Whoever has reached this space places his figure in one of the 5 yellow spaces. The first player to get his 5 figures on the 5 yellow spaces wins the game. One may also play around the whole game board.

The players take turns to roll and move to travel europe as war correspondents. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 147 (Leningrad) wins the game.

German propaganda game from WWII.

—user summary

Prepare! is a family friendly, pandemic inspired card game. We love playing games. We wanted a card game that could make a little fun of the current pandemic. We took elements of the card games we love, added in the pandemic, and you have Prepare! (From the publisher)

Prepare! is played similar to "Go Fish," with a pandemic/lock down humor twist. Players each start with 7 cards, and play passes to the left. On each turn, a player draws a card and can match 3 of a kind (ex: face masks, water bottles, toilet paper, etc), and place it visible to other players once three of a kind are acquired. Once the three of a kind are placed down, they are your safe supply, and cannot be stolen or asked for anymore through the game. A player may receive and can optionally play an Action Card (steal a card, ask for a card, draw cards). Play continues to the left throughout the rest of the game, until all deck supply runs out. At the end of the game, the player with the most points from their three of a kind sets wins. (Optional for younger kids: you can count how many matching sets, and the person with the most sets win, as opposed to points).

The wolf and 17 sheep are placed on the spaces indicated on the game board and the game can begin with 1 player taking on the role of the wolf and the other that of the sheep.
The wolf starts in the middle of the board whereas the 17 sheep occupy two neighboring sections.
The wolf begins moving from point to point, forward or backward in any direction. His task is to take away his opponent's sheep, which is possible if the sheep are not kept together and there is an empty spot next to them, which the wolf then occupies by jumping over the sheep, which is then removed from the board. 
The other player can win if he succeeds with the sheep, which also move back and forth one space, to enclose the wolf in such a way that it can no longer move. There is no pen for the sheep to escape to.

—user summary

LevAIthans (Lev-Aye-Eye-thans) is a adventure, card game, using adjustable, pre-made characters, influenced by Tron. Players, called Controllers, add various types of Bytes to their Byte Mat, or playing field. Each Byte has different base stats and abilities, but they can all be expanded with Programming Cards. Programming Cards fit into Expansion slots for each Byte, some are permanent and some are temporary. Some Bytes can "swarm", which means they work with other alike Bytes during the Hack and Defense phase.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingscraft: Benno / Benno der Gerissene Promo Card
description:
Double-sided promo card, included with Pirate Tales.

Front: "Benno"
Power: D6×D12
Speed: 4
Special Ability: "Pirate Flag" - After losing a battle against "Benno", the losing player must return one of their items of their choice (other than a potion) to the forge.

Back: "Benno der Gerissene"
Power: D6×D12, 1×re-roll
Speed: 4
Special Ability: "Pirate Flag" - After losing a battle against "Benno", the losing player must return one of their items of their choice (other than a potion) to the forge.

For the Evil Quest is an expansion for the game For The Quest. In it we will have new monsters, heroes, scenarios and spells. After succumbing to the Spells of the Book of Nokron, the mysterious Binder has pillaged his own Archive Tower and now aims to steal knowledge from the Elven Library in the Eternal Forest to complete macabre rituals in his Frozen Castle in the Barbarian Lands! The Heroes must stop him before he can corrupt all of Farlands!

For The Evil Quest! Campaign compatible with For the Quest to beat up the Binder!

—description from the publisher

We all want a safer and more comfortable community to live in. But we know that this responsibility lies in our own hands.
Come and help create a better favela for everyone in this strategy game.

In Favela Venceu you have a mancala mechanism to select the cards that will gradually increase the community size and shape.
Each card is a domino-like, with a house and a community building (school, sports court, cultural center or police), bringing a new inhabitant and a special action to the favela.
When all the cards have been placed, the players reveal their final score cards, which will bring points based on the inhabitants, community buildings and combination between both.

Traveling around Portugal? Plot your route and hit the road! A board game with 800 questions about our country, with different categories: history, geography, gastronomy, art and culture. Answer correctly to mark the cities you pass on the map and be the first to complete your itinerary. But be careful, on every trip there are unforeseen events and you may end up deviating from your route.

The Host a Heist Dinner Party follows in the tradition of Dinner Party games. This is a game meant provide a unique evening experience. The host explains the back story to everyone. Loki, a thief, has stolen the sword of Avalon. The players work together to perform a heist to return the sword to the city over the course of a dinner. Players discuss how they will get into Loki’s estate, get past the guard, the security dog, and bypass his security system. The players can role play the various characters such as the team leader, safe cracker, security system specialist, guard, and animal trainer. This is a cooperative game that takes place over the course of a dinner that involves interesting dinner conversation and culminating in cracking a safe. This is creative party game requires only the Tech Deck (a deck of cards) to play.

OBJECTIVE – The objective of Host a Heist Dinner Party is to get to the safe and crack it open.
GAME PLAY – The dinner guests work through six stages of the game: the introduction story, Challenge #1: Break into Loki’s estate, Challenge #2 Foil Helblindi the guard, Challenge #3: Get past Fenrir the guard dog. The players then work to bypass the security system. Then, they work together to decode the combination to crack Loki’s safe to retrieve the Sword of Avalon.
GAME END AND WINNING – Player collectively win by cracking the safe.
THEME – Host a Dinner Party Game

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dragon Eclipse: Foray into the Shadow Realms
description:
You'll embark on a journey into the Shadow Realm, discovering new landscapes, marveling at a moonless sky, and uncovering the mysteries of the creatures that dwell in the dark.

The Foray into the Shadow Realm action takes place after the end of the main Dragon Eclipse story. This add-on brings you three scenarios, adding +10 hours of gameplay to your Dragon Eclipse experience! You'll face off against six brand-new Mystlings, each wielding unique abilities and special cards to challenge you.

This expansion also extends the Roguelite mode by introducing a fresh map and a special set of cards that will allow you to use the Mystlings from this add-on in the Core Box Roguelite mode.

Includes almost 200 cards, 50 Adventure Journal pages, over 7 location maps, four new booster packs, and more.
This expansion doubles all Mystling action cards, allowing for more deckbuilding options as well as adding new Arenas. It enables arena PvP mode so that you can play epic 2vs2 or 1vs1vs1 duels!

Step into the thrilling naval epic of World War II's Mediterranean theater with this scenario reference for Flattops & Floating Fortresses, World War II naval tabletop wargame. 
Fight legendary naval clashes inspired by real-life naval battles, including audacious maneuvers of the Regia Marina versus the unwavering determination of the British Royal Navy. Take command of historic warships like the Barham, Formidable or Vittorio Veneto and Andrea Doria. Can you lead your fleet through intense engagements to victory at sea?

Experience captivating scenarios like the infamous Battle of Cape Matapan, where the Royal Navy orchestrated a deadly trap to assert their supremacy over the seas. Feel the ferocity of the Battle of Calabria, a tense showdown between British and Italian forces with the fate of nations hanging in the balance. Engage in the heart-pounding moments of the Battle of Cape Spartivento, as both sides unleash the full force of their naval might. But Mediterranean Thunder holds more than just epic clashes. This reference explores shore bombardments and thrilling patrol encounters as well.

Assemble your fleet and navigate through the ever-changing tides of war at sea. With powerful vessels and stealthy submarines at your disposal, seize the opportunity to unleash devastating gunnery and deadly torpedoes. Mediterranean Thunder challenges you to make crucial decisions in the heat of battle, testing your strategic acumen and resolve.

Discover the fascinating stats and attributes from both Italian and British navies. Lavish illustrations bring this naval reference to life, capturing the suspense and drama of naval warfare across the azure expanse of the Mediterranean Sea.

Whether you are an experienced war gamer, naval wargame recruit, or a history enthusiast, Mediterranean Thunder offers a captivating blend of strategy, action, and immersive storytelling that will keep you hooked, returning to the fray time and time again. 
Flattops & Floating Fortresses is an easy game to play for even casual gamers. 
Take command, rewrite history, and secure crucial victories for your nation as you lead your fleet to triumph in the Mediterranean Thunder. 
Join the battle and make waves in the destiny of nations.

—description from the publisher

Saltire is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

A crosscut is a 2×2 area containing two diagonally adjacent black stones and two diagonally adjacent white stones. A supercut is a crosscut along with eight additional stones orthogonally adjacent to like-colored stones in the crosscut.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point or swap two orthogonally or diagonally adjacent stones. A swap must decrease either the number of crosscuts or the number of supercuts on the board, and it must not increase the number of crosscuts.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color. Draws are not possible.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

Saltire seems to be the first naturally finite orthogonal connection game with no captures, no placement restrictions and no chain reactions.

—description from the designer

Tartan is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (squares) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. Squares at the intersection of even columns and odd rows are colored black; squares at the intersection of odd columns and even rows are colored white; all other squares are of a neutral color. On odd-sized boards, black and white squares can be swapped to obtain a different board.

A crosscut is a 2×2 area containing two diagonally adjacent black stones and two diagonally adjacent white stones.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty square or swap two opposite-colored stones that are part of the same crosscut. A swap must involve moving a stone to a square of its color.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color. Draws are not possible.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 赤いアリスと黒の騎士 (Red Alice and the Black Knight)
description:
Bored on a rainy day, Alice follows a red light to a strange land where the only colors are red and black. In fact, she's turned into a Red Alice. An angry queen accuses Alice of stealing her tarts, but Alice escapes with a new friend, the Black Knight. Join Alice and the Black Knight on their adventures!

赤いアリスと黒の騎士 ("Red Alice and the Black Knight") includes 32 cards, 14 black and 18 red. The cards are numbered, but OR cards offer a choice of numbers. This anthology includes rules for three games, each one being the designer's take on combining other popular games. Certain cards also have special effects that change from game to game.

Party (Daifugō (Tycoon) x Dabon, 2-6 players) is a climbing and shedding game with goal of getting rid of all your cards. Players must play an equal or higher number unless someone plays multiple matching cards; then players must play equal or lower numbers. If the deck runs out, the player with the most cards chooses one to keep and contributes the rest of their cards to become the new draw deck. There are also optional rules, including the ability to play all of your cards at once if you can add, subtract, multiply, or divide all the cards in your hand to equal the last card played.

In Tart (Cockroach Poker x Love Letter, 2-4 players), players attempt to guess other player's face-down cards while keeping their own secret. Players begin with three face-down cards. Each turn they play a card from their hand face-up. If they play a red card, they may guess one of another player's face-down cards. There are optional rules where different cards have special effects.

Checkmate (Chess x Rock Paper Scissors, 2 players) uses only 14 cards. Players place 5 cards from their hand face-down in front of themselves and will flip one face-up each turn. If the colors match, the higher number wins. If the colors don't match, the lower number wins. Players continue until one has won the best of five games.

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Jaws of the Lion – The Founders of the Lost Cabal (Promo Scenario)
description: A Jaws of the Lion promo scenario received via backing The Secret Cabal Gaming Podcast's 10th Year Extravaganza Kickstarter.

boardgame: 青い靴とオズの魔術師 (The Blue Slippers and the Wizard of Oz)
description:
A tornado carries Dorothy and her dog, Toto, off to Oz. Dorothy's house lands on a witch, freeing the little people of that land. A good witch arrives and presents Dorothy with a pair blue sapphire slippers. The good witch directs Dorothy to the Emerald City, where the Wizrd of Oz can help her return home. Join Dorothy as she makes new friends and goes on strange adventures!

青い靴とオズの魔術師 ("The Blue Slippers and the Wizard of Oz") includes 32 cards, 16 black and 16 blue. The cards are numbered, but OR cards offer a choice of numbers. This anthology includes rules for three games, each one being the designer's take on combining other popular games. Certain cards also have special effects that change from game to game.

In Shoes (Old Maid x Werewolf, 2-6 players), a player only keeps 2 cards in their hand. Players take turns drawing from other players' hands in an attempt to form pairs. If a player can't make a pair with the three cards in their hand, they must discard one card to the discard pile. Optional rules include special effects for the cards, including making a player immediately lose if they play or discard the 0 or Oz card.

In Liar (Cheat (BS) x Dobon, 2-5 players), players place a card or cards face-down and declare the number, which must be equal to or higher than the previous card. Players may play a card face-up, but they must draw 2 cards as a penalty. If a player accuses a player of lying and are correct, the previous player must take their cards back and draw a card. If the accuser is wrong, they must draw instead. Optional rules include special effects for different cards, and you may include a rule allowing a player go out if they can add, subtract, multiply, or divide all the number sin their hand to equal the previous declared number.

Quartet (Poker x Color Matching, 2-4 players) is a bluffing game where players place cards in their own tableau. Players hold only two cards in their hand at a time, and the goal is to have four cards in a row with the same color. Players may play cards face-down, but not if they did so on their previous turn. Optional rules add more opportunities to reach the goal and block the other players.

"Whether you are a child attending Sunday schol or a Bible scholar, you can have fun and improve your knowledge of the Bible by playing BIBLION!"

Biblion is a Bible trivia game where players must roll and move across the board. Avoid Wilderness Cards that cause misfortunes and answer trivia from the Question Cards to earn 10 Staters (or other sum agreed on by all players) to win the game.

CoGNaC is a unique deck of cards with which a variety of games can be played. Each card has three aspects to it: color, gem, and number (whereas standard playing cards have two: suit and rank). The primary game uses these three aspects to take tricks. The name and unusual capitalization of CoGNaC reminds you of the rule to determine the winner of the trick: Color over Gem, gem over Number, number above Color.

The included rules will teach you how to play a beginner trick-taking game and how to add complexity with a dummy and two types of bidding (sharing similarities with the card game Bridge). Also included are suggestions for how to use this deck with 6 traditional card games: Spades, Hearts, Rummy, Gin Rummy, Crazy Eights, and President. (you must already know how to play these games with a standard deck of cards before applying these adaptations.)

—description from the publisher

Five problem students enroll at a prestigious girls' school! On their own, these bad girls are out of control. All of them at the same school will be a disaster. They're all experts in certain subjects, but at this rate they're a perfect storm for failing grades and will have to repeat a year. How did they pass their entrance exams in the first place? A three-year battle between teachers and students begins now!

In 卒業promenade ("Graduation Promenade"), players take on the roles of teachers trying to help five students pass their class. Each round or school year made up of three turns or semesters. At the end of each round, teachers gains victory points for every student who passes the Final Exam. If students fail to pass, the teacher receives a penalty.

The play area includes a Class for each player and a Schedule, made up of rows and columns of School Cards that match the colors of different students. On each turn, players take all the cards from their choice of row or column and place them under students of the same color. Depending on turn order, players may switch out cards from their hand with cards in the Schedule. If all the cards taken by a player share the same color, the player may take a Motivator (a token that counts toward the Final Exam) or a Break Card (plays like a School Card but some have special actions). Players end their turn by selecting their place in the turn order for the next round.

At the end of every third turn, players count up the points under each student to see if the pass the Final Exam, and points and penalties are given out. At the end of the third round, the player with the most victory points wins and gets to choose one used Break Card for each student. Someone reads the corresponding ending for the students, which changes depending on which Break Card is chosen for them.

This game is a remake of 業method ("Graduation Method"), a Manifest Destiny game from 2013.

The Age Of Heroes is dead.
The telling of past deeds has been outlawed. The tombs, mines and deep underworlds have long been abandoned, sealed off and forgotten. In spite of this, there are a small handful of criminals and outcasts who defy the law and (under the cover of night) find the opportunity to slip into the depths and search not for glory but treasure. Problem is, these dark places harbor the foulest of creatures. For hundreds of years they have preyed upon each other until only the most savage and cruel of beasts remain.

The Age Of Heroes is dead, yet a hero you are.
Deep, Abandoned is a miniature agnostic game. It relies on you to supply the miniatures and terrain for the campaign-based adventures contained within. Blank sheets are included for you to create your own adventures.

—description from the designer

In a remote monastery the nuns are the head of a huge criminal underground. The leader of the corrupt monastery has died, leaving the position open. The nuns saw only one viable option: kill off the competition.

Your goal is to survive this unfortunate situation: 2 - 14 nuns all attempting to assassinate each other.
You will be struggling in a game of wits against 1 or 2 players at a time. Use your best judgement, your special abilities, and luck, to become the lone survivor.

GAMEPLAY DESCRIPTION:
Players are in a veritable "shoving match" with the players to their immediate left and right. All actions are played simultaneously then settled. Using attack cards, players will pit one number against the player to their left, and one against the player to their right. Whoever has the higher number will be the attacker for that round, and the other is the defender. Subtract the defenders number from the Attackers and move the pawn that many spaces toward the defender. If the pawn goes over the end of the track, the player on that end dies, and is removed from the game. Players also have a special ability to set them apart. If used wisely, it can turn the tides of the match.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Deep Six: A Game of Galactic Mayhem
description:
Deep Six is a fast-paced  Take-That / Area Control game set on a quirky dystopian space colony.

Players choose Leaders and must defeat their opponents' Leader, vying for control of Territory Tiles, entering combat with their recruited Units, and causing havoc with powerful Glyphs and unexpected Events. Territory control shifts frequently and strategic choices are made while keeping the chaos of the randomized draw deck in mind. Just like in life: You have to deal with what you're dealt!

The core game can be played with 2 players for the standard experience, or in a 4 player 'Allies' mode that teams up players against each other.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In this Monopoly Board Game: Marvel Studios' Eternals Edition, players travel around the board safeguarding as many properties as they can. The more they safeguard, the more rent they can collect from fellow Eternals in the form of cosmic energy units. Players get to choose from one of the 10 Monopoly Eternal tokens, each with a unique Super Hero ability that can change the course of the game. The last player to have cosmic energy units when all other players have depleted theirs wins! The game makes an awesome gift for kids ages 8 and up.

—description from the publisher

This version of the card came from RightStufAnime.com with a scratch off and redeem attached to the front of the card.

PR54: Edith Shirring (New - Private Maid)

Discover the fascinating world of whale sharks in Oslob! In this semi-cooperative board game for 2-5 players, you immerse yourself in the breathtaking environment of Oslob, where the goal is to skillfully bid on the movements of a whale shark to achieve your personal objectives.

Step into the shoes of a dedicated environmentalist and passionately advocate for the protection of the majestic whale sharks in Oslob. But be vigilant, for you are not alone. Tourists and researchers are also exploring the waters of Oslob Bay, each with their own interests.

Embark on the adventure, play Oslob, and become an active part of the change our world needs. Dive into the world of whale sharks and experience the challenges and responsibilities of environmental conservation up close.

—description from the designer

Maladicte is a completely insane card game where deceitful sorcerers at the forefront of immorality (you) exchange pleasantries with the help of surprising black magic. It is also a family-friendly card game whose gameplay is simple and easy to learn. The principle is to try to combine spell and curse cards to force your opponents to carry out a whole series of gestures, small tasks and language games which, by accumulating and combining, make situations just as comical as unpredictable.

Maladicte est un jeu de cartes complètement insensé où de fourbes sorciers à la pointe de l’immoralité (vous), s’échangent les amabilités à l’aide d’une surprenante magie noire. C’est aussi un jeu de cartes d’ambiance familiale dont le game play est simple et facile à prendre en main. Le principe est d’essayer de combiner des cartes de sortilèges et malédictions pour forcer vos adversaires à effectuer toute une série de gestes, petites tâches et jeux de langage qui, en s’accumulant et se combinant, rendent des situations tout aussi comiques qu’imprévisibles.
An Indian party game, which is essentially Indian Cards Against Humanity.

Luggage has been lost at various airports around the world.

Lost in "Plane" Sight is a deduction game where everyone knows what everyone has lost except their own.

Two to four players try to be first to recover their lost luggage, verify its contents and win each round.

Each suitcase contains one or more items, is found at a specific airport and needs a code to unlock it. Players need to determine all three secret mysteries to win.

Players draw cards and ask questions to narrow down the information they need to recover their bag.

Lasercut Components:
• 16x16 game board
• 40 airport code tiles
• 40 unlock code tiles
• 154 secret item cards
• 54 attribute cards
• 4 secrets holders
• 20 question tiles
• 40 possibility markers
• 4 dry erase boards
• 4 dry erase markers
• 12 coins valued 1-3 points.

Initial Setup:
Assemble the game board.
Give each player a secrets holder, dry erase board, dry erase marker, 10 question tiles and 10 possibility markers.
Shuffle the attribute cards and place face down in a draw pile.
Place the coins face up, nearby.

Basic Round Preparation:
Shuffle the three sets of secret tiles.
Carefully slide one number tile, one airport code tile and one item tile face down to each player.
Each player places their secrets holder over the three tiles and flips the holder over such that tiles are facing outwards.
Each player will now see the holders of the other players but not the contents of their holder.

Game Play:
Players take turns to either
Flip over an attribute card or
Ask a question or
Attempt a guess.

Flipping an Attribute Card:
When the card is revealed, all players report what is shown on other players secrets holders. Here are some  examples:
If the attribute is “METALLIC” and a player’s holder has the “TRUMPET”, then tell that player they have lost a metallic item. 
If the attribute is “Airport on ISLAND”, and a player’s secret holder has “Trinidad”, tell that player their location is on an island.
If the attribute is “CITY A-F”, and a player’s secret holder has “ORD - Chicago”, tell that player their city starts with a letter between A and F.
Asking a Question:
A player may ask a general question about one of their secrets.

Asking a Question:
A player may ask a general question about one of their secrets. This includes narrowing down the locker number and non-specific questions like “Can I hold my item?” or “Would my item cost less than $20?”.  If you think you know what your item or location is, attempt a guess instead.

Marking Possibilities:
Place your possibility markers on items, locations and numbers you think match the answers to questions. For example, if someone tells you’re your item is green, place the markers on the green items. When you find out your item is round, remove the markers from items that are not round. Use a process of elimination to narrow down your secrets.

Attempting a Guess:
A player may attempt to guess one of their secrets by using one of their question tiles. The tile is placed on either the airport code, locker number or item. If the guess is correct, they, highlight it on your dry erase board. Otherwise, discard the question tile as it cannot be reused in this round.

A player who guesses all three secrets will get the next highest value coin. Up to three players can receive coins per round. For example, the first player to guess correctly will receive the coin with the value "3".

Winning the Game:
The player who has the most coin values at the end of three rounds is the winner. In case of a tie, the youngest player wins.

Attribute Cards:
When an attribute card is revealed, all players must inform each other when they see someone’s secret tiles matching that attribute.
Specific Attribute    Missing item can be described with this attribute    See cheat sheet.
Airport in …    Airport is in this country or continent    See cheat sheet
Airport Code Two Vowels    Airport codes with two vowels    Examples: OOM, ACE, ODE, EAR
Airport on Island    Three airports:    POS, ORK, ARE
Airport Code Contains A,E,O        Examples: SEA, OLD, ART, ALL, ILE
Airport Code Letters 1st < Last    The first letter of the airport code is alphabetically less than the last letter.    Examples: EAS, 
COO, POS, ANK.
Not: UNK, ODE.
City a-z    The first letter of the city for the airport is in the specified range.    Examples for 
CITY G-L:
Gold Coast (OOL)
Kileem (ILE)
Will Adding x Make a 4-letter word?    When the specified letter is adding to the beginning or end of the airport code, will a four-letter word be formed?    Examples for adding the letter “S”:
S+EAT = SEAT
BEE+S = BEES

boardgame: Boss Monster: The Puzzle Reward Cards
description:
The 1000-piece Boss Monster-themed jigsaw contains an envelope with instructions to only open upon completing the puzzle.

The envelope contains a boss card (King Croak, Puzzle Master) and a spell card (Secret Piece) for use in the Boss Monster card game series.

1. WITHOUT DICE
The playing surface is placed in the middle of the table. Now everyone tries to place the 3 game pieces (large number discs) one after the other in the spaces with the red numbers, but with their eyes closed and their faces turned away. The spaces are targeted at least from the edge of the table. The numerical values achieved are added together. The unwanted black numbers are deducted from the plus points. Whoever achieves the best result after one or more rounds wins.

2. WITH DIE
The chips are distributed evenly and placed face up in front of each player. The highest throw of the die starts the game. Each player rolls the die once and covers the number he rolled on the game board with his chip of the same number. Plus and minus signs in front of the numbers do not count.For the numbers 7, 8, 10, 15 and 25 you roll the die continuously until this number is reached. Exceeding the number does not count and the next player continues rolling the die.
The winner is the first person to cover the game board with their number chips.

In PRECISION, Players take turns pulling cards and stacking geometrical pieces on an unstable, rocking base.

Payers pull the topmost card from the deck containing:
Numbers 1-5
Skip
Reverse
Circle

Players play until the structure falls over and whoever causes the collapse is the loser. If a player places the last piece on the board they are the winner and the game can restart.

—description from the publisher

A memory game to fix 10 rules to prevent illness. Move the four researchers to find the right match, avoiding five virus tiles; of you get One you could use the masks.
The attention to corona virus 19 makes It kind of obsessive.

A Supremacy 2021 Expansion --

Minor areas are no longer undefended!  A set of armies and navies to supply Minor countries with their own armed forces.

The BRIC Scenario is a variant of the base game you can play with 2-6 players, who will win?

Fight the war on terror in the modern age of nuclear conflict!

Pirates can blockade and interrupt sea lanes.   Terrorists can pop up anywhere on the planet.  They can launch suicide attacks, seize control of undefended areas and even steal nuclear warheads!  If you attack them, they can simply run away.

Their insidious spread can cause real damage without a concerted effort to keep them in check.  Ignored they can grow into a new Islamic superpower!

—description from the publisher

A new player controls the Terrorists.  The Terrorist player has no home country areas or capitals.  They can never be conquered or destroyed, but will rise up from other conflicts.

—user summary

This is a print-and-play game that requires 3 6-sided dice and a pencil.

Numbalaya is a roll-and-write game inspired by 3 different concepts from mathematics: Prime numbers, functions and graphs, and the Fibonacci sequence. Feel the excitement!

But don’t worry – knowledge of these subjects is not a prerequisite to enjoying Numbalaya. To succeed in Numbalaya, you will need to adeptly choose which dice and which action to use. But beware! The die you don’t choose can be just as important.

Each round, any player rolls all 3 dice which will be used by all players. Each player will allocate 2 of the 3 dice to an Action which they will perform, then the remaining die will be allocated to the Single Die Track. Play another round unless your game has ended which happens when you allocate the 6th die of the same number to the Single Die Track.

Highest score wins! Solo players should evaluate themselves against the bell curve.

-description from designer

A new room has appeared in Fogmoor Manor, an ancient observatory! Through its large windows, and using its archaic scientific instruments, you are able to spot strange things in the skies over Fogmoor Cove. Can you solve the mystery of what is moving amongst the starry heavens?

-description from designer

This new and exciting game is called SPELL’T! SPELL’T! is a spelling bee-based game where both spelling and listening skills are tested.

Teams will take turns facing off spelling words as a group. They will spell the words letter by letter, person by person, in a clockwise direction. Words are chosen from a deck of cards called SPELL'T! cards. Spell the word right as a team and you are awarded points. Spell the word wrong however, and a challenger round occurs.

In a challenger round you’ll be asked to define words and wager points before spelling attempts. The incorrect team will start by reading a word to the opposing team, where they will need to define the word and elect to spell or not.

There are 4 wild cards in the game called “Bee” Cards, which allow you to skip turns, skip words, double down points, or take a second chance at spelling.

The games are played to 150 points. It’s fun and appropriate for ages 10 and up, and only requires a minimum of 4 players. How well can you spell?

-description from designer

boardgame: Rome & Her Rivals: A Blood of Ancients Sourcebook
description:
Experience the intensity and excitement of ancient warfare in an exciting and fun tabletop miniature wargame. 
Immerse yourself in the epic battles of the ancient world with Rome & Her Rivals'  army lists and thrilling scenarios for some of the most famous and pivotal battles of the Roman era.

Rome & Her Rivals also offer comprehensive guides on how to play Blood of Ancients with or without a hex grid, and how to effortlessly convert your game to any scale of miniatures. 
Use any collection of figures at any scale to recreate the most iconic battles of the ancient world. 
Blood of Ancients'  elegant charge and counter-charge rules, allow you to experience an interactive thrill of ancient warfare like never before. 
Blood of Ancients core rules are recommended but not required to use these rules.

—Description from the publisher

Rely on chance and your malice to raise the mighty Cthulhu and defeat your opponents.

Run out of dice to win the game!
Obtain machine guns to attack your opponents, or send cultists to hinder their objectives.

Seek the knowledge of the scrolls to gain their power and succumb to the will of Cthulhu with his primordial dice.

Will you run out of dice before anyone else? will you win Cthulhu's favor?

At the start of the game, each player receives the same number of dice (ranging from 4 to 6 depending on the number of players).

The starting player receives the special Cthulhu die and rolls all his or her dice plus this last one. He or she first resolves the Cthulhu die and then the normal dice (those showing a result other than a blank face).

Players alternate turns in this way, trying to get rid of their dice and trying to harm the other players. The first one to do so is the winner.

boardgame: Kit Escape Game Famille: L'île du T-rex
description:
Your team of explorers have been transported to Jurassic, and is facing dinosaurs. You have 60 minutes to find a way to come back to our present time.
This is a complete kit to organize escape games within the family or group of kids.

—user summary

Un kit complet pour organiser des parties d’escape game réussies en famille ou lors d’anniversaires et de fêtes. Votre groupe d’explorateurs se retrouve nez à nez avec des dinosaures, transportés en plein jurassique. Vous avez 60 minutes pour revenir à votre époque.

Le matériel comprend : 
- 48 cartes/énigmes
- 1 grimoire de 16 pages
- 1 lettre aux aventuriers
- 1 grand plan de l’île avec des gages
- 10 tuiles à découper
- 2 jetons « T-rex » et « aventurier »

—description from the publisher

Use your cards to move around the map and be the first flea to reach the dog. Anything goes in this race so you'll have to use your bluffing skills to try to scrape a few meters and at the same time detect the lies of your opponents to be punished.
You have to win any way you can!

During your turn, you play face down any number of cards from your hand and declare the number of moves you are going to do.
