boardgame: Kingscraft: Carol die Verspielte Promo Card
description:
The card depicts SPIEL organizer Carol Rapp with the SPIEL mascot Meeps.
Carol die Verspielte gets stronger, depending on how many board games are in your collection.

First available at the Skellig Games booth at the SPIEL 2023 in Essen.

Originally available in Spielbox Magazine. Card P003.

A single promo card: Dürer-Fledermaus/Feldhase.
The pictures of the Dürer-Fledermaus (Bat) and the Feldhase (hare) are based on the paintings from Albrecht Dürer (1471-1528).
The bat gives you 5 points by at least 3 different bats.
The hare gives you 1 point but with unlimited hares on the side of the tree instead of just one.

In INVASION, you play as a Chieftain leading a band of Barbarians against rival factions to seize control of the largest number of Cities in the Empire.

Each turn, a player places one of their tiles, called Hordes, on a modular square grid board representing the Empire, to form larger groups called Tribes.

The largest Tribe adjacent to a City can lay siege to it. The player who is besieging the most Cities at the end of the game (when players can't find space for more tiles) wins.

Playing larger tiles seems like a priority, but here's the catch: the main rule of Invasion states that a player cannot play a Horde smaller than any of the Hordes they have already played.

As a result, each game is characterized by a constant decision about when to start using your larger tiles, thus sacrificing the smaller ones.

-description from designer

A card based party game for 2 to 6 adults and children that will turn you into hungry cats ready to dive on tons of... Catilicious sushi!

The goal of the game is to eat more sushi (and get more points) than the other players and become the next Cat Sushi Master!

Each round, players will fight with ancient instruments (bowl, sticks and napkins) to get theyr desired sushi card.

Each card grants points and wasabi points, used to buy upgrades in the kitchen, right after the fight.

Upgrades are split between ancient recipes (which let you score more point at the end of the game) and sensei shef (the actual Cat Sushi Master) techniques (which help you in fights)

The game ends when ther's no more sushi! 
Tally the points and who has the most it's the next CAT SUSHI MASTER!

-description from designer

Design, build and sell the highest quality buildings.

In this game you compete against the rest of the players by building buildings phase by phase with a simple and fun card tower system.

Will you be the best builder?

With two actions in turns you must choose to build, sell, buy, reserve or get more cards with a limit of 4 cards in hand.
The cards have a value in coins to use to pay for actions or to buy cards from your opponents and they also have a graphic duality that expands the possibilities of each card.
The player who gets the most points wins, either with the tallest building or with several shorter buildings.

There are three kinds of cards in a number of colours equal to the number of players. On your turn you flip the top card of your stack, and if it matches the colour of another open card, the first player to do the correct action wins the trick.

boardgame: Catan: Sternenfahrer – Das Duell: Der Glücksspielplanet Las Wega
description: CATAN: Sternenfahrer – Das Duell - Glücksspielplanet Promo contains one additional card for the base game, where you explore space, discover planets, and found colonies — all while warding off pirates.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Bolton Starter Set
description:
House Bolton rules from Dreadfort, their ancient edifice in the north. Descendants of the First Men, they are known for their sigil of a flayed man, a practice that they often partake in. Aggressive to the core, they fought in a rebellion against House Stark many years ago.

They have been likened to rabid dogs, lashing out against anyone that gets too close, even their own. Troops in House Bolton often fear their own commanders more than the enemy. Roose and Ramsay Bolton rule with iron, spiked fists. Their reputation for brutality and cruelty causes many foes to simply quit the battlefield. Better to run away and survive than be captured. Woe to anyone who ends up in House Bolton's clutches after a battle.

As with all starter sets for the game, the Bolton Starter Set comes with everything a player will need to get started in the game with the Bolton faction. It includes a rulebook, terrain templates, all necessary tokens, as well as the Bolton Tactics Deck and custom dice for the faction.

Perhaps unsurprisingly, the Boltons don’t have a lot of loyal allies. Therefore, House Bolton will be a mini-faction, with a Starter Set and a few dedicated units to go with them. The Starter Set has two units of Bolton Cutthroats, a unit of Bolton Blackguards (both of which have been previously released as Neutral Units), and a new unit, the Dreadfort Archers.

However, something players will notice is that some of these units come with two different sets of unit cards. This is because while these units can be used as Neutral mercenaries by other factions, they also have unique Bolton Faction cards as well. These are differentiated by the color and border art.

This holds true for other cards for the House. The Unit, Attachment, Commander, Tactics, and NCU cards also come in two forms, one to use when they are hired as Neutral mercenaries and the other when they are for a Bolton-specific army.

The one difference in the Starter Set is the Dreadfort Archers. This new ranged unit is used exclusively when taking an all-House Bolton army. These remorseless warriors will rain down arrows on any enemy, regardless of intervening units or terrain. They also don’t care if said enemy is engaged in close combat with their own allies. They will still attack, damning their own troops to possibly suffer Wounds as well. But, if it takes out more enemies than allies, it’s considered a net positive for House Bolton. Such is life in a House with such casual disregard for life.

The Starter Set also includes all new characters for House Bolton. Jeyne Poole and Fat Walda make their presence felt as NCUs, altering the battlefield from afar. They are not ones to hire themselves out, so they can only be used when taking a House Bolton force.

1 early-game helper card (HP2/3)

A brand new take on the best-selling Latinx card game that reflects a new NEW generation

Hilarious and insightful parody of the classic "Mexican Bingo" game called Lotería

Original artwork by creator Mike Alfaro and designer Maistro

Set includes 46 cards, 10 boards, and 108 emoji tokens

boardgame: Karak II: Victorius and Darius – Promo Characters
description:
Karak 2 promo pack.

The package contains two new heros (each with individual abilities):

     Victorius – The Swordsman
     Darius – The Scout


Victorius – The Swordsman
In the depths beneath Karak Castle, VICTORIUS learned just how selfish his motivation was to fight for the Dragon Ruby. In his quest to win the affections of a beautiful princess, he had almost forgotten his main mission in this world–to protect those who cannot defend themselves. So now he roams the villages around the castle, trying to prepare their inhabitants for the upcoming battle. Taste the swords, raise the shields, draw the bows! Each and every one of you is a hero at heart, and what we need today are heroic deeds! Let us stand together against the enemies and drive them back into the darkness!

Darius – The Scout
DARIUS is a young warrior, belonging to a fierce tribe from a distant jungle. He has journeyed to the depths of Karak Castle to take the tribe‘s coming-of-age test, which will prove if he is worthy to be called a warrior. Only if he succeeds in this test and proves that he can face tough challenges will he be able to stand
proudly alongside the most revered warriors.

A small punchboard with 2 meditation tiles for the game Satori, given away to buyers @ Spiel'23 Essen. It also contains a small Spiel logo to recognize them.

The tiles themselves are used in the mountain region on the map, the are mixed up in the 2 draw piles (era 1&2) with the other tiles.

The exact rules for them can be found here in the files section.

The newly instated Emperor Nero has commissioned a House of Poison to eliminate his rivals, but its Poisonmaster, Locusta, has other plans. Prepare yourself for a thrilling, competitive experience that combines strategy, deception, and a large dose of poison. Step into the world of ancient Rome, where you’ll embark on a quest to secretly complete contracts by deftly manipulating the Poison and Favor pillars of your opponents. Unleash your strategic prowess and set up cunning chain reactions… while expertly sabotaging the plans of your rivals. House of Poison is based on a Narrative Artbook of the same name.

To play, grab your player board and action guide. You will first select a unique character card and draw 1 of 7 secret contracts. Players will take 2 actions per turn, which can be directed at opponents or used for yourself. Your options include: giving Favor, giving Poison, using powerful board actions, or unleashing your unique character abilities. You can also complete Contracts during this step. When you complete a Contract, you will earn victory tokens - but don’t be too obvious about your schemes, or other players may guess your Contract and steal it from you!

The first player to 4 victory tokens kicks off the final round. The remaining players have a chance to match or exceed that count with one more turn. Players then reveal their token values (between 1-5 points). The player with they highest point value wins! The fate of Rome hangs in the balance, and only the most cunning and resourceful will emerge victorious. This is a game for 3-8 players, and can run 20-60 minutes, depending on the player count.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Hidden Ark: Extinct in the Wild Expansion
description:
A expansion punchboard for Hidden Ark that adds a few extinct fish species and some extra tokens and the rules for the expansion in English, French, and German.

Place them faceup next to the game board during setup. During the game, players may spend a rescue action to bring the species back into the wild. You don't need to use your rescue vessel, but you need to meet the requirement on the tile of having previously saved a fish of the depicted color. The extinct in the wild fish tiles will give you 9 victory points at the end of the game and instead of a permanent bonus you immediately receive a facedown opportunity taken from the opportunity pool.

Shuffle the opportunity tokens with the rest. The opportunity tokens in this expansion introduces permanent upgrades. When installing them, you will get a permanent advantage no matter which action row you use. The effects do not stack.

Step into the shoes of the sower in this fast-paced card game inspired by the Parable of the Sower from Matthew 13. Out-sow your opponent by making wise decisions about when and where you scatter seeds.

In the Sower, two players draw from shuffled identical decks, 'seed' cards 1 through 10 in yellow and black. Their goal is to play all ten black cards in order from 1 to 10 in the Good Soil area of the board. Playing cards into the other soil areas (The Path, Rocky Soil, Thorny Soil) will help players cycle through and reorder their draw decks and retrieve cards from the board.

You will start with three cards. On your turn, draw a card and play a card into one of the four soils. You will interact with each soil differently. Important to keep in mind is that The Path and Rocky Soil are areas shared by both players, while players keep their piles separate in Thorny Soil and Good Soil areas.

The interactions of each soil are as follows: Play a yellow card lower in number than any previously played into Rocky soil, then draw two cards and play any one from your hand into a different soil. Be careful, however, because once there are four cards in Rocky soil, they get moved to The Path and are out of the game. Play a black card into Thorny Soil to draw three cards from your deck and replace four cards either on the top or bottom of your deck in any order. Play any yellow card into The Path--those cards are gone for good--to draw a card and pick up a card from either the Rocky or Thorny Soil. Finally, play any number of black cards in order from 1 to 10 into the Good Soil to work toward winning the game.

The theming is this game is apparent in each of the four soils. Each soil's interaction is based on the Parable. Seeds that fall onto The Path are taken away by the birds. Seeds that fall on the Rocky Soil wither in the sun and blow away. Seeds that fall on the Thorny Soil are choked by the weeds. And the seeds that fall on the Good Soil flourish. The mechanics of the game are meant to reflect these ideas.

Players can expect to look for action combinations, dig through their deck as efficiently as possible, play tactically to to affect their opponent's choices--particularly in the Rocky Soil--and feel the mounting pressure as the bottom of the draw deck gets closer and closer.

-description from designer

boardgame: Nightmares of Sushi: Butt Eye Variant
description:
This 6-card promo was given out at Essen Spiel 2023.

Set-up:
Each player takes a 'Butt Eye' card.

Gameplay:
On your turn during the 'Eat Sushi' phase, discard this card to activate one of the following actions:
- Look at all the used Sushi cards from a player's discard pile.
- Look at a player's face-down Ability card.

Caesar Commands You...To Drink!

Under the dissolute Emperor Caligula, the Roman Senate gets down to the serious business: drinking.  But they keep on making laws.  The usual penalty for disobedience: take another drink!

Everyone is Roman Senator.  There are eight divine laws that are set in stone.  Every Senator (player) must follow divine law. There are up to twelve Caesar's Commands put forth by the Senators (players).  Laws must either require some action, or prohibit some action.  A rule can include a penalty, within the limits agreed at the beginning of the game.  If no penalty is specified, the penalty is "Take a drink."  Two special positions in the game are the Arbiter, and Fool.  The Arbiter's job: Keep the scroll, and fills in new laws (rules).  Advises on wording of new laws (rules).  Judges compliance with new laws (rules).

The Fool's job: Does not have to follow any of Caesar's commands, and may deliberately break them to lure others into error.  If they make someone laugh, that person drinks. The fool must still follow divine law. Everyone starts with a small amount of coins.  Coins can be used to pay fines and avoid drinking.

You win if: (1) You have twice as much money as the rest of the players put together (not counting what is in the pot), or (2) Everybody agrees that you win.

Travel North American city routes picking up wordy packages. A manifest indicates what needs to be picked up. Packages are identified by the letters in the manifested words and are taken from the adjacent cities on your route.

"Route Words" is a word game where you travel from city to city collecting letters to make words.

One to six or more players simultaneously compete to get the highest scoring words.

Lasercut Components:
• 16x16 game board
• 96 city route tiles
• 6 trucks
• 20 doubled sided manifest cards with 120 root words
• 6 dry erase tracking boards & dry erase markers

Initial Setup:
1. Assemble the game board.
2. Randomly scatter the city route tiles within the board.
3. Fix any “orphaned” cities.
4. Give each player a truck.
5. Shuffle and place the manifest cards in a draw pile.
6. Agree upon a winning target score, for example 200 points.
 
Game Play:
One player reveals the next manifest card (either side is fine).
Players simultaneously attempt to call out a word, adjust their score and move their truck to the last city on their new route.

Making a Word:
A word is formed by collecting all of the letters that match one of the manifest’s words from names of one or more adjacent cities along a route. Additional letters may be added to increase the score.

With six words available per manifest, it should be possible to almost always make a word.

 
Examples
Using the manifest word “AND”, the truck picks up letters from the route to VANDALIA via LOS ANGELES to collect GLANDS 
for 8 points. The bus will end up in Vandalia, ready to use the next manifest. Note that all letters from the manifested word must be used to form the word you collect.
 Solo Mode:
In solo mode, use the least number of moves to get to a score of 100 points.

Scoring:
Add up the point values of the letters in your word and adjust the score on your dry erase board.

Winning:
The first person to match or exceed the target score is the winner.

Strategies:
• Quickly examine the cities adjacent to your truck to find the optimal route for making the best word possible.
• Try not to get your bus stuck in a city that has fewer routes leading outward.

FAQ 
Can I use more than one word from the manifest? NO

-description from designer

Uncover an unusual disturbance amongst the spirits—and rescue the realm from evil! In this HeroQuest Spirit Queen's Torment expansion, return to the rich story of the HeroQuest board game and embark on 14 thrilling quests! The elven sage Silvana, brilliant diviner and ally of Mentor, has been experiencing visions of spirits surrounding her beloved late apprentice, Nelath, a Spirit Talker slain by Zargon. Playing as one of HeroQuest's brave heroes, you must enter the Wyvern Keep and end whatever vile plot has disturbed Nelath's eternal slumber—before a fate worse than death befalls her. Immerse yourself in the fantasy with 15 detailed miniatures, 15 beautifully illustrated game cards and quest book, and 6 dice. (Requires HeroQuest Game System to play. Sold separately.) This game has limitless replayability, and you can also build your own quests and stories. Gather friends together for an epic battle of good an evil. For 2-5 players, ages 14+.


     Now you can expand your HeroQuest Game System with this Spirit Queen's Torment Quest Pack, once only available as part of the HeroQuest: Haslab Mythic Tier package (Requires HeroQuest Game System to play. Sold separately.)
     As the bard hero, unveil a disturbance amongst the spirits and find the Spirit Queen to end Zargon's evil plot! The 15 game cards and quest book feature artwork that immerses you in the harrowing world
     This tabletop board game expansion comes with 15 finely detailed unpainted miniatures, including the bard hero and ghostly turquoise monster miniatures
     The Spirit Queen's Torment quest book, written by Teos Abadia, features 14 quests, set in perilous locations filled with monsters, treasure, spells, and hidden magical secrets
     Delve deeper into the HeroQuest world with this expansion pack that adds to the game’s replayability. Players can also create their own stories and design their own quests
     This HeroQuest expansion pack is an excellent gift for fans of cooperative games, fantasy board games, and adventure games.


—description from the publisher

Mysterious arcane forces are interfering with Mentor's magic. In this action-packed HeroQuest Prophecy of Telor expansion, return to the rich story of the HeroQuest board game. Playing as one of HeroQuest's brave heroes, you must enter the maze of Melar and put an end to whatever foul machinations are at play!

     The Prophecy of Telor quest book, written by Stephen Baker, features 13 quests set in ancient locations filled with monsters, spells, and threatening obstacles at every turn

boardgame: 超超長城 (Super Hyper Great Wall)
description:
超超長城 (Super Hyper Great Wall) is a 2 player deck-building game with some novel ideas. Players are competing to build a Great Wall, such as a famous Great Wall in another land. The wall is the victory points, so simply extending the wall as much as possible is one path to success.

However, the game uses a wide variety of cards, and as well as extending the wall, it is also used to store cards for later use.

The cards have no purchase cost. Instead, the player uses the powers of one card to gain more cards, dependant on conditions. So a player might play an action card get another card, if their hand is not too large. So there is a usage cost, but not a purchase cost.

Also, the starting hand is already strong. Players begin with powerful cards and good choices, and do not have to spend several rounds building up the action. The game is quite serious right from the start (the designer rates it for age 12+), but also beginners do not have to be familiar with the game to play well. The designer says the dilemma is whether to increase your hand or the wall, not puzzling over spending money.

There are 30 different types of card in the game, including a lot of folklore characters and historic weapons. The artist (黒子洋瓦 Kuroko-tile) was overworked completing the game.

When the game ends, the player with the most points is the winner, drawn from the length of wall and the cards used to build the wall.

description:
El huerto de los abuelos es un juego infantil cooperativo de memoria.

Objetivo del juego 
La familia tiene que recolectar todos los frutos del huerto pero, ¡cuidado!, solo hay un día para hacerlo.
 Preparación 
- Forma el huerto con las 15 losetas de huerto sin mirar los frutos.
- Coloca las 27 cartas de cosecha boca abajo.
- Por último, sitúa la ficha de Sol a la derecha del huerto (Este).

Desarrollo de la partida 
El jugador en turno levanta una carta de cosecha. La carta de recolección contiene 3 frutos, uno de cada franja: Fruto de árbol, fruto de planta y fruto subterráneo. A continuación, los jugadores 
levantan 3 losetas del huerto, una de cada franja. 
- Por cada fruto que no se muestre correctamente, el sol avanza una casilla. 
A mitad de recorrido, el sol se esconderá por el oeste. A partir de ese momento, se gira la ficha de Sol para mostrar la Luna pero el movimiento de la ficha no cambia.

A continuación, se coge la carta jugada y se lleva a la caja del juego.

Los jugadores ganan si consiguen poner todas las cartas de cosecha dentro de la caja antes de que se acabe el día.

También existe un modo competitivo.

Grandparents' garden is a cooperative children's memory game.

Objective of the game
The family has to collect all the fruits from the garden but, be careful! There is only one day to
 do it.
 Preparation
- Build the garden with the 15 garden tiles without looking at the fruits.
- Place the 27 harvest cards
upside down.
- Finally, place the Sun token to the right of the garden (East).

Development of the game
The player in turn raises a harvest card. The collection card contains 3 fruits, one of
each strip: Tree fruit, plant fruit and underground fruit. Then the players
They raise 3 tiles from the garden, one from each strip.
- For each fruit that is not displayed correctly, the sun advances one square.
Halfway through the route, the sun will set in the west. From that moment on, the token is turned
 sun to show the moon but the movement of the token does not change.

Next, the played card is taken and taken to the game box.

Players win if they manage to put all the harvest cards into the box before the day is over.

There is also a competitive mode.

-description from designer

Deep in the woods, there's an abandoned tunnel network that has been overrun by a horde of creatures. You will venture inside and traverse through multiple floors and into its depths. Your goal is to clear each floor, defeat the creatures within, and collect treasure.

Each turn, you will roll to determine your type and amount of movement. As you pass through each square you will resolve interactions as they take place such as collecting weapons and items or fighting an enemy. You continue through each floor until you find the exit. You can use collected items/weapons, gain levels, and roll against traps, enemies, and bosses.

The game is over at the end of any floor when you wish to stop, or after five floors have been cleared. Count up all of the points you've earned on each floor to determine your total score. Compare this score to our ranking system to be awarded a title such as "seasoned adventurer".

The map size and difficulty increase as you get deeper into the cavern, so keep an eye to your hearts and be wary of the number of enemies you want to take on. If you lose your hearts, it's game over.  Let your adventure begin.

-description from designer

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes:  Fleet Battles
description:
This is fan-produced set of rules for recreating the fleet battles as shown in the anime series Legend of the Galactic Heroes.  This is a modification for the rules Brigade: Fire and Fury.  A copy of these rules is needed to play this game.

Why has a set of Civil War rules been adapted for space battles? Simply put, the battles in LotGH are EPIC. Fleets consist of thousands of vessels, from battleships to destroyers, and millions of soldiers. Because of technology it is no longer possible to use guided weaponry, so commanders are made to adapt formations and fight the battles using Napoleonic tactics. Ships line up and fire volleys, hoping to score a hit, they charge, outflank, and form various breakthrough and fire formations. The battles are fought over long distances, with weapons firing out as far as 40 light seconds. The commander of the fleet, like Napoleon, watches the battle and makes decisions, hoping that his strategy takes the day.

It was apparent that the battles of LotGH would need to be land based. Fire and Fury presented a groundwork that was easily adapted for fleets that have adopted horse and musket tactics. As such, there was no great need for modification of the rules; the only big difference being that the infantry, cavalry and artillery stands were now replaced with ships. The way the game plays has some minor changes, but all-in-all the game plays like a typical Fire and Fury battle.

The game is meant to be played with the 1/12000 scale model ships from Hobby Base and Alba Creates.  The scale is 1" = 1 light year and 1 ship is around 500 vessels.  The 34-page booklet includes modified rules for playing the game under Brigade Fire and Fury, ship and leader data, organization tables, game charts, and counters of the ships that can be cut out and used as proxies.

You can get a copy of Fire and Fury at: https://www.fireandfury.com/
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Playing as the facility manager of a run down love hotel, you need to keep your hotel clean, managed the onslaught of customers and satisfy the needs of VIPs. Earn your salary and don't get irritated when someones messes with your cleaning schedule.

You play in a clockwise order and try to clean the love hotel level by level, earning cards for every room you clean - even multiple per level. Missed a spot? Play a turnaround card, yell "HANTAI!" and clean up behind you.

But be aware that any opponent might slap your hand or yell "AKAN!" when you play a card, voiding anything you just played. If you are lucky, another player counters it with an "AKAN-WA!", nulling the effect of the Akan. These are stress cards.

Cancellation cards are useless - unless you have two of them, which turns them into a wild card. The latter concept allows you to use any two same cards of any type as a wild card.  It can take the value of any cleaning order or act as turnaround, stress or even P.O.R.N. cards.

P.O.R.N.? Politicians, Oyabun, Rabbis, Numismaticians. These cards you can play any time in your turn, making it a special round where no one may replenish cards from the draw pile.

Once the pile is empty and as soon as no one can or want to play any cards anymore, the game ends. You count your collections piles. "OMEDETO GOZAIMASU" to the player with the most cards.

From Heldbergs Games:

A fuckin’ funny card game for 2-4 hardcore facility managers aged 37, give or take.

Do yer remember Ichien-San? Dead right, the one from the Yatai stall of the Tekiya by the temple in the park!

First she proper booted the opposition up the arse then went on to become Top Dog of the local Tekiya.

Anyhow she's moving on from the Greasy Spoon lark now and with her business cred she's mooching into the Love Hotel game and wants to crack it bigtime.

The old fashioned Love Hotel joints look knackered mate. Dodgy, worn out furniture, no-one's bothering with 'em anymore. Scruffy dives, they give everyone a bad name and leave the managers skint. Ichien-San knows the score mate, she's gonna blow it all out and build the biggest mega Love Hotel chain in Osaka.

For that, she needs you lot, the best managers out there, no problem! You can expect top dosh, mega bonuses and fab perks, at least that's the idea. 'cause you lot are the legit movers and shakers in this game! You're the crew that cleans, hustles and busts a gut in the shadows, keepin' the joints up and runnin. You're out of sight yet every room's got your unique stamp. The smartest mover who proper sorts things out can pack the rooms out with punters. That'll bring in not just the dosh but also the respect you crave from Ichien-San.

Place cubes, move and place. A new sensory puzzle board game!

What kind of game? A two-player game that combines strategy and luck elements. Easy rules, quick and easy to play, and deep strategy. It also has a luck factor, so it is a game that can be enjoyed by people who don't like strategy.

Rule: Choose one of the two color cubes and alternately place and move the cubes to create the first 4 of your color wins. Only 2 of 8 will disappear the other color cube, and you can only see what you put on the next color. You can use the "lock" which can stack 2 cubes to create your own color or secure your own location.

boardgame: Östergötland (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
It's time to take the "Östgötapendeln" commuter train between Norrköping and Linköping, sail the boat along the Göta canal and take the bus to Finspång in this local version of Ticket to Ride! The train routes are based on current and historical train routes, with some modification to maximize playability. Boat routes and bus routes have also been added to get the best gaming experience "Östgötaslätten" has to offer!

Apart from the regular tickets, there are also city-to-province tickets and province-to-province tickets (similar to those in the Switzerland map). These make up a super fun and flexible addition to your ticket hunting game. The boat routes can give you some more points and the bus routes can give you quite a few extra cards through a push your luck mechanism.

Using different types of spirits (peaceful, mournful, vengeful, etc.) to form chains, players attempt to match the unique patterns of various Location Cards, thus capturing them. Players are assisted by their Graveyard, a collection of otherworldly powers that can be revived after use.

When a Location Card is captured, players must decide if they wants to gain the card's full point value OR partial points and a Spatial Ability. Each player is also on the lookout for their Favorite Haunts, two locations that net them bonus points at the games end.

When only three locations remain, all points, be they full, partial or bonus, are added up. The player with the highest total point value is the winner and gains the title of Head Hauntcho.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Baltic Sea (Östersjön) (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride: Rails & Sails)
description:
Get ready for a great adventure on sea and land! Take the ferry from Travemünde to Trelleborg, then a quick visit to Denmark via the Öresund Bridge or the Helsingborg-Helsingør ferry. Then take the train to Gothenburg, cruise sail on the Göta canal and then hop on the train to Stockholm, where the ferry to Finland awaits!

Oh no, the clock is ticking, you had made plans to make it to Riga too... Well, maybe you can build a port in
Karlskrona instead. Or take new tickets? Fortune favors the bold!

This map follows the main rules of Ticket to Ride: Rails & Sails, but makes an effort to make train routes and tickets more valuable, harbors easier to build and ship routes not as overpowered as otherwise. But mostly, it just adds a fun version of some iconic routes of northern Europe!

—description from the designer

A mecha (mekka) card game inspired by real-time strategy games, trading card games, and miniature games.  The cards are sold in non-randomized packs or printed at home, but there are custom and exclusive art options to collect and trade.

Mekkablood is a turn-based card game about giant armored suits locked in combat.  Use resources to build buildings.  Buildings plus additional resources allow you to build mekks, upgrades, walls, and to play special actions.  Use your mekks to attack the enemy base and defend your own.  The core game is a 1v1 battle, but you can also play 2v2 for a 4-player experience.

boardgame: An Object Lesson to the Kaiser
description:
An Object Lesson to the Kaiser is a campaign system and tactical rules for pre-dreadnought naval warfare.

The mini campaign is covering the potential clash between Imperial Germany and the United States of America over Venezuela, and the Kaiser’s desire to find his “place in the sun”.

Also included is a brief history of the run-up to the campaign (and how it was averted), the latest edition of LFG’s Broadside and Salvo fast play pre-dreadnought rules and a complete set of ship data for the rules allowing the campaign to be fought out over a weekend or a few club nights.

-Description from the publisher
family: Game: Broadside and Salvo (Long Face Games)

description:
Death By Sketch is a drawing-battle game where you try to destroy your opponents base, and defend your own, by drawing monsters.

Players will generate monsters and determine their strength using dice, as well rolling to see the effectiveness of their attacks against other monsters and bases. Cards are also included which have varying effects, from either strengthening or weakening monsters to damaging bases directly, among others.

When players successfully destroy a monster or base they get to draw what happens to it. The last player with a base remaining wins the game.

-description from designer

Fight a titan, wage a war of conquest, have a drawing contest using only dots, or just try to build better words than your friends - all in the palm of your hand.

One Card Wonders is a library of single-card games that covers a wide range of genres and mechanisms, including area control, dexterity, hidden movement, abstract, racing, pattern recognition, dice games, drawing games, word games, and more!

The set includes the few simple components needed to play all of the games, each of which can be set up in seconds, and you can learn and play in minutes. The games can also all be played pretty much anywhere. You can have a space battle on an airplane tray, or

The games included in this set are:

     Space Ace
     Blown Away
     Zomboy +
     UFO Go!
     Fly, You Fools
     Tiny Titan
     Tug of Paw
     Clash of Coins
     Haring Around
     Dungeon Dash
     Realm of Ironfist
     Max Tagger
     Squarcles
     Astronomix
     The Letterator
     Tumble Bots
     Killer Looks
     One Card Maze
An additional card for the 4th level of the cathedral with resource costs of 9x red, 9x green, and 9x blue.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Grent – Bergkrieger von Greifenfels
description:
Grent, der Bergkrieger vom Greifenfels/Grea, die Bergkriegerin vom Greifenfels replaces Eara/Liphardus and plays with the purple materials (1 die, 2 wooden disks, 2 wooden cubes).

Rolling the die in combat triggers different special abilities depending on the value rolled, and with 14 willpower points, Grent/Grea can use the effect of a telescope without having to own one.

This hero board could be purchased at Spiel 2023 in Essen.

The trash business is booming and every player wants to get filthy rich. Bribe the city council, tune your garbage truck and dump as much waste as possible to become the new king of the dumpster!

In this game for 2-8 players, you have to draft council members to bribe and chose a route to take with your garbage truck. Players program their turns simultaneously and win while anticipating opponents' turns. In the end, the player with the most trash cash wins.

With 6 different cities to play in (different board set up and a different set of available council members), each game will have a different feeling to it.

The Trash Game offers three chosable game lengths and a game end matrix with different events to trigger the game's end. A short game can take 30-45 minutes, a medium game around 45-90 minutes and a long game usually takes around 150 minutes - regardless of the player count.

—description from the designer

In A Lava Story, you take on the role of a young volcano with a dream: you want to become the world's most visited tourist site.

Use tectonic plate movements to gain lands and increase your volcanic activity, let volcanic ash rain on land to make it fruitful, grow plants and lure in animals to make beautiful national parks. Or rely on rough natural beauty, raise mountains and rivers, geysers or hot springs. Whatever you do, try not to erupt once you have lured in all the tourists or you might scare them away again.

In this bag building, push-your luck, tie-laying game for 1-6 players you have to keep track of your volcanic activity as well as of your islands beauty. You play through 4 eras of eruptions and if your island features the right tiles, you'll get many rewards. After 45-60 minutes, the last era will be over and players will get one last chance to bring tourists to their island.

—description from the publisher

Space Race is a Cold War game from the USA where players race to a predetermined planet, whether it be from earth to Jupiter or Saturn to Neptune. It is up to the players to decide before the start of the game.

Players roll a dice and move their rocket playing piece the number shown in a circular path representing the planets orbit around the sun. A segment of the all the planets orbit is colored red and is called the "Anti Gravity Corridor". A player entering this red area can move their rocket to an adjacent orbit, so as to reach other planets.

If a player lands on an orbiting planet that is not the final destination then he rolls the dice again and receives either has a penalty or assistance on their travels.

The players whose spaceship reaches the destination planet first is the winner.

The game is promoted to be educational by teaching facts about the Solar System

Detective Box is a game that lets you experience a realistic and immersive murder mystery at home. You play as a detective who has to solve a complex case by examining clues, interrogating suspects, and following leads. The game is divided into three episodes, each one delivered in a box that contains various elements of the investigation, such as police reports, evidence, testimonies, etc. You also have access to an app that helps you navigate the case and interact with the characters. The goal of the game is to find out who killed the victim, why, and how, by using your logic, deduction, and intuition. Detective Box is a game for one to four players, and each episode takes about three hours to complete. The game is suitable for anyone who loves crime stories, puzzles, and mysteries

boardgame: Endless Winter: Annual Gathering Promo Card
description:
An Endless Winter promo card available at Essen SPIEL '23 fair.

Era I Culture Card with the following effects:

- Self: Gain 2 Tools and 4 Food.
- All other players: Gain 1 Tool and 2 Food.

You and your fellow players work in a boardgame booth at Spiel '23, and to keep your jobs, you need to keep a handle on all the orders coming in. Will you be able to deliver the right games?

In a round, you deal a certain number of cards face up and read them aloud so that everyone knows all the orders placed, then you deal these cards out face down to the players. On a turn, you need to name an order that's in someone else's hand. Fail to do so, and you're eliminated from play! If you collectively reveal a set number of drinks before everyone is eliminated, then you clear the level and are ready to take on tougher challenges...

What's the highest level you can clear?

boardgame: Wild Life: The Card Game – Stories
description:
Wild Life - Stories introduces 7 new expansions to the game to make sure no game feels the same as the last one: create new strategies, meet new animals, face new menaces, and find new ways to have fun with the animals of Wild Life: The Card Game.

-description on the back of the box

[The box actually mentions 8 expansions. But the expansion for 6 & 7 players was added to the base game instead.]

boardgame: Galenus: The Best Doctor Is Also a Philosopher
description:
A ten card promo pack for the board game Galenus. 
Contains: 
Rules
2 double sided overlay cards for the Black Bile skill track
1 double sided "Pneumatists" sect card
3 double sided regio cards and 2 Journey cards that have a player number scaled starting scenario "Anno 166 e 167"
1 Double sided player number scaled region card "Memento Mori"
1 Double sided player number scaled region card "Bacchanalia"

-description from designer

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach over space 102 wins the game.

The game is based on the animated Hanna-Barbera TV show.

The Rainbow is a colourful card game where players are trying to collect a winning hand, using wit and intelligence to try and outsmart their opponents. This requires the use of memory, strategy and powers of deduction along the way.

The goal of each round is to collect the 7 colours of the rainbow. Players work hard to collect and protect their hand but other players can use a number of special cards to stop, steal or swap cards that can totally obliterate a hand and ruin any chances of winning.

Players can work with or against each other to be the first to collect 1 card of each colour whilst avoiding being left with any cards that count against your overall score.

After a player wins a round, other players add up the cards in their hand and when they reach 120 points overall that player is out of the game (alternative endings are also included).

Players are dealt 7 cards each and then take it in turns to draw and discard cards taking any appropriate action, this is where the fun (or not so much fun) begins.

—description from the designer

The Clear Skies expansion is a collection of stretch goals from the Pampero Kickstarter. Included are additional components that can be used with the existing base game rules, as well as three new game concepts:


     Private Industries
     Investor Tendencies
     Companies

boardgame: Kabuto Sumo: Flor de Muertos Promo
description: This promo adds a new wrestler "Burying Beetle" with the "Flor de Muertos" as special piece. Was available at Essen Spiel 2023. Flor de Muertos is also available in Kabuto Sumo: Beetle Brawl.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Darkfleet: Command is a 2+ player versus tabletop strategy game where you command a crew aboard your ship during a space fantasy conflict of intergalactic consequence. 
Each player controls one ship and four crew, strategically stationing them in your wide array of ship systems to take down your opponents.

Darkfleet: Command requires only access to the rules, printed ship terminals, six-sided dice, and a friend or three.

—description from the publisher

Snow drifts slowly through bare branches and a still silence settles upon the Winterhaven Woods.

Winterhaven Woods: Fresh Snow is a pick and pass card game in which 3 to 6 players compete to create beautiful sections of woodlands before the North Winds bring heavy snowfall.

Over three rounds, players strategically select cards to add to their woodlands. Trees are planted to create woods, woods protect creatures seeking shelter, and predators hunt creatures roaming the meadows before cozying up in the den.

Players must balance selecting a variety of cards to achieve the most successful strategies and have the highest scoring section of woodlands by the end of the game.

Winterhaven Woods: Fresh Snow is a reimplementation of Winterhaven Woods (KS edition) that incorporates player feedback from the original.  Highlights include, significant development on points, player counts, and card counts for consistency at varying player counts, removing steal cards and take that mechanics from the base game, and adding player mats and round markers to assist with organization and gameplay.

Set aside the Badaboum penalty cards, In preparation for the game, the dealer should construct a new deck of cards by pulling sets of 4 of-a-kind; the number of sets being determined by the number of players.

The remaining cards will NOT be used. Shuffle and deal 4 cards to each player. One Badaboum-card is placed at the centre of the table. Each player looks at his (her) hand, keeping it secret. Since the object of the game is to get 4 animals of the same kind, the 1st player chooses 1 card to discard and hands it to his (her) neighbour on the left. Each player will have to do the same. When a player obtains 4 matching cards, he shouts "Badaboum" banging his hand on the centre card. All the other players must hurry to do the same. The player whose hand is on top of the pile, will be given a penalty card. In the end, the player with the lowest penalty score, is the winner.

In the game, players will play the role of lovely panda full with curiosity and imagination. While chasing after butterflies, the panda unintentionally discover an unforgettable and memorable journey! During the journey, the panda will enjoy the scenery of the mountains and rivers within different seasons of the year (12 months), meanwhile experiences the twenty-four periods in traditional Chinese lunisolar calendars (24 solar terms). At the end of the journey, the one who managed to capture the most special and beautiful scenery will be the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter: Hogwarts Magical Mayhem – 3D Board Game
description:
Witches and wizards, welcome to Hogwarts! Looking for fun new adult board games, kids board games, or Harry Potter gifts for women? Test your wizarding skills in a collection of magical trials with the Wizarding World Harry Potter Hogwarts Magical Mayhem 3D Board Game! You will love the castle gameboard that is over 1 foot (30.5 cm) tall! This game is for 2-4 players, ages 8 and up. Magical trials and challenges await you in this action-packed adventure game. As students of Gryffindor, work together to defeat the other Hogwarts Houses in a test of your wizarding skills. Throughout the castle, you’ll be competing in mini family board games to earn House Points. Experience real magical mayhem as you become immersed in the 3D Hogwarts gameboard and try to win the most points! Challenges include your favorite classes such as Potions, Herbology, Divination, Care for Magical Creatures, and Defense Against the Dark Arts. If you love Harry Potter Legacy, Harry Potter toys, or your Harry Potter wand, this is just the game for a fan like you. For gamers alike – kids games, adult games, or Hogwarts Legacy fans – this 3D castle game is sure to keep you competing all night long. Whether you are a Gryffindor, Hufflepuff, Ravenclaw, or Slytherin – test your wizarding skills with this immersive 3D board game – Hogwarts Magical Mayhem!

—description from the publisher

The game consists of playing cards depicting a classical music piece and an app.

The app plays a piece of classical music or describes verbally. The players try to be first to find and touch the corresponding card. If you are first and correct, you get one point, and the card is removed from the game  - if you are wrong, you lose a point, and the card stays.

There are varying game modes for shorter and longer games, and for different levels - on the harder levels less known parts of the pieces are played.

You are a forest conservationist, protecting Malaysia Rainforest. Each turn, you’ll play one species from your hand into the rainforest, and manage the food web in the rainforest. The species may earn victory points from other nearby species, while fulfilling the scoring condition of other species, hence forming a healthy and sustainable ecosystem. The forest conservationist with the most points wins the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stranger Things: Lucas' Slingshot Shooting Game
description:
Help your friends defeat the creatures of the Upside Down with Lucas’s signature slingshot. Each pack includes a 30-sheet pad of “rock” paper, 2 rubber bands, 8 malevolent entities, and 1 trusty slingshot with all of Lucas’s signature detailing. It’s your mission to save the day! Made of durable MDF and packed in a colorful window box, each comes flat-packed with simple instructions for quick and easy assembly.

Rules: Lucas's Slingshot Game is fun by yourself or with friends. Crumple the paper into "rocks" and use the slingshot to hit each monster from the Upside Down!

—description from the publisher

Promo card for Saboteur. 
Year 2023.

Preparation: At the start of the game, shuffle this card among the deck of cards.

Rules: When playing this card, you may displace the last played path card to another position in the maze lay-out, according to the rules of placing cards.

Let's Goals! is a family weighted card game for 1-6 players, ages 10+, with each round lasting between 5-10 minutes. The game consists of 2 main components, Goal cards and Icon cards. Each round of the game consists of 4 turns and on each turn, players discard a Goal card. It is as simple as that.

Players attempt to score the most points using their Goal cards in the most effective way. Each player begins with 6 Goal cards in hand and throughout the game, must discard down to 2.

The Goal cards have discard abilities that may be used to affect the Icon cards in front of players. When a Goal card is discarded, the player may choose to activate the accompanying ability, but only twice in a round.

Icon cards are laid out in front of each player (7 for a 1-4 player game, and 5 for 5-6 players.) These cards are not allowed to be rearranged unless discard abilities are used. The final layout/composition of the Icon cards determines whether players are or aren't successful with the final 2 goals left in their hand.

At the end of each round, players add their cumulative scores across their 4 discarded Goal cards and their final 2 Goal cards to see who won the round. There are varied ways a game can be scored to determine an ultimate winner.

quizzone HVEM VED? ("Who knows?") is a battle of trivia and how well the players know each other.

All players are involved in each question. In each round one player answers four questions in four specific categories, while the other players must guess wither the answering player knows the correct answers or not. This creates a fun and entertaining game for all, where it's possible to win just by knowing your opponents strong and weak sides best.

Just like the other games from quizzone, HVEM VED? is easy to play, and all players are involved all the time.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Vincent van Gogh travelled far and wide throughout his lifetime. The places where he lived inspired him to create his artworks. With the Van Gogh Museum edition of Monopoly, join the artist’s journey from the Netherlands via Belgium to France. During the trip, find out all about the paintings he created in these three countries.

The collaboration between the Van Gogh Museum and Monopoly resulted in a unique edition of the board game. The board consists of paintings by Vincent van Gogh, and his famous work The Yellow House (The Street) forms the basis for the board game. Two exclusive tokens – only available with this edition – were also designed: a paint tube and the bed from Van Gogh’s painting The Bedroom. A booklet with facts and information about Van Gogh completes the game.

The Van Gogh Museum is always seeking new ways of telling the story of Vincent van Gogh. The collaboration with Monopoly means that through playing, families all around the world get to know the life and work of the Netherlands’ most famous 19th-century artist. The Van Gogh Museum also supports the focus of Monopoly’s parent company Hasbro on corporate social responsibility and their mission to make the world a better place for children and their families.

—description from the publisher

Tegn & Spjæt (" Doodle Lake ") is a game filled with mad and colorful salmons, which you have to draw or act (mime), and finally guess. But it's not an ordinary fishing trip, if you land on a "Challenge" space you will be challenged to draw is silly ways, either with your non-dominant hand, with a rubber pencil, or with closed eyes. Are you ready to draw, act and laugh? It time for another round of Doodle Lake. Family Game of the year 2023 - Danish Play Association ( Legebranchen )and Family Game of the year 2023 Bog og Ide.

boardgame: All Screwed Up: The Adults-Only Game of Tangles and Turns
description:
Here's a twist you didn't see coming: meet All Screwed Up, the adult party game that gets up close and personal! All Screwed Up brings a hilarious edge to party games for adults. Stack the decks around the mat, then pull a card and read it out loud. Every player it applies to has to move in the way it says. For example, "If you've ever totaled your car, put your right foot on blue." Last one standing wins! Perfect for fans of group games for adults to play at a party, as an ice breaker with your coworkers, or on a date with your crush. Get ready to get twisted.

—description from the publisher

ANALYZE THE EVIDENCE AND FIND THE KILLER - Take a role of a lead detective and examine witness reports, suspect profiles, CCTV footage, and decipher numerous clues to catch the murderer. Are you ready for the most thrilling of mystery board games and unsolved cases?

WHO IS THE MURDERER ON THE AIRPLANE? - During a flight from Colombia to the US, a dead body was found in the aircraft restroom. Who among the 5 suspects is the culprit? Follow the trail of clues to uncover the details of the story and solve a murder game

REALISTIC EVIDENCE AND CHALLENGING PUZZLES - With over 35 pieces of realistic evidence and captivating puzzles this board game for adults will surely test your investigative skills to the max

DIFFICULTY, AGE & PLAYTIME - Unsolved case has a moderate level of difficulty and is suitable for players aged 14 years and above. With an average playtime of 2 hours, this murder case files to solve promises a captivating experience for everyone

—description from the publisher

The card game that goes with the famous Woodwalkers series by Katja Brandis.

Back at Clearwater High, the pupils have to do one-on-one duels for the subject "Combat and Survival." It is vital to choose the best shapeshifters for the four terrain types forest, mountain, steppe and city. Use your advantages wisely.

The duellers compete against each other in three rounds with six duels each. Each team must think carefully about the terrain before the duels and try to anticipate their opponent's strategy. The team with the most victory points after three rounds wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Ein Duellkartenspiel für Kinder ab 9 Jahren
Das Kartenspiel zu Woodwalkers von Katja Brandis

Zurück an der Clearwater High stehen nun im Fach „Kampf und Überleben“ Eins-zu-Eins-Duelle auf dem Stundenplan. Es gilt die besten Gestaltwandler für die vier Geländetypen Wald, Berg, Steppe und Stadt zu wählen und im Vorteil zu sein. In drei Runden mit jeweils sechs Duellen treten zwei Spieler:innen gegeneinander an. Denke vor den Duellen sorgfältig über das Gelände nach und versuche, die Strategie des Gegners vorherzusehen. Das Team mit den meisten Siegpunkten nach drei Runden gewinnt.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Toe Town: This silly board game is perfect for a hilarious and competitive family game night.

How to Play: Child-friendly cards make moving around the game board easy. Just pick a Toe figure and pull a card, then move according to the card's instructions. Just don't let Toe Cheese tag you out!

In this action-packed family game night board game, the whole family races around the board to outrun Toe Cheese – if you can! If he gets ya, it's off to Sweaty Sock Swamp... at least until the next round. Simply draw a card and move to the color it gives you. Just be warned. Every time you move, Toe Cheese does too!

—description from the publisher

Experience the fun of a board game, a puzzle, and a search-and-find all in one with Puzzle Play Games! Race to be the first player to finish their spaceship puzzle and make it back home to be declared the winner, then earn awards in the search-and-find! This puzzle-ing game is easy to learn and so much fun you’ll want to play over and over again!

THREE IN ONE: A board game, a puzzle, and a search-and-find!

BUILD THE BOARD: Work together to build the outer boarder of the board.

COMPLETE THE PUZZLE: Move around the board and be the first to finish your puzzle and make it home to win!

SEARCH AND FIND: Once the game is over, play the bonus search and find game using the spinner!

—description from the publisher

Experience the fun of a board game, a puzzle, and a search-and-find all in one with Puzzle Play Games! Race to be the first player to finish their doghouse puzzle and make it back home to be declared the winner, then earn awards in the search-and-find! This puzzle-ing game is easy to learn and so much fun you’ll want to play over and over again!

THREE IN ONE: A board game, a puzzle, and a search-and-find!

BUILD THE BOARD: Work together to build the outer boarder of the board.

COMPLETE THE PUZZLE: Move around the board and be the first to finish your puzzle and make it home to win!

SEARCH AND FIND: Once the game is over, play the bonus search and find game using the spinner!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wild Life: The Card Game – Echoes of the Past
description:
How many legends exist in the worlds about magnificent creatures that fill us with emotion? Echoes of the Past brings a group of towering creatures to the table that will alter the course of the game!

—description from the rules

The rules for this eight-card mini-expansion are printed on a card included with the base game.

Immerse yourself in the crazy world of a not-so-gutsy, but hilarious Corgi! In this game, you'll have to do a ton of activities to fill up your happiness gauge. Give out hugs, sniff snuggles and go on adventures in the park and flat, all the while avoiding the nasty dog and sneaky cat who are trying to thwart your mad quest for absolute happiness!

How to play?

Step 1: Draw an Action card or take an Action card from the river

Step 2 & 3: Move your corgi to a location and trigger its effect

Step 4: Turn over the Place card
Step 5: Choose between –

     Spend your action cards to complete an activity and win hugs
     Put a poo on one of the activities to get a hug when the activity is completed
     Do nothing


The first player to get 10 hugs wins the game !
Game mode available with the black cat and the naughty dog!

—description from the publisher

Bubble tea is life! As the owner of a bubble tea shop, your aim will be to serve as many orders as possible to be crowned best shop in town! But be careful not to accumulate too many bubble teas for fear of affecting your reputation! An addictive game to play with friends and bubble tea in hand!

How to play?

Player can choose between:

     Draw a card from Boba deck and feed the line
     Take Bubble Tea and Preparation cards from the Boba line


Once a player take cards, Sell your Bubble Tea to the line of customers, make combos and earn money !

Check that your Bubble Tea hasn't gone out of date or you'll lose money!

The richest player wins and become the best boba shop in town !

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Captain of the Fleet: Great Lakes Division
description:
Challenge the fury of the Great Lakes and deliver your cargo to various fresh water ports.  Overcome natural obstacles while building a fleet of lake freighters.  The first player to successfully navigate the headwinds, collisions, and misfortunes will be "Captain of the Fleet"!

Players will each be given a fleet of three ships and a 'Trip Log' describing the ports they will need to obtain cargo from and deliver to as well as their start position.  The players will then navigate the dangerous waters of the Great lakes by rolling a D6 die to move and drawing and completing the text on a 'Log Entry' event cards when they land on these spaces. Additionally players will have to deal with Ports, Canals, and Occupied Spaces, all of which may impede their progress.

Once the player's vessel finishes the tasks from the 'Trip Log' it remains on the board at it's final port and the player selects a another 'Trip Log' to repeat this process with their second ship.  The first player to complete a three of their ships with this process will have won the game and proven themselves capable of being 'Captain of the Fleet'.

—user summary

boardgame: Harry Potter: Unmask the Death Eaters
description:
In this team hidden identity game, you will play as a member of the magical community in the Order of the Phoenix during the second Wizarding War. You know everyone should be loyal to stopping He-Who-Must-Not-Be-Named and his followers, but are they? As you complete Encounters, you may need to battle some of the dark creatures that Lord Voldemort has recruited to aid him in the war including giants, dementors, and inferi. In the end, you must determine the Loyalty of those around you. Who among you is pretending to be a member of the Order but is actually a Death Eater spying for Lord Voldemort? Submit their names to the Wizengamot for questioning to win… Unless you are actually a Death Eater who must do whatever it takes to avoid going to Azkaban.

On the active player's turn, they will reveal an Encounter Card, Gather their Group, and Defeat Monsters as they try to determine the allegiance of those around them.

The game ends as soon as the last token space for The Second Wizarding War is filled on the game board, or the Snake reaches the end of the threat track the game proceeds to determine the loyalty of those around you.

-description from publisher

Promo card given out at Essen Spiel 2023 fair.

Stats
 - Artifact
 - Value 4
 - Weight 3

It triggers at the end of each "Select Space Trash" phase and redistributes randomly all cards played in this round.

Play as the Pintman and defeat Cromwell using a chicken fillet roll and a bag of cans in this Irish comedy board game designed for non-gamers!

Dungeons and Naggins is a co-op board game with an Irish comedy theme, and optional drinking game rules. It's great craic and it's easy for non-gamers.

You flip cards, roll dice, and gather loot, trying to defeat the boss and win.
Quick Setup, 2-6 players, 30 mins

Pour a drink and grab some friends, it’s time to play Dungeons and Naggins!

HOW IT WORKS:

HOW TO PLAY:
Players work together as they face the cards in the dungeon deck. Setup the Dungeon deck and flip the first card to begin

The Dungeon Deck = Enemy + Encounter + Little Goblin Fella cards + the Boss.
Most cards will ask one or all players to roll the D20, then add your hero's skill points (or subtract your weaknesses!) Rolling high is good, low is bad.
Gather Items to recover HP or give you an advantage in combat. 
Help each other to survive!

On your turn you and your allies will have to roll your D20 to overcome encounters. Add or subtract your hero skill modifier

Defeat enemies by rolling 13 or higher, removing enemy tokens. If tokens remain, your party loses health

Watch out for little goblin fella cards. Gameshow rounds to give or take your Health or items!

Keep turning and defeating cards until the boss is dead or one of you is!

Dungeons and Naggins is:
Quick & easy to set up, perfect for new players and non-gamers
Fun and simple to play, plus optional drinking game rules!
Funny, with authentically Irish humour
Easy to carry with you to the pub or a party!
Endlessly re-playable, You'll meet different cards every time!

—description from the designer

The all-new 30-card Movie Pack about our love-hate relationship with Hollywood and the delicious hog slop they keep feeding us.

This pack is included in the Cards Against Humanity Movie Night box which also includes microwavable popcorn, Calm-O-Naise seasoning, 4 packs of Fun Dip, and a digital ticket to watch the 10 short films nominated by Cards Against Humanity to be part of their film festival where the viewers vote who wins the $100,000 grand prize.

In Costu'Monsters, each player has their own monster on a large round piece of acrylic, and a bunch of monster clothing and accessories is piled on the table.

On a turn, reveal the top card from the deck. Each card shows monsters dressed in particular articles of clothing, and players must now race to dress their monster in the depicted clothing. Whoever dresses their monster correctly first wins the card, and whoever first wins a certain number of cards wins the game.

Starting from level 3, add a sleep card to your deck. When you see a sleep card, close your eyes until the card is played.
If a player notices that everyone closed their eyes, they know they have a sleep card they should play.
You can add up to three to a game.

boardgame: AI Space Puzzle Bonus Pack: Extreme Scenarios
description: A small booklet containing 10 extra hard setups.

A small pack of six extra cards for Broad Lines.
Like in the base game, each card contains 6 words describing what to draw.

Can you say the right things in Traitor Word and not be caught — or trick someone into accusing you of trying to do this?

Each player gets a handful of word cards, with each card featuring two words. Choose a start player, then flip a card from the deck to determine the starting word. The start player has a bit of time to say a single word that's related to the starting word, then the next player must say a word related to the first player's word, and so on.

Ideally a player can say a word on one of their cards. If they do — and no one has accused them of doing so by their next turn — the player reveals the card showing this word, then scores it for 1 point. If someone correctly accuses a player of saying a word held by that player, then the accuser scores that card as 1 point; if the accusation was wrong, then the accused scores 1 point.

The first player to collect 5 points wins.

Picky Packrats is a tactical, small-deck card game for 2 or 3 players. Players take turns peeking at hidden treasures, rearranging their nests, raiding the house and each other’s nests, and cementing their treasures in place to make the neatest nest. The most organized player tableau wins the game. Play 1-3 games at 10-15 minutes per game. Or play against Roborat in solo mode. Turns and games are quick, so play a best-of-three tournament for more fun. The game can be expanded to 4 to 6 players with two decks.

In Picky Packrats, you are a packrat neatly arranging the treasures you have collected in your midden (nest). Add treasures by raiding the house or other packrat middens. You are very particular and want everything 'just so.' Unfortunately, like all packrats, you are very forgetful, so you need to remind yourself what treasures you have already packed away, while you try to arrange them in your midden to near perfection. When you really like where your treasures are hidden, you cement them in place. Once you or another packrat cements everything down, you compare middens. The pickiest packrat—the one with the neatest nest—wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dragonkeepers: The Prophecies of the Ancient Dragons
description:
Available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

The Prophecies of the Primordial Dragons

Mini expansion with 6 tiles (3 gold and 3 silver).

Instructions

1. There are 6 prophecies: 3 gold and 3 silver.  At the start of the game, shuffle them face down and put 1 gold and 1 silver back in the box.

2. Reveal the other 4 prophecies.  They show in which position you have to place which color of dragon in order to fulfill the respective prophecy.  If you are the first to fulfill a prophecy, you may place it in front of you.  The others can no longer fulfill this prophecy.  At the end of the game you get the corresponding points (6 for a silver one, 14 for a gold one).  Note: Among the 4 prophecies there are always at least 2 that are not mutually exclusive and therefore you can fulfill both of them.

Genre: Fully cooperative Sci-fi Action Dungeon Crawler

Players: 1-4 (Ages 13+)

Estimated Weight: 3

Summary: Each player is a super soldier in the year 4335, part of an elite squadron known as Bravo Team. They are stranded on a long-abandoned world teeming with hostile wildlife. After disastrously crash landing in a region classified as "American Northeast" without any backup to come for years, players will have to survive by...

+  Exploring the overworld one location at a time, triggering story choices followed by combat missions

+ Collecting Items such as weapons and armor to add to their character's tableau, which resembles a walking arsenal, each Operative a living weapon

+  Finding Allies to recruit based on story choices and mission performances

+ Working as a team to overcome an assortment of challenges, but with no requirement for a full team. Solitaires can even help Bravo Team conduct its operation as an army of one and still follow the entire team's story in true solo mode, meaning some of the combat action thematically happens "off-screen"/ off-board for 3 players or less

+  Taking on an assortment of nightmarish creatures in small skirmishes or large-scale battles to reclaim this world one step at a time, no matter the cost...

Objective: Get to the northernmost location on the overworld map and accomplish its mission... which will be unique based on choices players make throughout the game leading up to that point.

Game Elements: CYOA story with an open overworld map to explore through a campaign with a "save" system for multiple plays (does not contain legacy elements.) Story branches change the game world, ensuring no two campaigns are the same. Missions include fast-paced tactical combat with simultaneous player actions on square grid arenas and their tactical reactions to an ever evolving Event deck unique to each mission. Players manage a tableau of cards and tokens to resemble each player's personal arsenal, character stats, and unique skills they can perform against enemies. Difficulty scales up for higher player counts.

Mechanics: exploration with an organic open world, CYOA narrative, action selection (menu of character combat skills to choose from), dice rolling, dice manipulation/ luck mitigation, square grid movement, RPG (stats building, item collecting, character skill strategy), dungeon crawling (unique arenas for tactical combat with a loot and progression system)

Main components: 1 region map (overworld), 1 game book (~100-page Mission Book), 30 Secret Sheet maps (for combat and exploration), 1 deck of enemy cards, 1 deck of Ally cards, 12 Event card decks, ~90 enemy and ally figures, 4 player figures, 4 player pads, tokens, tracker pads and tracker cubes for a dynamic stat tracking system (health bar, action points, etc.) The game book contains the campaign narrative adventure and mission details, such as enemy behaviors and environmental rules for each combat scenario

Howdy partner! This cowboy is keepin' a watchful eye over his farm and isn't afraid to hogtie any varmint who tries to mess with his herd. His lasso is fast, so step lively! Take turns trying to sneak his animals across the fence - but don't get caught! His magical, spinning lasso will stop eventually to rope you in! Whoever rounds up the most animals at the end is the winner!

Includes 1 One Trick Pony, 1 Cowboy Hat, 1 Hay Stalk, 1 Rock, 1 Fence, 15 Animals, 1 Spinner, and Complete Instructions - Requires 3 AA batteries, not included

Boss Fight is a high fantasy deck building, dice throwing game where you build up armies to take on high damage Bosses! No more grinding through hundreds of minions or pawns. Boss fight takes you straight to the action.

Every round, a new trio of Bosses emerge from the portal and you must fight! If you win, you can add the Boss to your army. If you lose, the Boss runs, flies or slinks away, only to return in the devastating final act.

Boss Fight supports multiple styles of play. Team up with your favorite adventure buddy to play cooperative mode, get down and dirty with an archrival in competitive mode, or set off on your own quest with solo mode.

Will you save the day or be decimated by the bosses? Strategy can only take you so far. In the end, it is up to the gods and the dice to determine your fate.

—description from the designer

Place a Dare Card on the spinner and give it a good flick. If it lands on you, you can do the dare for a point...or you can take a drink. Whoever has the most points by the end wins!

Includes: 250 Dare Cards, Spinner, Instructions

The Battle of the Little Bighorn, also called Custer’s Last Stand, marked the most decisive Native American victory and the worst U.S. Army defeat in the long Plains Indian Wars. Fought on June 25, 1876, near the Little Bighorn River in Montana Territory, federal troops led by Lieutenant Colonel George Armstrong Custer were attacked by a band of Lakota Sioux and Cheyenne warriors. This game is primarily a solitaire game with the player controlling the US 7th Cavalry. 
 If all Indian Leaders OR all 6 villager disks are eliminated, the game ends immediately and the 7th Cavalry are victorious. If either of these happen, the remaining Indians are assumed to surrender and agree to return to their reservation. Otherwise, the Indians have won.

A two-player variant is possible with the 2nd person controlling and rolling dice for the Indigenous American forces. Historically this battle was very lopsided, perhaps 600 Cavalry vs. 1,000s of braves. In the game the Cavalry will usually lose. When there are two players consider switching sides after the first game and compare results.

The game emphasizes theme and playability rather than strict historical recreation. Playing as the 7th Cavalry you won't know how many enemy units you will face.

-description from designer

description:
Backs are turned, paces are taken, wands are raised. The word is given and the magic duel has begun!

Players take the role of a magic student and fling spells at each other with the ultimate goal of removing the opponent's wand. Each deck is identical except for one unique spell that the individual student has prepared just for the occasion.

Rounds are quick. Will you be lucky enough to pick off their wand early? Or will you whittle away their stamina until they are left utterly defenseless?

You are a bunch of mice in the pantry and the cat is coming to get you. You have to scurry into your mouse holes but only one mouse per hole.

You both start off with ten pieces in the centre and need to make your way to the 19 grey squares on the outside of the board (there are 19 squares and a total of 20 pieces). You can move to wherever there is an intersection of lines. You can move one space in any direction including diagonally. You can also jump or multi-jump over your or your opponents pieces. You leave the jumped pieces there. Basically you lose by having your pieces still on the board when there are no grey squares left.

Suitable for two or more players, the set of 48 game cards will have you trying to get rid of all your cards the quickest
Fast-paced card game
Contains 48 cards and instructions
Suitable for two or more players
Officially licensed Harry Potter product

The Harry Potter Charms Card Game will bring some Wizarding World fun to your game collection

The PAC-MAN card game is a fun twist on the classic arcade game. Take control by bidding for turn order and playing PAC-MAN cards to claim the MAZE and BONUS cards. Each one you claim earns you points. The player with the most points after all BONUS cards have been revealed, wins the game!

boardgame: Vagrantsong: Iconic Maco Joe Promo Cards
description:
Celebrate character moments in our games with our Iconic Line, these large-scale, high-quality miniatures. Iconic Maco Joe is a 92.80mm tall “miniature” that includes 3 Junk Cards that are exclusive to the Iconic Maco Joe box: the Dented Lunchbox, The Bloody Bag, and the Ticket Puncher!
