Frække Tankestreger (Naughty Thoughts) is in all ways an inappropriate drawing game for adults. Here you have to draw and guess embarrassing, rude and tabu words and concepts.  A lot of them are under the belt, while other are just fun or weird. Frække Tankestreger is an engaging game, where everybody plays all the time - either as the drawer or as guesser. Points are up for grabs, and the quicker a word is guessed, the further the player may advance on the track. The result is a fun and different drawing game, where laughs and red cheeks are guaranteed.

The king passed away, and other generals and princes began to rise creating a chaotic period full of intrigues. In the name of protecting the people, each of them took their territories and power one by one, dreaming of the throne.

As a person who holds the Mandate of Heaven, each player will become a force in this turbulent time. Use your strategy to bring your power to the top of them all. Raising fame, launching wars, using infiltrators, etc all to achieve the one goal "Fullfil the Mandate of Heaven, unifying the land"

Set up: Each player is dealt 

    2 character cards, choose one and use the other to present the Supply ( health ) 
    1 Thien-menh card ( reshuffle card ) 
    and 5 playing cards


On your turn:
A player has 2 action points to perform 3 types of action 
a) Council meeting: draw a card ( if you use both 2 action points for this type; you will get +1 bonus card )

b) Build or upgrade buildings ( play a card to your playing zone in front of you and pay the cost )

The cost of playing a building card is calculated by subtracting the number on the new card from the number on the previous card. For example: to upgrade a number 3 red building to a number 6 Red building, you have to discard [6-3 ] = 3 cards from your hand.

The same type of buildings are stacked into one column, from the lower to the higher card.

c) Govern buildings 
There are 4 types of buildings ( Attact supply, Gain Victory points, Draw more cards, and Regain supply.

Each building can only be activated once in a turn.

The game will be played in many turns until one player achieves 1 out of 3 winning conditions

a) You are the only player who remains in the game, others have been eliminated 
b) You has achieved 50 Victory Point 
c) When the drawing deck runs out, players will play their full turn in that round. Then who has the highest Victory Points that are still in the game win?


     You can use the Thien-menh card to combine and reshuffle the discard + drawing deck hence stopping the ending condition (c).

description:
In this solitaire sci-fi adventure board game, You are an astronaut who crashed on an alien planet. Every day starts with rolling dice, which determines the daily options you have, some of these open up even more possibilities, with a deck of multifunctional cards that brings random events, buildable tools, and unexpected weather anomalies into the gameplay. You will fight the elements and try to escape.

The game consists of rounds, each round you decide what to do based on the dice and the available resources. You should administer the changes on the game board, use cards from the exploration deck, activate other combinations, then subtract your resources consumed that day and start the next round. The game continues until the victory conditions are met; you successfully send an S.O.S. radio signal and receive an answer and also build the escape pod. The other option is that your resources or health points are depleted.

-description from designer

boardgame: Det Dårlige Selskab: udvidelse #4 – Ekstra Dansk
description: An expansion for Det dårlige selskab. New cards, a few new optional rules.

Welcome to the culinary realm of Kitchen Quest, a tabletop card game set in a magical fantasy world! In this delightful game, players take on the roles of hero chefs, embarking on an epic culinary journey to become the chef chosen by the kings.

1. GAME FUNDAMENTS:
"Kitchen Quest" is a turn-based board game designed for 2 to 5 players. The core gameplay revolves around strategically utilizing drawn ingredients to craft meal cards, which can be used to either nourish your enemies or temporarily activate power-ups that enhance your abilities.

2. GAME SCOPE:
The game's scope is to fulfill the requests outlined on the King cards drawn at the start of the game, with each player receiving their unique card. Each King card presents 3 buffet meals, each of which can be acquired by vanquishing a specific enemy during the course of the adventure. The game concludes when a player successfully collects all three buffets as stipulated by their respective King card and wins.

3. PLAYERS TURN
To determine the initial player to take their turn, each participant will roll a die at the game's outset, with subsequent players proceeding clockwise.

During his turn the player will have 3 turn actions that allow him to do different actions, each move allows specific actions that the player must follow, can’t advance to the next move until he doesn’t complete the current one.
Player actions are:

- Action 1:
Draw an Ingredient
Use a Meal on the Hero
Use the Hero’s Power

- Action 2:
Discard an Ingredient
Ruin Another Player’s Meal
Craft a Meal

- Action 3:
Draw an Ingredient
Feed an Enemy
Make an Ingredient Exchange

The number of turns the players will play during a specific adventure card depends on the type of adventure card there is down, Neutral or Dungeon location.

- Neutral Locations: 
Each player will play only 1 turn, when the turn comes back to the 1st player will be revealed a new Adventure card.

- Dungeon Locations: 
There isn’t a defined number of turns that the players will play, the turns will continue until all the enemies are fed, after the feeding of all enemies, when the turn comes back to the 1st player will be revealed a new Adventure card.

4. START THE GAME & PLAYERS' INVENTORY
Before the beginning of the game each player will roll a dice to decide the first and last player for the rounds. 
Players have a max amount of ingredients and meals that they can bring during the adventure.
Each player will draw:
 1 Hero Card
 1 King Card
 3 Ingredient Cards

5. COOK PERFECT MEALS
To create a meal the player must have 1 or more required ingredients, the player can create the meal also if he doesn’t have all the 3 required ingredients but it will be harder to create a meal with 1 or 2 ingredients.

- With all the ingredients:
If the player has all the 3 correct ingredients he will be able to collect instantly the selected meal and discard the used ingredients.

- Without all the ingredients:
If the player doesn't have all the correct ingredients he can also try to create the meal if he has at least 1 correct ingredient by dice roll.

6. EXPLORE THE REALM
Adventure cards are the storyline of the game, when an adventure card’s quest is fulfilled the players will advance and reveal the next adventure card, each adventure card has a different location and a different achievement to fulfill:

- Neutral Locations: 
Neutral locations are cards that don’t reveal an enemy card, have a request that will be completed in only 1 turn.

- Dungeon Locations: 
Dungeon locations will reveal an enemy card, the request to complete the card is to defeat the current enemy.

7. COLLECT INGREDIENTS
Ingredients are used to create the meals that will allow the players to use them during the game to progress, there are 3 levels of rarity of the ingredient cards, and the rarity level changes the possibility of being found. Ingredients can be drawn during the player’s turn or drawn by adventure cards.

8. ENCOUNTER ENEMIES TO FEED
Enemy cards can be revealed only by adventures. To be defeated, a player must fulfill his meal's request during his turn. When an enemy is defeated, it will be given to the player that beats its personal king’s meal.

9. BECOME THE HERO
Each player will draw a hero card before the beginning of the game, heroes are strong cards that can change the outcome of the game, each one has 2 special abilities and 1 favorite meal, these power-ups can be passive or active and can be activated during the player’s turn, abilities are divided in:

- Class Ability: 
Passive ability that is the same for each hero of the same class, and can be activated at each moment based on the ability.

- Hero Ability: 
Active ability is different for every hero and can be played only in specific moments during the player’s turn.

- Favorite Meal Ability: 
When heroes eat their favorite meal, it will activate the favorite meal ability instead of the base ability.

Fan made challenge, as part of the Lookout magazine "Neues vom Ausguck", Edition 19, April 2023.

boardgame: Redwood: The Majestic Bird of Prey
description: The Majestic Bird of Prey is the first goodie for Redwood. Try to photograph the bird of prey to win more points.

As you breathe in the cool salty air that gently brushes across your face, the sound of crashing waves fills your ears. The gentle heat of the sun warms your skin while the grit of the warm sand tingles your feet. On the ground, seashells and sandcastles are scattered about among puddles of ocean water making a beautiful tapestry in the sand. It's time for your beach day!

In Beach Day, players draft cards to create a beach that is decorated with shells, sandcastles, water, and much more. After taking cards, you place them as tiles to arrange these objects in optimal formations based on modular scoring cards. The player with the best decorated beach wins!

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
A mysterious island appeared and a team was sent to explore it. Only four survivors returned, shaken, with with strange stories and strange powers. Now you lead a second team in search of the mystical flowers.

Nusa mystic is a trick taking game that takes place over three rounds. At the beginning of the round, reward cards are dealt out, and each trick wins one of the reward cards. Reward cards may provide flowers, which score based on majority control, direct points, or skulls. If a player takes too many skulls, they lose all points they have earned so far that round.

In each round, players are dealt six cards, with which they play two hands. In the first hand, five tricks are played and the final card is discarded. For the second hand, players select three of the five cards they already played as their hand.

At the end of three rounds, the player who earned the most points from the reward cards wins the game.

Players take the role of a neckbeard and use the skills and talents of their neckbeard player decks to impress (have more cards worth higher points placed around) the Waifu cards that are in play.

Each player draws 4 cards from their deck.
On their turn, they place a card next to any available Waifu slot and use any effect their neckbeard card may have.

Then draw back up to 4 cards  at the end of their turn.

When a Waifu card is full, the highest sum of points takes the Waifu. Second place takes a Selfie.

The first player to get 3 Waifu cards or any combination of 7 Waifu and selfie cards(1 Waifu + 6 selfies or 2 Waifus and 5 selfies), wins.

Go back in time to witness some of the greatest achievements, discoveries, and events of the 20th century. Players take turns moving around the category portal to collect cards and fill their dashboards with cards in chronological order to meet certain goals like collecting a card from each category or having the largest time span between two years. Players earn points based on these goals and the player with the most points at the end of the game wins! Time Jumpers can be played by 2-5 players ages 8+ in 30 minutes.

-description from publisher

boardgame: War of the Floating Islands of your Imagination
description:
Your dreams are at war.

In this Make-As-You-Play solitaire game, you will take control of one of the "islands of your dreams" that YOU create and name and lead it in battle through the gauntlet of all of the other islands, two enemies at a time.  (Whether your dreams are fanciful or twisted is none of my concern.)

To start your campaign, you will need 8 blank cards for making your islands (and more blank cards later on) and a standard deck of 52 playing cards (no jokers).

Each island in the game is represented by an arrangement of standard playing cards.  An attack from one of these cards can target any card of the same suit or rank within an enemy island.  (For example, a 7 of diamonds can knock out any 7 or any diamond.)  Islands are eliminated when they cannot attack anything or when all of their cards have been targeted.  YOU are in control of every attack, whether from your island or from an enemy, with the goal of keeping your own self afloat.

If you can last until your island is one of the last two floating, those two islands will grow and gain special abilities, and you will create a new island (making future games more difficult to win)!  Your premature elimination will see your island start to crumble (making future games easier) or possibly collapse entirely (...much easier).

-description from designer

7 Wonders: Architects – Medals brings new elements to the table for the 7 Wonders: Architects base game, without adding anything to the game's playing time.

First, this expansion includes two new wonders — Ur and Rome — to give players more choices for what to build.

Second, new science tokens exist that can be mixed with the original ones, tokens that give you the ability to, for example, keep military cards that you would normally discard or always have the cat ability available to you.

Finally, this expansion includes a new game element: medals. At the start of play, draw a random medal and place it face up between each pairing of players. (Each pairing already has a card rack between it, so now two items will be present.) When either of these players meets the conditions showing on a medal, they claim the medal and turn it face down, earning 4vp for this medal at game's end.

description:
Letter Together is a cooperative word game. It has 100 letter tiles, a strip with spaces for 30 tiles, and a downloadable timer app.

Players assemble the strip (from 6 joinable pieces), draw 15 tiles, and start the timer, with more time given for fewer players. They form a word at the start of the strip, and then draw more tiles, usually 2 less than the word formed. They then make another word that starts with one or more letters at the end of the letters already on the strip, and draw more tiles as before. This continues until either time runs out, or they fill the strip with 30 letters, in which case they win, and read the long compound word together.

There is also a competitive mode where 2 or 3 teams or individuals split the strip pieces evenly, take 12 tiles each, and play normally, with the first team to fill their strip winning.

—user summary

A serious-or-satirical political party card game for 3+ players.

Each round, one player reads a problem, and the others compete for a point within the time limit by giving their best speech using a solution. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

Why play politics?

Politics is an interesting game that people get to play if they went to a private school, but the rest of us don’t usually get a chance. It’s unique in that, unlike other games, we can’t tell how well players are doing based on the points they score, but instead based on how society looks.

Similar to sports like football, millions of us follow games of politics live, through televised matches such as General Elections. Unlike other games, it’s harder to get a group of people together to play ourselves (unless you have a lot of money, or a media empire).

Some countries play a version of politics where no-one new can join in, and the current players keep winning over and over again. Some countries even have a single-player version of the game. With Spin Machine, now everyone can join in.

What is Spin Machine?
Spin Machine is here to give you your chance in politics.

Spin Machine is a ‘serious-or-satirical’ political card game for 3+ players. Each round, one player reads a problem and the others compete for a point within the time limit by giving their best speech using a solution. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Spin Machine contains 200 solution cards, 100 problem cards, and a 30 second sand timer. The same things every politician needs when starting their career.

boardgame: DC Batman: Streets of Gotham City
description:
Batman is missing and now super vilains want to seize Gotham City and become the new boss in town.
But, they need some minions, henchmen and troops to achieve their goal !

You play as a provider, looking in the streets to find these henchmen.

DC Batman: Streets of Gotham City is an easy to play, hard to master, card game set in the DC Universe.

Each turn, you play one card, activate one card in play or draw one card from a Criminal Deck, in any order.

Each card is worth some points at the end of the game if it is in your hand. But of course, you need their effect to protect your hand, draw more cards or destroy others players minions.
And each card is working with another one to unlock powerful partners abilities.

You will also meet some Accomplice, minor villains like Catwoman or Deadshot with powerful effects but costly restriction.

You also work for two super villains at the same time. One your share with the player on your left, another with the player on your right. These two super villains give bonuses at the end of the game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Using pattern recognition, recall, and quick reflexes, be the first player to spot the piece of art from an array of forty cards with the hint provided by an app on your phone or tablet.

—user summary

boardgame: Zhanguo: The First Empire – Convention Promo Tiles
description:
Additional tiles first released at Spiel Essen 2023.

French and English rules for the promo tiles are included.

6 Palace tiles (2 each):
- Gain 2 VP for each of your Architects in the region you built the Palace.
- Gain 2 VP for each of your Generals in the region you built the Palace.
- Gain 2 VP for each of your Alchemists in the region you built the Palace.

2 Wall Tiles (double-sided):
- Gain 1/2/3 VP for each of your Terracotta Soldiers on a Mausoleum tile.
- Gain 2/4/6 VP for each of your Terracotta Soldiers at the entrance of the Mausoleum. You cannot score more than 3 Terracotta soldiers with this Wall tile.
- Gain 1/2/3 VP for each 4 steps your Ship moved.
- Gain 1/2/3 VP for each 20 VP you scored before endgame scoring.
family: History: Warring States Period of China

description:
Word for Word has 99 letter tiles, a bag to hold them, and a timer. Players start by drawing 9 letters. The first player forms a word from their tiles and places in the middle of the table.

In each turn thereafter, a player does one of the following to make a new word: 

     Rearrange the letters of the word.
     Swap a letter from their tiles with a letter in the word, possibly rearranging the letters. 
     Add one letter from the hand to the word, possibly rearranging the letters.
     Add one letter and swap one letter, possibly rearranging the letters.


Then they draw a tile from the bag to go back to 9 tiles, if necessary.

If a player cannot do anything to make a new word, they pass and are out for the rest of the round. Once everyone passes, the last player who modified the word gets 10 points for each letter in the final word.  The first player to reach a total score of 500 points (or another chosen goal) wins the game.

—user summary

Mario Party meets The Cabin in the Woods in Rituals, a cooperative multiversal horror game from Iconiq Studios.

You and your friends take on the roles of strangers who have become trapped by a malevolent force. You must work together to complete rituals, battle monsters, and escape before it’s too late!

Start by customising your board, combining pieces to not only shape the layout of the map but also influence the story of your game.

On each turn, players must navigate the 2D board to uncover keys, unlock doors, and solve puzzling rituals. Once every player has taken their turn, draw summon cards that contain statements such as 'IN A ROOM WITH A RED DOOR'. Add a token to the active monster card for each player who meets the condition, and when it reaches a critical point, it's time to do battle! 
 Each monster encounter is a quick and exciting mini-game. Test your skills with dice, coins, cards, timers, and more. Can you name 7 things that scare people? Balance 4 dice in a pillar on your hand? Close your eyes and stop the timer before it runs out? Every encounter guarantees laughter as your group tackles an array of fun and diverse challenges.

Once all rituals on the board are deciphered, it's time to make your escape! Some of you will make it out alive, others may not be so lucky. Regardless of the outcome, you'll all have a story to share.

—description from the designer

You are the Exiled Prince.

Catching your breath, halfway up the neverending stairs of Umbar.

Your sword is broken yet your heart still beats under your breastplate. So many steps beneath you. So many yet to climb...

Hark! is a short, printable, randomly-generated, replayable, solo fantasy adventure. The goal of the game is to climb 100 stairs before running out of one of the three main resources: Blood, Sweat, or Tears.

Over the course of the game, a player will roll dice to determine what they will encounter on a given turn, with each turn represented by the player climbing stairs.

Can you survive the long, treacherous path to the top of the stairway and face the dreaded "FINAL CHALLENGE"? Only those that persevere will live to tell the tale.

—description from the designer

Quangle consists of two decks of 55 cards:

     A blue-backed deck of word beginnings of 1 or 2 letters
     A red-backed deck of word endings of 2-5 letters. 


Rules are included for 9 different games that can be played, all of which involve pairing one blue and one red card to make a valid English word.

—user summary

Lyrical Liniment has 80 medicine-bottle-shaped cards, each with one or two letters, plus two wild cards.

To play a round, the dealer turns up the cards one at a time onto the table.  Once any player can make a word using at least 3 cards, they call out the word, and if it's judged a valid word, they gather and keep the cards that make it up.  If not valid, the get nothing and each other player gets a free card from the deck.  At the end of the round, the player with the most cards gets one point.

Each player has a turn being the dealer. After all the rounds, the player with the most points wins the game.

—user summary

A Reset Button expansion for Squabblin Goblins.


Shuffle one pile back into the deck, then replace with one face-up card from the deck.

Derhome is a tabletop version of the word game Wordle, that allows use of words up to seven letters. It includes:


     4 laminated worksheets for the guessers, with 6 rows of spaces for the guesses, and a keyboard on the bottom to mark showing which letters are or are not included.
     4 privacy screens for the guessers to hide their worksheets.
     A laminated master sheet to record the current secret word, and the scores of all players. 
     4 black dry-erase markers, for the guessers. 
     1 yellow and 1 green marker for the referee. 


It does not include instructions, but it's easy to figure out the rules: 

     In each round, a player is the referee and writes a secret word of up to 7 letters, and announces the length of the word. 
     The other players are guessers, and take simultaneous turns writing guesses of the secret word. 
     For each guess, the referee marks the guesses as in Wordle: letters that are in the right spot are circled green, letters in the word but the wrong spot are circled yellow, other letters are unmarked. 
     When a player guesses the word, the number of guesses is recorded on the master sheet. If they failed to guess it in 6 tries, a higher number like 7 is recorded. 
     After each player has had a turn as referee, the player with the lowest total guesses wins the game.

In Power City Arena you control a team of unique super heroes that fight for the control of Power City. All character have a unique ability that is always active and 2 super power that need energy to be used. Your goal will be to defeat other players' teams and be the last one standing on the board.

You start the game with a 15 actions cards deck that allow you to boost your characters, mainly charge their energy for their powers, you can also attack your ennemies or protect yourself. During the game you will be able to move around the board to seek for bonus that let you draw extra action cards, more powerful, or draw equipments that would increase your range, add extra damage to your attacks or give you extra health. If you seek for bonuses, you will make your deck stronger, give you advantage to your oponents by enlarge the possibility of actions.

Bonuses and turn order are set randomly at the begining of the game, each turn is by character order and not by players. The active character make all the action for himself but share the cards in hand with all the team the player control, so choose wisely wich character will use wich cards.

You start the game with a hand of 5 cards, each character's turn, you start by drawing 2 cards from your deck (if you start your turn on a bonus, you may draw card from the designated deck), you have 1 free movement to do whenever you want in your turn, you may use any number of cards from your hand, the only limit is for the attacks that are limited by 1 per turn (some bonuses can break this limit), then when you end your turn, you can't have more than 7 cards in your hand so discard the surplus.

When a character's health reach 0 he is eliminated and there is no way for him to come back, when all the characters from a team are eliminated, the team is eliminated and lose. When all other team are eliminated, you win the game.

-description from designer

The first expansion for Unrest, Uprising, plays over 3-5 games and introduces new gameplay twists for the Rebellion and the Empire. Each player takes on the role of the Rebellion or the Empire for the entire campaign, and the first player to win three games claims victory! The total playtime is 1-2 hours and can be played in a single setting or over multiple sessions.

Gameplay is the same as the base game with a few exceptions. Each player will have Enhancement cards to aid them during the game. Additionally, after the first game, each subsequent one in the campaign will include a unique Scenario that changes elements of the game.

—description from the publisher

In GridHack you’ll play the role of a hacker, invading a machine through the weak spots in code, and building your own hacking network to steal data. But you better hurry – you’ll be playing against the counter-hack program, which can introduce firewalls, freeze or corrupt your network and more.

GridHack is a game that takes place on a grid, similar to graph paper. Over a series of turns, you'll roll dice to add nodes, bugs, and grow your hacking network. Copy enough data before the counter-hack program completes, and you win the game!

On your turn you'll roll two dice. The result of the dice determine the actions you can choose to take on your turn - expand your network, or add a node or bug to your network. Doubles allow you to take both actions OR an action of your choice. You can also choose to ignore the dice rolls and take any action of your choice - but if you do, the counter-hack program gets to advance two steps.

After your turn, the counter-hack program goes. This is a pre-programmed series of turns designed to stop you from hacking the network and stealing data. The counter-hack program has one action it takes per turn. After the counter-hack program is complete, it is your turn again.

Play continues turn after turn between you and the counter-hack program, until you copy enough data and win, or the counter-hack program complete and you lose.

—description from the designer

2 parts of Moral dilemma behind you, and you are still friends? What's more, you're sure you know everything about yourself? Nothing could be further from the truth! The new version of your favorite party game and its over 150 new challenges will quickly dispel this myth! "Dylematy 3" is a party game for adults, full of difficult choices.
Sit in a circle in a group of 4 to 15 people and give each player 2 voting cards ("A" and "B"). Choose a deck to play and who will start playing. Each turn, the Dilemma card lands on the table – a moral issue with two possible solutions. Your task is to predict which option most people will choose at the table. The group can – and even should, we strongly encouraged to! – discuss possible solutions, after which each player makes a decision. You score if you have correctly predicted the majority vote.
The ultimate goal of the game is to collect 5 points, but the secret to having fun is to talk. The cards are to be an excuse to discuss more or less  crazy topics.

—description from the publisher (translated)

2 części Dylematów za Wami, a mimo to wciąż się przyjaźnicie? Co więcej, jesteście pewni, że wiecie o sobie wszystko? Nic bardziej mylnego! Nowa odsłona Waszej ulubionej gry imprezowej i zawarte w niej ponad 150 nowych wyzwań szybko obalą ten mit!
Dylematy 3 to imprezowa zabawa skierowana do osób dorosłych, pełna trudnych wyborów. Usiądźcie w kręgu w grupie od 4 do 15 osób i rozdajcie każdemu z graczy po 2 karty do głosowania. Wybierzcie talię, którą będziecie grali oraz tego, kto rozpocznie zabawę. A potem dajcie się porwać dyskusji o moralności i konsekwencjach swoich decyzji!
W każdej turze na stole ląduje karta Dylematu: zagadnienia moralnego z dwoma możliwymi rozwiązaniami. Twoim zadaniem jest przewidzieć, którą opcję wybierze większość osób przy stole. Grupa może – a nawet powinna, gorąco do tego zachęcamy! – dyskutować nad możliwymi rozwiązaniami, po czym każdy z graczy podejmuje decyzję. Punktujesz, jeśli udało Ci się poprawnie przewidzieć głos większości.
Ostatecznym celem gry jest zebranie 5 punktów, ale sekret dobrej zabawy polega na rozmowie. Karty mają stanowić pretekst do dyskusji na mniej lub bardziej odjechane tematy.

—description from the publisher

The Fractured Family is the first Issue of the Cosmic Power Story Arc. This 55-card set includes Fantastic Four heroes from across the Marvel Universe. The First Family faces challenges to the balance of power in outer space and threats to reality as we know it!

Scrambled 5s is a game about placing numbered tiles on a square grid in a crossword pattern.

Players can place up to 5 tiles in a line, forming sequences of up to 5 tiles, such that their values total a multiple of 5. Additional points are marked on special board spaces. The game ends when the tiles run out and the highest scoring player wins.

The special silicone board with raised ridges prevents pieces from sliding.

This pack contains a few components given away exclusively to Kickstarter backers.

3 Ship Cards:

     Sct 33 "Pincer"
     Sct 34 "Quel'Far"
     MD 23 "Teir'Dal"


4 Ship Standees

7 Action Cards:

     Plasma Overload
     Corps of Engineers
     We Nailed 'em, Sir!
     Give Them Everything!
     Let Us End This
     Better Part of Valor
     Shields Holding
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: Unsolved Crime Cases: Tod in den Alpen
description:
In this thrilling crime escape game, an exciting case awaits you in the middle of the Alps. Trapped in the snow and completely isolated from the outside world, a cold-blooded murder takes place in a small hotel. This can only mean one thing: The perpetrator is still in the hotel! But who was it? And will the murderer strike again? Maybe you will find something in the hotel rooms that will put you on the right track? Visit all the places related to the case, collect clues and evidence, question the witnesses and interrogate your suspects in an exciting multimedia finale.
Assist the hotel guests in this mysterious case and collect real evidence. You can record all your investigative progress on the clue board. Who will think creatively enough and convict the perpetrator? Only in the multimedia finale will you see whether you are right with your conclusions. A case for real investigators!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crime & Dine: Das Krimi-Dinner-Set – Babylon Berlin
description:
Invite your guests to an exciting dinner evening in 1920s Berlin. But be warned: murder is on the menu tonight! As the game ends, you are the main suspects in a mysterious murder case: on the evening of the theatre premiere, the director is suddenly found dead, but there is no trace of either the murderer or the murder weapon! The dinner and the party are already in full swing - so the perpetrator is among you. Together you slip into the roles of the guests and actors and try to find the murderer. Who will think creatively enough and bring the truth to light? Will you succeed in solving the murder in the end, or will you let the perpetrator get away scot-free?
This set contains all the materials you need for an unforgettable mystery party with 5-8 people. With the additional recipes, costume suggestions, decoration ideas and the accompanying playlist, the evening is guaranteed to be a complete success. The mystery game is ideal for a murderously fun evening with family and friends or as an event gift for a birthday.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crime & Dine: Das Krimi-Dinner-Set – Tödliches Bankett
description:
Invite your guests to an exciting dinner evening in 1910s England. But be warned: murder is on the menu today! As the game's end, you are the main suspects in a mysterious death: at the annual banquet of Lord and Lady Greendale, everyone who is anyone meets, but then the lord is suddenly found dead and there is no trace of either the murderer or the murder weapon! The dinner and the party are already in full swing - so the culprit is among you. Together you slip into the roles of the guests and servants of the banquet and try to find the murderer. Who will think creatively enough and bring the truth to light? In the end, will you succeed in solving the case or will you let the perpetrator get away scot-free?

—description from publisher

Three exciting adventures await you in this official Escape Game for the successful Netflix series Lupin! Puzzle your way through the interactive game alone or together with your friends. You can choose between three different cover identities of Assane Diop, each with a different gameplay. Can you get into the house of the Pellegrini family without being seen, collect evidence against the entrepreneur and disappear again within 90 minutes? The clock is ticking!

—description from the publisher

On a secret mission: Together with Samuel Katelbach and Elisabeth Behnke you are on your way to Moka Efti to find clues to the Armenian's crooked dealings. But your pursuers are hot on your heels and a race against time begins! Will you manage to escape in time?

boardgame: Lupin: Escape Game – Das Buch zur Netflix-Erfolgsserie mit 100 Rätseln
description: In this official Escape Game in book form for the successful Netflix series Lupin, exciting puzzles, encrypted codes and hidden clues await you. Will you manage to commit the perfect crime?

Paraphrased from their official German web site:
Flippi is a dexterity game that combines elements of pinball and mini golf.

It's manufactured from sustainable materials. The production is carried out with a lot of passion and enthusiasm in the publishers rehabilitation workshop. A rehabilitation facility for people with chronic mental illnesses.

No matter where you are - with this exciting Stadt, Land, Gartenliebe edition, you'll always bring a big dose of fun with you! In 26 categories you can test your gardening knowledge and prove your creativity. The game block convinces with a pretty design and interesting topics - perfect for everyone with a green thumb. From simple questions to tricky categories - there's something here for everyone.

boardgame: Der ultimative Spielblock: Kopf gegen Kopf – Wer ist es?
description:
Whether at home or on the road - the popular classic games in a handy take-away format ensure lots of fun. Particularly practical: the erectable anti-cheat divider - simply unfold and get started straight away.

Who is it? in three fun game variations - who asks the cleverest questions and guesses the character they are looking for first? In a cool new design, the block is guaranteed to provide ultimate fun. The practical take-away format makes the game block perfect as a travel game for young and old. And especially important: thanks to the anti-cheating partition that can be set up, cheaters don't stand a chance. Are you ready? Then grab your pens and let's get started!

—description from the publisher

The Phaistos Disc is still a mystery but a valuable item too because it may contains the knowledge of Minos king's Island and is protected by the gods such as Zeus !

In this abstract race, you will play a Hero and try to bring back the disc to your city, but you will also play a God to help your hero and interfere into the others race...

The game mechanism is easy and let you play your hero AND your God with only ONE chosen card provided by the Delphi's Oracle. Of course, you have to plan your way to victory, by managing your hand, erect temple and obtain ancient gods grateful help !

There is four (4) game modes including small resource management, some race blocking tricks and secret/victory condition revelation for the more experienced players.

—description from the publisher

In this adventure book, a gripping live escape game awaits you that takes you to Paris in 1904. The Eiffel Tower is threatened with demolition, but the designer Gustave Eiffel has suddenly disappeared without a trace. What happened to him? Has he been kidnapped? Take on the role of Eiffel's assistant, follow the mysterious tracks through the districts of Paris and unravel the monumental conspiracy surrounding the Eiffel Tower!
Travel through the districts of Paris in 1904, puzzle your way through history and unravel the mysterious conspiracy surrounding the Eiffel Tower. Can you follow all the clues correctly and find out Eiffel's secret? A real Escape Adventure!

boardgame: Peaky Blinders: Das Spiel – Bis zum letzten Atemzug
description:
Birmingham in the 20th century - The city is dominated by criminal gangs who have only one goal: To expand their power. And they will stop at nothing to do so. Robberies, blackmail, illegal betting and smuggling - under their leader Thomas Shelby, the Peaky Blinders try to get closer to their goal by any means necessary. But in the process they face a number of enemies. Among them is the opposing gang of the dangerous Billy Kimber.

In this official game of the hit series, you slip into the familiar roles of the enemy gangs and try to seize the power of the city. In the roles of the Peaky Blinders and as Billy Kimber's henchmen, you face each other in Birmingham. Either you help Thomas Shelby with his plans or you try to sabotage the notorious gang. But your true identities remain secret, so you can't trust anyone in this game!

—description from the publisher

Cartzzle is a new concept of board game containing a game, a work of art and puzzles. Compose this master piece of Vincent Van Gogh, then redo it by taking up challenges.

The box holds a single Cartzzle, an endless one you can make with both sides of the 50 cards. Cartzzles are pictures you must first piece together by laying cards on top of each other (with or without the template, depending on your dedication). Then, start again, using challenges this time! Removing cards, flipping them, and laying them elsewhere will change the picture and also allow you to meet some challenges (as example: turn of the church light in the picture).

Cartzzle is a new concept of board game containing a game, a work of art and puzzles. Compose this graphic interpretation of Stephane Escapa, then redo it by taking up challenges.

The box holds a single Cartzzle, an endless one you can make with both sides of the 50 cards. Cartzzles are pictures you must first piece together by laying cards on top of each other (with or without the template, depending on your dedication). Then, start again, using challenges this time! Removing cards, flipping them, and laying them elsewhere will change the picture and also allow you to meet some challenges (as example: Help the daddy to find is puppets).

Cartzzle is a new concept of board game containing a game, a work of art and puzzles. Compose this graphic interpretation of Pauline Détraz, then redo it by taking up challenges.

The box holds a single Cartzzle, an endless one you can make with both sides of the 50 cards. Cartzzles are pictures you must first piece together by laying cards on top of each other (with or without the template, depending on your dedication). Then, start again, using challenges this time! Removing cards, flipping them, and laying them elsewhere will change the picture and also allow you to meet some challenges (as example: Compose a scene that show only people going to left).

Welcome to the world of BattleMonsters. A barren dystopian world where humans are using monsters with wicked elemental powers as their slaves. In this world, wars are not only fought with weapons, but also with trained monsters unleashing the wrath of the elements upon their enemies. A great world war has razed the globe, severely crippling society. Nuclear warfare has led to great uninhabitable fallout zones, separating regions by vast radioactive wastelands.

People must fight for survival in this anarchistic society with little to no government. Colonies and republics ruled by the warlords and tyrants with the strongest BattleMonsters are constantly rising and falling in an ongoing post apocalyptic BattleMonster civil war. Do you have what it takes to become a beastmaster and lead your own party of BattleMonsters to victory?

This Rules Booklet contains the basic rules for your games of BattleMonsters!

Challenge your rival to a duel of an agreed upon Battlepoint value or embark on an epic campaign with villains and chatacable monsters!

This tabletop miniatures game for two or more players is all about little monsters duking it out against each other in epic pit fights. This booklet contains all the basic rules you will need to play games with your BattleMonsters miniatures, as well as tips for how to assemble and paint them.

This rules booklet is divided into sections, explaining the rules and procedures for playing single battles as well as how to incorporate a series of battles into larger narrative campaigns, where players can catch BattleMonsters and battle against all sorts of villains and bosses in a fight against your rivals to become the ultimate beastmaster!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: With Hot Lead and Cold Steel: American Civil War Wargaming Rules
description:
A set of wargaming rules for fighting large battles set during the American Civil War.

From the First Bull Run to Appomattox Court House, enter one of the defining conflicts of American history. With shot, shell, and sabre, guide the armies of the Blue and the Grey through this first modern war to determine the fate of a nation.

With Hot Lead and Cold Steel is a large-scale, mass-battle wargame for recreating the American Civil War. Designed to handle brigade and divisional level engagements while providing a balance between ease of play and period detail, With Hot Lead and Cold Steel is ideal for new gamers and wargaming veterans alike. It contains everything players need to raise armies and craft scenarios, whether based upon historical campaigns and orders of battle or those of their own devising.

-description from publisher's website

Boathooks is a set of late medieval naval wargames rules related to (but not requiring) their landlubbing forefather: Never Mind the Billhooks.

Boathooks is a light-hearted card and dice-driven game of skirmish combat on the seas, where a tactician’s approach counts for little in the face of the tides of fortune.

The rules were given away free with Wi426 - the June 2023 issue of Wargames Illustrated. You can order a copy of the magazine and the rules from the Wi webstore!


     A handful of medieval model boats and figures are required for each side.



     Create and customise your fleet with a variety of warships, galleys, support vessels (skiffs), and more advanced vessels (carracks, merchantmen, and great ships).



     Rally your seafarers with Captains and Admirals. Each player requires one Captain figure onboard each of their warships and an Admiral on their flagship.



     Lead your forces to victory and win the sea skirmish by capturing/sinking your enemy’s flagship or by depleting their morale.


-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A multi-deck of cards for playing any game you can play with a traditional deck, as well as a Pairs deck, and many other types of games.

Play with friends, receive cards, send cards, complete challenges and become the most extrovert player.

Send and impose extroverted challenges to prevent other players from running out of cards; and thus, be the first player who manages to start his turn without cards in hand, to win the game.

In most cases, the cards in your hand are not for you. They are for sending to someone else!

Choose a card from your hand and send it to the player you choose, that player must do what the card says! If he or she wants to win.

Start your turn with no cards in your hand to win the game!

—description from the designer

Players are "hobos" living on the road in the Depression-era.  Object of the game is to accumulate 1,000 miles traveled, find 3 days food supply, getting a job offer and $25 for new clothes.
—user summary

"Congratulations!  You are the only hobos left in America.  Enjoy the challenge, excitement and life of a hobo...riding the rails, hiding from the sheriff, searching for food, money & a job, so that you can say "I Quit the Life of a Hobo" and win!!
—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Waters Unvexed:  Three Battles of the  Mississippi River, 1862
description:
Waters Unvexed details the three largest naval battles fought on the upper Mississippi River (north of the city of Vicksburg) in the months of May and June, 1862. Both sides viewed control of the river as key to the war’s outcome. The Confederate vessels of the Mississippi River Defense Fleet had been formed from converted river sidewheel paddle steamers whose bows had been fitted with iron rams and reinforced with packed bales of cotton, as well as armed with various numbers and sizes of artillery pieces. The Union Mississippi River Squadron was made up of purpose-built stern paddlewheel gunboats and sidewheel rams. The Union vessels were fitted in many places with thin iron sheets and dubbed “tin clads”. The gunboats were all armed with powerful rifled cannon while their rams were only armed with their iron prows. The game portrays the battles of Island Number 10, Plum Point Bend and the First Battle of Memphis. Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

Each copy of Waters Unvexed is composed of the following:

•    Three 11 by 17 inch hex maps.
•    One sheet of 154 single-sided, un-mounted counters.
•    One players’ aid sheet.
•    8 pages of rules.

Players will also need one standard deck of playing cards, a six-sided die and one ten-sided die to play the game. A die roll of 0 is read as 10 and not zero. An opaque container (mug, envelope) is also needed to play the game.

Each turn represents 15 minutes of time. Each hex is about 200 yards across. Each unit represents 1 warship or a battery of 6-10 cannon.

Players use a standard deck of cards to determine who activates and with how many units, as well as when to determine random events and the end of the turn. Dice are used to resolve combats and other game events. Play is very interactive and true to the history.

-description from designer

This is a set of 7 Years War miniatures rules.  They are done on the scale of 1" = 50 yards, 1 fig = 50 men, and 1 turn = 15 minute. It uses percentile dice for resolutions.

Players are projected into an INCA universe. 
 They become archaeologists and must face trials. 
 The goal is to collect as much gold as possible before the temple burns down!

Progress of a game
The player who has visited the most countries starts the game.
In case of a tie, the youngest goes first.
The first player rolls the movement dice and advances the number of squares.
To find gold several solutions,
- reach the squares which have a drawn piece, on which a gold piece is placed at the start of the game.
- the player advances to a “Search” space, there he draws a card, and among 8 possibilities, he can win 1 or 2 gold coins for example.
- reach the "Entry" boxes, another possibility is to participate in the 3 events, by betting a coin on the big spider web, if you succeed you win a golden statuette (1 statuette = 3 gold coins )
- finally by throwing the "Action Die" we can obtain "spider web" and there we take all the pieces that are on the spider web.

To summarize, the player rolls dice, moves his "Archaeologist" pawn, draws cards, realizes the effects of the squares, participates in tests...
The game ends when the temple erupts, then each player counts their gold coins and statuettes, the one with the most wins.

Les joueurs sont projetés dans un univers INCA. 
 Ils deviennent des archéologues et doivent faire face à des épreuves. 
 Le but est de récupérer le plus d'or possible avant que le temple ne s'enflamme !

Déroulement d'une partie
Le joueur qui a visité le plus de pays commence la partie.
En cas d’égalité, le plus jeune commence.
Le premier joueur lance les dés déplacement et avance du nombre de case. 
Pour trouver de l'or plusieurs solutions, 
- atteindre les cases qui ont une pièces dessinée, sur laquelle on placé en début de partie une pièce d'or.
- le joueur avance sur une case "Fouille", là il tire une carte, et parmi 8 possibilités, il peut gagner 1 ou 2 pièces d'or par exemple.
- atteindre les case "Entrée", autre possibilité c'est de participer aux 3 épreuves, en pariant une pièce sur la grande toile d'araignée, si on réussi on gagne une statuette d'or (1 statuette = 3 pièce d'or)
- enfin en jetant le "Dé Action" on peut obtenir "toile d'araignée" et là on prends toutes les pièces qui il y a sur la toile d'araignée.

Pour résumé, le joueur lance des dés, déplace son pion "Archéologue", tire des cartes, réalise les effets des cases, participe à des épreuves...
La partie s'arrête quand le temple rentre en éruption, là chaque joueur compte ses pièces d'or et ses statuettes, celui qui en a le plus, à gagné.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering Universes Beyond – Doctor Who
description:
In this crossover expansion, Magic: The Gathering visits the Doctor Who universe, with a set of four preconstructed Commander decks themed around the various iterations of the Doctor, their companions, and their adventures together through time and space.

The "Blast from the Past" deck features the first eight Doctors and their companions, and is built around the Historic mechanic, with lots of legendary creatures, artifacts, and Sagas. The "Timey-Wimey" deck features Doctors Nine, Ten, and Eleven (and the War Doctor), and is built around the new Time Travel mechanic, which allows you to manipulate time counters on your battlefield and on suspended cards. The "Paradox Power" deck features Doctors Twelve and Thirteen (and the Fugitive Doctor), and is built around the new Paradox mechanic, which rewards you for casting spells from anywhere other than your hand. And finally, the "Masters of Evil" showcases the greatest villains of the series, featuring the new Villainous Choice mechanic to force your foes to choose their own demise.

Each deck also includes a 10-card oversized planar deck for use with the Planechase format, so you can fly your TARDIS from planet to planet, changing the rules of the game depending on where you land.

The set is also available in collector booster packs, which can contain any of the non-oversized cards from the four decks—including special foil, extended art, and/or alt-art versions that can't be found anywhere else.

Game Rover is the first game designed to play with your best friend (the one who was looking at you longingly on family game night). In this fast-paced game, players draw a card, set a timer, and have 60 seconds to keep drawing cards and trying to get their dog to do as many trick as they can before time runs out. Rules are written in both people and dog's native language (woof woof..woof). Game Rover not only provides endless fun for you and your dog, but also offers a sneaky opportunity to train practice tricks with them. Find out who your dog loves the most once and for all, or compete with other players and their dogs, to see who wins the coveted "Top Dog" status.

INCLUDE YOUR DOG IN GAME NIGHT: Your dog has been patiently waiting this whole time... it's about time for him to join the fun! Best for (humans) ages 8 and up.

CHOOSE YOUR PLAYERS: Play with multiple humans or dogs, you choose! Get your family together and find out who can get the dog(s) to do more tricks, or invite friends and their dog to play agaisnt them!

WHO IS YOUR DOG'S FAVORITE PERSON? It's fun for the whole family and we encourage all positive reinforcement methods to get your dog to complete the tricks – treats, toys, and even you doing it with him – it all counts for the fun!

Do you have strong opinions about stuff? Then RANT is perfect for you! Players must talk...er, rant, for 60 seconds about a topic that may be controversial. Maybe it's "pineapple is the best pizza topping" or "Bigfoot exists." Players must defend the position as if it's their own. The catch is, if the one ranting stumbles, pauses too long, or repeats themselves, the other players buzz in and a rotating judge passes judgment. The player speaking at the end of 1 minute gets the card and the point!

DING THE OTHER PLAYERS: The goal is to be the one talking when the timer runs out. So if someone else is talking, ring the bell if they hesitate, stumble, or repeat and take over with that much time left. Just don't do it yourself!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Arrowstorm: Battle Games in Medieval Britain
description:
CAN YOU WEATHER THE ARROWSTORM?

In this gridded-layout miniature wargame, you take the role of a medieval king, bishop, lord, or commander leading your army on the battlefield. You must choose where and how to focus your troops' actions and control the battle’s impetus. The battle is partly fought on the tabletop like other miniature wargames but is also fought just as hard on the Tactics Chart, providing an additional mind-game against your opponent.

Command your units to move and fight in the areas of the battlefield you wish, but leave the actual process of fighting to your unit commanders. This is represented by the random factor of dice and each of your units’ abstract but often-decreasing Battle Rating. Because of this, your focus is kept where it should be – out-thinking your opponent by using superior tactics and exploiting their weaknesses on the battlefield.

Arrowstorm is not intended as a heavy simulation of medieval warfare but as an enjoyable, simple, and fast-playing battle game. You’ll find that much of the action is stylized and streamlined. Don’t think of “turns” in the usual wargaming way – in each turn your units may fight or move several times, countered by your opponent after every action, creating a very fluid game but at the same time making cohesive planning difficult to achieve.

Please note that Arrowstorm shares the same basic rules with my Age of Penda book. Period-specific troop types, army lists, special rules, and minor rules differences exist.

THE ‘YOU COMMAND’ SERIES
‘You Command’ is a series of light miniature wargames by Origins Award winner Daniel Mersey (Lion Rampant, Dragon Rampant, Battle Ravens, and many more games). When you play a ‘You Command’ game, your goal is to defeat your opponent by out-thinking them tactically using the innovative Tactics Chart, and then by out-fighting their model army on the tabletop battlefield.

—description from the designer

However, you're not the only master criminal in the city of sin, so if you want to pull off as many heists as possible, you'll have to be clever.
You'll need to choose your heists wisely, because one wrong move could cost you the game, but playing too carefully can be just as risky. Every game will have you weighing the risks and either celebrating or commiserating once the cards are on the table.

description:
愛羅武粋逸 (あいらぶすいいつ) (A ira busu i itsu) is a game from Japan, where one player is trying to fool the other players (working together as a chorus (大喜利 Ogiri)).

Each player is a JKヤンキー (JK Yankee), a High School delinquent, from the same school or the local area (boys or girls alike).  The players take turns to try and buy sweets from a shopkeeper (the player on their left), without the other Yankees finding out, because wanting sweets will damage their reputation. In Japan, Yankee is a broad term from 1904 meaning thug or tough, later being styled on the 50s rockers/bikers style, and then with punks.

The turn player looks at a theme card showing a list of different confectionery they want to buy, and then writes on a whiteboard. But they try to write in a way that only the shopkeeper will comprehend. If the gang of their yob friends can work out the sweets the player is trying to buy, they win the theme card, worth 1pt. But if the player can disguise their intent, and the player who's the shopkeeper this turn can also read it, they win the card instead.

So the player is trying to write the characters in a way that the shopkeeper will understand, but the others will not. For example, to order a Macaron the player might write Makron. The game is timed with a stopwatch, with the same time being applied to the Yankee and to the gang. So the faster the Yankee can complete their writing, the less time the gang has to work it out.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points is the winner, being the best at disguising and deducing.

description:
ロングロングマホウ (Long Long Maho) is a party game from Japan. The players are part of a magic school that disguises its spells by using tongue-twisters. Players take turns reading ever more complex phrases until somebody fumbles their turn and is out. The last one standing takes the points.

The game has a set of cards with short phrases, like ボポボホ」(Bopo Boho) or ンルンンルヌ (Nrununrunu). The round starts out with a couple of cards being dealt up and the starting player reads them out loud, within a time limit. The game has a digital stopwatch.

The next player draws another card, and adds it into the set where ever they like and reads it out. Any player that chews their words, or goes over the time limit, is out. Play goes on like this, with more cards being added until only player can read it out loud correctly. That player score a point.

After five rounds, the winner is the player with the most points.

The designer makes it clear that they are not actually a wizard and these are not real spells and the game is perfectly safe to play. Perfectly.

マジカルカナグル (Magical Ka Naguru) is a two player card game from Japan. The players are fighting a magical battle, but use bluff and deception as well and magic and weapons.

Each player starts by constructing a deck for the battle. Then the players take turns by playing a card face-down and declaring what the card is. But the player can lie, and trick their opponent into wasting a good defence, whilst striking them with a staff.

But failing to bluff well can result in a harsh counterattack. If the other player responds with a weapon, the staff is useless and the player takes a hit.

The players continue taking turns until one is defeated.

The artwork is by Polygonotes in their distinctive stained glass style.

description:

In Who’s The Boss, you compete with 1-4 other players (assisted by a non-playing banker) to roll and move around three concentric tracks, with the aim of having the most money at the end of the game.

You have to try to get promoted to the inner tracks in turn, either by landing on the correct square, drawing the correct chance card, or collecting a set of five things. When one player is promoted to the centre of the board, the game ends and you count your cash.

These are simple and straightforward Napoleonic rules for miniatures, based on orders’ counters placed on the table face down. Orders directly determine unit’s actions and combat outcomes.
The rules are at Regiment’s level, the name by which units were known by Generals, therefore minutia pertaining to the Company and Battalion organization are not directly addressed, they must be considered as factored-in.
The scale and abstraction levels are best represented by 1:100 (15 mm) miniatures or smaller, but card counters can be used instead.
There is minimal use of dice, for marginal effects only.
Army lists are not needed: just take any battle map and setup Regiments as they were deployed in the real situation.
Any basing system will work, no base modifications required.
This edition will be for Continental wars only, From Montenotte to Paris.
There are no markers, bookkeeping or cumbersome tables.
Note: if not using the base offset system proposed, shaken and square markers will be required.

Characteristics:
Miniatures Scale: 1:100 (15 mm) or smaller
Simulation level: Regiment’s level; mildly abstract.
Speed: very fast.
Solo potential: very high.

Available:
https://www.wargamevault.com/product/455626/Bayonets-on-Hold

皇継の書状 (Koukei No Shojyou) is a card game from Japan that combines deck construction and a worker placement by using people cards upon letter cards. The game marks the 10th production of Polygonotes and features Hugame's innovative game system.

In the game, player's are aides of different princesses following the death of the emperor, trying to build up the nation. The previous emperor was a tyrant, shunned by the people, so many talented people were scattered all over the place. The aide's are using letters from the princess to bolster support from Masters in the regions and build their strength in the land.

The game works with a deck construction system and uses card placement to trigger the letters of influence (the title translates as Letter of Imperial Succession). The game is designed to be quick but intense, serious but easy to play though. As players develop their field, the goal is to send people to their own flag, building up Imperial Succession points.

Key to the game are the Letter cards. By placing other cards on a Letter, the player triggers action such as drawing cards, exchange cards for stronger cards, generate coins and so on. But the Letter cards are one-off and must be bought again and prepared before their effect can be used.

By drawing on the skills of a Master, the player can amplify their actions and attack their opponents by forcing them to reduce their hands of cards. By this way, the Masters let players use powerful cards quickly and not feel a mistake of buying the wrong card.

This combination of one-off Letters and powerful Masters means the player must try to build a strong set of experts to make the most of the preparation. By sending these cards to the player's own flag, the player who gets enough support wins by achieving imperial succession.

The Master cards also can improve by rotating in the tableau, so a merchant goes from generating 2 coins to 3 coins. This way, cards obtained early in the game remain useful as the players progress.

boardgame: ビルドリーダーズ (Build Leaders)
description:
ビルドリーダーズ (Build Leaders) is a deck building game from Japan. In the game, players take on the role of mentor to a juniour character (akin to merlin coaching King Arthur). The focus of the game is developing a leader, and players are building up their victory points by crafting a story around the character from their first meeting.

publishers: Pantarei Games (パンタレイゲームズ)

description:
Atomic Edge is a resource-management and area-control strategy game that pits you against your friends in a high-stakes race for control in an increasingly dangerous nuclear Cold War. Play as a nuclear superpower vying for control over the neutral territories of the world. A cold war has started between you and the other superpowers of the world and it is vital to your people that you expand and maintain your influence around the world. Use a combination of military, diplomatic, and subversive tactics to increase your Influence on the world stage.

—description from the publisher

ドランク (Drunk) is a card game from Japan, reflecting the after-work drinking culture where employees are obliged to join their boss drinking all evening.

The game has three kinds of cards, Drinks which range from tea to whisky, Locations including sushi restaurant and the boss's house, and Drunkenness cards to measure intoxication. This includes a special card that let's a player sober up.

Players take turns being the Boss, and go from one location to another playing Drink cards. These are affected by the location, so at a sushi place you cannot get whisky, but at a bar whisky has zero effects on you.

As the game proceeds, players get more or less drunk than the Boss. This makes the Boss or the staff draw more cards. If the Boss reaches 10pts of Drunkenness they are out (cold), and the next player becomes the Boss. The Boss has various things to call out, such as "Well, for now." and everyone in unison shouts "Kampai!". Later the Boss calls "I'm going to reorganize." and so on, so it feels like a drinking party.

After the players have gone round enough places, and everyone has been Boss, the player who has the least Drunkenness is the winner.
user summary

The publisher says: 
Players are divided into bosses and subordinates and compete based on the strength of their drink cards, and the losing player receives the drunkenness card. In the end, the player with the fewest points on his drunkenness card will receive the title of "Big Drinker" and win.

Can you keep drinking until the end without getting drunk at a drinking party with different conditions depending on the bar?
publishers: Fuji Game Factory (フジゲームファクトリー)

description:
Singularity Hex is a sci-fi horror survival game where 4 players work together to escape an ever-changing ship.

The goal of the game is to collect 9 artefacts by defeating monsters avoiding obstacles and using your character's special abilities all while working together because you are connected and if one of you dies everybody dies.

—description from the designer

Created by Brazilian writer Egídio Trambaiolli Neto, based on the Legend of King Arthur, Scottish and English knights try to win the sword in this abstract game, while Celtic Sorceresses need to protect it. Excalibur is the name of the sword that belonged to King Arthur. According to legend, Excalibur was forged in Avalon, the magical island of the priestess Morgana, the Lady of the Lake, located in the south of England. There are two versions about how the sword came to belong to Arthur, in those related to Lancelot and the Holy Grail, Excalibur was embedded in a stone and, in the other version, Exalibur was given to Arthur by Morgana, the Lady of the Lake. Excalibur could only belong to someone with a good heart, with extraordinary character and integrity, whoever got it would be the future king of Britannia, the old name of England when the tale appeared. In this game, knights from two different kingdoms must compete and at the same time face Celtic sorceresses, among the swamps, to have the right to conquer Excalibur, which is once again trapped in stone, right in the middle of Avalon. Only a true heir of Arthur will be able to accomplish such a feat.
The game happens in a board with and octagonal grid. Knights move from octagon to octagon, while sorceresses move from square to square. Captures happens when two peons of the attacker (knight or sorcerer)are placed adjacent to the captured item, but diagonally opposite each other.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
Create your own Aquario! The rules are very simple: no two same sea creatures or colours can be side by side.

But watch your opponent – he could break your Aquario!

The game consists of a total of 60 cards, which are all shuffled together.

Most of the cards are animal cards (fish, crabs, starfish and turtles in different colors and point value). The remaining cards are action cards that allow cards that have already been placed to be moved.

The aim is to place nine of these cards in front of you in the 3x3 grid with as many points as possible.

The game ends when a player has created a completely correct aquarium (no touching of the same species/colors) or the draw pile is used up. When the game ends the player who scores the most points for their aquarium wins.

boardgame: Der Pott: Mit Hüftgold kannste keen Häusken kaufen!
description:
The Pott – this game is named after the Ruhr area (Germany).

The term stands for a region of land that is lower than its surroundings, based on the interior of a pot, which is also colloquially known as a pot.

With regard to the Ruhr area, the region was an immigration area, a melting pot, for over 200 years. The coal pot, a bucket for transporting coal, is also used to explain the name.

But one thing is certain: everything revolves around mining and no other region in Germany is as typical, as diverse and as colorful as the Ruhr area.

Let's do a quick brainstorming session. What comes to mind? Mines, allotment gardens, dovecotes, drinking halls, mixed bags, football, bratwurst, beer, Grönemeyer, Schimanski, Opel Manta, culture and multiculturalism. Who can add more terms?

Of course, there are also many tensions and problems: the change in the pot is far from complete. But no matter which corner you come from, the Pott is home.

What could be better than winning the pot playfully in a friendly atmosphere? The main thing here is not to leave the playing field as the richest, but rather to have fun!

Let's quickly go to the drinking hall and get a mixed bag for a snack. Tackle? A tacken was the ten pfennig piece and in return you got sweet treats. Don't you know anything anymore? The grandparents can certainly tell a few anecdotes.

The game is reminiscent of Monopoly. However, the cards to be purchased do not have a fixed price but must be purchased using a bidding mechanism. There is also the possibility of shortening half the game board using a special route that runs across the board (the A40 motorway).

Mining history, traditions, people born on coal and with steel in their veins!
Who invests with the right instinct and relies on the fastest carrier pigeon? Good luck!

During the Game Setup, shuffle the 4 tiles and randomly place them in the last spaces of each row in the Academy.
The first player whose Decorateur reaches the last space of a row  removes the tile and immediately resolves its effect.

RooTile is a board game for 2-4 players with a solo 1-player mode where you connect flowers to water using root tiles.

Players place root tiles to try to connect their Flower tile at the top of the board with the water at the bottom of the board.  Action tokens can be used to move or rotate root tiles after they are placed, sometimes producing a sudden win.  Tiles are distributed randomly at the beginning of the game and are hidden from the other players.

Battle your friends in fast-paced, goofy challenges that are just as fun to lose as they are to win! Specially designed Pop Cups will launch your balls at high speed as you knock over cup towers, play chaotic games of catch, and compete to become the Top Popper!

Anyone can win this game of high-flying fun so grab your friends and prepare to Pop Off!

Proper Honey is a bright board game for 2-5 people, dedicated to one of the most delicious and healthy products in the world - honey! The game will allow you to become funny bear scientists who have set themselves an excellent goal: to prepare the best kind of honey and prepare for the winter. Players will plunge into the world of dangerous research, where the developments of their neighbors are brazenly used.

How to play?
Each player receives a swarm card, as well as a certain number of game cards and honey tokens, which are indicated on that swarm card.

Each round, all players secretly select a card from their hand and then reveal them together. The effect of cards often depends on what cards neighbors have played. Therefore, players have to calculate not only their own choices, but also those of others, and also predict some events.

Card effects are applied in a specific order - first from honey cards, then from exploration cards, and then from swarm cards.

Honey Cards – Allow players to take honey tokens. By fulfilling additional conditions on the card, you can receive additional chips. The conditions are related to the type of cards that your opponents played this turn or you yourself played in the previous one - if they match, you get a bonus
Research Cards – Research cards are applied in ascending order of numbers on the cards. Otherwise the principle remains the same. Research cards allow you to draw cards, but the maximum number of cards in your hand is limited to 9 cards
Swarm Cards – Swarm cards are also played by number. First, players count their honey and experience tokens. The one with the highest total discards the honey tokens and takes a swarm token as a reward. If a player plays a swarm card but fails to obtain a swarm token, he can choose either 1 honey token or 1 card as compensation. The swarm card is always returned back to the player's hand

"Take turns laying down cards and trying to think of a character, famous or not, who fits the description being built up on the table. Be aware that at some point, you'll have to justify yourself. The goal is to get rid of all your cards." - Unofficial translation of the French description by the publishers

Each player starts with a hand of 6 descriptive cards, each including an adjective or a clause that may qualify someone (e.g. "American", "is less than 40 years old", "is a Céline Dion fan"...). The first player to run out of cards is the winner.
At the beginning of a round, a descriptive card is drawn face up. Then, each player at their turn must complete the face up card(s) with a single card from their hand, so that the description of someone made of all cards at play builds up on the table. When playing a card, each player should think of a character/person that would fit the overall description (including their own added card). They should keep the identity of this character/person secret. Special action cards may also be played, for example to remove some cards played earlier. If they do not wish to play a card from their hand, they may draw cards until they find one to play. After playing a card, their turn ends and the player on their left is next.
Players continue to play cards on their turn until a player confronts the last person who played a card by saying "I don't know who that is". Then, the person being challenged must reveal the identity of the character/person they were thinking of (or otherwise reveal that they were bluffing!). The challenged player must argue why this character/person suits the description laid out by all the cards at play. If the majority of the players are convinced that the person/character fits the description, or if the explanation of the challenged played makes the challenging player laugh, then the challenged player wins the confrontation (or otherwise the challenging player wins it). The player who lost the confrontation draws new cards to add to their hand, as many as the number of cards played this round divided by 2 (and rounded up).
After resolving a confrontation, a new round begins, starting with the player on the left of the last person who played a card. The game stops whenever a player gets rid of all their cards and thus wins.

The game consists of rules, several maps, and two sets of counters that need tom be attached to cardstock and cut out.

You've been chosen to work at the World's Best Candy Company. Get ready to battle it out and climb the ranks to become the Confectioner in Chief. Do you have what it takes? Use your candy-sorting skills (and a lot of luck) to outsmart the other players and win the game.

Object of the game: Win each round by turning 10 candy cards (in numerical order) FACE UP before all other players. After each round, a player receives a promotion (Peppermint President, Gumdrop Director, Sweet Supervisor, and Confectioner in Chief). Beware! The crazy cards will dash your dreams and stop you from winning.

TRASH CARDS: Your turn is over. Sad day!

GLITTER GUMDROP: This is the most beautiful card ever. Replace any card in your sequence. Yum!


    For 2-4 players Ages 4+

    Fantastic idea for birthdays, holidays, and vacation/travel
    Easy to learn and fun for everyone of all ages

—description from the publisher

Sancho & Panza - The load bearers

Choose an adjacent figure to be "piggybacked". Piggyback means you place the chosen figure on top of the load bearer The load bearer takes the piggyback figure with him.

At the end of the bearer's movement, the piggyback figure is placed adjacent again. Then flip the load bearer over to the empty side. This now counts as a normal figure with a -1 effect. The player has -1 movement points for the next turn and in this phase the load bearer moves like a figure with no special effect, except for the -1 effect.

You flip the load bearer every time the figure moves, even if it was moved by the effects of other figures, and even if the load bearer is turned to the -1 side.

Examples:

1. Load bearer takes a figure piggyback, turn the figure over to -1 afterwards.

2. As a -1 marker, the figure moved, then turn the figure over to the load bearer side 3, Loader has been moved or shifted by the effect of another figure,

then flip the figure

description:
This set of 4 promo cards that were initially only given out to guests of Jason Hill's wedding. This includes:

Elegant Top Hot: Gear. Male Heroes Only. Whenever a Depth Event is rolled while holding back the darkness, you may recover a grit.
