boardgame: Romance of the Three Kingdoms: Complete Edition
description:
Romance of the Three Kingdoms is a board game that recreates the world of "Romance of the Three Kingdoms" novel, which unfolds on a grand scale. The player becomes one of the heroes and aims to expand their power with the ultimate goal of unifying the country.

The game begins with the "Peach Orchard Oath" event. This oath happens at the beginning of the "Romance of the Three Kingdoms" novel, where Liu Bei, Guan Yu and Zhang Fei exchange vows of brotherhood. Similarly, players choose their alter ego, the ruler, and their first vassal from over 200 characters. Characters have abilities such as valor, strategy, and governance. Military commanders can be relied on in battle, and civil servants play an active role in domestic politics. Since there is a limit to what a monarch can do alone, it is necessary to recruit human resources and make use of them in order to expand the country. This utilization of human resources is a major feature.

In this game, single combat is positioned as a prelude to group battles, and plays a very important role. Strategy is reproduced with 124 cards. There are many military strategies and tactics available, such as the ``fire plan and ``ten-sided burying plan, and even if you are inferior in military strength, it is possible to overturn the tide of the battle by using these skills. However, the success of a strategy depends on the character's abilities.

Various incidents of the "Romance of the Three Kingdoms" are simulated, including major ones such the Dengoku Seal and the Northern Yi Invasion, but also the death of a military commander and assassination. There are not only sudden misfortunes, but sometimes a unique opportunity to unify China.

This complete edition is a reissue of the 1986 game. It also includes the contents of expansion kits released later.

The previous version of the basic set included 147 military commanders, approximately 100 destiny cards, and 4 scenarios. With the expansion, 79 new military commander units, 25 destiny cards, and 3 scenarios have been added. In the new version, you can play with all of these additional elements.

The expansion also added many detailed rules on battles, judgment methods, movement, alliances, etc. The most significant change was the combat aspect, with balance adjustments made so that the role of military officers has a greater impact on battles. However, it was difficult to play with both basic and additional rules.

The new board incorporates these new rules into the basic rules, making the overall outlook better. We have also updated/added examples to help you understand the rules.

Of course, the component graphics have also been updated to make them easier to use.

Other changes:

     Changes in the monarch abilities records: in the old version, the monarch's abilities were recorded on memo paper,  in the new one, markers are used.
      Separate volumes: the old version included scenarios in the rule book, the new version has been divided into a rule and a scenario book.
      Scenario updates: with the increase of the number of military commanders in the expansion, military commanders who were supposed to be already dead in the basic set, appeared (such as Dong Zhuo invading the Battle of Guandu). Therefore, in the new edition, we have updated the basic set scenario by adding information on additional officers.


—description from the publisher (translated and adapted)
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Agents of Sherlock Holmes is a solo-only worker placement roll-and-write game where you partner with Sherlock Holmes to solve two cases of varying difficulty. Over 15 days, you strategically place agents around London, collecting items and actions to meet Holmes’ requirements for solving the cases.

This game introduces an innovative mechanic in the roll-and-write genre, combining worker placement with hidden movement for a deeply immersive and challenging experience. The pathfinding mechanic sets Agents of Sherlock Holmes apart from traditional worker placement games, as it requires not only gathering resources but also ensuring their safe return to Baker Street.

With 14 mission cards offering more than 90 potential combinations, the game provides high replayability. Each playthrough generates unique narrative moments, ensuring no two games are alike. Additionally, with a playtime of just 15 minutes (including setup and cleanup), it’s easy to fit in a game or even a pair of them in a short period.

16 lucky coins token

With the lucky coins, you get even more possibilities to discard and draw new cards during the Deck Phase to hopfully get the card you need!

-description from editor

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Hobby Next Promo Cards
description:
Two promo cards for Forest Shuffle: Moor Birch (P001), and Whinchat/Fire Salamander (P002).

The Moor Birch is similar to the other Birch tree cards: you draw 1 card when you play it, and it scores you 1 point.

The Whinchat makes you draw 1 card when you play it, and it scores you 1 point for each plant in your forest.

The Fire Salamander is a fourth copy of this animal, with new art. You may play a pawed animal as its bonus, and it scores you 5, 15, or 25 points depending on the number of Fire Salamanders in your forest.

The Maniac is a hidden movement horror board game in which you will have to survive the murderous rampage of a psychopath, but you could also be the murderous Maniac! A group of college students decide to reopen the old Black Pine Lodge in the mountains of Crystal Country. A place closed several times due to various misfortunes that have occurred over the years, but as soon as they arrive they realize that the place actually seems contaminated by something ancient and evil. They decide to explore the place, but it will only be when one of them finds a cursed mask that the real nightmare will begin!

In The Maniac at the beginning of the game everyone is a "Survivor" but the real game begins when one of them finds the cursed mask. From that moment on he will become "The Maniac" and will have the goal of eliminating everyone else. The Survivors instead will have to try to escape from Black Pine Lodge by activating one or more endings. The Maniac is a hidden movement game where no one will ever know exactly where the other is.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Maleficium: Fall of the Witch Hunters
description:
"Maleficium" is a strategy game where you play as a leader in the fight against the 17th century English witch hunts. A person who through reason saw the wrong doings, the maleficium, in what the witch hunters pursued. A person dedicated to bringing this horrible practice to an end and making the witch hunters fall from grace.

You play as one of the persons that saw beyond the hysteria in the society concerning witchcraft. By promoting Enlightened thought, Religious tolerance and Legal skepticism you will succeed and eventually your work will lead to changes in the law. You do this by gathering evidence, confronting witch hunters, dispelling hysteria, and championing justice, securing your legacy as the one that ended the witch hunts.

Buy cards from the market to acquire allies and evidence. The allies in your tableau give you actions and influence. Position yourself on the map to take actions and challenge witch hunters. Calm hysteria and thwart your opponent’s plans.

The game can be played competitively or as a solo game.

—description from the designer

Solitaire TacOps is a campaign system that simulates the battlefield space where operational decisions meet tactical realities. For Ortona, you start with four infantry battalions, one armored regiment, an artillery battery, and an engineer company. These are represented on counters ranging from platoons to squads, with tank groups and artillery guns. Players will make operational decisions such as when and where to engage the enemy, which forces to use, and the type of support to provide them with. The scenarios are then played on hex maps that use a megahex activation system to manage the opposition.

An innovative dice system handles both player and opposition actions, providing a wide range of outcomes with fast resolution. This allows you to keep your focus on the map action and the narrative that emerges from play. The scenarios, which last from 30 to 90 minutes, will pull you in as you attempt to achieve objectives with the fewest losses. Each scenario can be played solitaire, with cooperative setups also available for some of the larger ones.

While the scenarios can be played as one-offs, the TacOps system comes alive over the course of the full campaign. The outcome from one scenario will determine which one is played next, all leading to the final engagement in the streets of Ortona, where the Canadian 1st Division will find the well-prepared German defenses of the Fallschirmjäger Division. Destroyed buildings, rubble piles, mines, snipers, and other traps will challenge the tactical and operational skills players will need to survive “bloody December” in Ortona.

Components:
    Three double-sided 11" x 17" Maps
    One Dashboard Display
    Two Countersheets of counters and markers
    Dice
    12 Scenario Cards
    One Campaign Tracking Pad
    One Rulebook with Historical Notes
    One Learn to Play Booklet

-description from designer

description:
Monster Hunt is a set of miniatures war games rules for running games based on Monster Movies.  Films like; Godzilla, The Blob, Them, etc.

The scale used in developing the rules was 20mm, but any scales miniatures can be used.

There are two types elements involved; The Monsters and the Army.
The number of Monster involved can vary from a few Dinosaurs to swarms of Ants and spiders.  The Army have Infantry, Vehicle, Tanks, Artillery, Mortars and Machine Guns.

The game turn is very simple as follows;
1) Monsters Move and Attacks.
2) Squads Check Morale.
3) Squads Move and Attacks.
4) Start New Turn.

The rules are designed to be quick play and simple.

First published in the Ragnarok Fanzine in 2004.
The second edition of the rules; Ant Attack is available from the files section.

-description from designer

As a gardener, you must wisely relocate beautiful flowers to exchange them for coins, which will allow you to build houses, tractors, and tools, or trade your flowers for points, which will determine the winner of the game. Play over the course of approximately 40 minutes simultaneously with your companions by rolling three dice, which will affect everyone and determine the column where you can choose between plants, terrain, mowing the lawn, placing bees to follow the flower path, or preparing the cactus to attack your opponents. After making your selection, you can exchange the flowers for the two limited options in your inventory. Finally, make your purchases. In the course of 5 rounds, the player with the most garden points will be the winner.

-description from designer

You’re not going to become a legendary 1. Ordered listspace pirate overnight!  You’ll1. Ordered list have to equip your ship…hire a talented crew…take whatever jobs you can get…and maybe even commit a little PIRACY.  The ArchPiratus Core Set includes everything you need for two players to start their journeys towards INFAMY!

Jobs: Jobs are how you win. Take easy jobs, like salvaging a wreck, or hard jobs, like a high-speed heist. Do enough jobs, and you’ll get a chance to rule the entire sector.

Tech: Your starting ship isn’t much to look at. But buy and equip the right engine, rooms, armor, computer, and weapons, and you will be a force to be reckoned with.

Crew and Titles: You won’t get far without a talented crew. Pilots, navigators, commanding officers, security officers, and mechanics will help you get the jobs.

Piracy: Every pirate has to have an ace (or three) up their sleeves. Piracy cards will give you an edge, allowing you to perform abilities that either help you or hurt your opponents.

What are the other features of the game?

Our signature two-deck store: When refilling the store, choose the Crew Deck or the Tech Deck.
Multi-use cards: Many cards can be used for multiple purposes, and the strategy is up to you!
High-quality materials: UV coating and linen texture on the cards, with high-resolution printing on cards and packaging.

Welcome to the dangerous world of ArchPiratus!

—description from designer
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Add a whole new level of ArchPiratus gameplay with the Admiralty expansion!  Admirals are legendary leaders, granting your ship and crew global bonuses!

Core Set or Complete Set of the ArchPiratus game required to play

Add a whole new level of ArchPiratus gameplay with the Cosmic Events expansion!  Events can pop up at any time, creating unpredictable challenges and strategic opportunities!

Core Set or Complete Set of the ArchPiratus game required to play

Add a whole new level of ArchPiratus gameplay with the First Contact expansion!  Add all new alien crew members and technology to your ship...and visit the alien space station to contract exciting (and strange) new missions!

Core Set or Complete Set of the ArchPiratus game required to play

Add up to two more players to your ArchPiratus gameplay with the Four Player expansion!  Everything you need to expand up to four players:  More cards, playmats, and dice!

Core Set or Complete Set of the ArchPiratus game required to play

boardgame: Ancient Blood: The Order of Vampire Hunters – Follower Weapons Pack
description:
Contains 8 new weapon cards: Elynsor, Byrzor, Zhyrvalk, Tyrvyn, Lyteiron, Yrsana, Rythil, Mael.

Free pledge add-on for followers of the Ancient Blood crowdfunding campaigns.

boardgame: Ancient Blood: The Order of Vampire Hunters – Black Rose Wars Rebirth Crossover
description:
Landolfo II, Prince of Capula (crossover from Black Rose Wars Rebirth) is considered a special Creature and comes with his unique encounter. Due to his nature, you might have to face him at the peak of his power, accompanied by powerful vampires, or at his weakest. You will have to choose whether to run for your life or destroy his incarnation, severing his link to the magic of the Black Rose.

Expansion contains:


     1 miniature
     1 ID card
     6 Path cards

ArchPiratus: Complete Set includes the base game plus the four player add-on, the admiralty add-on, the four-player add-on, the cosmic events add-on, and the first contact add-on all in one box!

You’re not going to become a legendary space pirate overnight!  You’ll have to equip your ship…hire a talented crew…take whatever jobs you can get…and maybe even commit a little PIRACY.  The ArchPiratus Complete Set includes everything you need for two to four players to start their journeys towards INFAMY!  Includes ALL FOUR ADD-ONS:  Admiralty, Four Player, Cosmic Events, and First Contact!

Jobs: Jobs are how you win. Take easy jobs, like salvaging a wreck, or hard jobs, like a high-speed heist. Do enough jobs, and you’ll get a chance to rule the entire sector.

Tech: Your starting ship isn’t much to look at. But buy and equip the right engine, rooms, armor, computer, and weapons, and you will be a force to be reckoned with.

Crew and Titles: You won’t get far without a talented crew. Pilots, navigators, commanding officers, security officers, and mechanics will help you get the jobs.

Piracy: Every pirate has to have an ace (or three) up their sleeves. Piracy cards will give you an edge, allowing you to perform abilities that either help you or hurt your opponents.

What are the other features of the game?

Our signature two-deck store: When refilling the store, choose the Crew Deck or the Tech Deck.
Multi-use cards: Many cards can be used for multiple purposes, and the strategy is up to you!
High-quality materials: UV coating and linen texture on the cards, with high-resolution printing on cards and packaging.

Welcome to the dangerous world of ArchPiratus!

A hospital has experienced a parasitic zombie outbreak. Play as one of three survivors and attempt to defeat a zombie boss and escape the hospital.

The player takes their turn by choosing between one of two exploration card and resolving the card. The card may be a zombie, scenario, or room. Once the card is resolved, the players turn ends. The player will roll 2d6 to take the bosses turn, the boss may miss, attack, or use their special ability. When a boss attacks you they have a chance to give you decay. When decay and health levels meet the player dies.

The game ends when either the player defeats both stages of the boss or when the player dies.

-description from publisher

Gold Rush Mini is a matchbox game from Japan. The designer has revised their earlier game GOLD RUSH into a little game for two players.

The players take turns drawing a coin secretly from the matchbox. The coins have different values on either side, including a crossbones and dynamite.

The player looks at both sides and places the coin with one side face-up. Both players then bid on the coin using their gold bars. The winner keeps the coin but may not examine the hidden side.

At the end of the game, the coins are all flipped and their real value is totalled. The player with the most value in total is the winner.

This mini edition was produced following the 2nd edition of the main game also being sold out. The designer created a 1 day crowdfund, and the mini edition sold out instantly. Demand was so high, the designer started a second 5 day crowdfund for the 2nd edition of the mini game. These mini editions re-used the original matchbox artwork, so the 1 day edition has a cattle skull on the matchbox, and the 5 day edition has a native American skull.
user summary

Lupercalia is a matchbox game from Japan, for two co-operative players. The designer is hoping that it will bring the players closer to a better friendship.

Lupercalia is a Roman festival for cleansing and purging, held from February 13th to 15th. In this game, it stands in for Valentine's day, and the them is bringing male and female couples together.

The game has a small deck of cards in male/female pairs, 1 blue pair, 2 red and 3 yellow pairs. The players draw cards and work together to try and decide whether they will be matching or not. The probability in the game means that by the end, players should have a better chance of creating pairs successfully.

The designer says that should the game not bring you closer to your friedn, you should say 'one more time!' and play again.
user summary

This adds an additional explore card of the type Ship Upgrade to the game called "Beaming." It allows you to collect 1 when your closest opponent drops cargo.

This was handed as a freebie to people testing Drop Drive at Spiel 2023 and later available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

This adds an additional Ship Part card to the game called "Incubator" with two cargo slots.

This was handed as a freebie to people testing Drop Drive at Spiel 2023 and later available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Pizza On Fire is a bid and bluffing matchbox game from Japan. The two players are competing pizza chefs trying to bake the best pizzas first.

Each player has a hand of ingredient cards numbered 1 to 6 and a Burn card. Three pizza chips are stacked up, valued 11, 13 and 15.

The players take turn placing ingredient cards face-down, trying to reach the exact target value of the top pizza. However, if they get the total exact, they must wait until their next turn to play their Burn card and claim the pizza.

Instead of placing a card, a player can instead challenge the opponent to reveal their total. If it matches the top pizza exactly, the challenger wins the chip. If not, the other player wins the chip.

So players are trying to bluff each other into challenging wrongly, or to not challenge and let them bake the pizza.

The first player to win two of the three pizzas is the winner of the game.
user summary

Coral Reefs is an expansion to the base game Aquatica that introduces the Southern Tribes, more underwater creatures, new characters, and Manta Ray encounters that can turn the rules of the game upside down!

In Aquatica, an engine-building strategy board game, players compete to become the most powerful Sea King. They will play cards from their hands, activate powerful combos, capture locations, recruit creatures, and more—everything to gain the most Victory Points by the end of the game.

Coral Reefs perfectly complements the base game and its other Cold Waters expansion. It comes with several new modules with additional goals and mechanics, along with new components like coral reef miniatures and transparent reef cards.

Together with its expansions, Aquatica is a fascinating strategy to play with family or friends! The game takes around 50 minutes to play and is suitable for 1-5 players aged 12+.

—description from the publisher

Prepare yourselves, fellow Spymasters, for a thrilling journey into the fun and exciting world of espionage with 'Bridge of Spies,' a new upcoming code cracking bluffing game for 2 to 6 players, designed by Johann Chipol.

Drawing inspiration from the classic spy films of the 1960s, the game envelops you in its world with captivating Saul Bass-style artwork, casting you as a covert agent on a thrilling mission. The game is designed and tested to be easy to learn but also be delightfully complex with strategic thinking and social deduction.

Your aim is to correctly decipher a concealed 6-symbol combination placed on the world map, earning valuable intel points as you progress. Using currency cards and special agent cards, you can take actions and prepare to guess the code! Fellow players can engage in negotiations with you, to either offering help or bluff their way to the top! Are you a trustworthy person or a deceptive rogue?

—description from Designer, Johann Chipol

10 BILLION POINT is a push-your-luck matchbox game for two players, produced to support the opening of the game cafe in Tokyo named [10 Billion Point https://10billionpoint.com/].

The game has a deck of 13 small cards, six showing 0, three 00, and one showing 000, and one 1, and three blanks.

The two players take turns placing cards face-up and face-down, until a player thinks that the number 10,000,000,000 is formed. They shout out 10 BILLION! and must turn up one card to create the number. If they are correct they win. If not, the other player wins.

The game was produced through a crowdfund, but can be bought at the game cafe as a souvenir of a happy visit.
user summary

HexDragon a mind-tangling puzzle game where the longest dragon usually wins!

HexDragon is a hexagonal tile-based strategy game about building dragons for 1 - 4 players.

Utilising a very well known connection mechanic, we set out to create a strategy game for everyone. Adding the star token as a unique twist + a handful of clever tweaks to make it an awesome experience.

Rules and Features
Game starts with a 6-tailed piece in the center.
Everyone gets 3 "head" pieces and 3 "body" pieces and a star token. Body pieces are refilled to players as the play, but heads are not!
Players take turns putting 1 of their pieces anywhere on the board, or using the star token to reserve a place for some of their later turns.
Points are awarded for connections of any kind - be it tail-head, head-head, tail-tail or a loop.
A few key features make HexDragon way more fun than what you see at first glance.

—description from the designer

Wolf and seven goats is a reverse werewolf matchbox game from Japan. The game is based upon the classic folk tale of The Wolf and the Seven Young Goats, in which a wolf impersonates the Mother goat and gains entry to the goats' home to consume the youngsters.

In this game, the Mother goat is trying to identify which are her children, to allow them back indoors, but to keep out any wolves. However, if a wolf is identified, the wolves win the game and the goats lose. Only by letting only all the goats in do the goats win.

The game is one of the matchbox series of games, and has a set of cards for seven goats (three of which are special), and two wolves. The players have voting tokens. As the game was produced through a crowdfund campaign in Japan, additional cards may have been added at production, such as a goat card based upon the designer's prior game Amabie.
user summary

If You Know You Know! The new IYKYK party game from Spin Master Games is here to put you to the challenge: How well do you know your friends? Your coworkers? Your family? Your significant other? The questions are hilarious, and you are sure to die laughing at the adults-only NSFW card deck. But be careful- these are for adults 18 and up only- these questions get wild!

Getting started and playing this card game is super simple. A rotating judge asks the group a question about themselves from each of the three levels. Then, everyone chooses their answer. If you guess the judge's answer correct, you get a point. Whoever has the most points at the end wins! The funny questions span multiple topics, ranging from pop culture to food habits. Such as: Do I prefer my wings with a side of ranch or blue cheese? If I have to get somewhere, I’m using… Google maps, Apple maps, or Waze? My favorite Chris is… Evans, Hemsworth, Pratt, or Rock?

This adult game for 2-6 players is a fun time for couples games date night, relationship gifts, or just a casual Friday night of family board games. It's also perfect for relationship gifts, best friends game, or games for couples. Prepare for a night of laughs with this new board game based on the viral IYKYK internet trend.

114 BCE – 1450: Silk Road, is a 18xx-inspired economic game. You will play as traders of 1 of 5 different goods : silk, spices, tea, ivory, cotton, opening your own firms and trade on the silk road.

After 15 rounds (each round representing 100 years) or when somebody goes bankrupt, the game ends. The winner is the one with the highest capital (liquidate all stock before the end game) (excluding the one that went bankrupt).

In a round player take 2 actions of the following action:

Build/upgrade route 
Selling goods
Deciding goods
Investing
Opening a firm
Signing contract

Additionally, player could do as many free action they could:

Taking loans (max3)
Closing a firm
 Nationalized a firm
 Merge firms
 Buying shipment

-description from designer

Choose the zombie apocalypse your camps face when playing ZombiE Survival Infection. 
This expansion adds 6 different Zombie Strains for your camps to fight. Each strain has unique abilities, that modulate gameplay and help those killed off early exact some vengeance.

En Desierto Florido, 2 o 3 jugadores intentarán formar el grupo de flores más grande de su color para dominar el desierto y ser los ganadores de la partida.
Para esto, expandirán el desierto florido jugando las cartas de su mano, cubriendo una o dos flores presentes en la mesa. Las fichas de insecto servirán para alterar el curso de la partida. 
Cuando las cartas se acaben, el grupo más grande de flores conectadas ortogonalmente determinará al ganador.

In Desert Bloom, 2 or 3 players will try to make the biggest group of flowers of their designated color, to dominate the desert and be the winners of the game.
To achieve this, they will play cards from their hands, layering them with the ones already in play, covering one or two flowers in the board, and playing insect tokens to alter the flow of the game. When there are no more cards to play, the biggest group of orthogonally connected flowers will determine the winner.

Welcome to Would Be Aces, a solitaire wargame where you command a flight of P-47 Thunderbolts on missions to destroy various enemy ground forces. As your pilots fly their planes across the Western European landscape they will encounter enemy ground forces, enemy aircraft, obstacles, good fortune and crippling bad luck at every turn as you try to reach the objective.

The main mechanic of the game centres around the confidence level of the pilot, with a push your luck mechanic as they progress towards their objective plus the amazing resilience and toughness of one of the greatest aircraft of WW2 - the P47 Thunderbolt.

The game takes no longer than 1 hour to setup and play and has a low complexity streamlined design.

This is a print and play game with the PDFs containing all the rules, scenarios, maps, player aids and counters required to play. You'll need to print them out, mount the map and counters to card. We have kept the maps and counters to a few sheets (of either Letter or A4 size) so it is very inexpensive and quick to print out and get to the table.

The game comes complete with:
Rulebook
Tables Book
Sortie Record Sheet
Plane Sheets
Map Sheets
Counter Sheets

-description from designer

boardgame: Sky Team: CDG Paris-Charles de Gaulle
description:
An additional airport for Sky Team : Paris-Charles de Gaulle

Contain one postcard with two additionals missions (Yellow and red)

Note: Available as a Print and Play

boardgame: Marvel Studios: The Infinity Saga – Capture: The Ultimate Battle Game
description:
From Marvel: Avengers The Infinity Saga comes Capture: The Ultimate Battle Game, a game for 2-5 players, aimed at ages 8+ and takes approx 20-30min to complete.

To claim victory, use Marvel's most powerful heroes and formidable villains to CAPTURE the Infinity Stones and avoid other players' brutal tactics!
    
Strategic, frenetic and highly interactive, CAPTURE features 30 Marvel heroes and villains and features official character art (ie actors' likenesses) from the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

HOW TO PLAY HEADLINES:
Players are each dealt 8 cards and on their turn can do as many actions as they choose.  There are 4 action categories:


     Play a pair of the same coloured cards to CAPTURE an available stone (on the board)
     Play 3 of the same coloured cards to attempt to CAPTURE that coloured stone from another player
     Play a pair of GAUNTLET cards to CAPTURE up to 2 available stones 
     Play a VILLAIN card on another player.  All 6 Villains have a different impact and can be defended by a different HERO.


Once players have completed their actions they can then choose to discard additional cards, drawing back up 8 cards to finish their turn.  But watch out for rare CHILDREN OF THANOS cards when replenishing!  If players pick up one of these, they must immediately spin the game board and observe the impact- which could result in placing a stone back on the board!

Capture features basic set collection mechanics, many risk-reward trade offs, a slight vindictive edge and has received plentiful positive feedback in our 4 years of playtesting with the Australian gaming community!

—description from the designer

Who will be the quickest to find the right sea creature with all their dice?

At the beginning, each player has a dice cup and three dice. All the dice have the same motifs printed on them: dice fish, starfish, crab, seahorse, octopus and a submarine. Each player places their three dice in front of them so that the submarine is facing upwards. Another die is now rolled and placed in the middle of the table. This one shows a starfish, for example. Now all the players call out together "3...2...1... Dive!" and the action starts.
The first thing each player tries to do is to roll the three dice to get the animal from the centre of the table, but only one dice may be in the cup at a time. So the dice are rolled one by one! Once someone has hit the animal they are looking for with all of their dice, they shout out loud "Show up!" and everyone finishes their roll. The calling player then gets the die from the centre of the table and adds it to his own. If a player still has submarines in front of him, he must give up one cube. The first player to have five or six dice wins.

The dice game for children and families "Würfelfisch" is suitable for 2-5 players aged 5 and up. It takes about 15 minutes and is by the author Christoph Cantzler.

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

description:
Promo card given out at Essen Spiel 2023

4 points - own 5 scrolls
6 points - Have 7-8 wizards out on the board
8 points - reach at least the 4the silver space on the wand track

patisserie 3CARD is a two player card game from Japan using only 7 cards. The designer had published Cabaret 4 Card, in which players have only 4 cards each, but then thought if it could be minimised further.

The game was designed for a French patisserie in Kobe, where the chef wanted customers to play card games in the cafe.

In patisserie 3CARD, the two players have only three cards each. There is one start card which goes in the middle between the players. The 6 cards are shuffled and three dealt to each player.

The cards have simple logic instructions (Place, Do Not Place, Place Last One) and the players take turns placing one card to the left or right side of the middle card, or taking a card back. When placing, cards can be placed on top of another card, staggered to show the card below.

By placing the cards, the three cards form a sentence in Japanese. This sentence controls how the other player may proceed.

The first player to shed their three cards wins.
user summary

The designer says:
When you place a card, it becomes a sentence. It is a sentence like this: "Place a card called "Place"", "Do not place a card called "Place"", or "Place the last card called "Last card"".
The cards in your hand are played according to the sentence. If the sentence is not followed, the card in the field is added to your hand. The sentence structure is different between Japanese and English, so it is difficult to translate the sentence into English.
publishers summary

FARMER vs FARMER is a two player logical game from Japan. It takes the old puzzle of the farmer trying to cross a river with a sheep, a cabbage, and a wolf, and turns it into a two player game. Both players are moving their pieces back and forth across the river. 
user summary

Roll and write game where you take on the role of an engineer. Your spaceship has crashed due to malfunctions; make the necessary repairs within an estimated time of 10 minutes. It's a simultaneous game where all players use the same result of the dice, rolling 4 dice, with 3 to place in any of the 6 game areas while following specific placement rules for each area, such as ascending even numbers, repeating the number 5, among other conditions. After this, use the 4th differently colored die, which indicates an explosion occurs, damaging an area in one of the game zones, thus disrupting your repair strategy. At the end of the specified time, each player adds up their score in each area, and the one with the most repair points to escape the planet will be the winner.

-description from designer

Freedom Palestine is a game played with a single, two sided card.

On one side are paths to the Al-Aqsa Mosque (Dome of the Rock) which players race along to become the first player to the mosque.  On the back are a set of letters.  Players choose their path by randomly selecting a letter from the card back with their eyes closed. If you land on nothing, you lose a heart.  Lose 3 hearts and you're out of the game.

First player to reach the mosque (or the last player with hearts) wins the game.

Have fun at the school festival !

Join the exciting school fair and take part in the grand mission, with students acting as little merchants selling premium stationary, from pricing, store layout, to marketing! Who will take the no.1 winning spot at the school fair? Time to give it all you’ve got at My Little First Sale!

Before starting the game every player receives their own player board which is their shop, player screen and item cards.  Open the customer cards and place them in a row on the table.  They are students who come to buy things in each player's shop.

Every player places an item card on their player board.  He can set the selling price and the attractiveness of the items sold by placing ranking cards. The Player screen will help keep the placement of cards secret.

When everyone is ready we will remove the player screen and start selling.  The customers lined up on the table go one by one to buy the items they want.  The customer will see which store sells the item he or she wants at the cheapest price.  If there are two shops with the same price, The customer will look at the attractiveness of the item.

The game is played over 4 rounds and the player who earns the most money from sales wins the game.

-description from graphic designer

Epic fantasy strategy, reimagined for the kitchen table. Choose your faction and prepare for war.

Otherworld is a 2-4 player grand strategy game about two factions vying for control over a new land. Inspired by classic Real Time Strategy games such as Warcraft III and big map games like Crusader Rex, Otherworld aims to be a "4X-Lite." Simply put: it's the epic scope without the epic time commitment.

The factions of Otherworld are led by powerful heroes, each with their own strengths, weaknesses, and goals. Heroes are able to gather magic items and level up as they explore the game map. They are the key to victory; the stronger your hero is, the stronger you are!

True to its RTS inspiration, both factions of Otherworld play differently. The human Free Guild Merchants want nothing more than to build a thriving series of settlements and reap the rewards, while the Orc Clans seek out the thrill of battle wherever they go.

The orcs and humans each have their own unique army units, structures, special abilities, and economies, so a game of Otherworld changes as you switch sides!

—description from the publisher

Each player gets a Shashibo Cube. A card that shows a Shashibo shape is drawn from a deck of a chosen level of difficulty. Players manipulate their cubes to try to be the first to complete the shape that is shown on the card. Play through ten cards.

boardgame: Wandering Towers: Close The Raven Castle Promo Card
description:
Originally available as a free promo at Spiel 2023 from the AbacusSpiele booth. Also included in Wandering Towers: Mini Spell Expansion 2.

Close The Raven Castle
Effect: If you use this magic spell, you may close the Raven Castle, so that nobody can move into the Raven Castle for the moment. To do this, place this tile on top of the Raven Castle with the back side facing up. For now, no movement may be made that would land a tower or a wizard on the Raven Castle. When it is your turn again, place the tile back in the middle of the table with the front side facing up. The Raven Castle is now open again.

The tokens are distributed evenly among the players. At the start of the game each player places a token. Each player has one throw. Depending on, what the dice roll shows, he has to pay tokens to the bank or receives them from the bank.
Play passes from hand to hand until one player throws “Bank”. This player receives the entire contents of the bank. The game round is now over and a new round can begin. Generally, a game is over when as many rounds have been played as there are players participating. The tokens are settled by having the losers pay the difference to the winners.

—user summary

boardgame: Forgotten Victory: The East African Campaign 1940-41
description:
Forgotten Victory lets you wargame the fighting in Eretria and Ethiopia, an often-overlooked campaign in which the British conquered the Italian colonial empire.

It covers the Italian invasion of Eritrea in August 1940 to the final British conquest of Ethiopia in November 1941.  
The campaign is covered in 10 action packed scenarios featuring the fighting throughout the area.

These scenarios are for use with the Fireball Forward rules system. However, they can be adapted to any company level system in which the basic maneuver unit is a squad. There are a maximum of two companies per side with supporting armor.
  
All scenarios have been thoroughly researched and extensively play tested.  
Two especially interesting scenarios are Crocodiles and Tanks and Tribesmen.  
The first highlights a Belgian Police Battalion that invaded south western Ethiopia and was confronted by Italian irregular tribesmen.  
In the second, the British employed Ethiopian tribesmen supported by South African armor in one of the final battles of the campaign.   
Also included is a 12-page historical monograph.

This is not a stand-alone game system.  
You need the Fireball Forward! rules.

-Description from the publisher

Fantasy Gods compete for supremecy in a new land

Bring Faith to a Secluded Island that has never seen religion!
Based on the mythology of the Archaic Realm Fantasy Roll-Playing Game.

The land is pure, the people untouched, Nearly naked, the poor heathens muddle through their daily lives hunting, fishing and well, making more heathens. These poor fools believe the sun merely comes up on its own and rain just happens! You must put this to an end! Beware, though. You have competitors that arrived the same day you did - undoubtedly to spread their false faiths.

This cannot stand! You must prevail and bring these benighted savages to the light!

Archaic Realm Missionaries is a strategy game where you are the missionary leader of a fantasy deity from the Archaic Realm role-playing game. Your mission is to promote your faith on a secluded island whose inhabitants have never seen religion. Ever. Other missionaries have learned about this island and have arrived at the same time as you and will try to thwart you at every turn. Fortunately, even their believers can be converted or eliminated as need be. Used compassion or force to convert these poor souls to the true path!

Winning

There are three ways to end the game. 1) Player eliminated (no player pieces of any type left on the board). 2) Natives eliminated or converted (No natives left on the board). 3) A player has all believers played (all 30 of a player's cubes are on the board).

Game Play

1) After choosing a color, a deity, trait cards & event cards all players begin on their start space with a missionary and five believers. 
2)Players (in turn), draw an event card.
3) Select 1 of the two trait cards to play face up.
4) Collect coins from the bank as listed on the deity card.
5) Move your missionaries one space each.
6) Perform deity, trait and event card actions (all are optional) in any order desired.
7) Discard extra event cards (max of three per hand).

boardgame: 潜入軍師対潜入軍師 (Infiltration Tactician vs Infiltration Tactician)
description:
潜入軍師対潜入軍師 (Infiltration Tactician vs Infiltration Tactician) is a card game from Japan for two players. Two nations are at war, and both send their top spy over the border to disrupt the enemy. But each spy succeeds in taking control of the military, and now both agents are trying to make their host armies lose.

In this game, the players use a limited number of cards to direct attacks on each other. But the one player chooses the card to be used the other each turn. The cards with high values have the weakest actions, and the cards with the strongest actions have the lowest values.

When the game ends, the player with the most points on their cards is the winner. So a player wants to gain a high value card, but that will make them vulnerable to attack. But giving their opponent a low value card will cause more damage to themselves.

The designer was inspired by watching a TV show in which rival teams were involved in match-fixing, and both were instructed to lose.

The game has a deck of only nine cards, and in the basic game, a set of seven are used. In the advanced game, all nine are shuffled and seven dealt out, giving a variation each time. There are also two tokens, Defence and Sleep, which stay with the player affected until the next round.

The card actions include Defence, Sleep, Robbery, Assassination, Exchange and Standby. For example, Robbery lets a player take the lowest value card from the opponent, and Assassination lets them turns the lowest card over.
user summary

Goal of the game to roll a "Drunken Stranger" or get a better score than your friends so you can claim you're better at rolling dice they are.

How to play:
1. Roll all the dice
2. Determine if you have bitches a "bitch" is the highest number on any dice usually this is the six but there are a few exceptions the 8 & 10 and 12-sided die as well as the custom
3. Remove dice you want to set aside your highest dice to get the best past possible score. You must set aside at least one die per roll you can set aside as many dice as you want.
4. Reroll the remaining dice and keep setting them aside until you're out of dice.
5. Score add up all the dice The person with the highest score wins.

3climbers is a single player puzzle game from Japan. By using a set of cards, the player must advance three mountain climbers to the top of the board to win.

The players has two decks of cards they shuffle face-down. One deck has the numbers 1-3 three times plus a star as a Joker. The other deck has three Up cards and two Down cards.

The climbers, marked 1,2 and 3, start in the middle of the board and can go two steps to the top or one step down.

The player shuffles each deck separately and draws three numbers, and two directions, face up. Then they choose one number to apply to the climber, and one number to the step, and a direction. So picking a 1 for the climber and a 3 for the step and an Up for direction moves the #1 climber up two steps to the top (the climber does not go off the plan).

The used cards are put aside face up, so the player can work out what remains, and two more numbers and a new direction  are dealt up to choose from.

In tis way, the player is trying to get all three climbers to the top. Anything less and they lose.

Kult is an out-of-print collectible card game by Target Games and Heartbreaker Hobbies. The game is based on a role-playing game of the same name.

It was released in November 1995. The game had one expansion released in July 1997, called Inferno. It added 127 cards to the base set's 262. According to The Duelist magazine, the expansion was announced as having 121 cards and 2 chase cards.

Distinguished Mushling captains arrive to provide a strategic advantage in battle! Place your Captain Mushling meeple on the board to boost your Combat Value when battling nearby. This mini expansion is compatible with all variants.

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Frontier Suppression 銀河群雄伝説 辺境制圧
description:
This is a doujin game produced for sale at Japanese game shows. In order to get one, you needed to order the game in advance and then it could be picked up at the show. These were not available to the general public.

Ginga Gunyu Densetsu Ni: Henkyou Seiatsu or "Frontier Conquest" reproduces the Legend of the Galactic Heroes conflict using the "Sengoku Gunyuden" system.  It is a campaign game with a map that stretches the galaxy.  The game comes with an 8-piece 34"x 45" map, rules, two sheets of counters, and four tables/charts. You need to assemble the map and mount the counters.

While this is an expansion for "Reinhard Joraku," it may be played as a stand-alone game.

Scenarios include “Imperial Invasion” and “Lippstadt,” as well as an introductory scenario "Amritsar."

—user summary
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Deep within the ancient mysteries lies the keys to your destiny. Do you dare to seek your fate within the abyss?

Lock & Spell: Stranger Fates contains sixty new, heart-pounding fortunes to discover. From dark omens and horrible doom, to great riches and true love’s embrace, these alternative destinies are sure to thrill those brave enough to open the chests of fate.

Available as a 13 page paperback booklet or downloadable pdf.

—description from the publisher

This is doujin game (fan produced) that was available only at Japanese game shows. To get one you had to place an order in advance; the game would then be made, and you would pick it up at the show. It was not made for the general public.

This game deals with combat in the world of Fang of The Sun Dougram, an anime show from the early 80's.

—user summary

A tactical-class game with the theme of armored combat with combat armor as the main focus in "Fang of the Sun Dougram".

The basic engine is the "chit pulling system", which selects and executes actions each time a unit chit is pulled. In addition, by judging the initiative based on command ability, it is possible to secure action chit in advance, and by introducing it at any time, it expresses the excellent command of Major Zartsev and others. Combat resolution is a single roll of dice + damage chit with an emphasis on playability.

The game comes with a four-part map, a mounted set of counters, rules and player aid.  You need to cut out the counters and assemble the map.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Factory Inс.: A Smartphone Inc. Game
description:
In Factory Inc., you start the game as the director of a new electronics factory. Your goal is to make your factory prosper by hunting for new contracts, launching product lines, and make space for more and more equipment on your factory floor.

An Inc. series game in the vein of Smartphone Inc., Factory Inс. reinvents action draft and polyomino tile-laying mechanisms, using them with an innovative route-building mechanic in a thematic mix. Unique combinations of contracts, player characters, and optimization cards add a huge replay ability factor to this game.

In Dualities two players (Even and Odd) compete to get the highest score by claiming certain lines on a hexagonal board with five hexes per side.

The game commences with the empty board and players alternate turns placing one domino-style tile (formed by two hexagons of two different colours, black and white) on a free space of 2 adjacent hexes of the board. The game ends when there’s no free space to put a tile.

To get the score players add up the lines they have claimed; Even claims a certain line if the number of black hexagons along this line is even. Similarly, Odd claims a line if the number of black hexagons along this line is odd. White hexagons are neutral. There are two different ways of scoring taking into account: a) only the 9 lines parallel to a certain direction (basic play) or b) all the lines (27) parallel to the 3 different directions in the board (expert play).

—description from the designer

This is a doujin (fan-made) game that was produced for sale only at Japanese game shows.  It was not made for the general public and to get one you had to order in advance.  The game would then be made, and you would pick it up at the show.

This game deals with the Legend of the Galactic Heroes, an anime series from the late 80's to mid 90's.

—user summary

A strategy-class wargame with the theme of battles around volumes 1~5 of The Legend of the Galactic Heroes. As the Commander-in-Chief of the Galactic Empire or the Free Planetary Alliance, each player is responsible for the mobilization of troops, the personnel of Starfleet, and even the actual operation of troops.

The game is a simple reproduction of strategic combat in space with the sequence of move-reaction move-pitched battle-second move.

There are five scenarios as follows.
1. Idol of Heroes (July 796)
An all-out war scenario that begins immediately after Jan's capture of Iserlohn.
2. The Wise Man's Miscalculation (October 796)
It begins when Yang's intentions are derailed, and the Alliance forces invade Imperial territory.
3. Those who walk the path of supremacy (April 797)
Civil war scenario using only the Imperial territory map.
4. Twilight of the Gods (November 798)
Recreation of Reinhardt's masterpiece of war art, Operation Ragnarok.
5. Old Tree (January 796)
A thrilling setting in which the coup d'état of the Lippstadt Campaign and the National Salvation Military Council occurs in an event in the middle of the game.

The game includes a four-part laminated map, rules, two tactical displays, and a sheet of counters.  Assembly s required.

—description from the designer
