description:
Mouse in the House is a scoop them up before they scatter mouse game.  The game board is battery operated and it has a wobbly platform that shakes the game board so the little white and black mice move around frantically.  It is a two player game with each player having a vibrating cheese paddle flat scooper to scoop up your color mouse and put it in the basket in front of you.  The mice have little metal marbles underneath that attract to the black wheels underneath which are magnetic.  That is what moves the mice around the game board.

Game Contents:
1 Game Base
2 Battery Operated Scoopers
2 Holding Baskets
4 Legs
8 Black Mice
8 White Mice
Instructions

Requires 4 "AA" Batteries (not included)

Be the first Explorer to traverse each environment and collect all the Mythic Gems. Be warned, along the way you will need to overcome
hidden perils, maneuver changing pathways and outwit other
explorers attempting to disrupt your strategy and prevent
you from reaching the finish with your Mythic Gems.

Mythic Gems is a strategy based board game that plays differently every time you open the box. It relies on memory and forethought to outwit the other players, but the complexity and length of play is largely driven by the group playing the game. As you place your action tokens on the boards, will you remember what you laid down as the boards start to move or will you unknowingly activate one of your own traps? Work your way through the different paths, collect the Mythic Gems from each environment, and beat your competitors to the finish to win the game.

—description from the publisher

High-Command, a game in which there is no element of chance, is played by two, three, or four players on a game board. This board has a red central section called "Siege Zone", containing 36 squares. At the four sides of the Siege Zone are yellow sections called "Base Areas", each containing 12 squares. The Siege Zone and the four Base Areas together constitute what is called "Combat Territory".

—description from the rulebook

High-Command is a chess-like game for up to four players, either individually or as partners. Each player controls an army consisting of 5 soldiers, 3 fighter planes, 2 tanks, 1 captain, and 1 bomber.

The bomber has special rules for capturing opponent's pieces: if the piece is "watched" by a friendly piece, the bomber may not capture it. A separate rule sheet gives detailed examples.

—user summary

Creepy Crawlers Bug Zapper Game from ToyMax form the year 2000.  There are 3 game modes to play. The Game starts with the roll of the dice and then you collect as many bugs as you can. The game is designed like a real bug zapper with a cage that is electrified like the game Operation. If you touch the outer cover, you get zapped!  The game includes a real looking bug zapper, 20 bug zapper bugs and 1 bug zapper die.

Included in the box

Purple game top
Internal bug column
Game base
3 Frame Sections
20 Bug Zapper Bugs
1 Bug Zapper Die

Requires 4 C Batteries

Sea Dogs from Letters of Marque Campaign.

Includes: 12 new Sea Dogs cards and instructions.

There are 12 Sea Dogs in Sea Dogs II. 2 of those Sea Dogs use content introduced in the Letters of Marque package. If you don't have Letters of Marque, you can use those Sea Dogs but they provide no useable abilities to your Crew so we recommend that you do not.

If you are playing with Letters of Marque, when it is time to distribute Sea Dogs, deal an even number to each player and each player chooses one to use and discards the rest back to the box for future use. In a four player game, After multiple four player games if each player gained three Sea Dogs, all the Sea Dogs from Sea Dogs II would be in play.

If you are not playing with Letters of Marque, remove the Master Gunner, the Quartermaster, and then deal equal numbers of Sea Dogs to each player leaving two unused in a four player game. Each player chooses one to use and discards the rest back to the box. Assuming multiple four player games, each player will eventually have 3 Sea Dogs and there will be 4 left over in the box unused.

With the exception of the Quartermaster and the Master Gunner, all the rest of the Sea Dogs II pack are playable without Letters of Marque.

Money can buy you happiness, at least in the Imperium. Gain an economic boost to Trade and Power in this add-on pack. Make the stringent trade agreements treaties run smoother to ease your way to victory. Includes 16 Action Cards and 1 Faction Panel.

In this final 55-card installment to the Cosmic Power Story Arc, the young geniuses of the Future Foundation give your deck the scientific breakthroughs it needs to take on even the toughest opponents. Join Ant-Man, She-Hulk, Medusa, and more as they use their powers to defeat their enemies and keep their friends out of harm's way.

—description from publisher

The Frightful Foes is the second Issue of the Cosmic Power Story Arc. This 55-card set includes feared Super Villains from the Frightful Foes. This issue will dive deeper into the darker chapters of the Fantastic Four’s history and the people they once called friends who became the Frightful Foes.

—description from publisher

Print and play solitaire tile-laying game played on a 7x7 board with 36 tiles in total.

Starting with only 6 tiles, the player must optimize their play, draw tiles to empty the core while creating the 3 biggest enclosed groups possible.

It is a remake of micropul:

     Solitaire-only
     36 tiles instead of 48
     No dot-plus catalyst
     No big puls
     No tokens needed


With some twists:

     New art
     Bigger tiles
     7 x 7 grid
     Different challenges


Note: picopul is an official reimplementation of micropul.

—description from the designer

みにまむ会議 (Minimamu Kaigi) is a party game from Japan. Players are business people, preparing the agenda for a meeting. They need to convey their agenda to the company president, which is not easy because everyone is competing for attention. Plus there are spies working for competitors who are trying to spoil the meeting by planting false items on the agenda.

Players are given their roles in the business, and receive email cards telling what their topic is to get on the agenda today. Players can only hint to the president what the item is, and can only give one word at a time. The spies are part of this, trying to plant false information too.

At the end of the round, the president must try to confirm what the items are for the agenda. Players score points for succeeding in getting their topics on the agenda, and the winner is the player with the most points at the end.

—user summary

Word Turners Juniors is an adaption of the Word Turners game, aimed at ages 5+. Word Turners Juniors is a Word playing card game with the simple aim, 'who can make the longest word?' Word Turners Juniors is made up of lower case letters so that younger children can join in on.  the fun too! Pack contains 60 cards which comprises 1 instruction card, 52 alphabet cards, 7 choice card which can be any letter you choose! Enjoyable and educational game for family and friends.The game play is for 2 - 6 players who are dealt 7 cards each they then look at their own cards and decide how many cards they would like to swap,they only have 1 chance to swap so they then give the amount of cards they are swapping to the dealer who puts the cards to the bottom of the pack and deals the player back the same number of new cards, once everyone has swapped they then have to look at their cards and try and find the longest word they can! Then when everyone is ready they turn their cards to reveal their words.In case of a draw there are shapes on the cards that equate to points so the person with the most points wins! The game can go on and on so the timescale can be as long as you want as the game is very competitive and you'll have players saying best out of 3, 5, 10 games!!

-description from publisher

トリノス (Bird's Nest) (Trinos) is a card game from Japan. Players are little birds, trying to complete a nest with twigs of one colour.

The game has a deck of 36 cards, numbered 1-36, with each card showing that number of twigs. The twigs are in six colours, so a card shows one or two colours (also written on the card).

The players are dealt a hand of six cards, with a goal of getting all six to include one colour the same. Each player chooses a card and holds it face-down, and then all the chosen cards are revealed.

If all six colours are present, the player who selected the lowest number goes first, otherwise the highest goes first. Going clockwise, each player chooses a card other than the one they played, and places it face-down in front of themselves.

This is repeated, and the previous face-down card is now taken in hand.

The first player to get all six cards, including their face-down card, to have one colour the same, declares and is the winner.
—user summary

What’s that signal?!

Expand your cosmic journey with the "Strange Signals" expansion for Drop Drive. Explore eight unique anomaly packs that redefine gameplay, from an ancient Dropgate network to the completely improbable Whales of Deep Thoughts. Whether you're a seasoned commander or a fresh cadet straight out of space school, you will navigate uncharted challenges and rewrite the rules of space. Embrace the mysteries of the cosmos and lead your crew to galactic victory in this extraordinary expansion!

...it's time to drop deeper into infinite space!

Strange Signals is an epic expansion for Drop Drive, featuring 8 immersive anomaly packs that will create unusual and exciting story lines. Anomaly packs represent game-altering events, each including premium game content and unique components. Some anomalies are nods to well-loved sci-fi tropes, while others are machinations of our own design. Many of the anomalies in this expansion were created in collaboration with our amazing Kickstarter community!

Prepare to bend infinite space... and explore new dimensions! This expansion includes:


     Dropgate
     Belter’s Bazaar
     The Last Element
     Zest Planet
     The Whale of Deep Thoughts
     Pirate Port
     Maelstrom
     Furious Furballs

Investment tycoons is a simple financially educative game that resembles real life events and realistic market fluctuations.

In this game, players assume the role of investors who are buying and selling stocks of Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs), which, in return, invest the collected money into 15 different sectors/industries. For example, one Exchange-Traded Fund can invest in: Energy, Manufacturing, and Retail industries, while another ETF can invest in: Financial services, Media/Gaming, and Retail.

Each turn one card will be drawn for all players. Event card will resemble some famous real-life event (for example: Covid 19 pandemic, Suez canal obstruction or GameStop short squeeze). On the flip side of the card, event effects are listed. The event effects may increase or decrease values of entire industries in the range of -3 to +3. Before the event effects are revealed, players will have a chance to buy and sell stocks of available ETFs. The starting value of all ETF stocks is 10.

After all players have done all their desired transactions, the event effect is revealed. Upon reveal, adjust the values of all ETF stocks. Some may increase and some may decrease their value. After that, a new event card is revealed. The game is played in this fashion for 5 turns. At the end of the game, after 5 events have been played, the player that has the most value in his stocks and money (combined) wins the game.

-description from designer

Mineshaft Mayhem is a solo variant expansion for Dig Down Dwarf. A copy of Dig Down Dwarf is required to play this game.

In Mineshaft Mayhem you play as a dwarf trying to sort gems into bins as they are quickly coming down the conveyor. It uses most of the components from the base game, but uses them in a completely new way. Mineshaft Mayhem has a built in timer that creates a ton of tension and you must use your wits to get the most value out of the gems you are sorting.

Will you successful sort all the gems, or will too many gems get past you and jam up the gears? There is only one way to find out!

—description from the publisher

This is a doujin (fan-made) game that was only for sell at Japanese game shows.  It was made on demand, and you had to order in advance to get it, then pick it up at the show.  They were not made for the general public.

This game deals with the Mediterranean theater during WW2 and pits the UK versus the Italians and Germans.

The game contains three scenarios with different victory conditions.  It is a typical I-go-you-go game, and fleets are moved by areas on the map.  Fleets can pick up and drop off as they move.  When a fleet moves into a box with enemy forces, combat is resolved.

Combat is fought on a tactical map with rows A-G.  Ships move by rows and can attack at certain ranges.  Guns can shoot 5 rows and torpedo strikes can shoot 3 rows.  Ships can create smoke screens and can withdraw from combat.  There are air strikes in the game too.

The game comes with one sheet of rules, one sheet of scenarios, 1 sheet of counters, and two maps.  Assembly is required.

—user summary

Let's do the time warp again! Relive that first Rocky Horror Show moment or play this game and create that moment. Sing the song, finish the callouts, play a character. Be the first player to build Rocky in the Lab, then escape the Castle to win. It all starts with a jump to the left then a step to the right. Celebrate the 50th anniversary of the ground-breaking Rocky Horror Show with this raucous party game.

- Based on the Rocky Horror Show Play seen by 30 million people worldwide
- Build alliances, navigate the lab and escape the castle or to Transylvania as your favorite character from the show
- Celebrate 50 years of the Rocky Horror Show with this game
- Sing the songs, quote the famous one-liners and lyrics, and take part in the notorious call backs from live shows
- Will you play as Magenta and Riff Raff or Brad and Janet?

Contents: Game Board, 6 Character Pieces, 6 Character Cards, 30 Lips, 50 Rocky Horror Show Cards, 30 Lab Cards, 6 Time Warp Cards, 3 Game Cards (for Acts I, II and III), 6-piece Rocky Puzzle, Game Die, Instructions

The world is a treasure trove of hidden caches waiting to be discovered, and an enthusiastic group of adventurers is on a quest to find and gather these hidden treasures. Armed with nothing but GPS coordinates and their wits, these explorers set out to uncover these secret stashes. While some of these caches may be relatively easy to locate, others pose a challenge that demands a combination of skills, unwavering determination, and puzzle-solving.

In the game known as 'Kačeři na lovu,' players compete to become the first to gather 25 of these elusive caches.  Each cache requires a certain way of collecting it with a specific items which can be obtained on special location on game board.

The search for caches is further influenced by a collection of action cards, which present an array of events - negative, neutral and positive. These cards add an element of surprise and strategy to the gameplay.

The ultimate objective in 'Kačeři na lovu' is to be the first to accumulate at least one cache of every unique type and achieve a total of 25 successful cache retrievals.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. If two players meet on the same space, the person who occupied the space first has to skip a turn. The first to reach space 99 wins the game.

GLOBULE is a placement game from Japan that mixes dice, card actions, and Go-Moku style placement of numbered pieces to play three different games. The board and the pieces are in acrylic, and include a North Star piece, the board being the starry skies above you.

There is a set of 72 discs' numbered 1-6 three times in four colours. The game also has a d6, and set of cards numbered 1-6, and a set of 20 Effect Cards that trigger special actions in the game.

1. スターライン (Star Line) - This is a version of Gomoku with dice and cards. If you don't use dice cards, you can play it as Gomoku.
五目並べのサイコロ・カードあり版です。サイコロ・カードを使用しなければ五目並べとして遊ぶことができます。

2. フォーティーン (Fourteen) - If you can roll the dice and line up 4 pieces with a total of 14, you win.
サイコロを回して出た目の駒を合計の数字が14ピッタリに4つ並べることができたら勝利のルールです。

3. サーティワン (Thirty One) - This is a 2 on 2 team game rule. If you align 4 or more star pieces, the total number of the aligned pieces will be your score. The team that scores over 31 points wins.
2対2のチーム戦ルールです。4つ以上の星駒をそろえるとそろえた駒の合計の数字が点数になります。チームの各得点数が31を超えたチームの勝利です。

user summary

As an enthusiastic animal conservationist, you’ll be thrilled to hear that a hiker has discovered the footprints of an endangered animal in a dark corner, deep in the South American jungle. What’s more, he’s drawn a map of the sites! Could some endangered animals have really re-established themselves in that area? You need  to know at once! Get ready for the expedition and then, armed with your maps, you’ll venture into the jungle, each one of you in pursuit of an unusual animal. Who will be first to find the trail of the animal they are looking for?

In Yunka, using your map, you have to find as many footprints as you can in the expanding and sometimes changing terrain you have built together. The first to find the 3rd footprint of an animal (or the 6th footprint in the easier game mode), or the one with the most animal footprints when you run out of fresh tiles wins.

You can find a footprint when the terrain (that is the jungle you have built together) has a combination of lake tiles and cliff tiles that match those drawn on your map. So, your goal is to somehow build a combination of tiles in the jungle as described or notice when someone else does.

Descend into hell at vanquish its demons.

Deck Builder that can be played with a 40 card deck of standard playing cards; Either using an Italian 40 card deck or removing the 8s, 9s, and 10s, from a poker deck.

The game setup will remove the lower value cards to form the player deck and the rest of the cards remain as the enemy deck with the top 3 revealed as the enemy row.  If the player can remove all the remaining enemy cards they win.  If they can not perform a legal move they lose.

Players will perform actions each turn and each action is decided based on the suit of the card played:

Fighting - Eliminate a card from the enemy row

Banish - Move a card from the enemy row to the bottom of the enemy deck and eliminate a card from your hand.

Buy - Purchase any non-face card from the enemy row and add it to your hand.

Rest - Do nothing on your turn.

The final rule that requires some strategy is that the player may not take the same action twice in a row.  Meaning, a player can not perform a fight and then another fight action.  So while the Rest action might not do anything, it's the only action that can always be performed and can help players.

Players will start with a weak deck of cards and slowly buy stronger cards, cull weaker cards, and fight their way to victory.

-description from designer

boardgame: Billion Battles: The Multiverse of Fantasy
description:
Billion Battles is a fast-paced RPG that combines sneaky humour, creativity, and a touch of strategy. In this game, each player blindly selects a unique Hero and Power card, then a shared Goal is unveiled. Your mission? In 10 minutes, role-play your hero and engage in epic polemic battles trying to convince and outwit your fellow players as you make the most persuasive or funny statement on how you would achieve the shared Goal.

However, here's the twist that adds a layer of amusement to the game - each player is assigned a hilarious hidden phrase that they must wive into their speech without anyone else noticing. The challenge lies in seamlessly blending this phrase into your role-playing narrative while keeping a straight face.

The stakes are high, if someone catches you pronouncing your hidden phrase, you're instantly busted and out of the game. Billion Battles offers a unique and entertaining twist on role-playing games. Also, it includes various game modes, ensuring endless hours of laughter and fun for student parties or family game nights.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: サンカンスシスライド (Sankan Sushi Slide)
description:
サンカンスシスライド (Sankansushisuraido) is a two player game from Japan, where players are trying to combine pieces to complete three sushis, or write words on the board to connect 4 words.

Sushi dishes are actually the combination of シャリ (sushi) which is the prepared vinegar rice, and ネタ (neta) the different fillings or toppings.

In this game, one players has the sushi parts, the other has the neta parts, and they try to capture the other piece to complete a sushi dish. The first player to get three dishes completed is the winner.

The pieces are slid across the table surface in a square grid pattern, and the player needs to get the sushi next to the neta or the other way round, to combine them.

The pieces and board are made of acrylic, and instead of moving a piece, and player may write a word on the squares of the table, which affects how the pieces move: 
ヤジルシ (Yajirushi) - this lets the pieces change direction.
シカク (Shikaku) - this stops the pieces at the square written upon.

As well as controlling the movement of the pieces, this allows a sudden win. Instead of making three dishes, a player can win by connecting a line of four words in their colour in any direction.
user summary

Elin River Cruise is a quick, slice of life style game where players take on the role of River Cruise captains. Each Captain will have 5 guests seeking adventure at the three unique site types as well as the opportunity to shop and eat fine foods. 
At first, Captains will only know a little about their guests' preferences, but as the game continues and as meals are shared, the guests reveal more about what it is they want to see along the Elin River.
There's only so many hours in the day, Captain, can you meet the needs of your guests and receive the coveted 5 star ratings that will keep your company in business?

—description from the publisher

Rift Zone: Contact is a sci-fi miniatures wargame where you command physical units on the table while a companion app handles combat and game logic in real time.

You move, position, and commit to every decision, while distance, terrain, armor, and damage resolve instantly around you. The focus stays on timing and tactics, not calculation.

The game supports both quick skirmishes and longer campaign play. Units gain experience over time, evolving across battles rather than resetting each match, which changes how you approach risk and positioning.

Set on a distant world, the conflict unfolds between two factions. The United Earth Defense Force relies on advanced technology, range, and coordinated tactics, while the Scry’Ox Collective adapts through pressure and close-range dominance with bioengineered units.

Rift Zone: Contact is designed to keep the battle moving. Turns resolve quickly, outcomes play out immediately, and the battlefield shifts continuously as you play.

Two-player abstract where you are trying to line up all five of your pegs in a row.

Turns consist of moving any of your own pegs one space in any direction, including diagonally. Jumping is not allowed.

Four new cat heroines pounce onto the scene! Fight with claws, tails, familiars, and... thingies?! Battle against each other or cooperate against a heroine's powerful boss form!

This is an expansion. Bullet♥︎ or Bullet★ is required to play.

-description from publisher

Four artistic heroines express themselves! Fight with drawings, paint, sound, and... dice? Battle against each other or cooperate against a heroine's powerful boss form!

This is an expansion. Bullet♥︎ or Bullet★ is required to play.

-description from publisher

In 三国群英录 (Legends of the Three Kingdoms), you'll step into the chaotic era of the late Eastern Han Dynasty. Four dominant factions - Wei, Shu, Wu, and Independent forces - each led by three valiant generals. Your mission is to select a faction, form your team, devise strategies, conquer territories, recruit soldiers, and compete for victory. Each general possesses distinctive skills that can be utilized by expending morale. With cunning tactics, strategic planning, and unwavering courage, can you elevate your faction, unite the Three Kingdoms, and emerge as the ultimate hero?

In the game, there are four factions: Wei, Shu, Wu, and Independent. Each faction can select three generals to form its team and then draw a target card.

During each round, players have the option to move, recruit (strengthening their generals), or attack (targeting enemy generals or territories). Each general possesses unique skills that can be activated by expending morale. Additionally, players can employ strategy cards to change the course of the game; each faction features its own special card effects.

Gameplay

1. Action Phase
Players can perform the following actions in any sequence:


     General Action: Each general can execute one action.
     General Skills: Each general can utilize multiple skills.
     Strategy Card: Players are allowed to play multiple strategy cards.


2. Card Drawing Phase
Players draw two strategy cards and may then discard until they have six cards in hand. If the hand contains fewer than six cards after drawing, no discarding is necessary. Alternatively, players can opt to skip the entire card drawing stage, neither drawing nor discarding cards.

Victory Condition

     The game ends when a player's merit reaches the maximum limit, initiating the final settlement phase.
     Each player's total merit equals the merit displayed in the merit column plus the achievements from the target card.
     The player with the highest total merit wins.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Become a supervillain in Batman: Gotham City Sem Lei!

Dominate the districts of Gotham City, recruit the most experienced villains to increase your influence. Outsmart your opponents by climbing the ranks of the underworld.
Beware, Arkham Asylum awaits those who fail. Reach 10 influence points and become Batman's greatest enemy!

In this game, clearly inspired by Pit, players trade simultaneously trying to get a set of five villains of the same type to control one of the districts.

The last player gets no district and loses influence.
However, players who manage to get their hands on a set, in addition to influence, will also be able to realize their super-villain's special power.

After that, all cards are shuffled again and we start a new round. The player who achieves 10 influence wins the game.

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Double-Headed Snake – The Battle of Rantemario
description:
This is a doujin (fan-made) game that was produced only for sell at Japanese games shows.  To get it you had to place an order for it, the game would be made, and then you would pick it up at the show.  It was not made for the general public.

This is another Legend of the Galactic Heroes game from designer Nishiki Taitei, and uses SPI's Napoleon's Last Battles to recreate the Battle of Rantemario from the show.

The sequence of play is roughly:

A. Cadence determination 
B. Imperial Army Movement Phase 
C. Imperial Army Combat Phase 
D. Allied Army Movement Phase 
E. Allied Army Combat Phase 
F. Turn Update Phase

Combat is fought using a Combat results table.  Odds are calculated and a die is rolled for results. Victory is dependent upon the scenario played.

While this game uses the NLB system, it adds some new innovations.  Commanders have a Command rating A-D, and a tactical color-coding red, white, blue, green.  These are used to give certain abilities and bonuses to the fleets.  This brings the game closer to the feel of the animated series.

—user summary
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Civil War Battlefields focuses on engagements during the American Civil War. It blends strategy and tactical thinking into an immersive experience where you can lead a Union or Confederate army in battles that will determine the fate of the nation.

A turn-based game where each player, or you can play on your own, is responsible for deploying their forces on a randomly generated battlefield. Using support elements such as artillery, calvary, irregulars, etc. the players will seek to complete a mission as set out at the beginning of the engagement.

The game also takes into account tactics that were used in the war and the generals who fought. This is my first game using the CHAOS system where even the best laid plans can be disrupted by the surprises of the battlefield. The unknown is a quantity of its own when it comes to Civil War Engagements.

Players will use support elements and tactical maneuvers to assault or defend various points of the battlefield. Formations will suffer losses and those losses will translate into lower effectiveness as the battle wears on. Reinforcements and reserves are limited so the player must make wise choices as to where they want to focus their strength and how they plan to wear down the enemy.

Engagements last from one to three days and at the end the winner is decided based on the number of forces that remain and the situational objectives. With territory cards that represent many of the most common grounds of the Civil War, players can develop battles that mirror those of history, from Gettysburg to the Fall of Richmond.

boardgame: 戦国武将ゲーム (Sengoku Warlord Game)
description:
This is a children's game produced by Takatoku.  It is set during Japan's Warring States period (Sengoku-jidai) and plays 2-5 players.

To win the game a player must become powerful enough to either destroy all other opponents or become rich enough to enter Kyoto and declare themselves the winner.

On your turn you do one of the following actions: Departure, Tax Revenue, Navigation, Weapon Procurement, or Construction of Castles.

Money (koku) is raised by controlling territories. You draw a revenue card for this and follow the results.

With your money you can build armies of infantry, archers, cavalry, firearms and artillery in a territory by procuring them. This is done by paying 10,000 koku and spinning the wheel, which determines what is available. Navies can similarly be raised in territories with ports.

A player moves by the roulette's results; either 1, 2, or 3 territories can be moved.  A player can take control of a territory by moving armies into it and building a castle. The territory states how much to pay to do this. However, if there are enemy troops in the territory you must fight them.

Combat is resolved by drawing a card which dives the details of how victory is achieved (such as having the most guns).

—user summary

You’d better roll FAST to beat family and friends in this high intensity dice game.

Give the spinner a whirl to name the pattern, and the frenzy begins! The first player to match the pattern with six Xs in a row wins! Pick your dice to win or to sabotage by removing Xs from your opponents’ boards.

Six dice. Six rows. Endless kicks as you roll to six!

—description from the publisher

Contains the specific rules for each individual BattleMonster miniature!

Print this booklet to create your own BattleMonsters stat cards and campaign sheets!

—description from the publisher

Mad for War is a gaming system for recreating 17th century naval actions with miniature ships. The game uses a simple system for movement under sails and combat. Crew quality plays a central role and special characters allows to differentiate ships. The rulebook contains also scenarios, fleet lists and information about the historical background.

Without darkness there is no light, but darkness is all that is engulfing us and it has been spreading for years from the forest.

During the late evening we hear mournful screams and during night we see the silhouettes walking toward the town…

The sanctified items in the church are hot, we can’t even take them in our hands to fight the evil. What is happening, what is advancing toward no one knows! We need God’s merciful to save us from what is lurking from the Darkwoods!

Step into the role of valiant defenders guarding the last bastion of a fading empire, standing firm against the encroaching darkness of the foreboding Darkwoods. Alternatively, embrace the sinister allure of the demons and choose to align with the forces of darkness in this cooperative and competitive gaming experience.

In a world where each night brings forth hordes of bloodthirsty demonic creatures, the town's morale and faith must be upheld to survive. You can opt to maintain the flickering flame of hope within the town's people. Or, you can cast your lot with the corrupted Cults, collaborating with the demons in their relentless quest to obliterate humanity.

This deck-building and strategy game immerses you in a dark fantasy realm deeply inspired by the folklore, legends, and myths of Eastern European mediaeval history. Choose your path wisely, for the fate of the last remnants of humanity rests in your hands.

—description from the designer

You have been appointed as the Imperator over a galactic Empire that is in the throes of an existential crisis. The long-decaying Empire is now under threat from four enemies, and must defend itself from, and ultimately defeat, these alien and internal threats.

Utilizing a unique battle system that combines resource allocation with dice rolling, you must keep the enemies at bay until the Empire is strong enough to counterattack and damage one enemy so badly that it can no longer threaten you.

Each of the 12 rounds of this solitaire challenge starts with the selection of a policy that the Empire implements to improve its chances of survival. Next, resources are spent to build and improve the Empire's forces. Lastly, the Empire's forces are allocated to face the various enemies, strategically losing where necessary, and holding the line in other theaters where the peril is too great.

Meanwhile, as the Imperator, you must maintain the support of the Grand Senate, which appointed you. If they believe that you have become too ambitious, or that you lack the necessary qualities, you will be forcibly removed. By balancing the use of an iron fist and a velvet tongue, will you be able to maintain your grip on power while preventing the Empire from epoch-ending ruination?

—description from the designer

description:
Puzzle game inspired by nostalgic role-playing adventures. (print-and-play solo game with the integration of voice control)

Game Overview

Players: 1
    Playtime: 15-30 minutes
    Ages: 7+
    Format: Print & Play with pplication (Web application)

You will explore the dark areas, trying not to be devoured by monstrous creatures. A race against time to find all the keys and exit the maze before running out of assistance from the wise shaman guiding you from the phone.
Explore with voice control if you like!

"The lost shaman" is a puzzle game in which the device places keys and doors in increasingly complex mazes from which you must emerge safely, avoiding or fighting monsters and using the resources found in the maze. It is a hybrid system in which finding clues is a crucial process for success.

It takes advantage of voice control to explore, allowing you to focus more on the printed map. A tribute to those who love paper and those who love pixels.

Cold War Commander is an exciting set of fast play wargame rules that allows you to recreate combined arms operations covering the Cold War and post-Cold War era.

Players each take command of a Battlegroup, which can be anything from a company right up to a division. These rules provide a good balance between playability and historical accuracy, allowing players to successfully use real-world tactics in a game system that is easy to use and fast to play. The rules are suitable for solo, two-player and multi-player games, at home, at the club, or at a tournament.

Cold War Commander covers the period from the end of the Second World War through until the early 1990’s.


     Suitable for any scale miniatures, from 2mm right up to 28mm
     Simple command system that emphasises the fog of war
     Effective mechanism for casualty resolution
     Rules for infantry, tanks, artillery, recce, aircraft and engineers
     Flexible figure ratio meaning one base represents a platoon
     Minimal setup time, simply assemble your forces and play
     Contains 3 army lists in the book and extensive lists online
     Includes 15 scenarios suitable for any nation and theatre
     Suitable for solo, two player or multi-player games
     Everything you need to play in one book


—description from the publisher

We play as a hungry Zombie, who must get the most juiced brains. To do this, players must exchange cards in a chaotic game without turns in which the fastest one will be the winner.

Each player starts with a hand of cards of different values, which they must change with the other players until all their cards are of the same value. By doing so they will earn points equal to the value of the card. After several rounds, the player with the most points wins.

“As I was crossing the compass platform the captain hailed me, not with the usual rigmarole of 'Enemy in sight, bearing, etc', but with 'There's the fucking Scheer! Open fire at her!'… But the name of the enemy ship was of course the Graf Spee.”
– Lieutenant-Commander Richard Jennings, HMS Exeter's gunnery officer during the Battle of the River Plate

Heroic quotes are overrated, focussing too much on the individual or on the big picture. I sometimes feel the same about naval wargames. They are either so sweepingly huge that the tactical battles aren't as fun, or they are so granular that five minutes of combat takes an hour to simulate. 
There's The Effing Scheer is my attempt to create an exciting game that doesn't devour your time. 
You have half a dozen or less models per side. 
Terrain isn't really a worry. 
You just need a tape measure, a ruler and some 12-sided dice. Plus this ruleset.


     Realistic accuracy and damage (though luck is always a reliably unreliable factor).
     Small battles won't last an hour of your time. Even large engagements should be finished in under a few hours.
     Historical design decisions are built into the rule mechanics, e.g. the rate of fire of 6" shells over 8" shells means they hit smaller targets more easily, but won't pack as much of a punch against cruisers.
     Launching torpedoes, firing, critical hits, changing formation and more are all simplified to speed up play time. Your subordinates are professionals after all.
     Critical damage such as loss of power, damaged fire control, fires and magazine explosions are all possible (but not encouraged, unless on your opponent's ships).
     Advanced rules cover collisions, night battles, Long Lance torpedoes and more. 
     Six starter scenarios set across the Atlantic, Mediterranean and Pacific.
     Open architecture. How I designed these rules is shown in the Appendix if you fancy constructing scenarios of your own for the miniatures in your collection.


Overall, if you want a game which isn't going to take up all of your time, give realistic-ish results and allow you to roll some dice with friends as you refight the early naval engagements of World War 2, look no further.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
“As a consequence, carrier battles were risky, unstable affairs that hinged on striking effectively first. To do so, a carrier force had to locate its adversary before it was detected itself, or not long after.”

– Robert C. Rubel, Deconstructing Nimitz’s Principle of Calculated Risk

This is an expansion to my ruleset There's The Effing Scheer, which allows you to wargame early- to mid-war WW2 naval combat. This game adds an operational map for moving task forces, rules for fog of war, reconnaissance and detection, and aircraft carriers launching air strikes.

Aircraft carriers were a huge shift in twentieth century naval warfare. The ability to detect and strike enemies beyond line of sight upended naval strategy. The warship that had taken hundreds of thousands, if not millions, to build could be sent to the bottom by a handful of aviators shrieking in, dropping some ordnance then disappearing over the horizon.

When I first published Scheer over a year ago, I aimed to add a quick little expansion within a couple of months to bolt-on a mini-campaign system and get aircraft carriers into the fray. It has taken me a little longer, but it is a more substantial expansion that me and my playtesters are happy with.

As with the base game of Scheer, I wanted something that incorporated the fog of war and confusion of early carrier combat. Operating on half-information and making crucial decisions without all of the pieces in front of you are big parts of this game (in one Coral Sea game, I was defeated as the Americans by rushing my first strike on what turned out to be some cruisers, while the Japanese, now knowing where my carriers were, stuck effectively first and crippled both my carriers). I want to put you in the seat of Nagumo as he wonders whether or not to wait for better information or to launch now and hope you hit the right targets.

This game comes with rules for incorporating aircraft into the Scheer base game, generic operational rules for fighting multi-day engagements and four operational scenarios (Matapan, Java Sea, Coral Sea and Midway).

If you want to complete an operation in a day using a game which isn't going to take up all of your time, give realistic results and allow you to roll some dice with friends as you refight the early naval operations of World War 2, look no further.

—description from the publisher

The Ancient Knowledge: Heritage expansion consists of two elements:


     16 solo mode cards that allow you to play a campaign. You choose 1-5 objectives, placing them in the artifact slots on the player board (and thereby denying you the potential use of artifacts!), and whichever objectives you complete become powers that you can employ in future games.



     36 new builder cards and 12 new technology cards that you can shuffle into the decks, making them 25% larger.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Keep the Heroes Out!: The Sparkling Guardian Expansion
description:
This crowdfunding exclusive expansion adds a unicorn as a playable monster clan. The unicorn can drop rainbow-surprises all around the dungeon, providing magical boots to their monster friends.
This expansion also includes Dice Tower 2023 Promo cards

boardgame: はぐれちゃやーよ (Hagurecha yaーyo)
description:
はぐれちゃやーよ (Hagurecha yaーyo) is a card game from Japan, in which the players are trying to identify which one of fifteen different couples are splitting.

The game has a deck of 30 cards of 15 couples, and each card has a special ability that is triggered when it is played or when it is held. The deck is shuffled and one card is placed face down in secret. The players are dealt the remaining cards (they don't have to be evenly shared).

Players then take turns drawing a card from their left neighbour. If the player can determine which is the secret card, they can declare it and check if it is correct. If so, points are scored by all players. Otherwise, they lay their hand out and are out of the round.

Alternately, the player might have a matching couple in their hand, and can choose to play these down into the open, or keep them in hand. A player that exhausts their hand is out and will lose one point in the round.

If only one player remains, they have a chance to declare the secret card and then the round ends.

Points are scored by adding the number of cards in hand plus 1 pt, scoring positively for a correct declaration and negatively for anybody else.

After three rounds, the player with the most points wins.

The game also has two marriage declaration cards and these are used in an alternate game, where a player is trying to get both declarations cards and only one person card in their hand. At this point, they declare which player they are marrying and lay their hand out for the win.
user summary

boardgame: The Witcher: Path of Destiny – Wild Hunt
description:
It's not only a new Story, but also a completely new type of gameplay, focused on prediction and cross-plotting, capturing the feeling of pursuit and anxiety. The semi-cooperative game mode allows players to stand on two sides of the conflict - one of you will take on the role of Eredin himself, commanding a group of wraith-like Wild Hunt Generals, while the other will lead Ciri and her companions. The solo mode is also included, in which you will try to face the Wild Hunt alone!

Introduces a new playable character for use only in this variant - Eredin, representing the Wild Hunt.

Wild Hunt is not just another character. It was specifically designed for this expansion and directing it gives you a whole new experience and range of decisions in gameplay. Both the components and mechanics emphasize the scale of the threat posed by these wraiths of Aen Elle.

This expansion also includes a playmat along with 4 miniatures of the Generals - Eredin, Nithral, Caranthir, and Imlerith! Each of these Generals is assigned to one of four colors of cards. After playing the corresponding colors, Wild Hunt moves the Generals forward on the track. By doing so, he gains experience and victory points, but most importantly, he gains access to special Ability cards for Generals, through which he can hinder the game for his opponents.

The other players will cooperate, side by side facing a powerful opponent! Scoring Victory Points collectively, they will do their best to prevent Ciri's abduction. 
In addition to earning Victory Points together, players also cooperate in the battle against the Generals - by playing cards of the appropriate color, they can weaken the Generals, moving them back on the track. They receive General Weakness cards for doing so, which they can use to increase their chances in the competition.

There are also entirely new Side Quests for Wild Hunt and cooperating players.

The Wild Hunt expansion also offers solo gameplay - you will take on the role of Ciri or one of her companions to face the Wild Hunt governed by Automa. For the purposes of solo gameplay, we have prepared a special Hunt board, as well as 4 bonus cards, replacing the Generals' Weakness cards. Playing in solo mode also requires the use of the 4 new Generals cards effects

Tuns is a card game for 2 to 4 players that is inspired by classics like Spite & Malice and Russian Bank.

It features number cards from 1 to 9 and two special cards marked with a T and a -T.

The goal is to get rid of the cards you've been dealt first. To get rid of them you have to play them from 1 to 9 to certain stacks. You also draw cards from a deck and have special stacks to lay down cards and share them with other players.

—description from the designer

Battle your way through the treacherous terrain and evade the relentless pursuit of the dark Lord Sauron. Be the first player to reach Mount Doom and change the course of Middle-Earth forever.

This is a cooperative game where 4 Hobbit characters travel a route across 3 boards to reach Mount Doom before they are caught by the pursuing Sauron character. Players pick from a pack of cards which determine actions and events, which include collecting weapons and defeating Orcs.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: Battle For Helms Deep
description:
Defeat Saurons army at Helms Deep! Be the first player to find the icons needed to destroy the armies in front of them. First player to flip over their cards wins!

Up to 4 players move around the raised Helms Gorge game board, watching their opponent's moves, and collecting the tokens hidden under Helms Gorge. These tokens allow players to use the right cards to match the icons on the cards of Sauron's armies, so you can use them to defeat him by turning over all their cards.

Roll the dice, then be the first to grab the right Dino.

Place the 36 dinosaur cards on the table, then roll the dice to give an accessory, dinosaur, and card colour. The first player to grab the correct card using the grabber wins the round! Be the first player to grab 5 cards to win the game!

Prepare yourself for some prehistoric hilarity!

The Black Death is Coming!

Take on the role of rival apothecary shop owners from the renaissance period in Apothecary: Plague Doctors.

In this 2-4 player card game you’ll draft ingredients and actions to help you complete orders, hinder your opponents, and distract the guards (if you’re planning on selling some poison on the side). The player with the highest combined value of orders at the end of the game wins and just might survive the plague.

—description from the publisher

In Assassin’s Creed: Vendetta, you will play as an Assassin or a Templar and try to unmask your adversaries and strike at the right time during an evening.

Since the dawn of time, the Brotherhood of Assassins and the Order of the Templars have waged a merciless war to impose their vision for the future of Humanity.

With the killer game Assassin’s Creed: Vendetta, relive this centuries-old confrontation as an Assassin or a Templar and do everything possible to eliminate the members of the opposing faction.
You will have to show cunning, mischief and use stratagems to fulfill the elimination conditions and thus achieve victory.

Kill your enemies discreetly, or act in the middle of a crowd...

Tormenta de Hierro ("Iron Storm") is an air war game with MDF miniatures for two players, whose objective is to overcome the missions presented at the beginning of each game.

In this first 1.1 manual you will find all the information you need to play, along with the miniatures of this game.

Factions:

In this first instalment we will have 3 factions: British Empire, The Spanish Crown, and the Republic of France.

Each faction offers a unique condition that will be reflected in a passive and an active way.

—description from the designer

In Diora, players take on the role of managers of a radio production factory, who will compete with each other to see who has chosen the best strategy that will lead the plant to the best possible development. Thus, he will receive the title of deputy president and will go down in the history of the plant.

Each player alternately sends his workers to perform appropriate actions. The entire game lasts five rounds.

The player who gets the most recognition points wins the game.

The game was inspired by great games such as Marco Polo and Pretor. Therefore, I would like to thank the authors Simone Luciani, Daniele Tascini and Andrea Novac for the great mechanics that were implemented in my game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Old King's Crown: Waking Kingdom
description:
The Waking Kingdom expansion (formerly titled Wild Kingdom) adds new map tiles, cards, and four modules that enable experienced players to modify and randomize future game plays.

"Night" Kingdom Map Tiles allow random map setups and "Waking Kingdom" cards dramatically increase options. 
The Travelling Town Module expands synergies and combos. 
The Aeronauts Module allows you to recruit Aeronaut faction cards and add their unique abilities to your deck. 
The Whispering Tower Module adds new boons to buy and a neutral mythical card to the game.  
The Legendary Creatures Module unlocks a new mythical card for each faction bringing new strength and power to your deck.
In Solo Vocali, you need to collaborate with other players to find words that feature the exact number of drawn vowels.

A new Person, with this Power:

Draw 2 cards from the deck and 1 card from every other player. Keep 2 cards and give every other player 1 card

The incomprehensible mission of the ethereal Veilwraith, affected by its magnanimous patron The Lady, has resulted in a tear in the weave of time and space. As reality, possibility, nightmare, and dream coalesce, shades of what once was (or could be) emerge from beyond the faltering Veil.

These 3 exclusive Shattered Veil promo cards can be used in any of the three Kilforth Fantasy Quest Games.

- Description from the Art of Kilforth Kickstarter page

The promo cards (Veiled Diablo, Agent of Locus, Lamented One) are numbered VW1 through VW3.

Galactic War 2202 is a doujin (fan-made) game that was only produced for sale at Japanese game shows.  To get one you had to order in advance and then pick it up at the show.  It was not sold to the general public.

This is a strategic simulation war game which is based on the war between the two major interstellar nations – Galman Empire and the Bolar Federation, which was depicted in the TV anime "Space Battleship Yamato III".
 As leaders of the Galman Empire or the Bolar Federation, players will build ships, engage in fleet battles, and compete for full control of the star system.

Each turn lasts for about 2 months. The players are given about 20 normal ship units and 1~5 special ship units. 1 fortress unit represents 1 whole fortress.

Galactic War 2202 is a retheme of the Legend of the Galactic Heroes and plays much the same way with some changes to it to suit Space Battleship Yamato.

The game comes with a rulebook, map, 11 sets of counters, and several organizational and turn charts.  All of this must be assembled to play.

—user summary

Aboard the last lighthouse, save the last inhabitants of Myiemord from deadly nightmares. Brave the dangers of perpetual storms to recover Dream Soothers, the only remedy for preserving life on Myiemord.

The Last Glow is a survival game where you take on the role of the crew of the last flying lighthouse, made up of 5 members.

Gameplay: Each turn, carry out the actions of your crew members, move the lighthouse, light the storms, flush out and capture the dream baits before sending them to the planet. If you manage to send six before your lighthouse is destroyed, you win. 
But beware: every turn, new storms are born, while others intensify and even explode, damaging your lighthouse.

—description from the publisher

Grey Smoke and Blue Sky is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) game that was available at the Japanese Game market show. The game is about the American Civil War.

The game comes with a map, 16 cards, 3 double-sided leader cards and 20 3D printed army markers. You must also provide 50 six-sided dice for the game.

From the designer's website:
This is a game of intense dice rolling and supply management where you fight through four rounds and aim for victory for both the Confederate and Northern armies.

The game follows this sequence of play:


     Draw phase.
     Initiative phase.
     Action phase.
     Combat resolution phase.
     Victory determination phase.


Victory is determined by victory point totals, which change according to the year of the war.  Meet your quota and you win the game.

—user summary

Why is the world in such a state? Wasn't our planet once welcoming to humans? Maxence doesn't have time to ask herself these questions; instead, she has to drive straight ahead to escape the Erasers. With less than a day's ahead, she sets off on the dusty track...

Mad Maxence is a post-apocalyptic game where you play as Maxence, a tough, vengeful young girl. Behind the wheel of your car, you'll travel to different parts of the wasteland, equip yourself, brave the dangers, learn about your past and, above all, avoid dying before you find your escape route...

Gameplay: Each turn, choose a new location and consume a unit of time. In each location, you'll find either good things or problems, and sometimes you'll have to pass tests that are solved using 3 coins and heads or tails combinations.
If you exceed the "20" time unit, the erasers catch up with you and eliminate you.

—description from the publisher

Earth 2043. A conscious AI bursts in and initiates the G117 protocol to eradicate humanity as an "invasive species". Despite the surprise, humanity fights back and launches a last stand on Earth and in the Matrix to pull the plug on the AI before it's too late.

Opposition game where two players fight for the survival of their own species. One as the AI player, the other as the human one.

Gameplay: Wargame in which you must simultaneously take control of two planes: the Earth and the Matrix. In one, troops and drones battle, in the other deckers and bots.
A game lasts 10 turns and there are ten different actions, so you have to play them all.
Each turn, the first player selects an action from which he chooses either the operation points or the bonuses indicated. The second player must play the corresponding action (which is necessarily different) and then he becomes the first player... and so on.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The story of Which Side, The postal game struggle takes place in New York, during the winter of 1910-1911 and stages homes of workers who try to survive and organize themselves to take control of the means of production before the employers become too powerful.

Gameplay: Each turn, you'll have to deal with employer offensives that affect workplace hazards, housing insalubrity or police threats. It's up to you to counter them, while paying the cost of living in order to survive, and of course generating surplus resources (strikes, sabotage, etc.) to take control of the means of production.  Place the 5 workers in your Tenement wisely to strike the right balance between survival and struggle. But beware: illness, accident or prison are never far away.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Labour movement / Workers rights / Unionising

description:
Created in the Landes region (Aquitaine) just over 60 years ago, Toroball represents both the Landes' sense of humor and their relationship with festivities.

Each summer, at small village Ferias, a sort of temporary arena is set up, and on Sunday mornings, after little sleep, youngsters throw themselves into the arena in colored jerseys and play soccer like everyone else. it's just that there's a cow lurking in the middle.

Gameplay: Each player in turn moves his 3 Sunday sportsmen and women. They'll try to hit the ball in the direction of the opponent's goal, attract the cow to their opponents and, above all, avoid taking a nasty charge. The cow always moves towards the nearest target. 

The first team to score two goals wins!

—description from the publisher

Sancti et Malum is a solo game of cards and dice which can also be played as a team.

The game is based on the epic struggle between the Holy and the Evil; the player (on the side of the faithful Angels) tries to defeat the fallen Host of Satan.

There is a deck of 40 cards, each card face split into a pair of Holy and Evil content. Most of these are Angels, each with their own dice-rolling power and other aspects, OR they are modifier cards to enhance or hinder the performance of the Angels.

Enemy cards are drawn, and players choose from their hand the Holy heroes they wish to assign to the conflict. Dice are rolled and modifiers applied to best meet the powers of the foes on the battlefield. If either Angelic army wins five rounds, they win the battle.

But the eternal conflict goes on forever between the Holy and the Evil - Sancti et Malum.

-description from designer

The selfish and guileful Kylyna has long stayed young taking a drop from the Heart of the Forest, the Forest Source of Life. Once she obtained it by fraud, she has been adding it to her beauty remedies ever since. But over time the drop dried up and Kylyna again grew old. In her efforts to make her life and youth longer, Kylyna wants to find the Heart of the Forest and appropriate it. On her way to this goal, she will try to confuse you by any means. However, you can save the Source and prevent Kylyna from destroying the Forest if you are attentive and overcome all her dirty tricks. Help Mavka and Lukash as soon as possible to meet at the Magical Willow, under the roots of which the Source of Life is hidden, and save the Magical Forest and its inhabitants.

"Mavka: Magical Willow" is a cooperative memory and strategy game where all players form a single team against the game itself. Players take turns revealing cards from the forest, which can help counter Kylyna’s tricks or move characters along their paths. However, Kylyna constantly disrupts their progress with obstacles, and players must carefully remember card locations and plan their actions. Each turn involves flipping a card, resolving its effects, overcoming obstacles, and moving the characters closer to their goal. To win, Mavka and Lukash must reach the Magic Willow before Kylyna. If she arrives first, players lose immediately.

boardgame: Dune: Imperium – Uprising: Arrakis Revolt Promo Card
description:
A promo card offered by Dire Wolf for preordering Dune: Imperium – Uprising.

Type: Fremen Card
Cost: 6 persuasion
On gain: 1 Troop
Agent symbol: Blue
Agent Ability: with a Maker hook, Pay 2 spice to tear down the shield Wall and summon a sand worm.
Reveal ability: Increase persuasion by 1 and combat strength by 3.

The promo was also offered on those occasions:
- After playing the demo at SPIEL, Essen.

Famfrenzia is a card-based game for 4-12 players that is easy to learn and quick to play. The game has no player turns and comes with a companion web app. Inside the box you find 120 character cards, 29 action cards, and 20 announcement cards. The character cards represent 17 families from around the world and each character card shows details of the character such as name, country, generation, location, points, and two possessions that the character owns.

The objective of the game is to build sets that earn you bonus points. Set types include families, social events (based on possessions), generation sets, duos, and murder mysteries.

The game is played in rounds. Each round has 4-5 stages:

     Each player gets 2 character cards from the deck.
     The game master auctions two character cards and one action card.
     Players have 2 minutes to negotiate and exchange cards.
     At the end of each round, players can use one of their action cards.
     At the beginning of every other round, the game master reveals one announcement card and ask the players to carry out its instructions.


Players use the companion app to add/remove character cards, search/filter for cards they may need, see their completed sets and missing cards from other sets, bid auction amounts and keep track of their digital money, and see updated victory points. The game ends when the deck runs out of cards and the player with the most victory points wins.

—description from publisher

With the new expansion Dice Pioneers, new dynamics come into play. With each turn the players decide whether they want to take on the role of the train driver, station master, worker, builder or the passenger. If they don't like the associated actions, they decide on three action stones - but this tends to reduce the advantage and is therefore something to avoid.

—description from the designer

Treasure by Torchlight is a single player dungeon exploration game. The dungeon tiles are shaped like puzzle pieces so they can fit together seamlessly and allow for dungeons to be randomly generated and unique.

As you explore you’ll encounter enemies and treasure, but you’ll also be running out of fuel for your torch. If your torch goes out while in a dungeon things will suddenly get significantly worse, so keep that torch LIT!

Play as one of 3 different heroes, each with their own special abilities. Return home with your loot, exchange your treasure for items at the shop, select new quests to tackle, and re-enter the dungeon to level up your character and take on the final boss!

—description from the publisher

Pax Pamini is an 18-card version of Pax Pamir Second Edition. It is a print-and-play game for free download. Thank you to Wehrlegig Games for generously granting me permission to create and post this game. Pax Pamini is a two-player game, but a solo mode is also provided in which you play against a crafty bot named Mikhran.

In Pax Pamini, players assume the role of nineteenth-century Afghan leaders attempting to forge a new state after the collapse of the Durrani Empire. Western histories often call this period "The Great Game" because of the role played by the Europeans who attempted to use central Asia as a theater for their own rivalries. In this game, those empires are viewed strictly from the perspective of the Afghans who sought to manipulate the interloping ferengi (foreigners) for their own purposes.

In terms of gameplay, Pax Pamini is a fairly complex tableau builder. Players spend most of their turns purchasing cards from a central market and playing those cards in front of them in their court. Playing cards grants access to additional actions that can be taken to disrupt other players and influence the course of the game. That last point is worth emphasizing. Though everyone is building their own tableau of cards, the game offers many ways for players to interfere with each other directly and indirectly.

The big twist with Pax Pamini is there is no physical board. Player tableaus are organized into four columns, with each column representing one of four regions. Thus cards are in two places at once: the player's court and a location on the abstracted map. A card in your Kabul column, for example, is considered in your court. But any roads, armies and tribes on the card are also considered to be in Kabul, and can interact with your opponent's game pieces in Kabul.

To survive, players will organize into coalitions. Throughout the game, the dominance of the different coalitions will be evaluated by the players when a special card, called a Dominance Check, is resolved. If a single coalition has a commanding lead during one of these checks, those players loyal to that coalition will receive victory points based on their influence in their coalition. However, if Afghanistan remains fragmented during one of these checks, players instead will receive victory points based on their personal power base.

After each Dominance Check, victory is checked and the game will be partially reset, offering players a fresh attempt to realize their ambitions. The game ends when a single player is able to achieve a lead of four or more victory points or after the fourth and final Dominance Check is resolved.

A note about Pax Pamini's components: there are 18 cards at the base game. The game is playable at 18 cards, but I recommend also using the bonus cards provided for a more streamlined experience.

—description from the designer

Take on the role of a vowel A, E, I, O, U, but Someone's Y. Work as a team to try and figure out who isn't the real vowel by giving one word clues on your turn. The player who is Y will need to blend in to try and not get caught.

When you hear a word said that includes your vowel you may choose to take a token and use it later to pay for an ability or vote out Y. The player who is Y can take tokens after any word is said regardless of the vowels in it. At the end of the game, if Y has the most votes against them, they get one last chance to win by guessing everyone else's vowels correctly. So don't be too obvious as to which vowel you are, but also try to gain as much information from others as you can.

-description from publisher

You and your family show up together at the park, “let's have a competition,” one of them suggests. “Let's all split up and take photos of the scenery. First one to take all their photos and get back to the car wins.” You all happily agree, heading out along the path to take pictures of trees, benches and ponds.

Park Photography is a leisurely game for 2-4 players about taking photos in the park. In this game you will be exploring the park, and trying to be the first of your family to make it back to the car (after taking some great photos of course). Be careful when taking photos though, you don’t want to have too many pictures of the same things.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chilli or Chunder: Pepper X Finale
description:
This micro expansion provides an alternative Round 5 finale chilli pepper, the new Guinness World Record breaking "Pepper X".

Weighing in with over a million more on the Scoville scale compared to the previous Guinness World Record holder, the Carolina Reaper, attempt to devour this in three bites if you dare!

—description from the designer

You must locate the crystal if you want to have any chance of getting out of the station alive! Strategically use you heroes' powers to be the first player to find the crystal, eliminate your opponents, and make it out in one piece.

Crystal Search is a deduction and strategy game for 2 to 4 players. In order to win, you must survive your opponents' attacks during the FIGHT phase - then be the first player to find the crystal in the SEARCH phase.

This card game is available as a part of the Endless Dungeon Day One Edition video game.

—description from the publisher

In this original, solo, folk horror adventure game set in Medieval times that never were, you do not control a single character, but all the 36 Notables of Eldritchwood.

Like a storyteller, you weave a story of relationships, daring actions, deadly combat and difficult choices, moving from one character to the next as the story unfolds.

Hunt foul beasts like the Tatzelwurm or the Basilisk, meet supernatural entities like the Dust or Blackheart the Prince of Rats, learn magic from grimoires, socialize during events and festivities, get married, run from the Inquisition, and above all fight the evil that is threatening the lives and sanity of the good folks of Eldritchwood.

To play, you need this book, a pair of standard six-sided dice, a pencil, and a planner to keep track of events and dates.

—description from the designer

1822Africa places the 1822 system on a small map of the African continent with gameplay as fast as possible while retaining the 1822 game style.

How is this different from other 1822 games?

     Smaller and cheaper train roster - These help the game flow well at a fast pace. Most games only last six sets of operating rounds. 
     Smaller Map - To be fully built out over the course of a shorter game
     Shared Bid Boxes - There are only a “shared” set of three bid boxes, but unlike other 18xx games, there are two consecutive stock rounds.
     Concessions availability - The setup rules ensure that two concessions will come out in the first two stock rounds, but if other concessions are buried in the draw deck, the competition for the early concessions may be quite fierce.
     Earlier Phase 2 - In other 1822 games, the pacing of the early trains is somewhat slow (depends on the game/scenario). This allows minors a few turns to build up to be able to upgrade their L to a 2 (typically at a cost of 80). Since this game is meant to run faster, the L/2s are repriced to 50/100 with an upgrade cost of 70. This means that an SR1 minor can buy a 2-train from the start.
     Earlier minor acquisition - Since the goal of this game is to keep a fast pace, the restriction is waived that disallows acquisitions to take place in the first operation of a company. This means that a newly formed companies can participate in an acquisition from their first operation.


—description from the designer

Create two teams and take approximately 30 word cards (200 in the box) from the pile. One player has 1 minute to explain as many words as possible to the teammates. With the 'enlarged' screen in front of her/his mouth. Each word guessed is a point for the team. In round 1 you can say anything, except the word of course. In round 2 you can give one word as a clue and in round 3 you are 'on mute'. That's lip reading. There is a lot to laugh about in all rounds with that enlarged mouth!

Fun for the whole family. Brushing teeth before the game highly recommended.

boardgame: Huddle Up: Master the plays, dominate the field
description:
Huddle Up is a board game implementation of the popular sport American Football. In 1 vs. 1 you compete on the football field. First select your favorite playbooks and unique teams, the latter you can then improve in the draft and get new players with special skills in your team.

By playing your offensive plays you try to manipulate the dice in your direction and score points by touchdowns or field goals. The defense, of course, wants to prevent this by picking the right defense. Both sides have to keep an eye on the time and the referees. Play your timeouts and your challenge flag skillfully to lead your team to victory.

Whoever could score better after four quarters wins.

boardgame: Guinness Pub Quiz Trivia Card Game
description:
Enjoy these unique Pub Quiz questions just about anywhere: at home, at a friends, at your favorite pub, anywhere friends gather! Answer correctly to win the card. The first to collect 5 cards wins the round!

The player rolls the dice to determine which category they will be quizzed on.

Starts with "G": answers contain a word that starts with G
Game People Play: answers are recreation or sports themed
Since 1759: answers are about history since the start of Guinness
Anything Flows: answers are about anything
Pubular Culture: answers are about popular culture
Players Choice: player chooses which category (1-5) to be quizzed on
Unique and unusual questions are sure to keep everyone intrigued.

Set contains 100 Pub Quiz Cards (with 500 questions and answers) and one die along with a set of instructions.

—description from the publisher

Each player starts the game with 5 card in their hands, and after a card is played a new one is drawn from the pile.

Every turn each player plays a card in the table. The player with the maximum points in their card wins the round and get all the cards in the round. In case of a tie, the player who first played the card wins.

When there are no enough cards for all the players in the draw pile, the rest of the cards in their hand are played and the battle finishes. The played with the highest number of points (not cards) wins the battle.

When 3 battles (or 2 consecutive ones) are won, the player gets the "Gran Cruz al Mérito de la Guerra" Medal and wins the game.

Players can play individually, in pairs or even in asymmetrical mode. In this last case, the player playing alone draw cards in a different fashion: he can have a look at the two card on the top and get the one desired (the the one one returns to the top of the pile).

If a player wins a round and the gets the "Jefe de todos los ejércitos" (12 points) card and the 2 "General" (10 points each) cards he wins the battle. In the case that he additionally has a Cruz Roja (Red cross, 10 points) card and a plane (9 points) card, he wins the battle and the rest of the players discount one won battle from their merits. In the case that none of the other players had a previous won battle the winner obtains 2 battles and thus, get the "Gran Cruz al Mérito de la Guerra" card, winning the game.

A twist on Scrabble that includes two game kinds using a  roulette:

Firstly, you can play using the included roulette that obliges you to play your next word under a specific restriction:

     Begging of an already placed word
     Middle of an already placed word
     End of an already placed word


Secondly, you can start the game with the tiles upside down and turn as many tiles as indicated in the roulette. Then, you create a word using letters shown, taking all letters used with you. The rest of them remain for the next rounds.

—user summary

boardgame: Heroes of Ilirium: Dual Deck Duels
description:
Welcome to the grand arena of Heroes of Ilirium: Dual Deck Duels – a game where strategy meets fantasy, and where every choice you make shapes the fate of Ilirium!

In this dynamic 2-player card game, you don't just choose a hero – you draft two! And with 8 distinct heroes to pick from, the combinations are vast and strategies limitless. Our unique dual deck system breaks the convention: players wield not one, but two decks, each teeming with a diverse range of monsters and versatile utility cards. As you venture deeper, you'll find that each hero evolves, not just in story, but in power. With the collection of Soul tokens, heroes level up across three stages, each unlocking potent abilities that can sway the tide of battle.

But the intrigue doesn't end there. Every round unfolds on a new and unpredictable battleground. Tame wild beasts not native to your decks, harness their strengths, and surprise your opponents. While the heroes stand back from direct combat, their growing powers cast wide shadows over the battlefield, influencing duels and determining outcomes.

Are you ready to experience a game like no other? Dive deep, strategize, and become the master of dual deck dueling!

—description from the publisher

Promo scenario with commander Piché, containing one postcard with two additional missions (Yellow and Black) .

Note: Available in Sky Team Turbulence and as a Print and Play

Fox-3 is a tactical level miniature game in which players go head to head with an opposing squadron of jet fighters. The goal is to eliminate the opposing fighters by outsmarting, outmaneuvering and outgunning them.

Set in an alternate history where the world has spiraled into constant conflict, your first choice is which side you're on: are you with one of the sovereign Nation states, or one of the threatening Villain states? Or would you prefer to be able to join the highest bidder as part of one of the world's leading Private Military Contractors? Each of these factions will give access to a selection of fighter jets - some more advanced than others, but all with a unique character and play style. 
After selecting your jets, missile loadouts and pilots, you're ready for take off and engage the hostiles.

The unique and intuitive movement system takes in account critical aspects of Basic Fighter Maneuvering such as speed and altitude - don't expect to do that sharp high-g turn when you're flying the speed of sound.. 
Evade incoming long-range missiles, fire your own missiles when you have a clear shot, or switch to guns when the bandit's too close.

The Fox-3 core game contains everything to get you playing, including 6 miniatures of 3 different jet fighter types.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A card-based simulation where players will be assigned command of a front during World War 1. They will receive orders and will be assigned forces to execute those orders. While there will be brutal attrition, a well-executed strategy will mean the difference between death or victory on The Front.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: City of Glory: Angkor Card Game
description:
