A cooperative game of 4 villages employing laborers (with the help of elephants, buffalo, artisans, chisels) to harvest rice, build a central temple, avoid invasions and establish a monarchy.
You have to complete the temple and fill the thrones before the deck (or rice) runs out. But build too fast and events may overwhelm you!

A collaborative card game that captures the spirit of ancient Angkor.

Every aspect of City of Glory: Anglor card game is intended to recreate the experience of the ancient Khmer kingdoms of the Angkor era. It's a game that requires players to work together through communication, collaboration and strategy to win or lose as a team.

Each player oversees a village where labourers can farm rice or help build an Angkor temple. Train your labourers as apsaras to dance for the gods or as artisans to decorate the temple. Grow rice to support your kingdom. Buy buffalo and elephants to grow and build more quickly. Complete the temple before the gods no longer favour your city.

Each game includes a set of 237 cards, instructions for collaborative and competitive gameplay, temple and village playing areas, and informative winner cards that allow you to compare your completed temple with those of Angkor, the city of glory.

Recommended for ages 12 and up, this game is for 2-4 players. Made in Cambodia, the temple game cloth is dyed with 100% Cambodian mud.

Iron Age is a classic empire-builder with modern mechanics set in the European Iron Age. The goal is to forge your empire’s legacy to ensure its place in history.

Expand by war and diplomacy, recruit epic Heroes, build marvelous Wonders and make brilliant Discoveries to collect Victory Points and effects that strengthen your empire.

Players adapt their abilities by managing their selection of Battle and Command Cards in their hand. Some are replayable each turn and some are handed to the targeted opponent after play. Clever planning and a silver tongue for diplomacy is required to reach your goal before the others.

You can conquer the world as Rome, Greece, Persia, Egypt, Carthage or Gaul, each with their own set of 3 Heroes, a starting Discovery and a unique Legacy Bonus.

There's a maximum of 7 turns per game and each turn has 3 phases - Income, Action and End. The actions are: Recruit, Build, Influence, Trade and Move.

If a player reaches maximum expansion or claims enough achievements, the game will end early.

By the end of the game, the player with the most Victory Points is declared the winner and earns the title "The Great".

Iron Age is approachable grand strategy in a small box. It features casual gameplay with intense competition. Entertainment and loud outbursts guaranteed.

Based on engine building, worker placement and hand management.

—description from the designer

This is a game of wild associations and intuitive connections! Pick two or more cards from the deck, blend their concepts, and secretly jot down a word that perfectly ties them together. For instance, link “frozen” and “sweet” to guide fellow players towards guessing “ice cream.” Earn points for both correct guesses and matching wrong answers with others, as it’s all about syncing minds and thinking alike!

Rivivete tutte le emozioni della trasmissione televisiva dell'anno!

Tra quiz pazzi, avvincenti prove musicali e gare di mimo, potrete misurare la vostra abilità con quella degli avversari. Ma nella prova conclusiva sarà tutto rimesso in gioco, con colpi di scena veramente imprevedibili!

Relive all the emotions of the TV show of the year!

Between crazy quizzes, exciting musical tests, and mime competitions, you will be able to measure your skill with that of your opponents. But in the final test, everything will be put back into play, with twists of scene truly unpredictable!

Vindication: Remnants of the Ancients contains fifteen new cards to mix into the game (with gear icon upper left):

Companions

     STONEWORK GOLEM (Y)
     STONEWORK GOLEM (R)
     STONEWORK GOLEM (B)


Traits

     INVENTOR’S INCLINATION
     TRANSHUMANISM


Relics

     THE GO-FASTER GADGET
     STONEWORK CONTRAPTION
     ONE MAN BAND, WITHOUT THE MAN
     INSCRUTABLE DEVICE


Pets

     A SINGLE GEAR
     STONEWORK FLEDGLING


Monsters

     ENRAGED STONEWORK STOMPER


Loot

     MOBILE PLATFORM
     STONEWORK GLOVE
     BACKPACK ENGINE

These two promo cards for Vindication were created to support Quackalope:


    Quacka-wrait - Pet Spirit
    Medallion of the Raven - Relic

boardgame: Vindication: Board Game Co. Promo Cards
description:
This pair of promo cards for Vindication were created for Board Game Co. in 2022:


    Alexei, the Unyielding - Puppet Master 312
    Alexei's Cube of Unholding - Relic

Before the game begins, players choose one of three difficulty levels for their Bible trivia questions (Easy, Medium, or Hard). Players race to the finish by rolling a die on their turn and advancing their player piece the number of spaces rolled. The first player whose piece reaches the finish wins!

Players will be asked Bible Trivia questions of their chosen difficulty level when their piece lands on a red ladder or a rope. When a player's piece lands on a red ladder they are asked questions until they give an incorrect answer or reach the top of the ladder. For each question answered correctly the player moves their piece up one section of the ladder.

When a player's piece lands on a rope they are asked questions until they give a correct answer or reach the bottom of the rope. For each question answered incorrectly the player moves their piece down one section of the rope.

If a player's piece lands on any part of a blue ladder or blue chute they automatically move their piece to the top of the ladder or bottom of the chute.

Whenever a player's piece lands on a green arrow an action card is randomly drawn and the player must perform the action indicated on the drawn card. Typical action cards move a player's piece forward or backward several spaces. After moving forward from an action card a player ignores ropes, chutes, and slides but the above rules still apply for ladders. After moving backward from an action card a player ignores ladders but the above rules still apply for ropes, chutes, and slides.

Multiple player pieces can occupy the same space on the board. The first player to reach the finish line wins!

The first expansion for Dragons Down. Now you can adventure in the sinister swamps and perilous plains... or add them to your existing base game to create an epic experience.

Allows for up to 6 players
Two new terrain packs (Sinister Swamps and Perilous Plains)
12 new map tiles
30+ new monsters
8+ new natives
25+ new treasures
12 new missions
10 new items
6 new lineage cards (adding the Half-elf and Half-orc lineages)
10 new classes (assassin, bard, battle mage, black knight, executioner, paladin, ranger, runesword, soulless, and trickster)
2 new civilization locations
3 new merchants

—description from the designer

Revelations: Skirmish is a fast-paced, reactive table-top combat game. Each player builds their force of infantry, drones, vehicles, and powerful WarMechs to achieve victory on the battlefield.

Every game of Skirmish allows players an intense and enjoyable tactical experience, filled with destructive combat. All of the rules and stats are available for free.

—description from the publisher

Revelations: Skirmish is a game for 2 players using small army sizes (up to 12 miniatures per side). An expansion (available to download for free) makes the game enjoyable as a solo experience.

The game sets itself apart from many others by making everything available to print at home. The rules are free and the miniatures needed to play are available to buy as STLs for 3D printing at home.

—user summary
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Embark on a journey through an ever-changing, player-constructed hexagonal board, where every playthrough presents a fresh set of challenges and opportunities. Set in a post-apocalyptic sci-fi world, players must navigate a brutal landscape. Strategic prowess is paramount, as each movement, resource allocation, and confrontation shapes their fate. The inclusion of impactful cards, representing machines, helpers, and equipment, adds a layer of customization and adaptability to gameplay. Player interaction takes center stage, with alliances and conflicts unfolding against the unforgiving backdrop of this world, where loyalties can shift as swiftly as the desert sands.

You are the leader of a group of prisoners. Your goal is to repair a spaceship and escape the planet before the overseers catch on to your plan. But you’re not the only one with this idea. Other prisoners will be trying to do the same thing, and they won’t hesitate to sabotage your efforts if it means they can get off this rock before you do.

The race is on, and you have only five days to gather the resources you need, repair your ship, and take off before the overseers catch you. You’ll need to scavenge for parts, negotiate with other prisoners, and fight off any who stand in your way.

The task ahead of you is daunting, but you’re not alone. You have your fellow prisoners to rely on, and together, you might just be able to make it off this planet alive. But be warned: this journey is not for the faint of heart. Death lurks around every corner, and every decision you make could mean the difference between life and death.

Are you ready to take on the challenge? Can you outsmart and outmaneuver your fellow prisoners to make it off this planet before it’s too late? The clock is ticking, and the fate of your group rests in your hands.

—description from the publisher

Earth 2043. The apocalypse has taken place, and human societies have collapsed, along with their populations. The survivors have decided not to be fooled twice, and have organized themselves locally, without a state, according to the principles of anarchy.

We invite you to simulate the construction of this new society, before the ShoTgun's revenge, which is the theme of our next single-player game.

Gameplay : Each turn, select a location and resolve roleplaying situations that changes while time is passing (turns). Your goal is to establish your anarchist community according to its principles, while the reaction of AuTority is growing in the shadow.

At the end, you will get a situation that will be transpose to our next solo game “WAy of the ShoTgun” as starting conditions.

—description from the publisher

Stealing Scenes: Action is a fast-playing limited resource management card game where you play an actor’s stunt double that is trying to be noticed by the director. You do this by stealing scenes using the resources in your hand, as well as the resource your actor possesses. Once you have stolen enough scenes, you win.

The game consists of 156 cards, broken into three decks: Actor Deck (10), Resource Deck (84), and Scene Deck (50).  The are also 8 double-sided Reference cards and 4 Rules cards.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

Add these 4 Promo Cards to the Project cards deck during set-up and expand the variety of patterns you can build during the game.

Dungeon Pages: Overland Adventures takes the adventure above ground, introducing 12 more sheets for 2024, 12 more heroes combinable with original Dungeon Pages, and new additions like traveling merchants, side quests, and new tile types.

Get out of the dungeon and get some fresh air! In Year 2 of Dungeon Pages you'll find new heroes, items, monsters, relics, and weapons. Also an all new Merchant, Side Quests as well as some seriously tricky boss fights!

Scribble Me This is about drawing pictures, solving riddles based on the drawings, and laughing with friends! It is one of five games included in The Pipnetic Book of Cut and Play Party Games: Drawing Edition.

At the start of the game, each player is given a card with an answer to a riddle (but they don't know the riddle) and then they draw pictures representing those answers.  When everyone is done drawing, the pictures are displayed and then the riddles are read, one at a time. For each riddle, players try to identify the drawing that shows the answer to the riddle.

Players score points based on how many drawings they guess correctly and on how many times their own drawings are guessed correctly.

—description from the publisher

Each player is an apprentice witch. Players randomly select a book of witchcraft with four potions listed per page. Five potion cards will be revealed from the deck of potion cards, with each potion listing a specific combination of ingredients required. Players will begin with five ingredient cards. On their turn, they pick up a sixth card either from the face down ingredients deck, or from the top card of the face up discard pile of ingredients. If a player has the corresponding ingredients to make one or more potions, they discard the ingredients face up, take the potion card(s), and place them face up in their player area. The potions row is then replenished so five cards are face up. Players may negotiate and trade ingredient cards, one for one, with other players. At the end of a turn, players will draw or discard ingredients so that they have five cards in hand. The first player to complete four potions from one of the pages in their book of witchcraft wins the game.

The North Forest Stash expansion contains 41 cards for the main deck of Flapjacks & Sasquatches. The cards include tools, treasures, implements, utensils, helpers, and friends. A single "Bacon" die is included.

boardgame: Flapjacks & Sasquatches: Salute to Bill & John
description:
A pair of promo cards for Flapjacks & Sasquatches. These were released as an appreciation for two Northwoods Heroes on the occasion of the passing of the Prolific Games axe.

The following promo cards are included:

"The Rules Say So... Now!"
"Bork Bork Bork"

Skirmish is an action-packed, blisteringly fast tactical battle game that will keep you on the edge of your seat. Choose your ideal party of three fighters and conquer the field with up to four of your friends. Will you be the last leader standing? Test your mettle as you set out to the battlefields of Skirmish!

In Skirmish your goal is to eliminate the opposing players Leaders while keeping yours alive. Each player will select a Leader and two additional fighters drafted from a pool of available Fighters. Each Fighter has unique but simple abilities to help players pick from a variety of play styles; aggressive, defensive, board control and many more. Up to four players can play at the same time which makes every move count! If you are able to eliminate the other players leaders while keeping yours alive, you win! Play and reset time between games is fast and simple, allowing you to adjust your strategy for the next game or your tables meta.

Whether it is Inter v. Milan, Madrid v. Atletico or Colchester v. Southend, nothing can match the passion and excitement of a derby football game. With a third of the season played and performances so far nothing to cheer about, old rivalries are rekindled as the days tick by to the biggest derby in the PB National League Top Tier – it's Blues v. Reds.

In Pitch Battles, as the manager of one of the two teams, you will pick your starting line up from your squad and chose your formation. Using you own deck, you will be playing cards to direct players, moving their tokens to tackle, gain possession, pass your way through the opposition’s defence and shoot for goal. You will look to your assistant coach to provide tactical guidance, whilst your team captain will try to inspire and rally the troops. Get the ref on your side and encourage your supporters to cheer and spur your team on. Maybe a bit of intimidation from the fans will sway things your way, encouraged by some  dubious challenges. To win the beautiful game you may need to play ugly. This is the local derby and winning is all that matters!

—description from the designer

Intrepid members of the LRDG and SAS travel hundreds of miles through the deserts of North Africa, braving sandstorms and extreme temperatures to raid German airfields.

The 02 Hundred Hours: Desert Raid adds two opposing forces ready for night raids in the desert, plus the new weather events. Now you'll battle the elements as well as the enemy!

The miniatures in this set can represent either LRDG or desert-based SAS, or a mix of both. For SAS, use the recruitment cards from the main 02 Hundred Hours set.

A courageous group of Partisan fighters resists the German occupiers, gathering information, forming plans and launching night-time raids on the enemy.

The 02 Hundred Hours: Partisan Resistance Cell expansion contains new models and forty unique cards that add plan cards to the game, allowing the partisan player to choose special tricks and stratagems before the battle starts: cut the phone lines, receive coded messages, or even place sausages to distract the guard dogs! Plan cards are a bit like the event cards, except that you choose three (normally) before the battle starts; the more partisans in your force, the more plans you will have to choose from.

This expansion includes recruitment cards for Jedburgh teams: three-man groups tasked with helping the local resistance cells to prepare for D-Day. Jedburgh miniatures are sold separately.

The 02 Hundred Hours: Operation Torchlight expansion features new models and 24 unique cards, including including options to field Royal Marine Commandos!

The top secret Special Operations Executive assembles expert teams for the most dangerous objectives. A courageous SOE agent receives intelligence that an RAF pilot has been lost carrying vital reconnaissance photographs. The Highland Major, famed for his tracking skills, hand-picks a force of Royal Marine Commandos for the rescue mission. A demolitions expert is added to provide an explosive diversion while an SOE assassin eliminates targets quickly and quietly...

The 02 Hundred Hours: Guards of Facility 9 expansions contains new models and 24 unique cards, including the "It's a Trap" event to turn the tables on the attackers!

At a classified location in Western Europe, German scientists carry out chemical research to create deadly new weapons. The facility is overseen by the officious commandant, ably assisted by his adjutant and a wounded veteran officer who has been reassigned away from the front line. Guards patrol the facility wearing gas masks to protect themselves from the toxic fumes while a sinister Gestapo officer keeps a watchful eye on everyone...

—description from the publisher

Learn Iranian mythology and monsters while playing a familiar card-shedding game with these custom cards.

Compete against other Iranian mythical creatures based on their Intelligence, Strength, Agility and Magic, ranked from 1-10. Strategically play the special command cards to beat your opponents.

In the decorative box, there are 52 gold-dipped cards:
12 Mythical Birds 
12 Divs (beasts) 
12 Jinns 
12 Mythical Marine Creatures 
4 Supreme Creatures

The player that uses all of the cards in their hand, before other players, wins.

Celebrate Iranian culture with these intricately designed cards and game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Middara: Unintentional Malum – Vestalia 2023 Resin Kit A
description:
Kill monsters, get loot, and level up.... in a bikini.

This product contains 5 highly detailed resin miniatures, featuring Damocles, Rook, Justice, Shayliss, and Remi.

These miniatures may be used as alternate sculpts for their corresponding figures in Middara: Unintentional Malum - Act I.

All characters include full color initiative cards, adventurer cards, art cards, and 9 new items for use in your games of Middara.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Middara: Unintentional Malum – Vestalia 2023 Resin Kit B
description:
The sexier the armor, the better the stats.

This product contains 5 highly detailed resin miniatures, featuring Padric, Nightingale, Zeke, Effie, and Zafir.

These miniatures may be used as alternate sculpts for their corresponding figures in Middara: Unintentional Malum - Act I﻿.

All characters include full color initiative cards, adventurer cards, art cards, 1 familiar card and 8 new items for use in your games of Middara.

These models come unassembled and unpainted.

—description from the publisher

Match cards by colour and creature to score points. Each of the 5 creatures has a special power to give you more control over your hand and card sets.

Strategic Choices:


     Curate your hand by drawing from either the deck or discard piles.
     Be careful which cards you discard, because other players may pick them up.
     Select which creatures to play based on their powers and the sets you are trying to build.


				
				
					How to PlayYour primary objective is to score points by building sets of creatures. A set is 3, 4, or 5 cards of matching colour or creature type. Each card may contribute to both a colour set and a creature set (e.g. Red Rabbit scores for both the Red and the Rabbit sets).

				
				
					Your TurnYou have a hand of cards. Play cards to the table to form sets. These sets score at the end of the game.

On your turn:


     Draw 2 cards from any piles (deck and/or discards).
     Play 1 card to your sets.
     Discard 1 card to your personal discard pile.


When you play a card to a set, you may optionally use the creature's power.

				
				
					Creature Powers
     Rabbit: draw another card.
     Dragon: score +1 for a set of Dragons.
     Qilin: play another card. You need to discard a card to use this power.
     Fox: snag a matching colour card from another player.
     Tortoise: protect yourself from foxes. 


The Tortoise has an additional special power: you may play it out of turn if you have it in your hand when someone plays a matching Fox!

				
				
					End Game & ScoringThe first time the deck runs out, shuffle all discards into a new deck, and then play until the deck runs out again. (The game includes a Phase Card which helps keep track of the deck running out the first time). You then finish the round and everyone counts up their scores:


     3-card set: 1 point
     4-card set: 2 points
     5-card set: 3 points
     +1 point if you have a set of Dragons

This version of the card came was an exclusive promo from ComicCon 2016.

PR37: Tamara Orlova (Russia)

During your Starting Phase
Put this card at the bottom the pile of Private Maids in the Town. Gain +2 Servings. You cannot use this effect if there is no Private Maids pile.

A trick-taking game for two to six players where rival witch covens (the Skull, Moon, Bat, Ghost, Cauldron, Broom, and Spider) battle to gain thirteen points. Special cards allow one to draw an extra card and change the trump suit mid round.

Beyond Kuiper is a game of space fleet combat for 1-6 Players. Each Player starts in command of a starship with a customizable captain and crew. when the battle starts each player must maneuver their crew to best operate their spacecraft and ensure it is functioning at the highest possible level. Defeat your opponents by any means necessary including Turbo Lasers, Boarding parties, alliances, and betrayal!

Every Game will create a different story of an epic space conflict with infinite replay ability!

—description from the designer

Z-Engine / Z-エンジン is a racing game in which you modify your engine and customize your car during the race itself.

The game was included in Game Link vol. 6, a magazine released in December 2010, and it consists of a double-sided map, a single-sided map, counters, and rules.

High on a hilltop, there stands a dilapidated old mansion. Some say the place is haunted but you and your friends don't believe in such myths. Except for the one about Old Man Rotter's gold that still remains in the place. What will you find inside Rotter Mansion? - vast riches or the wrath of a vengeful spirit?

Escape from Rotter Mansion is a push-your-luck dice game for 1-4 players. In it, you and a partner meeple try to escape from a ghost in a haunted mansion. Will you reach the exit in time or will you be trapped inside forever?

Escape from Rotter Mansion is a bespoke board game created by designer Scott Rogers (Rayguns and Rocketships, ALIEN: Fate of the Nostromo). Only 10 copies of the game were offered for sale in the Fall of 2023 while Rogers battled cancer.

The game features luxe components created specifically for the game by Panda and includes a cloth playmat, metal coins and a velvet "coffin lining" in the box. The project was a labor of love for Rogers and is truly a unique collector's item.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Xenocyte Impalers Enemy Pack
description:
The cold dark halls of the derelict ship are home to more than just stumbling undead Necronauts and jittering robots that have been corrupted by the power of the Void. Vile alien creatures also lurk within the dead ship, nesting in the bowels of the hulk, and swarming any explorers that wander too far into the dark. The Xenocyte Impalers crawl on viciously sharp legs, with a whipping tail to impale their foes. Deadly fast, they fade into the shadows before descending on their targets for the kill.

Shadows of Brimstone: Xenocyte Impalers Enemy Pack contains everything you need to encounter the deadly Xenocyte Impalers: six Xenocyte Impaler models, 6×30 mm bases, the enemy record sheet, and all of the threat cards needed to add these vicious alien enemies to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dark Beneath the Ice Supplement
description:
The frozen Norselands are already treacherous, but now you can make out something dark - beneath the ice! This does not bode well for your group of heroes.

The 15-card Shadows of Brimstone: Dark Beneath the Ice Supplement includes new Norse Darkness cards, Growing HungerDread cards, and Norseland Encounter cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – Beacon of Doom Supplement
description:
The 15-card A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – Beacon of Doom Supplement is themed around the mysterious lighthouse on the coast near Tidewater and features the Tendrils of the Kraken, the new lighthouse location, and the first appearance of Tidewater town elders!

-description from publisher's website
family: Game: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic

description:
You can purchase the DiceFest 2023 Goody Box only during DiceFest Weekend and only on the FFP Web Store!

Includes:


     A Flooded Cave / A Transporter Room Map Tile with Map Cards
     DiceFest-Exclusive Veteran Huscarl Miniature with “Fallen Titan Helm” Base
     New Team Abilities Pack (3)
     Extended Art Card: Demonic Speed
     Lost Explorer’s Helmet
     Viking Battle Axe
     Targa and Jargono Combo Threats
     DiceFest 2023 Keychain


- Description from the publisher

All elements are for Shadows of Brimstone this year.

Step into the cinematic realm with Play Rewind Eject, a lively tabletop card game that draws its essence from the whimsical schoolyard game of "Kiss, Marry, Kill" intertwined with a social deduction mechanism.

In this game, players find themselves amidst the glamor and drama of the big screen, engaging with movies that have either topped the box office charts or won the hearts of audiences worldwide. Whether a blockbuster aficionado or a casual movie-goer, the familiarity with the featured movies enriches the camaraderie and rivalry as players navigate through the reels of cinematic adventure.

In Play Rewind Eject, players are propelled into a variety of play modes: easy, normal, and tournament, each offering a unique level of challenge and interaction. The heart of the game lies in the strategic deployment of the "Play Rewind Eject" cards coupled with keen social deduction to outwit opponents, all while reminiscing over the iconic films that shape the course of the game.

The gameplay is driven by a deck of cards, each showcasing a movie that either soared in box office revenue or nestled in the hearts of movie lovers. With each draw, players are enticed to make choices, strategize, and delve into discussions, fostering a vibrant atmosphere of collaboration and competition. The game is further enriched by the "Play Rewind Eject" cards that steer the course of action, compelling players to make pivotal decisions that could either spotlight them as the stars of the game or relegate them to mere extras.

Play Rewind Eject is designed to cater to a gathering of up to 8 cinephiles, ensuring an inclusive and engaging experience. The packaging is not just a vessel for the core set but a treasure chest that has room for four additional expansions, promising an ever-evolving cinematic journey.

The Forgotten War is a supplement for the popular Blitzkrieg Commander IV wargames rules, focussing on the Korean War from 1950-1953.

Written by Mark Fry and Rob Anderson, The Forgotten War contains 8 new army lists as well as 6 historical scenarios complete with maps.  New rules include Line Commanders (for Wave Attacks), Street Barricades, Static Smoke Dischargers and Medical Bases.

A copy of Blitzkrieg Commander IV is needed to use this supplement.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Cenobite Thurible and Cenobite Leotard Promo Cards
description:
This is a set of two promo gear cards, the Cenobite Thurible, and the Cenobite Leotard. They were included with the Dark Of Star miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

Cenobite Thurible (Item, Lantern, Other) - Archive a death token: For your next attack this round, use the accuracy, strength, and speed of a dead survivor in your campaign in place of your own.

Cenobite Leotard (Armor, Leather, Metal) - Ignore the frenzy brain trauma.
On arrival, gain a death token for each permanent injury you have recorded.

Gêm Owain Glyndŵr is a board game for 2-4 persons based on Owain Glyndŵr's revolt between 1400-1405. The board itself is based on a map of Wales during the period. The aim of the game is to travel around Wales visiting Welsh and English castles. It's a simple game, suitable for school, family and friends and also for Welsh learners.

boardgame: Hogs of War: The Miniatures Game – Team Lard Expansion
description: Ready to ramp up the fun? Hogs of War: The Miniatures Game – Team Lard Expansion is here to shake things up. Get your hands on a new squad of adorable, detailed piggy soldiers, each ready to battle in their own unique way. Team Lard brings a new set of rules and extra cards, adding unexpected twists to your Hogs of War experience. Just remember, you need the original game to join the hog wild fun with Team Lard. So grab your expansion pack and let the piggy pandemonium commence!

Ubongo Shimo follows the Ubongo formula of everyone trying to solve a puzzle quickly.

" Ubongo Shimo " is the latest family game by Grzegorz Rejchtman, creator of the best-selling "Ubongo" series.
This time, all players will try to arrange a pattern on their cards as quickly as possible so as not to cover the indicated special fields.
Four difficulty levels allow you to adapt the game to the age and experience of players.

Each player gets a set of 10 blocks in the color of their choice.
At the beginning of each round, players reveal new cards (4 difficulty levels are possible). 
The drawn card indicates which blocks that must be used to solve the challenge.
Then, all players simultaneously try to arrange the pattern as quickly as possible so that all white squares are visible through the holes in the blocks.
The first player to complete the pattern calls out "Ubongo" and turns the hourglass.
The rest of the players have one minute to complete their challenge and score a point.

320 puzzles with 4 levels of difficulty

Number of players: 1-4 
Age: 8+ 
Game time: 25 minutes

Game elements:
40 level 1 cards (green cards)
40 level 1 cards (yellow cards)
40 level 3 cards (orange cards)
40 level 4 cards (red cards)
40 blocks (10 per player)
9 gems (3 yellow, 3 green, 3 blue)
1 hourglass (60 seconds)

In Pindua, you change the color of the five suns to earn points by creating patterns in the sky. You do so by manipulating sun cards on the table. Flip them, switch them, or invert them all to match your pattern cards. Take turns with your friends until no pattern cards remain, then count the points you have scored on your completed pattern cards.

If you are good at recognizing patterns on the table and in the gameplay of others, you will rule the sky.

boardgame: Monsters and the Things That Destroy Them Double Pack
description:
[product contains the first two sets, The Dark and The Deep. Also contains bonus card from the recent Kickstarter.]

"It feels like a Love Letter version of Fantasy Realms! A great simple, quick, card game that provides a lot of fun." -Landon Squire, How Lou Sees It

Monsters is as portable and easy-to-learn as Love Letter and Coup. It features bluffing and strategic planning. Players with creativity and deductive reasoning will love it. There's multiple unique sets/cycles of the game, so once you know the rules for one set, you know the rules for all the sets. The game plays best with 2 or 3 players, but supports up to 4 players.

Incident 2: The Deep (mythic sea monsters)

Each Monster grants between 1 and 12 points at the end of the game, but watch out: most Tactical cards ("things") cancel 2 specific Monsters!
 Your turn: Take a card into your hand from the pool of 4; if it's a monster, it wakes up, triggering its ability.
 If you are over your hand limit of 3, discard face-down to your personal discard pile. (Peek at your discards at any time.) 
 When the deck is empty: Everyone discards down to 2 cards and compares their scores to determine the winner.

A Hard Day’s Knight is set in a fantasy medieval world. The player is able to select a knight from one of the four different territories and goes on an epic quest.

The game is played by moving your knight around a game board and visiting the four different territories. Each territory has unique monsters that the knight must defeat to obtain one of the four keys to the Valley of the Gods. Once a knight has required all four keys they may enter the Valley of the Gods to fight the Guardian. Only if the Guardian is defeated a player wins the game and becomes the supreme champion of the land.

To defeat a monster the Knight plays a Battle cards to perform an attack move. The attack moves range in difficulty, with more difficult attacks causing more damage to the Knight’s foe. To calculate if the attack move is successful the player rolls a D12 and if the number is equal or greater than the number on the battle card the knight hits their target. If a knight fails an attack roll, they are hit and will take damage. The knight will also collect gold, magical items and be able to level up abilities to aid them on their quest.

—description from the designer

There are six key colours, Blue. Yellow,Purple,Green, Red, Orange. These colours are repeated on the flags crossing the playing board, the six ship tokens and the six sets of questions and answer cards, There is also a category under the Jolly Roger flag. These are held by the Jolly Roger, a volunteer crew member who must be present at every game, The JOLLY ROGER issues each player a ship token and the matching set of question cards which they will be reading aloud. The Jolly Roger keeps the Black and White Jolly Roger questions for himself and any spare boxes left over as follows:

6 Players   1 box each
5 Players   1 box each +1 spare to Jolly Roger
4 Players   1 box each +2 spare to Jolly Roger
3 Players   2 boxes each
2 Players   3 boxes each

All Boxes of cards must stay on the table at all times during play and the cards remain inside the boxes except when one is selected.
The Jolly Roger will read the questions from any category he holds plus te Jolly Roger questions.
There are 200 questions per category and one hundred Jolly Roger questions.

A MARINER OF THE MONTH CERTIFICATE WILL BE DISPLAYED
SHOWING THE OVERALL WINNER FROM EACH CALENDAR MONTH

The Kraken wakes! Unfortunately for your pirate ship, it has its sights set on a crunchy hull snack. Do you have the wits and luck to defeat it and come away a wealthy victor? Or will you join Davy Jones at the ocean floor?

In this crunchy solo roll-and-write, with a clear win/lose condition, you will roll 4 dice at the beginning of each turn. 2 for your Pirates and 2 for the Kraken. Use your two Pirate Dice to perform actions. Will you Dive for helpful items, Repair your ship, or even Attack the Kraken? Destroy all three tentacles to win and then tally your wealth.

Each turn, after your dice are spent, it's time for the Kraken to Attack! Using the 2 Kraken Dice and polyominos, you'll fill in the two sides of your ship with damage. If you ever run out of space for the shape you need to place, you sink! And the Kraken enjoys its crunchy snack!

—description from the designer

“Ship unmanoeuvrable. We shall fight to the last shell.”
– Vice-Admiral Günther Lütjens

This is a scenario for my ruleset There's The Effing Scheer, and its expansion Striking Effectively First, which allows you to wargame early- to mid-war WW2 naval combat.

I really enjoyed researching, writing and playtesting this scenario. And it is a very challenging scenario for the Kriegsmarine. On the face of it, the tiny German task force should have no chance against the sheer numbers of the Royal Navy.

But then a wargamer's inquisitiveness kicks in... 
Did they need just one lucky hit to win that battle? 
Could they have brushed past a pair of cruisers the day before to prevent being shadowed? 
What if the Germans had tried a different route? 
What if Holland had let the German task force slip past his capital ships while waiting for Tovey to come up? 
What if that Catalina had never spotted Bismarck? 
Or that torpedo hadn't jammed the rudder? 
What if Bismarck had made it into range of its land-based air cover? 
For such a sure thing of a scenario, there were a lot of variables.

The German mission to harry the British convoy routes in mid-1941 was always to be a perilous one, as the quote above illustrates. British intelligence, combined with the substantial strength of the Home Fleet, gave the Royal Navy all of the cards, yet Lütjens and his task force came surprisingly close to success. There are a fair few special rules for this scenario to represent not just the huge scope of this operation (at double the distance scale of my other scenarios) but also to capture the game of cat and mouse the two navies played for several days.

This scenario allows you to play as Tovey, aiming to bring the elusive German warships to heel as the net slowly tightens around them, shepherding your convoys to safety. 
Or maybe you will play as Lütjens, hoping to repeat his earlier success in Operation Berlin by slipping past British task forces and wreaking havoc in the North Atlantic. 
Just don't expect all your ships to make it back.

-description from the designer

The game board represents a pond that is divided into smaller and larger spaces. In the middle of the pond is a small raised plate as an island. The game includes spiders, frogs and flies. The spiders initially use the small island as a place to stay. The large spaces are intended for the frogs, while the spiders and flies are supposed to move on the small spaces. Outside the pond, on the edge, there are four crosses of yellow, green, red and blue colors. These positions are to be assigned to the frogs at the beginning of the game. The small green spaces on the banks of the pond are protective places; frogs cannot penetrate here. Up to four people can play, and each player receives a spider, a frog and four flies. Movement is determined by a die. The spiders may enter the game, when a fly of the same color reaches the space under the spider.
The frogs eat spiders and flies and the spiders eat frogs and flies. The last remaining player wins the game.

The rules include a version without a die.

The game was published about 1940.

—user summary

The Right of Kings is a 2 to 4 player game inspired in the millennial struggle between realms to overpower one another in an endless campaign to find the rightful ruler.  The purpose of the game is to reach the Oreka, the medallion of balance. Every round is a crusade where players thrive to assemble their court, build an army and unveil the right King before any other player.

“In a dive-bombing attack, it’s not just a case of getting one plane or ten or even fifteen. You’ve got to get them all, you can’t afford to get hit.”
– Lieutenant Commander Elias B. Mott, Gunnery Officer for USS Enterprise

This is a scenario for my ruleset There's The Effing Scheer, and its expansion Striking Effectively First, which allows you to wargame early- to mid-war WW2 naval combat.

One of my favourite parts of wargaming are the what-ifs of history and how on the grand scale of things, so much seemingly comes down to luck. The best laid plans and all that. The Eastern Solomons struck me as an interesting engagement because both sides seemed to share an equal amount of luck, or at times a lack of it. It is almost like Coral Sea all over again.

The Americans start unlucky. They send a third of their carrier force away to refuel as their intelligence tells them they've got enough time until the Japanese offensive towards Guadalcanal will arrive. They are unlucky not to find the Japanese fleet carriers as the one accurate scout report is lost in the ether, and both of their strikes are wasted on secondary targets (though I'm sure the crew of Ryujo may feel differently). The Japanese were lucky enough to get the drop of the Americans, getting Shokaku and Zuikaku into strike range without being detected themselves, and getting the first punch in. American radar sends their CAP intercept up at the wrong altitude and the Japanese bombers attack. They strike first, but not effectively.

Then the luck seems to shift. 
Enterprise is hit by three bombs and its steering locks in a circle as the second wave closes in, but then the second Japanese strike fails to find them. The strike from Rabaul turns back due to bad weather and Ryujo's strike fails to neutralise Henderson Field. Nagumo, feeling his work is done, pulls the carrier air cover back, allowing the Cactus Air Force to sink one of their precious transports and parry the invasion. Both sides pull back claiming victory. The Japanese held the field, but any tactical victory seemed phyrric and strategically the Americans still held Henderson Field.

As always, I've added a few special rules to bring this scenario to life, allowing you to step into the shoes of Nagumo and Fletcher as they duel for the third time in 1942. Will Henderson Field fall? Or will the Americans sink the last two carriers who struck Pearl Harbor less than a year before?

-description from the designer

“The most dangerous moment of the war, and the one which caused me the greatest alarm, was when the Japanese fleet was heading for Ceylon and the naval base there. The capture of Ceylon, the consequent control of the Indian Ocean, and the possibility at the same time of a German conquest of Egypt would have closed the ring and the future would have been black.”

– Winston Churchill

This is a scenario for my ruleset There's The Effing Scheer, and its expansion Striking Effectively First, which allows you to wargame early- to mid-war WW2 naval combat.

I have been interested by the what-if nature of the Indian Ocean Raid of 1942 for some time. While researching Coral Sea and Midway for SEF, I was struck by the fact I knew so little about the British capital ship losses (a carrier and two heavy cruisers) just a few months before these two huge, better known clashes. 
What had happened? 
And how had the Kido Butai got out of it unscathed?

After the string of British defeats in the Pacific with Japan's entry to the war, the British were on the back foot. Enter the Eastern Fleet, a motley mix of WW1 battleships, modern aircraft carriers and a jumble of other vessels thrown together to block further Japanese expansion. But it never directly fought the Japanese carriers, instead Somerville chose the far more sensible option of keeping the fleet 'in being' so as to remain a long-term threat. But what if he'd gone another way?

This scenario allows you to play as Somerville, aiming to engage the veteran Kido Butai in favourable night conditions and finally shatter the brittle sword of the Japanese carrier fleet. 
Or perhaps you will play as Nagumo, seeing if you can remove the Royal Navy thorn in your empire's side as it continues to expand in all directions?

-description from the designer

Put your knowledge of Southern Ontario to the test.

Charades, songs and multiple choice questions are on the menu.

Are you ready to discover Ontario's big cities?

On the way down Highway 401 is a classic trivia-type game. First we form 2 teams and appoint a facilitator and the game can begin! We offer 3 game modes, each more entertaining than the last.
 “Classic” mode requires you to accumulate 10 points (or 10 cards) to win. In “Retention” mode, teams have the privilege of eliminating players from the opposing group with each correct answer.

In the party game Taboo, you're trying to give clues to your teammates so that they'll guess a particular word, but you can't say just anything you like. Some clues are off limits!

When you're the active player, hold the deck of cards so that you and the opposing team can see the top card. At the top of the card is the word your teammate must say to score the card, and you can anything you want to help them figure out what to guess other than the word itself (duh!) or the five words/phrases listed on the bottom of the card.

For example, can you get your teammates to say "bacon" without saying "pig", "eggs", "breakfast", "sausage", or "eat"? If you do, you score the card, then move on to the next card, trying to guess as many cards as possible before time runs out. However, if you say a taboo word (or make gestures), the opposing team will buzz a buzzer and score the card themselves.

Taboo Uncensored uses the same gameplay as Taboo, but features more risqué words and phrases.

Two dead people are found at an art gala: How did the Botox doctor and the art hunter die?


     Analyze interrogations
     See deceptively real evidence
     Research online
     Find clues in emails and social media
     Immerse yourself in real investigative work

A music producer is found dead in his studio. What do an aspiring singer, a pushy stalker and an enemy record label have to do with it?


     Analyze interrogations
     See deceptively real evidence
     Research online
     Find clues in emails and social media
     Immerse yourself in real investigative work

A woman disappears on a cruise. What does the crew have to hide?


     Analyze interrogations
     See deceptively real evidence
     Research online
     Find clues in emails and social media
     Immerse yourself in real investigative work

"It hurt so much when she died — but then she came back, and it hurt so much more."

In the solitaire horror game But Then She Came Back, a loved one has returned from the dead. But she's not as she was – parts of her are missing. Changed. Twisted. You need to protect her, while feeding the ravenous ugly thing inside of her – finding her fresh victims. If you come up short, you'll need to sacrifice your friends and family, cutting yourself off from your support network. And if she is still hungry, you'll cut off pieces of yourself – an arm, a leg, your eyes, and finally, when you have nothing left to give, your heart.

Each day, you'll go out to get the things you need: blood, to feed her; knives, to protect her; and locks, to protect yourself from her. Each day, you'll lose a piece of her as the thing inside her continues to grow and hunger – demanding more and more from you, even as you're able to do less and less. Desperation gives way to despair as the promises you made when she was alive threaten to swallow you up.

The game will end in one of two ways: either you'll die, or you'll let her down. And you don't know which is worse, and which is kinder. And you pretend you don't know which one you'd prefer.

This is a game about horror and heartbreak, grief and exhaustion. It is for mature audiences.

Altar is a card game where two players face off using a 40-card deck selected from an online database. Each deck can contain up to 3 copies of the same card, unless specified otherwise.

In Altar, each player embodies an Infanteer, a being capable of materializing the energy of the Dream Plane in the real world through Altars, powerful machines of unknown technology created by a forgotten civilization. The Dreams (represented by cards) generated by the Infanteers belong to one of six different Pulses: Order, Chaos, Truth, Lies, Life, and Death. The Pulses can be seen as fundamental ways of understanding the surrounding world. Each Pulse has its own identity and primary strategy to win a game. Truth seeks to triumph through cooperation and territorial control; Chaos is willing to sacrifice its own forces to lead to the opponent's loss; Death manipulates the flow of life to destroy the opponent's resources; Life is a quiet force that focuses on strengthening its allies; Lies uses trickery and traps to gain the upper hand; Order loves... to maintain order at any cost. A deck can contain cards from any number of Pulses.

One after the other during their turn, each player places cards from their hand on the field and activates their abilities to eliminate cards from the opponent's deck. A player automatically loses the game as soon as their deck no longer contains any cards.

—description from the publisher

The Grave Danger expansion introduces the threat of an undead minion, and the fun of charades to the Which Craft? hidden role game.
Unearth a whole new level of fun with 4 new characters, a new action card, and a cursed item card!

Campus Paths is a strategic simulation of university life. Players take on the role of students, juggling academics, social life, work, and wellness, while acquiring skills and filling their curriculum vitae with experiences as they prepare for their careers after graduation. A collaboration between Resolve, an applied game design research group at UNB and UNB's Career Development and Employment Centre, the game was designed by Scott Preston with consultation from career counsellor Nat Perry (M.Ed., C.C.C.).

The game takes place over four rounds (one for each year of a typical undergraduate degree). Players take turns drawing from a deck of action cards and choosing one to play. How many they draw depends on their current wellness level. The cards depict activities that a typical undergraduate student might do in college (join a student club, meet with their academic advisor, run for student council, etc.) Their choices impact four key areas (academics, wellness, curriculum vitae, and cash flow), all of which are tracked on a player sheet. At the end of each round, players may receive scholarships or summer jobs, depending the strength of the academics or the C.V. Then they pay for the next year's tuition and another round begins.

Included is a Facilitator’s Guide that contains suggestions for how to run a session and tips for generating a productive post-game discussion.

—description from the designer

The game is composed of two decks of cards: one deck of sixty Action cards and one of forty Location cards. Each Location card has three of five possible symbols: Valour, Strength, Courage, Wisdom, or Spirit. Each Action Card has two random symbols on it. The deck of Location Cards is placed face down in the center of the table and the top three cards from it are laid face-up in a row. This is called the ‘Display’. The Action deck is placed face down nearby as a draw pile and the top card is turned face-up beside it to start the Discard pile.

Each player is dealt three Location Cards, which they place in a row face-up in front of them. This is their ‘Array’. The player is then dealt three Action Cards, which they keep hidden as part of their hand. At the start of their turn, each player either draws the top card from the Action deck or takes the top card from the face-up Discard pile, unless it is a Special card. Players may now do one of three things: 1) Play an Action Card from their hand to match one of the symbols on any of the Location Cards in their Array, or play a Special Action card from their hand and follow the instruction text; or 2) Re-arrange Action Cards which are already matched to Location Cards; or 3) Discard all your Action or Location Cards and draw three new ones. The player’s turn has now ended.

At the end of their turn, the player must have three Location Cards in their Array, and three Action Cards in their hand. They must draw or discard as necessary, and also make sure there is at least one face-up card in the Action discard pile. If the Action Card deck runs out, the discard pile is turned over to form the new one, then the top card is turned face-up to form the new discard pile.

When all three symbols on one of a player’s Location Cards have been matched, the player declares it ‘Completed’ and places it face-down in their ‘Completion Pile’. They then draw a new Location Card from the Display and add it to their Array, and turn up a new card and add it to the Display. When a player has completed six Location Cards, they are the winner, unless the players agree beforehand to play to eight.

Cupset is an unpredictable four player strategy game filled with upsets and underdogs!

Each player tries to eliminate their opponents' pieces by engaging in battle, winning, and stacking on top of them and becoming the Top Dog!

Each player starts with ten cups. Each turn you may choose to move around the board or declare a fight with an opponent's adjacent piece. The winner of the battle stacks on top of the loser, eliminating their piece and growing their own stack.

To battle, the attacker rolls the die to determine the winner, but the odds on the die change according to the size of the cup stacks. Even stacks have even odds, but taller stacks have better odds, with the Top Dog stack having the best odds of all! But watch out, even the smallest stack can upset the odds and become the new Top Dog!
 But that's not all! Each player controls two secret "underdog" pieces that can quickly change the course of the game!

The last surviving player wins the game and reigns as Top Dog of the yard!

—description from the designer

This was an expansion that set the conflict in the city of Tokyo.  The map was inside the magazine TACTICS no. 58 (September 1988).  Instructions were on the back of the map.

The Japanese Arkham Horror game had the extra counters necessary to play this.

The Game of Clock is a rather obscure Australian game that was meant to entertain and also teach children about the time. It is essentially a race game using a spinner and moving the hands of the clock

The game board has two clock faces with brass hands. Underneath each clock is a spinner with 3 inner circles of numbers. A player spins the spinner, then adds the numbers to give a count that moves the minute hand on their clock face.

The length of the game is predetermined before starting. i.e From 12 O'Clock to 3 O'Clock or 180 minutes. The winner is the first to reach the set time.

The game also provides facts about time, seasons (Southern Hemisphere), and celestial movements.

In 55 and 54 BC, Julius Caesar landed in the south of the island of Britain, then known as Britannia. This was the first contact between Rome and the various British kingdoms. Numerous kings rallied to Caesar to survive, or to benefit from Rome's protection against the hostility of other British tribes: these were the first client kingdoms. This system would develop over the next century, so that by the time of Claudius' Roman invasion in AD 43, many client kingdoms were already well established.

Among these were the Regnenses, Brigantes and Iceni, who fought mercilessly for power. With Rome's support, they all tried to obtain the title of King of the British, in Latin: REX BRITANNORUM.

Rex Britannorum is a strategy game that can be played solo, with 2 or 3 players. Victory is achieved by occupying roads and cities in the different regions of Britannia displayed on the game board. Moreover, each player will have two personal objectives to complete, which will earn them additional victory points.

Commit your troops in the right place and at the right time to outplay your opponents. Resolution of battles is not random, so use your sense of strategy wisely to become the Rex Britannorum.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Liven up a party with friends with this murder mystery party game that will take you casually through an evening or even a whole weekend! A crime has been committed in the peaceful town of Bumble Town, where the famous Sherlock Holmes spends his retirement. To get to the bottom of the crime, you should collect as many clues as possible. But you can only get them from your fellow players by enticing them to do certain secret tasks for you!

BattleTech Adventures: Ghosts of Obeedah takes players into a dangerous hunt for one of the last great secrets of the Jihad. From ground zero of a lethal pandemic to the wreckage of long-fallen societies, join the search between the “safer” havens of a universe at war, and find out what happened to the fanatical monsters whose deadly legacy still haunts the Inner Sphere to this very day. This adventure includes biographies on several key personalities, special rules, and adventure Tracks designed for use with BattleTech, A Time of War: The BattleTech Role-Playing Game, A Time of War Companion, and MechWarrior: Destiny, as well as data and record sheets for key worlds, units, and non-player characters unique to this story.

—description from publisher

Gangstar consists of 45 cards with the roles of Gangster, Gang Boss, Cops and Joe. Depending on the number of players, all cards are distributed and held face down in the hand.

In clockwise order, 1 to 3 cards of the gangster type or 1 card of the gang boss type are placed face down on the table and played to any player. The next player must then react as follows:


    When receiving Gangster, he must play back the same color and number. If the player does not have the color or has fewer than the required number, nothing happens. 
    When receiving a gang leader, the player must play back all cards of the same suit. If he does not have any of the gang leader's colors, he can keep the card. 


There is also a special card "joe" which acts as a log bird to confuse other players.

Then the game continues. If you have a gang consisting of a boss and five members of the same color, you can play them in front of you.

The aim of the game is to have as many gangs as possible in front of you.

Crowdfunded football card game that tests your knowledge against the clock.

Draw a card for the subject (i.e. players who have won the world cup) then draw a rime card (30 seconds). Players then 'bid' in increments for their knowledge ('I can name 6') until challenged by an opponent. If the player successfuly meets their total within the time, they win the point. If they fail, the challenger takes the point instead.

boardgame: Hidden Games Hangover: Express in den Tod
description:
On the way to a family event in a luxury train, the conductor finds a corpse at night. The travel company claims to remember nothing. There are only a few hours left until arrival to determine the course of events and identify a murderer.

Players take on a character and find and discuss motives and alibis. They use the browser-based app to immerse themselves in the action and to cooperate with the others and recap the events.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Auf dem Weg zu einen Familienevent in einen Luxuszug findert der Schaffner in der Nacht eine Leiche. Die Reisegesellschaft behauptet sich an nichts erinnern zu können. Es verbleiben wenige Stunden bis zur Ankunft um den Tathergang zu ermitteln und einen Mörder zu identifizieren.

Die Spieler übernehmen eines Charakters und finden und diskutiert Motive und Alibis. Dabei nutzen sie die browserbasierte App, um ins Geschehen einzutauchen und mit den anderen zu kooperieren und die Geschehnisse zu rekapitulieren.

—description from the publisher

In March 1939, the small, newly-created nation of Slovakia declared its independence from Czechoslovakia and immediately became a satellite of Nazi Germany. Slovak troops invaded Ukraine in 1941 and fought alongside the Germans for five years as the most minor of allies. The puppet government began mobilizing reservists just four days before the German invasion, and sent its troops over the border six days later. Those soldiers proved far less enthusiastic about the adventure than their proto-fascist leadership. Slovak formations performed poorly for the most part, with just one notable success.

Puppet Brigade is a Campaign Study for the Panzer Grenadier series, focused on the battles of the Slovak Army’s Mobile Brigade. There are nine scenarios plus the brigade’s background story, and 88 die-cut-and-mounted playing pieces. This is not a complete game; you’ll need Fire in the Steppe and Broken Axis to play all of the scenarios.

Puppet Brigade is a partial reprint of our long out-of-print Slovakia’s War expansion, with just the first chapter from that book and the 88 pieces that first appeared in First Axis.

—description from the publisher

For their June 1943 offensive at Kursk, the German high command bet a lot of its chips on the Grossdeutschland Panzer Grenadier Division. It had more tanks than any of the Regular Army panzer divisions, plus additional weapons from machine guns to Tiger heavy tanks, and additional assault guns and other battalions. Add to that enormous combat experience and unit cohesion (thanks to special perks for Grossdeutschland soldiers – who, for example, could not be sent to a different unit after a hospital stay, or seized by military police to join an “emergency” unit). On top of all that, the 200 Panther tanks provided for the Kursk operation all went to this one division.

Grossdeutschland at Kursk is a Panzer Grenadier Campaign Study, with 11 new scenarios based on the actions of the German Grossdeutschland Division during the Kursk campaign. There are two chapters, each with a battle game to link the scenarios together. You’ll need Kursk: Burning Tigers, Broken Axis, and Eastern Front Artillery to play all of the scenarios.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rap Battles: The Hip-Hop Rhyming Word Game for Wannabe MCs
description:
Mad Libs meets Method Man, Missy Elliot, and more with this hilarious word game to test rhyme skills, pun abilities and quick wits. It’s the perfect gift for all hip-hop fans, aspiring MCs and street poets.

Channel your inner MC with this quick-fire word game designed to test your rhyme skills against family and friends, settling once and for all who is the greatest rapper.

Fast, furious, hilariously funny, and endlessly playable, Rap Battles is the essential card game for any aspiring MCs, wordsmiths and hip-hop fans, playable with up to 6 individuals or groups. The challenge: to come up with that killer burn, pun or rhyme to leave your opponent without a come back, in stitches, or both.

—description from the publisher

Stay Low is the perfect card game for anyone who loves a good challenge. The objective of the game is to avoid accumulating points by discarding cards from your tableau of 4x3 cards (ten of which are face down at the start of the round).

Each card is numbered from -2 to 12, and on your turn you can do one of two things: take the top card from the discard pile, exchanging it face up with a card from your display, OR take the top card from the face down draw pile. If you do the latter, you can either exchange the card face up with one card from your display, or you can discard it and turn over one of your hidden cards.

The round ends when one player has revealed all their cards, at which point all other players turn over their remaining cards. All the numbers on the cards are added up for scoring. If the player who triggered the end of the round does not have the fewest points, then they must double their score. The game ends after one player has 100 or more points. Whoever has the lowest score wins.

—description from the publisher

Duke it out as root beer swilling gnomes in this raucous tavern showdown!

Tavern Tumult is a 4-player fighting game set in a whimsical tavern full of rowdy, tough-as-nails, root beer swilling, gnomes. Weather the barrage of tiny fists, dish out some damage of your own, and don't forget to raise a frothy mug and toast to friendly carnage!

The goal of the game is to be the last gnome standing. You accomplish this by moving around the board, drawing/playing action cards, and finding root beer barrels. If you run out of health points at any time during the game, you have been eliminated. Remove your gnome from the board and decide which one of the remaining players you're going to root for.

Play until your "Wits End"

The game is played by drawing cards and swapping them with the cards in your tableau. The goal is to get the fewest points at the end of the game. The game ends when After the eighth round any player declares that they are done or the draw pile is exhausted. The points are tallied and the player with the lowest total wins.

Obtain the LOWEST point total by one of SIX scoring methods.

Setup

Players are each dealt 8 cards. 4 face down (no peeking) and four face up. The remaining cards are placed face down as the draw pile. One card is turned over to start the discard pile.

NEVER LOOK AT THE FACE DOWN CARDS

There are four suits. Blue, green, red and yellow.

Play

The player to the left of the dealer starts.

On your turn you MUST draw the top card from either the draw pile or the discard pile.  You may replace one of your cards with the card that you drew.

Play continues counter clockwise for a minimum of 8 turns. After 8 turns any player on their turn can vocalize "WITS END".  The other players have one more turn  to try to reduce their points total.

Scoring. (The important part)

1 regular points
2 all color bonus
3 all star bonus
4 four or more bonus
5 split color bonus
6 straight shot bonus

Over 3 rounds, players will face off in this light strategy card fighting game. Determining their strategy for the fight, they will take a limited number of cards from their match strategy (deck) into each round. Revealing cards simultaneously one at a time each player will endeavour to land heavy hits on their opponent whilst blocking anticipated incoming hits. Bonuses can be gained by stringing together combos of cards, but is that what your opponent is trying to do or are they bluffing, hoping you will try to block while opening yourself up to taking extra damage from a different attack?

At the end of each round players return to their corners to be sponged down & discuss new tactics. Two cards used in the previous round are discarded from the game and a new hand built from the remaining cards in their deck before the fighters square off again. Get knocked to the canvas and you will need to roll the knockdown dice to see if you recover and regain your feet before the 10 count is reached. It gets progressively harder to stand after each knockdown, and hitting the canvas for a fourth time means you have lost on a TKO.

Easy to learn & quick to teach. New players will be off & fighting each other in no time, & the more they play the more they'll begin second guessing their opponent, acting out the moves, & talking in an 'announcer' voice.

So will you be the heavyweight champion or are you nothing but a lightweight?

—description from the designer

When you thought you've seen it all, something like this comes along. Zombies!!! has spawned many different games with creatures that go bump in the night, and now they are all in one place: The Museum of Weird. But the creatures aren't the only strange things at this museum. You're in for the ride of your life!

Zombies!!!: Museum of Weird takes you on a journey through 20+ years of Twilight Creations' games. Visit the different rooms, fight the zombies that have been waiting for you, and hopefully make it out alive — if you're lucky! Movement is a challenge here...

Pletrix: Rainbow is a box of expansions from the Kickstarter campaign. It includes:

Rock Die - Turns the rolled shape into stone. The shape cannot be drawn that turn.

Forbidden Die - Prohibits the use of the color rolled. All dice matching the color cannot be used that turn.

Twister Die - Grants new special abilities based on the die roll.

Vision Die - Adds an additional dice mitigation element into the game.

Additional Settings - More game sheets with new panel shapes and rules. Including: Rainbow, Pipeline, Mega, Retro, MegaRainbow, Seventy and a Solo Mode for all expansion settings.

https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/smazziescazzi/smazziandscazzi?ref=bl3xnr
>>> LIVE ON KICKSTARTER NOW! <<<

Roll a 365 dice and draw an ordinary day. You are having a beer with your gang of friends in the little village bar. In that little hole of a world, even the little grandson who comes in to get sweets for his grandmother has a history of mischief as thick as the sole of a Buffalo. You are playing trumps and the only winner is boredom, but suddenly (the) Noodle(s)? Tagliatella - your brother's friend - comes in, and with him the mathematical breakthrough! You're already up on your feet, about to shout "Yo bro'!" when that ridiculous ringtone for your aunt goes off from your smartphone at a criminal volume. 
"HI DEAR, IT'S GRANDMA(AUNTY?! GO AND GET ME SOME CREAM FOR SORES AT THE CHEMIST'S, I'M ALL STUCK…"

Smazzi & Scazzi is an alternative to pick up and deliver games, a gateway with ruthless overturns and comically irreverent overtones, set in the worst squares of suburban towns where a micro-world of crazy characters with borderline habits live in captivity. Each player has errands to get out of the way before they can return to the bar as the winner. In order to do away with the errands, they can either actually run them or pawn them off on another player!
Each errand (described by the TROUBLE (DODGE) cards) is a piece in a mosaic of interwoven stories. Each character who gives you assignments will have something to tell, short stories of escape from everyday life, great adventures or petty crimes; recipes of dubious taste, but also indestructible book odysseys. A game within a game or a comic within a game, which you can discover game by game or flick through card by card.
 
To add even more spice to the matter, there is the contribution of an exceptional illustrator, the one and only person capable of giving volume to this human circus, Enrico Macchiavello, a Genoese artist with an immediately recognisable style, author of numerous works that for years have filled the happiest memories of all Italians (among the most important we recall the magnificent animations for the Ceres adverts, Skifidol, the album covers for Meganoids and Fragments, the comic 'Little Boy Goes to Hell’ and the more recent Zoombi). If you too want to do as the Italian General Investigations & Special Operations Police Division and follow him, you would do very well!

—description from the designer

In Northerners, players become jarls, leading their Viking clans into the untamed lands of Westfoldir—a place of rich resources and opportunity, but also fierce competition. After harsh winters and dwindling resources in their homelands, four powerful jarls arrive, each determined to claim supremacy over these new lands. In this strategic 4X game (explore, expand, exploit, exterminate), players must balance resource gathering, village building, and tactical combat, all while keeping a wary eye on their rivals and seeking the favor of the gods.

With a dynamic map that expands as players explore, Northerners offers a constantly shifting landscape of alliances, territory grabs, and sudden confrontations. Each jarl can recruit warriors, gather workers, and construct buildings to strengthen their clan's hold on the land. But, to achieve victory, jarls must also navigate the whims of the Aesir—the Norse gods—who can bless their efforts or hinder them with challenges. Quest cards and blessing cards add layers of strategy, offering unique opportunities for players to gain advantages or disrupt their opponents’ plans.

Victory requires cunning, resilience, and a deep understanding of the Viking way: defend what’s yours, honor the gods, and never back down from a fight. Will you rise as the supreme jarl, or will your ambitions be buried in the cold soil of Westfoldir? Northerners is a game of survival, strategy, and Norse myth brought to life, offering high replayability through its rich mix of exploration, resource management, and tactical warfare.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dixit: Disney Edition – Chip 'n Dale Promo Card
description:
Included in Dixit: Disney Edition – Promo Cards, but it was released/given out as a single card at conventions like Spiel Essen 2023.

Promo card for the game Dixit: Disney Edition.

boardgame: Dixit: Disney Edition – Princess Dress Promo Card
description:
Included in Dixit: Disney Edition – Promo Cards, but it was released/given out as a single card at conventions like Spiel Essen 2023.

Promo card for the game Dixit: Disney Edition.

boardgame: Dixit: Disney Edition – Villain Promo Card
description:
Included in Dixit: Disney Edition – Promo Cards, but it was released/given out as a single card at conventions like Spiel Essen 2023.

Promo card for the game Dixit: Disney Edition.

boardgame: Dixit: Disney Edition – Olaf Promo Card
description:
Included in Dixit: Disney Edition – Promo Cards, but it was released/given out as a single card at conventions like Spiel Essen 2023.

Promo card for the game Dixit: Disney Edition.

In war, War? WAR!! Choose to lead 1 of 4 warring factions determined on conquering the lands of Elohssa, destroy and outwit your enemies by simply playing the highest card, easy... right?

Players choose between 4 mighty factions each with their own  unique characters and land abilities and assemble their hand of army and royalty cards.  By simply playing 1 card face-down and revealing simultaneously, the strongest value card survives and scores victory skulls - the lowest value is also rewarded with a powerful seal. These seals allow players to sneak a look at other cards before they reveal, swap cards, increase and decrease the strength of any card to name a few.

war, War? WAR!! What is it good for? Absolutely fun!! Pick up and play, a quick 2 to 4 player travel sized game with more meat on it's bones than first meets the eye.

—description from the publisher

Attention, brave souls, for you are about to enter the realm of darkness and enigma - "Dark Forest." This board game will immerse you in the depths of despair and secrets, in a place where no glimmer of light has ever penetrated.

The outside world has vanished, the village is no more, and hope has dwindled. You must step into the abyss of this ominous forest and unveil its horrifying mysteries. Four brave individuals, each with their own dark pasts, find themselves brought together in a chilling tavern. They don't know if they are chosen or mere pawns in this grim game of destiny.

Among them, an old mage who reminisces his ancient secrets in the darkness. A former professor of the College of Mages, whose insatiable thirst for knowledge has left only a bitter void. A warrior who has recently returned from a bloody conflict, his soul scarred by battle for the king. A young huntress, trading carcasses and bringing them to the local tavern, where she has found her refuge. The tavern keeper, a former herbalist, whose connection to this place is as mysterious as the Dark Forest itself.

In the Dark Forest, every turn is a step into the abyss, where darkness clouds the mind, and only chance can shed light on the path. Players explore locations, revealing cards that may hold treasures or horrors on their reverse sides. They might stumble upon quests, where choices will determine who remains in darkness and who attempts to unravel the bleak mysteries.

The game is steeped in darkness and tension, where every decision is a choice between life and death, between discovering the truth and delving deeper into a chasm of lies and secrets. In The Dark Forest, your soul is at stake, and only the bravest will emerge from it alive, undeniably changed.

—description from the designer

This is the football board game you have been waiting for! Challenge your football knowledge, gather your friends and family, form teams, and prepare for a game that will bring joy and knowledge.

Quiz d’Or is a tailored experience for football fans of all knowledge levels. Dive into the rich history and beauty of the game, as you navigate trivia questions and choose the right strategy to beat your opponents.

If you’re a fan of the sport , this game promises to ignite your passion for the beautiful game and keep you entertained for hours

Quiz d’Or promises an engaging experience, it's a celebration of the game that brings people together, fostering camaraderie and building lasting memories.

—description from the publisher

Explore a dark, unsettling world and battle your opponents in this turn-based strategic board game in which you take on the role of a leader of a sinister secret society. Your objective is no less than to take over the world! Peppered with biting black humor, Comploty is the world's first board game based on conspiracy theories that actually exist.

You'll get to know more than one hundred conspiracy theories, from investigative revelations to creative pipe dreams to totally whacked-out and red-hot dangerous narratives, from the QAnon movement to chemtrails to the secret Nazi stronghold in Antarctica. Look forward to the most important facts, the most curious claims, and the funniest anecdotes from the fascinating, dark, and bizarre world of conspiracy myths. They will be conveyed to you in a nutshell whenever you draw a Factcheck card.

Throughout the game, you'll recruit the most ruthless henchmen and occupy the most important key positions in research, economy, media and politics with your allies. You'll stock up on secret weapons and sabotage your opponent's plans with attacks and other nasty means. You'll forge devious conspiracy plots of enormous proportions that, if successful, can decide everything at once. You'll acquire psychic abilities with which you'll be able to manipulate your own or your opponents' moves.

Of course, you'll also have to cope with one or another setback when the investigative journalists and mainstream scientists get on your nerves with annoying fact checks or your opponents slow you down with acts of sabotage. The last player standing after this thrilling showdown wins!
