As you walk through the cities of Colombia, it is possible to see street food stalls (fritanguerías) run by very friendly and warm people, everywhere. Their food is very varied and with very traditional products. Suchicharrón! portrays the experiences of this people with a fun competition.

In Suchicharrón! The players are this fritangueros from the neighbourhood's famous Fritanga stand, run by Doña Gloria. Doña Gloria have decided to retire and through this competition it will be decided who will be the new owner of her stand.

It is a game for 2 to 4 players where they will have to heat the fritangas cards in their grill, add sauces, steal the fritangas from the other players to score the 10 cards at their showcase before the others to close the game.

The player with the most points at the end of the game will be the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Adventure Games: Books – Die Wölfe von Paris
description:
The second book in the successful Adventure Games series; one book with several possible outcomes and over 100 different  options.

Jeanne Portefaix is on a dangerous mission in Paris, in 1789 and 2019. She was unable to complete her training as a timekeeper because her academy was destroyed. In the 21st century, Jeanne wants to solve and avenge her father's murder. A mysterious wolf eye artifact at an auction in the Louvre is her first clue, but then men wearing wolf masks attack. This is the start of a turbulent adventure in which many more secrets await.

Choose your own adventure in this book: investigate game locations, solve puzzles, find and combine items. An interactive reading experience!

The Divine Girl calls to us for help! But not only the vikings heed her call - strange, dark hunters reach the shores of Thor's Cliff!
Thors Cliff is an area-controll/skirmish/pick up & deliver game set in the Vikings Ages.
The goal is to either collect three red or blue crystals to receive the blessing of the divine Girl, the daughter of Thor.

boardgame: All Hallows' Eve: Crusty Ray Monster Expansion
description:
Clean up the trash with Crusty Ray, a monster that utilizes the discard pile to unleash dirty tricks and bring terror to the streets of All Hallows’ Eve. Soon no one will be able to forget trash day!

All Hallows' Eve: Crusty Ray Monster Expansion includes a 28-card monster deck and miniature.

boardgame: All Hallows' Eve: Trick or Treater Expansion
description:
Four new Trick or Treaters hit the streets each with new abilities to outsmart the Monsters!

Includes 4 Trick or Treater figures, 4 cards, 4 candy tokens and 1 lights out token

boardgame: All Hallows' Eve: Skirmish Costume Set
description: The Trick or Treaters aren’t the only ones dressing up this year! With the Skirmish Costume pack you can hit the leaf strewn streets with your Fighters from Skirmish in All Hallows' Eve! They pack a punch and are sure to give the Monsters a frightening night!

Mines of Dazzak'Oda is a game about Dwarves returning to their ancestral homeland to delve into long forgotten mines in search of riches and glory!

Players choose 1 of 6 Heroes, each hero having a unique ability.
At the start of their turn, players draw cards that represent adventuring gear (Axes, Pickaxes, etc.) Once their Gear Cards are drawn, they delve into the mine by drawing from the Mine Deck.

In the Mine, players can encounter monsters and resources, as well as special events. Events can be helpful or detrimental to the player, and some events allow direct player interaction.

The goal of the game is to use your Gear Cards to make your way through the mine, earning Gold Coins by collecting resources, all while avoiding danger! At the end of the game, the player with most gold coins wins!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Dystopian Wars: Fortune and Glory – 2 Player Starter Set
description:
The Great Powers vie for control of the world's trade routes, unleashing mercenary forces to disrupt shipping and sow chaos and confusion to destabilise their rivals. Two of the most notable of these privateers are The Crimson League and The Honorable Eclipse Company.

The Crimson League is led by Princess Scheherazade. Escaping the traditions of her royal family, she joined the Covenant of the Enlightened only to leave when the Egalitarian and Custodians schism came to the fore. Now, she operates following her own unknowable plan. Angry at the power of the Order within the Sultanate, she lashes out, taking contracts that appear to have nothing to do with the religious zealots, only for some to realise later that there was design all along.

The Honorable Eclipse Company have secured lucrative escort contracts with significant clients around the globe. The HEC offer security and defence solutions to those who cannot apply direct political or military action in safeguarding their investments. The HEC have had major investment from the Union of Federated States and often that Great Power has used the Company when the deployment or involvement of a Union battlefleet may be politically unwise. Though some might call the involvement of the HEC an unwelcome influence by the Union in global affairs, in truth, many commanders have found that their need for support in a theatre of engagement can only be solved by hiring the Honorable Eclipse Company.

Fortune and Glory contains two mercenary battlefleets that can be used with any Faction making this box set a must-buy for any Dystopian Wars player. Add additional airpower to your existing forces, but make sure you pay them or you could be their next contract!

Contents:

1x Excelsior Class Heavy Aircruiser. (May also be built as Venture or Custodian Class)
    3x Steward Class Sentry Airships. (May also be built as Constellation, Republic, Ticonderoga or Ranger Class)
    5x Bogota Class Carryall
    5x Union SRS Tokens
    1x Lyceum Aerial Dreadnought
    3x Nasr Class Skyship. (May also be built as Awsbiri or Muharib Class)
    3x Hirka Class Skycutter
    3x Alsaqr Class Skybarque
    3 x Sultanate SRS Tokens
    2x Titan Class Heavy Conveyer. (May be built as Passenger, Freight or Oil Variants)
    2x Merchant Ships
    2x Escort
    2x Small Ship Tokens
    2x Ground Assault Detachment Tokens

-description from publisher's website

In World Explorer the Boardgame, players try to score points by carrying out classic expeditions, make scientific discoveries or simply travel to iconic locations around the world. In order to do any of those things each player needs to gather resources of different kinds, numbers, and combinations. Resources for expeditions and scientific research includes knowledge & ability, bravery, and financing. To travel you only need the combined resources of an airplane ticket and spending-money. The resources are collected by rolling the two dice. The more combined dots on the dice, the more resources you get to collect.
The game includes a board with a map of the world, seven types of cards (the various resources, the different assignments, "change of circumstance-cards") and game-pieces to mark the explored sections. The cards are sorted in to the seven different categories, shuffled and placed beside the board with the backside up. The players therefore don’t know for instance what particular resource, expedition, travel-destination etc they are going to collect, just the type of card. To determine who gets to start the game, the dice are rolled. The player with the highest combined dots on the dice becomes start player and the rest follow clockwise. Based on the number of dots in this first dice roll all players also get to collect resources or buy any other type of card or combination of cards that the number of combined dots will allow. And then the game begins.
On each turn the active player starts by rolling the dice. As in the starting round the player then gets to buy resources or other cards, based on the number of combined dots. The cards vary in cost between 3-8 dots. This means that if a player rolls a 2 combined, he/she doesn't get to collect any cards, but can still take other kinds of action in the turn, if possible. If a player rolls a 7, he/she are not allowed to collect any cards and are also forbidden to act in any other way in the actual turn. In this case all other players get to collect a card with a max value of 4. On each turn the active player, besides from rolling the dice and collecting resources or other cards, can carry out an expedition, make a scientific discovery or travel to a location (or any two or three of these actions combined), if the player has collected the proper cards and resources to do so. The player can also play a "changed circumstance-card", to either gain personal advantages or create trouble for the other players. The active player can also initiate trading with one or more of the other players. When the player has done all he/she can or wants to do, the turn goes on to the next player.
The game is typically divided in to three phases. In the first phase the players often gather resources and other cards to keep the number of possibilities of strategic choices for the rest of the game open. In the second phase the exploring gets more intense as the players start using their collected cards more actively. The points start to roll in and the map gets filled with game-pieces. In the third phase - the "end game" - players often tries to score easy points by travelling or to collect some of the extra points, in order to gain the final necessary points to win. At this stage many of the "changed circumstance-cards" are likely to have been played, which often makes it more difficult for one or more of the players. Points are scored by completing the expeditions, scientific discoveries or by travelling. The points scored on the expeditions vary based on difficulty - the harder to achieve the more points a player scores. And as in the old days of exploration you must get there first, as there are two each of the expedition cards! Any player that manages to make scientific discoveries in all the four different areas gets 5 extra points. Likewise, any player who manages to travel to all five continents gets 5 extra points. The game ends when a player has gathered 40 points.

Dragon Dell is a fantasy themed strategy card game where players compete to be the first to establish a small village in a land teeming with hostile dragons. Players will face off waves of wild Dragons (and possibly dragons sent by other players), as they fight to grow their village.

The game is played over a series of rounds, which feature simultaneous play.  During a round, players will draw magical Orbs and combine some into stronger Orbs. All players then do the same with dragons (draw dragons and breed some into stronger dragons). Next, players will utilize command cards to decide in secret if they will go into battle and what their dragons will do (help them in battle, or go attack other players). All commands are then revealed at the same time.

Finally, all players who chose to go to battle will do so, each fighting their own battle. They will utilize the orbs and dragons they gathered earlier, along with their characters' special abilities, to fight off the wave of dragons attacking them (along with dragons sent by other players).

Each player who wins their battle will gain a new character in their village, but will also face a larger wave of Dragons in the next battle. The first player to get six characters and defeat the sixth wave is the winner.

—description from the designer

Transformers Deck-Building Game: Bonus Pack 6 contains five standard cards (Courage/Starters: Groove, Blades, Streetwise, First Aid, Hot Spot), as well as one giant card and standee (Defensor [Combiner]).

Die fliegenden Holzschuhe (German; "The flying Clogs"), is a tactical level coop wargame for 1 to 3 players set in the second world war. You are in command of the catapult tender ship "Bussard" (German; "Buzzard") and her three BV-138 float planes. Your tasks involve, but are not limited to: 

     sea rescue
     reconnaissance and counter-reconnaissance
     troop transport and evacuation
     mine searching and laying
     bombing runs
     submarine hunting


The game is played on a hex map with each hex representing 200km. Each round, representing roughly an hour, you will move your aircraft around the map in search for radio signals or other suspicious hints to find your objective of the day.

But don't take these tasks easy. Enemy long range reconnaissance aircraft, escort vessels, and bad weather will show you that this work above the hostile arctic ocean is only for the most foolhardy or foolish men.

If you encounter enemy forces, the game will require you to make a check to evade enemy fire. After that it is on you to decide to either counter attack, or get the hell off. Every time you fail to evade, a random system on your aircraft will be damaged, and after a second hit be destroyed. This includes your precious equipment, like radar, radio, and bomb guidance systems, added weaponry, and any passengers you picked up on your sortie.

This game can either be played in single scenarios, as a historically linked campaign, or a randomly linked campaign. Campaigns offer a ascending difficulty, as well as upgrades for your vehicles and crewmen.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Caterpillars is a simple three dice roll-and-write game in which players have to assign points to their three caterpillars. In the end, the lowest and highest scoring caterpillar are eliminated and the median caterpillar determines the final score.

Players roll three dice, and choose which die or dice indicate the square to write the remaining dice points in. Three times during the game, a player may set aside a die roll for use in a later round.

—description from the publisher

Chase is a creative strategy game played on a chess board where players restrict the movement of their opponent in order to trap them and win the game. They must be careful, however, not to trap themselves in the process.

The objective of Chase is to jump, block, and taunt your opponent, and collect fees, so that their pawn(s) no longer have freedom of movement.

The game ends when an opponent has no more legal moves remaining, allowing the other player to win the game.

Qo is a 'creative strategy' game played on a game board with an 8x8 grid where players remove the lodestones of their opponent to keep the game in balance. It uses the new DiCe game mechanic where every move you make also benefits your opponent (and every move you make against them can also work against you).

Qo can be considered a di-centralized version of Go, though with necessarily different rules and gameplay.

The objective of Qo is to keep the game in balance by removing blocked lodestones until the game ends. The game ends when either opponent runs out of lodestones or when either opponent has formed a complete horizontal or vertical line with their lodestones.

Qo has just 3 simple rules. Yet, during gameplay a paradox of elegance forms when players realize the beauty of what is happening on the board. See if you can figure out how it works.

1800 is a Japanese doujin (Fan-made) game that was only available at Japanese game shows, such as the Tokyo Game Market. It was made on demand, and you had to order one in advance and pick it up at the show.  These were not available to the general public, and in most cases, the show was the only place where the game could be obtained.

1800 is a game of exploration and conquest in the Industrial Age.  The players become monarchs and spread their influence around the world, building colonies, and waging wars of conquest in order to become the dominant power.

Here is a description from the designer's web page:

This card game is set in the colonial era from the 16th to the 18th century, and players take on the role of becoming the great powers of the time.  This is a game where you compete to acquire private land cards. If you can't get a colony card, collect force cards and take a neighboring country's colony. Build up a large colonial empire by the end of the game by using diplomacy cards, avoiding wars, and leasing colonies.

Recently an expansion, that added Denmark and Sweden, was added to the game.

The game requires assembly.

—user summary

In a realm where steam-powered inventions reign supreme and visionary tinkerers are prized for their craft, mechanical prowess and creative ideas are the currency of progress. Here, the ever-turning gears of industry spur enterprising minds to bring new ideas to bustling Workshops. Groundbreaking discoveries are made through meticulous tinkering in these creative hubs. Eureka Moments, true sparks of genius, ignite the appreciation of the renowned Inventors Guild, driving artisan crafters to reach new heights and create inventions that will leave a lasting impression. Your mission: curry favor with the Guild and claim your place among the most celebrated inventors of the steampunk era.

This is the first part of the Gundam RPG game by Tsukuda.  It introduced Space Warfare.

It includes rulebook, scenario book, two unpunched counter sheets, two mounted maps, two player aids, and 30 data cards,

It can be played on its own and does not require any other games in this series.  Further sets enhance the experience and add things like land warfare and colony battles.

—user summary

This is the third game in the Tsukuda Gundam RPG series.  Each game in the series adds a new aspect to the gameplay.  This volume adds additional rules, such as hand-held firearms, and more scenarios.

It includes rulebook, scenario book, one unpunched counter sheets, one mounted map, two dice, two sets of unpunched data cards (60 total), two player aids.

It can be played on its own and does not require any other games in this series.  Further sets enhance the experience and add things like land warfare and colony battles.

—user summary

This is a fun and easy tower stacking party game. The player (ducker) has to place game pieces (ducklings) on the tower. When there are at least 3 pieces on the tower, the player can start playing joker ducklings. A joker duckling makes one player either play 3 game pieces or a joker card. When a player topples over the tower, everyone else yells "Duck you!" to their face. The player then picks up all the pieces, and the game continues from that player. If you are the first one to get rid of all your game pieces, you win!

This add-on includes all 6 Mystlings standees and their cards (Aquakin, Bluemane, Cloudyne, Crystail, Cthonid, and Lear) that haven’t won the polls during the Gamefound crowdfunding campaign.

Every Mystling pack includes: 3 Mystling action cards, 2 study cards, 4 sheet cards, and 1 quest card.

Special edition adds 6 miniatures and 2 pages for the "Mysting Encyclopedia".

boardgame: Malware Mayhem Hacker Duel: Cyber War Edition
description:
Malware Mayhem Hacker Duel is a two-player face-off using a tower-defense style game must defend their high, medium and low value data which is placed into one of three systems. Each player also has one secret that can be moved between the systems as long as the data is still intact. The objective of the game is to capture the secret.

Each player builds cyberweapons including a botnet, trojans, and zero-day exploits that can be used to attack the opponent's systems. Each system can be defended by firewalls, anti-virus and intrusion detection systems. An attack consists of three phases: breaching the network perimeter, hacking the system, and then in a later turn attempting to capture the data and secrets.

Each hacker is assisted by an Agent who helps raise funds, restores disabled cyberweapons and defenses, or uses one of their unique special abilities (each agent has two) such as sending spam or initiating ransomware. Each hacker can employ up to two agents by hiring or firing from the pool of available agents.

The currency of the game is cryptocoins. The DarkWeb Marketplace allows weapons and defenses to be bought and stolen data to be sold.

The game includes two decks of cards which are used to collect resources and to perform special actions (or enhanced actions).

Malware Mayhem Hacker Duel, Cyber War Edition features a hacker duel between the White Hat supported by the Hactivist agent and Black Hat hacker who is supported by the Scammer agent. Additional agents included in this set are the Freelancer and the Script Kiddie.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Malware Mayhem Hacker Duel: State Actor Edition
description:
Malware Mayhem Hacker Duel is a two-player face-off using a tower-defense style game must defend their high, medium and low value data which is placed into one of three systems. Each player also has one secret that can be moved between the systems as long as the data is still intact. The objective of the game is to capture the secret.

Each player builds cyberweapons including a botnet, trojans, and zero-day exploits that can be used to attack the opponent's systems. Each system can be defended by firewalls, anti-virus and intrusion detection systems. An attack consists of three phases: breaching the network perimeter, hacking the system, and then in a later turn attempting to capture the data and secrets.

Each hacker is assisted by an Agent who helps raise funds, restores disabled cyberweapons and defenses, or uses one of their unique special abilities (each agent has two) such as sending spam or initiating ransomware. Each hacker can employ up to two agents by hiring or firing from the pool of available agents.

The currency of the game is cryptocoins. The DarkWeb Marketplace allows weapons and defenses to be bought and stolen data to be sold.

The game includes two decks of cards which are used to collect resources and to perform special actions (or enhanced actions).

Malware Mayhem Hacker Duel, State Actor Edition brings geo-politics into the equation featuring a hacker duel between the Superpower hacker supported by the Deputy agent and the Rogue State hacker who is supported by the Threat Actor agent. Additional agents included in this set are the Spy and the Cyberterrorist.

—description from the publisher

Arcana Towers is a Tarot-themed board game set in a fantasy realm of Four Kingdoms. It revolves around all the cards found in a Tarot deck. The deck is split into two: the Minor Arcana and the Major Arcana.

The first goal is to collect face cards (Royalty Cards) from the Minor Arcana (which functions like a deck 56 playing cards) in order to collect stones (coin pieces) and draw from the smaller deck of 22 Major Arcana cards.

The overall objective is to win the game by collecting 11 towers which are denoted on a player's black die and red die. You gain towers a few different ways: by collecting 11 stones of a single colour, by collecting 3 Royalty Cards in a single Court on your board, or finally by the effects of Major Arcana Cards that typically grant stones or towers. All of your turn options are marked on a player's Turn Actions tile. There is an Opening Action, Main Action (4 choices) and an Elemental Action (fuelled by a unique Elemental ability and cards). Specialty Cards may also be played before any of these Actions.

Players take turn after turn until one of two things happens: 1: someone reaches 11 Towers, or, 2: the Major Arcana Deck runs out of (22) cards, (if the deck runs out one last turn is taken, and the player with the most Towers and Stones wins the game!

-description from designer

Gather prides of lions, bobs of seals, murders of crows, and all sorts of quirky animal collectives in Flamboyance: Unleash the Pack!

In detail, you draw cards from a common deck. Cards can be used to form sets of same-colored cards or for their special power. Every card has a different term for an animal collective, and some cards give you a reward if you know animal collective terms. Collect enough sets to win.

Chloe and her friends went for a hike in the forest. They loved the squirrels jumping around from tree to tree. On the way home, they got extremely tired. It would be amazing right now to turn into a squirrel and just leap home! To make time pass more quickly, they headed home by jumping on the shadows of trees and fences, just like squirrels. Help them find the correct shadows, so they can get home!

In this game the players get cards with pictures on them. The pictures are built of 2 or 3 shapes. In each turn you have 30 seconds to reach into the bag and try to feel the correct shapes that are on your cards. Build the shadows of your cards with the shapes you draw from the bag. The player who completes all their cards first wins the game. 
The game has a cooperative mode and 2 easy variations for smaller children.

Live fast and die young is the motto of this game.

In The Game of Death, players race along the board obtaining jobs and families (and sometimes herpes), trying to make sure they don't reach the end of the board or run out of money while living life in the fast lane. If they do, it's game over!

For fans of the Squid Game series on Netflix, play this marbles game like your life depends on it! Players, welcome. For this game, you will be playing in teams of two. Find someone you wish to play the game with. Each player will take one bag. Each bag has a set of ten marbles. Using your set of marbles, you will play marble challenges of your choice against your partner. The player who manages to lose two challenges is eliminated. Let the game begin! This game is for 2 players, ages 18 and up- perfect for drinking games for adults party and college dorm room essentials. The game includes 6 exclusive marble challenges inspired by the show in easy, medium, or hard levels. “Find someone you wish to play the game with.” Simple But could you choose knowing the stakes were your opponent’s life- or your own? Children’s games take an ominous turn in Squid Game. While the stakes of these games aren’t so extreme, try playing with the intensity of a show contestant. Attention! The next game is about to begin.

SQUID GAME MARBLES: Play 6 exclusive marble challenges inspired by the jaw-dropping Netflix show. Choose from games in easy, medium, or hard levels. Perfect for parties- adult games for game night, board games for adults & more.

HOW TO PLAY: For this game, you will be playing in teams of two. Find someone you wish to play the game with. Each player will take one bag. Each bag has a set of ten marbles.

HOW TO WIN: Using your set of marbles, you will play marble challenges of your choice against your partner. The player who manages to lose two challenges is eliminated. Let the game begin!

Contents: 20 Marbles, 2 Bags, 4 Punch Sheets, Instructions.

A set of 5 promo cards packaged together that backers received for backing Zombies!!!: 20th Anniversary Set.

High Roller
Card says: Play this card and roll the Die. On a 1-3, lose a heart token, 4-5 gain a gain a bullet token and on a 6 gain gain a heart and bullet token.

Elder God
Card Says: Place this card in front of you at the beginning of your turn. Add +2 to all rolls. Discard at the end of your turn.

Sibling Rivalry
Card says: Discard a heart token. Target a player who must win two combat rolls of 5 or better.

Dead Man's Hand
This card says: Item: Discard this card. Draw up to 5 cards instead of 3.If you do not finish your turn on the helipad, move pawn to the starting line.

What'd Ya Find Boy? 
Card states: Collect all tokens on your current tile.

Get ready for an exciting and strategic card game - Tapped Four!

To play, the first player puts down a card, preferably their highest point card. Players take turns laying down cards equal to or lower in value than those on the top of the "Stack" (the play area in the center).

You can play cards from your hand or those on the table. When a player completes a set of 4 or more identical cards or plays a "TAP IN" card, they shout "TAPPED" (and for extra fun, give the table a tap), move the "Stack" out of play, and continue their turn.

Watch out for special "Trick cards" like the "TAP IN" card, which acts as a wild card to grab the "Stack." The "TAPPED OUT" card gets passed to a player, causing them to lose their next turn.

The goal? Be the first to eliminate all your cards to win the round. The player with the lowest score after a series of rounds becomes the ultimate "TAPPED FOUR WINNER."

There’s a giant sleepover at Baby Fox’s: the whole kindergarten is invited, and everyone brought their friends too. Mother Fox put down big mats in Baby Fox’s room, and made the bed for the cubs, everyone changed into their pyjamas. But no one actually wanted to sleep! Instead, they hid under the blankets. Help Mother Fox find where everyone’s hidden, and put them all to sleep!

In this game there are cards with baby animals in their pyjamas. You find tiles showing only a part of the animal cards because when they are hiding you only see an ear, a leg of a tail from them. You randomly draw 9 tiles and create 3 groups of 3 tiles from them. You have one minute to memorize each tile then you have to cover the tiles with the 3 little blankets. Shuffle the animal deck and in each turn you have two options:
1.    Draw a card and if you think you saw that baby animal hiding under one of the blankets you take the card and it’s the end of your turn. If you think the animal isn’t hiding you can check the next card and decide to take it or leave it. Eighter way your turn is over and the next player comes.
2.    If you think you forgot which animals are hiding you can choose to peek under one of the three blankets and refresh your memories. 
When the deck is empty you reveal the baby animals under the blankets and collect points if you remembered correctly. You can play many rounds and compare your scores at the end.
The game has a cooperative version and variations for smaller and bigger children.

Dot Duel is a fast-paced, card-collection game where players race to find the number represented on the rolled dice. Speed is rewarded, but players must be careful not to misfire or they may find themselves in a high-stakes duel that might make or break their chances of winning!

The game is relatively simple. Included are a pair of dice and the Dot Duel Deck - 57 cards, each different from the other. Every card has 8 symbols representing a number 2-12 on it, and there may be more than one symbol representing the same number on a single card (though no symbol will be exactly the same on the same card). Every pair of cards has EXACTLY one matching symbol between the cards.

A turn starts with a roll of the dice and six of the cards face up in between all players. Then all players race to find and accurately count the number of matching symbols to the rolled number on a card, making a claim for the card once they’re confident they are correct. Opponents check their claims, and can initiate a duel if they are incorrect. Claim a Double Dot, when two of the showing cards show an exactly matching symbol of the rolled number, and claim all six cards at once. Play ends when all cards are collected and victory is claimed by the player with the most cards.

Duels are an integral part of the game. In them, players put up cards from their collection in a one-on-one face off where they must race to correctly count the matching symbols among all the showing cards. Winner steals cards from their opponent, and a Double Dot allows you to steal their entire deck!

There are other variations of gameplay for beginners and younger players that promote fun for any audience.

-description from designer

In this head-to-head strategy game designed by Tetris creator Alexey Pajitnov, players alternate placing a single Tetrimino on the board, trying to outwit each other. The player who successfully places the last Tetrimino wins.

Contents: 1 x Unit, 14 x Tetriminos & Instructions.

Second expansion to Próba Ognia. It introduces new Locations, new Scenario, new cards for Firefighters, sparks and conflagration.

This time Firefighters have to protect fields around the city. Once there is a spark on each of them, they immediately lose. The Element player cannot move the Element tokens, but have many cards with effects allowing to swap sparks with fire or conflagration. Moreover this expansion introduces a Wind mechanism - most of the Element cards refer to the direction of the Wind, making its actions more predictable, yet dangerous.

It was released during Próba Ognia Championship 2023.

Compete to become the most successful luxury railway owner! To win, you must be part railway operator and part saboteur, using fiendish tactics to delay or destroy rival railways. In the luxury railway wars, there is only one rule:  No mercy!

Summary

Build the longest and most profitable train. Sabotage your opponents as they try to do the same.  Earn revenue by coupling (making card combos) to yield higher profits while taking advantage of Sabotage cards and station modifiers. End-of-game bonuses for five category cars and longest trains. Some cars have mini-games (like slots in the casino or the comedy clubs).

Nail-biting Finishes

Constant creation and destruction/modification of trains as players compete means it’s difficult for one player to get too far ahead. This creates a natural balance where every player can compete to the end. There's always a chance for a dramatic underdog victory.

Object of the Game

Reach the end of the luxury train tour with the most money.

It’s spooky season, and it’s time for potions! In Craft & Peddle, a card game for two, players are alchemists crafting and peddling Potions (and maybe even trying some of the potions themselves).

Players use Action Cards to acquire and sell Potion Cards or use special abilities.  At the end of the game, bonuses are awarded and the player who was the most successful alchemist wins!

In the Valkyrie Incident of 3030, CogDrives (mechanized battle suits) were used extensively in the conflict to turn the tides of war.

In CogDrive Neon, two players compete to recruit and deploy CogDrive cards to unify their reserves and win the clash.  This is a deck building game utilizing multi-use cards which provide multiple paths to scoring, and at the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

Each CogDrive drive can be used to recruit additional CogDrive cards for the player's deck, discarded for a special ability, or played to the player's side of the Clash.  Each CogDrive features one of four colors, and the OverDrive mechanic allows players to get even more out of their played CogDrive cards.

Basara includes expansion content for settlements facing Smog Singers as their Node 2 Quarry monsters. Smog Singers are included in the Gambler's Chest Expansion.

Install guide
Character card
Pattern card
Pattern gear card
Settlement Event card

Basara comes with 2 miniatures. A painters scale model kneeling, perfect for hobbyists and a players scale model for gamers looking for a fun survivor stand-in.

Basara is packaged in a box that includes:

1 x Painters scale, hard plastic Basara miniature
1 x Players scale, hard plastic Basara miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 50mm base and elaborate scenic insert
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
Large matte art print

Starving, Basara approached the pit. A rich, toothsome aroma lingered but nothing remained. Disappointed she plucked the red weed pushing up between the crystals at her feet. She nibbled experimentally as she hurried to catch up to the others. Numbing and invigorating spice burned her mouth, glittering smoke sizzled from her lips. When she stepped into the lanterns light, the others were blinded by kaleidoscopic refractions of rainbow-hued sweat pouring down her face. From that moment, her instrument had a new sound.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Compact Quiver and Killjoy Promo Cards

Creatures of Rock is a exciting and easy-to-learn strategy card game for players of all ages and all levels of strategic skill.

Creatures of Rock is a fast-paced strategic card game where players try to form a 4-member band with the most Star Power whilst disrupting their opponent’s band. During the game you can collect or steal opponents’ equipment to earn bonus Star Power. Watch out for Wild Cards as these allow players to disrupt other bands carefully laid out plans. Make sure you play strategically, as the game can end at any moment once 3 stage call cards are played.

Object of the Game:
To win you must form a 4-member band with the most Star Power before the final “Stage Call” & the game ends.

Card Types:
Band Member Cards:  There are 4 types of Band Members: Singer, Guitarist, Bassist, and Drummer, each with different base Star Power and Combo Bonus. There are Multi-Talent Band Members who can be 1 of 2 Band Member Types when they take to the stage.
Equipment Cards: Can give bonus Star Power at games end.
Wild Cards: Make the game a little crazy.
Stage Call Cards: Decide when the game ends.

Setup:
Shuffle the deck & deal 4 cards to each player. Keep your cards secret.
Place the remaining cards in a Pick-Up Pile face down. 
The Player with the next birthday goes first.

How to Play:
At the start of your turn, you can do 1 of the following:
-Take the top card from the Pick-Up Pile to your hand 
-Take 1 card from the top 2 cards on the Discard Pile to your hand
-Take 1 equipment card from another players “Stage Setup” to your hand

Next, you can place any Equipment Cards face down in front of you, this is your “Stage Setup”. Your Band Members will combine with the Equipment on your “Stage Setup” at the games end. Your “Stage Setup” can have a maximum of 5 pieces of Equipment. You can’t discard or take back to you hand any piece of equipment once it is on your stage.

At the end of your turn, you must only hold a total of 6 Cards. Discard cards into the Discard Pile until your hand only has 6 cards. “Stage Call” Cards cannot be discarded at this time. Discard cards face up.

It is now the next players turn.

Wild & “Stage Call” Cards can be played at any time during the game. When played Wild Cards go onto the Discard Pile. When played “Stage Call” cards go onto a separate pile.
 Ending the Game:
A game ends when 3 Stage Call cards have been played, or at the end of the turn of the player who drew the last card from the Pick-Up Pile. Once the game ends no more Wild Cards can be played.

When the game ends, place any Equipment Cards from your hand onto your Stage Setup until it contains a maximum of 5 pieces of Equipment.

Each player selects 4 Band Members from their hand to take to their Stage. A Band Member does not need an Equipment Card to take to the stage.
Get the total Star Power of your band by adding the Star Power of each member (the number in the blue star).

Then add on any bonus from Equipment and/or Band Member combinations. 
The Player with the band with the highest Star Power including any bonus power wins the game.

Creatures of Rock is made by small game designer Funable Games

Compete with your friends in Arbolito, a card-stacking dexterity game!
Together, create a colorful tree by hanging leaf cards on its branches one by one. Try to get rid of your cards faster than your opponents, but be careful as the leaves may fall at any moment!


     Connect new cards with those already hanging on the tree as carefully as possible.
     Activate special effects.
     Be the first player to get rid of all cards and don’t let them fall!


—description from the publisher

We play pretending to be like trees dropping their leaves onto the forest floor, where there are seeds, mushrooms, and squirrels that give us points if our leaves land on them.

Some cards are placed on the floor, and then we stand up with the remaining cards in our hand. We drop them one by one, trying to make them fall on cards of the same type.

The cards left on the ground are leaf cards and have different points. They are placed face down, but we can see and try to remember the ones we place, attempting to collect the ones with more points.
The cards being dropped are leaf cards, because while standing, we simulate trees in autumn, shedding their leaves.

It involves skill and memory, primarily aimed at a younger audience.

—description from the designer

Happy Halloween is a competitive game where players take on the role of goblins on the scariest night of the year. They must collect disenchanted trios of matching or same-coloured treasures to score more points than the other goblins.
Each player initially starts with no cards. They are all placed in a pile face down in the centre of the table and, in turn, each player decides which of the possible actions to take: trick, treat or disenchant.
Deal: the player takes two treasures (cards) from the central pile and places them face up in his or her playing area in view of the other players.
Truco: the player enchants (steals) one of the cards from any player's playing area and takes it to his or her playing area but enchanted (horizontally and face up).
Unenchant: the player places all the cards in his or her playing area face up and vertically.
If the player has chosen trick or treat as an action, at the end of the trick or treat, he/she can score in groups of three cards that have the same type of treasure or the same colour, but only the cards that are disenchanted (vertically). Then, in groups of three, he will place them face down forming a pile that will be his treasures already obtained and that nobody will be able to steal from him.
The game ends when the central deck is exhausted and, at that moment, all the face-up cards of each player (enchanted or not) will automatically become part of the scoring deck (face down) of the player on the left. The player with the most face-down cards will be the goblin who has collected the most treasure on Halloween night and, therefore, will have won the game.

--

Demuestra que eres el duende más ingenioso en la noche de Halloween y acapara el mayor número de cartas al final de la partida siguiendo las caprichosas normas que guían su avaricia. Roba tesoros a los demás duendes y haz lo posible por evitar que te quiten los tuyos, pero recuerda que ¡será imposible retenerlo todo! No todo vale: Cuando los duendes se encaprichan con un objeto o un color no descansan hasta que juntan tres iguales.

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Marvel Studios The Infinity Saga – Ultimate Edition
description:
MCU superfans, assemble! Take on opponents with your vast knowledge of the Marvel Cinematic Universe in this trivia game for the best and biggest Marvel movie fans. Have the upper hand and seize all six coveted category wedges (Power, Reality, Space, Soul, Mind, and Time) to win! Includes full game board and custom Marvel-related movers such as the Tesseract, Loki’s Scepter, Aether Casket, Orb, Vormir’s Gate, and Eye of Agamotto.

Conquer the Marvel Cinematic Universe with Knowledge: Join forces with fellow MCU enthusiasts in this trivia showdown! Show off your expertise as you answer 1,800 questions spanning MCU Phases 1-3 films, aiming to secure all six infinity category wedges like Power and Space to claim victory.

Marvel-Themed Strategy: Dive into the immersive world of the Marvel Cinematic Universe with custom movers like the Tesseract and Loki's Scepter. Test your knowledge, outwit opponents, and master the multiverse as you journey through the full game board.

For True Marvel Devotees: Calling all devoted fans! Engage in epic battles of wit and wisdom, proving your status as the ultimate Marvel movie aficionado. With iconic category wedges and exclusive Marvel-themed movers, this game is your ticket to becoming the ultimate MCU guru.

Welcome, brave Survivor, to the dawn of a new era in the majestic Land of Bendada. A cataclysmic earthquake has shattered the land and the once-mighty ruling House of Jo'orka Hanzy had fallen, where the legendary Nedca City of Abanda now lies in ruins. From the ashes of destruction, five elite Houses have emerged, their destinies intertwined in an epic struggle for dominion over this newborn world. Will you seize this extraordinary opportunity to erect a new Nedca City and etch your name into the annals of Bendada's history?

As the visionary architect of your House's fate, you shall navigate a treacherous landscape of strategic choices, where every decision holds profound consequences. Will you channel the depths of your brilliance to construct awe-inspiring structures that stand as testaments to your unconquerable will? Or shall you embark on a daring quest to unlock the secrets of technological marvels, harnessing their boundless power to surpass all rivals in sheer ingenuity? then again, you may embrace the call of battle, brandishing your weapons with unmatched prowess and crushing your adversaries beneath your unwavering might.

Victory, dear Survivor, lies within your grasp. Ascend to greatness by raising your Influence, forging unbreakable Bonds, vanquishing all foes who dare to defy you, completing legendary quests that will echo through eternity, or delving into the profound mysteries of the five sacred Gems of Bendada. Within these intertwined paths to glory, your imagination knows no bounds, and the destiny of your House shall be shaped by the boundlessness of your dreams.

Embrace this unparalleled opportunity to rebuild a shattered world, to sculpt a new Nedca City that will stand as a testament to your unwavering spirit, and to etch your name in letters of fire across the grand tapestry of Bendada's history. The time has come, Survivor, to seize your rightful place among the legends of this epic saga.

Whether you’re dishing out socks to your fellow player, a delicious necrotic jelly to a necromancer or just planting buttonbush in the beaches, It’s time to spread some joy across the land!

Mouni is a board game that combines the challenges of survival and strategic thinking with a humorous and exciting gaming experience. The goal of the game is for players to collect 60 points and pass the Municipality field, while using various cards to gain an advantage or make it more difficult for their opponents.

There are four main types of cards in the game: event, object, instant, and crunch. Event cards give one-time tasks that must be completed immediately. Object cards help players in different ways, such as giving extra steps or other benefits. Instant cards can be played at any time and have an immediate effect on the game. Crunch cards help players with various properties on their journey.

During the game, players can roll, move, and complete various tasks on the fields they step on. There is also the possibility of fighting, where players compete against each other for points or cards.

Mouni is an exciting and varied game where players need to think strategically and find the best method to win. The humorous elements and varied cards of the game guarantee that every game party will be a unique and memorable experience.

—description from the designer

A "Mouni" egy társasjáték, amely a túlélés és a stratégiai gondolkodás kihívásait ötvözi egy humoros és izgalmas játékélménnyel. A játék célja, hogy a játékosok összegyűjtsenek 60 pontot, és áthaladjanak az Önkormányzat mezőn, miközben különböző kártyákat használnak fel, hogy előnyhöz jussanak, vagy megnehezítsék ellenfeleik helyzetét.

A játékban négy fő kártyatípus található: esemény, tárgy, instant és krícsör. Az esemény kártyák egyszeri feladatokat adnak, amelyeket azonnal végre kell hajtani. A tárgy kártyák különböző módon segítenek a játékosoknak, például plusz lépéseket adnak, vagy más előnyöket biztosítanak. Az instant kártyákat bármikor ki lehet játszani, és azonnali hatással vannak a játékra. A krícsör kártyák pedig különböző tulajdonságokkal segítik a játékosokat az útjukon.

A játék során a játékosok dobhatnak, léphetnek, és különböző feladatokat teljesíthetnek a mezőkön, amelyekre lépnek. A játékban lehetőség van verekedésre is, amely során a játékosok egymás ellen küzdenek a pontokért vagy a kártyákért.

A "Mouni" egy izgalmas és változatos játék, amelyben a játékosoknak stratégiailag kell gondolkodniuk, és meg kell találniuk a legjobb módszert a győzelemre. A játék humoros elemei és változatos kártyái garantálják, hogy minden játékparti egyedi és emlékezetes élmény lesz.

The local monks have heard about you developing the prefecture and have come to you to build theme a new 5-level pagoda in the center of prefecture. you are trying to please both the emperor and the monks by being the most resourceful prefecture and the quickest to get the pagoda finished.

boardgame: 魔法少女ヴァーサス (Mahoushoujo Versus)
description:
魔法少女ヴァーサス (Mahoushoujo Versus) is a two player fighting game from Japan.

A team is formed by four magical girls. Three of them form a formation and start the battle! Defender, Attacker, Supporter. Master 3 different positions!

If even one person on the opposing team reduces their life to 0, you win! Even if you lose due to damage, there is always a comeback!

You become a 'small animal companion' who guides the magical girls, and forms a team with four magical girls of your choice. After confirming each other's teams, start the battle by placing three people as Defender, Attacker, and Supporter. Each magical girl has three command cards with the commands Defense, Attack, and Support written in pairs.

Each team's deck consists of 12 command cards from all four members of the team. The command cards of magical girls who will not participate in the battle this time will remain in the deck, so it is important to form a team while keeping the balance in mind.

Each turn, you choose two of the three command cards in your hand and play them half stacked to use three commands. The Defence command causes the magical girl placed in the Defender to use her special ability Defence Arts. Similarly, the Attack command is used by the Attacker, and the Support command is used by the Supporter. Defenders receive attacks from Attackers, and Supporters support them.

Repeat these steps and win if even one magical girl on the opposing team has their life reduced to 0.

Example of a magical girl:
[Flame and Wings Protection Girl] Suparna Sowaka
Her real name is Karutaya Kureha. She is a guardian girl who specializes in attacks. She transforms with the help of a bird that can control fire. Her special move is Sowaka Karura Burning, which turns flames into giant wings and burns the enemy from two directions.
【炎と翼の護法少女】スパルナソワカ
本名は「歌留多屋くれは（かるたや　くれは）」。
攻撃を得意とする護法少女。炎を操る鳥の力を借りて変身する。
必殺技は、炎を巨大な翼に変え、2方向から敵を燃やし尽くす「ソワカ・カルラ・バーニング」。
description by publisher

GAME PREPARATION
Each pioneer chooses a character and receives 25 NEWTOPIA points at the start. In addition, 5,000 euros of starting capital is placed on the game board in the communal safe and taken out if necessary. The remaining money remains in the playing box, the "bank", and is taken out when instructed.

GAMEPLAY
The youngest pioneer in the round starts. He rolls the dice and moves forward by the number rolled. If the turn ends on a field with a number, the text for the corresponding field in the event booklet is read out loud and the task is carried out. If another person lands on the same space, the event must be carried out again. At the end of each month there are so-called "decision fields". You have to stay on these fields, even if a higher number is rolled. If there are still numbers left, they expire. If a pioneer reaches the "decision field", all other pioneers have to follow straight away. The person who gets onto the field first reads the task, leads the discussion and makes the final decision. Once the decision has been made, he can be the first to roll the dice again and move on.

GOAL OF THE GAME
It's not about getting to the finish line first. The winner is the person who has collected the most NEWTOPIA points after one year in NEWTOPIA.

—description from the publisher

HATTORI is a two player abstract game from Japan. Players move their pieces alternately orthogonally or diagonally. The winner is the first player to bring three of the neutral pieces home or capture three of their opponent's pieces.

The game is played on a small 5x5 grid. One player has five black tiles, the other has five white tiles, and there are five small green tiles which are neutral.

In opposite corners of the board is a section of three squares, which is each player's base. They start with three pieces in the base and with two adjacent outside. The green tiles are laid diagonally across the board between the bases.

The player's goal is to either bring three of the green tiles onto their own pieces and inside their base, or to eliminate three of their opponent's pieces (thus making it impossible to satisfy the first win condition).

The player pieces move in the direction of the piece's corners. A piece that is turned 45 degrees to the grid therefore moves left or right or up or down the grid, as a Rook in Chess does. A piece aligned to the grid will move diagonally across the grid, as a Bishop in Chess does. A piece can move as far as the player wishes but not through another piece including the neutral tiles.

On their turn, a player can Move, or Throw, or Wait. Whatever the player does, the piece used must be turned 45 degrees at the end of the action (which the publisher reminds us is easy to forget).

When a player moves, they can end their move on a green tile, and place it on their tile.

If a player's tile is empty, and an opponent's tile has a green piece, the player can move their tile onto the opponent's, taking the green tile and eliminating the opponent's piece. This opens up the possibility of tit-for-tat moves, immediately losing a tile. However, pieces inside a player's base are immune from attack.

Instead of moving a tile, a player can Throw a green tile from one piece to another of their own empty pieces. The throw has to be in the tile's orientation, after which it is turned.

Finally, a player can Wait, by simply choosing a tile of their own and turning it 45 degrees. Simply passing is not allowed.

The game was produced in a small tube, with the rules sheet also showing the grid board.
