They will gain souvenirs when they do, eat, see or buy something. To do this, players will have to manage their time, money and energy. Additionally, there are cultural experiences, events and missions.
When the weekend is over, the player with the most souvenirs, culture, missions and placed visited first will win the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rebel Tide: The American Civil War
description:
Rebel Tide: The American Civil War, 1861-1865 is a two-player Strategic level board-wargame that places you in command of either the USA (Union) or CSA (Confederacy) during the Civil War.  Each turn consists of a year, during which multiple card plays occur.  These give the players movement, combats, entrenchments, and other actions.  At the end of each year, players must make critical decisions on which cards to re-buy in an attempt to win the war outright or to win by placing the other side in a disadvantageous position.  Rebel Tide is based on the popular, action-packed Imperial Tide/Pacific Tide game system by Gregory M. Smith, with many combat and strategic decisions to challenge players in just a single evening’s game.

The core of the game is the unique card re-buy system, in which players take their annual production (adjusted for blockades and blockade runners) and decide which cards they need for the upcoming year.  Cards not only provide for reinforcements, but allow for movement, combat, and entrenchment.  Which cards to rebuy is without question one of the key decisions the player must make to prepare for next year’s operations.

The game has infantry units for all of the major participants, cavalry, and artillery units. Naval operations are mainly abstracted, although the Confederate player must worry about Farragut invading a port from the sea.

Besides the focus on card play, the game features a small footprint (one standard map) and also is designed to be played in just a single evening – estimated at 3 hours for experienced players to fight the entire war.

One new mechanic in Rebel Tide is the Political Track, which is an abstract measure of the support of England and/or France to the Confederacy. If this track reaches the maximum early in the war, it can trigger an early CSA victory.

Another new key mechanic in the game system is the addition of historical Leaders, who range in ability from excellent (5) to poor (2). Bad leaders may be “sacked” and removed from the game and randomly replaced by the expenditure of a movement action. Leaders are vital in advancing after combat and also can add strength to an attack’s total combat power.

A continued mechanic that was very successful in Imperial Tide is the use of Resource Points. Each nationality has a set amount, and there is an option to buy an extra point each year. Although mainly intended to replace infantry strength, they are extremely flexible in that they can be used in limited amounts for movement and combat operations. They can be thought of as a sort of “operational reserve” and should be used judiciously by players.

This game is highly accessible to those familiar with Imperial Tide or Pacific Tide, both designed by Gregory M. Smith. The game system lends itself very well to capturing the bloody and numerous battles of the U.S. Civil War.  While Rebel Tide is designed as a two-player gaming experience, an additional option for solitaire play is provided with the inclusion of a Solitaire Bot. This “bot” creates action priorities for the non-human player, and has the advantage of being simple enough to implement without spending an inordinate amount of time as the non-player.

Product Information:

Complexity: 4 out of 10
Playing time: 3 to 4 hours
Solitaire Suitability: 8 out of 10 (Solitaire Bot system)
Players: Two (with option for solitaire play)
Map Scale: Strategic; Abstract
Time Scale: 1 year per Turn; each turn being composed of multiple card plays
Unit Scale: Strategic (Infantry points, Cavalry points, Artillery points, and Leaders)

Game Components:

One Mounted game map, 22” x 34”
One counter sheet, 9/16” counters
Two Player Aid Cards
48 Game Cards (24 per side)
5 Year Cards (double-sided)
Rules Booklet
Two six-sided dice
Box and Lid set

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In a realm threatened by an evil wizard known as the Monster Master, the fate of the land hangs in the balance. The Monster Master has started his sinister summoning ritual, enslaving monsters new and old to break the chains that bind him to the spirit world. As an elemental spirit who locked him away eons ago, you've assembled a diverse party of adventurers to quell his legion of monsters before they can destroy the Veil between worlds. Will you be the ones to thwart the Monster Master's sinister plan, or will you bring darkness to the land?

Fame & Fortune is a captivating and intense strategy RPG set in a fantastical world where you and your party must outwit mythical monsters. Your primary objective is to defeat the Dragon King before he and his minions defeat 6 adventurers from you and your ally's teams. Accumulate a combination of Fame and Fortune Points to unlock in game abilities and to purchase rewards.

Fame & Fortune promises 20-30 minutes of heart-pounding excitement per player, as you strategize, battle, and race to emerge victorious in this gripping, fantasy-filled RPG adventure... & One lucky player gets to be the bad guy.

—description from the publisher

Spira Toss is a unique table-top game that uses a metal ball which you shoot through a spiral loop.  The spiral loop can be adjusted to reposition the direction of the ball.  The landing point consists of 3-sided squares with either X - O or a number denomination.  This allows 2 game versions and either 1 or 2 players.

Game one can be played with one or two players and play like Tic -Tac-Toe.

Game two is played with two players and you are shooting the marble to score 100 points

The pointing system is as such

Column one (Green) +30, +50, +20
Column two (orange) -25, -20, -30
Column three (Purple) +25, -10, +10

The colors do not designate anything but a more colorful game surface.  There are two dial number counters on the back of the mini game area

This is definitely not like the easy game play of traditional Tic-Tac-Toe and almost like Toss-Across but you have far less control of what you are using to reach Tic-Tac-Toe.

The added difference of the other games is that this game also has a variation of playing to achieve 100!

—user summary

This game is a journey through your neighborhood park! You never know what you will step on.  There is a game board, a spinner (Shaped like a roundabout, you know the one shaped like a circle with bars that kids spin so fast, they fly off!) pawn pieces in the shape of gym shoes and 3 types of GOO!  Once set, your adventure begins as you travel through the park finding goo obstacles and other park themed happenings.  You win by getting to the end with the least amount of GOO on your shoe.

Tuk Tuk is a pickup and deliver family puzzle game where each player takes the role of Tuk Tuk driver in Bangkok. Players place cards on the board to create many paths in the city to bring tourists around famous attractions in the city. The player who earns the most money at the end of the day becomes the famous Tuk Tuk driver. With variable player power, riding a Tuk Tuk has never been more fun.

—description from the designer

In this game, players try to read words. These words are encoded in over 150 different ways. This does not make it easy to read the words. A total of 600 word puzzles are available and they can be solved either alone or in a larger group of players.

This game is base on the complexities of the English Language.  Understanding not just the language, but all of the "Idiomatic Expressions" that go along with it.  One example would be this:

Yellow meaning Card
What does "built a fire under" mean in this sentence?
The captain "built a fire under" his troops.
a. corrected
b. stirred them to action
c. threatened

The answer is "b" of course!

The game is then played in a similar fashion as Trivial Pursuit. You roll the die, then move the number of spaces on the board.  Land on specific colors to match the 4 game question cards.  A player continues as long as they answer the question correctly.

The winner is the player that has collected a card from each category (color card) and land on the center (end) space with an exact roll.  And then must answer the final question by picking the color card of choice.

—user summary

Think of the game Ants In The Pants in that you need to get your donuts into the fryer similar to getting the ants into the pants.  There are twists and turns with this game because the donuts are very light and as you toss them into the fryer, they twist, turn and whirl upwards on a blast of air coming out of the fryer.

The donut chef has a stick that hangs basically upside down so as a donut catches the air it will be forced up onto the stick.  The first person to get all 3 donuts into the fryer (stick) wins!

—user summary

It’s 1944 and the European battlefront is a confusing, disorienting place to be. Lines shift quickly, orders get lost and suddenly you can find yourself behind enemy lines. For some, this is not a barrier but an opportunity… An opportunity to infiltrate lines, inflict chaos upon the enemy and maybe get a little bit of something for yourself while you’re at it.

Enter The Strays. A group of independent-minded soldiers with a mutual goal for personal gain. Their refined skills as leaders, scouts, medics, scroungers and killers will enable them to take out bunkers, outposts, warehouses and labs behind enemy lines well before artillery and the air force level them.

The Strays is a miniature agnostic game. It relies on you to supply the miniatures and terrain for the campaign-based missions contained within. Blank sheets are included for you to create your own missions.

—description from the designer

Contents:

     4 V2 Rockets miniature set
     4 Peace Cards expansion
     7 plastic damage markers


Peace cards are played from the hand with an additional card from the top of the draw deck. You add the OP's from both cards and may spend them on any operations.

A computer problem paralyses Amsterdam airport and some flights, including yours, have been diverted to Düsseldorf. It’s early October, and the skies are already full.  What can be going on...?

An additional airport for Sky Team: Düsseldorf

Contain one postcard with two additional missions (Yellow and Black)

Note: Available in Sky Team Turbulence and as a Print and Play

Lovebirds is an exciting social deduction game perfect for wedding celebrations and events! Players take on unique roles as they are divided into three teams. The Bride and Groom aim to unite at the end of the game, while the Ex-GF and the Playboy try to separate the bridal party for their own motives. Using their wits and deduction skills, players must identify the traitors and foil their plans. Get ready to put your deduction and deceptive skills to the test in Lovebirds. The ultimate wedding game of romantic mystery and amusement!

In Lovebirds, you and your guests  assume hidden roles and join one of three teams: Team Lovebirds, Team Playboy, or Team EX-GF. Each team has a different goal: Team Lovebirds wants to make sure the BRIDE and GROOM end up together happily ever after. While team playboy wants to unite the PLAYBOY with the BRIDE, and team ex-gf wants to unite the EX-GF and GROOM.

To win a game of Lovebirds, you’ll need to use all your charm, wit, and strategy. You’ll have to bluff, flirt, expose, and manipulate your way to victory. But be careful, as your opponents are trying to do the same! Can you figure out who is on your side and who is against you? Can you stop the other teams from interfering with your romantic plans? Can you unite the perfect couple? Find out in Lovebirds, the ultimate game of love and betrayal!

—description from the publisher

When your parents tell you to cook your own food.

Hot Pot, also called Steam Boat or 火鍋, brings friends and family together over a simmering feast. All the cards are dealt out evenly amongst the players. Players take turn loading up the pot with their favourite ingredients, such as shiitake mushrooms, pork belly, wombok and crabsticks. Keep an eye on what's cooking and scoop them up before anyone else. Wait too long and you might end up with nothing! There's a few action cards thrown into the mix, to spice things up. Stop! It's not cooked yet! Timing is everything if you want to enjoy the perfect meal.

Sharing is caring, but in this game, winning is tastier!

—description from the publisher

Belt Trade is a 2-6 player game. The main aim of the game is to gain as many credits as possible by bidding on and trading goods.

Each player is a space caravanner attempting to gain the most credits by bidding on trading goods between earth and the asteroid belt. Player's will use the items they gain during one phase to count points. Then they will use those very items to bid a trade for the next phase of items!  Your opponent's can disrupt your plans by bidding higher than you on items you sought after or by playing cards that prevent you from picking up certain items. Being vigilant in keeping track of what items have already been traded and obtained. Not doing so could lead to your opponents gaining more credits than you.

—description from the designer

Floating high above the ground are the evil sky prisons that track down and trap any dragon they can find.  With only 3 dragons left; The Fire Dragon, Water Dragon and Thunder Dragon; it's your job to direct those dragons from castle to castle to help them free their friends.

Dragon Hero is a solo or cooperative game intended for a younger audience.  But the complexity can be scaled up for anybody! Each castle has a weakness on each side.  Once you've moved the proper dragons into place to take advantage of those weaknesses you've freed the trapped dragon. Free all trapped dragons before going through the 9 movement cards and you win!

This is a quick, light, portable pocket game that can be played solo by young gamers or play cooperative as a family and have each person control their own dragon.  Work together to figure out the right movements to free all the dragons and win!

—description from the publisher

The player has one herd of gazelles and tries to drink the water at the place near the Lion square without being dealt the damages. There are carnivores in the Lion square.

Players try to minimize damage on their side by strategically placing carnivores on Lion Square.

The players place cards one by one in a 3 x 3 area, and scoring, the players get the penalty points from them.

The game ends when all nine cards are placed, or the turn begins the turn player who has no cards left in their hand.
Then, the three cards on your side will be penalty points. Furthermore, all cards that are "taken" during the game are worth -1 point per two cards.

Repeat this until any player reaches 0 points or less from 20 points, and the player with the most points at that point wins.

Arrange the Oracle cards while taking turns in dealing out the cards.

When it's your turn, you reveal three cards and deal them one each to yourself, the next player, and the player after that.
Arrange the dealt cards in a 3x3 grid.

Cards that have already been placed face up cannot be moved or stacked on top of each other.

Then, if you cut out 9 pieces in a grid, you will calculate your score.

Scores are obtained by the number of cards lined up in ascending order and by the line where three cards of the same color are lined up.
Compare their scores (round points) with their neighbors on both sides, and if they are higher, each person gets 1 point.

You win if you get a total of 3 points.

—description from the designer

Battle Of The Kacoon Sea (空母対空母　決戦カクーノ沖海戦) (Aircraft carrier vs. aircraft carrier decisive battle Battle of Kakuno Gulf) is an early game from the Japanese doujin game publisher KogeKogeDo. It was produced as two versions: Empire and American. A player could play the game as either side, or combine the two versions to play a full game.

The game is based upon the Battle of the Coral Sea, a significant naval battle in the Pacific theatre during World War II. During this battle, the two navies did not actually make contact. Instead, both sides used aircraft launched from carriers to attack the other side. The game simulates this form of combat.

The game is packaged in a large plastic envelope which has the rules, a large playing sheet representing the carriers in an abstract form, and a set of cards for aircraft from each side. The technical drawings of the aircraft are taken from Wikipedia Creative Commons.

In the game, a player must position aircraft on their flight deck and send them up into formations, where they are met and countered by opposing aircraft. The player uses dice to resolve combat between the planes. Then the surviving aircraft must return and land safely. A player can only keep an aircraft in a holding pattern for one turn. If it cannot land after that, it is lost into the sea.

Each player has fighter aircraft and bomber aircraft. The objective is to get the bombers past the fighter defence and to sink the opposing carriers for victory.
user summary

In this game you found your own parties, put forward candidates, compete against each other in the election campaign and forge coalitions. You collect victory points by being in government for as many rounds as possible and implementing policies. Whoever has the most victory points at the end wins the game.

The game is divided into 3 repeating phases:

Election campaign - You are fighting for seats in parliament.

Formation of government - 2 or more players with a majority form a government.

Game rounds - 3 rounds in which you can implement policies or prepare for the next election campaign.

—description from the designer

In diesem Spiel gründet ihre eure eigene Parteien, stellt Kandidat:innen auf, tretet im Wahlkampf gegeneinander an und schmiedet Koalitionen. Ihr sammelt Siegpunkte, indem ihr möglichst viele Runden in der Regierung seid und Wahlversprechen umsetzt. Wer am Ende die meisten Siegpunkte hat, gewinnt das Spiel.

Das Spiel untergliedert sich in 3 sich wiederholende Phasen:

Wahlkampf - Ihr kämpft um Sitze im Parlament.

Regierungsbildung - 2 oder mehr Spieler:innen mit einer Mehrheit formen eine Regierung.

Spielrunden - 3 Runden in denen ihr Wahlversprechen umsetzen oder ihr euch auf den nächsten Wahlkampf vorbereiten könnt.

—description from the designer (German)

Become the Industrial Tycoon who dominates the production chains in this strategy roll and write print and play board game. You create production chain which goal is to create steel.

Use your dice to create a terrain in two of four colors, which give you resources when they connect with other terrains of the same colors, then build necessary structures. Those structures must be connected to operate.

Players deliver ores from mines to steelworks, then prepared steel from steelworks to factories, and finally, store finished goods in warehouses. Player who creates the most functional chains becomes the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Villannex ～ 拡張セット「兵力と経済」 (Villanex - expansion set "Military Force and Economy")
description:
Villannex ヴィラネックス～合併競議会～ 拡張セット「兵力と経済」  (Villanex - Merger Competition Council - expansion set "Military Force and Economy") is the very long title on the box front of the expansion set.

This is the Expansion set for the 1st edition of Villannex. These expansions were then incorporated into the 2nd edition Villannex (2nd Edition + expansions).

From the publisher:
This expansion set includes two expansions and one mini-expansion that can be added to the main unit. You can play by adding each expansion or mini-expansion alone, or you can play in combination. Additionally, there are some cards that can only be used when playing two expansions together.

A different location from the previous merger. There was also a village scheduled to be merged here. The problem seems to be the same everywhere, and it seems that the village chiefs are competing against each other.

However, one thing that was slightly different was that these villages had their own military forces and abundant economic power, and they exerted pressure on the merger council. Use these pressures or splash them. Let's aim to become the new village chief!
description by publisher

MAD TEA PARTY is a three player only trick-taking card game from Japan. Themed on Alice In Wonderland, the players are split between one Challenger and two Disrupters working together. The cards have key symbols on them, and the Challenger must win more than half the keys to win the game.

The Challenger can declare Trump, but there are also 4 Cheshire Cat cards in the 32 card deck, which are always Trump. These are labelled A, AA, X, XX, with the first two being strongest Trump and the latter being weakest trump but have keys.
user summary

A new shop has opened up in town and this one has a soft squishy center...

Stuff the Magic Dragon is a charming toy shop filled with delightful plush and soft toys to delight the children, and grownups, of the town!

It is a CARDBOARD ALCHEMY EXCLUSIVE, featuring artwork from Sandara Tang.

Featuring one promo shop card, Stuff the Magic Dragon, to add to your game. This promo is only available in English.

—description from the publisher

Fly your planets twice counter clockwise around our sun to win the game. A crossing comet and gravition, generated by magnetic planet balls, creates disturbance.

Die neun Planeten unseres Sonnensystems werden durch Magnet-Kugeln dargestellt. Jeder Spieler führt mehrere Planeten gegen den Uhrzeigersinn um die Sonne. Gravitationsfelder verursachen Störungen und bremsen den geführten Planeten.
Ein Komet kreuzt regelmäßig die Bahnen der Planeten und kann die Planeten zerstören.
Zehn Konstellationskarten geben Siegbedingungen vor, wenn sich mindestens 5 der 9 Planten auf den neun dort gekennzeichneten Positionen befinden.

にょきにょきウドラ (NyokiNyokiUdolla) is a game from Japan, featuring Udolla who is the mascot for Tachikawa, a city inside the western part of the Tokyo metropolis. Udolla is a large white monster with tentacles from the top of its head, which is based upon Tachikawa udon, a local specialty dish.

In the game, players move their own Udolla around Tachikawa, building up love by visiting the sites and attractions in the city to collect heart chips. Players use these chips to build their own Tachikawa, with the most wonderful Tachikawa winning the game.
user summary

Diablo: The Board Game is a co-op narrative-driven adventure board game in which players will gather a party of custom characters — known as Champions — and venture through the world of Sanctuary, help those in need, explore dungeons and tombs full of undead horrors, battle the legions of the Netherworld, and traverse the Realms of Creation.

—description from the publisher

Night Night is a fun bedtime game with 8 ways to play. Created for babies (yes, babies!), toddlers and up!

Create a bedtime routine, tell stories, count sheep and play games to develop language, imagination, observation, memory and more!  Includes familiar bedtime objects, friendly characters, fun locations and sleepy yawns all beautifully illustrated by Parker Jacobs (Yo Gabba Gabba!) giving you new ways to bond with your children.

Night Night is part of the Small Fries Games range designed to grow with your family.  The games and activities are suitable for different stages of development and can be played by children of different ages at the same time.

8 in 1 games and activities for 1 - 4 players. Suitable for ages 0 to 4+. (5 are co-operative, 3 competitive.) The colourful, A6 sized laminated cards are designed for little hands and made in the UK. (FSC card, single use plastic free and Carbon Balanced with the World Land Trust.)

—description from the designer

Roubo no Museu (Portuguese for "Museum Heist" - not to be confused with Alex Randolph's game with the same name in English).

The objective of the game is to be the thief who has the most valuable objects stolen from the museum at the end of the game. Players draw specific roles that have requirements regarding the objects to be stolen. During the game, action cards are drawn that allow movement, the exchange of objects and the eventual return of objects to the museum.

Because of the darkness, players are forced to choose stolen objects only by their silhouette, which can lead to the theft of unwanted items. Players can also steal objects from each other.
The game ends when the action cards run out: the player who has the items with the highest value in their possession - including the bonus for stealing the objects indicated on their role card - wins.
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
Let's cook up some fun with Yuck or Yum? a rhyming card game with 8 ways to play. Created for toddlers and up!

Cook silly stews, match recipes, find the poop and other games to explore the concepts of rhyme, prediction and deduction plus develop language, memory, observation and more.  Includes delicious and disgusting ingredients playfully illustrated by Parker Jacobs (Yo Gabba Gabba!) giving you new ways to bond with your children.

Yuck or Yum? is part of the Small Fries Games range designed to grow with your family.  The games and activities are suitable for different stages of development and can be played by children of different ages at the same time.

8 in 1 games and activities for 1 - 4 players. Suitable for ages 18m to 4+. (5 are co-operative, 3 competitive.) The colourful, A6 sized laminated cards are designed for little hands and made in the UK. (FSC card, single use plastic free and Carbon Balanced with the World Land Trust.)

—description from the designer

This extended edition includes the basic version of the "Chicken Chicken" and two expansions: Easter Egg and Christmas.

Chicken Chicken is a crazy card game for people of all ages. Count the eggs that appear in the movie and make the cut when there are at least 5. Watch out for the crazy cast that causes quite a bit of confusion. A party card game full of laughter, dynamics and emotions.

boardgame: Five Parsecs From Home: Fixer's Guidebook
description:
Armed with this third expansion in the series, you will be able to expand and modify your game to offer new and exciting possibilities as well as undertake new missions, making the world of Five Parsecs more detailed and elaborate than ever before! Join a faction and get rewarded doing jobs for them alone, but beware as things hot up on the streets when they clash with other factions! New movement and wound systems for grittier play, random terrain and environments, plus new stealth and city streets missions!

New material includes:

-Generate random table terrain setups with four different environmental types.
-An alternative wound system that can result in a wider range of effects when a character is hit. 
-More detailed grim post-battle injuries for those who want a grittier game experience. 
-An alternative movement system that divides the table into a 3x3 square grid and allows fast, open-ended movement. 
-Faction rules. Now, you can work for various employers who will reward your loyalty as you do more jobs for them.
-Factions will also get involved in struggles with each other, making your planetary campaigns more exciting and dramatic. 
-New Stealth missions featuring high-tension commando-style raids and infiltrations against sentries. 
-Street fights take you downtown. Roam the streets of a mega city, undertaking missions, dealing with the locals and hoping you can make it out of there before the Enforcers show up and start arresting everybody.
-The package is rounded out with a new mission selection system that folds the new mission types into the core rules, allowing for any mission to be a street fight or stealth job.

Finally, a comprehensive campaign setup and campaign turn sequence summarise all of the options from all three expansions.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: アメリカンコミックコレクション (American Comics Collection)
description:
アメリカンコミックコレクション (American Comics Collection) is an auction game from Japan. Players are bidding on volumes of comicbooks, trying to complete series. The game has a deck of cards for the four titles with seven volumes each. Each card has symbols, the lower the volume number, the more symbols.

At the end of the game, the winner is the player with the most points, calculated by multiplying the symbols written on the cards and the total number of volumes, plus the remaining cash in hand. Getting the #1 and #7 issues gives high value.

The auction is a straight bid process, but the amount each bid increments is set by the volume number. Completing a series helps, but exhausting other players' funds by pushing the bid up is useful.

—user summary

Horrorshow is a televised romp through a sadistic, otherworldly game show’s studio full of monsters and mayhem where we humans are low on the food chain.  The 36 contestants are trying collectively not only to escape, but to do so with enough points to keep the sponsors happy.

Mechanically, you will move your 6 teams of contestants down many corridors to obtain studio cards (representing rooms in the game show's studio) that are worth points.  These cards can also combine to spell EXIT, without which those points are worthless.  Unfortunately, these studio cards will also often cause a small drop in sanity.  So why the hurry to go in?  Well, chasing these contestants through the corridors are the insatiable Horrors who will have a strong part to play in dwindling the number of your contestants throughout the game.

You must manage the sanity of your contestants, worry about which contestant completes each corridor and claims the studio card, mind the number of cards left in the movement card deck, and keep up a brisk pace in the corridors ahead of the fast-moving and eponymous Horrors.

And even if you manage to get someone out... you need to hit a particular points threshold to win.

This game can be difficult.

—description from the designer

This is vaguely based on the game Eat At Ralph's because both games involve expelling the foods you feed him.  That is the only similarity because each has a unique mechanical system for the expelling of the food.  Phil is a large mechanical head with a small opening for his mouth where you feed him different foods.  There are dice and cards involved in the game.

Game Play:
All the p[layers roll their (soft) die  at the same time
(There are stipulations)
1. Everyone rolls the same number:  Reroll
2. All players that roll the same number skip that turn of feeding Phil
3. The remaining players or the ones that did not roll the same number which could be all four players, then must feed chuck based on the highest number going first

Fill Phil's mouth with the amount of food you rolled on the die
As you fill his mouth he begins to expand until he eventually POPS open!
If you made Phil UP CHUCK, then you flip over one of 4 shirt cards over in front of you.  The last one to have a clean shirt wins.

Food types,
chicken, ice cream, pizza and hot dogs

I hope you are not afraid of clowns!  This game uses a stand up cardboard clown with white cup eyes and a cup for a mouth!  Unlike the traditional game Tiddly Winks, this plays much different and uses a special playing piece that they call a "Wink" which is a wink connected to a wink and each wink side has a different texture on it.  There is a smoother side and a courser side depending on which is easier for you to launch.

Unlike Tiddly Winks, you are aiming to get one of these special winks in either of the Clowns eyes or in his mouth.  The points are different because the eyes are harder to get the wink into.

Points
10 points for each wink that lands in his mouth
50 points for each wink that lands in his eyes

The winner is the individual player or the team that gets to 150 points.

Parts include the clown and stands - 6 Blue and 6 Red Winks and 3 white target cups (which look like the top of a paint spray can)

Knott's Scary Farm themed Monopoly game.

"Stake a claim on mazes and scare zones to bury your opponent in a fog-filled nightmare."

"Haunting characters and some of your favorite scare zones makes up this special version of the classic board game! Bring the chills and thrills to your home! Perfect for collectors and players alike."

Examine images and race to find words that start with different letters. Each word scores you a token on your bingo board. The first five-in-a-row wins! The game has two levels – younger players play with picture boards. The game supports learning to read. Special cards provide excitement! There's also a classification game to develop vocabulary and logical thinking skills.

A dice-rolling game with football theming. African wildlife and culture is important in the theme as well.

Players roll a die to move around the board. A player then draws a card from the conference space they landed on and rolls the five red dice. If the total is equal to our larger than the card’s “team score” the game, that card and the cards respective number of “bowl points”.

The first player to 10,000 bowl points wins.

Two stranded teams of astronauts face off in a tournament of mini-games that's scored like a tug-of-war. Players fight over the remains of their landing craft, held ransom by the local aliens. Through brain teasers, social manipulation, dexterity challenges and more, one team will earn the right to put their ship back together and blast off for Earth! The other team will get ritually sacrificed.

—description from the designer

Is the 50's and you discovered the thrilling world of the fast-food takeaway! But also, your adversaries!

Takeaway! is a game for 1 to 4 entrepreneurs, where you must administrate your movement and goods to buy new products and monopolize the market!

The player with their Food Truck at the start of the Road takes a turn.

In your turn you must advance your Food Truck and then make a choice:


     Get X Resources of the type shown on the Tile you landed.


Or

     Buy the Tile you landed by paying X Resources of the type shown on the Tile.


“X” is the number of empty Tiles you move through! So, plan your movements ahead!

Every Tile you buy is going to give you special actions and benefits through the game, but also, they give you income at the end of the game!

But beware! Tiles with a Food Truck on are blocked for all the players, so be careful with your choices, they could block your ticket to victory!

The player with most money wins the game!

—description from the designer

Give each players some counters. Not many if you want a quick game. The more counters you start with, the longer the game. Each player contribute some coins to the pot.
The game is played with a die by two people. The aim of the game is to see who can fly around the world the fastest. One player starts at No. 1, the other at No. 42, so in the opposite direction. Whoever flies past the other gets two tokens from the pot. If the players end up standing on top of each other, the flying tour starts again. If you get to 42 or further, you win the pot. Anyone who lands on the Moon must miss a turn.
Play continues until one player is out of counters.

—user summary

The Tobruk Game is an Australian post World War 2 strategy War game where two opposing armies set out to capture each others flags.

Each side has 33 pieces of different army ranks, from Generals to Sappers and mines and a spy. The board is a grid and armies deploy their pieces in any arrangement from behind a lake.

Players take turn to advance any piece except mines and their flag. A playing may attack an opposing piece and if their piece is of the same rank or higher then the attack is successful and the enemy piece goes off the board. Mines destroy all attackers except the Sappers. The Spy piece has the ability to move backwards and in attack it is superior to all pieces except mines. If attacked the Spy piece it is defeated by all.

The winner is the side that attacks and captures the enemy's flag.

—user summary

description:
PARADOMINETOR (パラドミネーター) is a trick-taking game from Japan, with a sci-fi theme of time paradox. Players represent various time-travel organizations that are seeking to reach the Space-Time Management Bureau first.

The game is played with chips instead of cards, and these chips are kept behind a player's screen. One side of the chip shows the suit and rank, and the other side shows the time: past, present, or future. Each chip is unique in the game.

Players follow suit as in a normal trick-taking game, but when chips with the same suit and rank are played, they become trump and are resolved based on their time phase; the earliest phase wins the trick, with the latter one earning half a trick.

After fifteen tricks, the player with the highest score wins.

•••

タイムパラドックス、サイエンスフィクションがテーマのトリックテイキングです。プレイヤーはタイムトラベル技術を手に入れた大小さまざまな組織の代表となって、SF設定で当たり前のように存在している、時空管理局を目指します。15トリック行い最多トリック獲得者が勝者です。カードではなくチップでゲームを行います。同スート、同ランクのチップが複数存在し、表のスート、ランクの情報、裏の過去、現在、未来の情報で、3つの情報が合わさりオールユニークとなります。基本マストフォローですが、テキサスショーダウンの様に場にある色は出すことができます。同スート、同ランクのチップが場に出ると切り札化して最強となります。パラドックスで勝ち残ったプレイヤー同士で裏面を確認し、より過去のプレイヤーが1トリック、そのほかが0.5トリックを得ます。
description by publisher
designers: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)
artists: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)

description:
SPACE OPEN is fast to learn and easy to play. And yet it always remains exciting because there is a cool mix between strategy and luck.

The Plot:
Cats are tired of dogs playing and having fun. they want to get rid of them, so they shoot all tennis balls into space.
the dogs are going to space to bring their precious tennis balls back.

The game is played in a series of rounds. Every player gets 5 cards and uses them to move on the board. The goal is to collect the tennis balls and to bring them into the spaceships. The first team to fill both spaceships wins the game.

Can play 1 vs 1 (2 players) or 2 vs 2 (4 players).
The cool thing about the game is that every player can decide if he wants to hide or fight. No one is ever really safe until the game is over.

-description from publisher

With the addition of these additional cards, players will have new ways to play that create new spatial challenges and add completely new gameplay elements! These promo cards are a special THANK YOU to all backers for being early supporters and will not be included with future editions of the game!

The modern pentathlon sport was invented by baron Pierre de Coubertin at beginning of XXth century, as a new olympic contest. The boardgame "Pentathlon Moderne" allows two to four players disput the five disciplines (fencing, swimming, riding, pistol shooting and running) of this sport on a boardgame. The aim of the game is to win more gold medals than your opponents.

Each player directs 5 athlete pawns and makes them progress on the different tracks by playing cards. The initial hand of cards is ten. When a player plays three of a kind, for example three "horse riding" cards, he must roll the dice to move his pawn forward on the equestrian course. You can also play a penalty card which cancels a set of threes placed by an opponent, or a bonus card which allows you to roll the dice twice on your turn. You can also simply discard a card and draw a new one.

Each time a discipline finishes, a gold, silver and bronze medal is distributed to the top three finishers.

—user summary

The following crowdfunding exclusive expansions are part of the Crowdfunding Exclusive edition of Cretaceous Rails:

Cretaceous Skies Expansion

Take to the skies, adding an aviary to your resort for tons of action chaining opportunities. This expert level expansion adds a high degree of strategic depth with very few additional rules.

Variable Start Expansion

For a little extra variation, draft variable start cards in reverse turn order at the beginning of the game. Each card determines which tourists, dinos, jungle tokens, and cards you start with, as well as how many rails you start with.

End Game Scoring Expansion

End Game Scoring Tiles add extra scoring conditions for more strategic depth.

5th Executive expansion

Explore deeper strategies with the 5th Executive expansion. With four new resort cards that add a fifth executive, open up more opportunities to develop your resort in new and exciting ways.

4 Volcano Miniatures

Bring the cretaceous jungle to life with four beautiful prepainted 3D volcanoes!

-descriptions from the Kickstarter page

HAWAII: The Card Game is a family-friendly card game that takes players on a Hawaiian adventure. The game combines elements of Go Fish with an educational twist, making it easy to learn and enjoyable for all ages. The theme of the game revolves around exploring the diversity of Hawaii and learning how to pronounce Hawaiian words.
In this game, players take turns asking each other for cards that match certain categories. Players aim to collect sets of cards, and the goal is to collect the most sets.

Desert War: Area 88 is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) board-wargame that was produced for sale only at game shows in Japan, such as Tokyo Game Market. To get one you would place an order in advance, the game would be made and brought to the show for you to pick up. It was not for sale to the general public.

This game is based on the anime series Area 88, and deals with mercenary fight pilots fighting for a fictitious kingdom called Aslan.

This particular title uses the "Commando" system from Decision Games. It is a solitaire game where you are trying to earn enough money to end your contract with the Asland Defense Forces and return back to Japan.  Each mission you complete brings you closer to your goal.

Game comes with rules, map, event sheet, and counters.  Assembly is required.

As a powerful Wizard, you know that the key to unlocking your true potential lies in collecting Crystals and mastering Spells. In the game of Cursed Crystals, you have the opportunity to put your skills to the test and become the most powerful Wizard of them all.

Super easy to learn, challenging to master. Every time you play this game it is different.

Object of the Game:
You are a Wizard who gains power from Crystals and Spells. In order to win you must hold the most power of all Wizards before the 3 Curses are cast.

Card Types:
Crystal Cards: Are the source of your Power Points
Spell Cards: Spells boost your Power Points or have in game functions
Curse Cards: These decide when the game ends

Setup:
The Eldest Wizard shuffles & deals 4 cards to each other Wizard. Keep your cards secret.
Place the remaining cards in a pile face down. This is the “Wizards Library” Pile
The Wizard to the left of the Eldest Wizard goes first.

How to Play:
At the start of your turn take the top card from the Wizards Library to your hand.

Next, choose to Cast a Spell and/or Curse. 

     Follow the instructions on the Card
     When a Spell is cast discard it into the “Forgotten Tomb” Pile
     When a Curse is cast place the card aside on the “Altar of Death” Pile.


At the end of their turn a Wizards must only hold a total of 6 Cards. Discard Crystals or Spells into the Forgotten Tomb until your hand only has 6 cards. Curse Cards cannot be discarded at this time.

It is now the next Wizards turn

Cards that say ‘Cast Any Time’ can be used at any stage of the game.

Ending the Game:
A game ends when 3 Curse Cards are in the Altar of Death, or at the end of the turn of the Wizard who drew the last card from the Wizards Library. Once the game ends no more Spells can be cast.
At the end of a game each Wizard must only hold a total of 6 Cards. Place Crystals or Spells into the Forgotten Tomb until your hand only has 6 cards. Curse Cards can be discarded at this time.

Declaring the Most Powerful Wizard:
Add up the value of Power Points in your hand, including any boosts from your End of Game Spells.
The Wizard with the highest total wins.

—description from the designer

Players will recruit sheep to create a powerful army for battling each other over planets and powerful space gems. Strategies can be built upon various resources such as actions, special actions (Space gems), sheep units, and many more. In each round players will compete with each other for a planet, resources, a set of space gems. Ones with the most powerful army will get planet and a gem,but a player can choose to surrender and bring all the resources they get back to their empire. Both planets and resources are counted as victory points. Who gets to 16 points first will rule the "BAA's Universe"

A player who reached 16 victory points first wins the game.(Planets and resources are counted as victory points which can be earned each round.)

—description from the designer

description:
Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Imperial Chancellor is a doujin (fan-made) game that was only available at the Japanese game shows, such at Tokyo Game Market.  It had to be ordered in advance and then you could pick it up at the show.  It was not made for the general public.

This game has two sets of rules and can be played in two ways.  First, it can be played using rules that are similar to the game Imperium by GDW.  Second, it can be played solitaire using the Commando system from Decision Games.

In either game, you are playing the role of the Imperial Chancellor and are trying to accomplish your objectives in the game.

The game includes 2 sets of rules, map, counter sheet, events generator, and a player aid/turn sheet.  Assembly is required.

There are four starting places on the board. Each player takes 4 figures of the same color and places them in the starting position of the same color. The players take turns to roll and move. When moving, one skips over all other figures and only counts the empty spaces. The winner is the player who brings his 4 vagabonds to the finish line first.

One can only start after rolling a 1 or 6. Whoever rolls 1 places a figure on space 1 of their driveway. Whoever rolls 6 moves forward 6 spaces and comes to their own intersection and can roll the die again. On the roll of a "one, he may immediately jump to the finish. On any other roll he has to enter the round course around the board.

If a figure lands on a space with a figure of the same color, it sits next to it, rolls again. But if a figure hits a figure  of a different color, it is pushed off the path. The pushed figure must go into the starting place at the next intersection behind, even if it is a foreign starting place.

After completing the circuit, the figure has to stop at its stopping point. Only the next time a 1 is rolled it is allowed to jump from the stopping point to the finish. As long as a figure stands at its stopping point, subsequent figures of its color may jump over it's back to the finish with every throw that extends beyond the stopping point. If a figure of another color is sitting at the stopping point, a figure of the color of the stop point is blocked and may not jump over it to the finish.

—user summary

Put yourself in the shoes of a captain of the human or alien fleet. Use your skills to overcome your enemy in frantic and exciting battles between large spaceships. Choose a side and fight to prevent the blockade from falling or to overcome it. An officially licensed game from Corvus Belli with the factions and sides represented in their universe.

—description from the designer

Roost is a card game about strategy, memorisation, alliances and, most importantly, birds. Players must avoid being at the bottom of the pecking order and stay aligned with the flock to fly away to overall victory. Roost features a modified rule set for 2-3 players and comes with 36 additional cards that can be swapped in to bring new dynamic twists to the game.

Your goal in Roost is to have the lowest score at the end of the game. Players start the game by only knowing half of their hand and must use abilities, alliances and memory to ensure they win each round. On each turn, a player can either pick up a card from the:

     Unknown Pile - the player may view it privately. Then, they must choose to either place it directly onto the Discard Pile OR replace 1 of their own cards with it OR place it on top of an opponent's face-up card.
     Discard Pile - the player must replace 1 of their own cards with it.


At any time, players can match cards onto the top discard pile card.

Most birds contain abilities where you can switch, steal, discover, enact revenge, boost your final score or FLIP cards. Flipping cards means that players can now, at any point, stack birds onto the face-up cards of an opponent, creating some massive point hauls if not dealt with promptly. Some other abilities involve play direction changing, collecting specific bird breeds, and even taking a significant point hit to enact an ability in the following round.

Event cards introduce one-time game cards, which can be extraordinarily crucial in certain circumstances and give players who are losing miserably a chance of a comeback. A round of Roost ends when a player calls 'Roost', with all other players getting one more turn. If the player wins the Roost, they get a point deduction bonus; otherwise, they get harshly penalised. Beware of Roosting too early, as some cards specifically penalise jumping the gun. Roost is played over multiple rounds, with the game ending when a score threshold is reached for the amount of players playing.

—description from the designer

Set on a 4x5 grid featuring land, water, and shore terrain, Fortify presents a dynamic battlefield where you must outwit your opponent. Command three unique unit types: Tanks, Infantry, and Battleships. As you take turns, you'll enlist new units, skillfully maneuver them into advantageous positions, fortify your formation for added strength, and launch fierce attacks on enemy units.

But Fortify is not just about tactics; it's about strategy. Tanks are lightning-fast, Infantry enlists at double speed, and Battleships fortify on the shores. These special abilities add depth and excitement to every move you make.

To emerge victorious, you must fortify four strategic squares on the grid, creating intense moments of surprise and anticipation! And for advanced players, two additional units—The Chopper and the Artillery—are included, offering a multitude of strategic possibilities.

With two map options and 5-10 minute fast game sessions, will you outmaneuver your opponent and claim victory in this thrilling war of wits?

—description from the designer

A robbery takes place in the most crowded house ever, nothing is safe and everyone is a suspect!

Are you the thief? Collect everything you can and escape before it's too late! The police are already on the lookout and if he catches you the game is over.

Aren't you the thief? Pick up as many objects as you can, but be careful who you cross paths with, after all, no one knows who is who or even who else is at home... From Publisher.

In the Runebeasts Deck-Building Game, capture and summon creatures in your quest to be the ultimate beast-tetherer.

Runebeasts gives players the chance to evolve their beasts to make them stronger. Players can even try to catch the coveted legendary runebeasts! To win the game, players will have showdowns with their opponents around the table.

In this twist on classic deck-builders, each player will start with an asymmetric deck full of beasts. They will use those beasts on their turn to fight and attempt to collect more powerful beasts in the market, to collect gems and get bonuses, or to power up and evolve their beasts!

Each of the 10 different beast types provide different benefits, so you can customize your deck to fit your play-style. Will you roll the die and take your chances with legendary beasts, or take a more conservative approach?

Halfway into the game, players will start to engage in showdowns; programming their beasts in their hand and gaining points for every battle won. Earn 10 points to win the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Big Business: The Famous National Money Game
description:
This vintage game was released by Transogram in 1948.  They actually labeled it their "Gold Medal Game".  The game centers around finances and provides each player with $100,00 to see who ends up with the most money! The publishers claim that it is The Newest National Money Game". This business game is played as each player is given $100,000 and then advances around the board.  Assets are tracked to eventually determine the winner. You can also play the quick version of the game where as soon as one player goes bankrupt all other players then count their money to see who has the most, the winner!

ROMPANION (ロンパニオン) is a party game from Japan, in which players tell a story and then discuss it to reach a conclusion that is not just black or white.

The designer was inspired by the idea of create a more nuanced outcome to a discussion and produced a game with a gradual scale in five stages, with matching colours and symbols. A player draws a combination of cards and tells a story based upon the prompts on the cards. The other players discuss the story with a view to bringing players to accept their analysis.

The player that brings more people to their view is the winner.

description:
Bad Ideas (lead to better ones) is a card game that develops creativity through play. When the cards are combined together, they create a randomized design problem to solve. Then it's up to you and the other players to think of the most inventive solution. The game can be used in for corporate brainstorming, education or played with friends.

How to Play
tools
use the bundled dry erase markers on the blank erasable cards or markers on paper

timekeeping 
use a smartphone timer, egg timer, or anything that allows custom times between 1 and 5 minutes.

play
choose a ‘PLAY MODE’ for gameplay and judging details

winning
after the round is over a winner is chosen by blind vote: 

     assign numbers to the players ideas
     write your choice on a blank whiteboard card or paper scrap
     reveal your choices a the same time and tally them up
     you can’t vote for yourself!


wild cards
if a ‘draw 2’ card is flipped over during play, 2 new cards should be pulled from the deck and used instead

skill level
if you want to really practice your drawing skills, multiply the play time listed by 3

Play mode: Iterate
cards
all players use the same set of 3

play

     flip the user card and sketch any product for that user [1m timer]
     flip the goal card and modify your idea to fit that goal [2m timer]
     flip the constraint card and modify your idea to fit that new constraint [3m timer]
     take turns sharing all 3 ideas


voting
judge players on how creatively their idea has evolved

Play Mode: Blue Sky
cards
each player is dealt their own set of 3

play

     flip over all the dealt cards at once
     sketch out a product idea that helps the user achieve their goal while accounting for the constraint card [5m timer]
     take turns sharing your ideas


voting
judge players on how creatively they interpret all 3 cards dealt to them

boardgame: Lightning Strikes!: The World Wars of the 20th Century
description:
Lightning Strikes! gives players the chance to write a completely new narrative of 20th-century world history. In the full campaign game players create a new global history by managing their countries from the outbreak of war in 1914 through 1954, navigating potentially several world wars, as well as intervening lulls of global peace, while overseeing the development of exciting and devastating new weapons, until one player’s power bloc emerges victorious. There are also many shorter scenarios (from single campaigns to an entire world war) for players with limited time or focused interest. Lightning Strikes! is based on the legendary World in Flames family of detailed wargames.

description:
Mecha Battle 2 is a predecessor to ZOD.

The game is about mecha combat in the future, much like Battletech. Competing corporate powers fight for supremacy in urban ruins, with infantry, tanks, aircraft and large robots.

The game comes with plastic infantry, mecha, and bases, plus unit data cards, a card factory, dice and rules.

This is a monopoly game made for the town of Red Wing, Minnesota, it is a first edition that was presented to the Red Wing Area Chamber Of Commerce.  This is the lovely town that makes Red Wing Shoes and Red Wing Pottery!

Holotype: Mesozoic Europe adds a whole new continent of Dinosaurs, Marine Reptiles, and Pterosaurs. It also introduces ancient Crocs with the new Specimen type of Crurotarsi. New mechanics like Focus Boards, Bone Beds and new Milestones add even more options that can be used with Europe or with North America.

Focus Boards give each player unique starting bonuses as well as small goals that can be completed during the game for instant rewards and extra Victory Points at the end of the game.

Bone Beds are a new type of card that can be gained via Field Expedition cards. They allow players to return to a location they discovered to extract a guaranteed type and amount of fossils on a later turn.

With Europe and the Crurotarsi mini-expansion, 3 new Milestones are added to Holotype. These Milestones and new Variants can be used with any version of Holotype you are playing.

The core Holotype: Mesozoic North America game is required to use this expansion.  This product is intended as a game supplement and cannot be played by itself.

-description from designer

If you don't drink, you can't win — and if you drink too much, you lose. Enjoy the dilemma.

Possibly safe energy drinks / ぎりぎりエナドリ is a card game in which you build up energy drinks one card at a time, trying not to overdo it.

Hikadyat is driven by stories told across many generations, from various cultures and times. We are very ambitious for this competitive Collectable Card Game (CCG) because of its nature of being highly collectable for collectors and strongly competitive for TCG players.

Gameplay: A shared tableau-building game where you only score at the end. You will only score cards that match the suit of the two cards in your hand. This game will satisfy players’ itch for clever card play and spatial scoring.
Players can enjoy mastering one deck at a time, or mix and match decks for even more competitiveness and replayability with multiple decks sold separately.

The objective is to accumulate the highest score. Players will gain points from the cards in the shared tableau that match the suit of their hand cards at the game's conclusion. This not only requires strategic thinking, but also provides the ultimate proving ground for players to demonstrate their adaptability and foresight through card placement and hand management.

Al-Kisah, meaning 'The Story' in Arabic, is a highly competitive 18-card game that showcases a diverse array of folklore, fairytales, and stories from various cultures. This deck is based on Malay folklore. Features Malay folklore such as Curse of Mahsuri, The Devouring Rock, The Love Story of the Gunung Ledang Princess, Putih Merah, King of the Fanged Wings, Legend of Badang, The Mousedeer & The Crocodile, and Si Tanggang the Ungrateful Son.

A perfect starter deck with its simple scoring cards.
Whether you’re a newcomer discovering these stories for the first time or a long-time fan of Malaysian folklore, this deck offers an easy entry point into the world of Hikadyat while still giving plenty of room for clever plays and exciting matchups.

—description from the designer

Only the light provided by the Tuputupuas Messengers can repel the darkness that is trying to harm your Tikis.

In Tuamotu, you summon the various Tuputupuas Messengers embodied by Rays, Sharks, Turtles and Geckos to transmit the protective light to you.

Will you have enough light before darkness falls on you?

To win, you'll need to collect 25 shells and avoid holding cards with high Tiki values.

To do this, players will have to match pairs of cards to make the Tuputupuas messengers appear.

As soon as you put a pair of cards together, you win shells and you can apply effects to all the players (discard cards, return shells, donate a card, block cards from the island, renew the island).

On their turn, players can take an island card, pay for a shell on an island card, pay for two shells and discard a card, pay for 5 shells and renew the island. He can then place the pairs of cards in front of him and apply the effects in order. He may not have more than 3 cards in his hand at the end of his turn.

The game ends when a player has collected 25 shells. The scores are then determined by subtracting the Tiki values of the remaining cards in hand from the number of shells collected.

-description from publisher

A real time social deduction game for 4 - 8 players that last 10 minutes.

Players take on a hidden role at the beginning of the game: either as one of the plotters who seek to blow up the houses of parliament, or as a secret agent, working against them from the shadows in this fast paced game of subterfuge.

At the beginning of the game, one bomb card is drawn and placed face up per player. These cards will have a list of components that must be attached to that card in order to prime the bomb.

For the plotters, the primary focus is to draw and play cards from the component deck, matching the symbols on the bomb cards. These are played face down, and once four have been placed, these cards are shuffled and revealed. If even one component doesn't match then those components are discarded and the bomb resets.

The role of the agent is to act like a plotter but to spoil the priming of bombs by placing components that don't match. Subtelty and deception are paramamout as careless agents will be easy to spot.

All players begin the game with access to a pool of suspicion tokens. These can be given to players who are suspected of being agents. Three or more will see the receiving player eliminated from the game.

in a way that As the game progresses, players will arouse suspicion and are voted out if they have three or more suspicion tokens.

Players can also draw from the investigation deck. These cards are designed to help root out the agent but come at the expense of precious time that could be otherwise spent priming the bombs.

Featuring a 10 minute sand timer, once the game begins it doesn't stop until the plotters achieve victory or time runs out and they're caught.

-description from publisher

The game usually lasts up to 30 minutes, up to 6 players can participate, with the possibility of an additional seventh who runs the bank and takes care of reading the questions and dealing the cards.

At the start of the game, all players get 8 cards from their country, which determine how much money they get and how much they move at the start of the game. This starts in the selected rocket on the planet Earth, on the starting part of the game board, divided into 4 stages of the journey to the red planet.. Earth - Moon - International Space Station - Mars.

Each stage has an initial set of 60 quiz questions on the history of space and the history of space exploration, astronomy, chemistry and physics, and space programs, missions and vessels. The player moves the rocket around the designated fields of the stage by throwing a dedicated rocket game gadget called Lucky Star. A successful throw gives the rocket momentum and gives the player a number from 1 to 5. The player is then asked a question. If the answer is correct, the number drawn is doubled, if the answer is incorrect, it remains the same. The player then moves the rocket by the number of squares gained. Players then move their rockets around the playing surface by squirming. If they manage to land on the moon, they can buy extra tickets there for booster or rocket safety, and if they successfully land on the International Space Station, they get the option to buy a private space station. This comes in handy when competing rockets collide, because without a collision safety card or mentioned station, the rocket returns to the stage's starting point. When the rockets reach Mars, they have four landing sites available. When a rocket occupies one of these, it only needs the last minimum frc to land on Mars, which must be exactly 1. Whoever lands on Mars first wins the game!

-description from publisher

boardgame: 怪盗カプリッチョ (Capriccio the Phantom Thief)
description:
怪盗カプリッチョ (Capriccio the Phantom Thief) is a deduction game from Japan.

A group of phantom thieves sneak into a millionaire’s mansion on a desert island. However, it turns out to be a cursed mansion!

The house layout constantly changes and, to open each door, you need to clear the child's prank-like mission! Meanwhile, a thief must not forget to steal at all times! Get out of the mansion and escape the island by boat while stealing jewels from the resident's room! What? Before you know, the resident is dead!

It's utterly insane to be on the boat with the murderer! So it's time to vote on who took the advantage of the confusion and killed the resident, and get that person off the boat!
description by publisher

Ninjanken / 忍者んけん is a rock/paper/scissors-style game in which you can throw one of five "jutsu" (hand symbols) against other players to try to defeat them and gain scrolls.

Each round, each player simultaneously throws a jutsu while holding a Ninjanken summary card with their other hand. When a player scores by landing rock vs. scissors, etc., they move their finger along the scroll chart on their card to show everyone else their score; additionally, they must fold down the jutsu they used, which means it's now off limits for them to use.

To reset their jutsu, they must throw the "okay" jutsu — but if someone throws the "devil horns" jutsu at the same time, then the player slides their finger toward the bottom of their card. If they get horned again, they're out of the game!

In Gravekeepers, you become a guide to the spirit world. While possessing two gravekeepers, you patrol a cemetery of four rows and four columns, listening to the troubles of ghosts wandering in the graveyard and guiding them to the spirit world. Be careful not to let yourself fall down by accepting too many worries...

In more detail, first lay out the graveyard cards in a 4x4 grid, then place ghost cards on specific cards based on the icons on them: white ghosts on white white, black on black. The white gravekeeper moves vertically in the graveyard, while the black gravekeeper moves left and right.

On a turn, declare "white" or "black", then move the matching gravekeeper. Collect all of the ghost cards in the row or column next to where the gravekeeper stopped, then place one card from your hand face down on the graveyard card at the intersection of the two gravekeepers. Finally, activate the effects on the empty graveyard cards in the row or column where you collected cards.

At the end of the game, calculate your score based on your cards in hand and card effects. Whoever has the most points is eliminated, then the player with the most ghost cards wins.

ミュルマミウスの虜 (Myurumamiusu no Toriko) (Seven Knights Bewitched) is a co-operative game from Japan. (The Japanese title translates as Prisoner Of Myrmamius).

The publisher says:
This is a 'logic puzzle / asymmetrical / invitation / hidden / cooperative game' where the knights are divided into two camps, Knights and Witches.

The knights line up in order without being fooled by the lies of the witch Myrmamius. When a knight sees a witch's tile, he becomes a 'prisoner of Myrmamius' and turns over to the witch.

「騎士団」と「魔女」の2陣営に分かれて、騎士団が魔女ミュルマミウスの嘘に惑わされることなく、順番に隊列を並べる『論理パズル・非対称型・招待隠匿・協力ゲーム』です。騎士が魔女のタイルを見ると「ミュルマミウスの虜」となって魔女に寝返ります。
description by publisher

スパイスピン (SPYSPIN) is a two player game from Japan. The Cold War has begun, and Eastern and Western spies roam the city, trying to outwit and capture each other.

The players have four spies, with each tile showing an innocuous, innocent side and a revealed spy side. The tiles each have different patterns of movement, and these are also varied on the flipside. So the fashion shopper Karin moves 2 places left or right, but flips to spy Karin to move 2 places up or down. Likewise, other spies move orthogonally and then diagonally, and so on. An icon in the corner shows the flipside movement.

Players start by deploying their spies on the 5x5 board, and then take turns moving a spy, which must be flipped after moving. The first player to catch all the opponents spies is the winner.

The game is packaged in a DVD case.
user summary

The publisher says:
・Competitive puzzles with different movements between front and back sides
・A battle between spies who can read each other's targets until the end.
・Children can play with it, and adults can worry about it too. A brain game that the whole family can play

・表と裏で動きが変わる対戦パズル
・先の先まで狙いを読み合うスパイたちの戦い
・子供も遊べて大人も悩む。家族で遊べる頭脳戦
description by publisher

When Thunder Rolled (WTR) is a solitaire simulation of the United States Air Force's contribution to Operation Rolling Thunder during the Vietnam War. Rolling Thunder began on 3 March 1965 and concluded on 31 October 1968. During the campaign the USAF and United States Navy bombed targets throughout North Vietnam. The area around Hanoi and Haiphong was the most dangerous area and was split between the USAF and USN. WTR simulates the USAF campaign using the F-105D as the primary bomber.

WTR gives you the opportunity to assume the role of a USAF F-105D pilot, attempting to survive 100 missions over North Vietnam. The game is based on the format originated by the Korean War simulation Able Dog.

A few things I want to say before you take a look. The first is that the counters are...well, let me say I'm not a graphics guy. They're the best I can do but they allow you to play the game. The second is that, as are other games of this genre, When Thunder Rolled is a dice-roller. If you hate this type of game, you'll hate this one, too. The third is that I have taken some shortcuts that make parts of the game somewhat abstract (air-to-air combat is an example). Lastly, if you have comments, positive and negative, please PM me. I'm happy to adapt things that will improve the game. If you love the game, I'm glad. If you hate it, I understand that, too. But I hope you'll at least try it, even if you don't want to fly a full 100 mission tour.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 実録! ほんとにあった呪いの神経衰弱 (True story! Real Cursed Nervous Breakdown)
description:
In 【閲覧注意】実録！ほんとにあった呪いの神経衰弱【最恐ホラー】 ([Warning] True story! Real Cursed Nervous Breakdown [The Scariest Horror], players are turning cards and looking for matching images. If they find a matching image, they take the pair of cards out of the game. But if they find an unmatched pair, they draw a new card, look at it in secret, and place it face down with the rest.

However, there are two cards showing a weeping doll which is cursed. If a player turns up the cursed doll, all the players must immediately use their protective talisman, a votive prayer sheet, by slamming it down on the doll. The last player to do this receives a candle, meaning they are one step closer to death. If another player is then too slow, the candle moves on. But if the player with the candle fails a second time, they are killed by the curse and eliminated from the game. There is a terrible demon card too, which immediately kills the player with the candle.

All the cards depict very disturbing photographs of normal locations such as a bathroom or a cable car, but showing ghosts in the mirror, or shadowy figures, or bloody handprints. Further, some cards carry penalty instructions, such as requiring the player to cover one eye, making it harder for them to react when the cursed doll appears.

The last player to survive the increasing horror is the winner.

description by publisher

Object of the Game: To win the most cards to add to your collection. Each player should play the game on his own (One, Two, etc).
Preparation: Players should shuffle their cards and then stack them in a pile in front of them with the faces upward.
Each player then gets two card stands and must place the two top cards of his deck into the card stands with the printed side facing him.
Play: Any player can now challenge any other player by moving one of his two cards toward the player he wishes to challenge. The person challenged must respond by moving one of his two cards toward the attacking card. The winner of each challenge picks up the two cards just played, and puts them on the bottom of his pile. Each player then puts a new card from his pile into the empty card holder and resumes play.
Winning: The winner of each challenge is determined by colors as follows:

     Cards printed in red (Authors) beat green (Artists)
     Cards printed in green (Artists) beat blue (Ambizmags)
     Cards printed in blue (Ambizmags) beat red (Authors)
     Cards printed in orange (Supers) beat red, green or blue
     The card printed in purple beats any other color
     In case of a tie, the highest numbered card wins


Ending: The game ends when all players agree to quit.
Keeping cards: Winners get to keep the cards they win.

boardgame: La Spada di Ghiaccio: Il Gioco da Tavolo
description:
A cooperative game, in which participants can play the roles of various heroes of their choice, with the aim of finding the ice sword before the Antagonist. Pawns, action cards, thematic decks and the inevitable randomness of rolling the dice will accompany players on a gaming adventure waiting to be discovered.

—description from the publisher (translated)

A un anno di distanza da quando Marco Nucci e Cristian Canfailla ci hanno riportato a visitare il mondo dell'Argaar, e per la prima volta in un assoluto esperimento inedito, sarà possibile immergersi in prima persona nella leggenda con l'imperdibile gioco da tavolo della Spada di Ghiaccio. Un gioco cooperativo, in cui i partecipanti possono interpretare i ruoli di vari eroi a scelta, con l'obiettivo di trovare la spada di ghiaccio prima dell'Antagonista. Pedine, carte azione, mazzi tematici e l'immancabile aleatorietà del lancio dei dadi accompagneranno i giocatori in un'avventura di gioco tutta da scoprire.

—description from the publisher
