description:
Historical Background

Tang Dynasty
In 618, the Tang Dynasty unified China after the collapse of the Sui, which had left the empire in turmoil. Unlike the Sui, the Tang initially pursued peace with neighboring states. However, one of Emperor Gaozu’s sons, the ambitious Li Shimin, harbored other ambitions. 
Though he had played a decisive role in unify war, when his father refused to name him heir, he staged the Xuanwu Gate Incident, killing his brothers and forcing his father to abdicate. Upon ascending the throne as Emperor Taizong, Li Shimin consolidated power, crushed the Eastern Turkic Khaganate, and emerged as the dominant force in East Asia.

Goguryeo
A new king, Yeongryu sought reconciliation with Tang and wished to heal the scars of past wars with the Sui. Yet, many nobles saw this as humiliating for a kingdom that had once defeated the Sui’s million army. At the center of this political rift stood Yeon Gaesomun. When the king attempted to eliminate him, Yeon struck first—leading a coup, purging the monarch and his allies, and installing a new ruler under his control.

Goguryeo-Tang War
For Emperor Taizong, Goguryeo was the final obstacle after his victories against the Turks. In 645, despite opposition within his court, he personally led a massive invasion of Goguryeo, commanding hundreds of thousands of Tang soldiers in what would become one of the most legendary wars of East Asia.

Whether this was the pride of a hero or the hubris of a tyrant—history alone holds the answer.

—description from the publisher

Players hire monsters as riders, buy special stunt cars/motorcycles/trucks and learn how to execute the most spectacular performances (using meeple placement). After that they visit different cities to show tricks to the public and gain fame (using dice and push-your-luck mechanism). The goal of the game is to be the first player who successfully performs certain amounts of shows (depends on player count).

Spanish-American War, 1898 is a two-player game of the Spanish-American War of 1898 by Vance von Borries. It recreates the strategic naval and land campaign against Spanish forces in Cuba and Puerto Rico. One player controls the American and Cuban units, while the other controls the Spanish ones. The game is played in game turns, consisting of alternating player turns in which each player moves his pieces and resolves any resulting combat. When all the game turns have been completed, the game is over, and the winner is determined.

The game is divided into separate but closely related land and sea systems. A campaign game combines both of these into one strategic game. The introductory scenarios should be played before attempting the full Campaign Game. Some specially marked land and sea rules apply only to the Campaign Game. You can ignore them when you play introductory scenarios. The Campaign Game uses all the rules. Using separate land and sea commanders, up to four players can play the Campaign Game.

Product Information:

Complexity: Medium
Playing time: 3 to 6 hours
Solitaire Suitability: Medium
Players: Two to Four (suitable for solitaire play)
Map Scale: variable (for both areas and hexes based on map)
Time Scale: 1 Turn = 20 Minutes
Unit Scale: 1 Unit = 1 ship or battalion/regiment

—description from the publisher

A card game quiz for "creative" people.
Answer questions about formatting, fonts, designers and more. For each question 4 possible answers are printed on the front, on the back the correct answer is written with a description.

Optimize your resources each time Luna scans for materials or data with this new structure tile and discovery die.

Deploy and use this structure on any planet.

Coloma: Deluxe Upgrade Pack includes all of the Coloma Deluxe Edition content so players can upgrade their retail copy.

This GameFound exclusive contains:


     40 Poker Chips
     30 custom Gold Nuggets
     6 custom Score Markers
     1 custom Round Marker
     1 Metal First Player Marker
     6 new Characters, each with a unique ability not found on characters from the base game, including:



    Robber - You may spend 1 Wagon action (forgoing its usual movement Ability) to gain 3 Gold Nuggets from the Gold Cart (not the Gold Supply on the board!).
    Homesteader - You may spend 1 Tent to build a Town Building card from your hand, paying the Saw cost on the card.
    Renegade - Each time you add a Dude to your Graveyard, draw 3 cards. 
    Range Rider - Each time your Wagon moves over (or stops on) a vacant Horseshoe space on the Frontier Map, draw 1 card.
    Maverick - Discard 1 card to gain either 1 Buck or 1 Dude.
    Vigilante - Discard 1 used Barrel to place Dudes according to the usual rules.


[The custom components in the deluxe edition replace the standard components from the retail edition.]

AWESOME STRATEGIC FUN FOR YOUR NEXT GAME NIGHT WITH FRIENDS AND FAMILY. Get your competitive juices flowing with this super addictive game of planning your moves and obstructing your opponents'

LIMITLESS PATHS TO VICTORY AND WAYS TO RUIN YOUR OPPONENTS’ DAY. Place marbles 1 at a time on any level, try and get 4 in a row. Rotate a board if your foe is close to winning to change their strategy

HOURS OF CLASSIC FUN FOR UP TO 4 PLAYERS. Designed for anyone 6 or older with stacking, rotating playing surfaces. Be 1st to get 1 marble on each of 4 levels to line up in a straight or diagonal line

QUICK AND EASY SET UP AND CLEAN UP. Simply stack the boards together to play, then disassemble and pack back in the box when you’re done. Neatly stores away on shelves or storage bins
The game starts with the throw of a dice, the highest number starts and then the players take it in turns, clockwise from the player’s right.

Place beads/marble on any platform one at a time, one player at a time, in turn.
To Win Match (line up) 4 beads/marbles of the same color in a consecutive line either Across, Diagonal, Horizontal, or Vertical, but remembering the 3D nature of ROTATE it is easy to overlook a winning position.
Each player starts with 4 lives, (optional: - extra larger live marbles can be included in the game for each player matching the color of their beads/marbles) this can be recorded on the same paper as the outcome of each leg.

     The lives can be used one at a time, at any time during the game to ROTATE any of the three platforms Second, Third, or Top platform to stop your opponent from winning, but not to put the players who ROTATE into a winning position, this is important as it will stop stalemates, the players who ROTATE then miss a turn.
     The ROTATE can happen up to 4 times depending upon the number of lives left.


The game is played over an agreed amount of legs, 3, 7, 9, or 11 but an odd number either way, with each game lasting between 5 and 15 minutes.
The overall winner is the person who wins the most legs.

—description from the publisher

This is a Japanese doujin game (fan-made) that was only available from Game shows in Japan, such as Tokyo Game Market.  To get one you ordered in advance and reserved a copy, and then picked it up at the show.

Point: 203 is a game about the Russo-Japanese War 1904-05 and takes place near Port Arthur.

The game is about taking the heights of point 203.  If the Japanese do this, they win the game, if not, the Russians win.

This game is a predecessor to the Houten 1905 game.

—user summary

boardgame: Resident Evil: The Board Game – The Fallen Five
description:
The Fallen Five expansion includes advanced minis and rules cards for all the fallen S.T.A.R.S. from the journey through the Spencer Mansion.

Now, with this exclusive set, all of your player characters can join the advanced ranks as you play, unlocking new abilities, narratives, and objectives

A community-driven add-on, the Fallen Five expansion gives you the chance to give the fallen S.T.A.R.S. a second chance at becoming advanced in the Spencer Mansion.

This expansion includes:

     Advanced Albert Wesker mini and character card
     Advanced Brad Vickers mini and character card
     Advanced Enrico Marini mini and character card
     Advanced Richard Aiken mini and character card
     Advanced Edward Dewey mini and character card

boardgame: Resident Evil: The Board Game – Gold Tyrant
description:
This is an exclusive expansion that includes a T-002 Tyrant model in gold plastic and boss behavior cards in a foil finish.

The Gold Tyrant can be used in the Battle Game mode and was created as acknowledgement of the Resident Evil board game community to reflect the Resident Evil video game legacy.

This expansion includes:

     T-002 Gold Tyrant Monster Mini (40mm base)
     Foil T-002 Gold Tyrant Behaviour Cards

Eliminate all enemies or capture the sacred stone to lead my chosen race to victory.

Choose the race to lead to victory.

Build your deck with a variety of hero units, regular units, and special skill cards from the chosen race.

Proceed with your turns in accordance with the 4 phases (Unifact, Flip, Control, Attack) and engage in battles to emerge victorious in the war!

—description from the publisher

This is a two person game that involves lifting a rocker arm up and down.  As you do that you a spool will roll along the curved arm back and forth.  The curved arm acts like a track for the spool to ride on.  You and your opponent are working to make the spool land on the center support which is called the goal.

It is very much like the carnival game where you roll a ball to stop perfectly in the grooved section of the rail.  The Spool has to exit the rocker arm at the perfect speed and position to land on the goal

Scoring: One point is given to the player that gets his spool to land on the goal.  Once the spool lands on the goal, each player starts over and play continues until you reach 21 points

—user summary

boardgame: War of the Galactic Empire: Imperial Conflict (Lippstadt) and Alliance Invasion
description:
This is a Japanese doujin (fan made) game that was made for sale at Japanese game shows, such as Tokyo Game Market. To get one, you had to order in advance and then pick up your copy at the show. It was not made for the general public.

War of the Galactic Empire - Imperial Conflict (Lippstadt) and Alliance Invasion is a game based on the anime Legends of the Galactic Heroes, which deals with two sides: the Empire and the Free Planets Alliance. Both sides have been fighting a war for over 150 years. In this game, Lord Reinhard takes on the Goldenbaum Monarchy, leading to a civil war within the Empire. While this is going on the Alliance takes advantage of the situation tom launch an invasion of Empire Territory.

The game has a eight-part map, two sheets of counters, One fleet organization sheet, One player aid, and rules. Assembly is required.

The game is based on the Kenshin Joraku rules from Game Journal no.46. They have been adapted and modified to simulate the nature of this space odyssey.

A second part to this game called Twilight of the Free Planets Alliance: Operation Ragnarök is also available. It adds the Alliance portion of the galaxy and two scenarios.  The first scenario is the Empire Civil War (Lippstadt) and the second is the Alliance's invasion of the Empire.

—user summary
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

boardgame: Twilight of the Free Planet's Alliance: Operation Ragnarok
description:
This is a Japanese doujin (fan made) game that was made for sale at Japanese game shows, such as Tokyo Game Market.  To get one, you had to order in advance and then pick up your copy at the show.  It was not made for the general public.

Twilight of the Free Planet's Alliance is a game based on the anime Legends of the Galactic Heroes, which deals with two sides: the Empire and the Free Planets Alliance. Both sides have been fighting a war for over 150 years. In this game, Lord Reinhard invaded Alliance territory. In the anime, this leads to the destruction of the Alliance.  This campaign is known as Operation Ragnarök.

The game has a very large six-part map that will fill any dining table, two sheets of counters, two fleet organization sheets, two player aids, and rules. Assembly is required.

The game is based on the Kenshin Joraku rules from Game Journal no.46. They have been adapted and modified to simulate the nature of this space odyssey.

A second part to this game, called War of the Galactic Empire - Imperial Conflict (Lippstadt) and Alliance Invasion, is also available. It adds the Empire portion of the galaxy and scenarios for the Empire's civil war and the invasion of its territory by the Free Planet's Alliance.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Even before the surrender of the last Axis troops in Tunisia, the Allied governments had reached the controversial decision to invade Sicily as the next phase, to take the first step on the continent.

The amphibious invasion of Sicily, code-named “Operation Husky,” with a force of 3,000 ships, more than 3,000 aircraft, and 100,000 men landing on the first day was the largest amphibious operation of World War II. After 38 days of hard fighting, the Allied forces, composed of the 7th US Army and the 8th British Army, entered Messina, completing the conquest of the island.

Sicilia 1943 – Operation Husky reconstructs this important campaign, on a large map (… to maintain the right amount of simplicity and effectiveness. Game turns represent one day.

The system involves a very intense interaction between players, and is based on the uncertain moment of activation of their Formations, and on the type of operations they will be able to carry out following the directives received at the beginning of the turn.

The rugged terrain of much of Sicily affects operations throughout the game, from the logistical importance of the road network to the possibility of using armored vehicles, lethal in open terrain, but vulnerable on narrow mountain roads.

Air forces (of both sides) and naval forces (Allied only) can be very important... if activated at the right time.

The game includes two challenging campaign games, one historical and one hypothetical, and numerous minor scenarios that concern some individual phases of the campaign, such as landings and counterattacks, the battles of Primosole and Troina, and others.

N THE BOX
4 standard maps (3 km-hex)
2 small maps (scenarios)
4 counters set, 13 mm side 
Set up charts
Rules aid, tables
Orders display - Air display
Rules booklet
2 dice
English, Italian

—description from the publisher

description:
Host your own In Real Life or Virtual Murder Mystery game night with the ultimate Halloween themed murder mystery game. The host your own murder mystery kits are perfect for a night in with family, a dinner party game with friends or a team building exercise for colleagues.

Madsy Entist, the world-famous eccentric inventor, has invited all the monsters she knows to a party at her haunted mansion. And what a group she has invited! Count Dracula, the Wicked Witch of the West, the Phantom of the Opera—they’ll all be there, plus many more shambling, oozing, grunting outcasts.

At the party, Madsy intends to unveil her latest invention, “NORMALLO,” a potion that can turn any monster into a normal human being with just a swallow. But what’s this? Someone has stolen the only bottle! Who among all the creeps and ghouls at the mansion tonight could have done it?

Relay Blitz is a fast-paced 2-6 player track themed board game. Players navigate around a track through double dice rolling, using items, and the special baton abilities to take down opponents. You win by being the first player to get your relay of 4 runners around the track before any other player.

Sum It All Up is a strategic yet simple mathematical game. The game is about collecting tiles that add up to a target number. In the default game, the target sum is ten. When dice are used, the target sum varies between 2-12.

COMPONENTS: 

     lasercut game board
     49 two-sided tiles
     pair of dice.


GOAL: 
Collect the most points or tiles.

SETUP:  

     Randomly layout 49 tiles, your choice whether to use numbers or die faces. 
     Decide whether the winner will have the most points or the most tiles.


GAME MODES:  

     Tens: collect number that add up to 10.
     Dice: collect numbers that add up to the dice roll.
     Solo: try to get rid of all tiles in “Tens” mode.


‘TENS’ GAME PLAY:  
On each turn, a player has 30 seconds to do the following: 

     Find two or more numbers that add up to 10.
     Call out the tiles.
     Collect the tiles.


‘DICE’ GAME PLAY:  
On each turn, a player has 30 seconds to do the following: 

     Roll both dice.
     Find two or more numbers that add up to the total of the dice roll.
     Call out the tiles.
     Collect the tiles.


ENDING THE GAME:
Keep playing until no one can collect any more tiles.

WINNING THE GAME:
The winner will have either the most tiles or most points.

COLLECTING NUMBERS:
To collect numbers, the tiles must be aligned in one of the following ways:

Adjacent: all collected tiles are touching in any direction.     
Line of Sight: in a straight line ignoring empty space.
     
—description from the publisher

Pack of promotional cards given to those who participated in the Verkami crowdfunding. Includes the following 3 cards:

Issha, Pontífice
Thrissa, urdidora de planes
Jarvis, el cazador

boardgame: Ezra and Nehemiah: KS Promo – Scroll Tiles
description:
A set of 6 extra Wood Scroll tiles


     Gain 1 additional Banner for each Silver you spend when taking a Main Action. 
     Gain 2VP when placing Wood in the Temple, instead of 1VP. 
     Lose 1VP Immediately. Score your lowest-scoring tucked Character Card twice during each Sabbath. 
     Pay 1 fewer Gold, Stone, Cinders, or Wood, or 2 fewer Silver when turning over Development Tiles. 
     You may spend Cinders in place of Stone or Wood when building Gates and Walls. 
     Gain 1 additional Blue Banner for each pair of 1 Red Banner and 1 Grey Banner you have when taking a Main Action.

boardgame: Brandy Land: The Old Fashioned Game
description:
It's time for an Old Fashioned adventure! Muddle your way through Miltown and cool off in the Ice Cube Tundra. Beware of Hodags in the Swizzle Stick Forest and try to avoid a Wisconsin Goodbye as you end your inebriated expedition.

As you move down the game board, you will draw cards with various effects including the chance to trigger a classic dice drinking game such as Pig, Liar's Dice, Ship-Captain-Crew and 7-14-21. The first to the finish line (or last one standing) wins!

Perfect at the cabin, in the supper club, or anywhere you find yourself drinking Wisconsin's classic cocktail.

The Norse heroes join the battle against the Apocalypse using powerful runes and permanent artifacts. The Norse expansion adds rules for archdemons, a 5-6 player mode, new events, and 4 brand new villains.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Four Horsemen: The Chinese Expansion builds on the core game with Chinese heroes, events, and relics. The Chinese heroes operate differently depending on whether Yin or Yang is in effect. The demonic horde also grows in power with the Four Perils and Vortexes, dangerous rifts in space that destroy followers who get too close.

Four Horsemen: The Egyptian Expansion builds on the core game and adds heroes and villains from the Egyptian pantheon.

The Egyptian expansion also adds wonders, powerful buildings that can be harnessed for additional divine benefits. Build the Colossus of Rhodes to kill demons. Construct the Hanging Gardens to manipulate the horsemen. The demons also get stronger with new demonic gates that spawn new minions each hour.

For the alphabet's great architects of cunning stratagems, Word War One is a card game that challenges players to use their vocabulary and strategic thinking to outplay opponents.

Each participant combines letter cards to form words and uses them as offensive or defensive moves on a quest to deplete their opponent's health points. Special cards introduce game-changing twists, ensuring no two battles are the same. Victory is achieved by being the last one standing, using all cards, or constructing a predetermined Boss Word.

Word War One is much more than making fancy words and all about making clever use of letters and timing to deliver devastating blows. Articulation and management of cards together with the unpredictability brought by the Special Cards guarantee no game is the same and keep you playing over and over. 

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pest Friends: An Educational Pest Management Game
description:
Pest Friends is an educational strategy board game designed to help youth and adults understand the principles of Integrated Pest Management (IPM), an ecosystem-based approach for managing pests and the damage they cause within economic thresholds. The game is designed to allow players to explore the different facets of IPM and how they affect a crop and species living within it. This is done through a facilitator-led simulation where players make decisions that ultimately lead to a unique outcome based on their choices.
The facilitator carries out the effects of a player’s action within its scenario while players make decisions as pest managers contending with pests in an agricultural field or similar setting. Their role as managers is to keep pests in check and minimize the amount of damage done to a crop while using their time and money efficiently.
Each game consists of eight rounds. At the end of a game, players tally points based on their crop yield and the amount of money they have collected/earned. The game can be played with multiple scenarios, but this set only comes with Scenario 1: Lunar Wheat.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Puffin Rush: An Arctic Animal Adventure
description:
Help your hungry puffins make their way across the snowy Arctic to find a fish and get it home to their burrow. What other Arctic animals will you uncover along the way and will they help or hinder you?

Start from 1 of 4 sides of a 6x6 grid of snow tiles. On each turn you may move orthogonally to a new tile, whether revealed or not, taking the action for that specific tile. Find the fish and make it back to your puffin specific burrow before anyone else does and claim victory (and a tasty meal)!

It might be trickier than you think!

The whole area is covered in snow and who knows who you will bump into! Could it be a helpful reindeer or a friendly snowy owl? Try to avoid those hungry Arctic Foxes and the Grumpy Polar Bear!! And let's hope you don't run into a Blizzard!

This is a fast-paced game of chance, but who will win?!

boardgame: Мавка: Між двома світами (Mavka: Between Two Worlds)
description:
Mavka, Lukash, Huk, Kvus and Shust spent an amazing time at the fair in the Human World, but it's time to return to the Magical Forest. But this is not the reason to stop having fun. The friends decided to organize an unusual race, visiting various places in both worlds and exploring the paths between them. Guide your character along the magical paths and help him(her) win this race.
 In Mavka: Between Two Worlds game, each participant receives personal task indicating location they need to get to. Once in the right location, the player takes a token that shows the next goal. Choose the most successful paths, use location runes and properties, and try to get ahead of other players by moving around the playing field. The player who reaches the third final location first wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Монополія Знамениті місця Києва (Monopoly: Kyiv Famous Places)
description:
Monopoly Kyiv Famous Places is an exciting game that allows players to get to know different sights of Kyiv and develop their financial management skills. This game is suitable for both adults and children and can be a great way to spend time with family and friends. If you like games that combine strategy, luck and fun, then Monopoly Kyiv Famous Places is perfect for you.

—description from the publisher

Earth Matters is a conversational card-driven game for 2-5 players, eight years and older.

Some kids are lucky to live where they can play in the wild, which can be any outside place. Earth Matters brings the outside - inside.

Brainstorm with Tell Us About cards, imagine with Snapshot cards, laugh with Act Out! cards, and learn with Eco Action cards. Collect Critical Habitats, Energy Tokens, and Earth Treasures until a player lands on a Game Over Space. When that happens, the player with the most points wins!

Earth Matters is a card-driven, engine-building, limited-resource, competitive game. Check out our video on the game website to see the game parts.

Playing Earth Matters is an empowering and fun way for kids and families to talk about climate change. Well-made, with FSC-certified paper and water-based ink, this beautifully illustrated game provides experiential learning through interactive play.

—description from the publisher

The farm animals love to share their things with others, but since they are always in the clouds, they never remember where or who they left them with. Fortunately, they have Enola's sense of smell and she is always willing to lend a helping paw.

Despistados is a game of observation and visual speed in which you will have to keep track of the lost object through the memories cards and quickly vote for which animal you think the object has on the last card to be the person with the most clouds at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher
Each pack includes 4 Player Boards in new colors and 4 matching Railroad Ink Markers.

Welcome to the godly tug-of-war for the final fate of Havocville.

Havocville is a board game for 2 to 5 players where you are a God shaping the destiny of a vibrant city! Strategically place Land and People tiles to boost the influence of different Factions – Crown, Valor, Craft, and Field. But here's the twist: you're not just building; you're predicting! Score points by backing the Factions that rise to Dominance in key city Aspects like territory, population, and loyalty. Will you master the divine game of influence, outwit your fellow deities in tense challenges, and forge Havocville's fate to your glory? Dive into Havocville, where every tile placement shapes not just a city, but your path to godlike renown
—description from the designer

boardgame: Richard Osman's House of Games: The Card Game
description:
Each of the seventy two quiz cards contain a question pertaining to one of four quiz categories.

The quiz categories as seen on the television series are:
This Round Is In Code: The letters of the items in a given category have been replaced by a simple code. A becomes 1, B becomes 2, C becomes 3, etc.
Roonerspisms: The player must respond to a question with a two-word phrase with the first letters or sound of each word swapped (a spoonerism).
Opposites Attract: The clue words for each item of the category are the opposites of the word in the correct answer.
Answer Smash: The proper response consists of two elements. The second element of which is always the last part of the first element. This is always the final round of play and the tiebreaker if necessary.

Three cards of each quiz category are selected randomly. The question on each card is hidden from the sight of the players until it is time to be played. When played it is placed in full view of all players. The first player who signals readiness to respond can answer the question. A correct response scores that player the card. An incorrect response results in no penalty to that player but leaves the question open for the other players.

The game continues until all twelve cards have been played. The player who has scored the most cards is the winner.

Assuming no tiebreaker questions, there is enough for six games without repeating material.

—user summary

This is a solitaire game.

You take on the role of captain of Japanese Regiment 236 (fictitious) and guide it through 10 scenarios.

The game is played with 60 terrain cards which are arranged in five rows of five cards each.  You must guide your battalions through the perils that await you.

One additional feature is the satisfaction of your troops, morale plays an important part in how your troops function.  Be a good commander and the troops will follow and act dutifully, if not, then there will be problems.  Therefore, rotation of your troops is also important.

Comes with 60 cards, 110 counters, 2 dice and rules.

—user summary

Follow Thorgal into the hell of the Qâ country
Thorgal, Aaricia, Kriss of Valnor and their companions face a bloodthirsty demigod and fanatical warriors to liberate the country of Qâ and avoid a war that would set the world ablaze. Cross the Great Ocean and the jungle of Qâ to reach the great pyramid of Mayaxatl and challenge the powerful Ogotaï. Show courage and cunning to defeat the Chaams who are tracking you, while progressing as quickly as possible towards the city of Mayaxatl. Use your heroes' specific skills. Only by acting together can you hope to achieve victory!

—description from the publisher

Help the Scooby gang find a hidden treasure! The player who arrives first to the Lost City and gets the treasure wins.

Each player rolls the dice and advances that number of squares. There are two groups of trap tiles placed on the board (ghost ship and seaweeds) which are shuffled and placed on the corresponding areas of the board. When a player falls in a centaur or ghost pirate square, the traps of the corresponding group are reshuffled (even if there is a player already over one).

When a player lands in a trap, the tile is revealed and the player moves backwards as many squares as the tile indicates (3-6) or stays on the same place if it’s blank.

To get the treasure (and win the game), the player needs to roll the exact number or move backwards the number extra squares.

—user summary

Temple Punks is a solitaire roleplaying roll & write game.

At around 20-30 minutes of gameplay, Temple Punks aims to create an enticing solitaire experience with multiple possible gameplays with its character creation system offering a wide variety of abilities and play style.

Gameplay
You roll 4 dice, 2 white and 2 black and place these dice on action spaces between two boards that will trigger movements, spells, attacks and resource gathering. The black dice, though, when placed on the map board will trigger monsters moving in the ruins, which you will need to dodge until you have the necessary gathered resources to kill them. 
It is a game about controlling when you trigger certain events by choosing the right special actions you have chosen while creating your character.

Winning the game 
You win Temple Punks by successfully delivering a number of artifacts decided at the beginning of the game and killing two monsters of the right type, also decided at the beginning of the game.

—description from the designer

You're Cancelled is a party game that creates hilarious moments when culture and context collide. The goal of the game is to get yourself cancelled three times.

The game is played in the same format as Apples to Apples or Cards Against Humanity. Each round one player will be the judge or "The Offended." The Offended will select one card from each of the following piles: Background, Status, and Occupation. Together these cards will form the role that the Offended must use to judge the phrases submitted by other players.

Each player will have a hand of 6 phrase cards. From their hand they will pick the phrase that they believe will be most offensive to The Offended, and pass it, face down, to The Offended. Once all players have submitted a card, The Offended will shuffle the cards then read each one aloud. Next, The Offended picks the card that most offends the role they have selected and then says "You're Cancelled" to whoever submitted that phrase.

After the round, all players who submitted a phrase card will draw a new card. The player to the left of The Offended becomes the The Offended for the next round. Play continues until one player gets cancelled three times.

—description from the designer

This French game was played in the 16th and 17th centuries and similar to Backgammon, on a board. The rules have been unfortunately lost, but it was likely also a gambling game, as was the fashion in those days.

The game is the origin of the expression "left in the lurch", (in French, demeurer lourche) referring to a hopeless losing position.

The game was listed by Rabelais in his work, Gargantua and Pantagruel, in 1534.

The 1586 English Courtier and Country Gentleman said that "In fowle weather, we send for some honest neighbours, if happely wee bee without wives, alone at home (as seldome we are) and with them we play at Dice and Cards, sorting our selves according to the number of Players, and their skill, some in Ticktacke, some Lurche, some to Irish game, or Dublets."

Shakespeare mentions Lourche in Coriolanus and the Merry Wives of Windsor.

Addison (1892) notes that the game is also recorded as Ourche which "suggests that lourche stands for l'ourche, the initial 'l' being merely the definite article," and that ourche may have meant the 'pool' i.e. the pot into which the stakes were placed and thus may have an origin in the Latin urceus, a "pitcher" or "vase".[9]

Godefroy (1888) also states that the game was known in French as Ourche and distinguished from Trictrac.

Samuel Johnson, citing Skinner, says that Lurch derives from l'Ourche, "a game of draughts much used among the Dutch", and that l'Ourche in turn comes from the Latin orca, "box" or "corner".

Listen to the sounds and travel with your imagination to create fantastic stories!

     SHAKE THE SHAKER to hear a sound
     Start CREATING a story starting from that sound
     SHAKE AGAIN or PASS the shaker to another player to continue with the story.


Play with your friends for an evening of wacky stories or with your family, you will help the little ones develop their imagination thanks to the magic of sounds. Also playable by blind people.
More than 150 sounds available.

boardgame: Stars of Akarios: Battles of Akarios
description: Battles of Akarios is an expansion for Stars of Akarios, introducing one-off space combat gameplay scenarios. Battles introduces the “Battle-Book” - an all new large-format scenario book to get you into the action quickly, and new streamlined rules to make space combat even more fast-paced.

Compenso is a fast-paced strategic card game designed for 2 to 4 players.

Each player has a mission: to match the ranks of their hand of cards with a set of community cards on the table. With a personal cache of coins, players can influence the ranks of these communal cards by adding to their positive or negative sides.

Quick rounds and constant shifts in the game dynamics require clever tactics and a touch of luck.

The ultimate goal? 
Outmaneuver your opponents to align your hand with the community pool, and adapt swiftly to emerge victorious in this engaging game of match and balance.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Age of Apocalypse
description:
The end of the world has come. After defeating the likes of Magneto, Mister Sinister, and Stryfe, the X-Men and X-Force have returned home expecting some well-deserved rest. Instead, what they found is worse than anything they could have imagined: Professor X is dead, the world is in ruins, and a ruthless tyrant rules over what remains of humanity with an iron fist. Brace yourselves, heroes, for the Age of Apocalypse has arrived!

Welcome to the conclusion of the “mutant trilogy” of expansions for Marvel Champions: The Card Game! As the game’s seventh campaign expansion, Age of Apocalypse brings a number of classic X-Men characters to the table, including two new playable heroes, Bishop and Magik, each of whom comes with a pre-built deck ready to play from the get-go. Face off against the forces of Apocalypse, the X-Men’s greatest and most powerful foe. This expansion includes five brand-new scenarios, each of which can be played individually or as part of a larger campaign.

—description from the publisher's website
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Kuru Dynasty is an immersive board game that transports players into the epic world of the Mahabharata and the legendary Kurukshetra war. In this strategic tabletop experience, players step into the shoes of the iconic Kaurava and Pandava armies, each vying for supremacy in the battle that shaped the destiny of a kingdom.

As you embark on this epic journey, you'll be faced with the pivotal decision of choosing a Hero, your faction's Leader, who wields unique abilities and commands an army of devoted allies. The game features asymmetric powers, ensuring that no two factions play the same way, and an asymmetrical starting deck that reflects the distinctive strengths and weaknesses of each side.

The heart of "Kuru Dynasty" lies in its combination of engaging game mechanisms. With card deck building, you'll craft and tailor your strategy as you gather and enhance your army's capabilities. The game also incorporates elements of area control, requiring players to plan and execute their tactics with precision to dominate key regions of the battlefield.

In this ever-evolving war-torn world, market manipulation adds a layer of intrigue and strategy as players can trade resources and cards to their forces or undermine their opponents. Engage in fierce combat, deploying your armies and unleashing powerful Hero abilities to secure your faction's place in history.

The ultimate goal is to lead your faction to victory by reducing the survivability of your rivals to zero. As you navigate the complex web of alliances, betrayals, and fierce battles that defined the Kurukshetra war, "Kuru Dynasty" immerses you in a world of strategy and legend, offering a unique and thrilling gaming experience.

—description from the designer

Courageous is a co-operative card game, where you and your fellow players are an alliance of Heroes who must protect the Realm from Monsters that roam the lands.

Players battle one Monster at a time, taking turns attacking it until it is vanquished. Each Monster has eight openings which the Heroes use to make their Attacks. Attacks are made by spending Courage Tokens and playing a set of cards from their hands that defeat a revealed value on Monster Tokens.

Heroes have special abilities that aid them in the Battle, whether it is enhancing Attacks, sharing cards with other Heroes, keeping Courage up, or helping other Heroes with their actions.

Time runs out when all Monster Tokens have been used and the Heroes must Retreat, (only to fight this Monster again in the Nemesis round). The Heroes then move on to the next Monster.  If they must Retreat a second time, the Heroes lose, the Realm is lost and the game ends.

When Heroes have vanquished the four Monsters, including the Nemesis Monster (if there was one), the Heroes are Victorious and have saved the Realm, and the game is won!

This licensed magnetic game is disguised as a cassette.

It has 2 game modes:

- game 1 (a new hope): 2 players. 7x4 lineal board with normal squares and explosions. The integrated roulette generates 1-6 results. The player who spins the highest number starts. Each player spins the roulette and advances the number of squares indicated in the result. When a player falls in an explosion marked square, the other player advances his victory bar 1 point. The first player who scores 10 points, wins.

- game 2 (the empire strikes back): 1 player. 9 squares lineal tug-of-war board. The player choses a red or blue pawn and place it in the start (S) in the middle of the board (with 4 squares at each side). The player spins the integrated roulette which generates random Vader (red) or R2 (blue) icons. If the result is of the same color of the player’s pawn, it advances one square, else it retrocedes one. When the player reach the final square, wins. If the player goes back to the opposite square, loses.

The aim of the game is to be the first to climb Mount Everest by rolling the dice and then dare to descend again. The fastest climber win.

Simple dice game packed in a cube

boardgame: Sebastian Fitzek Killercruise: Das Würfelspiel – Die Meisterschaft
description:
Tired of hunting the psychopath together?
Play against each other in this competitive version.

Each player gets a different Plan and the player who finds the psychopath first is the winner.

The promo-expansion contains 4 plans (E - H) and was given away by moses at Spiel 2023.

boardgame: Uri, Schwyz und Untergang: das DRS 3-Duell der Kantone
description: The DRS 3 cult game as a party game. Two teams compete against each other - each with a real Swiss wooden cow. Which team will succeed in driving the opposing cow to its doom with knowledge, skill, wit and luck? The entertaining and informative DRS 3 Duel of the Cantons is all about the 26 cantons, Switzerland and the people who live here.

TTMC: Format de Voyage vol 2 is a mix of party and trivia games. Self-assess your knowledge from 1 to 10 on a variety of uncommon, interesting and random themes, this time with questions about trave!

Be the first to reach the final card by answering the questions you chose. Each team will self-assess itself from 1 to 10 on a multitude of topics. Each grade has a more or less complex question: the higher the grade, the more difficult the question. If you answer it right, the team will go up the number of boxes corresponding to their self-rated score. For example, if you answer correctly question 6, you will advance 6 boxes.

It's funny, accessible to everyone and there is plenty to play with: 130 cards and 860 questions, with the added value of original cards to spice up the game. It can be played as a standalone game or an expansion of the original TTMC.

The goal of the game is to be the first to get to the end of the board by answering the questions asked.

The goal is to grow your logical brain and have fun.

This is how it goes:
Build a world with segments, fill your ships with fuel and cargo, discover and visit all tradeposts, figure out the most clever routing. Exchanging cargo at a tradepost allows you to place a claim. The winner is the one who has claimed all tradeposts first.

Claimgames is a versatile logistic game, that allows you to invent new versions with newly arranged boards and changed rules. Up to now we ourselves have created more than 20 games, that you can find at our website www.claim.games

The base game is for two players and contains 5 ready to play games. The first extension has material for up to 4 players and 4 extra games. The last extension contains even more material for creativity and offers another 12 games with up to 24 segments.

—description from the publisher

It’s finally here! A strategic card game that both kids and adults can enjoy.

It’s a race to collect all 5 pets, but every round presents you with some intriguing real-life questions: Should I save or spend my money? What’s the value of waiting? But don’t be intimidated! It all boils down into a simple choice of 3 card types: coins, pets, and time.

Each round opens with card drafting. Then each player takes turns playing their cards in an open-hand fashion. The cards represent 3 categories of resources. Coins increase your ability to buy. Time improves your bid to go first next round. Pets are collected to reserve the right to buy them. But when to buy them and at what price makes all the difference.

This game incorporates various classic board game mechanics in a simple format that makes it accessible to players of all ages. The themes are intuitive and universal. Open-hand play gives adults the option to guide children. And the ability to read is not a pre-requisite to playing this game at its fullest. You'd be surprised at how strategic a 5-year-old can be!

-description from designer

Iron Coffins is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the German Kriegsmarine U-boat war against Allied shipping in the North Atlantic during World War II.

As the captain of a Type VII U-boat during 1940-1943, your ultimate goal is to locate and sink as many enemy Allied ships as possible, while at the same time bringing your U-boat and crew safely back to base after each patrol. 
It won’t be easy due to persistent enemy aircraft and escorts continuously hunting your U-boat, but the German people are depending on you. 
Don’t let the Fatherland down!

With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. 
And with water depth rules, compressed air rules, oxygen rules, numerous crew skills, weather effects, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Iron Coffins offers memorable experiences and high replay value. 
Are you ready to serve the Fatherland and defend Germany against the onslaught of Allied shipping?

-Description from the publisher

description:
The Nurts: Parenting Made Fun game allows you to play and adopt different strategies based on your child's personality and what life throws at you!

Strive to raise the most fulfilled child by playing more actions of what they are good at while managing your time and cash. Play as many Actions and acquire as many Assets as your remaining resources allow. Each round, a Wild Card is drawn and rules apply to every player. This process continues until there are no more Wild Cards left in the game. The player with the most points wins the game.

This box will pack a punch! A highly strategic and fun game, where you raise the most fulfilled child and compete to out-parent each other! Suitable for adults of all ages. 90% of parenting is figuring things out. There was no playbook for this kind of thing before we came along – but we’re creating one.

boardgame: Woodcraft: Man vs. Meeple Promo Card

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Lannister Attachments I
description:
Customise your Lannister armies with 11 new unit attachments! Enhance your troops with Guard Captains, Assault Veterans, the Clegane Butcher and more.

The limitless gold beneath Casterly Rock flows freely in the War of the Five Kings, hiring the best troops money can buy. House Lannister's professional soldiers are tough and disciplined, ready to shed their enemies' blood for coin. Kitted ou in the finest weapons and armor, these officers are adept at leading from the front and keeping their men focused on the battle. If there's a criticism to be levied at Lannister forces, it's that money can't buy loyalty. But, that said, money certainly buys a great deal of steel.

Contents:
11 Miniatures
  ‣ 2 Guard Captains
  ‣ 2 Assault Veterans
  ‣ 2 Champions of the Faith
  ‣ 2 Sentinel Enforcers
  ‣ 2 Turncoats
  ‣ 1 Clegane Butcher
11 Attachment Cards

The set includes 48 playing hands (or scenarios) intended for beginners.  Over time, the base game included different starter sets (frequently Group 15/16).  This set (Group POG) was included with some copies of the base game; however, since other copies of the base game came with different sets, this set was made available separately.

The rule book includes a glossary and a player aid on the back cover.

Skyjo Junior is a card game aimed at children, designed as a simplified version of the original game "Skyjo".
The objective is to score the most points by making columns of cards of the same value to form a 3x4 grid. The cards represent fruits with values ranging from 1 to 5.

The game aims to teach children the concepts of numbering, strategy and decision-making while entertaining them.

boardgame: Spiders from Outer Space: A Solitaire Sci-Fi Game
description:
Out of nowhere, an alien Spaceship appears over New York. With no communication forthcoming despite numerous attempts by Earth’s various space agencies, there seems no option but to board the alien craft and investigate. The first such mission resulted in the discovery of a race of spider-like alien creatures. Pictures were sent back to Earth before all involved in the boarding mission were brutally killed by the Spiders. The people of Earth have now put together a team of elite Soldiers to board the alien craft. This time they will be tasked to kill any Spiders they come across, and to complete special mission objectives. The team shall be called the Spider Squad.

Designed from the ground up as a solitaire game, this follows the traditional Lambo system making it easy to learn, quick to set up and play but with meaningful decisions over 10 tough to win missions. Full colour mission maps and game rules are included, along with suggested counters to remove and mount on card if desired.

Good luck up there...

—description from the designer

description:
KNOCK KNOCK! Hello friends! 
Let’s play and learn with our favourite characters in Knock Knock! Yo Gabba Gabba! - a colourful card game with 8 ways to play. Created for babies (yes, babies!), toddlers and up!

Knock on the door and see who's there, learn words, match cards and make sets (music, art, nature and sport) to develop language, observation, memory and more! 
Illustrated by Parker Jacobs of Yo Gabba Gabba! fame. Playing games and having fun with family and friends is awesome!

Knock Knock! Yo Gabba Gabba! is part of the Small Fries Games range designed to grow with your family. The games and activities are suitable for different stages of development and can be played by children of different ages at the same time.

8 in 1 games and activities for 1 - 4 players aged 0 to 4+. (6 are co-operative, 2 competitive.) The colourful, A6 sized laminated cards are designed for little hands and made from FSC card in the UK. (Single use plastic free and Carbon Balanced with the World Land Trust.)

—description from designer

Are you ready to become a master mixologist? As a young recruit at the renowned Foxtrot Bar, your mission is to craft mouthwatering cocktails to delight your customers. Grab your shaker, hone your skills, and prove your mixology expertise! The Mix is THE ultimate cocktail-themed strategy board game!

Gain experience, enhance your bartending skills, explore new recipes, expand your bar to serve more customers, and establish yourself as the top mixologist at the Foxtrot Bar!

The Mix offers a blend of engine building, actions, resource management, and worker placement. Prepare to be immersed in the world of mixology!

One round consists of players placing all their Mixologists and resolving the actions.  The game ends when 15 orders have been finished. The player with the most victory points wins.

Attack on Titan UVS is a collectible card game using the UniVersus CCG game system that will let you bring the manga masterpiece to your tabletop with both original and reimagined artwork from the Attack on Titan manga.

Heartless! is a solitare dungeon crawler featuring dark themes and difficult luck based gameplay.
The game is played with 4D6 and a deck of 54 playing cards. Heavily inspired by "Scoundrel" and other dungeon crawling solitare playing card games developed over the years.

In Heartless! you see your Character, a face card from the deck, face off against enemy after enemy in high risk, luck based dice combat. Using your regular dice pool of 3 dice, or 4 dice if you are fortunate enough to find a Weapon, represented by the diamond suit cards of the deck. This additonal die added to your pool is called the Slayer Die and is vital to finding sucess in this game. Companions you find strewn through the dungeon, non diamond/heart suit 2 and 3 cards, can also help you earn your way to the Slayer Die in combination with having a weapon.
Often times you will find your Character placed in a situation they are unlikely to win (like when they are paired against the almighty boss monsters of this realm, the Ace Foe cards!) And in cases like this your Character can Flee, taking only a Flesh Wound in the process! Or you can just Trip one of your companions you've gathered along the way to suffer the enemy's wrath instead (how cruel, utterly Heartless!)

Heartless! has a unique damage system surrounding the Hearts suit cards, specifically the Ace of Hearts. Shuffle up the hearts cards and put the 10 of Hearts on top. This is your LifePool. The most important card of the Health deck, the Ace of Hearts aka Your Beating Heart, is shuffled somewhere in that lifepool deck. When you take damage from enemies you simply take cards off the top of the Lifepool until you have a lesser value of health than when you started with. That means you could possibly go from being completely healthy to being right down to your Beating Heart in a single hit!When you die, you are Heartless!
Finally, this game makes use of Lord cards, the Jokers in the deck. These entities can heal you, give you the most powerful weapon in the game, the mythic Lord Sword, and even revive you after death! Forever maintain caution, traveler, for these deities are known to thirst for blood.

Slice, dice. Human Sacrifice! 
Can you traverse the twisted depths with your Beating Heart in tact? 
May the Lords be with you.

Work... like a dog! Dogville is a tableau-building worker placement game with over 50 unique purebreds and mutts – each with custom, breed-specific traits. Dogs thrive on responsibility, so adopt the ideal pack and match each dog’s traits to activities and authentic dog careers that make their tails wag. Their Joy (VP) will be your key to victory!

Make Fetch Happen! The game is played over 3 Stretches, each consisting of 3 Rounds and followed by a Road Trip. Each Round
consists of 3 Phases. The main Phase consists of Players taking turns taking 1 Action at a time: Adopt (up to 4 dogs), Play, or Pass.

Once all players pass, the Round ends. After 3 Rounds is a Road Trip, and the game ends at the end of the third Road Trip.

—description from the designer

YOUHUE is a multiplayer casual card game in which the Goal is to score the most points by mixing basic color cards to create a HUE.
Each player is dealt 5 cards, and players take turns drawing and playing or discarding cards.
If a player has all the base color cards in his hand that match the base color required by a HUE card, can play those cards to make a HUE.
If not, must discard one card from his hand and his turn ends.
Special combos and wild cards grant unexpected turns in game flow.

—description from the designer

Side A:
Set aside this card during setup, this side face-up. Ignore its effects until all pawns are placed on the reef. At the beginning of each player's turn, if that player's species has the most icons visible on the highest level of the reef, that player takes this card (if there's a tie, nothing happens). If that player already has this card, flip it over.

If you have this card during scoring, receive: 1 point

Side B:
At the beginning of each player's turn, if that player's species has the most icons visible on the highest level of the reef, that player takes this card and flips it. If that player already has this card, or there's a tie, nothing happens.

If you have this card during scoring, receive: 4 points

boardgame: First in Flight: Richard Pearse Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Blue side: Setup: Set aside 3 random Technology cards as your "Ideas".

Ability Side: Whenever you Gain a Technology card, you may select one of your Ideas instead.

Exhaust: Pay 1 Coin and 2 Time to Gain a Technology card.

Originally available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Ant-dulegence (1 star Wine)
Setup: Use only 3 Wine tokens. Do not place Wine in the first 1/2 of trail. Place small food tokens near Anthill.

Take a Wine: Place it beneath a small food token without a Wine.

Scoring: For each food with a Wine, the player(s) with the most matching food of that type score an additional 1/2 times their score for that food (rounded up).

Pheromones (1 star Special rule)
Requires Bites New Recipes
Set-up: Place the deck of food cards facedown in the mid-point of the trail.

Move an ant over the deck: Draw top card, look at it, and place it facedown near the Anthill. Place your choice of perfume token on it.

Scoring: If there is a space available, Positive perfume tokens move the matching ants one space up the Anthill; Negative perfume tokens - one space down. Ants may share spaces.

Ant-nado (1 star chocolate)
Setup: Set 2 Chocolate in the stack to be taken when moving onto the Anthill. Place remaining Chocolate as normal.

Play: Take a food token next to any ant. Turn that ant to face backwards. It takes its next move backwards, then turns facing forward. If there is no matching token backwards, the ant will move to the start of the trail and take the first token.

Also included are 3 positive perfume and 3 negative perfume tokens

Oshi Push is a collectible card game (CCG) based on VTubers, content creators who use virtual avatars to livestream and create content for platforms like Twitch and YouTube. In this game, you can collect cards and design decks based around your favorite Vtubers!

2023 Promo Card 2

Originally available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

3 power

SNEAKY

Count Zunyer Garcias: The Count enjoys two favours. During Step 6 in the Order of Play, take the top prestige guest from the supply and optionally take one guest from your discard pile and place it into your active hand.

Thomas, Lord Vasel: Lord Vasel has two unique favours
1 During Step 7 in the Order of Play, select any tile from the market, excluding monuments, without paying anything. This favour does not prevent the player from making normal Step 7 purchase(s).
2 During Step 8 in the Order of play, pass the Lord Vasel card to the player on your right. That player puts the Lord Vasel card into her active hand.

Michael, Viscount DiLisio: The viscount enjoys two favours. During Step 6 in the Order of Play, take 500 from the bank and optionally dismiss one guest (from anywhere - the current activity, active hand, or discard pile). Normal dismissal rules apply.

Camilla, Countess Cleghorn: The countess has two unique favours.
1. During Step 4 in the Order of Play, add the top casual guest from the supply to the activity (in excess of the guest count). Personal service is not required. Ignore activity limitations (e.g., gender, family, suites tiles); this guest crashes the activity, whatever it is.
2. During Step 6 in the Order of Play, draw 5 casual guests and keep 2.

Culture card (shuffle into the Era II deck at setup)

This is a promo expansion released as part of the 2023 Support the Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign. This expansion adds six Salieri cards to be added in to the period deck. The Salieri cards will impact the market of available cards by either occupying a spot in the market or wiping all available cards in the market back into the period deck.

Salieri was a contemporary of Mozart. Their relationship is unclear: some people say that they were rivals,
others say that they barely even met. Nevertheless, each of them was very influential in their time.

Setup: Shuffle the Salieri cards and add 1 of them to each period deck, along with the Opus and Memory cards. Discard the 6th Salieri card without looking at it.

Playing the Game: When one of the Salieri cards appears in the row of cards do the following, depending on the type of card


     Empty Music Stand: If this card appears, each player immediately takes 1 Story Point counter of their choice. This card cannot be bought, and it will remain in the row until the Maintenance Phase. Then discard this card.



     Salieri: If this card appears, shuffle all the cards currently visible in the row of cards and place them back at the bottom of the current period deck (right above the next Period Bonus tile in the deck). Then discard this card and refill the row with new cards.

Know your stuff or when to bluff!

Can you name an animal that lives in cities, has paws, and has a tail? What if it had big ears (and was spikey)?

Try to name more animals as the traits pile on, or play a new trait and test another player's knowledge! Just hope they can't name it before it gets back to you!

—description from the publisher

2024 Imagination Gaming Seal of Approval

There is a legend about a treasure hidden by a sly and slightly mad scholar. It is said that to find it, you must gather all pieces of a puzzle guided only by the unclear, twisted visions of Avalon places. Only an adventurer of unmatched courage and wit will be able to do so.

To add this mini-expansion to the game, find Secret card 501 in this deck and place it face up in the play area. Then take all remaining parts of Treasure seeker cards and add them to the Secret deck. While playing the game, pay close attention to both the Location cards and the revealed Treasure Seeker Secret card, as you may find some resemblance between them.

When you are certain that you are in a Location that matches your Treasure Seeker card, you may perform the Search for Treasure Action visible on the revealed Treasure Seeker card.

Chaque joueur tentera de récupérer le plus de points d'influence pour devenir le prochain gouverneur.

Étape
1. Récupérez des cartes dans la rivière
2. Utilisez vos cartes pour récolter des ressources
3. Échangez vos ressources pour construire des monuments qui vous octroierons des bonus permanents

EN : Far Far West is a game using card collection, engine deck and techonology tree mechanics.

Each player will try to collect as many influence points as possible to become the next governor.

Step
1. Collect cards from the river
2. Use your cards to collect resources
3. Exchange your resources to build monuments that will grant you permanent bonuses.
