boardgame: Le Val III: Le Maître des Sphères
description:
Le Val III : Le Maître des Sphères (The Master of the Spheres) is the second extension of "Le Val"

Its form is one book of 126 pages. 
It complete the game rules with new elements, some variantes and strategic tips (overture, classic formations,...) 
The narrator is Helios (the Sun, the big bad guy) of the story.

You have to get Le Val II (the first extension) to play The Val III

Two sets of each 24 pieces, feature addition and Subtraction respectively. The two sets will be generally be used seperatly, each giving 36 selected examples and their answers, but they can be combined to make a much longer and more complicated game.

The game may be played by 2, 3 or 4 players or as a matching activity by a child working alone.

The shapes are placed face down on the playing surface and players select an equal number at random. Players take turns at playing the first piece to start the game and the following players, in turn, position any connecting piece matching the problem to answer or answer to the problem.

Aplayer unable to make a match misses tat turn and the winner is the first to match all his pieces correctly.

A dexterity game published on the back of Kix cereal boxes in 2022.  Players flick pieces of cereal across the board and score based on where the piece lands on the baseball-themed board.  First player to score 100 points wins.

Going off the board scores 0, so there is risk in going for the higher point values.  The irregularity of Kix cereal pieces also adds challenge.

Great War is in its last stages, after almost five years of unnecessary bloodshed nations in the conflict found out their plight was orchestrated by a dark cult. From the shadowy island of R'lyeh that was risen from the deep and obscured by magical fog, humankind was puppetered into war that took millions of lives for nothing. True face of the enemy has been revealed by a group of investigators and without any doubt presented to leaders of once mighty empires. Now, time is almost night for the old god to awake from its sleep that took eons, great Cthulhu will bring madness and destruction to the whole world. But, there is still slim hope - a powerful relic from the past, Scepter of Hypnos would be able to put the old god back into his eternal slumber.
This is the story of humanity's greatest challenge, where all nations were brought as one to strike a blow into the very heart of evil. Mission is simple: land forces onto R'lyeh beaches and push trough horror and madness all the way to Necropolis, where group of investigators will perform a ritual with the scepter and save the world.
But, no one knows what terrors await human forces...

SEQUENCE of PLAY

Each turn is consistent of 6 phases: Expedition phase, Event phase, Supply and Support phase, Combat phase, Counter-Attack phase and Morale phase.

Main Mission and Objectives

Capture at least 2 black areas. Hold an area with Tomb of Cthulhu with a brigade that has Investigator's unit attached to it till the end of a turn.

National plan – Secondary Objectives
Roll one d6 to get a random secondary objective or use one of your choice.


     French Republic – Capture all areas of the central peninsula (small island connected with bridges to the main landmass).
     German Empire – Hold both areas with Portals.
     Ottoman Empire – Hold Fort Marsh and Fort Barnabas.
     United Kingdom – Finish the game with at least 3 Relics or Artifacts in your possession.
     American Forces – Capture all green colored areas.
     Russian Empire – Capture all areas with +4 terrain bonus.


Important: if objective of secondary mission is to hold an area, during the turn when you are finishing a Main mission and objective, you also must hold areas as stated for the secondary objective. This means that in that last turn when you are winning the game, those areas must be held (there must be at least on friendly unit in that area that is not exhausted).

Winning and Losing a game

To win a game you must fulfill Main and Secondary mission objectives.

You lose the game if turn counter reaches a space with Cthulhu icon on it and you still didn't finish Main and Secondary mission objectives, or if a special Investigators counter is destroyed. You may also choose to resign a game if you see that due to the loses to your units it is not possible to finish mission parameters anymore.

—description from the designer

Zombie Horde is a cooperative board game for 1 to 5 players. In Zombie Horde, survivors live in a colony, trying to survive the aftermath of the zombie apocalypse. The colony is the heart of the game.

GAMEPLAY
The game is divided into rounds, and one round is composed of 2 phases: the Colony phase, and the Exploration phase.

Each phase is divided into steps.

COLONY PHASE: the Colony Phase takes place on the Colony Board, and is played following 7 steps in order. These 7 steps form one phase.

EXPLORATION PHASE: The Exploration Phase takes place on the mission map tiles. During this phase, players activate their survivors in any order, even alternating them, and perform up to 3 actions. When the survivors finish their activation, the zombies activate. Players resolve the effects of zombie activation, then the phase is over.

When both phases have finished, that is 1 round. The next round starts from the first step in the Colony Phase.
The game ends when the cure is found, or all survivors are dead.

-description from designer

Atlantis is an economic game about becoming the wealthiest citizen of Atlantis.  This is done by acquiring trade routes and merchants, building centers, Robbing, and bribery.

The turn sequence is as follows:
1. Travel
2. Supply
3. Construction
4. Obtain resources
5. Trade
6. Robbery

The player with the most resource tokens at the end of the game wins.

In Convergence Alpha you step into a deckbuliding cyberpunk dystopia. Dive into the gritty underbelly of Convergence Alpha, where every move is a calculated risk and every decision could reshape the destiny of this world.

Construct your unit deck wisely, comprising Infantry, Range, and Armor cards with numbers. Choose your strategic depth and pick your starting deck and units suitable for this mission.

Engage in the Actions to attack, move, conquer, and purchase units strategically. Roll the dice to determine the outcome of an attack, shift units across rows, and flip territories to your faction's side. Be mindful of your deck composition and choose the opportune moment to thin it, ensuring your draw deck remains finely tuned for the challenges ahead.

Convergence Alpha offers a dynamic blend of strategy, luck, and deck-building mastery. Will you seize control of the territories and emerge as the ultimate tactician? The battlefield awaits your command!

-description from designer

A tactical game with real shooting, rudiments of economy and magic cards.

The players maneuver across the battlefield and use their weapons and magic take down the enemy fortress. The game involves shooting from the catapults and crossbows each side has.

Equipment:
Playing field
16 warriors 
2 cannons 
2 castles 
2 crossbows
2 catapults
Magic cards
Game banknotes

Each turn the players roll two dice.  The total dictates how many actions they get.  Movement on a clear field is one point, across a bridge is four points. It costs 2 points to move up and down the fortress.

A shot from a cannon costs 7 points, a crossbow costs 6 points, and a catapult costs 5 points. A device that has its front blocked by an enemy soldier cannot fire.

Players can use their income to buy magic cards, additional soldiers, and extra shots. You get bonus money from successful shots, melee, and for being in control of a mine.

Melee happens when one soldier moves into the space with another; both roll a die and high side wins.

To capture the flag a soldier must enter its space and pay 3 points.

The winner of the game is the first to remove the flag from their opponent's fortress or eliminate all their soldiers.

A collection of three games pulled from the design notebooks and older releases of designer Sid Sackson.

In Cinema, players engage in light negotiation to create movies that will (hopefully) be big hits at the box office and during awards seasons. Players take turns being The Producer, soliciting items from other players to assemble films, matching directors, actors, and actresses to scripts that suit their talents. After a set number of rounds, awards are given out to the most prestigious films, and the money earned from them is added to the money players have acquired during the game. The player with the most money wins!

In Dig Site (originally published as Corner, two players (or four players in two player teams) take turns moving their trowel tokens to different rows of the board, taking gems where their trowel and their opponent's trowel intersect. Sets of gems score more points the more you have of them, but be the only player with a gem type and double your score!

Included also is Sackson's classic Bowling Solitaire.

The setting for the game: SunFire: Factions Against Fate is set in a vibrant medieval world powered by mana. Throughout the world there are a subset of people who can harness the power of mana in fantastical ways. These people have organized themselves into powerful factions that operate in cities across the continent. The game is set in one such city - Solamar - where six factions existed peacefully, until recently. The survival of Solamar has been challenged by an existential threat, the SunFire. Appearing without warning, the SunFire is a powerful beam of energy from the heavens that strikes down at Solamar and areas surrounding the city, leaving a scorched wasteland in its wake. The populace turned to the factions and their ability to manipulate mana for salvation. Initially, the factions worked collaboratively, designing a powerful arcane device to create a temporary magical shield over Solamar, protecting it from the SunFire. Ozonestones, glowing rocks mysteriously left in the wake of the SunFire, are consumed to fuel the device. Now, amidst increasingly urgent pleas from the people of Solamar to continue to fuel the device, tensions among the factions have been stoked. Their collaboration has turned to rivalry as each Faction believes they are the key to the city's salvation. Factions vie for the support of the people, while keeping Solamar protected from the SunFire.

The gameplay: Each player chooses a faction to lead and must ensure that the city is protected by the SunFire while earning the admiration of the people of the city. Players must guide scouts outside of the city to collect ozonestones and utilize them as currency to recruit new followers, build settlements, and construct buildings. Ultimately, each player seeks to accumulate 13 or more Control Points, which represent their influence over the population, to win the game. Each game is played in a series of rounds made up of five phases: exploration - explore outside of the city and collect ozonestones, donation - determine how many resources will be donated to defense of the city versus kept to enrich the faction, construction - what how new followers and structures to purchase, SunFire - feel the wrath of the SunFire and determine if the city is protected, and rewards - actions are rewarded with additional Control Points.

Players must devote ozonestones to both the competitive - enriching their faction - and cooperative - protecting the city - aspects of the game. Too much devotion to the city and the other greedier players will accumulate Control points quicker, too much greed and the citizens of the city will punish the player's faction.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Darkstar's Retinue
description:
A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Darkstar's Retinue gives House Martell players a hard-hitting unit for their army, a highly maneuverable unit that outflanks their foes, cutting them down swiftly before charging forward again onto the next foe.

Darkstar's Retinue are best employed on flanks and far from other units. There they dash forward to strike like vipers, inflicting heavy casualties and sowing panic. They can remain in the fight or retreat to the flanks to await their moment again.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description:
A collection of three games pulled from the design notebooks and older releases of designer Sid Sackson.

I'm the Boss: The Dice Game sees players negotiating deals with each other in order to earn the most money. Players will be rolling dice to attempt to become experts in various subject fields and to become the Boss of deals for increasing values of cash. Experts can offer their services to complete a deal for whatever fee they can negotiate with the current Boss, or play cards to affect the outcome of a deal. Whenever a deal is reached, the Boss pays out their agreed-upon money to all who contributed to the deal, and the game ends after 8 deals have been completed. The richest player wins!

Banana Blitz is a trick-taking card game similar to Hearts where players are working to avoid taking Banana cards, but must be careful not to let any one player collect too many in a single hand, lest everyone else suffer a penalty. In addition a small set of Trump cards (that count as Bananas for the purposes of scoring) and two special cards mix up gameplay and force players to think on their feet.

Scope is a game where players compete to try and cross off the most boxes on a grid of numbers, with each player getting an identical grid and using the same set of cards to cross them off. A deck of 20 cards numbered 1-20 are shuffled and five removed at random, and each time a card is flipped players can cross off either a single box matching the number of the card or multiple boxes that sum up to the number, provided they are all vertically/horizontally adjacent. The player who has crossed off the most boxes after all 15 cards have been flipped wins!

Try to crack the 3D Core Puzzle to enrich your experience and delve into the Nemesis universe even more! This promo gives you an additional riddle to be solved.

—description from publisher

Get ready for an epic duel filled with riddles. Puzzle Duel Promo is a set of cards for two players with various puzzles to solve. Both competitors play cards from their hands on their opponents and force them to crack the riddle! The person who does it faster wins the round! Do it a few times and defeat your opponent with a short duel game set in the 7th Sea World!

—description from the pubilsher

Море Клинта (Clint's Sea) is a pirate game of exploration, trade, and conquest.  You must buy goods, take them away from other pirates, raid, and conquer other islands.

The player who conquers all the islands wins.  In case of a tie, the most money wins.

—user summary

Last Year of Carbon is a cooperative AND competitive strategy boardgame. Each player acts as a renewable energy company in a world running on the last drops of non-renewable fuel (gas, coal, etc.).

You must work together to power the planet, while simultaneously building up your infrastructure and convincing the public that you (and only you) are the best company to replace carbon based fuels.

Do you have what it takes to survive & thrive during the Last Year Of Carbon?

Every game of Last Year of Carbon takes place over 4 seasons, with 12 turns per player (one turn per month in the year) Half way through, a new deck with increased effects is swapped in. Additionally, before every round, NEWS CARDS are flipped causing world-altering effects... but who knows... it might be... good? The Last Year of Carbon is an adventure of discovery. Where each card can make or break your entire strategy. Where one player's actions may change how every subsequent player takes their turn. Where fingers become crossed, hopes become dashed, yet maybe, just maybe, we'll make it to the next season. To start again the nail-biting anxiety of creating a house of cards that may topple at any second. Only one person may stand victorious, but everyone can lose.

—description from the designer

This is a lightweight wargame from Tehnolog that is similar to Oil War but deals with robot combat.  It consists of only three pages of rules.

The game includes:


     four playing boards
     16 robots
     dice
     Robot data cards
     rules of play


There are five types of robots:

     Sniper
     Missile carrier
     Armadillo 
     Mortarman
     Soldier


The game consists of a hex board with mountains, quagmire, clear, and river hexes.  Each has a different cost to enter.  A clear hex costs one, mountains are impassible, a river costs two turns to transverse, and if a robot gets caught in a quagmire, it cannot move for the rest of the game.

Each turn the players roll a die for initiative, with the high side going first.  They then move their robots and fires them.  Each robot can fire once, except for the missile bot, and each needs LOS, except for the mortar bot.

Firing is done by a die roll and comparing it to the number in a hex.  If that number is rolled then the bot is hit and must retreat one hex back (done randomly).  This could put it in a quagmire.

Optional rules allow each player to place four pillboxes for their defense.

The objective is capturing the spaceport on each map. Further instructions say that you are to "Conquer four planets," so I guess the best out of four games wins?

—user summary

Alien is a game of exploration in an alien environment.  It is played by laying down tiles and moving on them with your soldiers.  You may encounter a hostile Alien along the way.

The game includes model soldiers, wound and hatching markers, an alien counter, spaceship interior hexes, and "hole" counters.

The game is very similar to GW's Lost Patrol, but the rules are much simpler, only two pages!

Each turn you roll a die for the number of action points your trooper receives.  With this, you can move, explore, and fight.

To move into a hex costs 1 action point, whereby you can explore the next hex for 2 action points--then draw a new tile and place it.  Each hex is drawn is from a pile of 20.

You can also spend action points to open hatches. No more than two soldiers can be in a hex at a time.

A soldier can shoot once at the end of its move.  A die is rolled to determine damage.

After all soldiers have moved, the alien gets a turn. The trick here is, that the alien can attack several times and it can move through the ventilation shaft. Each move and attack costs 1 action point.

To win the game the soldiers need to explore all spaces on the ship or kill eliminate the alien.  The alien wins if it kills all the soldiers.

—user summary

boardgame: Trekking Through History: 2023 Spellenspektakel Experience Promo Card
description:
Free Dutch promo card for Trekking Through History.

If you visited the "Trekking Through History Experience" during Spellenspektakel 2023 (4-5 Nov.) in Utrecht, The Netherlands, you got this promocard.

It consists of 1 card, dated 2023, that can be added tho deck III of the base game.

Like Golf. With Your Finger!

A dexterity game that allows you to hit all the shots from the world of golf on a smaller scale.

Players compete over 9 holes of stroke play or match play with a 7-iron, pitching wedge and putter. Each hole is designed by the players by arranging the tee box and green along with some obstacles. The player with the lowest score wins the hole. The player who loses the hole can choose who designs the next hole.

Stroke play: The player with the lowest total score wins
Match play: The player who won the most holes wins

—description from the designer

In this party game with a slightly videogame and slightly retro flavor you will play to send your "friends" into game over. In this fun game you will play as an 8-bit character and use all your cunning to stay on top!
Learn in 5 minutes, play for hours : This game is very easy to learn, each card has very intuitive symbols, and even explaining it will be very easy for you, learn it and explain it in a few minutes, and have fun for hours!

The aim of the game is to remain the last player in the game, during the game you will have access to various ways to eliminate your "friends", you decide how to eliminate them based no the way you prefer! There are those who prefer to slice them and those who prefer to shoot them from a spaceship, there is something for al tastes!

There are 2 ways to lose in ARCADE IT:


     Game over, you've finished al your lives! Each character has 3 lives, if al lives are lost the player is eliminated from the game. During the game it may be possible to recover lives, however you can never have more than 3 lives.



     if you run out of cards


Setup: each player chooses 1 character card, than shuffle the deck and place it in the center of the table and deal 10 cards to each player

The characters have unique abilities, we recommend playing without using these abilities at the beginning. Each character can only use his ability up to 3 times per game. Furthermore, he can only use his ability once between our current turn and the next one. Some characters require a specific time to use their ability, for example the character Lily can activate her ability only when it is targeted by a card, so as to transfer its effect to another player.
On the board it is possible to keep track of how many skills we have used and the remaining hearts.

A roll and write game where players compete across various different sports to win the most gold medals.

The players roll the dice and take in turn a dice and write the number on their score sheet in one of the 5 winter sports. Each sport has their own specific goals to how the player can win the medal at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

Four nations of dwarves search for treasures hidden in Dragon Mountain. Tenacious dwarves tirelessly dig tunnels, trying to penetrate the cave, where a slumbering cunning dragon sits on a mountain of treasure. Dwarven mages, studying ancient tomes, create arcane amulets and open spells to deceive the cunning dragon and help the dwarves of their people.

Who will be the lord of the treasures of the mountain?

The fate of your dwarves depends on you!

The game comes with:

     44 tiles
     72 cards
     43 tokens
     1 dragon model
     4 sets of 4 dwarves (16 total)
     1 die
     rules


This is a game where you are trying to get to the dragon and steal the treasure. There can be up to four teams trying to do this.  It is also possible for players to attack one another. The map is modular and can change during the game.

—user summary

Between December 1941 to August 1945, a sea war was fought in the Pacific unlike any before, as the long-range striking power of aircraft carriers ended the 250-year reign supremacy of the battleship.

Pacific Command is a tabletop wargame of WWII naval combat in the Pacific which puts you in control of dozens of warships and hundreds of aircraft; fighting battles as much about bluffing and surprise as strength of arms. The game has a particular focus on fog of war and aircraft carrier logistics, and zooms out to a grand scale to let players tell stories as full of dilemmas, uncertainties and double-blind gambits as the battles of Coral Sea, Midway and Leyte Gulf.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: 200 new general knowledge questions with 2000 answers.
100 new questions with 1000 answers. The questions are health-themed, with topics such as biology, psychology, diseases, treatments and medical achievements.

オーチャードプラン (Orchard Plan) is a game from Japan. Players develop their own orchard farm by placing tiles to expand their production. After selling the crop to create income, players can then invest in better equipment to improve production.

Each player has their own board showing the orchard, and starts with one fruit tree tile. The fruit tree tiles show spaces for 0 to 4 cubes, which are cubes in three colours, red, blue, yellow, for each variety of apple. The player also has a small truck, that is moved about to harvest the crop. The truck and exit the player's board from one side and enter from any other side, but must pass over tiles to harvest fruit. Players can place fruit cubes but only on free spaces and not where their truck is placed.

The game has a marketplace, and a series of long thin tiles are placed under the market to indicate demand. Each demand tile shows a number of cubes in each colour wanted, and as contracts are completed, the tiles move up and fresh tiles are placed from the left.

Each player keeps their income hidden behind a screen and spends money to acquire new fruit tiles, seed their crop and harvest.

At the end of the game, the wealthiest player is the winner.
user summary

Pueblos del Mar ("Sea people") is a filler-wargame about the population migration and conquer between 1300 and 1000 BC. It has a great abstraction, in order to create a short game for a lot of players. 
In the game a calendar inform about the pass of time, and it has events that bias the game (affecting different historical factions on their good times), then each player throws special dices that designate their turn actions, after that they perform this actions in the map. 
You need to create a great empire in order to win.
Will your people resit the Sea people's era?

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Grumpy Granny is not your average board game. It's a unique, action-packed game designed for 1 to 5 players. Your mission is to rack up those precious points by playing cards strategically while ensuring Granny doesn't lose her cool. With each card you play, you could either conquer the game or end up facing Granny's wrath!

Objective: Collect, Strategize, and Beat Granny!

Your mission? Outsmart Granny and collect the most points while playing your cards strategically. The cards in your hand hold the key to your success, but watch out for Granny's unpredictable moods!

—description from the designer

Promo card of the monster rider which was available only during crowdfunding campaign on CrowdRepublic platform. 
NeuroJoker can act as a wild animal, or a robot, or a raptor, but rolls only 1 die.

During Kocie Królestwa ("Cat Kingdoms"), you will develop your Kingdoms by seizing new Areas and exploring them, as well as constructing buildings and recruiting Subjects. Each game will be slightly different, because the Recognition Points you will be awarded with will be decided by the Queen’s Decree selected at the beginning. Waste no time and prove who amongst you is the best!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: 2023 SlugCrew Minor Reward
description:
In the first few days of 2020, we eagerly announced new rewards for SlugCrew, our team of enthusiasts running SlugFest gaming sessions at game stores and conventions. Melvyn, the minotaur mage was the reward for folks who ran lots of games. We were all looking forward to a great year of gaming.

Then some stuff happened…

But now, in-person gaming is back, and so it’s finally time to put our minotaur friend out to pasture. Don’t worry—he’ll be back very soon in an upcoming retail release. In the meantime, we’re excited to finally announce new SlugCrew rewards!

We start off with the Minor Rewards. These three Red Dragon Inn promo cards are available for any SlugCrew member who earns 30 SlugCrew Points (about 6 hours of demos). But if you get 150 SlugCrew Points (about 30 hours of demos), you can earn the Major Reward – a sneak preview version of a future Red Dragon Inn character! Master of Ceremonies Jin!

- Description by the publisher

Contents:

     Lucky’s Last Call (Drink card)
     Battlin’ Baguettes (Prize card)
     Hook Hand (Prize card)

Fried Potatoes – Original Edition

				
				
					OverviewA fast-paced card game of tricks, bluffing, and total chaos.

Fried Potatoes is a quick, strategic card game where every round builds towards a single, high-stakes showdown. You’ll play numbered potato cards in four colours, trying to win tricks, but only the last trick matters. Win that one, and you avoid penalty points. Lose it, and into the fryer you go.

Specialty cards flip the game on its head. The Hot Ketchup Card burns away all colour, the round becomes colourless, and the highest card wins. But don’t count on strength alone: The Lucky Loser can still turn the weakest card into a winning play when you least expect it.

You can also raise the stakes mid-turn by declaring a Fry Your Opponent move. This forces opponents to make a tough choice: fold and skip the round, or stay in and risk it all. Use it when you’re confident you’ll win, or when you want to bluff your opponents out of the round.

It’s a game of psychology and timing. Do you play your strongest card early to take control, or bluff your way through and save your best for last? Will you hold back a specialty card to surprise everyone in the final round?

				
				
					Key Mechanics
     Win tricks by playing the highest potato card of the declared colour  
     Only the final trick each round determines who gets penalty points  
     Specialty cards change rules, values, or entire rounds  
     Raise the stakes mid-turn to pressure opponents into folding  
     Play smart, bluff well, and outlast your opponents  


				
				
					Gameplay Facts
     2–6 players | Ages 7+ | 15–25 minutes  
     Easy to learn in 5 minutes  
     Compact and premium: comes in a sturdy magnetic box, easy to bring anywhere  


				
				
					Components
     32 Potato cards (values 1–8 in 4 colours, each with colour-blindness indicators)  
     24 Specialty cards with unique abilities  
     Rulebook  
     Premium box with magnetic closing

アイスティーブレイク (Ice Tea Break) is a party game from Japan, themed on drinks, cakes and sweet dishes. Players combine two cards and ask a question, such as "If you want a pet, this is something scary." or "If you want a date, this is the country." The other players have to deduce the answer based upon the chosen cards.

The publisher says:
Iced Tea Break, a tea party chat communication game, is an exciting communication game with mysterious themes. Each player presents an answer to a sweets question that combines two cards, and the questioner chooses the answer they like. How about playing a game in your spare time?

お茶会雑談系コミュニケーションゲーム  アイスティーブレイクは不思議なお題で盛り上がるコミュニケーションゲームです。2枚のカードを組み合わせたお菓子なお題について各プレイヤーが回答を発表し、出題者が気に入った回答を選びます。  ちょっとした空き時間におちゃをするようにゲームをするのは如何でしょうか？
description by publisher
designers: Kota Sugawara (すがわら こうた)

description:
Players test their holiday spirit and creativity by coming up with valid answers to Christmas-themed category questions.

Turn over 5 "category" cards and 1 "letter" card. Then flip over the 1-minute timer. Players have until the timer runs out to come up with answers matching the "category" card that begin with the designated letter. For the first 4 categories, the goal is to be unique. For the 5th category, there is a twist! Players test how well they know their opponents by attempting to match their answers to earn points.

When the time is up, players share answers with the group, earning points if others agree that their answers are valid. If an answer is up for dispute, majority rules! Lobbying for your answer is an integral part of the fun of Christmas Categories.

A typical game lasts 3 rounds but can go longer if players are having fun.

boardgame: Doomlings: Overlush Stretch Goals and Rebalance Pack
description:
This deck of cards includes all the stretch goals and a rebalance pack:


     5 Description cards (not playable)
     6 Kickstarter Exclusive holofoils (not part of the Overlush collection, marked "Special Edition | OVL")
     6 Gene Pool Spirit Bear counter cards (1 per player)
     5 Spirit Bear Trait cards
     30 Meaning of Life cards
     34 Rebalance Trait cards
     5 Rebalance Age cards
     4 Rebalance Meaning of Life cards

boardgame: The Sherlock Files: Vol VII – Sinister Secrets
description:
The Sherlock Files: Sinister Secrets includes three new confounding cases for you to solve. Track down a priceless diamond necklace that vanished after a glamorous Hollywood party. Unravel the sordid tale of a man who was murdered before the eyes of his own mother. Investigate the supernatural secrets of a haunted hotel room that kills anyone who tries to stay in it!

Decipher clues to determine which are relevant to the case and which are not. Share what you deem relevant with your detective partners. Which theories will you chase?

How will you fare compared to the world’s greatest detective?

Work together to solve each case and find out!

Prepare to put your thinking cap on and get tactical with this Sinking City Strategy Board Game.

It’s a beautiful day in sunny LA when suddenly reports on the TV and radio declare that a seismic event has occurred off the Californian coast. As the sea level rises, and the city begins to flood, strange creatures are seen swimming through the city’s streets. To make it out of the city, players will need to scavenge for supplies, craft items and use all their cunning to escape the rising waters and the creatures that lurk beneath the waves.

The board will flood as the game progresses and it's up to you to decide how the water spreads. Will you make alliances with other survivors or cut them off from escape? Work as a team or go it alone in this easy to master strategy game.

“Ding… Dong…” The regal sound of wedding bells fills the air as your team of wedding planners prepares for the ceremony and the reception in the banquet hall. But to your horror, you open the doors to find the whole place in disarray! Chairs are knocked over, decorations are ripped,  and even the cake is worse for wear. You're under the clock as you put your puzzle-solving — and communication — skills to the test in order to set things right before the happy couple even arrives.

The Marriage Mix-Up, title #9 in the Holiday Hijinks line, is an escape room-style game in just 18 cards! As a group, you will solve a series of puzzles to unravel a mystery.

Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smartphones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game but can pass it on to another group for their use.

This entry in the series takes about an hour to solve and is rated 2 out of 3 in difficulty. You will need at least two players to solve the puzzles.

Color Koi is a roll and color game where players roll dice and color their koi fish in their ponds. The game is designed for 1-99 players and takes around 20 minutes to play.

The game is played over a series of rounds. On each turn, one player roll two dice then each player decide which dice use to color in his koi fish. The game ends when all players have completed their koi pond. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Fairy Tale Files: Who Framed Mr. Wolfe?
description:
Fairy Land's most famous granny has been savagely killed! Who could do such a thing? The main suspect is Mr. Wolf, but is the little girl in the red hood really so innocent? What could the motive be for such a heinous crime?

Collect the clues and solve the puzzles to find out!

The Fairy Tale Files are lighthearted murder mystery detective games inspired by iconic fairy tales. We bring you the delight of your favorite childhood stories with an adult twist! Join the Magical Mishaps Agency to solve the clues and find the killer!

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
There's always room for improvement on the Gear-Gum assembly line, and you're just the bot for the job!

In Turbo Servo, 2 players will compete to create the ultimate, assembly line machine. On their turns, each player will draft and place a dual-servo card in their pyramid-shaped machine, top to bottom, until they have completed all 5 rows.

Each dual-servo card has 2 different parts (6 total unique parts) which each score differently in the end game, depending on placement. As you play your servos, you can rotate previous servos to create chains, combos, and rearrange the parts in each row and column to maximize your machine's efficiency.

Player with the most points from their completed machine wins!

boardgame: La Vallee de la Mort: The Battles of Na San and Dien Bien Phu
description:
La Vallee de la Mort: The Battles of Na San and Dien Bien Phu is a new edition of the games published by The Microgame Design Group and Against the Odds. This new edition has the two games on Na San and Dien Bien Phu, as well as ones on Operations Albatross and Condor all in one package. It also incorporates all of the accumulated addenda from the earlier editions into all of the relevant components.

Each copy of Vallée de la mort should contain the following components:

•    Two 11 by 17 and two 8.5 by 11 inch map sheets depicting the terrain the battle was fought over along with charts and tables, and a Terrain Key. Note: The Terrain Key on the Dien Bien Phu map is used for the other two maps as well.
•    352 double-sided, un-mounted units.
•    30 pages of rules and charts for the three games.

Not supplied with this game but needed for play are one or preferably two six-sided die.

The new High Flying Dice Games edition of La Vallee de la Mort is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh and features graphics by Tim Allen.

Test your knowledge of the TV Show Friends.
The box contains 50 Quiz Cards, each with a "fan question" (worth 1 point) and a "super fan question" (worth 2 points), plus one card with instructions.
First to ten points wins.

—user summary

boardgame: A&A Blitzkrieg '40: The Battle for France
description:
Blitzkrieg '40 -The Battle for France is the second in our Armies & Alliances WWII series.  If you liked Arnhem '44, then you will really like Blitz '40:  221 minis including separate infantry figures, tanks, artillery, mechanized, recon, fighters and bombers made for each nation.  Two big, beautiful map sections that fit together to create a truly impressive campaign area - from the German frontier with fortress Holland all the way to Paris at 9 miles/hex!  Event cards introduce aspects from the historical contest and add to the re-playability and "fog of war".  The combat system is similar to Arnhem '44 but adjusted for division vs. battalion scale, and the air rules include Air Superiority, Interception, Interdiction, Close Air Support, Combat Air Patrol, and Ground Attack missions.  This game may use minis and big maps, but make no mistake, it is a REAL wargame, not just a great-looking one.  Players can even choose to use counters instead of minis - both are provided to fit your personal preference!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Oh no! The annoying but adorable Monstrys have entered in the paint store and are spreeading everything in their path.

So much so that Peloso, Unimordio and Antenis have painted themselves in white and do not remember what their true colors were. Help the Monstrys by painting them yourself! Be the fastest by taking the pencil and making the correct mixture, if you don't want to be covered in paint, of course...

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Mèn’che is an historically popular card game in the Namur and Bastogne area [in Belgium]. In order to breathe new life, a new game that casts the traditional version in a new light, evoking the rich fauna and flora of the Ardennes, has been designed.

The game components are 32 playing cards, 1 memo card and 16 tokens. The cards have a strength level (from 1 to 13) and they carry some points (from 0 to 4). Cards 9 to 13 are named the triyonfes and are unique : Mite (red), Coûne (blue), Canète (green), Mac Mahon (black) and Zè (red). 

The aim of the game is to be the first team to get rid of all their tokens (starting with 3, 4 or 5, depending on the desired length of the game) after playing several rounds.

A round is composed of dealing the cards, announcing the color of the mèn’che (trump card), announcing who has the triyonfes in their hands, playing the tricks and counting the points of each team at the end of the round. Depending on which teams announced the color of the mèn’che, the wining team discards a token or give it to the opposite team.

The game is made for 4 players but variants for 2 or 3 players are also explained in the rules.

The game field is represented by planets connected to each other by trade routes. The more trade routes lead to the planet, the more income they bring.

Players start the game with a single merchant ship and take control of trade zones and paths. The richer trading zones a player controls, the more income they receive.

They can spend this income to buy more ships for different purposes. Players not only place their merchant ships on the most profitable trade routes, but also hire pirates to blow up rival ships, police corvettes and stations to protect against pirates, and open new trade routes.

The first player to earn 20 Income Points wins.

Back to the front! You must cross no-man’s land and defeat the German Army, or all hope will be lost.

Trench Knife is a mint tin solo WWI game. You play as the Allies. Using a variable Action Point system, you must maneuver your squads, suppress the enemy, and assault the German trenches, in the attempt to take a vital point in the war. The Germans have the upper hand, with HMG nests and The Red Baron on their side.

Artillery rains bombs and chlorine gas from the skies tearing up the battlefield. As the Allied French and British soldiers, you have limited time to make your push through barbed wire, mud, and trenches, while angry bees of hot lead rip through the air.

Push the Germans back or die!

—description from the publisher

In Tiramisu, players assemble cakes to score more points than their opponent! Roll dice and collect cake layers that are equal to the value of the dice roll, then stack the layers on cake stands to match cakes on your menu. Push your luck to try and assemble Tiramisu, or play it safe by assembling Cheesecakes and Tortes!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Caps & Stems: Mushrooms of the World
description:
In Caps & Stems: Mushrooms of the World, players compete to earn the most Reputation points by playing cards to add mushrooms on the map. Caps & Stems: Mushrooms of the World has nearly 150 different mushroom species in the base game based on the traits of real mushrooms.

Players play cards representing a variety of fungi, stacking them according to similar traits, to score points and gain spores. Spend spores to acquire the traits you need to assemble your mushroom (delicious, poisonous, smelly, ringed, pored, etc.) then grow them onto a shared map - scoring points based on the traits of neighboring mushrooms. Earn expertise in a particular trait to unlock special powers and collect secret bonus cards give additional points.

—description from the designer

From Tehnolog.ru:
Mini-games of the Europa series are a line of exciting board games with elements of strategy and magnificent figures of warriors. The theme of the games touches on the most important events in the history of Europe, and the figures correspond to historical eras.

From Tesera.ru:
General, your armies are ready for battle! Lead them through fields, forests, and mountains, conquer villages, and use the help of the local population wisely. Correct calculation and a little luck will lead you to the desired victory!

A mini-strategy game with miniature warriors for two players that will help you and your child develop strategic skills. Fight on the side of the Crusaders or the Russians to defeat the opponent's army and conquer the enemy castle, leaving the enemies out of work!

User:
Europe: the minigame series, is a selection of five games that are designed to be played by those as young as age 5, and in 10-30 minutes.

They deal with different historical subjects.  The five are:

1. Crusades 
2. Viking Fury
3. The Last Legion
4. Russian Valor
5. Knight Battles

Each set comes with:
-4 figures
-10 tokens
-1 game board
-1 die
-set of rules

The rules consist of four pages and have a very low complexity level.

The armies are plastic figures, and there are different varieties depending on the game being played.  Figures include Barbarians, Romans, Vikings, Knights, Novgorodians, and Crusaders, each set having 4 poses and different strength for the game. It has not been determined if every game in the series has the same figure poses, so in this sense there may be some collectability with these games.

The goal of the game is to take over the player's capital. To do this they maneuver their forces and attacker each other.

Movement begins with a player rolling a die to determine the weather for the turn.  On a clear day armies may move 2 hexes and on a rainy day they may move 1 hex.

Combat is determined by the attacker rolling a die and adding the strength of any tokens.  This is compared against the defensive value of the targeted army for the result.

Master Of Innuendo is a fill-in-the-blank card game where players can flex their naughty bone by using words and phrases on the Innuendo Cards and Party Cards to make blush- and giggle-worthy versions of their favorite movie titles on the Title Cards. Players get 7 Innuendo Cards each per hand, and there are 3 Party Cards that are laid out in the center of the table for every player to use.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Terracotta Army: Dice Tower 2023 Promo Tiles
description:
Originally available in Pack Bard from Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Two new tiles with new abilities for the Footman and Kneeling Archer.

During setup choose if you want to play with both or one of the promo tiles, then cover the corresponding specialist on the main board.

Footman - Each Scoring Phase: Count all warriors in the 8 spaces surrounding the Footman. The player who has Domination among these Warriors can flip to its active side one chosen Weapon token. There is no reward for Presence.

Kneeling Archer - When placing, rorate so that the Kneeling Archer faces a Warrior. Counts as a Warrior of the same type as the one it is facing. Breaks ties for the player whose Warrior it faces. When built, it is worth 1 VP per each Warrior in a group with that Kneeling Archer.

This fast-paced math game helps to sharpen mental math skills through an action-packed gameplay that both kids and adults will love:

     Praised by teachers for making math fun in both the classroom and home.
     Unique action cards can quicky change the game, adding another level of excitement.
     The perfect STEM game to play with kids, teens and adults.


How To Play?

Set up: Deal 15 card decks to each player and lay 5 cards face up in the middle of the table, this will be The Field.

How to play: Players can only hold up to 5 cards from their deck at any time. You can use any of the 5 cards in your hand to build an equation that equals any of the number cards in The Field by using addition, subtraction, multiplication and/or division.

Once a player comes up with an equation, they must quickly place the cards down over the number in The Field, creating a new number to be used. That player then draws cards from their deck to hold 5 cards again. Continue getting rid of the cards in your deck by building equations.

Winning Condition: First player to get down to 1 or no cards wins!

There are 2 card types in the deck:

NUMBER CARDS: Use these cards to build equations
ACTION CARDS: Use these cards to help yourself or give another player a tougher time. Anyone can play an Action Card at any time!

Dice Spray is a micro Roll-and-Write game in which players draft dice and attempt to fill in as many squares on a 6x6 grid as possible in 10 turns.

Emperor is a game inspired by the Tehnolog game "Mortal Combat," and is a game for kids.  The game is part boardgame, part building, and part dexterity, as the players must maneuver the battlefield, build their fortification, and attack the enemy by melee and shooting.  The game includes two shooting cannons.

The game comes with:
-2 firing cannon w/shells
-16 wall pieces
-8 gates
-16 warriors (8 per side)
-a playing field
-magic cards
-bank notes
-player aid
-rules

The first player to destroy all their opponent's warriors and castle, wins the game.

The game went on to inspire Emperor 2.

Airlift Stalingrad is a solitaire wargame that in an abstract way represents the failed operation to resupply the encircled 6th
German Army and save them from destruction in Stalingrad, during the World War II. In this game, player will have to
manage day-to-day airlift operations and also hold the front line against the Soviets, while working toward two
possible solutions – to breakout from the encirclement or to be relieved by the Operation Winter Storm.

Game Components:
One large map (board) on which game is played
12 tan (or yellow ) wooden cubes representing Junkers transport planes
15 gray wooden cubes representing army groups of the 6th Army
36 Operational cards
4 Blizzard cards
4 Lull cards
1 Paulus Surrender! card
1 Disrupted token
3 Parachute supplies tokens
6 Fortification tokens
8 Soviet tokens
1 Anti-air token
4 Iron cross tokens
3 white and 1 black d6 dice
3 red d6 dice
4 yellow wooden cubes for tracking various effects
6 gray wooden cubes for tracking fuel for a Breakthrough operation
1 cloth bag (called Event bag)
Cubes that are used for the event bag:
4 red
2 gray
2 tan
6 white

How to win the game:
You can win the game in two ways. By doing a successful roll for the Operation Winter Storm, or the Breakthrough!
Order token must be either on the Operation Winter Storm or Breakthrough! Spot.
In both cases weather must be Clear.
In case of the Operation Winter Storm, your Fuel reserves must be 4 or higher, while for the Breakthrough! They
must be 5 or higher.
If all of this is true, at the end of your turn you roll 2 d6 dice. If their sum is lower than the sum of your current
Morale and Food reserves, you win the game, if not you lose the game. You can only perform this roll once, but if you
have any Iron cross tokens (re-rolls) you can use them, but any DRM bonus from the Operational card for that turn is
not applied to this roll.
Lose conditions:
1. If you don't have any planes in your Airlift pool at the end of your turn, your game is lost
2. If all of your army groups are destroyed or in Hospital, game is lost
3. If you draw Paulus Surrenders! card from the Operational deck, you immediately lose the game
4. If 3 or more of the areas of your pocket are under Soviet control, game is lost
5. If all of the Operational cards are in the discard pile and you haven't execute successful Operation Winter
Storm or a Breakthrough! roll at the end of that turn, game is over
6. If you fail a roll for Operation Winter Storm or a Breakthrough!; you immediately lose the game

Goring through one turn, following a turn sequence:
1. Draw one operational card
2. After you draw a card, if possible, use 2 actions from it.

On the card are usually 4 possible actions you can perform for that turn. Each action is connected to each other with a line. You can only use 2 out of 4 actions, and they must be connected in orthogonal manner. For instance, you can either use top 2 connected actions, or bottom 2 connected. Or two leftmost one under another, or 2 rightmost one under another, connected.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It is a dawn of aviation warfare, the Great War of 1914-18.You are one of the freshly pilots, just came from the flight
school. Your expected lifespan on the front is 14 days. Will you be just another name on the tombstone, or will you
became an Ace?

14 Days is a solo wargame, simplified and abstracted to the core, with low number of components, perfect for a
campaign play on the run.

Game Components:
- 6 white d6 dice
- 1 red d6 die
- 5 red wooden cubes
- 4 white wooden cubes
- 3 yellow wooden cubes
- 2 blue wooden cubes
- 2 black wooden cubes
- one meeple in the shape of an airplane
- one bag for the wooden cubes
9 Cards:
- 1 double-sided Aircraft card (Allied/German)
- 1 double-sided Map card
- 1 Dogfight / Events card
- 1 Tables and Charts card
- 5 Terrain cards with randomized DRMs on the back

Object of the game:
Fly and survive all 14 missions of a campaign. Score points for every mission and check how well did you do on the European battlefield. You are encouraged to give some narrative to your achievements, so keeping a diary in a style of an airman on the front line is preferable.

Game Setup:
Basic setup is simple – shuffle all terrain cards in a face down deck. Place all wooden cubes in the bag, except white ones, and set you dice nearby. Choose the Allied or German side of the Aircraft and Map card (following the emblem on the card) and place Tables & Charts and Dogfight/Events cards close by.
Use the Mission log on the back of this rulebook and a pen to write down your mission results.

Mission types and mission end:
There are three types of missions: Patrol, Reconnaissance and Bombing run.
For Patrol and Bombing run you just need to finish with all encounters on the last card and return to base for mission to be successful.

For Reconnaissance is the same, but both you and wingman must return to be base for mission to be successful.

Mission can end in three ways, by returning to base on your accord before reaching the last terrain card in the column (failed mission), by getting shot down from the skies (failed mission), or in case of Reconnaissance your wingman gets shot down (failed mission), or you can finish with the last terrain card and return home (successful mission).

In any case, when returning to base you need to roll on the Landing table, applying all DRMs accordingly. You need 4+ to land successfully.

Remove any white cubes from the bag together with any other used or unused white cubes to have a new supply that will be placed back into the bag during an Aircraft prep stage.

Mission scoring:
If mission is successful – gain number of points as stated on the Map card.
-1 point if wingman has died.
-1 point if your Landing was unsuccessful.
1 point for each enemy plane destroyed.
Points for each special target destroyed (based on the type)*

    


• Reconnaissance plane = 1 point
• Reconnaissance balloon = 1 point
• Bomber = 2 points
• Zeppelin = 2 points

If mission is unsuccessful – you lose double the amount of points you would get by succeeding the mission. Example: if
you would score 2 points and mission ended in a failure, you wold gain -4 points instead.
Write everything down in your Mission log. If you survive the campaign, final amount of your points will give you a short summary how well you did as an airman in Great War.

Leveling up and Ace status:
It is possible to level up through the campaign.

-description from designer

description:
You are a German submarine commander, going on a hunt for Allied ships at the beginning of 1941. In next few years you have to sink as many ships as you can, scoring enough tonnage to win the game.

Components:
54 cards with variable usage. Cards are split into: Air Attacks, Ships and Escorts. Ships can come into three different sizes with different tonnage as: Small Freighter, Medium Freighter and Large Tanker.
Escorts come into three different types: Green, Trained and Veteran Escort. Escort ships also are marked with a big letter E on the top right edge of a card.

Game objective:
Play 2 patrols per month (each month is split into 15 days period, or two weeks), sink ships and record their tonnage, until the end of December of 1943, if you survive that long. Depending on the tonnage.

you sank you will either achieve total victory, some kind of consolation victory or total defeat. Dying in this game is considered a defeat.

Sequence of Play:
You are doing one patrol per 15 days (half of the month), starting from January of 1941.
Each patrol is consistent of encounters. Number of encounters depends on you, but the type is always random.
Your Patrol starts by drawing one card and using its Encounter value.

– If its an Air Attack! You will not have an option to react or do any action. Draw one card to see how many depth charges airplane has used to hit you, then draw that much cards for damage. Then, if you are not sunk by that attack, decide to either return to base or have another encounter.
– If its a Group encounter, first draw a card to establish a time of day and weather (using both icons in a row from that card) and then draw number of random ships to form a group and continue to battle phase. Same thing is done for a Convoy encounter, but you must form a specific group of ships and escorts.
– Battle phase – torpedo attack – may have up to 2 rounds
– Before starting with a torpedo attack you may use an action called WAIT. If you are not satisfied with a current day of time and weather, you may replace that card with the new one from the top of the deck. You have to keep the new card and you can only perform this action once per battle phase.
– You may also decide to DISENGAGE and skip the battle phase after you have form a group of ships. But, you can do this only once per Patrol! If you do it again you must return to base immediately, ending a patrol for that month.
– To attack ships chose your targets and then depending on the weather you my fire up to 6 or up to 8 torpedoes by drawing cards from a deck and placing them face down in a column, under your targeted ships. You may split number of fired torpedoes per target as you wish, you may also decide not to fire a full complement. You my fire at multiple targets of your choice.

Marking your tonnage and time passage You can either use a chart provided with the game or keep a diary in a RPG style of writing. In both cases you must mark that half of a month has passed and write down your tonnage you manage to score from your patrol.

If your submarine had 4 to 5 damage, you must skip half of a month before going to another patrol. If you manage to survive whole three years (1941-1943), summarize all tonnage and consult the chart below to see how did you do.

-description from designer

In addition it comes with a black cloth pouch that can be used during the setup of the game to randomly assign workers on the 3 cargo spacecrafts.

Ability: Get 1 coin from each other player. If a player can't pay, take 1 barrel (from supply).

When you bought the game at Spiel 2023, you would get 2 free promo cards out of 6 available and this was one of the possible picks. If you'd pay 3 euro, you would get the remaining 4.

Projekt Q: Vanvid is a cooperative game for 1-5 players based on Jussi Adler-Olsen's crime thriller series about Department Q.

This board game tells a rich story as players control the familiar investigators Carl Mørck, Assad, Rose, and Gordon through a week of investigation. 
Mørck and Department Q delve into a dark chapter of Copenhagen's history in order to find a psychiatrist reported missing in a snowy March night.

It is not necessary to have read the books, and the game contains no spoilers for the plot of earlier or later books.

Projekt Q: Vanvid is the first game in a planned series of games.

In this series, participants collaborate to solve a cold case.   The story begins on Monday morning and concludes no later than Saturday evening.

Can you solve the mystery before time runs out?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vanaheim: Weapons and Armour Expansion Pack
description:
Adds 18 new cards to the Vanaheim game, increasing your hero's chance of surviving with new equipment across all levels for all classes - and their animal companion.

Two new buildings dramatically change your Town; you can now search for specific equipment in the Alchemist and Smith and store excess items in your base of operations.

Adds unique item cards at level 2, 3 and 4 for mission rewards.

boardgame: Vanaheim: Scrolls and Staffs Expansion Pack
description:
Adds 18 new cards to the Vanaheim game, increasing your hero's chance of surviving with new equipment across all levels for the Sage and other magic users.

Scrolls are high power one shot weapons that are used at range to smite your foes. Use the Hall of Words building to ensure you have copies of these scrolls so you can restock at the town.

New staffs, wands, potions and armour to bring more damage to the battlefield at all levels.

6 scrolls, 3 wands, 3 staves, 2 potions, 2 items of clothing and 2 buildings.

Everyone at the studio is buzzing about the latest film. This murder mystery has just one problem: the director’s cut reveals the culprit too early! Rearrange the scenes to build up the suspense and turn a mixed-up mystery into a sleeper hit.

Expand your Moving Pictures experience, or play this entirely on its own as a standalone entry. Moving Pictures is a quick little game for two with two phases in the game. In the first, you take turns playing cards without communication face-down into a pile with the goal of having them in order. Then comes the editing phase, where you resolve abilities and can make deleted scenes. If you have any scenes still out of order, you lose. Otherwise you move on to the second act, repeating the process and then the 3rd act if you continue to do well. If you ever wanted to produce a movie, here’s your chance to do so on a budget! Add in a detective flair to your experience as you delight over the elegant artwork, and trigger fun abilities as you go along trying to piece together the film.


The cameras are rolling on our newest blockbuster: Moving Pictures: Marked for Mystery! This cooperative game for 2 players was designed by Chris Klimowski and is sure to be a big hit.

In  Moving Pictures: Marked for Mystery players take turns placing numbered scene cards from their hand into the shared Timeline row in hopes of getting all 6 cards in order. The catch? They can't speak or signal to each other, so they'll have to watch each other's play closely for clues. Each card also has an effect listed which can impact placement, swap cards around, or even allow players a rare opportunity to share information.

Getting it right once is a feat in itself but a full game of Moving Pictures: Marked for Mystery is played over 3 Acts. The Timeline grows with each Act, making each round harder than the one before. Thankfully you also have access to 2 Deleted Scenes: if the round would end with scenes out of order, you can spend a Deleted Scene to hide one card. These Deleted Scenes don’t refresh, meaning that you only get 2 mistakes before the credits roll.

COMBINING GAMES
Marked for Mystery can be  combined with another Moving Pictures game. If combining, choose one scene card each numbered 1-16 to play with, and any two Deleted Scene cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hall of the Skinwalkers: Vanaheim Adventure
description:
In the first expansion to Vanaheim, players journey to the lair of the Skinwalkers, rescue powerful allies, find new silver weapons and destroy the Werewolf overseer before he builds an army!

In addition to a replayable level 2 adventure, this pack contains 4 new town cards, 10 items (including 2 Level 5 weapons), 8 treasure cards, 4 new monsters, 4 new spells and 3 new skills.

Breakfast Shy postcard game, include in December 2022 Board Game of the Month Club Postcard package.

Very light game rules, a game that could be on the back of a cereal box!


Be the first player to build a complete robot by collecting all of the pieces to assemble the 3 parts.

To start the game, both players simultaneously roll 3 dice each and then each player spends their dice as fast as they can. After spending or deciding not to spend your dice, immediately reroll all 3 of your dice without waiting for your opponent.

Spending Dice: Use the values on your rolled dice (either individually or adding multiple dice together) to make purchases. Each spent die can only be used once before it is rerolled.

Dice can be used to purchase pieces or helpers. To buy a piece, spend one or more dice that total the number of the piece and cross off that piece. Pieces are grouped by their associated part; after purchasing all the pieces for a part, fill in the circle for that part. Players may instead spend 2 dice of any values to buy a helper. Circle an uncircled helper. Each helper has a specific value and can be crossed off once to count as a die of that value. (This is an additional die value which can be used alongside the rolled dice.)

The game ends immediately when one player has filled in all 3 parts. They win!

boardgame: Fury of the Jotnar: Vanaheim Adventure
description:
The legendary Jotnar, monstrous elemental giants have ripped open fiery portals deep within the earth to Vanaheim. Will you sieze the opportunity to pass through the Rifts, explore a new realm, find new treasures, and possibly a quick but painful death?

This 50 card expansion adds new buildings, Jotnar of each level (and their elite versions), treasure, items, events and new decision map cards.

Decision map cards allow for risks, dangers and rewards. Jotnar Rifts allow access to their treasure rooms - if you survive. The Mine and the Temple expand your town, and bring a new companion: Vala, the travelling healer.

And of course, there's a giant slaying weapon - if you can find it before the Jotnar find you.

Breakfast Shy postcard game, included in the June 2023 Board Game of the Month Club Postcard package.

Very light game rules, a game that could be on the back of a cereal box!


Each turn, roll 2 six-sided dice. Place each of these values into a different, unfilled pea (circle).

To place a value, it must be exactly 1 higher or lower than each adjacent filled pea. Adjacent means directly left, right, up, or down and does not include diagonals. For the purposes of placing a new value, ignore the values of any smushed peas and any peas in pods that are adjacent to the intended placement. After placing both values, start a new turn.

If you cannot or choose not to place a die value, instead smush any pea (including a previously-filled pea) by crossing it out. These will count against your score.

Instead of placing the values rolled, you may instead create a pod. Circle any 3 peas (filled, unfilled, or smushed) that are continuously adjacent — in other words, A is adjacent to B, which is adjacent to C. Pods may not overlap. Filled peas within a pod must always have the same value. Any unfilled peas when the pod is created can only be filled by using that value; this is an exception to the normal placement requirements. Each pod’s value is independent of each other pod. Peas in a pod may be smushed as normal. After creating a pod, start a new turn.

When all peas on the board have been either smushed or filled, the harvest is complete.

Count up the number of smushed peas to determine your score. The lower your score, the better you’ve done. Complete the harvest without smushing any peas to make Peater proud!

boardgame: Through the Shimmering Portal: Vanaheim Adventure
description:
Piece together the mystery of opening the Vanir gates in a huge series of quests that will take you to new worlds, where you will face new threats and, if successful, gain incredible new powers.

New decision cards will give you moral dilemma that will offer rewards, at a price. Visit a familiar hut to recruit companions, new and old, who can be equipped with weapons and armour and support your adventures in new ways.

Adds above-ground map cards to explore, new monsters and wilderness creatures, items, treasures, Town buildings, and above-ground locations.

Classified is a cooperative, code-breaking party game in which you need deduction, creativity and forward thinking to win. Which photo shows the next target? Scribble out numbers and spell words to give your allies clues. Can you pass secret messages to your field operatives and complete your mission and outsmart the enemy?

To set up the game, lay out 6 Mission Tokens and 6 corresponding Photo Cards. Each player will then take a Mission Card, which will indicate which Photo Card is theirs. Next, pick up a Code Card. In Special Ops 1, you must scribble a message on your Code Card to transmit to your fellow spies, this should be a word that describes your secret target Photo Card. If your Photo Card is guessed correctly then the target has been identified and you will win the Code Card.

In Special Ops 2, players can only use the code cards that you won in Special Ops 1 to transmit messages. Pick a target player, and shuffle the Mission Cards. Put one Mission Card face down in front of them, they must look at it secretly. The Photo indicated by the Mission Card is their target for this round. The target player may now use any of the remaining scribbled-on Code Cards to help everyone else guess the right Photo. 
If the Photo is guessed correctly, the players win that Photo; however, if it is not guessed correctly the enemy will win it. Once all players have had a turn as the target, count how many Photos your spies have won and how many Photos the enemy has. If your team has more Photos than the enemy, then the players win, but if the enemy has the same number as your team or more, the enemy wins.

It's time for the flashiest auction of the year. To play Bragging Rights you'll need foresight, bluffing, and big kahunas! Each of you must splash the cash and try to buy the most valuable items in the auction. But wait… any money you have left over turns into bragging points! Will you gain enough points to earn the envy of your opponents and win?

Phase One of the game is the auction, where players bid for job lots for a chance of securing valuable Item Cards, from private yachts to diamond rings! There are four types of 
collectible with the Item Cards. They are: Watches, Transport, Antiques and Crypto. Crypto is a trump suit. Will you manage to get your hands on items that are worth bragging about or will your opponents outbid you?

In Phase Two, it's time for the drinks party, get ready to compare and show off about your items!
Everyone picks up the Item Cards they won at auction. 
The person who has the most money puts down any one of their Item Cards – face up. Each player must then put down an Item Card. When possible, Item Cards should match the type of the original card. If you can’t match the type, you may play any card – including Crypto. After you’ve all played one Item Card, it makes up a trick. A Crypto Card beats all other cards – even if the other cards have a higher value. The only way to beat a Crypto Card is to play one with a higher value!
Finally, if you can’t match the type, or play a Crypto Card, you still have to play an Item Card. Put down whatever you can. Unfortunately, it means you immediately lose the hand.
Whoever plays the highest Crypto Card wins the trick. If no one plays a Crypto Card, the person who played the highest Item Card of the original type wins the trick.

Bragging Rights ends after 12 rounds, when you’ll have played all your Item Cards. The person with the most tricks wins.

Instant Karma is a party game that celebrates when the Universe, in the most fitting ways imaginable, has punished jerks for screwing over or being shady to others. In this game, which contains 300 hilarious real-life stories of poetic justice, players try to bluff each other by trying to think up just desserts that are more plausible than what really happened!

In Instant Karma, One player (the guru) reads out the start of a real-life story of a situation where instant karma occurred to the others. All other players then each write down a made-up, but believable ending to the story and hand it to the guru. The guru then reads out the real ending and all the made-up endings, in random order. The others must guess which is the correct one. You win a point for each player who is fooled into believing that your made-up ending is the real one, as well as for picking the real ending.

boardgame: I'm Getting Too Old For This Sh*t
description:
When did Miley Cyrus twerk on stage with Robert Thicke at the VMAS? In this fun family game, you just have to answer one simple question - when did stuff happen? Delve in to recent history to find out if you are too old for this sh*t, or if you’ve still got it!

To start the game, one player takes the top card from the pack and reads out a question. All questions will be about events that have occurred in recent history. Players then shout out what they think is the correct answer.
One variation of this rule is to use pencils and paper to write down your answers instead and hand them in to the questioner. This way, no one can copy or be influenced by anyone else’s answer.
Players earn three points for guessing correctly, and one point for being one year out. The first player to reach 15 points is the winner!

A fun party game in which players pitch a movie remake from randomly generated genre, movie star, and setting cards to try to get their movie made!

To start a round of Movie Madness, each player is dealt 1 actor card. Then, one player is chosen to be the first Producer. The Producer deals out setting cards and three genre cards and announces them. Once the Producer shouts out ‘Action!’, players now grab a setting card from the display.

Each player pitches a film that’s a cross between two of the three genres, featuring their actor card and their setting card, preferably with a short title. The Producer divides the cards into the players' score piles between the pitches they like best. Whoever has the most cards in their score pile at the end of the game is the winner!

Embark on an epic journey into the enchanting realm of Pandora, a world teeming with adventure and strategy. Prepare to immerse yourself in a universe filled with excitement, where your destiny awaits, formidable challenges arise, and triumph is the ultimate goal in this captivating board game experience.

The game's heart lies in Secret Missions. Each player pursues their own path, striving to complete missions that unfurl throughout the game. As the game nears its conclusion, victory hinges on accumulating Victory Points.

Each turn includes the following actions, which can be performed in any order:

1. Movement: Navigate the board to the desired destination.

2. Build: Build bases to strengthen your character and gain advantages.

3. Fight: Engage in a battle for points among players or attack monsters where you team up with other players in the same territory to defeat enemies.

4. Train: Improve your skills and gain lasting benefits by training at a base. Ending your turn after training.

5. Gather: Obtain gold from the board or try to draw Pandora cards. Gold collection and Pandora card attempts are mutually exclusive.

6. Completing secret Missions: Try to complete secret missions to set you up towards the final scoring at the end of the game.

7. Character actions and resource trading: Use character-specific abilities and possibly exchange resources with other players.

The game continues until either the last card in the Pandora deck is revealed or a player reaches the indicated victory points on the VP (Victory Points) tracker. If any player would be defeated during the game, all players will lose the game all together.

After game reach it’s end the player's bonus points from secret missions are then tallied, and the player with the highest total emerges as the victor, etching their name among the legends of Pandora.

-description from designer

Trick Pages is a solitaire game in book form that challenges you to defeat monsters via trick-taking.

Initially the 25 cards in your hand have no effects — only their value as cards. The enemy monster plays cards automatically based on die rolls and a specified system. Win enough single-card tricks, and you defeat the enemy...then you have to fight the next monster with the cards remaining in your hand.

If you run out of cards before defeating all eight monsters, you die, at which point all of the cards return to your hand, all of the monsters respawn, and you return to the beginning of the book.

However, by defeating monsters you earn money, and you can use that money to purchase card effects, ideally allowing you to defeat monsters with fewer cards. Effects apply only to cards in hand, so if you've played strong cards already, you might have to wait until your next life to apply magic to them.

Monsters have different abilities, not just more life to defeat, so you need to develop tactics to defeat them, possibly by taking detours in the book dungeon to earn money through side quests. In the end, you must defeat eight enemies to win.

Shortly after your “happily ever after,” the two of you, the stable stalwart and the free-spirited idealist, come to an impasse.

Cards have either your suit or your friend’s suit. Your deck’s dominant suit will decide where your convictions lie. Cards of your suit enable new actions, but you’ll also want to shift your
cards into your friend’s deck if you want to change their mind.

Can you cooperate towards a balanced resolution? If you are both stubborn you’ll separate.Although, you could dominate the narrative instead…

Gameplay consists of the two of you taking turns performing actions to shift cards between the two of you, the shared support deck, and the reject pile.  Players can take special actions with cards of their own suit, or they can take the default "express" action to place cards from their hand into the other player's deck.

The game ends when one player concedes with the other player's cards, or when the support deck runs out.  The resolution of the game depends on the make up of the players decks.  If your deck has more cards of the other player's suit than your own suit, then you've changed your mind to favor your partner.  If both players change their minds enough, they cooperate.  If only one changes their mind, one player dominates the narrative.  If neither changes their mind, they separate.

-description from designer

Clue: WWII Spies & Espionage is a highstakes game where danger lurks in the shadows for six secret agents on a mission to carry out covert operations that will thwart the enemy and secure victory for the Allies!
