Players will move around the European Theater of Operations to determine WHICH mission-critical item was taken,  WHO is the fake agent,  and WHERE is the imposter's base of operations.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
The world’s resources are depleting at a critical time, and you need the right infrastructure to survive. Take turns burning cards to activate either the trucks ? or mills ? of your tableau. If you build enough bread ? to quell your unrest ? before the end ☢️, you survive. There’s more than enough bread for everyone… provided you work with each other rather than against.

? Activate your trucks to gather (or steal) cards, either for yourself or for others.
? Activate your mills to build infrastructure, adding cards to any player's tableau.

With these 2 actions and 5 different types of infrastructure,
the players are equipped with all the tools they need to:
• Search the deck for materials, like the pivotal bread .
• Build up a tableau to efficiently distribute bread .
• Give cards to a friend so they don’t burn through the deck.
• Steal cards from a greedy hoarding player.
• Build walls  to stop a rampant thieving player.

The game finishes when the end event ☢️ appears, which is shuffled into the latter part of the deck. Unrest events ? along the way will set the players back, and force them to burn more cards to activate their infrastructure.

Can you hold a cooperative spirit, even as resources dwindle and you’re forced to contend with unexpected famine?

-description from designer

In "Spandau 1780", players direct influential personalities and actively shape the development of the region around historic Spandau between 1780-1880.
The goal of each player is to fill his cabinet of wonders with all kinds of rare works of art and thus compete with the other players.
The player who has collected the most genuine works of art at the end of the game wins.

Spandau 1780 starts with each player selecting a character from 1780 represented on a player board.
The player then decides to play either with the standard income of resources or flips the board to choose the character specific income.

Spandau 1780 is played in turns where player earn coins and goods, play and draw cars as well as expand their influence on the game plan.

Each player is presented with a lot of options to choose from:

     Expand influence now to gain more income later?
     Invest in common wares or luxury goods?
     Participate in a military campaign, gain influence with the crown or examine artworks?


Each round there is an auction of a new piece of art. The winning bet determines who starts the round.
For each genuine piece of art there is also a fake one. If a fake one is exposed, it is removed from the game.
At any time every player has to offer one piece of art to be exchanged by a piece from another player. This is a good way to get rid of fake art. But be aware, if exposed the fake art is removed and another piece of art has to be offered immediately.

As soon as a players art collection reaches twelve, the game ends. And only the player with the most genuine pieces wins in the end.

-

What do players like?

The combination of 

     turn based economy building, 
     short and long term strategy, 
     hard choices if to keep cards to play later or expand influence now
     and all that just to collect art


Different winning strategies:

     collect as many pieces of art as possible or invest resources to find genuine pieces?
     expose and remove fakes or trade them secretly?


And last but not least he historical setting

     played on a historic map, with many references to people, goods and iconic buildings


-description from designer

Deck 'Em: Ancient Dynasties is the second board game creation by designer and self-publisher Matthew Toellner. The game features a historically-accurate progression of civilizations and their rulers in the middle east and mediterranean sea regions during the late bronze age and early iron age (1900-400 BC). Game design is inspired by Britannia (board game), Age of Empires (PC games), and YouTube history videos.

FEATURES
Print and play.
Online play via 3rd party tools.
Rules for automated NPC (non-player-civilization) actions!

—description from the designer

Захват Реганы ("Capture Regana") is a game dealing with the invasion of the magical planet Regana by the Empire's "Polaris" troops.  Defending the planet are the soldiers and mages of Regana who can use magic.  The planet's palm trees help produce the necessary ingredients for their spells, which is why the Empire is invading.

The game comes with:

     2 forces of 12 figures (24)
     4 palm trees
     3 fortifications
     2 harvesters
     30 cards
     2 "checkboxes"
     3 dice
     playing field
     rules


On the invading player's turn they roll the dice, with the number rolled being their total action points.  These points are used to move, harvest, and attack. The defender does the same, but they can also use their points to play magic cards.

The objective of the defending forces is to either capture one of the flags from the enemy fortresses or eliminate all 12 invaders.  The invaders win if they harvest three palm trees, or size control of the tower of mages, or eliminate all 12 defenders.

—user summary

It is finally here...SOLO MODE! The player will face off against the Shadow who is trying to collect points, the elemental stones, and spread Darkness throughout the Realms. The Solo Mode is extremely low upkeep while also being quite difficult to beat.

The Shadow will be spreading darkness throughout the realms while also racing through the dungeon to collect the Elemental Stones. If darkness spreads into each realm, the player immediately loses. However, if the player is able to defeat the Shadow's Final Form, then the player immediately wins.
The Solo Mode is only played with the Base Game of Adventure Realms. This only utilizes the base game board and should not be played with the expansion boards.

—description from the publisher

The solo mode is part of "The time trigger" expansion but also sold separately.

Tiny Epic Crimes is a fast-paced, crime-fighting, deduction game for 1 to 4 players that offers multiple ways to play. The game can be enjoyed competitively, cooperatively, or solo.

Players take on the role of detectives, and depending on the game mode, will be working either together…. OR separately…. to crack the murder case. Time matters, and the first 48 hours after any crime are the most critical. Players race around Echo Ridge City collecting evidence, such as the murder weapon, forensics, or the getaway vehicle. This secret evidence is sifted through using the novel Tiny Epic Decoder System, which keeps all of the murderer’s other evidence hidden. This knowledge eliminates suspects and helps identify the true killer.

But crime doesn’t stop for one investigation. Players must answer the call of duty, juggling their time as other criminal activities arise across the city. Resolving these events allow players to gain useful alibis and powerful Force Cards, but knowing full well that their actions will take them hours closer to a reckoning.

In their final hour, players will stake their careers on a final guess: who they think the murderer really is… hoping that their wits and detective skills are enough to bring the correct suspect to justice. Can you hold this city of crime together long enough to crack the case, or will it go cold and your time on the force run out?

The Deluxe edition of Tiny Epic Crimes includes Tiny Epic Crimes: Crooked Capos Mini Expansion packaged inside of the base game's box. Crooked Capos has 2 separate modules that can individually be added to the game: "Crooked" and "Capos." In the Crooked module, players are dealt Loyalty Cards which state whether they are “CLEAN” or “CROOKED.” Players can investigate whether another cop is crooked, getting either big rewards (if they are right) or big penalties (if wrong). In the Capos module, the Capos represent an extra challenge and reward for the officers! Capos are treated like mobsters while in the city, but are not like regular mobsters. Instead, each can only be arrested by following the requirements listed on their card.

Triominos Conquest features the classic Triominos gameplay with a twist! Score the most points by conquering the largest area.

In addition to the triangular Triomino tiles that feature numbers at each corner, the game includes three types of "conquest" cover pieces: Triohex, Triotri, and Triodia — that is, a colored hexagon that covers six tiles, a colored triangle that covers four tiles, and a colored diamond that covers two tiles. Each player has their own set of conquest pieces.

On a turn, you must place a Triomino from your hand next to one or more Triominos in the playing area so that the numbers on the Triomino you place match the numbers on each adjacent edge it touches. If this placement creates the shape of one of your conquest pieces, you can place that conquest piece on these tiles to claim them; if your tile placement creates a shape that includes a smaller conquest piece, you can replace the smaller conquest piece with your larger one and return the smaller piece to its owner.

Once all the tiles have been placed, sum the numbers on the tiles under your conquest pieces to determine your score. Whoever has the most points wins.
Stalingrad was to be one of Tehnolog's first true wargames.  It contained no plastic figures and was a typical hex-and-counter wargame, but with standees instead of counters.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Clickport is a revolutionary strategic game in which players, acting as server room workers, will create patterns on the board using Ethernet cables. They can do this by plugging and unplugging different colored cables. The player who completes their pattern first wins the game!

—description from the publisher

The best server workers gathered together to finally decide, who would be the fastest. The servers are waiting. The cables are red hot. Let the race begin!

Speedport is a dexterity game, where all that matters is your speed. Players must create patterns on their boards using cables, and only the fastest will win!

HOW TO PLAY? IT’S SIMPLE: 
Take your server and 4 cables. 
Put one pattern card on the table. 
Lay out this pattern as fast as possible by plugging the cables into the server. 
The one who arranges the pattern first needs to shout SPEEDPORT! 
If the pattern matches, the pattern card goes to the player as a point. 
(The game is played up to 5 points. Do not remove the cables from the server after the first round of the game).

—description from the publisher

ペンギンかんそくたい (Penguin Expedition Team) is a co-operative memory game from Japan. Players must identify 8 different kinds of penguins.

The player is a member of an expedition to observe penguins, and studies penguin ecology in Antarctica. But in order to find penguins, observation is only possible in summer. If the player can find all the penguins within the specified number of times, the player wins. Since it is a cooperative game, you can advance the game while consulting with others.

このゲームは協力型の神経衰弱です。
プレイヤーはペンギンを観測する探検隊の一員となり、ペンギンを見つけるため南極大陸でペンギン観測を行います。
規定回数内にすべてのペンギンを見つけることができれば、プレイヤーの勝ちとなります。
協力ゲームなので相談しながら、ゲームを進めることができます。
description by publisher
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

description:
Once upon a time, in a galaxy far, far away, raged... no, not Star Wars, but quite ordinary trade showdowns. The galaxy was very tiny (only 4 star systems), the only useful resource was energy, which was used both as fuel for ships and as money.

But there were all kinds of entrepreneurs, adventurers, galactic swindlers and star bandits! And they all offered their services (far from free of charge) to every trading magnate, and when asked “Who are you?”, they modestly lowered their eyes and quietly answered: “Hero!”

The game comes with:

    16 Hero Cards
    21 Item cards
    4 Base Cards
    48 Ship mats:
        24 Fighters
        8 Raiders
        8 Transports
        8 Cruiser
    
    1 Game Board
    2 Dice
    2 Sheets of money counters


Players play on a board that is Monopoly-like. However, payers are not restricted to this level, and they may go across the board using the "space" areas in between.  Players that land on another player's spot must engage them.  They can try their strength, or if they are not so strong, they can try their wit. Players engage in trade and smuggling, have bar encounters, hire sidekicks, attack bases and each other, all in an attempt to accomplish their goal.

To win the game you must do one of the following:

     Eliminate all other enemy Heroes
     Capture all enemy ships
     Have the most money


The game is played for a number of turns decided by the players beforehand, with 8-12 being the norm.

boardgame: ならんで！鳥獣戯画 (Line up! Chojugiga)
description:
ならんで！鳥獣戯画 (Line up! Chojugiga) is a set collection game from Japan. The Chōjū-jinbutsu-giga is a famous set of four scrolls, depicting frogs, rabbits, monkeys in anthropomorphic stories, with the fourth scroll showing humans too. The scrolls are a national treasure and often reproduced today.

The publisher says:
This is a game where you create an ancient Japanese picture scroll called Choju-giga.
The aim is to take the revealed cards, arrange them, and create a highly valuable picture scroll.

鳥獣戯画と言われる、日本古来の絵巻物を作るゲームです。
公開されているカードを取り、並べて、価値の高い絵巻物を作ることを目指します。
description by publisher
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

description:
ロケットカンパニー (Rocket Company) is a children's game game from Japan, with simple set collection.

The publisher says:

Flip the cards and complete the rocket!
Flip the cards and let lots of rockets fly.
A rocket is made up of three parts: a top, a tank, and an engine. If the number of tank parts is large, a long rocket can be made. The player who sends the most rockets wins.

The winner is the player who connects rocket parts of different thickness and fires as many rockets as possible. The rules are simple, so even small children can play.

カードをめくってロケットを完成させよう！
カードをめくって、たくさんのロケットを飛ばしましょう。
ロケットは「トップ」「タンク」「エンジン」の３種類からできています。タンク部分の枚数が多いと長いロケットができます。
多くのロケットを飛ばしたプレイヤーが勝ちです。

太さの異なるロケットのパーツを繋げ、なるべくたくさんのロケットを発射させたプレイヤーの勝ちです。
簡単なルールなので、小さいお子さんから遊ぶことができます。
description by publisher
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

description:
Fight as your ancestors once did to create your own myths and legends!

Darklands is a tabletop skirmish wargame in which YOU command the hosts of man and beast that your ancestors once did for glory, power or simple survival. Fight breathtaking battles on your tabletop with some of the most finest resin miniatures ever created!

What if dragons really flew, in those ancient times, and giants really walked the earth? What if berserkers actually were bear-men, man and bear joined together? What if all of the myths and legends we take for granted were real and our ancestors fought against - and with - creatures and beings and realms we thought were nothing more than the fantasies of our uncivilised selves?

Darklands answers all of those questions for you and more, so take a deep breath and re-create the true history of our world with the Darklands: Second Edition Rules found within this hardback book!
 The second edition of Darklands plays much faster than the first edition rules but loses none of the immersiveness and atmosphere of the original. Combat, shooting and sorcery is greatly streamlined but the game's structure is roughly the same - players alternate the activations of their units, performing actions and reactions with them until each unit on the battlefield has activated, at which point the battle hour (what we call a turn) ends and the units that are left steel themselves for another round of combat, shooting and sorcery!

Welcome to the world of fundamental particles! Here, the rules of Quantum Mechanics and Einstein's Relativity make particles appear from nowhere, travel through walls and behave in extraordinary ways! Will you ever be able to understand how it functions? With Tutti Quantum, you'll learn seven simple rules for connecting particles so you can build the most extravagant Feynman diagram. Each graph corresponds to a real-world particle phenomenon, whether that is an explanation of the blue sky or the discovery of the Higgs boson. The rarest processes are worth a Nobel prize... and if you find them, you'll win - the game, that is!

What Justin, Britney & Co. would have preferred to hide from the public… Stupid mishaps, incredible misfortunes, absurd types of death: Even V.I.P.s such as movie stars and pop stars, athletes, politicians, writers and artists are not immune to this! And that's exactly what these black stories are made of! Who manages to solve the cases around embarrassing celebrity mishaps?

They are black, enigmatic and thoroughly evil: black stories - This is THE cult series at moses. The morbid, creepy, frightening and sometimes entertaining puzzles are about death and accidents that can't be more gruesono. By guessing, exactly these stories are to be found out behind it. Not always so easy...

In this roll and move game you play as either Harry, Ron, Hermione or Luna. 
First, you race around Diagon Alley (outer circle) where you need to collect 6 school supplies. Next, you board the Hogwarts Express (inner circle) for the final trip to school. The first person arriving at Hogwarts wins.

An exciting crime game for true master thieves!
Crime thriller experience paired with cooperative gaming fun: In “The Perfect Plan” an exciting story with a very special task awaits, because the players plan the perfect coup together. Your goal: to steal a painting by a famous painter from a museum. A variety of plans, maps and clues help the players to develop the perfect break-in plan to break into the museum undetected - and, in the best case scenario, to escape undetected with their loot. The team can make free decisions based on the clue cards, which provide an extra dose of thrill. An atmospheric, cooperative gaming experience full of teamwork, creativity and masterful planning.

boardgame: Go Big or Go Broke: Bet You Can't be Beat
description:
Go Big or Go Broke is the family game where extreme effort meets laughter and fun! This family-friendly party game combines simple actions with utmost enthusiasm and strategic betting. Challenge your friends and family to tasks in this next-level charades like game that is brought to you by a female-owned brand.

This party game gets players to bet who will win the round. The game can be a family-friendly play or turned into an adult party game by challenging you to use real money! There’s five different categories for more fun including vocal, impersonation, silly, physical and direct challenge.

—description from the publisher

Speed is required at Wortwerk. 7 randomly drawn letters are in a row and varied categories ensure long-lasting fun: With the help of the series of letters, all players simultaneously form a word that fits the category. The trick: Letters may be omitted or added as desired. Thus, the existing series of letters "SNNBLME" quickly becomes "sunflower". The more letters are used, the more points there are. Eloquent players can combine cleverly and secure the most valuable points in front of the others. An innovative word search with brains that should not be missing at the next game night!

boardgame: The Isle of Cats: Treasure Pack
description:
The Isle of Cats Treasure Pack is an expansion for The Isle of Cats, adding new options to the rare treasure tiles in the game.

—description from the publisher

In Dodo, you had to land a wobbly dodo egg safely in a boat — and in Dodo Ahoi! this boat is now setting off on a great journey...but unfortunately no one has secured the load, and now the egg is rolling dangerously fast towards the edge of the boat. Can you save the egg before it hits the water?

Communicate with other players to place the right cards and steer the boat through the open ocean until you find the right island to serve as a resting place for this precious egg.

It's a great way to have fun with friends, parties, and group dates! A little embarrassing, mean orders will make you laugh out loud!

Recommended age: 10 years old and up
Number of players: 3 or more

[Introduction]
All players raise their hands and swear, "I will be obedient and follow any instructions on the card."

[How to play]
- First, shuffle the cards.
- The person who lost the rock-paper-scissors game turns over a card and carries out the command written on the card.
- The more seriously you play without being embarrassed, the more fun it will be.
- When the deck of cards runs out, you can shuffle them and play again and again.

★ For the cards with blank speech bubbles, everyone should think up and write funny commands.
designers: Tsukue to Isu (つくえといす)

description:
Learn shapes and colours. Choose your animal and try to be the first player to fill all the spaces, by spinning the roulette spinner.

—description from the publisher

Pola Magnetic Game is a brand new 3D Magnetic Games System! It is a whole constellation of games coming to you. Play in countless ways with this amazing system or even design your own games! Explore two ways to play we have ready with more to come. The Pola Magnetic Games System is your playground!

Pola can be played anywhere thanks to its magnetic components. It doesn't require 20 minutes of setup or special boards! Just grab a friend and start playing, on a couch, in a park, on a beach, or even in space!

Pola was created in collaboration with people with visual impairments to make our game fully accessible for them as well!
French version of the black ninja. Adds a new character one can pick, who uses various swords and throwing knives to do battle.
Adds a new character one can play, who, in true mime fashion, pantomimes the use of various weapons.
An alien disguised as a school teacher. Adds a new character one can play, who uses school supplies (rulers, pencils) until finally she gives up the facade and whips out her laser gun.
Adds a new character one can play. Mostly a biter, it also uses random junk or pieces of its last meal as weapons.

boardgame: Dorfromantik: Expansion Content from Dorfromantik – The Duel
description:
The game material for the expansion for Dorfromantik: The Board Game comes in the box of Dorfromantik: The Duel.

There are 4 special tiles, 7 new order tiles, 4 more success cards and more counters.

The success cards are in German language.

Every year the grand arenas of Championland overflow with hopeful athletes and excited crowds. Pin your opponent three times to become a true Champion!

Wrestling is one of the purest competitions in Championland. This head-to-head test of each athlete's physical and mental acumen draws huge crowds who analyze every move to see who'll come out on top. The best wrestlers spend their time doing the same, knowing that even the slightest advantage might keep them on their feet.

In Championland: Wrestling, players take turns placing cards and guessing at their opponent’s cards to score knockdowns. On a player’s turn, they’ll play a card from their hand face-up. The opponent plays a card face-down. Now the active player must guess whether the face-down card is higher than, lower than, or equal to the face-up card. Get it right and they’ll earn a knockdown; get it wrong and their opponent takes control, playing a new card face-down and comparing it to the previously revealed card.

Each round continues until one player wins by either getting two knockdowns in a row OR getting a guess of “equal” correct. The first player to win three rounds takes the match.


Championland is a series of standalone 18-card games which uses a single rules system for all entries, then adds individual rules for each specific game to capture the experience of that sport.

Every Championland game includes two 9-card decks numbered 1-9. Each player takes a deck, shuffles it, and fans it out in their hand. On a turn, they’ll play only a single card and the card played MUST come from the furthest edge of their hand on either side. Players cannot reorder their hand, so they must make the best of the choices they have available!
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
In the Valley of the Vikings, the annual barrel bowling tournament is about to begin. Are you brave enough to compete? By tactically playing your cards, you knock down the right barrels and thus cleverly position your game pieces on the dock. Only players who are well positioned can capture the most gold coins and sail away. Courage, tactics and a willingness to take risks are required, but players who risk too much on the dock and fall into the water will go away empty handed. The player who captures the most gold coins wins.

Contents:
42 playing cards (barrel cards and bridge cards), 24 gold coins, 4 wooden discs, 1 game manual.

6 nimmt!: 30 Jahre-Edition is a 30th anniversary edition of 6 nimmt! that includes the standard base game that's playable by 2-10 players, as well as cards used in four variants of the base game (created by fans to commemorate the 25th anniversary of the game) and separate cards used in a new co-operative variant. Each variant must be played on its own, not combined with another. They are:

• Even/Odd: During set-up, place a double-sided even/odd card to the left of the lowest starting card, placing it even or odd side face up to match that card. Only cards of the same type can be added to that row, so if the card is on odd, then even cards can be placed only on the other three rows. If you take the row next to this card, move this card next to the row with the lowest number at right, flipping it if needed.

• Mountain Climbing: During set-up, place a double-sided card to the left of the bottommost starting card with the face showing the up arrow in the corner and down arrow on a row of cards. Cards placed in this row go in descending order instead of ascending. If you play 35 and the descending row ends in 40 and an ascending row ends in 28, you'll place your card in the descending row since the difference between 35 & 40 is less than between 35 & 20. If you take this row, slide the double-sided card up to the next row; when it reaches the top row, it then starts moving back down.

• The Jumping Cow: During set-up, place the jumping cow card to the right of the lowest starting card; this card counts as part of this row. When someone places the sixth card in this row, they claim the number cards other than the one they played, then the cow immediately jumps to the end of the row with the lowest number at right; if this row now has six cards, they claim these cards as well!

• Number Spinner: During set-up, each player takes a number spinner card along with ten number cards. Once during the round, they can play the number spinner along with a two-digit number card. During card placement, the digits on this player's card are reversed, e.g. 41 becomes 14, so if 14 is the lowest number, they place their card first. However, once placed, the digits are treated in their original order for all subsequent card placements.

• Beat the Buffalo: This co-operative variant is for 1-6 players, who try to score less collectively than the buffalo. During set-up, deal ten cards face down to the buffalo board; additionally, take 0-16 random action cards depending on the player count, then lay two of them face up. Each turn, all players choose and reveal their cards, then flip the buffalo's top number card. All of the cards are placed in the rows like normal, but at any time before, during, or after this process, players can choose to use one or more action cards, replacing each with a new card immediately. Action cards let you make a row off-limits, place a card inside a row, move an already placed card, and more. (In a game with one or two players, the buffalo must score twice as many points as the players for the players to win.)

Saboteur: 20 Jahre-Edition, a twentieth anniversary edition of Saboteur, includes the base game; the Saboteur 2 expansion; the special cards from the 2016-2023 World Championships; the mini-expansions Tunnel Toll, Wardrobe Cavern, and Tunnel Party; two new mini-expansions: New Goals and Treasure Chests and two new game variants.

A free baseball game that is very detailed and much user content.  Contains physical cards and seasons you can print out. The web game also includes a method to play using the computer.  The one item you do need is Excel.

“Agents without a Clue” is a strategic deduction card game for 3 to 8 players. Players take on the roles of secret Agents trying to find and kill the Ace Agent among them to keep top secret information from getting leaked. The catch is that nobody knows who the Ace Agent is, including the Ace themselves!

The game starts by each player choosing a character to be their secret Agent; the characters that are available are random each game, as there are a total of 17 but each player only gets to choose from two and will discard one. Each character's envelope contains a unique Rank card, a unique Signature Ability card, and a Signature Gun card. The player with the highest Rank will, at the end of the game, be the Ace Agent; but which character has the highest Rank is random each game! Higher ranked Agents will also have more powerful abilities, but are more likely to be the Ace Agent! Any player who is not the Ace Agent will instead be a Rookie. The deck of equipment cards is then drafted amongst all players to give them a starting hand, but do they not play them right away. They must be careful which cards they keep, because they'll have to use them all before the game ends!

With game setup complete, players then take turns, one at a time, playing a single card's effect from their hand. These can be guns for shooting each other or other utility items for defense, deduction, or even adjusting the Rank of other players. Most cards have more than one effect, but you must choose one to play out. Players only have a single hit point, so they must be careful to keep enough defense cards on them! If a player dies from being shot, their turns are skipped unless they are revived. Players will use the cards at their disposal to figure out or influence who the Ace Agent is and act accordingly to achieve their goal. While characters' Signature Ability cards are super powerful, they also give away a player's identity and should be held onto until the right time.

The goal of the game for the Ace Agent is to have either the highest ranking Rookie be dead or more than one Rookie be dead (even if the Ace himself is dead). The Rookies' goal is to work together to kill the Ace Agent without accidentally fulfilling his goal for him. The end of the game occurs after all players pass their turn in a row (from being either dead or out of cards); upon which all character's ranks are publicly revealed to determine who the Ace Agent is and whether the Ace Agent or the Rookies succeeded.

-description from publisher

Zap or Trap is a game of sending secret signals.

Secretly communicate with your partner to ZAP a matching group aliens back home (even though they want to stay on Earth) or warn your opponents’ aliens that it’s a TRAP. Rules are so simple you'll be zappin', trappin', and dying laughin' in no time.

It's an excellent team game for 4 or 6 players. Variant rules will allow groups of 3 or 5 to play as well.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Work together to help all the clownfish swim through the ocean to get to the fish school before Principal Shark arrives and locks them out! The clownfish are ready to make their way to school by crossing the ocean while avoiding sticky seaweed and collecting helpful treasure. But they better be quick! Principal Shark is on is way, and if he gets to the school first, he’ll lock all the doors and make the clownfish late for class.

In Clownfish Cove kids race through the ocean, trying to collect the seaweed and treasures that will help them along the way, while outswimming Principal Shark. Players will move across the board using their numbered cards to collect their seaweed and treasure tokens on their way to the school. Work as a team to move all the clownfish from their home to the fish school.

Clownfish Cove uses a bright and colorful underwater setting that makes swimming to school fun and imaginative. This cooperative game is a perfect introduction to basic counting, strategic thinking, and teamwork. Minimal reading makes this game perfect for kids ages 4 and up!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Armored Genesis Mospeda: Valkyrie vs Regios
description:
Armored Genesis Mospeda: Valkyrie versus Regios is the 2nd edition of Super Dimensional Fortress Macross: Minmay Attack. It includes all the previous material with the addition of Mospeda units.

The game is a doujin (fan-produced) game that was only sold at Japanese game shows. It was not made for the general public. To order, you had to place an order in advance. The game would then be made, and you would pick it up at the show. The game requires assembly.

This game reproduces the fleet battles that were fought in the world of “The Super Dimensional Fortress Macross”. It mainly deals with the First Macross War.  In addition, the ships from the anime show Mospeda have been added to the game, so the players can do some Robotech battles between the Zentraedi and Hunter's expeditionary forces.

The game is a hex-and-counter wargame, typical of many wargame types, with ZOC's and CRT's. It comes with a map, counters that need mounting, player aids, and unit data sheets.

The sequence of play is:

Phase 1: Special Weapon/Special Action Declaration
Phase 2: Initiative Decision
Phase 3: Movement Phase
Phase 4: Stealth/Dimensional Movement
Phase 5: Air Operations
Phase 6: Bombardment/Missile Attack
Phase 7: Special Weapon Judgment/Special Action Execution

There are units for Zentraedi, Maltrandi, UN Spacy, and Mospeda.

The game required assembly of the counters.  Players will also need to find a hex map to play on (although a small A4 map is included that can be photocopied many times to create your map).

—user summary
publishers: Hitachi Mito Game Club (a.k.a. Mr. Nambu and Prince Mito)

description: Players create words using consonants on cards combined with vowels on an electronic die that changes (rolls) on its own. A D6 decides how the consonant will be used and you collect cards when submitting a successful word.

1-2-3-4-5 I Love Coup is a dexterity party card game.
Player will compete to play their card to the play area as fast as they can in order to empty their hand. The rule is easy just play cards in the numerical order 1 > 2 > 3 > 4 > 5. However, some cards will contain symbols that change the play condition that players have to follow. Whoever, play the wrong card or against the rule, has to gather the card pile to their hand as punishment.

1-2-3-4-5 I Love Coup tells the stories about the history of Coup d'état in Thailand. This game makes a satire story about Thai politic that the Thai politic always go in a cycle, starting with 'Campaigning' > 'Election' > 'Set up a Government' > 'Protesting' > 'Coup d'état'

—description from the publisher

For thousands of years, the pyramids of Egypt have kept their secrets, and throughout this time, pyramid robbers have been trying to get to the countless treasures of ancient kings.

Desperate daredevils are not deterred by the curses of the pharaohs, death traps and ruthless guardians of the pyramids - revived mummies. And his brother, a treasure hunter, who met in the labyrinth of the tomb, will not hesitate to put a bullet in his hapless colleague. In short, adventures and dangers, victories and disappointments are waiting for you, friends!

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Mortal Combat was a Childrens game of dexterity whereby each side was trying to eliminate each other's soldiers.

The game came in two larger boxed sets and two mini sets.  The contents of each "set" were:

Larger boxes:
-8 figures
-1 crossbow firing device.
-1 cannon firing device
-1 catapult firing device
-projectiles
-Gates and wall pieces
-a spinner
-one page of rules

Even the box was needed because it was used to measure movement of troops and distances between the combatants.

Each player sets up their forces two box lengths apart.  Both sides setup their castle and position their four soldiers and firing device.

A turn begins with both players spinning the spinner, with the high side shooting first that turn.  A soldier needs to be within 3 centimeters to be able to fire a device.  Both sides take turns shooting at their opponent.  Each soldier that is struck, even by ricochet, is out.  Players can also forfeit shooting and instead advance one of their soldiers, and the firing device next to them, one box length (to get the weapons closer). A cannon or crossbow can only be fired every three shots.

The first player to knock out all their opponent's soldiers wins.  There are additional rules to continue the game as a campaign, which ends when the opposing player has no room left to deploy.

This game would later inspire the games Emperor 1 and 2.

Nomad City: The Founders is a strategy game based on the Arknights universe featuring resource management, area control, and pattern building. You will play as the entrepreneurs who manage and develop Nomadic Cities, titanic mobile platforms that ensure the survival of civilization in this hazardous land called Terra, from the ground up. You will compete with rival entrepreneurs for resources and control of Pioneering Zone 105, gain trust from Columbian Union, and develop a prosperous Nomadic City.

Each player has a total of six main actions to choose from during each turn.

     Deploy: Allows you to place your influence.
     Allocate: Allows you to move your influence.
     Explore: Allows you to discover resources in the wilderness.
     Move City: Allows you to move your Nomadic City to show your influences.
     Build: Allows you to build more structures in your Nomadic City.
     Special Action: Through pattern building, you will unlock stronger special actions during your gameplay.


There are also 15 different characters from Arknights that will grant assistance for the players. The game ends after eight complete rounds with two turns in each round. After a final scoring of resources and area controls, player with the most VP wins the game. The winning entrepreneur will gain jurisdiction over Pioneering Zone 105 and become the true master of this place.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: 7 футов под килем (7 Feet Under Keel)
description:
The sea... Distant countries, exotic ports, calms and storms.
Weary ships, that have crossed the seas and oceans more than once, are heading to their native shores. And how many more dangers and adventures lie ahead! Valuable cargo is waiting in a distant port. We need to hurry! Haul loose the mooring lines! Weigh anchor! Set a course for adventure! The journey takes place on a real map of the world with the designation of seas, ports, and countries. Players act as captains of sea transport ships. Their task is to deliver valuable cargo to its destination...

—description from the back of the box (translated)

A quick card game with simple rules that still challenge you to think through each move. 
Players aim to pair up all the good characters and their animals to ensure no one is left alone. Cleverly connect the pairs, strategically discard them from your hand to be the first with no cards left. But beware of the wicked dog owner who might suddenly appear and thwart your plans!

—description from the publisher

Rychlá karetní hra, která má jednoduchá pravidla, ale přesto vás donutí přemýšlet nad každým tahem. 
Hráči se snaží dostat všechny dobré postavy z filmu a jejich zvířata k sobě, aby nikdo nebyl sám. Chytře propojujte dvojice, odkládejte je z ruky pryč tak, aby vám jako prvnímu nezůstala žádná karta v ruce. A pozor na zlého pána, který se může znenadání objevit a vaše plány zhatit!

—description from the publisher (Czech)

boardgame: 湿地生存法则 (Wetland Survive Rule)
description: The Wetland Survive Rule is an ecological puzzle pure hand-painted science popular science game jointly planned and developed by Shanghai Natural Museum and SAEC. The crisscrossed wetlands nourish countless creatures, and all kinds of creatures perform their respective functions in the wetlands, forming a web of life and maintaining the ecological balance of the wetland. In this game, players can be predated by ecological principles, carry out ecological investigations, and use special skill to increase moisture.Diversity of species to build a more stable and harmonious wetland ecosystem.

The game depicts the struggles of 2nd New Zealand Division to control the area around the Italian village of Orsogna in December 1943 and the stubborn German LXXVI. Panzerkorps defense lead by the 26. Panzer-Division.

Based on the domestic animation Intellectual Property "Incarnation" as the background production of a support 1-5 people card library building game. You will play as a leader of the hunting team, form your own team, and lead the hunters to the dangerous ground, collect supplies, fight with the spinal poison and the eating pole beast, and fight for the survival of the lighthouse!

Вторжение ("Invasion") is a game by Zvezda.  It is a wargame in which two sides are trying to take out each other's HQ unit.  The game is entirely land-based, and there are no aircraft.  However, there is an expansion that can be added for this.

The game includes:

    1 map
    2 sprues, each with:
        2 Tanks
        2 APC
        2 Self Propelled Artillery
        1 Truck
        8 Soldiers
        1 HQ
    
    1 die
    rules


The game is a precursor to their "ATAKA" game.

—user summary

Воздушный Бой ("Air Combat") is a standalone game by Zvezda that deals with modern air combat involving jets and helicopters.  The game can also be used as an expansion for Zvezda's Invasion game.

The game comes with:

     1 map
     6 runways
     3 sheets of air defense counters
     2 sprues, each with:
        8 helicopters
        8 jet fighters, fighter-bombers, tactical bombers, and interceptors
    
     1 die
     rules


This was a precursor to Zvezda's game "Ataka."

—user summary

Players compete to build dams as beaver families. Spin the arrow that shows a log's length. You don't like the result? Spin it again. But there's a chance your beaver will return empty-handed.

Place the log on the construction site. Each piece of wood has its length marked on it, either as a numeral, or a number of knots on the other side. Match the length of the logs with the width of the river using simple maths and counting to 10.

For a greater challenge, combine the river boards to build dams counting up to 20.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gachijo: Four Ninja and the Castle of Treasures
description:
Control the Castle and Slide the Walls! Create New Paths for your Ninja!
An exciting Japanese Castle Exploration game using wits and ninjutsu in a castle that moves!

Embark on an adventure as the Four Ninja, sent on a mission by the warlords.
Collect Treasures, Crystals, and Items in the mysterious moving castle, and complete the mission.
Slide the Maze Walls and create pathways for the Ninja. Use ninja tools and weapons to navigate the dangerous castle. Call upon and control ancient spirits. The Gachijo castle is full of supernatural powers which may help you in your quest.
—description from the publisher
designers: Banana Moon Studio Sapporo (バナナムーン･ステュディオ)
artists: Banana Moon Studio Sapporo (バナナムーン･ステュディオ)

boardgame: Whatever the Cost: The Battle for Corregidor February, 1945
description:
Whatever the Cost is an introductory level mini-game on the final US airborne offensive in the Pacific Theater against the Japanese held island fortress of Corregidor. The Japanese took the US fortress in May 1942, completing their conquest of the Philippine Archipelago. When he was ordered earlier to evacuate Corregidor and take command of Allied forces assembling in Australia, General Douglas MacArthur vowed to someday return. The recapture of Corregidor was seen as both necessary to complete the liberation of the Philippines, and for MacArthur, personal redemption for the earlier US defeat and surrender.

—description from the designer

Trivial Pursuit, tank-themed: This edition was published by the Deutsches Panzermuseum in Munster (German tank museum, Munster). It has 330 qustions to the 6 themes tank technology, anti-tank combat, tanks in pop culture, personalities, WWII tanks, cold war and modern tanks.
The players have to put the periodic element cards from their hand to the table in line.

Welcome to the championship of.... potatoes?!

Train your jumping potatoes and form your team for the annual competition. To do this, you will have to use your cards well and try to get the best ones. Of course, the competition with other trainers will be fierce.

The game is played in two stages:

The first one is an auction to win the most valuable potatos , each of them with one of five different jump styles that will be realized in the second stage of the game. To win an auction players will play a number card (numbers go from 1 to 6) or a combination of cards. The highest value will choose first from the current market of potatoes. After eight rounds of bidding players will end with a team of elite trained or noobs potato-jumpers. Also, be carefull of not spend so many auction cards or you will end with less potatoes than the rest of players.

The second stage is an actual ability contest, where players will try to throw their potatoes and land in the pool... I mean box, according to the jumping style of each one. The potatoes that will land in the box will value points according to their level (from legends, which worth 4 points, to noobs, which only worth 1 point).

Finally, when all the potatoes have jumped to the pool, regardles they miss or land it, players will count how many points have win. The player with the highest puntuation wins.

Will you get your team to win the competition? A fun game of potato throwing and high jumping!

Get ready to fill the streets of the city with color!

You only have one minute to place all your tiles and form your graffiti mural... In each round the score changes, will you get the best combination or will you look for the prize for speed?

In each round players will have to place, the fast as they can, their tiles according to a given shape, but also looking for the combinations of tiles which gives them the most value of points, either by connecting colors or different motives. All of that in less of one minute because the quicker player will also have bonus points.

The game is played several rounds, each of them changing the shape of tile placement and the point value of colors and motives combination. At the end the player with more points accumulated will win.

A game for quick hands and quick minds.

-description from artist

The duel between 2 computer scientists, where your goal is to unplug your opponent's server before he unplugs yours!

First, players split the board in half to separate playfields and secretly plug in 5 cables however they like. Then take turns, playing one of 3 cards they have in hand at the same time. Then they compare whose card is of a higher power. The player with higher power subtracts the power of their opponent's card and then chooses which ports on the grid should be unplugged. The other player doesn't unplug cables but instead uses the ability of the card they played. Then both discard their cards, draw a new card and play continues in the same way until one player doesn't have any more cables.

—description from the publisher

Operating outside the law, they must avoid or bribe the sheriff to make a living.

An expert difficulty variant setup and gameplay card for Rumors of Gold, that adds a Sheriff die, which can affect your dig.

Gorbino's Quest is a strategy game of economic development and civilization building in a fantasy setting for 2-6 players aged 13 and up.

Inspired by Concordia (2013), Mosaic: A Story of Civilization (2022), and Brass: Lancashire (2007); Gorbino's Quest combines the elements of tag collecting, hand management, resource management, income, loans, market, investments, resource production and area control. GQ is an action-selection game. Actions include:

Loan
Lose income, but gain Coins

Production
Produce resources at the province of your choice

Trade
Buy and sell up to two different resource types

Development
Purchase Technology cards that contain tags as well as upgraded actions

Reset
Place all your discarded cards back into your hand and gain income

Build
Build new cities and gain income

Treachery
Steal resources, buy resources at a low cost, or take the actions of other players

Choosing a wise sequence of actions will have the player gain their desired income, actions, resource production, cities and tags.

Each Round
After all players have taken their turns, do the following:

1. Advance Technology Track: Discard the rightmost technology card from the track and shift all remaining cards to the right.
2. Income: Gain or pay coins based on the income tracker.
3. Rotate First Player Marker: Rotate it clockwise; that player starts the next turn.

Scoring
Game ends when no cards remain on the Technology Track

Score calculated by how many 'tags' players have
And by using the scoring section of the board. An example would be pink tags x number of cities.

—description from the designer

Chess-à-Tête is a strategic Card-Based Twist on a Classic Game!
​
Experience chess like never before. Prepare to engage in a battle of wits, strategy, and cunning tactics with Chess-à-tête is a game that combines the timeless appeal of chess with the excitement of card-based gameplay.
 In Chess-à-tête, you'll not only be strategising but also manipulating the game with action cards and unique abilities, ensuring that every match is a thrilling and unpredictable experience.

The aim of the game is to dwindle down your opponents defences, strategise your moves and plan your attacks wisely. Chess-à-tête is completely independent and does not require a chess board. '

—description from the designer

On your turn, throw the color die and take a wooden piece of the same color and put it onto already placed ones constructing a stack. A player looses the game if the stack crashes. If the last piece is added and the stack still didn't crash, all players win.

Campaigns in Europe (CIE) is a two-player series of games focused on campaign scenarios at the division/corps level. The system uses the same maps as War in Europe (WIE), with updated graphics and a more detailed terrain analysis. The counters have been upgraded and are presented with historical identifications. The game system utilizes a Move/Fight, Fight/Move sequence of play to portray simply doctrinal differences among contending armed forces. It also utilizes headquarters units to project logistics and combat support capabilities.

Case Blue 1942 covers the German offensive to the Caucasus and Stalingrad. It ends at the point where the Soviets launched Operation Uranus, their Stalingrad counteroffensive.

Each game turn (GT) represents around one week in clear weather and two weeks in mud and snow game turns. Each hex represents 20 miles (32.5 km) across. German, Finnish, and Western Allied units are shown mostly as division-sized units, with Soviet and Axis Allied units depicted as corps.

Campaigns in Europe (CIE) is a two-player series of games focused on campaign scenarios at the division/corps level. The system uses the same maps as War in Europe (WIE), with updated graphics and a more detailed terrain analysis. The counters have been upgraded and are presented with historical identifications. The game system utilizes a Move/Fight, Fight/Move sequence of play to portray simply doctrinal differences among contending armed forces. It also utilizes headquarters units to project logistics and combat support capabilities.

Italian Campaign covers the Italian campaign, from the Allied invasion of Sicily through the Anzio campaign.

Each game turn (GT) represents around one week in clear weather and two weeks in mud and snow game turns. Each hex represents 20 miles (32.5 km) across. German, Finnish, and Western Allied units are shown mostly as division-sized units, with Soviet and Axis Allied units depicted as corps.

boardgame: D-Day to The Rhine 1944 (Campaigns in Europe Volume 4)
description:
Campaigns in Europe (CIE) is a two-player series of games focused on campaign scenarios at the division/corps level. The system uses the same maps as War in Europe (WIE), with updated graphics and a more detailed terrain analysis. The counters have been upgraded and are presented with historical identifications. The game system utilizes a Move/Fight, Fight/Move sequence of play to portray simply doctrinal differences among contending armed forces. It also utilizes headquarters units to project logistics and combat support capabilities.

D-Day to the Rhine covers the campaign in France, from after the Allied landings at Normandy through the drive to the Rhine. It ends at the completion of Operation Market-Garden.

Each game turn (GT) represents around one week in clear weather and two weeks in mud and snow game turns. Each hex represents 20 miles (32.5 km) across. German, Finnish, and Western Allied units are shown mostly as division-sized units, with Soviet and Axis Allied units depicted as corps.

Introduction:Vastuz (from Proto-Germanic: Wahstuz, meaning "size, growth") is a diversification game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of a initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. It can be played on a 7–sized board for long games or a 5–sized board for short games. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point. You can start a new group or grow one of your preexisting groups by adding a stone, as long as you don't merge groups. If you cannot place a stone, pass your turn, otherwise passing is not allowed.
     Steal a stone from each adjacent enemy group to your group, as long as the groups have the same shape (even if they are rotated or reflected). You can only perform this action if the newly placed stone is adjacent to a friendly stone. The stolen stones are placed adjacent to your group simultaneously, allowing for further growth.


After growing your group, you must continue stealing stones from adjacent enemy groups that have the same shape. Additionally, if you steal a stone from an enemy group that acquires the same shape as one of your adjacent groups, that group will also steal a stone in response and place it next to it to continue growing. These processes continue as long as your groups can keep growing. If your groups can no longer grow, you can no longer steal more stones.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stones. The player with the highest score wins. At the end of the game, each player receives one point for each group of their color of different sizes, plus an additional half point for the player with the largest group. In case of a tie in size, the next largest groups are considered.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

				
				
					Notes:Vastuz is based on Nick Bentley's game Bug, which involves capturing enemy groups with identical shapes. However, Vastuz implements it differently. Instead of capturing the entire enemy group, the player chooses a specific stone from the enemy group with the same shape and adds it to their own group. Additionally, unlike Bug, Vastuz does not impose limits on group growth.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legends of Expedition: Lost Rubies of the Amazon
description:
The day has come. We embark on our journey to find the Lost Rubies of the Amazon. We will meet the perils and challenges that lie ahead with courage. Our story will be shared with the world. This will be our Legend of Expedition.

Live the adventure of an Expedition crew in a fast, dice rolling, solo game. Traverse the unknown in search for legendary locations and treasures. Roll dice to complete challenges and dangers along the way. Manage your supplies in order to survive the journey.

—description from the designer

Embark on a celestial journey with 13 Moons, a beautiful card-matching game that blends strategy with the beauty of lunar phases.

This cozy card game encourages you to connect with the ever-changing face of the moon.

In 13 Moons, players collect points by arranging sets of moon phases and special lunar events. The game's mechanics ensure a dynamic and ever-evolving experience.

What sets 13 Moons apart is its unique tie to reality – the game rewards players for aligning their strategies with the actual moon's position on the day of play. Extra points are bestowed upon those who synchronize their in-game moves with the real-life lunar calendar, adding an extra layer of connections with one of our most beloved celestial bodies: The Moon!

Whoever reaches 354 points first wins. Why 354? Because that's the number of days in a lunar year!

—description from the designer

Long Dark Sea is an exploration and adventure card game, where deck-building and resource management are its main mechanics.

Play solo or cooperatively with up to 4 players through an interactive board designed from a modular system that allows customization for each game.

Set in a bespoke science fiction universe, embark on adventures in the quest for ancient relics of great power through a campaign mode that divides each scenario into three distinct acts.

The use of the innovative system allows you to explore, battle, and obtain resources during raid cycles to later enhance your character during supply cycles.

Will you survive until the third act, face the final Nemesis, and escape with your life while claiming the loot?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Commanders at War: Stalingrad – Cauldron of Death
description:
Commanders at War: Stalingrad - Cauldron of Death is an operational-level wargame in the “Commanders at War” solitaire book wargame series.
The player controls the German 6th Army during Case Blue, the 1942 Eastern Front offensive attempting to reach Russia’s coveted oil-rich region. Standing in the way is the industrial city on the Volga River - Stalingrad. The stage is set for one of the most important and brutal battles in history.
Players get a chance to recreate the titanic clash between Germany and the Soviet Union in a dynamic solitaire wargame with a challenging enemy bot opponent. Only two D6 dice are required with minimal rolling; one roll resolves attack, defense, and potential events. Simple rules allow players to concentrate on gameplay while enjoying a deep and engaging solitaire operational wargaming experience.
Never in the history of warfare has there been so many great commanders fighting in a global war. Masters of warfare, strategy and tactics.
The Commanders at War series gives players a streamlined and quick-playing solitaire wargame to recreate and rewrite history from the perspective of operational-level tacticians during the most important battles of WWII.

Features:
Game Features
Unit Scale – Division
Map – Hexagonal. One hex = 10 km.
Turns – Abstracted-Weekly
Playing Time – 60-90 Minutes
Players – One (Solitaire Play)
Complexity - Medium
Dynamic enemy Bot system.
The player sets up units freely west of the Don.
Command Orders allow for the use of limited specific actions at any time.
Comprehensive Play Aid with Quick References.
Easy to follow Bot Play Aid.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
BB Blast is a dynamic, print and play board game that transports players into the heart of intense airsoft battles. With 1 v 1 or 2 v 2 fast-paced matches, players choose their class—Light, Medium, or Heavy—dictating their movement speed and points for weapons and items. Dive into the action as you strategically equip yourself with airsoft weapons, including Pistols, Shotguns, Submachine Guns, Snipers, and Machine Guns, along with tactical items like Rubber Throwing Knives, Grenades, Trip Wires, and Riot Shields.

This game really stands out with its engaging and accessible gameplay suitable for all ages. The game is designed to keep players thinking critically, offering a quick 5-15 minute average playing time, allowing you to jump right into the action whenever the gaming mood strikes. What sets BB Blast apart is the fusion of strategy, customization, and the thrill of airsoft battles, delivering a unique board game experience.

—description from the designer

This version of Vinyl adds some new mechanics to the game. We have opened up the Trade Counter. You will now be able to trade unwanted or low-value records in to gain attributes that you may use in later turns.

Collection rewards have been reduced to max out 4. The lower end will now be 2 albums in a small collection and this will increase the number of records that a player may retire during the game. In addition, the Extended Play format and the Long Play format have been added as attributes and these albums can provide a bonus to your collections for end game scoring. There is still more, however.

We have also added 12 new Loyalty Cards which will help to bolster your end game scoring even further. Taking all of this into account that is over 150 new components added to the game!

We will also introduce gameplay with 5 Genres in play instead of 4. This will give players a different style of game in this expansion. There are 6 Genres added so that players can choose which 5 Genres they would like to play with.

—description from the publisher

The Masters of Metal version of Vinyl builds on the base game of Vinyl and introduces the basic collections and memorabilia. Historically, memorabilia (such as t-shirts and posters) was purchased for a favorite band. The memorabilia added value to the collector's collection. In MoM, players can acquire memorabilia and attach it to a collection. The memorabilia enhances the collections value (or length). In order to attach memorabilia, like icons (decade, live, picture disc) needs to be arranged so that the icons are adjacent between albums. The more albums and memorabilia the longer the collection. Unlike the base game, the basic collections only require the player to match bands and rewards players for long dedicated collections. MoM is compatible with Top Shelf, Publisher Expansion, and the Clearance Bin.

Vinyl: British Invasion builds on the original game of Vinyl and introduces the Listening Booth and singles (aka 45s). Historically, 45s were purchased to initially and eventually encouraged buyers to acquire the associated LP album or even another album from the band. In BI, players can acquire 45s by matching the band icon. Each 45 has an "A-side" with locked attributes that can be unlocked at the Listening Booth. At the Listening Booth, players will discard a magazine card with one matching icon to flip the 45 to its "B-side" giving the player more points and the opportunity to use the 45 to acquire LPs with similar attributes in future rounds.

Memory-style game to build Latin-German swearwords out of pairs. Just for the fun of learning or revisiting Latin words.

The game plays like memory (unlike memory you don't keep matching pairs - put them back on the table, again). One word is the first half of the full word, one the second (1:1 match). To avoid unclarities, the matching parts have the same color - wrong combinations result in funny new creations, adding to the fun. The cards contain also an explanation what the resulting words actually mean (for those that did not know the word before).

The King of Symrania is dead and his successor must be chosen! As a member of one of the most reputable noble families of the kingdom, your mission is now to get workers and resources from the guilds that control each of the cities to expand your influency.  Form an army of mercenaries, control the markets, use magic or fill your gold reserves and obtain resources. Only one of the candidates to the throne will win the crown!

In Guilds of Symrania the objective is to be the player with the most victory points when at least one of the two players has exceeded 7 points at the end of a round. Each player will have a board with a map of the kingdom with twelve cities, each associated with a numerical value between 1 and 12. In each turn, the active player will roll two dice and all players can receive benefits from the cities. Each player can decide whether to add the two dice together (obtains resources) or use them separately (obtains workers).

Guilds of Symrania is a quick-to-learn, quick-to-play roll and write game using the core " roll a dice, everyone get stuff" mechanism. It’s also a strategic engine builder using a player sheet with all the workers and resources that you can get an add on it.  How the cities are placed at the beginning of the game, the resources you get and the order you do have interesting implications in development of game beside the strategy you take: quick and explosive gains, low luck and steady income or big end-game combos to launch from last to first or a mix-and-match approach.

Guilds of Symrania is “an easy to play but difficult to domain” game that you can enjoy from the first game in solitaire mode or multiplayer.

—description from the designer

Soy Battle is a simple and fast-playing party game for 2 to 4 players with intuitive, dynamic and engaging game mechanics. The goal is to have a sushi all-you-can-eat experience using your chopsticks, and whoever eats the most wins!

Six randomly selected numbered pieces of sushi are placed on the game table. The roll of the dice determines which piece is to be picked up. As you see the result of the roll a challenge of skill and speed between the players begins to pick up the correct piece using chopsticks.

Hands forbidden at the table!

Once a player firmly holds the piece of sushi between his/hers/their chopsticks he/she/they can place it on the dunking saucer in front of him/her/them. The sushi piece is not safe yet, it will only be safe once it is placed on the little boat. Thet's because a possible outcome of the dice will see diners competing for the sushi present on the saucers. The sushi will be safe after winning a second piece, thus moving the previous sushi from the saucer onto the small boat.

Every time a piece of sushi is replaced in the center, and dice are rolled until all the pieces are picked up. The winner is the player who, by adding up the numbers on the conquered sushi pieces, gets the highest score.

—description from the publisher

In 2004, the American rover Opportunity landed on Mars. The mission to search for signs of life on the planet was expected to last 3 months. Instead, because of luck and mission ingenuity, Opportunity continued for more than 14 years. She and her twin rover Spirit helped discover some of the first clear signs of ancient water on Mars and reinvigorated exploration of the red planet.

Opportunity is a fast-playing solo strategy game that gives you the chance to control the mission yourself. How successful will you be?

You mission: Move point-to-point across Mars. Make as many Discoveries as you can. Keep going for as long as you can. Score as high as you can.

You'll need: One deck of poker cards and two different colored six-sided (d6) dice (one for rolling, the other as a game piece).

—description from the designer
Six additional tiles. Use randomly two of them and place them below the outer temples. When a player completes a column with a stupa tile, he gets the tile's reward.

boardgame: Fun Fact Trivia Deck: Party Mix
description:
The Fun Fact Trivia Deck (Party Mix Edition) is a  wildly entertaining twist on trivia. Tailored for curious minds aged 8 and up, this game packs a punch with 100 cards of mind-bending questions, ranging from death-defying facts to cosmic curiosities. With a mix of multiple choice and true/false, it's a laugh-out-loud learning experience that's perfect for any gathering, designed to challenge, educate, and amuse in equal measure. Whether it's family game night or a party with pals, this deck promises a hilariously nerdy good time.

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Dendria (from the Greek: Dendron, meaning "tree", combined with the suffix -ria used in many geographical names) is a territorial game for two players: Light Brown and Dark Brown. It is played on the hexes (plots) of a hexagonal or modular board, initially empty, the latter composed of modules of 7 plots (one central and six around) that must connect to each other by at least two adjacent plots. The recommended size is 6 plots per side or 13 modules, but boards of 5 or 7 plots per side, or 7 to 19 modules, are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of pieces of their own color, divided into three categories: seeds, trees, and sprites. In the physical version, double-sided discs are used (front = seed, back = tree) and pawns to represent the sprites.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected pieces of the same color and category. A single piece is also a group.
     A group of seeds is a seedbed.
     A group of trees is a forest.
     A group of sprites is a host.
     A piece sees a plot when it is connected to it along an infinite straight line with no intervening pieces.
     A hungry seed is a seed that sees the edge or corner through empty plots or modular lagoons; otherwise, it is a nourished seed.
     A protected forest is a forest where all of its adjacent empty plots are seen by friendly sprites; if it has no adjacent empty plots, it is protected if it sees more friendly sprites than enemy sprites.
     The power of a sprite is the distance, measured in empty or occupied plots, that separates it from the nearest edge or corner of the board.
     The leafiness of a forest is the number of trees it contains according to the triangular score, which is calculated by summing all natural numbers from 1 up to the size of the forest.


				
				
					Turns:Dark plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Sow a hungry seed of your color by placing it on an empty plot adjacent to no more than three non-touching friendly seeds, sprites, or trees.


Then, optionally, you may convert one or more friendly or enemy seeds into friendly sprites only if they are nourished and see more friendly pieces than enemy ones.

     Move a sprite of your color in a straight line through empty plots, so that its power decreases, although it can increase if you perform a conversion that increases the number of friendly trees; being able to jump over seeds of any color or over friendly sprites, and land on an empty plot or on an enemy sprite to capture it by replacement. You can also jump over lagoons on a modular board, but you cannot land on them.


Then, optionally, you may convert one or more friendly or enemy seedbeds into friendly forests only if all of their adjacent empty plot are seen by friendly sprites.

     Pass your turn.


During its movement, the sprite may perform conversions both from its initial plot and from each plot it passes through. In any of them, it may replace seeds with sprites or forests, or convert unprotected enemy forests into friendly ones.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass on consecutive turns. At the end of the game, points are counted by summing the leafiness of the friendly forests, plus an additional half point for the player with the largest forest, or in case of equality, the next largest forests are compared, and so on, until a pair with different values is found.

The player with the highest final score wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player chooses a whole number of points that is added to Light's score at the end of the game as compensation for playing second, and then the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the komi pie rule.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The City of Kings: Scenario Pack 1
description:
Scenario Pack 1 is an expansion for The City of Kings that adds 14 new scenarios, 1 extended scenario, and 1 special story to The City of Kings. It was first made available as a free PDF.

Discover more about the world and overcome new challenges set out by scenario specific rule cards.

The extended scenario allows you to explore the entirety of the Ageless Realms and Azure Rise through an epic 6 - 12 hour scenario.

For the ultimate adventure the special story allows you to create your own adventure as you fight old enemies and prepare for a battle like no other. But be warned, an epic adventure can easily take 12 hours or more to complete.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The City of Kings: Earthshaker Costume Promo
description:
A promotional card originally released during The City of Games festival in 2023.

This promo introduces a new equipment card to the game and is part of the Promo Card: Costume Pack which includes all four Costume promotional cards.

Equipping this item will increase both attack and health, making it a very powerful item.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Reif für die Insel: The Outlaws
description:
A promo item for Reif für die Insel that adds 10 outlaw monkey tokens to your bag. Outlaw tokens are numbered 1-10.

Play Reif für die Insel as described in the rulebook, but with the following changes:
Set up

Draw 10 banana tiles from the bag and lay them face up on the table as the outlaws’ hoard. Only include good bananas – black bananas, storm clouds, and parrots should be replaced and returned to the bag.
Add the 10 numbered outlaws to the bag.

Play
Any outlaws the starting player draws from the bag
at the start of a skirmish go beneath the next free space in the banana row, just like parrots. They form a group with the next banana to be drawn. Multiple outlaws and parrots can be in one group.
Also like parrots, place the outlaws you win next to your island.

The Feasting Phase
At the very start of the feasting phase, before even the greedy parrots strike, the outlaws make their move. All players can trade their outlaws for bananas from the outlaws’ hoard. The highest-numbered outlaw goes first, followed by the rest in descending order.
When it‘s your outlaw‘s turn, choose a banana from the outlaws’ hoard and place it next to your island. Then return your outlaw to the box. Alternatively, you can choose not to take a banana and instead save this for the next season.
After resolving all the outlaws this way, the feasting phase resumes as usual. Bananas you grabbed from the hoard can be stolen by parrots or added to your points pile if ripe enough.
If there are any bananas next to your island at the end of the feasting phase, place them on free spaces on your island. If you don’t have enough space, return bananas you don‘t want to the box – either from your board or beside it – until you‘re down to 5 in total.

Embark on the treacherous journey to complete the colonization of Mars, getting it ready for the billionaire tourists who will soon be arriving. Without food or water and only a massive stash of alcohol to stay alive, you must navigate these completely sauced astronauts as they each race to conquer their mission before launching back to Earth.

Players are each dealt 3 task cards and 5 cards from the deck. Each player must acquire the necessary resource cards to successfully complete their (sometimes mindless) tasks. The first player to complete all their tasks wins! Beware of unruly obstacles like drawing a Passed Out card, keeping you from completing your mission. But fear not worthy space traveler, the Rocket Rope Ride card or a lucky roll of the dice will definitely help sober up your drunken astronaut.

And nobody likes to drink alone, so watch out for sneaky fellow astronauts getting you drunk and swiping your resources to complete their tasks before you! Drunk cards can be an advantage to you when played against another opponent. Playing matching Drunk cards affords players the opportunity to swipe a resource card or a card from their opponent's hand.

Additionally, players can experience other challenges along the way with skip, double skip, and shuffle the deck cards.

*Please note: Drinking is not required or part of the gameplay. However, due to the nature of the game we put the age requirement as 21+ yrs.

—description from the designer

Manic Minute is an exciting NEW party game that's hilariously fun for the whole family! Arguments are mandatory.

How to play: Players each receive a pen and paper. The first player turns over a category, players must write down as many answers as they can in one Manic Minute. When the timer finishes, each player stops writing and one by one reads out their answers to the group. Answers that match another player get crossed off and are worth zero points - so consider blocking your opponents by guessing what they might be writing down too! Players must defend their answers and arguing is the point of the game! Do you think Victoria sponge counts as a dessert? Do Amy and Aimee count as the same name? Its all up to you in Manic Minute!

At the end of the round each player totals up their points and the person with the most points wins the round! Another player turns over a card and the next Manic Minute can begin!

Manic Minute is a game for all the family and can consist of as many or as few rounds as you like, the game can also be slightly modified for younger or less able players; such as giving points for every correct answer or working in teams instead of as individuals.

Black Spirits: Combat Cards is a card supplement for board games based on extremely popular dark fantasy action RPG video games. It replaces those board games' dice components to enable tactical combat that forces players to make difficult trade-offs, leaning in on the delicate balance of aggression and preservation in a way that is faithful to the ethos of the video games they are based upon. The set is backwards compatible with all aspects of the board games it's suggested for and truly drop-in; no house rules, no component errata, no additional tweaks necessary.

Lean in on the clever strategies that those beloved action RPG games demand.
