The engine this set includes lets players make careful choices about when to extend their offensive strategy, when to take a conservative approach, and choose which critical moments they're willing to leave in the hands of fate.

Use the enemy's offense to your advantage! Among the cards that allow players to customize their approach toward battle, a brand new parry/riposte mechanic that rewards careful planning and positioning is featured. The set also features a collection of cards that are exclusive to player classes, highlighting a unique playstyle for each class. Using a class card in your deck rewards you for drawing on that class's strengths, and colors gameplay in a way that truly sets each class apart. Finally, Black Spirits: Combat Cards includes several difficulty modes to cater to both brand new players, as well as veterans. Each player can separately choose what difficulty to align to, and players within the same party can even choose not to use cards at all if they prefer chucking dice.

So all players need to do is create their decks, plan carefully, and steel themselves for death.

—description from the designer

The Gamefound edition comes all prepackaged in the big box. It has a multilayer insert. The Coregame's 5 chapters and all Gamefound exclusive Stretch goals all fit in comfortably:

1. 'The Brothers' (2 cards and standees)
2. Plastic Damage markers
3. 2x 6AI cards, 1x map hex
4. 'Mnemonaught' (card and standee), 6x AI cards, 
Last Journey scenario sheet
5. Master of Puppets (card and standee), Augment deck (x11 breakers with unique illustration) 
6. Plastic experience markers
7. The Cat (card and standee), 4x side quest cards, 8x cybercat augment cards
8. The Depths of Ascend (2x cards and standees), depths of ascend scenarios sheet, 8x mission cards
9. Unknown Zone:  2x 6AI cards, 1x map hex
10. Lost knight Scenario sheet, Inugami (card and standee), 7x adventure cards, 3x companion cards
11. 4x Pathfinder packs (scenario generator)= 50x cards
12. Art bags and 2x map hexes
13. Cybershark (card and Standee)
14. Yokai Project: Hashiman and Nur-Ona (2x card and standee), Yokai scenario sheet 
15. Living City Expansion = 56x cards, 11x Augmentation cards, 10x Living city tokens

boardgame: Fistful of Lead: Galactic Heroes – Codex Galactica: Grimdark Edition
description:
The Galaxy is locked in an endless war. The Empire, guided by their undying Emperor faces enemies on all sides and even those from within.

Herein lies a list of Mankind’s forces, its enemies and catalog of known psychic powers.

Warning: Possession of this text is heretical and punishable by the Inquisition.

Do you like the Grim Dark genre set in the distant future, where a stagnant human civilization is beset by hostile aliens and supernatural creatures?  Do you crave a universe populated with a mixture of humans, aliens, and supernatural monsters, wielding futuristic weaponry and supernatural powers?

boardgame: Oathsworn: Into the Deepwood – Dennis the Geek Ally
description:
The Promo Card shows Dennis of the YouTube channel staygeeky

Dennis The Geek

Might: 1 red dice
Defense: 4

Ability: Stay Geeky - Whenever you critial, one other character may Battleflow one card once.

Generals is an addition to the strategy board game Relic that opens up new possibilities for players. The add-on uses a modular principle - new mechanics can be added to the base game independently of each other.


     Control a new class of troops - generals, whose strength depends on the achievements of the faction.
     Forge alliances and declare wars using diplomacy cards, benefiting from faction relationships.
     Make decisions based on event cards that change the course of the game.
     Choose the path to achieve victory, the size of the empire still plays an important role, but is not decisive.

This is a baseball boardgame from Hobby Japan.  It features cards from the teams in Japan.

Information about the team players is on each card, and these are used to determine the results of play.  Careful organization of the team players is required to achieve the best results.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to the world of Botanical Bliss, a captivating print & play, roll & write game where you can unleash your inner gardener and create a stunning oasis.

Dive into the art of flower growing and strategically plan your dream garden, filled with vibrant flowers and charming decorative elements.

Featuring relaxed gameplay and endless creative possibilities, Botanical Bliss is enjoyable for everyone from board game lovers to nature enthusiasts!

Botanical Bliss is a roll and write puzzle game sent to all backers in a print & play, digital format. All you need to provide is the writing tools and dice! The main objective of the game is unlocking these pesky eye pleasing patterns!

Featuring randomness from dice rolls, strategy in your choices and placement, and passive competitiveness between players. 
Take turns rolling for your flower specifications, planting them in your garden, and reaping the rewards of your careful planning and coordination.

On your turn, roll 3 dice and place them on your sheet in their correct order and position. Decide whether to stick with the
rolls, or use a dice mod. Then fill in flowers in your garden based on the conditions you rolled, trying your best
to complete patterns.

If a pattern is achieved, roll for decor and decide where to place it in any qualifying area in your garden.

Repeat until day 20, or until a player fills their entire garden plot!

A variant of memory. There are 31 pairs of square tiles, each with a picture of a Russian geographic denomination (mostly cities). One card is subtitled in Russian (using the Cyrillic alphabet), the corresponding tile with the German translation (not: transcription). In addition, there is one pair of tiles with the logo of the publisher and another one with "Russisch kommt!" (The Russian language is coming).
The educational aim of the game is to learn the Russian alphabet.

In the epic saga of "Ballad of Fire," an expansion of Firesiege, players confront raging flames engulfing the city of Medda. As if the inferno weren't challenging enough, the fearsome Zagal, deploys grotesque creatures to swarm the citadel in an attempt to conquer it, the Dharn. The fate of the city lies squarely in the hands of the players as they resist the siege and reclaim control over the menacing sand creatures.

This game version introduces several enhancements to the original core set:
Joining the legion of Valkun is a new troop – the Valkun archers. 
With formidable prowess from a distance, they lack the strength to withstand formidable foes like the queens but make up for it with their deadly accuracy in dispatching enemies from afar.

Additional command tiles, tokens, including flames and Dharn tokens, bombs, and a pivotal new element – a new Hero!

Meet Nakra, the mysterious mercenary. Despite not being a Valkun, Nakra's enigmatic origins only add to his allure. Rescued by Norad, he defies the typical mercenary code by fighting not out of obligation but gratitude, aligning his efforts with the player's commands in the resistance against the Skreel horde within the battle-scarred streets of Medda.

-description from publisher

boardgame: インチキはナシ (Inchiki ha Nashi)
description:
Card game with regional brand “Ichikawa No nashi” trademark

"Inchiki ha Nashi", a local card game from Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture, is a psychological warfare game in which the outcome is determined by bargaining between a pear and an ant.
Just like in the card game "Doubt", the first person to spot the fraud and lose all the cards in their hand wins.
Can be enjoyed by elementary school students.​​

Number of players: 3 or more
Playing time: 15 minutes
Recommended age: 8 years and older

[Set contents]
Instruction cards: 12
Number cards: 35
Judgment card: 1

[Game preparation]
Shuffle all number cards face down and deal 4 to each player.
Make the remaining number cards face down into a single deck and place them in the center of the table.
Shuffle all instruction cards face down and put them into a single deck next to the number cards.
Place the judgment card next to the number cards.
Decide the starting player in any way and start playing!

[Game flow]
Flip over a card from the instruction card deck and announce the instructions.
The instruction card states the number of pears that can be played, so players must play the same number of number cards face down.
At this time, you can play multiple cards if they are the same as the number indicated.

Example) If the number indicated is "2"
1 Play one 2
2 Play two 2s
3 Play three 2s
etc.

If you don't have a card to play, you can choose
1 Pass and draw one card from the number card deck
2 Cheat and play a number card

・The way to cheat is as follows
Example) If the number indicated is "1"
1 Play one number card other than 1
2 Play a combination of number card 1 (or more than one) and one card other than 1(including ants)

※1 and 2, 1, 1 and 3, 1, 1, 1 and ants, etc.
※Playing more than two cards other than the number card indicated is prohibited
※Ants can only be played by cheating, so play them without being found out

After playing a number card, it is the role of
the next player to point out whether the player is playing it honestly or cheating.
If you think you are not cheating, put the "No Cheating" judgment card on top, if you think you are cheating, put the "Cheating" judgment card on top.

After that, the player turns over the number card that was face down to show whether they played it honestly or cheated.

・If you played it honestly but someone points out that you cheated, the player who pointed it out must draw two number cards
from the deck as a penalty.
・On the other hand, if the cheating is discovered, the cheating player must put back the number card they played and draw
two cards from the deck as a penalty.
・If you cheated by playing an ant card and someone points out that you are not cheating, the player who pointed it out must
draw two number cards from the deck as a penalty.
・If you cheated using only number cards,there is no penalty for the player who pointed out that you are not cheating and overlooked the cheating.

After this exchange is over, the next player in the clockwise direction draws an instruction card in the same way and follows the instructions.

[End of the game]
・The first person to run out of number cards wins.
・However, cheating when trying to win is prohibited.
・If you have one or more number cards left and they all have the same number, if they are the same number as the instruction card, you can say "win" and play your number card to become the winner.
・If they are different from the instruction card, you draw a card from the deck and the game continues.

・Once a winner is decided, the total
number of number cards held by the losers becomes the score.

Example) A, B, and C are playing, and if B becomes the winner, A and C will have negative points equal to the number of
number cards they have, and B will have positive points equal to the total number of cards remaining for A and C.

・Write down the scores, play twice, and the person with the most total points will be the winner.
designers: Tsukue to Isu (つくえといす)
artists: Keiko Sasahara (ささはら けいこ)

description:
Introduction:Fiodar (from Old Irish: Fhíodair, meaning "weaver") is a territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. It can be played on a 7–sized board for long games or a 5–sized board for short games. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A thread is a set of connected stones of the same color in a straight line. A single stone is also a thread.
     A weave is a set of connected threads of the same color. A single thread is also a weave.
     A cut is a configuration in which two friendly stones are on both sides of a single thread of a weave, crossing it with an imaginary straight line along the line of the thread.
     A cut weave is a weave where each stone it contains is part of at least one cut thread.
     A territory is one or more connected empty points. You own a territory if all the stones connected to it are of your color.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point, then remove all cut enemy weaves from the board. At the end of your turn, there must be no cut friendly weaves left, unless enemy weaves left cut. After this action, the board position must be different from the board position at the end of your previous turn. If it is the same as the board position at the end of any of your turns other than the previous one, the game ends in a draw.
     Pass your turn.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turn consecutively. At the end of the game, points are counted by adding friendly territory and captured enemy stones, plus awarding half an extra point to the player who passed his turn first.

To make the game fair, before the game starts, the first player places a black stone on an empty point and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

–description from designer

You wake up to the sound of a clock striking midnight. 
Its dark.
Your head aches.
All you remember from the night before is dancing in a nightclub with a beautiful tall dark haired stranger. 
You lean over and put on the lamp, look around and find yourself in a small locked room.
The room only has a single bed, side table with a lamp, wall clock, backpack and notebook and pen lying on the floor besides the bed. 
The only way out of the room is through the door which has a padlock on it. 
You need to find a key.

This is a pick up and play, solo, horror, choose your own adventure card game. The rules are explained as you play the game. In order to win the game, you need to escape from the cabin before time runs out at dawn. Time is tracked by throwing a dice and moving down a time track on a card, before reading each card. You will either win by escaping from the cabin or lose when time runs out. Your decisions will either have a good outcome or a bad outcome which could suddenly end your game.

-description from designer

Introduction:Haugaz (from Proto-Germanic: Haugaz, which means "height, hill") is a board game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of a square board with diagonal lines marked, with the board initially empty. The recommended board size is 6 points per side. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a set of pieces stacked on top of each other. A single piece is also a stack.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, you can either pass your turn or perform the following actions in the following order:


     Place a stack of height 1 on an empty point (called a 1-stack).
     Move a stack to an empty point in a straight line, either orthogonally or diagonally, at a distance equal to its height. You cannot move a stack that was just placed on that same turn.
     Increase the height by 1 of all friendly and enemy stacks that are at the same distance as the height of the stack you just moved, by placing an additional piece of the opposite color on these stacks.


You can pass your turn before or after performing a placement.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when the players pass in consecutive turns. In case of a tie between the highest stacks, the next highest stacks are compared.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stack of height 1 and a white stack of height 1 on any empty point, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-stack pie rule.

–description from designer

boardgame: Warhammer Quest: Cursed City – The Last Days of Haven
description:
Welcome to the Last Days of Haven, a "what-if" scenario in which we explore an alternative timeline of shorts, where the heroes of Ulfenkarn ultimately fail their task and Radukar‘s plans of the city come to fruition. In this, the heroes cannot cast Radukar down and find themselves with no escape from the city. Worse still, an angry vampire lord bears down on them, with all the wrath and fury of his dead legions. As a seemingly endless horde of monsters closes in on Haven, the heroes prepare to make their final stand.

In this article, we provide a set of rules for the final defence of Haven. heroes will start at level 1, with no equipment, and progress through 20 waves of enemies. If they somehow survive that, they will face a final fight with Radukar himself. Indeed, the last living beings in the ciry are the heroes, left for Radukar to personally finish him - though whether this is a grand gesture to a defeated foe, or a petty vengeance is yet to be determined and is of little consequence to his victims. Can you survive until the end, or will you fall by the hand of the Wolf King?

"The Last Days of Haven" was published in White Dwarf 494 in November 2023.

Association Matching Card Game "Omompakaru"
You can discover new charms of the people you play with.

A cooperative communication game where everyone comes up with the same answer when they associate a given topic.

Is your choice really the only choice for everyone else? What is the gender of the players in the room? What is their generation? What is their occupation? etc...
Use your power of inference "Omopakaru" and the hint cards to find the only answer.

Number of players: 3-6
Playing time: 15 minutes
Recommended age: 7 years and older

[Set contents]
Topic cards: 12
Reserve cards: 2
Hint cards: 30
Life cards: 6

[Game preparation]
Shuffle all the hint cards face down and deal 4 to each player.
Make the remaining hint cards face down into one pile and place them in the center of the field.
Make all the topic cards face down into one pile and place them next to the hint card pile.
Prepare life cards for each player, starting from 1, and arrange them face up from the top in descending order of number to make a deck. (The remaining life cards will not be used in the game.)

Decide the starting player in any way you like and start playing.

[Game flow]
Flip over one card from the deck of topic cards and announce the topic. Players take turns clockwise, starting from the starting player.

《Turn flow》
During your turn, you can choose to either "1. Give a hint" or "2. Do an association challenge." If you have no cards in your hand, you will do an association challenge.

1. Give a hint
-Choose a hint card from your hand and give a hint by answering the question.
-Place the hint cards you have used face up in the center of the field so that everyone can see them.
-Draw one hint card from the hint deck and add it to your hand. (If you have no deck, do not draw one.)

2. Do an association challenge
-Everyone will announce their associated words using the hints they have given so far. When taking on a challenge, everyone says the associated word at the same time.
◯ Challenge successful → If everyone's associated words match, the tasks are laid out face up. (So you can see how many were successful)
× Challenge unsuccessful → If even one person's associated word does not match, the association is unsuccessful. The task card is discarded.
The game continues with the turn of the person who took the challenge by turning over a new task card.

[Game End]
・Game clear → Successfully complete 5 tasks.
・Game over → All life cards are gone.

Even after clearing the game, you can continue playing and see how many tasks you can complete successfully.

Paper Nomads is a nomadic adventure game.

Use your shepherds to collect sheep, goats, and camels to add to your herds and protect them. Trade with other players or raid their vulnerable herds to build your own as you lead your Bedouin tribe and trade your herds to achieve five improvements to your camp. The first player to complete all five improvements to their camp wins the game.

—description from the designer

Saving Mankind is an engaging and fully cooperative board game that requires all players to collaborate and unite their efforts in a race against time to rescue humanity from the brink of extinction.

Saving Mankind aims to educate and entertain players while promoting awareness of resource conservation and the fight against global warming. It encourages players to take action for a more sustainable future, making it suitable for players of all ages, from pupils to families and friends. Each round offers an engaging and enlightening gaming experience.

Players must strategically select their role cards from a diverse set, including Scientist, Programmer, Movie Star, Politician, and more. Each role provides distinct initial resources, prompting players to make deliberate decisions and contribute to the shared objective.

The board of this space-themed board game is a mesmerizing blend of modern artistry and realistic cosmic imagery, making every move feel like a captivating journey through the cosmos.

—description from the designer

Would you like to try yourself as an entrepreneur? Are you interested in what it's like to be the first person in a company? With the Cégépítok board game, you can put yourself in the shoes of the hottest entrepreneurs for a few hours! Are you able to make good decisions to make yours the most successful company? Plan smartly, use your resources judiciously and use tactics boldly!

Cégépítok is a board game based on simple game rules, but with many twists and surprises, in which you can become a full-fledged entrepreneur step by step.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Kipróbálnád magad vállalkozóként? Érdekel, milyen lehet egy cég első emberének lenni? A Cégépítők társasjátékkal néhány órára a legmenőbb vállalkozók bőrébe bújhatsz! Képes vagy jó döntéseket hozni, hogy a tiéd legyen a legsikeresebb cég? Tervezz okosan, használd megfontoltan az erőforrásaidat és taktikázz bátran!

A Cégépítők egyszerű játékszabályra épülő, mégis számos csavart és meglepetést tartogató társasjáték, amiben lépésről lépésre válhatsz vérbeli vállalkozóvá.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Violent Skies: 1940 – Dynamo to the Blitz
description:
Sat inside the cockpit with the engine rattling through your bones, you turn to engage. Managing your energy and fuel, can you gain position to get a shot off? Or will the bandit get the better of you? Sometimes it is better to disengage than push a losing fight.

Violent Skies is a solitaire game of aerial combat in the Second World War. Using a game card that acts as your Cockpit and also as a mission generator. The player and Bandit take alternating actions to try and get a shot on the opposition's aircraft.

This first season of games puts you in the cockpit of the main fighters during the tumultuous months after the invasion of France. Starting with the aerial operations over Dynamo (the Dunkirk Evacuation) to the Battle of Britain and the last daylight bombing raid as the Blitz starts, you will be flying sorties, shooting down bandits and trying your best to survive.
Each sortie lasts between 5-10 minutes, and the player has a logbook to advance their career. As the player completes a campaign, they have the option to keep the logbook and continue their career in different aircraft and theatres.

All is needed is at least four D6, a small marker for the advantage track, and a pen or pencil for the logbook. Everything else is included in the minimal construction print and play PDF.  Three optional counters are included and need to be folded and cut out but the rest is ready straight off the printer.

Included as flyable aircraft are the Spitfire MkI/II, Hurricane Mk1, Bf109E3/4, Bf-110C.

Each aircraft comes with two sortie cards (your cockpit and mission generator), campaign tables, optional damage table, Skills sheet and counters.

There are Logbooks for the Lufrwaffe and RAF in low ink and full colour, as well as a detailed example of play.

-description from designer

.....and remember, watch out for the "Hun in the Sun" and never underestimate a "fat old puffer"
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: Violent Skies (Ben Rawlings, Pocket Warfare Publishing)

boardgame: Киберпанк-2 Битвы в стальных пещерах (Cyberpunk 2: Battles in the Steel Caverns)
description:
Cyberpunk 2 is a new version of the previous game.

This is a game about cyborg battles and gathering raw materials in the dystopian distant future. Stylish décor (a dim dump field lined with a variety of obstacles and containers), miniatures of bipedal cyborgs and six-legged zhucybers. Real shooting with plastic shots, and the damaged parts of the cyborgs' bodies are removed, respectively limiting their capabilities; Thus, the increased firepower of bipedal modifications is compensated by reduced survivability. There are many different finds on the field, and cyborgs can also have different specializations, whether it's assault or, for example, electronic warfare.

—user summary

boardgame: Киберпанк-3 Засада на UNL-2 (Cyberpunk 3: Ambush on UNL-2)
description:
This is the third edition of the Cyberpunk game.

The game is about cyborg battles and gathering raw materials in the dystopian distant future. Scenery (a dim dump field lined with a variety of obstacles and containers), miniatures of bipedal cyborgs and six-legged zhucybers. Real shooting with plastic shots, and the damaged parts of the cyborgs' bodies are removed, respectively limiting their capabilities; Thus, the increased firepower of bipedal modifications is compensated by reduced survivability. There are many different finds on the field, and cyborgs can also have different specializations, whether it's assault or, for example, electronic warfare.

—user summary

This is a card game from Tsukuda.  Each player uses their cards to try to assault their opponent and attempt to pin them. Players can respond by using one of their counter cards.  the assault-defense system can go back and forth.  The person to pin their opponent wins.

Game comes with:
-100 cards
-game board
-rules

Another dice game from Steve Jackson, part of his nanogames series. Each player takes eight dice of a different color and everyone rolls at once to construct the body of their dragon. 1 and 3 are the head and tail, respectively; 2s are the body; 4s, 5s, and 6s are passed to the next player and rerolled next round. The goal is to mix colors together to get bonus points and be the first to earn 10 points, but if there is a tie, the win is shared equally.

Printed on the back of the rules for Halloween Dice (renamed Jack-O'-Lanterns).

アニマノレース (Anima-no-race) is a game from Japan with animals racing to survive. Each player has a squirrel, a rabbit and a bear to move, and each animal needs its own food (nuts, carrots, fish). Instead of rolling dice, all players choose a tile to show their move, and after revealing players decide which of their three animals to move. The game is designed to balance choices between moving or getting food, and stealing food for victory points by landing on the same space as another animal. The player with the most points at the end is the winner, and the bears receive bonus points as well.

The publisher says: 
Squirrels, rabbits, and bears escaped the dried-out forest with only a bit of food. They are trying to reach the paradise called Animanoland where only three animals can live. On their way to escape, they need to get food from other animals in order to survive.

Basically this is sugoroku game but with tiles instead of dice. Players moves 1 of their animal tokens out of 3 each turn with stealing/being stolen their food each other. The first three animals reached to the paradise will get bonus points. The player with the highest points, the total point of the bonus and the food they stole from other animals will win.
description by publisher

You need 3 Animal Astronauts of the same kind or three astronauts with the same background color to get stars. Whoever has the most stars wins.

Setup:
All tiles are shuffled in the middle and each player gets 3 face up in front of him.

Gameplay:
During your turn you can take one of the tiles in the middle (either face up or down) and switch it with one of your 3 starting tiles. Put them in front of you face up, others can take them later, but you can’t anymore during this round. 
If you manage to get 3 of a kind you get a star and 3 new tiles for the next round. 
The game ends when all 12 stars are distributed

Dinner Time is a party game for 4 to 10 players with the secret identities and competing in 2 teams, racing to reach their team goal first.

Players will be secretly assigned a role (Team Roach or Team Rat). And will take turns to manipulate the shared Dinner pile in the center of the table and try to reveal their goal, food for Team Roach and poison for Team Rat, from it.

On your turn, you must take 1 of 5 actions; Adding food or poison into the Dinner pile, peeking into the Dinner pile to get some information, revealing the dinner cards, playing a special action card or just pass. Whenever the last piece of any team goal (the 8th Food or the 8th Poison) has been revealed. The team that achieved their goal will be the winner.

This is Zvezda's rulebook for fighting ancient to medieval battles using the Art of Tactic system.

In order to play the game, you require bases for your troops.  Each base contains and identification flag and a data card that is used when moving, fighting, or checking moral during the game.

Most of the Zvezda boxed Art of Tactic sets include figures, bases, data cards, and rules.  This book was sold separately.  It came with a car protractor and data cards.

A game about a duel between humans and rats. Both sides explore and try to destroy the other.  You can upgrade units during the game (by rolling the dice), which gives increased combat effectiveness or new abilities. The sides are almost identical in strength, only the human mages have stronger ability.

—user summary

Earth's Space Tracking Stations have detected several giant man-made space objects in the moon's shadow. Specially created international units of COBRA are preparing to welcome the intruders.

COBRA fighters not only intercept alien scouts, but also collect and study alien artifacts, creating their own samples of offensive weapons and defense systems based on them. Will the brave defenders of Earth have enough time and strength to prevent an attempted alien invasion? Time is counting down!

—description from the publisher

Roll the dice and create unique tales with Rory's Story Cubes! In this new version of the game, immerse yourself in the world of Paw Patrol and include your favorite characters in your stories.

—description from the publisher

Империя ("Empire") is a conquest and building game, much like Risk and Civilization, with only two pages of rules.

The map is divided into territories. Each territory has the portrait of its dictator on it along with a gold symbol for how much income it gives, and symbols for the units it can recruit.

—user summary

The turn begins with a roll of the dice to check for rebellion. If it is "6", then some province has rebelled. Rolling a pair of dice indicates its number.

Next, players take turns buying armies with the available money at approved ancient Spartan rates, placing them in the provinces where they are hired.

Then they begin to move armies to neighboring provinces for conquest. Armies are led by a general. Without it, it is not an army, but a collection of recruits who cannot fight on their own but are able to simply move around. Most often, as conscripts in the direction of the "military registration and enlistment office", that is, the commander. And only this "head" (and this is what a commander looks like) is able to unite forces into an army and lead into battle. Since each side has only four generals, no empire can afford more than four directions for simultaneous aggression.

When entering someone else's territory, a battle begins. Even neutral countries at the beginning of the game snap back very painfully. So, there is no easy loot in the game.

In the mechanics of combat, the dice has a great influence as a generator of battle randomness. First, both sides play rock-paper-scissors, choosing a value between 1 and 6. When announcing the values, refer to the "Battle Scheme" in the center of the map.

The winner gets the right to an additional roll of the dice in the battle. The second additional roll of the dice is played in the same way. Next, each player calculates the strength of his army (infantry = 1, cavalry = 2, etc.), adds the main roll of the dice and, if he wins an additional roll, also its value. The opponent does the same, after which the obtained values are compared. The result of the battle is described in the "Battle Table".

I would like to note that the winner, no matter how huge the advantage, always loses at least one infantryman. Or an analogue of his power if the attacker does not have infantry. War requires sacrifices, even if it is "small and victorious."

But on the other hand, a pleasant surprise awaits the invader. Lovers of "robbing cows", rejoice! A roll of the dice is used to determine how many coins have been requisitioned from the undoubtedly grateful population to the liberator. And then there's the ODN fee, ugh, the tax will be collected from this province at the beginning of the next turn. In general, war is a profitable business here. True, if not for the rivals, who are probably of the same opinion.

Then the next player attacks, and so on.

From time to time, successful expansion is disrupted by unfortunate uprisings in the rear, often leading to the "breakaway" of the province from the empire. The restoration of constitutional order takes time for the transfer of troops and breaks plans for the conquest of peace. Plundering a newly enslaved province, as an easy form of compensation for the inconvenience caused, sweetens the "bitter pill" of losing momentum.

The turn ends, and it repeats all over again. (end of article)

The game comes with numerous miniatures for the infantry, cavalry, elephants, chariots, ships, and fortifications.

—description from the designer (translated)

boardgame: The Unknown: Eisige Intrigen – Krimi Podcast Adventskalender
description:
Immerse yourself in the world of crime fiction - right in your ears!
With the Crime Podcast Advent Calendar 2023, every December day becomes an acoustic mystery adventure. In our crime thriller podcast advent calendar, two professional hosts take you into the action and let you dive deeper into the story day by day.

Your crime thriller podcast: daily suspense for podcast fans
You don't need an extra app or special software. You can immerse yourself in our 5-10 minute crime episodes directly in your browser on any internet-enabled device. Perfect for all podcast and audiobook fans. Turn every day of December into a little crime thriller highlight!
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
In Survive The Serengeti, players compete to guide their wildebeest safely to the other side of a savannah filled with hungry predators.

A card game for 2-5 players, each player starts with an identical hand of wildebeest numbered 1-12. Each turn a predator is revealed that will force a player to discard a card if they are the unlucky player who guesses incorrectly. All players choose, then simultaneously reveal one of their wildebeest, hoping not to be lunch.

This is a "play the players" type of game, full of double-think, bluffing, and reading your opponent.

—description from the designer

Nemesis CCG is a two-player dueling card game where each player builds a team of champions to fight against their opponent’s team of champions in a winner-take-all battle! Assemble your Team, Build your Deck, and Crush your enemy. The first Player to defeat all of their opponent’s champions wins the game!

Assemble your Team -- Choose 3 or more Characters from up to 20 original characters to create your unique Team of champions.  Nemesis CCG's Rank Card system means there are literally BILLIONS of unique team combinations.

Build your Deck -- Build your custom deck around the Characters that you have chosen.  Each Character has five unique Ability Cards.  You can further customize your deck by incorporating S.T.A.R. Cards - 18 Standard Tactics And Resources Cards that any Character can use.

Crush Your Enemy -- Each turn, use the cards in your hand to have your Characters launch attacks against the opponent team.  Once all of the opposing Characters have been defeated, you win!

—description from the publisher

Last Heard is a game centered around dark humor and is about having an eye for the morbidly funny aspect of death and disaster.

It’s a variation on the idea of the last words someone speaks before they die: Last Heards are the last words someone hears before they go to that big mansion in the sky.
Players of Last Heards make combinations between Text Cards (containing a quote of the last words someone hears before they bump off) and a Photo Card, depicting the lethal situation that led to their unfortunate end.         
         
An impartial referee (known as “The Executioner”) decides which combination between Text Card and Photo Card wins the round.
Last Heard is also a board game. The player who wins the round, throws a dice and moves forward on the board. A surprising feature of Last Heards is that the player who reaches the finish first, also wins the game.
The playing board features “Verdict” cards. A player who lands on a “Verdict Square” on the game board, has to pick a Verdict Card and vill follow ze instructions on ze card. Will you be the first one the reach the finish?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mexico (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description: Unofficial user-created map of Mexico and ticket cards.

boardgame: 午茶跳跳 (Tea Time Hopping Race)
description: Bunny and Chick are in a race. You secretly (and not so secretly) support one of them, and try to collect as many cakes as possible during each race. There are cakes to be collected during the race. If the contestant you support wins a race, all supporters share the prize. After three races, the player with the most valuable cakes wins.

boardgame: 僵尸貓的試膽大會 (Zombie Cats Daring Contest)
description:
The zombie cats are holding a competition to decide once and for all who is the most daring of them all.

On your turn you keep flipping over cards until the total reaches at least 7. Once you reach 7, you may claim the cards as your points, or you may also continue to draw. If you reach exactly 10, you win a trophy. However if you exceed 10, you end your turn empty handed.

There is one special die. If you forgo claiming any cards, you may roll the die instead. You may get to steal a trophy, steal cards, but you may also lose cards.

Play until the deck runs out. The player with the most points is the most daring zombie cat and wins the game.

パンダポーカー (Panda Poker) is a card game from Japan, roughly styled on Texas Hold'em Poker but with Pandas.

The publisher says:
There are no suits or numbers on the cards, only pandas of various patterns depicted. Some are real, some are complicated fakes.

The game is played by memorising the two cards in the hand in an instant, and then selecting five cards from seven in front of you (including five cards that are later revealed). In this way, you create a Poker hand such as one pair, followed by betting and competing for chips. This is a party game where memory and bluffing are important.

テキサスホールデム風パンダポーカー
ルールは大体テキサスホールデム。カードにはスートや数字がなく、描かれているのは色々な柄のパンダだけ。本物もいれば、ややこしい偽物もいる。これを手札2枚を一瞬で記憶して、後に公開される5枚を含む7枚から5枚を選びワンペアなどの役を作り、チップをベットし競り合います。 記憶力やブラフが大事なパーティゲームです。
description by publisher
artists: おーつぼじゅうじん (Jyujin Ohtsubo)

description:
In a plastic suitcase, there are 5 dice, 32 cards and some chips (points). Each player starts with 10 points of life. By rolling special dice showing gangsters and the loot you try to lower the points of your opponents or to get the loot. Each player starts with 3 cards and may play one in addition to their die roll. If you don't succeed in lowering or getting points you draw a card as well. A player with no pints left is eliminated. You win if you get the loot or are the last man standing.

—user summary

boardgame: Øresund Runt (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Sweden and Denmark are so close yet so far apart, so alike yet so different. The neighbouring countries are separated by Øresund, a strait 118 kilometers in length. It is crossed in the north by ferries and in the south by the Øresund bridge, connecting the rail networks of the two countries and allowing Swedes and Danes alike to travel around Øresund.

Øresund Runt is a fan expansion for Ticket to Ride which lets you visit the eastern-most parts of Denmark and connecting with the southern-most parts of Sweden.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: DiceFest 2023 Promo Cards
description:
These 5 cards were initially exclusives for those that attended Dicefest 2023, and these are not the same cards included in the goody box. They can now be obtained randomly as inserts in bits box orders from Flying Frog’s website. These cards include:

Doctor Benning’s Skull (#1069),
Aztec Eagle Helmet (#1070),
Dead Man’s Cards (#1071),
Sweet Perfume (#1072),
Tentacles from the Void extended art (#art-02)

The aim of the game Česko pro pamětníky is to answer the questions correctly and get one "mark" from each circle. Players move around the game board and answer a question from the circle their piece ends up on after a roll of the dice.

Bunch of Butts is a real time simultaneous card matching game.

Each turn, players simultaneously flip up a card. Players can call out the butt card names if they see a match. The first player to call a match takes the matching pair of cards. However, if the silent but deadly card is in play they lose those cards. If a spank card is in play, the first player to spank the foam rubber butt can steal a card from another player.

The backs of the cards are of five types: “Bun”, “ch”, “of, “But” and “ts”. The first player to spell out “Bunch of Butts” wins the game.

boardgame: Astro Knights: Atomic Gauntlet Promo Card
description:
Originally available in Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign.

Atomic Gauntlet
Weapon
Cost: 7
Attack: Deal 6 damage.
Ability: Each ally may draw up to two cards and then discard that many cards.

boardgame: Astro Knights: Tai Sabaki Assault Promo Card
description:
Originally available in Tantrum House Season 10 Kickstarter campaign.

Tai Sabaki Assault
Weapon
Cost: 8
Attack: Deal 7 damage.
Ability: Each ally may discard a card. For each card discarded this way, power up the Homeworld once.

Based on the book "Roadside Picnic" by Arkady and Boris Strugatsky.  The game follows the book.

Footprints left by the aliens after their visit changed the nature of the Earth. Zones of "contamination" remain, bearing hidden treasures.

Despite the mortal risk, brave "stalkers" dare to enter these Zones in order to take out the objects left by the aliens, such as the ship "Khabar". The leakage of Khabar is being suppressed by governments in every possible way.

Stalking has been outlawed in the vicinity of the Zones, and special Institutes for the Study of Anomalous Phenomena have sprung up.  The Zones are fenced with barbed wire and guarded by police and troops. Life in the cities adjacent to the Zones is unbearable, and you need to buy a Visa to leave. Money can only be obtained by successfully selling to dealers.

You take on the role of one of these "Stalkers" in the game. The game is for those who love risk, fantasy and adventure.

A children's game where you are trying to be the first to cross the finish line.

On a player's turn they roll a die and move that many spaces along a predetermined track.  Some spaces have benefits, and some have penalties.  If you land on the same space as another player, they go back to start.

First player across the finish line wins.

The game comes with:
-game map
-6 figures
-one die
-rules

A children's game where you are trying to be the first to cross the finish line.

On a player's turn they roll a die and move that many spaces along a predetermined track.  Some spaces have benefits, and some have penalties.  If you land on the same space as another player, they go back to start.

First player across the finish line wins.

The game comes with:
-game map
-6 figures
-one die
-rules

In this game, each player strives to be the first to destroy the enemy's warriors.

Players take turns. During his turn, the player makes one shot with the help of the dice. The right of the first move is determined by the roll of the dice. The player who gets the right to shoot is the first to call the opponent's sector A or B and rolls his dice. If the number of points on the dice coincides with the number of the hex, on which the opponent's figure stands, then the shot was accurate.

The opponent must make a defensive throw with his dice. If the number of points of the defensive throw coincides with the number of points of the "shot", then the protective equipment saved the warrior from being "wounded", the figure of the warrior remains in the same place. If the points of defense and shot do not coincide, then the figure of the warrior is "wounded" and moves to the yellow field of its hex.
The next time the unit is wounded, it should move from the yellow to the red field. If a unit is on the red field and is wounded again, it is considered destroyed and is removed to the center of the hexomap, where the sector designation is depicted.

The ARMOR icon in the card next to the unit figure saves the unit from one wound if it is on a yellow field. The ARMOR Usage Card is out of the game if it does not have the MEDKIT icon on it. If there is a MEDKIT icon, the card stays next to the unit, but lies down halfway so that only the MEDKIT icon remains open. The player will need the FIRST AID Kit a little later - on the red field.

The MEDKIT icon saves the unit from one wound if he stands on the red hex. After using the First Aid Kit once, the card is out of the game.

Cards provide additional protection for warriors. There are two symbols on the cards: "yellow shield with a blue stripe" - ARMOR and "red cross" badge - FIRST AID KIT. Each of the badges saves warriors from being wounded once.

Game come with:
-Hexopoles - 4 pcs 
-Flashcards - 12 pcs 
-TRKP squad figures - 6 pcs 
-Heavy line infantry figures - 6 pcs 
-Dice - 2 pcs

18xx Checkers is a simplified Print and Play version of Tresham's 1829 Mainline designed to use common gaming material that most people own.  One or two players will buy shares in four different Railroad Companies, lay track and operate trains to collect revenue.

The playing surface is a standard checkers board and standard playing cards are used to represent share certificates.  Other common gaming devices needed are checkers, a couple of 6 sided dice and play money.

The game itself includes rules, a one page Share Price Index/Player Aid, and a single page of custom square Track and Train tiles.

18xx Checkers has been described as "a really accessible introduction to 18xx but also a very interesting game on its own"

18xx Checkers is different from most 18xx genre games in that it uses square tiles, the game board is randomly generated, and like 1829 Mainline, available shares are drawn from a shuffled deck.  Thus no two games are the same.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – Captain Picard: Federation Expansion
description: An expanding galaxy Picard returns to the site of the Battle of Wolf 359 to establish peace between the squabbling factions and restore the primacy of the Federation in interstellar matters. This expansion is ready to play straight out of the box and comes with pre-designed card decks that make the most of the characters abilities. Alternatively, you can mix cards and mission specialists from other away teams to customise your decks and add even more variety.

boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – House of Duras: Klingon Expansion
description: An expanding galaxy The Duras Sisters take the remains of their house to Wolf 359 in the hope of finding plunder to finance their return to Klingon politics and to redeem their honor - by any means necessary. This expansion is ready to play straight out of the box and comes with pre-designed card decks that make the most of the characters abilities. Alternatively, you can mix cards and mission specialists from other away teams to customise your decks and add even more variety.

boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – Q Organized Play Kit
description:
Q, an enigmatic entity from the Q Continuum, delights in spreading chaos in the lives of mere mortals, instilling valuable lessons in the process.

This Organized Play Kit has everything you need to play a four-game campaign for up to ten players. Perfect to bring your gaming group together for a big event or ongoing meetings.

The Q Organized Play Kit contains everything you need to support an event for ten players, including:

10x Q Miniatures

10x Q Character Cards

150x Cards

62x Tokens

boardgame: Схватка за ковчег (The Battle for the Ark)
description:
In this game, up to four teams are trying to get to the center of the board.  The map is divided into levels, and as a player gets to the circular corners, they are allowed to move into a closer level to the center.  Players that land on another one can be eliminated.

The goal is to get a figure to the center of the map.  There, the last four will fight for supremacy.

Game includes: 
-map
-12 figures
-tokens
-1 die
-rules

Assault on the Bastion is about assaulting a fortress that is full of treasure.  The boss and his army are going to have a tough time breaching the defenses.

Each turn consists of three phases:
1.Manuever
2.Shooting
3.Battle

Manuever - a player rolls a die for how many actions they get.  A piece can move one space for one action and may do up to the total rolled in actions.  You can also split the total actions over several figures; if a five was rolled you could move two figures two spaces each and another figure one space.  Up to two figures can be in a space.

Shooting - The defender then shoots adjacently with his archers, needing a 6 to hit.  The arc of fire is front, back, left, right, but on a tower you can also shoot diagonally.

Battle - Figures that are adjacent (not diagonally) can fight.  Both roll a die and the high side wins and removes a figure.  If there are additional figures in the space, then roll again, until the space is cleared. There are some modifiers to this roll.  Archers are minus one to the roll and fight after any other knights in the space, and the boss is plus one.

If the attackers enter the treasure room they win.  If the boss is ever killed, the attacker lose.

The game includes:
-map
-Attacker army with 15 figures (8 knights, 6 shooters, 1 master)
-Defending army of 15 figures (8 knights, 7 shooters)
-one die

Dice Duelo is a semi-abstract game where two players take the role of a gambler-duelist in the Wild West.

Players roll their dice secretly and take turns placing a die one by one on the board to form a better Poker combination.

This duel is not won by the fastest finger, but the strongest mind. It is not about how good your dice roll is, but how you use it. Bluff your way through, outwit your opponent to use his dice to your advantage.

It’s quick: Shoot down your opponent in a round of 6 bullets.
it’s dirty: Use tricks to surprise your opponent.
It’s clever: Explainable in five minutes, playable for years.

—description from the publisher
A set of two additional problems for ArcheOlogic.

In this game both sides are trying to enter each other's castle.

During a turn, a player rolls a die to determine how many actions they get.  A piece can move one space for one action and may do up to the total rolled in actions.  You can also split the total actions over several figures; if a five was rolled you could move two figures two spaces each and another figure one space.  Only one figure can be in a space.

A piece can kill an enemy piece by landing on it, this ends its moves for the turn.

Pieces that land on a teleport symbol are teleported to another symbol.

Tower spaces provide immunity to death from for the figure that is on them.

Game includes:
-Playing field
-Set of 8 figures in two colors (16 pcs)
-one dice
-game rules

Fröschis (engl. Froggis) is the German version of the game תעשו מקום (Make Room), published in 2020 by Shafir Games in Israel.
In Fröschis, you want to line up your cards in numerical order, eventually getting rid of your card row entirely.

The deck consists of cards numbered 1-8 with six of each, along with four frogs and eight trash. To start the game, deal each player a row of eight cards face down.

On a turn, take either the top card of the deck or the discard pile, then place the card in the proper place in your row — a 1 goes in the first spot, a 2 in the second spot, etc. — as long as the card you're replacing is face down. Reveal that face-down card, and if you can place it in your row — again, replacing only a face-down card — then do so. If you reveal a frog, it's a joker that can serve as any number. If, for example, you place a frog in the 4 spot, reveal a 6, then place the 6 and reveal a 4, you can place the 4, then move the frog to a different face-down spot. If you reveal trash or a number you already have face up, discard it to end your turn.

If you have eight face-up cards in a row, each other player takes one final turn, then you start a new round, and all players who completed their row are dealt only seven cards instead of eight. Now you have to get only seven cards in a row to win the round, but all 8s count as trash for you, possibly making it harder for you to do so. Keep playing rounds until someone gets down to a row of only one card, completing it with a 1 or a frog. That player wins when they do so, and multiple players can win if they complete their one-card row in the same round.

This free demo version doesn't have all the cards of the full version.

A fast, fun, pocket-sized 18-card game for the whole family.

Don’t get stuck in the smoke! Each round use your limited Wind cards to move the Smoke card to another player. If you can’t move the Smoke away, you lost the round-–a Smokeout! When the game ends, the player with the fewest Smokeouts wins!

Its straight-forward rules make this a great game to break out at the campsite or living room when you're looking for light-hearted interactions between players. The youngest players learn the fun--and repercussions--of who and when they choose to send the Smoke. They'll quickly discover picking on any one player is a losing strategy!

—description from the designer

After Earth is safe from Xeta rays, the SoS (Saviors of Solar) has been reported from additional surveys that other planets are still affected by the Xeta rays. Among them is Jupiter. According to the observations and studies, it has shown that there are significant changes in the surface, color, and size of the Great Red Spot.

For this reason, the SoS joined forces with space agencies from various countries and launched spacecrafts to study and record the effects on Jupiter with photos. A good photo represents performance and efficiency of your agency’s technological advancement. So, let’s begin the race to the 29th latitude to obtain the best photo of the Great Red Spot.

Tower Tactic Games (The Stronghold) collaborates with Thai game designer who creates Cabbage Farm, The Coding, 27 Meters, and 28 Days. 29 Degrees is the last game from the Solar Crisis Series that is easy to play for players of all ages.

—description from the publisher

Forget the old-fashioned bet your luck games because this is the new advanced “Slot machine” in which everyone can become a millionaire overnight! However, only one of you will be able to win this game. You must decide how to move the token that determines the prize through the main engine or the substitute engine to the desired location in order to gain the most points.

Slot Machine is a Family and Hand Management for 2-5 players where you must plan to move the Jackpot Token in the Slot Machine onto the position that you think has the greatest chance of winning the prize. Relying on luck may not be enough in this game. Thus, plan your next move and win the jackpot!
    
Let's have fun and laugh together in this Slot Machine game from Goalbeat Montree, the designer of "Foxy & Wooly" and "Sriracha" and find out who will win the prize in this game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Битва за планету Велиан (Battle for the Planet Velian)
description:
The board consists of a starting zone for the figures (numbered 1-6), a space that is a hospital (where a piece goes when it is attacked and loses) spaces where a piece is protected, and spaces where an attack may be made to an adjacent space (determined by the direction of the arrow).

Players roll to see who goes first.  That player then selects a figure and rolls a die, with the number rolled being the number of spaces it can move. If the figure moves onto a space with a token, they collect it.  If they land on a space with an attack icon, they shoot a piece in the adjacent space the arrow is pointing in.  If they land in the same space as an enemy figure, both roll and the high side wins.  In both cases, the figure is sent to the hospital and must roll a 6 to revive.

The goal of the game is tom be the first to collect the most tokens and return to their base. The first to do so gets a bonus of +50 points to their total.

Comes with:
-playing field
-cards 50 pcs.
-figures "militia of the planet Velian" - 6 pcs.
-figures "Skanda" - 6 pcs.
- game dice
- rules

Knight's Marathon is a sort of "foot" racing game by Tehnolog.

The players determine who goes first and that player rolls two dice.  He can move one figure that many spaces or select two figures and move them the distance shown on a die (if you roll 3 and 4, one soldier moves three and the other moves four).  If you roll doubles, then you get a bonus turn, whereby you can use the number rolled for another round of moves (as before).

If a piece lands on a red square they advance to the next red square. If a piece lands on a blue square, then they advance to the next blue square.  If the opponent's figure is in the same spot, then it must withdraw to the next red of blue space.

The first player to get all their knights tom their proper camps wins.

Game comes with
-playing field
-two sets of 6 soldiers, one for each player (12 total)
-2 dice
-rules

The game deals with a fight over an ancient lighthouse by orcs and Vikings.  The goal of the orcs is to get one of their tokens to the top of the lighthouse, while the Viking are trying to prevent this.

A turn consists of three phases:
1.Manuever
2.Hand-to-hand
3.shoot

Maneuver - a player rolls a die for how many actions they get.  A piece can move one space for one action and may do up to the total rolled in actions.  You can also split the total actions over several figures; if a five was rolled you could move two figures two spaces each and another figure one space.  However, no piece may move further than three spaces. Only one token can be in a space.  There are additional costs for climbing stairs.

Hand-to-hand - Figures that enter another space occupied by an enemy figure fight.  Both roll a die and the high side wins. If the difference was only one then the piece is returned to their side of the map, if by two then the piece is killed and removed.

Shooting - The player then shoots adjacently with their figures.  Viking roll one die per figure, but Orcs roll two. On a 6 the piece is killed and on a 5 it is wounded (the piece is returned to owner's side of the map). There are different modifications for tower shots.
 The game includes:
-Playing field
-Game tokens 18 pcs (8 of one color and 10 of another)
-2 dice
-Rules of the game

boardgame: Monster Stunt: CyberCross AI-1000 Promo Card
description:
Promo card of the monster vehicle which was available only during crowdfunding campaign on CrowdRepublic platform.
CyberCross AI-1000 can act as a motorcycle, a race car and a truck at the same time. Also any monster in the game can operate it.

boardgame: Бой в волшебных руинах (Battle in the Magical Ruins)
description:
The map is divided into hexagons that have a die symbol in them.  That symbol shows a die value from 1-6, which is the roll necessary for a figure to enter it.

Players take turns rolling dice for their figures and moving their pieces into a hex that they are allowed to. For example, if I rolled a four, then I could move into a hex that has a four in it. After entering it, you can continue to move into the next hex if it has a four, and so on. All of a player's figures have a chance to move, so make sure that you do not block their path.

If an enemy piece is in the hex, then they fight a melee.  Both roll a die, and the high side sends the loser's piece back to start.

To win you must bring three of your figures to the base on the other side of the board.

includes:
-Playing field
-5 Siegfried figures
-5 Centurion figures
-Legend
-Rules
-Dice

boardgame: Мушкетёры Острова Магов (Musketeer of Mages Island)
description:
The objective for the Musketeers is to get three of their figures to the Guild. The Guards must get three of their figures to the Castle.

The phasing player rolls a die for how many actions they get.  A piece can move one space for one action and may do up to the total rolled in actions.  You can also split the total actions over several figures; if a five was rolled you could move two figures two spaces each and another figure one space. Players can also travel thorough the tunnels. Only one figure can be in a space.  Each Musketeer piece that enters the guild gives them +1 to their die roll, and likewise, each Guard piece that enters the castle gives them +1 to their die roll.

Figures that are adjacent (not diagonally) can fight.  Both roll a die and the high side wins and removes a figure and places it back in their starting area.

The game includes:
-Playing field
-Musketeer figurines 5 pcs 
-Guardsmen figurines 5 pcs
-Dice
-Rules

boardgame: Donut Shop: Blue Plate Special Pack
description:
It's never too early for dinner at the donut shop with this blue plate special. This promo mini-expansion includes two new ways to play. The first is the "Secret Orders" set of new orders. The other is a set of special X2 scoring bonus tiles.

Contains:

5 Donut Tiles
1 Four Pack Box Tile
5 Order Cards
2 Rules Cards

-description from publisher

boardgame: Тайна Забытого Подземелья (Secrets of the Forgotten Dungeon)
description:
Mystery of the Forgotten Dungeon is a game where two players are trying to find a secret manuscript located inside a dungeon.

The game includes:
-Game map
-10 miniatures (five for each player) 
-11 cards 
-2 red doors with stands
-2 blue doors with stands
-rules
-one die (with numbers 1-3)

Players take turns rolling a die and moving that many of their figures one space each, in any direction but diagonally.  Only one piece per space. When a room is entered the card is revealed and resolved. Players continue in this way, exploring rooms. Some rooms require a colored key.

If a player lands on another they fight.  Both roll a die and the high side wins, and sends the other back tom start. They also get to steal a card.

The winner is the player who finds the manuscript and exits the dungeon.

boardgame: Color Field: Community Tools Promo Pack
description:
Color Field promo pack featuring 6 new community tool cards: H-Frame Easel, Premium Turquoise Acrylics, Premium Coral Acrylics, Premium Navy Acrylics, Premium Lemon Acrylics, and Surrealism.

Contains 6 cards.

-description from publisher

The board consist of a square-grided board with starting zones on each side and a laboratory in the middle. Both sides are trying to get three of their warriors into an abandoned Labatory. Entry is only possible through the red or blue doors.

Players take turn rolling three dice. Each number rolled corresponds to a soldier in their starting area.  For example, if 4,5,6 was rolled, then soldiers 4,5, and 6 could move one space each.  If a 4,2,2 was rolled then soldier four would move one space and soldier two would move twice. You can move in any direction, including diagonally, but you may not enter a space with another figure. Only one figure per space is allowed.

A move may be forfeited in order to shoot.  A shot can be taken up to three squares away.  Two dice are rolled; if at least one is a 6 then you hit your target and it returns back to its starting area.

If you occupy two of the same-colored squares (red or blue) you are allowed to open that same-colored door on the laboratory.  Thus, if you wish to get inside the laboratory, it requires two figures to be on the outside-colored squares, which allows another figure to open the floor of the Labatory and enter it.

If you land on a space next an enemy soldier you fight. Both roll a die and the high side wins.  The figure is returned to their starting zone. Ties reroll.

To win, you need to get three figures into the laboratory and on their colored squares.

The game includes:
-Condor Case - 6 figures 
-Tornado Case - 6 figures
-Playing field
-Rules of the game
-3 dice

Table Cooking: Enjoy Your Meal is a cooking game with demand/supply prices in which players want to complete yummy menu orders for money and points, not to mention personal quests during game which bring endgame points.

Each player has three zones in their starter kitchen for stocking ingredients, buying ingredients, and picking new menu orders, and each zone can be upgraded during play.

The game ends when a player has completed seven menu orders, at which time everyone scores for completed menu orders, their personal quest, and leftover ingredients.

A magical meteor crashed in the Tassacra desert, sparking a fierce competition among fantasy creatures. They're all after the magical shards the meteor left behind to become the top faction in Zephyrund. Possessing these shards increases your faction's influence, or they can be used to craft powerful artifacts, boosting your strength on the battlefields.

In Tassacra, you play four rounds called Days, and the winner of the game is the player with the most victory points at the very end. During the course of the play, you can earn victory points in many different ways, such as defeating foes, winning battles, scoring end-day objectives, stealing from your opponents, selling resources, and more.

In this strategic race, players select asymmetric factions of magical beings, each equipped with distinctive abilities and strengths. Factions also have different activity peaks during specific times of the day (dusk, day, and night), but all players will take eight main actions by the end of the day. To win, you will invade the desert, move units, engage in combats, scavenge magical shards, and resupply. Additionally, you can craft items to upgrade combat abilities, sell shards to the market, and complete quests.

You will attack and get attacked. In combat, active players roll combat dice, which can be modified by upgrades and combat cards.

There are just four days before rivals and the desert sands claim the shards. Get your warriors ready – the battle in Tassacra begins!

boardgame: Киборги на тропе войны (Cyborgs on the Warpath)
description:
Киборги на тропе войны ("Cyborgs on the Warpath") is a typical roll-and-move track type game.

Players begin by placing their token on the starting box.  They then roll to see whom goes first and that player rolls the dice and moves the number indicated along the track.

If they land on a red or blue box, they must follow the path it tells them to.  This continues through each level of the game.

The winner is the first one to reach square 44 wins the game.

The path has an intricate design, which can send a player in different directions with all sorts of consequences.

—user summary

Operation Axiz is a solo-only, doujin game themed around "Operation Axis" which occurs at the end of the movie "Mobile Suit Gundam: Char's Counterattack".

The player takes on the role of the Londo Bell forces, trying to prevent the Neo Zeon forces from dropping the asteroid Axis on Earth by destroying the asteroid.

On their turn, the player draws mobile suits, ships and events from their deck, and must decide to either "patrol" to intercept incoming Neo Zeon Mobile Suits, or attempt to set bombs on Axis in an attempt to destroy the asteroid.

—user summary

Kronologic are modern alternatives to Clue: find the culprit, the weapon and the location. Each turn, the active player needs to interrogate a suspect (by aligning cards under perforated cardboard tiles), giving away information to everyone at the table, but giving the active player extra information (how many time a suspect visited a specific location, at what time, etc.)

Be the first to solve the mystery to win the game.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 36 wins the game.

—user summary

No matter where you are - with this exciting City, Country, Bingewatching edition you'll always bring a big dose of fun to the round! In 26 categories you can test your series knowledge and prove your creativity. The game block convinces with cool design and exciting topics - perfect for all series lovers. From simple questions to tricky categories - there's something here for everyone.

—description from the publisher

No matter where you are - with this exciting City, Country, Goal Edition you'll always bring a big portion of fun with you! You can test your football knowledge and prove your creativity in 26 categories. With its cool design and exciting questions, the game block will convince both amateur footballers and professional footballers, whether big or small. From simple questions to tricky categories - there is something here for everyone. 
The person who fills in everything the fastest wins.

—description from the publisher

No matter where you are - with this original Stadt, Land, Suff edition you'll always bring a big portion of fun to the round! You can test your knowledge of the liquid gold and prove your creativity in 26 wine-related categories. The game block convinces with cool design and exciting topics - perfect for all party people. From simple questions to tricky categories - there's something here for everyone. 
The person who fills in everything the fastest wins.

—description from the publisher

Battle to collect cards and win gems in this interstellar and highly competitive card game. The first player to earn 50 gems wins.

In Snarkas, each player receives a hand of cards, and 4 cards are dealt onto the table. On each player's turn, one card is played. That card is either added to the table, or used to collect cards from the table through matching or adding.

The game includes cards numbered 1-10, along with 2 different robot characters (Asters and Jolts), and ZAP cards that clear the entire table. Some cards have gems on them, which will add to a player's point total.

If any player is able to clear the table with any card other than a ZAP, they yell "SNARKAS!" and earn the shiny Snarkas gem worth 10 gems!

boardgame: Bloodborne: The Board Game – Forsaken Castle Cainhurst Extra
description:
Expansion to Bloodborne: The Board Game, containing the Reiterpallasch Hunter. This expansion adds a single Hunter to Bloodborne: The Board Game to add another character for players to choose from. The Reiterpallasch Hunter makes good use of dodging in its base form to damage enemies and can use quicksilver shot in its transformed form to destroy weak enemies without retaliation.

This expansion contains a single character, including its character sheet, sidearm, and figure. It does not adjust the gameplay of the core game and does not add any campaigns.

Don't Catch a Crab is the perfect strategic card game for rowers. You can draw out of the draw pile, or strategically draft rowers from other players to get the perfect lineup. Be careful though as you can catch an ejector crab and remove yourself from the game if you pick the wrong card. This game is perfect for the down time at regattas, hangouts with friends, or even the bus ride. The game plays fast so you can fit it in real quick, or have multiple rounds to extend the fun. Happy rowing and don't catch a crab!

—description from the designer

Become a Super Stylist and put together cool outfits!

Four girlfriends use a common wardrobe, full of wonderful pastel-colored and well-combined black and white clothes.  They have a busy schedule: after school they often play sports, prepare for exams, and have been invited to a tea party.  They also have theater and festival tickets and end the long week with a pajama party.  Be their style consultant and help them choose the right outfit so that they can appear impeccably fashionable everywhere!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Hammer and Sickle: Hunger and Utopia in the Russian Civil War, 1918-1921
description:
Hammer and Sickle is a fast-paced, four-player asymmetric game covering one of the most desperate and world-shaping conflicts of the 20th century, in which four competing ideologies battled for supremacy over the former Russian Empire. Each season, players will alternate using Action and Commander cards to rally their forces, spread or clamp down on Agitation and Nationalism, and launch offensives into enemy territory. An event card played by each player at the start of the season will determine initiative order and have a unique effect on the game, while at the end of each season players must gather Food from the countryside (Sickles) to feed their factories (Hammers) and generate Firepower, or else risk rising Agitation and eventual Anarchist uprisings! After six seasons the player who has completed the most objectives and whose alliance is most successful will be victorious, determining the fate of Russia.

Components:
    One 22” x 25 ½” Mounted Game Board
    153 Regular-sized Playing Cards
    160 Wooden Pieces
    One and a half Full-color Countersheets
    Four Player Screens
    Two Custom 6-sided Dice (for non-player system)
    One Non-player Faction Foldout
    One Rulebook
    One Non-player Rulebook
    One Playbook
    One 3” Box

—description from the publisher

Arena of Mages is the first installment in a series of games released by Tehnolog.  It was released in 2001 in two large, boxed sets, along with 10 supplements.

The first boxed version included: 
-a playing field (made up of 4 sections)
-12 standee markers
-2 player aids
-72 soldier cards 
-48 artifact cards 
-18 event markers 
-2 game dice 
-rules

Excellent introduction to this genre of games, suitable for even the littlest ones (but not only) that is set to fill all that downtime you have during the day: pick up the pad and read.
