Introduction:Hedu (from Old English: Hēahþu, meaning "height") is a finite game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of a initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a set of pieces of the same color placed on the same cell. A single piece is also a stack.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.
     A group is a maximal set of stacks of the same color and height that are connected. A single stack is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stacks it contains.
     A region is a maximal set of connected empty cells, bounded by stacks of any color or the edge of the board.
     A group controls a region after comparing (i) the size and (ii) the height of all groups adjacent to the region, according to whether they are the largest or highest. If (i) does not determine who controls it, use (ii) for the largest groups. If (ii) does not determine it either, repeat the process with (i) and (ii) in that order for the next largest groups. If it is still not determined, the player with more adjacent stacks controls it.
     The value of a region is the height of the group that controls it.
     To increase is to add pieces on top of a stack.
     To reduce is to remove pieces from a stack and taking them off the board.
     A prisoner is a piece removed from the board when reducing a stack.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:


     Place a stack of your color on an empty cell adjacent to a region.
     Simultaneously reduce the height of all enemy stacks, then the height of all friendly stacks, according to the number of adjacent regions for each.
     Simultaneously increase the height of all stacks, both enemy and friendly, according to the number of adjacent regions for each.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stacks. At the end, each player calculates their score by summing the value of the regions they control, the number of captured prisoners, and half a point for the player with the largest group in both size and height.

The player with the highest score at the final position wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stack on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

				
				
					Notes:A special thanks to Coray Clark, who made a material contribution to the game rules, particularly in the hierarchy of region control, which prioritizes group size over height. While the original idea was to prioritize height over size, the current approach is better as it provides additional means of retaliation.

–description from designer

Embark on a thrilling adventure in the Star Wars universe with “Survive the Sarlacc”! Brace yourself as the menacing Sarlacc unfurls its tentacles and prepares to devour all who dare to enter the Great Pit of Carkoon. Will you be the last player standing?

Relive the iconic Sarlacc Pit battle scene from Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (Episode VI) like never before. Celebrating the 40th Anniversary of this legendary film, Survive the Sarlacc transports you back to Tatooine, where you can immerse yourself in the heart-pounding action and become a part of the epic battle.

Designed for Star Wars enthusiasts and true fans alike, this game is a must-have for your collection. Suitable for ages 8 and up, Survive the Sarlacc accommodates 2–6 players and offers an average gameplay time of just 15 minutes, ensuring fast-paced excitement for all.

Inside the box, you’ll find a range of captivating components, including 42 Defense cards, 15 Tentacle cards, 12 Action cards, 6 Character tokens, 6 Special Ability cards, 1 Almighty Sarlacc card, and a meticulously crafted Pit of Carkoon game board. Rest assured, the game is printed using FSC paper and soy inks, making it environmentally friendly and recyclable.

Players draw from the circle of cards placed around the Sarlacc Pit. Defence cards are for protection, action cards can help or hinder, hero cards inflict an attack, and tentacle cards will try to drag you into the pit.

But watch out, the greatest threat of all is the mouth of the Almighty Sarlacc!

Will you be the last player standing?

Gear up, choose your character, and prepare for an adrenaline-fueled struggle for survival in the jaws of the Sarlacc. Survive the Sarlacc is your ticket to an unforgettable Star Wars gaming experience. May the Force be with you!

Keep in mind that if the predators find you, you will lose all the fruit you have so far when you flee back home, so you will not be able to continue searching for the rest of the day.

Остров сокровищ (Treasure Island) a game about treasure hunting.  Players must first find three precious stones of the same color and bring them to the old pirate who will then give them a map.  They must then go and find the treasure. 

Players begin in one of the cities on the map.  They select a path to travel on and roll a die.  The number rolled is the total spaces they may move. When a player rolls a 4,5 or 6 they get a $100 bonus from the bank.

There are three modes of travel: land, sea, and air. Moving on land is free, moving by sea cost $200 per turn moving on it, and air cost $300 per turn.  To transition from one type of mode to another costs $100.  Air travel is from city to city.

Event cards (circular tokens) are gained by landing on them.  They can also be purchased by being in proximity to them and paying a fee of $100 times the distance to the card.  So, if I was three spaces away, I could pay $300 and get the card, or I could get closer and pay less.  Cards have different abilities and can be bad or good.  Some are worth cash, or cause the loss of cash, and some are colored precious stones.

Once the player acquires the three stones of the same type they must go to the pirate and obtain a map (card with a location on it).  Once they reach the designated location on the map, all players roll a die.  If the total value of the dice is even, then the map was real and the player wins.  if not, the map was fake, and they must start the process all over again and bring the pirate (swindler) another three stones.

—user summary

Byōdō helps people learn and develop mathematics through games and emotions. It is based on a Game System of numbered hexagonal tiles and polyhedral dice that aids in visualizing and manipulating mathematics in an entertaining and social way.

A Unique Hexagonal Universe
The hexagonal tiles of Byōdō are not just game pieces; they are gateways to a mathematical universe without end. With their unique shape, each tile can connect in countless ways, allowing you to create and solve mathematical challenges in ways you never imagined.

Awaken Your Mathematical Creativity
From simple sums to complex equations, Byōdō invites you to explore mathematical concepts in an entirely new way. There are no strict rules; you decide how to use the tiles to create patterns, solve problems, and develop your mathematical skills in an exciting manner.

Perfect for All Ages and Levels
Whether you're a student beginning your mathematical journey or a lover of numerical challenges, Byōdō caters to all levels. As you progress, the game grows with you, offering an educational and entertaining experience for the whole family.

Connect with the Byōdō Community
Join a global community of creative minds passionate about mathematics. Share your creations, participate in challenges, and discover new ways to play with Byōdō. The possibilities are truly endless when you join our community!

Take Byōdō Worldwide'
Thanks to our focus on quality and innovation, we've created a game that transcends borders. Take Byōdō everywhere and share the excitement of a unique gaming system with friends, family, and colleagues worldwide.

Your Mathematical Journey Begins Here
Get ready for an unparalleled gaming experience. Byōdō is not just a game; it's your gateway to a universe of limitless mathematical possibilities. Explore, create, and challenge your mind with Byōdō today!

Wacronyms is a quick-thinking party game designed for adults, focusing on creativity and humor. The game's theme revolves around forming witty and wild acronyms, abbreviations, and initialisms from given letters, often leading to hilarious and unexpected results. Players are presented with a variety of topics, ranging from familiar situations to wacky concepts and emotions, and are challenged to create acronyms using specific letters provided in the game.

The gameplay involves drawing Topic and Letter cards and using these letters to craft an acronym (referred to as a Wacronym) that relates to the given topic. Players have the freedom to choose the order of the letters, which adds a layer of strategy and creativity to the game. For instance, given the topic "A government agency or job that should exist" and the letters "BTC", players might come up with acronyms like "The Candy Bringer" or "Ban Corporate Taxes."

The goal of the game is to win rounds by impressing the judge with the most creative or amusing acronym. The first player to win five rounds is declared the champion. The game also features Special Round cards to vary the rules, adding an element of unpredictability and fun. "Wacronyms" is suitable for 3-12 players and can be played for as long as desired. The game's content, designed for adult players, encourages spontaneity and laughter, making it an ideal choice for parties and social gatherings

—description from the publisher

If you don't have enough money, use your goal cards instead or sell already bought items  for the second hand price. If you still cannot pay, you are bankrupt. boardgame: The Struggle For Zorn: The Red Blight
description:
The Struggle for Zorn: The Red Blight is a 1-5 player semi-cooperative fantasy wargame. Players collectively represent the various Houses of the Kingdom of Zorn, and these Houses have joined together to defeat the existential threat of the vile Red Blight. The Red Blight is an evil entity that has just launched an invasion of peaceful Slumbering Troll Valley, the northern-most province of the kingdom. The monsters of the Red Blight are now holding the citizens of the valley hostage and have absconded with the province’s valuables, treasures, and vital resources. The Houses have been summoned by King Rufus V of Zorn to launch a Royal Crusade to free the valley and rid it of this blight. The game’s players will each control a Commander, a Magic User, and a House Army, made up of various types of military units. Each player attempts to defeat enemy units and capture Treasure Tokens, and by so doing earn Glory Points. The player scoring the most Glory Points will be awarded with the title “Hand of the Crown” and win the game. However, players must cooperate and assist each other as well. If the Red Blight achieves certain conditions, all the players lose the game, regardless of their situation or amount of Glory Points. If players can stave off The Red Blight’s attempt to exploit the valley for four full days, the monsters will lose access to the malignant power that sustains them. If this evil has been successfully thwarted, peace and freedom will be restored once again to the valley and one of the intrepid Houses will gain the ultimate royal reward. Will you become Hand of the Crown, Sire?

-description from designer

ハイロークエスト (Hi & Low QUEST) is a card game from Japan for one to three players. In the game, the players are on a sword and sorcery quest, and use their adventurers and mages to fight a series of strange creatures as the explore a dungeon.

The players are trying to work out whether the next card revealed will be a higher or lower value than the current card. By successfully completing a run of cards, the players earn Quest Cards, which they can use to manipulate the card values when they are wrong.

The winner is the player with the most stars on the Quest Cards they have collected.

boardgame: Wings for the Baron: Deluxe Edition
description:
The Great War has been going on for nearly two years, and the Western Front has become a series of fruitless attacks and counterattacks. Germany must control the air to break the stalemate and win the war. The army turns to the aeroplane manufacturers to build the best planes to drive the Allies from the sky. Wings for the Baron gives you control of one of six companies, competing for lucrative contracts and the prestige of producing the best designs. Can you steer your business to produce the best fighters, bombers, and/or reconnaissance planes? Can you manage rising inflation, outwit your opponents through subterfuge and espionage, and lead your company to success?

Wings for the Baron is a 1 to 6 player game of economic and technological development in the German aeroplane industry during World War 1. The Standard Game focuses on German fighter development, and the Advanced Game adds development of bombers and recon planes. Experienced players can play the Standard Game in about 45 minutes and the Advanced Game in about an hour, making Wings for the Baron perfect for repeat play. The Deluxe GMT Edition adds to the gameplay of this beloved game by adding components and rules for a new sixth company (Siemens-Schuckert), as well as all-new art by artist Tim Allen and deluxe components.

The Standard Game of Wings for the Baron revolves around each company’s fighter efficiency as compared with the other German companies. The company with the most efficient fighters attempts to claim contracts first, while companies with less efficient fighters act later in the turn. If all the contracts are awarded and some companies have not rolled yet, they have to wait until the next turn (Hint…they need to improve the efficiency of their fighter so they roll earlier in the phase!). Companies earn money by using their factories to fulfill contracts. Each fulfilled contract earns a million papiermarks.

Wings’ Advanced Game adds two aeroplane types (recon and bombers), multiplying players’ choices. Recon and bombers enable the company to earn even more papiermarks (assuming they have factories that were not building fighters). Recon and bomber contracts, unlike fighter contracts, are unlimited—you can build all three types to earn papiermarks!

Each turn, a company has five actions they can perform: Build additional factories, conduct Espionage to learn the technologies from another company, increase your plane’s efficiency with new Design technology, conduct Research to find new technologies, or take your papiermarks to a neutral country and convert to gold by performing a Bank action. But there’s a catch: you can only perform a limited number of actions each turn! You will have to carefully combine these actions with your chosen Events, which can impact rival companies or benefit your own. Events range from obtaining a Talented Engineer for your company (which helps your Design rolls) to improving one of your designs by using technology obtained from captured Allied aeroplanes.

As the war rages on, the specter of Inflation lingers every turn. Inflation is unpredictable, and you may lose none, 25%, or 50% of your papiermarks every turn! Savvy owners will know when to use their actions to convert their papiermarks to Gold and when to risk Inflation. Inflation never causes you to lose your carefully stashed Gold! Additionally, the effects of the war are felt through the resolution of a War Status card which impacts Allied and German Morale, available contracts, and Allied plane technologies.

The game ends when one or both sides’ morale markers reach zero or when Inflation reaches Hyper-Inflation. Each company then determines their total wealth (papiermarks, Gold, plus such markers they got during play). The company with the most money is the winner!

Wings for the Baron also includes solitaire and 2-player variants designed by Ken Keller which lets you (and a friend) pit yourself against two (or three or four!) other Non-Player Companies (NPCs) in the Advanced Game. This solitaire variant is a tremendous challenge and uses simple procedures to run the NPCs. No flowcharts or complicated procedures necessary!

    Six Double-sided Player Boards
    One Mounted Double-sided Game Track
    Three Countersheets

    Six Wooden Cubes
    One Solo Player Aid

-description from publisher

description:
The Heart of Gamelyn Mercenary is an exceptional character renowned for its unparalleled expertise in safeguarding your troops at any expense. Owning this figure will not only make you the object of envy within your gaming circle, but more significantly, it will ensure your survival, giving you the opportunity to boast about your triumphs!

Contains 
• 1 Miniature
• 1 Mercenary Card

-description from Gamefound campaign

boardgame: Heroes of Land, Air & Sea: Two Worlds Game Board Mat
description:
This gaming mat boasts an extraordinary alternate new world map on the reverse side. This captivating addition will put your tactical skills to the test, offering countless hours of replayability. Immerse yourself in a whole new realm, where every move counts and every decision shapes your destiny.

Oversized 48" x 34" double-sided and stitched edged neoprene gaming mat.

—description from the publisher

ぐるぐるチャーム (GURUGURU Charm) is a trading card-style fighting game from Japan, with players fighting using traps, magic and weapons.

The players are dealt 20 cards from the deck and select up to 7 cards to set aside that will add to their attack. Then they select 7 hand cards to use in battle, with a character card with skills. The remaining cards are shuffled into a draw deck.

The card values are from 1 to 5, with higher values beating lower values, and 1 beating 5.

A card is turned up from the draw deck to set a weather condition for that round. Players can then choose a card to place face-down as a trap. Then they choose a card to fight with, and hold it face-down until all players are ready to reveal, with a shout of SENO!

The players can then activate their traps and also use magic in the combat. The strongest fighter wins the round and the losers mark up damage on their own score card.

Players then draw back to 7 cards in hard and begin a new round. If the deck is exhausted it is shuffled over.

When a player falls to zero hit points, they are out. The last one standing is the winner of the game.
user summary

With this game, a player can enjoy strategies using card battle deck construction, summoning, traps, and magic. Players use cards to battle against their opponents. By playing attack, trap, and magic cards from their hand, attacking their opponent with a summoned character, players try to be the first to reduce the opponent's Hit Points to 0. Through luck and strategy, even a weak character may be able to defeat a powerful enemy.

TCG(トレーディングカードゲーム)風、カードバトルデッキ構築、召喚、罠、魔法を使った戦略が味わえます！

このゲームは他のTCGと同じように、カードを使い 相手とバトルをします。
手札から攻撃、罠、魔法カードを出して、 召喚したキャラで相手を攻撃し、 先に相手のHPを0にするゲームです！
運と駆け引きによって弱いキャラクターでも、 強力な敵を倒すことができるかもしれません.

description by publisher

HVRTING is a Party Game to celebrate Pain.

HVRTING: The Extreme Haunt Card Game offers guests the opportunity to experience an immersive horror experience from the comfort of their own home. Two to eight friends can get together, gather items that most normal haunt-goers should already have, and customize the deck to their level of intensity–and then the fun begins. Your friends are now your enemies, and you must strategically work to obtain four resource cards by earning them, stealing them, or bartering for them–and escape. But while you are gathering the resource cards, The Director has placed a series of restraint, endurance, and torture cards among them to make you hurt.

—description from the publisher

Yes, Chef! is a unique skill and action game where kids check the order card, use their spatulas to flip their plates to match the dishes shown, then race to be the first to ring the bell and fill the order. But if you see a cockroach, you must slap that order card instead to squash the bug. The chef who collects the most order cards wins in this fast-paced matching game!

Includes: 12 Two-Sided Plates, 4 Spatulas, 26 Order Cards, 1 Service Bell, 1 Instruction Manual.

Time to go on vacation with your friends in Avanti! — but you will find out where you are traveling with the camper only if you combine the clues as to where you are NOT going! Whoever solves it fastest wins the most points...

Iconoclasm is a cooperative expansion for Eschaton. Through deck-building, players will be raising cults alongside one another, forging an alliance to wage war against the Inquisition. As the cults are rallied, players will amass armies in the realm to fortify the Dark One’s empire. It is imperative that the cult leaders work together to hold the Inquisition at bay and ensure the arrival of the fires of Armageddon.

In an effort to out-maneuver the rallying cults, the Inquisition will make strategic incursions across the realm, strengthening their resolve and bolstering their resources. As their grip on the realm tightens, so too does their tactical advantage and ferocity. They will stop at no end to see the Citadel fall and the Dark One banished to the void.

—description from the publisher

Fairy Tale Campaign Trail is the fantastical party game of ridiculous debates and nonsense arguments! You, the player, are running for mayor of your own fantasy village, and must use the silly solution cards in your hand to solve the totally-not-made-up issues that face your citizens!

Each round, the moderator draws a problem card and reads it aloud to the table. Each player then selects a solution card from their hand, and players take turns to debate why their ridiculous idea is, in fact, the best way forward. But each debate comes with a twist: the moderator can choose a modifier card to challenge other players in weird and wacky ways. You may find yourself talking like pirates about the merits of bee grenades, or singing the praises of three gnomes in a trench coat.

The first to win five debates wins the game!

-description from publisher
New questions to Smart10, now with questions about celebrities.

In this mini-expansion, timing is everything. When the drawbridge is down, you can move from the road into the city to score additional points.

This expansion was developed for the Carcassonne base game, and its rules still apply. You can combine The Drawbridges with other expansions.

A card game very similar to Career Poker. Starting with the first player, then in turn order you play one or more cards of the same value from your hand. A following player must overbid by playing at least the same quantity of cards. If you play the same quantity of cards, the value of each of your card must be higher. If you increase the quantity of cards, the value may be lower. The first player who got rid of all his cards gets the figure "aunt Lisbeth", the most valuable personality. The last player gets "neighbor Albert" and must shuffle, distribute and discard all the cards during the next complete round ("manche" or "Durchgang"). The rest of the cards that couldn't evenly be distributed  is distributed to the player with "neighbor Albert". After several complete rounds, the player who hold the most time "aunt Lisbeth" wins and the player who hold the most time "neighbor Albert" loses.

—user summary

boardgame: Butterfly Effect: A solitaire weather deduction game for rainy days.
description:
You love to look at the clouds. You always have.
They call you a dreamer. Don't listen!
One day they'll know what you are:
A true expert in your field!

The weather is represented by a small deck of cards. Each card shows a weather (sunny, cloudy, rainy or windy).
On turn, you assign one of your forecast tokens to any of the four weather types and flip the top card of the deck.
If you're wrong, you lose the token.
To win the game you have to forecast the weather correctly seven times in a row.

- Are you joking? That's random! You're just guessing!
You'd better not tell a meteorologist!

But of course there is more to it...

—description from the designer

World Wonders: Mundo Wonders Pack adds nine new wondrous monuments from the ancient world to World Wonders, along with a new gameplay mode. This mode features nine cards that add even more strategy to the scoring of new monuments, allowing for higher scores depending on the objectives.

The expansion also comes with cards to use the new wonders in the normal game mode.

According to the legend, Chactún can be found deep in the jungle. An ancient city protected by a fearsome ancestral guardian named Chac Bolay.

Avoid the dangers of the jungle, overtake your opponents and be the first to discover the mysteries of Chactún.

Prepare the expedition, avoid the dangers of the jungle, and overtake your opponents.

Roll the dice and try to get a result that is within the range of your top path card. You can use the cards in your hand to modify the result.

If you roll doubles a random jungle effect will be unleashed on the entire table.

If you roll a 7, Chac Bolay, the guardian of the lost city, will jump to a new player.

If you succeed, you can set aside the path card and decide whether to continue playing or take the set aside cards to your hand.

If you don't succeed you will lose your turn but you can try to draw a card from another player.
 The goal is to be the first to discard the entire path deck.

-description from publisher

Legendarium is a Roll and Write game of strategy and risk taking. It's a fast paced game, taking on average 15-30 minutes per game, allowing you to easily fit it into your game night, or to play it multiple times (we usually play at least two games).

You will forge the story of the rise and decline (and possible destruction) of 5 fantasy kingdoms through 4 ages of myth, scoring points as your kingdoms act in each age. You will also initiate Mythic Moments to add additional flavor (and points) to your myth that you are building.

Each round, you will choose from the available dice to grow one of your kingdoms.  Each successive die must be higher or the kingdom will start to decline.  Points are given based on the number of boxes filled for each kingdom.  There are four ages to the game.  as soon as any player fills all the boxes for any kingdom, the whole group will proceed to the next age.

In Legendarium Folktale you'll manage the rise and fall of 5 folktale kingdoms such as fairies and goblins. It increases the inter-player dynamic as you will be changing each other's scorecards through mischief and favors.

This expansion gives a new kingdom sheet with additional rules that encourage more player interaction.

In Legendarium Primordia you'll be reaching back further in time, to the age of Titans, Gods, Elementals, and Eldritch Horrors, near the dawn of creation. These factions are never destroyed, but can be sealed away for an age. In this era of creation, when the world was young, what kind of a world will your creation myth take?

This expansion gives a new kingdom sheet with additional rules.  Kingdoms can no longer be destroyed but can be locked away for an age, preventing additional scoring.

Use your cunning to place those loyal to you in positions of power within the king's court. Assassinate rivals and forge deals with other players to secure the votes you need to gain influence over the king and win the game.

Each round, a new position within the king's court becomes available. Players may put forward a suitable courtier as a candidate or draw cards from the courtier deck to strengthen their hand for the next round. The candidate with the most votes will fill the empty position and give the player who put forward that candidate influence each round. Players with candidates up for the vote make deals with other players to secure votes or play secret cards to turn the outcome by force. The player with the most influence will win the game.

Court of Influence is a game of manipulation, deal-making, and statecraft.

—description from the designer

Diner flip is a quick, 18 card, 2 player game where players try to get the most points by bidding on cards and adding them to their scoring tableau. The trick is that winning or losing the bid determines what card you get and also its orientation in your tableau. Try to match diner cuisine icons, and get the most of each type of music record to score points. Plan your strategy but be prepared to alter it as your opponent may take the card you want. Play two games and the player with the highest score wins.

-description from designer

Race to collect a winning hand of colorful Galaxy Children and Dracos in this all-ages card and dice game. But Explorers beware! There are dangers lurking in the depths of space, and your decisions – combined with fate – may save or doom fellow players.

Created, developed, and illustrated by Sarah Rebecca, this 80 card, tarot-sized deck comes ready to play with instruction booklet and one D10 with no deckbuilding required. This is the 2nd edition of the game, which features a beautiful and sturdy game box that can hold the original 80 card deck, instruction booklet, dice, and up to 2 expansion packs (sold separately).

boardgame: Carry Me Plz! A Pig Hinders the Hero Party
description:
Send your crew to go on adventures. The fewer efforts your crew contributes to the mission, the earlier you can choose the reward! Play the right crew at the right time, and try to make a fortune by being the laziest team!

Preparation

Shuffle the Adventure cards to form an Adventure deck.
    Each player takes one set of 8 character cards and one Player Aid card. Shuffle your Character cards to form your personal deck.
    Draw three cards from your deck as your hand cards. You are ready to play!

Gameplay

The game is played over several rounds until the Adventure deck has five or fewer cards left. The player with the most gold wins!
Each round has 3 phases:

Reveal the New Adventure
    Reveal 2 Adventure cards from the deck and place them in the center of the table for all players to see. 
    If there is only 0 or 1 Gold card, reveal additional Adventure cards until there are 2 or more Gold cards.
    Send out Your Crew
    (1) Each player chooses one card from their hand and places it facedown in front of themselves. When everyone is ready, reveal the cards. These characters are the adventure team for this round.
    (2) Check the values (based on the player count) on the Adventure cards, the highest value is the Risk Value for the current round.
    (3) If the total Strength of the played Character cards is equal to or higher than the Risk Value, the adventure is a success. Otherwise, the adventure fails.


Success
    Following the initiative value from low to high (0 -> 7), players take turns to pick one of the Adventure cards (Gold or Goblin) as their reward. Keep your reward faceup in front of yourself.
    - If there is a tie for the initiative value, those tied players are SKIPPED. They don’t have a chance to pick the Adventure card. (Their Strength were counted, though.)
    - If the Adventure cards are all taken, the remaining players won’t have a chance to pick the card.

Failure
    The phase ends.

End of Round
    If there are still Adventure cards left on the table, remove the one with the most << from the game.
    Each player discards their played card to their personal discard pile. Draw cards from their personal deck to refill their hand to 4 cards.
    Start a new round from Phase 1.


Game End
If there are 5 or fewer Adventure cards left in the deck after the (A) Revel the New Adventure phase, the game ends after that round. Tally your total Victory Points (gold and Goblins). The player with the most VP wins!
If the Adventure deck runs out and there are fewer than 2 Gold cards revealed on the table, the game ends immediately.

Components:
Character cards x48 (8 cards x 6 sets)
Adventure cards x30 (Gold x18, Goblin x12)
6-player Adventure cards x6
Player Aid cards x18 (6 cards each in EN, JP, CH)

boardgame: Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory – Extended Edition
description:
This Extended Edition includes Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory base game + Crisis & Control expansion.


Put another way, the Crisis & Control "expansion" includes the components from the Kickstarter edition that were omitted from the retail edition. This Extended Edition is the Kickstarter Edition:


    570 Cards (325 in regular edition + 245 from Crisis & Control)
    248 Cardboard tokens (222 + 26)
    2 rule books (1 + 1)



Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory base game

The Nation is in disarray and a war is waging between the classes. The working class faces a dismantled welfare system, the capitalists are losing their hard-earned profits, the middle class is gradually fading and the state is sinking into a deep deficit. Amidst all this chaos, the only person who can provide guidance is... you. Will you take the side of the working class and fight for social reforms? Or will you stand with the corporations and the free market? Will you help the government try to keep it all together, or will you try to enforce your agenda no matter the cost to the country?

Hegemony is an asymmetric politico-economic card-driven board game for 2-4 players that puts you in the role of one of the socio-economic groups in a fictional state: The Working Class, the Middle Class, the Capitalist Class and the State itself.

The Working class controls the workers. The Capitalist class controls the companies. The Middle class combines elements from both the Working class and the Capitalist. It has workers who can work in the Capitalist's companies but it can also build companies of its own, yet smaller. Finally the State is trying to keep everyone happy, providing benefits and subsidies when needed but trying also to maintain a steady income through taxes to avoid going into debt.

While players have their own separate goals, they are all limited by a series of policies that affect most of their actions, like Taxation, Labor Market, Foreign Trade etc. Voting on those policies and using their influence to change them is also very important. Through careful planning, strategic actions and political maneuvering, you will do your best to increase the power of your class and carry out your agenda. Will you be the one to lead your class to victory?

Hegemony is heavily based on actual academic principles such as Social-Democracy, Neoliberalism, Nationalism and Globalism, and allows players to see their real world applications through engaging gameplay. There are many ways to achieve hegemony- which one will you take?

—description from the publisher


Crisis & Control

You have successfully led your class to victory... but the battle of ideologies is far from over! The old world is in crisis, and the new world struggles to regain control. Now is the time to make a difference!

Crisis & Control offers new challenges and additional game modes that will test your governance skills like never before! Crisis Response cards and Alternative Events add more diversity to your games, class-specific Hidden Agendas incentivize new politico-economic strategies, and the Automas provided allow you to experience all of the game’s roles, no matter the player count!

—description from the rulebook

Crisis & Control contains all the gameplay material that was given in the form of stretch goals during Hegemony's Kickstarter campaign. All that content was included in the Extended Edition of Hegemony which is what the campaign's backers received.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Rondeli is a solo game in which you take the role of a new manager at the famous restaurant Rondeli. Actions on a rondel are taken using the mancala mechanism. Play and utilize multi-use cards to feed hungry customers and make it through the day with as many fed as possible.

—description from the designer

Snille & Skrivkramp is a Swedish board game about academic intrigues. It is a satirical strategy game. Players control a university with a bunch of professors and PhD's, vying for positions and publishing the most prestigious articles before their opponents to win.

Each players use action cards to play their 6 persons on the same 3 action spaces, Education (giving money), Socializing (giving more action cards), and Research (giving "writing time", used to publish articles which score victory points). All persons are played in stacks, and most of the actions cards revolves around placing and manipulating positions on these stacks. All resource gains are dependent on how many and which persons are on a certain action space. You will always give your opponents some resources, and enabling their future turns, so the main challenge of the game is to navigate this with strategy and timing.

Some small examples of the satirical part of the theme:
Among the cards to play are "defamation campaign", "Sauna with the head of department" or "new coffee machine".
Among articles to write are "article about the pointlessness of everything", "incomprehensible article that seems intelligent", or "AI-generated article".

The game plays until someone has published a fixed number of articles (decided before start of play, allowing for different lengths of the game). Then everyone plays a single round, and then the player with most victory points on their articles win.

—description from the designer

The International Game of Justice is an educational card game based on the case law of the International Court of Justice, the Permanent Court of International Justice and the Permanent Court of Arbitration. The IGJ is designed for law students and international legal enthusiasts who desire to engage with international case law in a playful manner. However, the card game can also be played by and with non-lawyers if one ignores the advanced rules.

Each IGJ set consists of 55 cards (bridge size) and is delivered in its own paper box. The Jessup Editions differ from the Standard Editions in design only.

Tra Asburgo e Borboni: Camposanto 1743 is a two player light-weight Tactical level board-wargame.

Para Bellum XII presents a wargame that uses a simple and original system to simulate a 18th century little-known battle: Camposanto 1743.

The two players have a certain number of Order Points available and must activate their wings, one at a time.
The combat occurs between two lines that fire at each other until one of the two retreats or rout.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
'Schlock! B-Movie Magnate' is a word drafting game in which players assume the role of B-movie makers during the golden age of Hollywood, racing to create outrageous low-budget films for release to hungry audiences.

Players will compete in the Backlot to get the word and picture tiles they need to make their movies. A neat mechanic allows you to trade off between turn order and getting more tiles or better audience. Choose the right tiles to attract your audience...and creatively put together the most fun Science Fiction, Horror and Western B-movie titles!

When ready, players will release their movies, maybe explaining a little of the crazy plot..."Ancient Planet of the Invisible Virus...in 3D! " - pardon? Players are awarded an opening night bonus which decreases as the game goes on...Do you release now and get a good opening night, or wait to make a better movie? - Your choice!

When a player releases their third movie, the end of the game is triggered. The round is completed and other players complete their remaining movies. Movies are then scored according to how well the movie matches each of the three types of audience. Players may have fun rating each other's movies and then the player with the most Fame wins.

—description from the publisher

In Arctic Ignited, immerse yourself in a dieselpunk saga set in the frozen expanse of 1921. Navigate the frigid battlegrounds where every move shapes the destiny of the icy continent. Construct a formidable unit deck featuring Infantry, rivet Mechs, and Artillery cards. Handpick your starting deck and units, aligning them with your strategic vision for the Arctic.

Engage in strategic Actions—attack, move, conquer, and purchase units—against the relentless Arctic storms. Roll the dice to determine the outcome, shift units strategically, and convert the base of the opponent to your faction's control. Optimize your deck's composition, thinning it at strategic intervals to ensure a finely tuned draw deck for the challenges that lie ahead.

Arctic Ignited promises a riveting blend of strategy, chance, and deck-building finesse in a dieselpunk dystopia. Will you conquer the territories and emerge as the master tactician of the frozen battlefield? Your destiny awaits amidst the howling winds and icy landscapes of Arctic Ignited!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Market Mayhem: The Action Packed Stock Trading Game
description:
Introducing Market Mayhem – The Action Packed Stock Trading Board Game that mixes finance basics with fun gameplay. Strategy, luck, and savvy trading skills collide in this epic quest for stock market domination! With game rounds lasting 90 minutes, diverse investment strategies, thrilling Market Mover Spaces and Market Mayhem Cards that shift stock prices, it's a race to the top!

Our dynamic gameplay also comes alive with its unique Market Mayhem Investor Interaction Spaces! These spaces elevate the game, adding layers of unpredictability and strategic decision-making. From System Hack disruptions to Tax Day fees and Short Squeeze manipulations, each space brings a unique challenge.

Market Mayhem is a game where finance meets fun! Immerse yourself in the thrill of strategic investments, calculated risks, and unpredictable market moves.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mini Express: Map Pack 2 – Japan and Spain
description:
Mini Express Map Pack 2 - Japan & Spain contains a double-sided game board with new and challenging rules for Mini Express.

Japan

The Japanese National Railways was privatized and broken into 6 regional passenger railway companies in 1987. In the Japan map, players try to build new lines connecting the 6 newly formed Japan Railways (JR) companies. With a bold expansive map that covers all of Japan from Okinawa to Hokkaido, players can build ferries, bridges, and underwater tunnels to connect the 4 main islands of Japan.

Spain

Experience the Spanish railway's incredible transformation from 1975 to the present day! This map recreates the journey from the last steam locomotive to the AVE, the longest High Speed Rail network in Europe. Collect demand tiles to trade for bonuses, and experience the excitement of the transition from regional trains to the AVE.

ガーリックガールズ (garlic girls) is a Kalah (mancala)- style game from Japan.

The players, as junior high school girls getting hungry after school, enter a mysterious Chinese restaurant called "Mancala-tei".
You can freely add toppings to create the ideal ramen, but take care to avoid eating too much garlic served by the hospitable manager and losing points!

On your turn, take your player token together with all the garlic tokens from one of the seven mancala plates arranged in a circle.
Then place these garlic tokens, one at a time, in clockwise order from the next plate, and place your player token on the last plate. Then you can draw a topping card from the top of the card pile next to that plate.

To achieve a high score, arrange the topping cards you have acquired in such a way that the patterns on the cards are linked on both sides of each card. When you do this, any garlic tokens remaining in your hand will increase your garlic level.

You lose points if your garlic level exceeds the upper limit or if your have 10 or more garlic tokens in your hand, so take care.

プレイヤーは放課後のお腹を空かせた女子中学生の1人として不思議な中華料理屋『満唐亭（Mankaratei）』が舞台となります。
自由に具材をトッピングし、理想のラーメンを完成を目指す訳ですが、サービス精神旺盛な店長から振る舞われるニンニクを食べ過ぎて、失点を得ないようにしましょう！
手番では円形に並べられた７枚のマンカラ皿から自身の駒とニンニク駒を受け取りります。
隣のマンカラ皿から順にニンニク駒を１枚ずつ置いていき、自分が欲しいカードが置かれたマンカラ皿に自身のコマを入れます。
手に入れたカードにはトッピングが描かれており、この絵柄が左右で繋がるように並べることで高得点を得られます。
このとき、手元にニンニク駒が残っているとニンニクゲージが溜まってしまいます。
このゲージが一定の数値に達する、もしくはニンニク駒を１０枚以上持っていると失点を得てしまうので、注意が必要です。
user summary
publishers: Yureru tsubomi wo mederu kai (揺れる蕾を愛でる会)

description:
Welcome to the unique show of Clap, where your voice harmony, rhythm and melody will be the stars. Do you have no rhythm? Don't worry, it'll be fun!

Choose one player to be the metronome, they'll be in charge of keeping the tempo and dealing the cards. The metronome chooses the cards according to their difficulty and shuffles them.
The first times you play, we suggest starting with value 3 cards.

The metronome deals one card facing up to each player and starts marking a time signature (one, two, three, four) repeating it throughout the round. This will mark the rhythm to follow.

After the first time signature (one, two, three, four) the rest of the players will clap or hit as indicated on their card at the same time.

In each time signature, before number three, the metronome will give a new card to one of the players. Those will have to switch between their notes from now on. For each number of the time signature, they will sing a note. A player shall never have two cards more than another.

If at any time, the metronome detects a mistake, stops the song shouting 'CLAP!'. All the players keep their card with the highest score, except the one who made the mistake, who gives their highest card to the metronome.

If nobody makes any mistakes and all the players get to ^play their 4 cards during a time signature, each one keeps their card with the highest score and the metronome doesn't keep any.

The remaining cards are shuffled again and the player on the right of the previous metronome becomes the new one.

The game ends when all the players have played as a metronome once. The one with more points wins.

The expansion for Anno 1800 - The Board Game advances industrialization and offers new possibilities and combo moves. Innovation cards and gears increase efficiency, airships promote trade, expansion and influence, patents protect your own industries, and new contract cards open up entirely new strategies. The expansion includes a solo mode and is only playable with the base game. Discover Martin Wallace's Anno 1800 game now with even more possibilities and intense fun.

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Who can steal faster than a shadow? In Lucky, the entire crew is gathered to split up the huge haul of gold, silver, jade, pearls, and gemstones...but everyone wants the biggest haul of themselves, of course. This requires the right tactics and dice luck.

You all take turns placing your treasure cards in the middle of the table. Now the person whose turn it is rolls up to five dice, depending on the number of cards on display that are to be won. If the roll succeeds, the treasures from the center or those of the other players become their own. Treasures are never safe from the others, though, and can be stolen again...

In this game, you will play the role of a scheming hacker, hired by a space agency, with the intention of using it for your own benefit. Your mission is simple: operate to discover valuable and strange mineral deposits on the arid alien landscape. But be careful! While looking for treasures, you could find remainder of old missions from your own space agency, which failed and left space junk all over the alien ground. You must avoid these failures from being discovered, while competing for being the player getting the location with the highest number of mineral deposits.

Choose between sending a command to Curiosity or placing a study object in the area. At the end of the round, only the third or fourth command sent at each coordinate will be processed by Curiosity.

All the pieces of study that curiosity comes across will be revealed.

Tactically choose your sprays. Find the best combination to paint the most creative work and beat your opponent in this Graffiti Duel.

Choose from your spray cards to build your 3x3 grid, the player whose spray column is closest to the value of the mural will win their points.

Combine spray and make scoring combos to be the best graffiti artist in this Graffiti duel.

ふしぎ商店街 (Fushigi Shotengai) is a game from Japan, themed on the very familiar covered shopping street that is common across the whole of Japan.

There are two games that can be played, a racing game かけっこ(Kakkeo)and a memo game where you are finding tags at night. As the game progresses, you can make combos that speed you along.

For the racing game, each turn, each player chooses people in the shopping district who will lend their help. Some characters will let you advance a few squares, some will steal money from other players, and some will use that money to advance the pieces all at once, and depending on the character's power, exciting developments will unfold at every turn!!
description by publisher

How to play かけっこ (Kakekko) racing game.

Place the tiles that correspond to the starting point, bus stop, and jewellery box on the daytime board and arrange the remaining shop tiles (daytime) in a random order. Place 5 jewel pieces in the treasure box squares. 
Shuffle the Resident cards and place them face down next to the board along with the money chips. Place all player pieces at the starting point.

Depending on the number of players, each player chooses two Resident cards as helpers, so that other players do not know.

Players then go through the Resident cards chosen in numerical order, from 1 to 9. Where players have chosen the same kind of helper, such as borrowing money, the lower value goes first.

When a Resident helps by moving the player piece backwards or forwards along the street, the player takes the action on the space they land on. If this causes them to advance to another tile, they continue with the action given there. If a player moves through one of the jewellery boxes, they collet the jewel they find there.

The game ends when all the jewels are taken, with the most valuable total being the winner.
user summary

Vérité ou conséquence 18+ is a game in which players must spin the roulette wheel to determine whether they will have to answer intimate questions or complete dares otherwise they will see their character lose clothes. The first player to find themselves with a naked character loses the game!

boardgame: ねこのひたい。(Neko no Hitai)
description:
ねこのひたい。(Neko no Hitai) is a children's deduction game from Japan. The title means The Cat's Forehead, which is very small, so there isn't much room to hide things there. There are only 12 hiding spaces in the game.

In this game, players compete by hiding and searching for fish, and the points earned depend on where the piece was hidden.

The 12 number tiles are arranged at random, and everyone get a fish in their colour. All the players look away whist the youngest player starts by hiding their fish under a number. The player searching chooses a tile, and if the fish is there, the hiding player gets the points. Play continues clockwise, with more fish being hidden (a player is out until their fish is found).

Alternately, a fish one can be hidden which yields double points.

The winner is the player with the most points by the end of the game. 
user summary

Ransack is an exciting card game where players become crafty thieves trying to escape a dungeon with the most treasure. Players use cards to dodge monsters, steal from others, and strategize their way to victory.

The goal? Be the thief with the most wealth by the end of the game. It's all about clever moves and outsmarting opponents to claim the top spot as the ultimate treasure hunter.

Ransack is a 20 minutes game game for 3-6 players with 
90 cards featuring, weapons, objects and monsters.
10 double sided Treasure cards.

—description from the designer

Welcome to the tense and strategic world of Falcon Tactics! In this tactical board and card 
game, you will be transported into the heart of tactical operations involving police forces and 
terrorists in exciting confrontations.
In Falcon Tactics, you will experience the adrenaline of covert operations, the need for team 
coordination, and the importance of making quick strategic decisions. Each match is a battle 
for control, where military police officers must defend against terrorists determined to plant 
deadly bombs.

Get ready to dive into a world where every move can make the difference between victory 
and defeat. With deadly weapons, tactical grenades, and strategy cards at your disposal, you 
will have to overcome obstacles and challenges on a modular map that offers countless 
possibilities.

In this rulebook, you will find all the information you need to dive into the gameplay of Falcon 
Tactics. From map building to team combat, we’re here to guide you every step of the way.
Get ready for action, for drama, for relentless strategy. In Falcon Tactics, only the most 
cunning and coordinated will emerge victorious. Do you have what it takes to lead your team 
to victory?

Get ready to become a true Falcon Tactics!

To play, in addition to the deck, you will need 2 (two) 6 (six) sided dice, 8 markers to 
represent your Terrorist and Counter-Terrorist characters, with a pencil or Pen, and print the PDF 
sheet for each captain and 72 Small Tokens for the sheets of Terrorists and Counter-Terrorists.

6-sided dice(D6): They are commonly found in other games, or even in convenience stores, 
but can also be replaced by cell phone applications.

8 Tokens: You can represent your characters with miniatures from our collections of the 
the featured card that comes with every edition of “Falcon”, or specific miniatures (sold separately), 
or with any other miniature, or small object, that fits on top of a quadrant of cards, such as: 
game pins, coins, bean berries, etc.

Pencil or Pen: Use to mark on the captain’s sheet.
Printable PDF Captain Sheet: In the manual, there is a very important printable sheet for 
playing and marking the crucial notes for the game to work. (The form can be found at the end 
of the Manual in the extra items for printing.*

72 Small Tokens: You can use any object that fits the markings on the Counter-Terrorist or 
Terrorist sheets with these markers

This is part a series of games that was in Japanese Tactics magazine issues 48, 50, and 52.

In issue 48, the players take on the roles of either rabbits or rats.  This is a kind of RPG, and the battlefield can be whatever the Game Master decides, so you may be trying to get sustenance or to other objectives.  However, in your way are many obstacles. For the rabbit there is the farmer and fox, and for the rat there is the lady of the farm and various birds and owls.  There are additional foes that can be thrown into the mix, such as a squirrel, cat, dog, mole, beaver, snake, even barbed wire. Each of these had their own ability stats which were resolves using one or more d6.

The later issues featuring the game added different complexities and critters.

All issues came with a map and paper tokens, with the rules and charts being inside the magazine.  The tokens were standees.  Assembly was required.

—user summary

From Japanese Wikipedia:

A TRPG in which the player plays a small animal such as a rabbit or a cat as a player character (PC). Other than the fact that PCs are animals, PCs do not have a common purpose. What the PC needs to do depends on the scenario prepared by the game master, and at one time the goal of the game is to hunt for survival, and sometimes the goal of the game is to explore the city of humans.

According to the design concept defined in the rulebook, the atmosphere that Rabbits & Rats is aiming for is "The Rabbits of Watership Down", "The Adventurers" (Gamba's Adventures), "The Adventures of Miss Bianca", and "Rocky Chuck the Mountain Mouse". ", "Dr. Raccoon and His Friends", "Seaton's Book of Animals", etc.

In this game, the player characters are able to communicate with each other even if they are of different races due to the common language of the animals. (However, you must succeed in the skill assessment.)

The player character is determined by choosing a preset animal race. Each animal race has its own set of stats and initial skills, making it a kind of archetype-based character-making system.

The animal races in the rulebook are rabbits, badgers, rats, squirrels, cats, dogs, otters, sea otters, snakes, and moles.

The act judgment belongs to the percentage roll. If two decahedral dice are rolled to obtain a value in the range of 10~1, and the value is less than or equal to the skill value used for the action judgment, the judgment is considered successful.

Many of the skills are unique to animals, and some of them are not often found in regular TRPGs. (For example, when performing actions that are common sense for humans but should not be performed by wild animals, such as "crossing a river using something similar to a boat instead of swimming," it is necessary to succeed in determining the act of "conceivable" skills.)

As the Infinity Rogue, you're more than a spacefaring adventurer - you're a luck-pushing, hell-raising maverick with a knack for diving head-first into the galaxy's most perilous pits. This time, you've crash-landed into the depths of the infamous Space Dungeon.

Brace yourself to fight, dodge, and endure the relentless onslaught. Fortune favours the bold? Maybe, but push Lady Luck too far, and she's ready with a cosmic-sized sledgehammer to deliver a brutal reality check.

Objective
Find the Key Card: Navigate the Dungeon Level, exploring rooms to find the crucial Key Card.
Defeat the Boss: After you've got the Key Card, head for the exit. However, you'll find a challenging end-of-level Boss waiting to test your skills.
Win the Game: Battle your way through all three Space Dungeon levels to escape and claim victory!


     Tactical Card-Based Gameplay: Push-your-luck Blackjack style. Build your player deck with legendary items and enhance your odds of survival. Tailor it with each shuffle, balancing risk and reward to carve your path to victory.
     Roguelike dungeon crawler: You’ll face Brutal Opponent Threats (BOTs), crafted to be almost sentient, ensuring every battle is not just a challenge but a duel for survival.
     Unending Roguelike Journey: Every game and every Dungeon Level of Space Dungeon is randomly generated, and with four levels of difficulty, it guarantees infinite unique adventures.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Mini Express: Expansion Map Pack 1 & Map Pack 2
description:
ONE box TWO expansions FOUR maps!

With the Mini Express Map Pack 1 & 2 Expansions, your total map count is now up to six!  We invited local designers from the United Kingdom, Taiwan, Japan, and Spain to enhance the Mini Express experience.  Each new map brings new landscapes and special rules to explore, and several mini-expansion rule modules that can be used on any map.

EXCLUSIVE Wooden Components!

This Map Pack Collectors Box is a must-have for any Mini Express enthusiast!  Enhance your game with beautiful wooden pieces designed to enhance your gaming experience.  They can be used in place of the cardboard tiles, or as additional supplements to increase your table presence and upgrade your gaming experience!

Bet x Battle is a 10-card Poker-esque bluffing microgame. Each round, you play 2 cards from your hand face-down, then start Betting: You can raise, fold, or call. Y’know, the usual Poker stuff.

Then Battle: flip your cards over to see who won! Don’t just play your best cards all at once, though, because any cards that you play get passed to your opponent!

How many moves ahead can you read? Maneuver & position, and bluff & bluster your way to victory!

—description from the designer

Wine of Sicily is a battle of wits for 2-6 players, distilled down to one single card. The card represents a goblet of wine. Its reverse side has two orientations: Poisoned and Unpoisoned. Each round, the Sommelier takes the Goblet and chooses whether or not to Poison it by rotating it to the associated orientation, then hands it to another player face-down. That player can choose to Drink from the Goblet, or peek at the card and Pass it to another player, who then chooses between the two options, but watch out: If all other players Pass, the Sommelier must drink from the Goblet themself!

If you drink poisoned wine twice, you are eliminated from the game. You win the game if you drink unpoisoned wine twice or all other players have been eliminated. Will you leave the goblet unpoisoned knowing that no one else will want to drink from it? Or will you poison it, knowing that someone else will know that you know that no else will want to drink from it and thus will want to drink from it?

—description from the designer

Attention, citizens! Your city is currently under attack by a UFO! The codes necessary to destroy the UFO are hidden in a secret Factory. Your mission is to retrieve these codes and install them while being transported by the UFO. This is the only way to achieve victory! Exercise caution, and good luck to all; you'll need it!

The codes are situated at the city's center. Each player must move their runners closer to the center while the UFO also traverses the city grid. At the end of each turn, the UFO initiates its beam. If you happen to be in the wrong location, you'll be beamed up and out of the game!

Additionally, you have the option to hide your runners in a house for one turn to remain safe and hidden. It's a race against time; the game ends once the correct code is installed in the UFO while being beamed up. Thus, you must approach the UFO while it moves, which isn't easy as it beams each round!"

description from the designer

boardgame: The Great Australian International Car Race Game
description:
The Great Australian International Car Race Game is a fast moving car race for up to 6 players.

Each player places his car on the starting grid and then rolls the die to see how far he moves.  Some squares on the track have events printed on them.  The first to the finish line wins!

Published in Adelaide, South Australia.

boardgame: Santa's Workshop (Second Edition): 1st Christmas Promo Pack
description:
5 Promo Toy Cards
5 Reindeer Cards (Randolph) - (2 standard, 3 advanced)

Randolph - Randolph allows to you to place another elf right way.

A game adapted from the challenges of the youtubers Mc Fly and Carlito. With the party game Bonsoir! YouTubers Mc Fly and Carlito have not been idle. In this Party Game adapted from their videos, you will find their whole crazy universe

The different games
By playing Bonsoir!, players embark on a fun and lively journey punctuated by crazy challenges. Each square on the board represents a challenge to be overcome: 
"We call random people": Call a random person and say a forced word in the conversation.

The "Trivia Contest": Tell a story to players who have to decide if it's true or not. Earn 100 likes per wrong answer.

The "Too Honest": Raise your hand if you've committed this sin and earn 100 likes if the majority wins.

The "Blind Test of Synonyms": Guess the title of a song from synonyms.

The "Double Baccalaureate": You have 30 seconds to give as many words starting with the same letter from a theme.

The "Overrated or Underrated": From a list of words, you must choose one and say whether you find it to be overrated or not. The other players must vote. The goal is to get as many players as possible to agree with you.

The "Mix'N'Twist": You have the time of an hourglass to validate the most "Mix'N'Twist" which consists of combining two delicious foods separately but disgusting when they are together.

The "Rock, Leaf, Toucan": Challenge the player of your choice and win two rounds of this game made famous by McFly and Carlito.

The "Cheh!, Deso not deso, Seum" boxes: The player who lands on one of these squares must put 100, 200 or 300 likes respectively into the Thumb Market prize pool.

The "Compliment" box: All players must give a sincere compliment to the player on this box and give them 100 likes each.

The "Thumb Market" square: The player who lands on this square, earns all the likes present on the square.

TORX is a mind-bending, brain-twisting game with attitude. It' is a command game similar to Bop It & Brain Warp, but much spunkier and very different. TORX has four different games - "Follow My Command", "Find My Shape", "Torx Says", "Pass Torx". You must bend or twist the "handles" to follow the commands. It is an addictive game and speeds up as you master it! It has an on/off switch and if the game is idle for a while, it will beep until you turn it off. The game is approximately 20 inches long when straightened; with both arms folded, it is approximately 8" x 10" x 3" . This game is great for hand dexterity and following instructions...a great memory building game!

—description from the publisher

Showdown is an original two-player battle board game. Players can play the roles of samurai, swordsman, stick monk, ninja, and knight Spartan. They advance and retreat on the board, use skill cards to attack and defend, and use tricks and tactics.

Enjoy the thrill of the clanging collision between cold weapons.

boardgame: 秘宝の郷 (The Hidden Treasure Village)
description:
秘宝の郷 ("The Hidden Treasure Village") is a game that was presented in issue 64 of TACTICS magazine (March 1989).

The game deals with an investigation by a team trying to locate a treasure that was hidden by a Lord during the Sengoku Jidai. The players act as the investigators of this team and must not only uncover the treasure, but they must also protect themselves against espionage, sabotage, and hidden dangers.  that is trying to uncover.

The game is a chit-pull system, where the players draw from a cup.  Players take turn moving and doing an action.  The action can be things like drawing a counter, espionage, sabotage, and metal detecting.

The winner is the player who discovers the treasure before turn 60.

—user summary

At the start of the game, the movable color slide is in the neutral space in the middle. The players take turns to roll the color die and move the slide by pulling on the cord.
Whoever is the first to roll yellow begins the game and can choose one of the two target colors - red or yellow - and pull it forward to the first yellow color space in their own target direction. If you roll black, you can “quickly” pull to the next space of the same color. 
If you roll a color in front of the goal that no longer appears, you skip the tug of war once. Whoever pulls the color plate into their own target space first is the winner.

—user summary

The Necronomicon Gamebook - Kadath represents an extension of the Dreamlands chapters in Dagon and Carcosa, and broadens the gaming experience, but can also be read individually.

The Necronomicon Gamebook - Kadath will take you to the dark heart of the Dreamlands, where the truce between the nightmare priests is about to shatter.

Nyarlathotep, the Crawling Chaos, wants to awaken his lord, Azathoth the Daemon Sultan that bubbles at the center of all infinity.

Our reality exists only in the broken mind of this primal entity, consumed by its own power. Awakening the Greatest God could mean the end of everything.

However, Azathoth is not the only sleeping abomination upon which the fate of all existence hangs. Cthulhu, a blasphemous deity who waits dreaming in the lost underwater city of R'lyeh, does not accept the end of his oniric sovereignty.

And here you come in, attracted by an ancestral call during the ritual that every century opens a bridge towards the Dreamlands, you will go beyond the boundaries of time and space to move the needle of the Armageddon.

Description
80 pages B/W
116 playable sections
2 literary adaptations
25 different endings
Many horrible ways to die!

—description from the designer

Turn on the voice-recognition bed, choose from 12 funny and original characters!

Ask your patient! How's his nose? His head?

To question your patient, press and release the button. You'll hear a beeping sound and then you'll be able to speak.

Examine and manipulate their analyses such as their temperature, blood pressure or x-ray!

Take notes on your diagnostic pad and compare them to the diseases on the poster to identify your disease!
Solve the puzzles and discover the clues with your detective tools and play the winning combination to unlock the electronic padlock in time!

Join the lively gathering of garden animals as they come together each summer for an extravagant feast in ﻿Garden Getaway. A surprise awaits them though: the furious Gardener, upset to find them feasting on his veggie patch!

Run as fast as you can to outsmart the Gardener by navigating through the chaos, learn decision-making skills, and immerse yourself in a colorful world with charming characters!

Includes multiple ways to modify the game to play with younger or older children.
Delightful wooden player pieces and dice.
Fun for adults and children to play together!

Choose your words, eat letters according to the level and put them together so that the rest of the participants can guess your board. Get clues and help by completing each level, try to reach the end while keeping some clues, but don't fail or you will have to start over.

How to play:

Take the whiteboard and give a marker to each participant,
The wordmixer draws two cards and puts two words together, removing as many letters as the level the group is at, and writes them on his board, then lets the rest guess which words it comes from without giving any more clues.

The more correct words, the more levels the group will advance and the more advantages they will obtain.

—description from the publisher

A cozy, non-violent fantasy engine builder game

Story: Roam the lands, foraging, exploring, crafting, and building. Leave this place better than you found it. Not to prove that you're not the monster others think you are, but because it makes you happy.

Overview for Solo Rondel Game: Cards are multi-use. They can be discarded to move, or consumed for action points or card draw. Additionally, they can be played onto the rondel, improving your actions. Build up an engine at the cost of whittling your deck away. Gain hearts for playing cards and using the actions of certain cards when you visit them. Score at least 16 hearts to win!

In Nekojima, “The Island of Cats” in Japan, an electricity network is developing to supply the various lively districts of the island. The installation of electric poles becomes more complex due to the narrowness of the territory and its curious population of cats strolling on the cables.

Nekojima is a wooden game of skill and dexterity in which you have to keep an entire installation in balance. Players take turns placing or stacking denchuu — 電柱, or electrical poles — respecting the locations without any hanging cables touching. Be careful not to be the one to bring down the structure. This game requires reflection, concentration and skill.

In competition, the player who knocks down the structure loses.
In co-operation, the goal is to go as far as possible.

The Collector's Edition include the base game, a wooden box and contains several expansions from the Kickstarter campaign as:

Torii Extension:
Obviously, nothing should touch the Torii, neither the cables, nor the poles, nor the cats.

The Lazy Cats: 
Cats will land on the poles and prevent you from piling up

Tsunami expansion:
Hanging cats must face dangerous waves that disturb their tranquility. When a red flag is raised, it means the neighborhood is ravaged and the cats must seek refuge in another safe district.

More Cats:
Introduces new and more capricious cats: the calico cat, the red cat and the tabby cat.

Jump into the far future where eager ship jumpers try their luck at salvaging derelict starships.

Introducing Dangerous Space, the followup to the breakout 2023 success Dungeon Pages.

Dangerous Space is a dungeon crawl experience in futuristic starships.

This solo, roll & write game has a tactical emphasis on dice management and careful placement.

With more puzzle-optimizing challenges than Dungeon Pages, combined with an all new tech tree, you’ll find yourself racing the clock, dodging enemy laser blasts and confronting some deep space terrors.

2024 Year-Long Adventure Set

Every Friday in 2024 you will receive a new sheet. Each new sheet will introduce a new mission (Rescue, Retrieve, Commandeer, and Eliminate), and each month will introduce a new character with their specific tech tree, and a new ship. Each character top can be cut off and combined with a ship/mission bottom for a new way to play.

VanWoow is a charming board game tailored for enthusiasts of caravaning and small-town exploration. It invites players to embark on a journey through picturesque locales, each offering unique experiences and local events.

Visit Alange during the vibrant orchid bloom, search for dinosaur fossils in the ancient soils of Prejano, or stroll through the scenic overlook and fountains route in Fulleda—every town adds a new flavor to your adventure.

Players strategically select which small towns to visit, aiming to maximize the diversity of their experiences and participate in as many local traditions as possible. With its engaging mechanics and thematic focus on travel and discovery, VanWoow offers a delightful experience for those seeking adventure from the comfort of their game table.

This set of six 16mm dice can be used to randomly determine the weather for your game.

Each die represents a different region, with unique faces on each.

Black ("Chicago") – mostly sunny or windy or both.
Dark blue ("Tierra del Fuego") – cold, no sun.
Gray ("Seattle") – usually cloudy or rainy.
Green ("Miami") – usually sunny.
Orange ("Austin") – very changeable.
Red ("Phoenix") – usually hot.

A remastering of Yahtzee mechanic with a new twist. Make dice combinations of 5 dice and use them to compete on six game tracks: leap over your opponents, build ramps, and play super combos. Use your cards to influence the dice and make super moves. The final victory points depend on the position on the tracks.

In addition to the main competitive mode for 2-5 players and the solo mode for 1 player, there is also a campaign challenge with various in-game achievements.

The game tracks are double-sided and three-layered. Double-sided components with different tracks, combos and quests tokens make the game highly replayable.

The game is graphically designed in Art Deco style. The elegant design may appeal to classic game lovers.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Sunlantern Armor Promo
description:
This is a set of two promo cards, the Sunlantern Armor hybrid armor set bonus card, and the Sunlantern Mask gear card. They were included with the Sunlantern Armor - Aya miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The Sunlantern Mask reads:

+ 2 movement

When a weapon with 5 or less strength is to the left of this, it gains Range: 4 and the thrown keyword.

boardgame: Hazardous Movement Scenario Pack 3: City of Steel
description:
City of Steel recreates 1942-1943 battles both in and outside the city of Stalingrad.

Compatible with the Advanced Squad Leader game system, CoS utilizes two excellent maps: Valor of the Guards (VotG) and Red October to depict the fighting inside the city (7 scenarios), while 3 additional scenarios portray Operations Little Saturn, Uranus and Winter Storm (the attempt to relieve Stalingrad).

Finally, these scenarios can be played individually or as a "mini-campaign" in which each side accumulates victory points for both wins and achievements.

boardgame: Hazardous Movement Scenario Pack 4: Making the Grade
description: Making the Grade recreates World War II battles the world over: from the frozen forests of Finland to the steamy jungles of Guadacanal, from the Winter War of 1939 to the last, desperate attempts by the Germans to stem the inexorable advance of the Red Army on the Eastern Front battles in 1945.
