The Combined Army has shown its might, unleashing massive forces on its primary targets on Paradiso and Concilium Prima.

A chapter in the history of the Infinity Universe comes to a close, bringing an end to the Human Sphere as we know it.

Endsong is an expansion for Infinity that opens up a new stage in the universe and game, welcoming the release of the new Reinforcements rules with a complete overview of all Infinity factions' reinforcement and reaction groups, as well as their respective troops.

In addition to broadening the universe and backstory, preparing it for a new phase, this expansion also covers the Nomad mothership Bakunin and its Observance, the Hassassins of Haqqislam, and ALEPH's Steel Phalanx.

This book also features three narrative scenarios to expand on the aftermath of the events of Endsong, which can be played in ITS to shape and determine the future of Concilium, either in campaign mode or as standalone games. In addition, there are four top-level painting guides to unveil the secrets of professional painting for the most emblematic troops of Bakunin's Observance, the Hassassins, and the Steel Phalanx.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Bonnie and Clyde, Love at First Shot
description:
Bonnie and Clyde, Love at First Shot is an engaging solo game where you step into the shoes of Clyde Barrow. You must navigate a dangerous world, evading the police while trying to win Bonnie Parker's heart. Every decision matters, from resource management to avoiding law confrontations, as you carve your path to destiny.

In this game, Bonnie’s presence through her poetry is a constant influence on your choices. Her spirit and writings add a romantic and narrative dimension, as you, playing as Clyde, seek to earn her affection and forge the bond that will unite them in history.

Gameplay: Each turn, you have (at best) 4 actions to distribute in order to optimally manage your resources while paying the cost of living and avoiding being imprisoned too often or too quickly. You need to accumulate poems, love, and boredom, as well as ammunition, before stealing a 1927 Ford A and embarking on the roads of "Bonnie and Clyde, Love and Death" before being killed by the police.

-description from designer

boardgame: Justin & Dave's Awesome Would You Rather...? Board Game
description:
Deal an ‘A’ and a ‘B’ answer card to each player and place your mover piece on the start position. The person to the player’s left picks up a card and reads the dilemma.

The player must decide between ‘A’ or ‘B’ and places their answer card face down. Other players decide what they think the player has chosen and cast their votes.

Everyone flips their cards OVER. If no-one guessed what the player chose the PLAYER moveS ahead 3 spaces, otherwise anyone who guessed what the player chose moves ahead 1 space.

The first player to reach the finish space wins. If more than one player makes it to the finish at the same time – you’re all winners!

Contains rules for two games.

Ternion Factor: on a unique grid of squares and triangles, players vie to get all their pieces on the board by occupying intersections and forcing their opponents to make unfortunate moves. Each turn a player does three actions, which have three interwoven consequences of adding or removing pieces, with a bit of luck thrown into the choice process. The first person with all eighteen pawns in the play area at the end of their turn is the winner.

In "Escape!": this is a Halma variant using this board. Escape! is a strategy game where pawns are jumped across the board to reach one of the intersections beyond the blue line on the other player's side of the board. The first player whose pawns all Escape! is the winner.

A betting game to see who can correctly guess the number of sequences in a randomly created grid of tiles. Forty numbered tiles are shuffled and place in a 4x10 grid and players make secret bets on how many "runs" will be present. Then the tiles are flipped and everyone earns or loses points based on how close they were to their bet. First player to get 10 points wins.

—user summary

Packlash is a card game where two players have a 15 card deck with a different way to win. Each deck is filled with cards that either win you the game, get you closer to winning the game, or prevent your opponent from getting closer to winning. Items are your win condition or a static ability that changes the game until they are removed. On your turn you can play items and actions to get closer to winning; whereas your opponent can play reactions to piggy back off of your progress. Be the first to fulfill your unique win condition to succeed!

龍城飛將：抗戰之太原會戰 ("Siege Songs: Battle of Xuzhou") is a two-player board wargame that simulates the battle of Beiping- Tianjin and the battle of Taiyuan, from Jul.7 , 1937 to Nov. 10, 1937. "Dragon City" is the third game in the War of Resistance series.

Each turn represents 1 week and each hex is about 10-12 km across. Each unit is from battalion to division. One player commands the Imperial Japanese Army （大日本帝國陸軍） and the other player commands the National Revolutionary Army （國民革命軍）.

In Siege Songs, each player uses operation points to activate Army HQ to lead combat units to move, barrage, combat. In each turn, each player draws tactic markers respectively and roll for initiative. The units include infantry, artillery, cavalry, and tank, etc. The NRA side needs to defend Xinkou, Pingxingguan, and Taiyuan in different scenarios to win the game.

The components includes two 80cm*57cm inches map and 3 15mm die-cut counter boards.
family: Series: War of Resistance (Formosa Force Games)

description:
You are the archangel Raphael. Your mission is find pairs of lovers from the Minor Arcana and connect their fate to each of the 22 Major Arcana of the Tarot deck. Nobles will help you by blessing the unions of certain lovers, but time is running out...

The Lovers is a solitaire game played with a Tarot deck. The player takes on the role of the the archangel Raphael and must find 22 pairs of lovers from the Minor Arcana to connect their fates each to a different Major Arcana before the deck runs out. Lovers must share the same rank or the same suit, and their values can be added, subtracted, multiplied, or divided to match the value of a Major Arcana card. Nobles (represented by the court cards) can be used to bless certain unions, altering the individual or total value of the lovers. But they must be used sparingly, since the end of the deck means the end of the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 秘宝の番人ゾンビゲーム (Keeper of the Hidden Treasure: Zombie Game)
description:
This is a game designed by Ginichiro Suzuki, and produced by Epoch. It is a game where you are exploring the underground, uncovering treasures, and fighting monsters, all in an attempt to get to the castle and have the most treasure.

Hailed as a Masterpiece in Japan.  It is not so much the game, but the art of Ritta Nakanishi that makes this game so popular.  Ritta is noted for his military illustrations and military model art.  This game is fully illustrated from top to bottom (well, not the bottom of the box) with his wonderful art featuring all sorts of horror.

In addition, the entirety of the box is used.  In fact, there are four tops/lids that are illustrated and used in the game.  The result is a three-dimension board where the players move through different levels.  There are even pitfalls at the highest level, as well as caves, in addition to foldable terrain pieces.  To top this off, there is a facade that fits to the back of the board featuring the game's objective. All this creates a highly immersed game experience.

From the box cover:
The zombies have awakened, and the greatest terror of the century is upon you! The warning bell is ringing! The evil clutches of the zombies are closing in!  If you don't escape quickly, you'll turn into a zombie!

—user summary
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
まとりて！ (Matorite!) is trick-taking card game from Japan using magic cards.

Magic has the attributes of fire, water, earth and wind, and if the lead player uses magic of the fire attribute, the other players must also use magic of the fire attribute.

However, if there is no fire-attribute magic in the hand, magic of another attribute can be used.
Attributes have affinities, such as "Fire ← Water ← Earth ← Wind ← Fire".

If you can use water-attribute magic that is effective against fire-attribute magic in such a situation, you will be one step closer to victory.

まとりて！は魔法カードを使用したトリックテイキングです。
魔法には火・水・土・風の属性があり、親が火属性の魔法を使用したら他のプレイヤーも火属性の魔法を使用しなければなりません。
ただし、手札に火属性の魔法がない場合は他の属性の魔法を使用することができます。
属性には「火←水←土←風←火」のように相性が存在します。
このような時に火属性に対して有効な水属性の魔法を使用することができれば、勝利へ一気に近づくでしょう。
--description by publisher

Dedication, focus, patience and speed are needed when venturing into a Coinx Duel!

Keep your eyes open, as the eyes staring back at you from the mission board are always watching. Make sure to finish the logic puzzle in the fastest way possible, to beat your opponent and jump to the next mission. The fastest Coinx Dueler wins the round!

Coinx Duel is a 2-player abstract strategy game to be enjoyed with friends and family members of all ages!

—description from the publisher

Mischievous Moments will quickly become your new favourite adult party game. It is a game for 2 to 16 players consisting of 69 mini-games, all with a theme of humour, drinking or risqué behaviour. The unique grading system lets the players decide how intimate they want to be. The game is completely flexible and will be different every time you play.

A mobile app is provided to provide instructions and help manage the game for the players. All players can log into the game to see the random card draws, instructions and player choices on their own devices.

A game board and 16 quality playing pieces are also provided to track the progress of the players through the game.

The game is LGBTQ+ friendly and can be enjoyed by everyone aged 18 and over. The grading system allows the players to dictate what level of intimacy there is between them, from no physical interaction to activities which would make seasoned swingers blush.

The game can last from 30 minutes to several hours and often lasts longer than the players do with all the drinking and excitement which it generates. Play can continue until the end - and a winner announced - or played for a certain period of time. The object is to have fun with your friends, to get to know them better and to help improve intimacy among couples rather than simply winning a game.

The game is suitable for individual couples and groups of friends whether in a relationship or not. It has proved popular with the swinging community during our testing but is suitable for all levels of intimacy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ジャンヌダルク暗殺 (L' assassinat de Janne d' Arc)
description:
ジャンヌダルク暗殺 (L' assassinat de Janne d' Arc) (Assassination of Joan Of Arc) is a game of identity concealment from Japan.

Players become knights, royalty and nobility fighting for the realisation of the prophecies of 'war', 'politics' and 'miracles' and the victory of France at the same time as Jeanne d'Arc at the end of the Hundred Years War.

Some become infiltrators of England, trying to prevent the French victory or assassinate Jeanne d'Arc herself.


    An asymmetrical invitation concealment game set in late medieval France, in the vein of Werewolf and Avalon.
    Players can either be pious French knights or enemy English insiders.
    Two camps battle through the end of the Hundred Years War over the fulfilment of nine prophecies!


正体隠匿ゲームです。
プレイヤーは、百年戦争終盤のジャンヌダルクと同時代に「戦争」「政治」「奇蹟」の預言の実現とフランスの勝利にむけて戦う騎士、王族、貴族となります。一部は紛れ込んだイングランドの内通者となり、フランスの勝利を阻止するか、ジャンヌダルク自身を暗殺しようとます。
＊中世末期フランスを舞台にした、人狼やアヴァロンの流れを組む非対称招待隠匿ゲーム。
＊プレイヤーは敬虔なフランス騎士か、敵国イングランドの内通者になります。
＊２つの陣営が９つの預言の成就を巡って、百年戦争末期を戦い抜きます
--description by publisher

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: Pacific Theater
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII related to the conflict in the Pacific.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
In Gaivotas you are just that: a seagull (Gaivota in Portuguese) trying to catch the biggest and best selection of fish.

Each round a series of fish cards are revealed on the table and players secretly choose which fish they want to catch, revealing their choice simultaneously.
If only one player has chosen a fish card, he takes the card, but if more than one player has chosen the same fish card, the seagulls flap in the air and catch nothing, getting only what's left on the fisherman's boat or even leave empty-handed.
Each fish card collected must be placed at one end of the player's line of cards, and the fish cards only worth points if the players manage to put together a school (fish of the same type next to each other).
When the deck of fish is finished, the player who has collected the most shoals is the winner.

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: Battle of Britain
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII related to the historical Battle of Britain.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)
Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII with content related to the initial stages of the WWII in Europe.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
Skeletens is bone-rattling 2-4 player card game, where each player vies for battlefield supremacy with an army of skeletons! The first player to enlist 10 of their calcium-rich, creaky comrades are declared the victors.

Setting up the game is easy! Shuffle the deck and deal each player 5 cards. Players take turns to play a card of any type onto their battlefield OR draw a card into their hand.

Go wide with skeleton spam or tower up to protect your crew.  Turn your opponents' skeletons to dust with attack cards or bolster your fortifications with defense cards. Just remember you need skeleTEN to win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: Eastern Front
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII related to the aerial battles on the Eastern Front.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: African Campaign
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII related to the aerial battles that took place over the Northern Africa.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
MONOPOLY: The Witcher lets players journey the Continent to buy, sell, and trade dangerous monsters from the popular video game franchise, such as Bruxa, Crone, Leshen, and more! Work magic around the board with custom show object tokens, including a Flaming Book, Lute, and Crystal Skull. Upgrade properties with Houses and Manors while managing Bounty and Law of Surprise situations. Be the last player with custom crown currency to win!

What's Included:
1 The Witcher Monopoly Game Board
6 Collectible Tokens (Crystal Skull, Flaming Book, Kaer Morhen, Lute, Roach on Roof, Swallow)
Custom Witcher Banknotes
28 Title Deed Cards (locations are monsters)
16 Chance Cards renamed Law of Surprise 
16 Community Chest Cards renamed Bounty 
32 Houses 
12 Hotels renamed Manors 
Dice 
Rules

Antyk is a card game that can be played by 2 to 5 players aged 7 and over. The average game lasts 15 minutes. The aim of the game is to be the player with the most Olympus points, which means stealing and earning points, as well as spending them to play cards. It's up to you to find the best strategy for reigning supreme on Olympus.

To do so, the game proceeds as follows : 
At first, a god card and 10 Olympus points are distributed at each player.

On each turn, players draws 2 cards and can play as many as they like.

When a "power" card is played, an Olympus point is stolen from the player with the corresponding god, or taken from the reserve if it is of his own god. These cards are discarded afterwards.

For every other types of cards, players needs to pay the appropriate number of Olympus points to place the cards and use their powers.

A player loses when he runs out of points.

To win, a player needs to be the first to gather 20 Olympus points, to be the only player left with points or to be the one with more points when the card deck is empty.

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: Advanced German Aircraft
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII that introduces Advanced German Aircraft into the conflict.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: Special German Aerial Weapons
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII introduces special weapons on the German side.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

boardgame: Age of Dogfights WWII: Transport Aircraft and Gliders
description: An expansion for Age of Dogfights: WWII introduces transport aircraft and gliders as new content for your aerial battles.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
Take turns throwing Pop Darts at the Orange Target Marker.  Closest Dart = 3 points
Every other Dart that sticks = 1 point
Landing on the marker = 10 Points
Who ever scores the most points each round gets points after they cancel out opponents score.
Play to 21

A dart version of Cornhole, Horseshoes, or Curling.

boardgame: Fracas: The Battle of the Pets
description:
We all have our favorites, but which pet is best?

This is a fun, chess-like strategy game that animal against animal to earn the title of top pet.

This is a themed 4-player version of MetaCheckers: Battlefield with all-wood, laser cut pieces. Choose one of four animal checker armies:


     Dogs
     Cats
     Rabbits
     Parrots


Each checker army has a hidden king checker.

The game uses 24 board tiles, each made of four squares. Players each get 6 board tiles which they take turns placing on the field of play, connecting the tiles to form a unique battlefield. Players place their 12 checkers strategically on the squares of their tiles, with the kings hidden, face down at the beginning of the game. Eliminate your opponents by capturing their king.

On their turn, players roll two dice to determine how to move a checker. One die has the symbols for chess pieces, the other is a standard d6 numbers die. Whenever a 6 is rolled, the player may opt to reroll with the KANGAROO Die and the numbers die for 6 additional useful moves.

Players eliminate their opponents by capturing the hidden king of the opposing army, which then leaves the board. Last player standing wins.

—description from the designer

Your ship crash landed on a strange planet. You appear to be mostly unharmed, but the same cannot be said for your ship. Key components of your main engines, your medical lab, and your weaponry have been scattered across the planet's surface. As you set off to find these parts and repair your ship, you also find something quite strange: a medical device, a few capsules, and a button with writing that your translators interpret as "PURGE." You wonder what such a device could possibly be used for, when a deadly mist begins to descend...

In Deadly Mist, you must explore this planet for the three missing parts of your ship, collect them, and make it back to the Crash Site. While you do so, you must clear the ever-spreading Miasma and avoid taking deadly Infection cards into your deck.

—description from the designer

A Vanguard paves the way for the rest of the forces in a faction. Bound by honour and pride, their duels are often the first of conflicts between two opposing forces in an arcane war.  Their victories amplify morale and their defeats ominous signs for their Battle Mage masters …

TKG ARENA: The Vanguard Duels is a game set up for 2 players.

Components required for this game:

STANDARD DECK (DECK):     1
PLAYING CARD STAND (STAND):     2
OINKS:    1 from each element

Winning Condition

The player with the most HONOUR points wins the game when it ends.

Ending Condition

The game ends when one of the players reaches 12 or more HONOUR points.

Setup

A player starts the game by designating the following areas: A draw deck area O, a discard area X and five adjoining spaces that is designated A, B, C, D and E on the card stand. The players should be seated facing each other with sufficient space between them to place the trumping order represented by the spaces T, 1, 2 and 3.

X    A    B    C    D    E    O


     Determine the starting player by taking turns to draw cards from a shuffled standard deck. The player who draws the highest face value starts first. In case of a tie, the tied players keep drawing till the tie is broken with a player drawing a higher face value. The other player decides the initial trumping order.
     All 36 cards are re-shuffled and each player receives 18 cards faced down as a drawing stack at their respective area O.
     Each player draws 5 cards from the top of the draw stack and places them on the stand one by one in the sequence drawn. The 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th card drawn should be located on the stand at positions A, B, C, D and E respectively. All the front facing contents of the cards face the player and the backs of the cards towards the opponent.
     Players are not allowed to switch the positions of the cards freely during play.
     The area X is used as a space to place discarded cards during play.
     The Oinks representing the 4 elements will be placed in the spaces T, 1, 2 and 3 to indicate the priority of the elements. The trumping element has priority over all other elements during play.


T        1    2    3

E.g. Fire (Ashw) is in the T space – Any Fire (Ashw) card will beat cards of any other element, when both cards are Fire (Ashw) cards, the card with the higher face value will win.  Moving forward, let’s assume that Water (Esbner), Earth (Ohif) and Air (Ukelele) are in positions 1, 2 and 3 respectively, this means when face values of opposing cards are the same, the tie is broken using this priority: All Water (Esbner) cards will prevail against Earth (Ohif) cards which will prevail against Air (Ukelele) cards. Water (Esbner) cards will also prevail against Air (Ukelele) cards.

On each player’s turn

The player can choose to execute ONE of the two following actions.


     End the round immediately by discarding 2 cards from his/her draw stack at O. All players proceed to scoring the round. This move cannot be chosen when there are less than 2 cards in the draw stack at O and 5 cards in the discard space X.
     Draw the top card from the draw stack at O and may choose to either discard it into X or replace it with one of the cards placed in A, B, C, D or E. In this case, the replaced card goes into the discard space X. 3 further possibilities stem from here.
         If the discarded card into X has an odd face value (1, 3, 5, 7, 9), the player must reveal one of his/her cards by placing it face up in front of card stand while maintaining its position relative to the other unrevealed cards.
         If the discarded card into X has an even face value (2, 4, 6, 8), the player must reveal the top card of his draw stack at O and keep it face up until it is drawn.
         If the discarded card into X is of the same element as the immediate previous discard card, the player can choose to re-arrange all the elements in the trumping order.
    


At any point of time, if the draw stack at O has no more cards, the last turn takes place followed by scoring of the round. Otherwise, the other player takes his/her turn and the game proceeds.

Scoring

When each round ends, players will pit their cards against each other in the respective spaces on the card stand. E.g. Corresponding cards in space A of the players are compared, followed by those in spaces B, C, D and E. Trumping priority are resolved first. This is followed by face value priority. Cards with higher face values win those of lower values. Ties in face value priority are resolved by spaces 1, 2 and 3 in the trumping order explained earlier. For each card that has higher priority over his/her opponent’s respective card, a victory is scored.

Over 5 spaces, the player with more victories will claim HONOUR points equal to the number of discarded cards in the opponent’s discard space X. If the ending condition is met, the game ends and the winner is the one with most HONOUR points. Otherwise, the HONOUR points are recorded, all cards (inclusive of discarded or unused cards) in the game are re-collected and the set up for a new round begins.

boardgame: Vad du borde lärt dig i skolan
description:
The nerd or the class prankster, who were you in school? Once again, up to proof! You who thought you got away just because you left school - forget it! Now it's a battle of the brains with this quiz game about what you should have learned in school. Test yourself and not least those around you.

Contains 400 questions in 8 different categories.

Droplets is a light, yet strategic word game all about dropping and collecting letters to form words. 
 
GOAL: 
Make and score the most valuable words.

SETUP:  

     Give each player a dry erase form and marker or pre-printed game sheet.
     One player calls out two “seed” words from the back of the dry erase form.
     Players simultaneously drop letters from each seed word into each of the seven rows, two letters per row, one from each word. See examples below for clarity.


GAME PLAY: 
Players have five minutes to simultaneously form words horizontally in each row by adding one or more additional letters from their stockpiles. As you add a letter, mark it off from the stockpile. You cannot reuse letters. Words must be at least three letters long.

SCORING:  
At the end of five minutes, assemble your letters into words, unscramble as needed. Score each completed word, then add up the total for your final score. The player with the highest score wins. If there is a tie, the player with the alphabetically-highest word wins.

EXAMPLE: 
(See images for example).
Wendy calls out COUNTRY and OUTCOME as the seeds.

     The letters from both words are written down vertically in order.
     Letters are added from the stockpile and marked off  
     The letters in row 6 are unscrambled.


as used. 

     Words are scored to get the final score.


  
STRATEGIES:

     Before dropping the initial letters from each seed word, think about what new words you might wish to form for maximum points.
     Try to use the top value letters.
     Call out your own, more complicated seven-letter words.

This pack contains 15 additional missions (61-75) that can be played seamlessly with the B 17 Twenty five missions base game.

—description from the designer

No one knows who first discovered Dark Matter. They are surely long dead by now. Some call it the most valuable substance in the galaxy, as it augments both lifeforms and machines. Others call it a curse, for while the benefits are undeniable, there is also a heavy cost. It slowly takes control of its host. Only found in deep space, hunters will go off the grid for years looking for it. If they return, they come back with remarkable abilities, but little of the person that began the search.

A 35-card Moonrakers expansion that introduces a powerful and dangerous substance to the game — Dark Matter. The crowdfunding-exclusive Collector's Edition includes black foil cards.

The origin of Dark Matter is as unknown as the depth of its darkness. Often fabled as 'the most valuable substance in the galaxy,' its rarity is only matched by the danger inherent in its use.

Able to augment both organic life and machine, Dark Matter slowly takes control of its hosts — leaving only a shell of what previously existed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kyudo: Booster – Pan, dans le mille !
description:
The booster gives you access to 5 “Pun in the bull’s eye” cards which are placed next to the game track.

After having made your dice rolls, if you obtain 4 dice of the same color, you have the possibility of not filling out your sheet and taking the “Punch in the bull's eye” card corresponding to this color. If this card is already in an opponent's possession, you can steal it from them.

While card is in front of you:

During your turn, after having made your dice rolls, you can decide to place all or some of the dice of this color on their Arrow side. You can have several “Punch in the Bull” cards and apply the effects of each of them.

Hilarious and insightful parody of the classic "Mexican Bingo" game Loteria. Each player is given a bingo card with a set of images (e.g. "El Hipster" and "Las Bitcoins") organized in a 4x4 grid. A card deck containing each image is shuffled and revealed one by one with each player covering the matching images on their own card. The first player to cover four images in a row wins!

Hitmen Hotel is a competitive logical deduction game for 2 to 4 players. Players are Spymasters, leading a team of secret agents to assassinate their target, which is the player on their left. Each player has a location within the hotel that is secret to other players. On your turn, play a card and ask one of the two questions about your target's location, receiving a Yes or No reply. However, other players may glean information from the colour and number of the card you played, so be careful in choosing your cards. The first player who successfully deduces their target's location and carries out the assassination action wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: How Did the Chicken Cross the Road
description:
How did the chicken cross the road? According to this game, it could be anything from a catapult to a pterodactyl. They're tearing down a novelty factory to make way for a new freeway! The first Rubber Chicken to make it back will save the day and become the king of novelties. It's a race to build an idyllic garden path back home as your opponents build freeways blocking your way.

Up to four players take turns placing tiles to build a road from work to home. Players can also play obstacles to other players' roads. First chicken to make it to work wins.

Dwars is a fast-paced card game combining tactics and bluffing for 2 players. Here you are in the shoes of clans battling for control of Toulahô mountain.

One player plays the dwarf clan, while the other plays the goblin clan. Each player receives a 25-card deck, consisting of 5 copies of each of 5 different characters, each with its own power.

Players take turns placing character cards in front of them. When a player has all 5 characters in front of him/her, he/she immediately wins the game.

During his turn, when a player places a card in front of him, he places it face-down, giving his opponent time to counter if he wishes. If this is not the case, the active player turns his card face-up and activates his character's power.

boardgame: The Fox Experiment: Patron and Science Promo Cards
description:
Includes 1 double sided Patron Card and 2 Science Cards. The Science Cards have the same effect, but different flavor text.

The new Patron card:

Side A: Earn 8 points if you have no other Sponsors claimed
 Side B: Earn 8 points if none of the pups you created in the last round have a friendliness score of less than 13

The new Science card:

When scoring studies: You may assign 1 pup to 2 study levels. (This may be 2 levels of 1 study, or 1 level of 2 different studies.)

An 18 card expansion for Aethermon: Collect.
Includes:
Solo Mode
1 player tracker
4 Character Direction Trackers
5 new Artefacts
5 campaign missions

This pack contains all the physical card stretch goals from the Gamefound crowdfunding campaign.

Arise as a Divine God of Lust and build the Ultimate Harem, in this competitive card game, inspired by harem style anime shows and visual novel games. Divine Harem is a 2-4+ player strategic card game for lustful people, full of sexy anime girls, just waiting to join your harem. It's simple to pick up and play and yet strategic enough to ensure multiple interesting games between friends.

The goal of the Divine Harem game is to build a complete Harem Pyramid, which is made up of three Harem Girls, two Harem Toys and one Harem Pet. The first player to complete their pyramid wins the game.

During a turn, players can perform 2 actions of the following:

     Draw a Card from the Deck
     Place a Card in your Harem
     Play a Harem Power Card


(players can perform two different actions or two actions the same, as they see fit. E.g. playing two power cards)
Beware: When you place a card into your Harem, other players can attack your card, with cards from their own hand to try and prevent you from placing it into your Harem.

A new fan made expansion in the Crawler's Grimoire line for DungeonQuest Third and Revised editions. This expansion consists of a deck of 38 cards that allow players to purchase powerful items from the mysterious Undead Merchant. These items may increase your chances of making it out of the dungeon, however it allows for a lot of new player interaction... better watch your back! These items will help you to survive monster encounters, harm your fellow players, heal yourself, control the Sun Track, choose your dungeon tiles, et-cetera, allowing for a whole slew of new fun!

This custom expansion for DungeonQuest features a deck of 52 brand new cards for use in DungeonQuest Third Edition and Revised Edition. This expansion adds a ton of variety to your game as you progress along the Sun Track and go deeper into the dungeon. Will you have good luck or terrible luck? Either way, the journey will be a very memorable one as you encounter all sorts of beings, woes, treasures, and events.

This custom expansion for DungeonQuest features a deck of 64 brand new cards for use in DungeonQest Third Edition and Revised Edition. This expansion allows you to continue playing as a ghost after you have died, haunting your fellow players and trying to drag them into the afterlife! Alternatively, it allows for an automated ghost to track you down and slay you if you are playing solo!

This is a simple game from Tehnolog dealing with an episode during the Battle of Borodino.

Both players have four soldiers, a cannon, and a redoubt.  The players set up their forces 1 to 1.5 meters apart and roll a die to see who goes first.

Each player, in turn, fires one shot for every soldier they have.  Each shot must be from behind their line of fortifications, and no shot can be made unless a soldier is within 5cm of their cannon. If there is no soldier within 5cm, then the player, at the start of their turn, must place a solider within 5cm of their cannon.  If a player hits their opponent's officer, then they get a free shot.  The first side to eliminate all their opponent's soldiers wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Vikings' Tales: Edge of the World
description:
Get ready for adventure! Are you ready to set sail for the Edge of the World? Put together the best crew and start trading goods while fighting battles and heading further towards the unknown. The Viking chief, who has accumulated the greatest fortune, is the winner, but there are many perils along the way. This adventure game suits 3-4 players and includes impressive wooden game pieces. Edge of the World is a game you will want to play again and again!

The board depicts Europe. Players move their ships playing dual-use cards that have movement and combat points. When players visit cities, they can buy goods, sell goods or raid the city. Once some collects 150 points, they must head back to their home city and the first player to meet them there can challenge them to a duel to see who wins the game.

Sacátelo Vos arrived to raise the tone of the previous ones. Brother of Tomalo Vos, it adds the spicy component.

How to play? The cards are dealt and the Strip card is placed in front of one of the players. On your turn, you have to try to get that card off of you and pass it to another player. If you can't, you lose a life... and a piece of clothing.

If you are already a recurring player of Tomalo Vos, we are happy to let you know that you will be able to mix them.

In Deep Down, each player represents a salvage crew attempting to make their fortune. Players complete contracts by retrieving objects from the bottom of the ocean and executing technical jobs. Those contracts will pay the players who in turn can use their hard-earned money to improve their crews and get the right equipment for the job.

The game progresses as players score points. Those points are earned for accomplishing any of the following:


     Completing contracts
     Boosting reputation
     Exploring uncharted regions
     Fulfilling ambitions
     Collecting corporation shares


—description from the designer

boardgame: CRYPTOLY: To the Moon and Beyond
description:
Cryptoly is a strategy board game that revolves around the world of Bitcoin, altcoins, NFTs and more. You can expect airdrops, ICOs, NFTs, mining, staking, exchanges, wallets and much more. The game can be played flexibly and can be scheduled in advance or by time cards.

During a turn, the player can take a number of actions. Exchange coins at the specified rate on the DEX (Decentralized Exchange), but also negotiate with other players. Buy and/or sell mining/staking cards or your NFTs. The turn ends when you complete the task on the action field.

The game can be ended flexibly. There are various ways to do this. By agreement after a certain time (e.g. one hour), by agreeing on your own rule (e.g. being the first to get 30 Bitcoins) or by drawing a time card.

The winner of the game is whoever has the most Bitcoins at the end. The altcoins are converted at the last valid exchange rate (exchange rate card).

-description of the publisher

Two teams of four tanks play football on a field that consists of a grid of hexes. Pieces' movement is determined by dice, and it is a combination of turns in place and forward moves to the hex in front. A Super Heavy Tank (a "Heavy Tank" for the purpose of this game) can move in each turn for the number of steps shown on the die or less, and a Recon Tank (a "Light Tank") is faster, so it can move maximally for two steps more than the number on the die (or again, less, if it suits the player). In each turn, a player chooses which two pieces to move; in some cases, it is possible to move 3 or all 4 pieces in one turn.

Each piece can push the ball during its movement. Due to their higher speed, Light tanks have an advantage when pushing compared to Heavy tanks; however, Heavy tanks can hit the ball with their large cannon barrel when turning the turret. The length of the ball movement is determined with a red-dotted die, which is rolled at the same time as the movement dice.  The ball bounces off the borders of the field according to a very intuitive rule, and if it reaches another tank on its trajectory, it stops on the last empty hex. When the ball is hit or pushed in such a way that it ends up on white hexes on the opposite side of the board, a goal is scored.

Although the length of movement of the pieces and the ball is determined by the dice, luck does not play a huge role. To win, it requires a lot of thinking in order to plan and coordinate your team both in attack and defense, so you get into many favorable positions for execution, while allowing your opponent as few opportunities for counterplay as possible.

Tankball is in some way a Tank Chess derivative, as it uses some of the Tank Chess pieces, and it was conceived right after Tank Chess became a reality thanks to a Kickstarter campaign.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kristian på sporet af... Den gyldne skat
description:
Finally the readers of the popular series about "Kristian på sporet" (Kristian on the trail) can travel with Kristian through jungle and desert, over mountains, under water and through the dangers of the big city, on the hunt for the golden treasure.

Kristian is on the trails of the worlds largest gold treasure. But in order to reach the treasure, you have to fight your way  through quicksand, avalanches and the dangerous alleys of the city, he has to reach the bottom of the sea, and through a dense jungle, before he reaches his target. Help Kristian on his adventure through the five challenging worlds, collect diamants, find shortcuts, fight dangers, and reach the treasure before your opponents. The fastest player wins the treasure and the game!

The game contains a large board, diamants, dice, sandtimer, player pieces and vehicles to traverse the five worlds.

The goal is to be first through the boards many challenges. You will meet dangerous animals, unforeseen obstacles, and there are 260 cards in the game, that you draw, that will test you with challenges, games, questions and funny tasks.

The game is for the whole family. If you can't read, you can play a slightly easier version of the game with cards that does not require reading skills.
 The game is illustrated by Franck Wagnersen, who has also illustrated the books in Kristian Gintbergs popular series of books.

—description from the publisher (translated)
Amherst-Opoly is a Monopoly clone based on the town of Amherst, Nova Scotia.  Board squares are replaced with local businesses, landmarks, and main streets of the town.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
MISPHÈRE RULES

Game Overview:
Misphère is a unique and engaging stacking game designed for one or more players. The goal is to skillfully stack pieces on a half-sphere without causing the structure to collapse.

Components:
-One 6-sided dice with colored faces: Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, Orange, Purple.
-30 tetracube pieces in six different variations: Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, Orange, Purple. There are 5 copies of each tetracube variant.
-One half-sphere.

Gameplay:
In a regular 2-player game, each player chooses a color from the dice faces and rolls the dice until one of the chosen colors is rolled. The player whose color appears first goes first. If you are playing alone, skip this step.
The starting player selects a tetracube piece of their chosen color and carefully places it on the half-sphere.
The other player (or the same player in a solo game) rolls the dice and selects a tetracube piece of the rolled color to place on the half-sphere.
Players take turns rolling the dice and placing tetracube pieces of the rolled color onto the half-sphere.
When all 5 copies of one color are stacked on the half-sphere, players no longer need to roll the dice. From that point, they can choose their pieces to place.
The game continues until the structure collapses due to an unstable placement of a piece.

Winning Conditions:
The player responsible for causing the collapse of the structure loses, and the other player is the winner of the game.

Variant Rules:
-Solo Play: In a solo game, the player attempts to stack as many tetracube pieces as possible before the structure falls. There's no need to determine a starting player; simply enjoy stacking for personal achievement.
-3 to 6 Players: Play clockwise.

-description from publisher

TrainBow is a roll and write game of collecting goods, shipping goods to stores, and making connections along a rainbow track. Each turn 2 dice are rolled, players play simultaneously, either connecting the dice to form one big action with potential bonuses or 2 smaller actions.

Completing connections on tracks gives the  players an extra die for themselves only which can be any value 1-6. Shipping goods to stores offers big bonuses too, but only if you stay after them. Players will race to close shops, but they still want to make the most of global scoring bonuses chosen randomly at the start. Make those connections to get big points in this Train game for 1-100 players!

—description from the designer

Landing Strip is a roll and write game that utilizes two dice to have the players help guide the various airborne travelers in to land safely. Dice may be combined for a larger value or used for a smaller value. The players will have to be mindful of each traveler seeking a path as paths overlap and leaving one where it was can disrupt the path of another.

Players will also be able to place unused dice toward their coffee breaks scoring bonuses for doing so. But be aware that too many unused dice which may result from challenges with planning will result in unwanted baggage. Can you get all of the travelers in safely? Landing Strip is a game of air traffic control for 1-100 players.

—description from the designer

Our first party game!!: Unicorns Love Rainbows! They also love Cupcakes, sometimes they love both. Help Unicorns find Rainbows, Cupcakes, or both in this fun and funny party game! The players each draw a card then act out their card to other players to make those connections. Link a hungry Unicorn with a Cupcake and you both score a point! Find a Rainbow for the speedy Unicorn to go for a jog and score a point as well! Or even go for 3 points with connections between your Unicorn who wants it all and other players who have a Cupcake and a Rainbow! Grab a new card and keep the fun going! 
 Lay scored cards down portrait for 1 point, landscape for 3 points. Play till 5 points, then give it another go! But remember if you can’t make a connection you’ll need to draw a new card. But that just means more options to act out!
There are also separate rules for 1-3 players to engage in rapid-fire real time play of playing cards from their hand to a face up row of cards. Can you be faster than your opponents? 
Unicorns Love Rainbows is played with 54 cards and can be played by 1-8 players.
 —description from the designer

This is a Promo Card for Ensemble, distributed during events and shows.

It's a new Remembrance card to be shuffled with the deck and represents the box of Ensemble, with a little difference: the orchestra director is now facing the players.

—description from the designer

This is a promo card for Pyramidice initially distributed during the "Lucca Comics & Games 2023" event and during the game presentation at the "Museo Egizio" in Turin. Later available from Game Boy Geek Season 12 (2024).

The effect granted to the player that owns this Scarab is: before choosing whether rest or play, you may peek at the first card on top of the God Deck. You also skip the Phase 4 of your work turns (Support the cult of the Gods).

—description from the designer

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Boonlake.

To play this realm, you will use dice and resources to outline levers. When pulled at the right time, the levers can help you maximize your score to 6 stars.

-description from developer

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Can't Stop Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Can't Stop.

To play this realm, you will first mark possible future rewards, which you don't gain immediately. After marking a reward, you may choose to stop in that column and gain all marked benefits. But be careful, because you might not roll the needed number anymore and be left with nothing.

-description from developer

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Fleet – The Dice Game Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Fleet: The Dice Game.

To play this realm you must mark rewards and go deep in the sea. When you discover a treasure, you engage its ability for the rest of the round. These abilities change the rules of a round of Rolling Realms. Can you use them wisely to get to 6 stars?

-description from developer

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Hokkaido.

To play this realm you must draw patterns on the map. Mountains can only go in the middle zone. You gain benefits along the way. Can you match the patterns smartly so you maximize your score for towns, forests, and mountains?

-description from developer

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Potion Explosion Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Potion Explosion.

To play this realm you must assign dice to potions. Upon completion, you unlock a powerful one-time ability that will help you in this realm and in other realms. Complete all three potions to score 6 stars.

-description from developer

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Praga Caput Regni Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Praga Caput Regni.

To play this realm you must assign dice to turn the wheel. Every spin of the wheel awards you with benefits and stars. Can you spin the wheel enough to make it to 6 stars?

-description from developer

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Skoventyr.

To play this realm you must make your way through a dark forest in order to stay ahead of Gamle Erik. If he's catching up, you can sacrifice a tree to slow him down. Will you get to 6 stars while saving the forest?

-description from developer

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Underwater Cities Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Underwater Cities.

To play this realm you must build a network of cities and tunnels. Every city that is connected to the start city grants you new resources and stars. Will you complete the tunnel network and make it to 6 stars?

-description from developer

Think you have what it takes to beat the Vibe Master? Catch The Vibe is here to put that to the test. In this adult party card game, you'll battle to see who knows their friends best.

It starts with the Vibe Master, who draws a Topic Card (like "Talking in a Baby Voice to Your Partner," "The Smell of Gasoline," or "Open Casket Funerals") and 2 Range Cards (like "Yummy/Gross," "Wild/Tame," or "Brave/Cowardly") to set up the ranges of the board. The Vibe Master then secretly picks where they'd assign the topic on the board, and everybody else must guess.

Earn more points the closer you get to the truth! The perfect way to pump up the vibes at any game night, barbecue, or party.

A tiny card game with a fifteen-card dueling deck numbered 0-4, for two players, the goal being to annihilate one's opponent by removing their three life points. Players start by choosing one of the nine characters provided, each offering a unique advantage (get an extra life point, do extra damage, etc.), then draw five cards from the dueling deck and over four rounds play one card to either inflict 1-3 damage or block 1-3 damage.

If both characters are still alive after four rounds, the used cards are shuffled back into the deck and players replenish their hands of five cards, and heal one life point if needed, and duel again.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Game of HAM: Expansion – Superhero Pack: Volume 1
description:
As the name suggests, we take a heroic approach to this expansion, but with a twist. Instead of doing pop culture content, we make up our heroes and powers, including things that should be superpowers. Ready to power up that game night?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Game of HAM: Fairytale Endings – Volume 1
description:
Here we dance in the realm of fairies and princesses in a semi-thematic pack. All cards here tie into fairytales in some way, or just poke fun at tropes and cliches with a unique new flavor… ready for your fairytale ending?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Game of HAM: Gazing at the Abyss – Volume 1
description:
Evil… psychopathic… cruel… horrible violence… this is what you’ll find here. There is nothing sweet… nothing caring… just hatred. Be careful when you face monsters because when you stare at the abyss, it stares back at you…

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Game of HAM: That's Offensive. – Volume 1
description:
Some might say the cards in this expansion are spicy, while others will just say that’s offensive.  Most of the cards in this pack are the kind you wouldn’t break out in polite society, and the cheeky stuff you’ll find here won’t leave you surprised why…

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Spanish Core Box Promo Cards
description:
These 8 promotional cards were included as a limited printing extra for the first buyers of the Spanish edition Core Box. They are made up of 4 alternative art versions of vampires from the Core Box:


     Hidra (Brujah)
     Guv'nah (Malkavian)
     Lixue Chen (Toreador)
     Randolph Marz (Ventrue)


And 4 new vampires that were originally a Kickstarter extra (Humo) or cards for the Season 1 Organized Play Kits (the other three) in the English edition of the game (and were later included in the English Season 1 Promo Pack):


     Humo (Brujah)
     Natasha Blank (Malkavian)
     Freddy Usher (Toreador)
     Martine Diaz (Ventrue)

From the creator of video game classic Puyo Puyo comes a party game to test your acting abilities.

Use only facial expressions and the tone of your voice (and sometimes less than that) to portray different scenarios.

Does that “Ha-“ sound angry, heartbroken, or curious? Players draw a card that determines a topic sound like “Ha-“ or “Eh” along with a list of eight different scenarios. Individual players then draw a card that assigns them a scenario from the list to enact. Without using any body gestures, players act out their scenario to each other and try to guess who was assigned what.

This is a stripped down version of the full game

In Fabula Rasa, players take turns telling a constantly changing story as is typical of oral traditions. The motifs on the cards provide visual bullet points for people, objects, and locations that are central to the action, things that must be remembered in the correct order, with cards disappearing and new ones being added each round. Those who, as a narrator, manage to present their version of the story without errors will be rewarded accordingly.

A free demo version of Fabula Rasa.

A set of rare items your weary travelers can earn when they participate in a Matsuri event! (as these festivals randomly pop up in your adventures) 
These items offer your group interesting and unusual abilities!

The Matsuri Item cards easily integrate with the base Onmyodo game, and come in a kawaii little red pouch for storage!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Битва на песке (Battle on the Sand)
description:
Battle on the Sand is a miniatures game with real shooting.

The game takes place on the Planet of Innosand, and a garrison of Empire infantry is trying to hold a fortress against a force of besieging militia.

The players setup their forces 50cm apart and then roll for initiative. The side with the initiative then rolls a die for the number of action points they get.

An action point can be used to:
1. Fire the walker or multigun. The multigun can only be fired a maximum of three times per turn. To fire the walker all friendly militia must be closer to the fortress than the walker, and for the multigun to fire the infantry must have a soldier within 5cm of it.
2. Move a walker, gun, or soldier 10cm.  Moving up and down the stairs of the fortress costs 10cm.

When enemy soldiers are at a range of 5cm or less to one another, they fight a melee.  Both roll a die and the high side wins.

To win the attackers must eliminate all enemy soldiers or capture the flag atop the fortress.  The defenders win if they kill all enemy militia.

The game comes with:
-1 Firing Robot Walker
-1 Firing Multigun
-6 Militia troops
-6 Empire Infantry
-Shells
-Fortress
-Flag for the fortress
-1 die
-1 measuring device
-Rules

The photo on the box also shows two palm trees, but these are not included in the rules manifest.

Includes three crazy cases that are fun for the whole family!

 • Suriguri - Figure out why a meerkat was stolen from the zoo, and then mysteriously returned only a few days later.

 • Where Is Alba? - Follow the footsteps of a young girl who went somewhere after school and still hasn't made it home.

 • Doggy Business - Get to the bottom of some stinky business . . . who left dog poop in the park?

For each case you will follow the clues, piece together the evidence, and then identify who did it, how, and why.

This is a completely standalone game, and does not require any other Sherlock Files games to play.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Fire in the Library: Tool Cards Promo
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign as part of the Weird Giraffe Games Promo Pack 2023.

Includes 7 cards.

Add these cards to the tool cards from the base game and proceed as usual.

Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign as part of the Weird Giraffe Games Promo Pack 2023.

Includes 4 cards.

Add these cards to the deck of treasure cards from the base game and proceed as usual.

Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign as part of the Weird Giraffe Games Promo Pack 2023.

Includes 6 cards.

Mix in the expansion with the base game cards before starting the base game setup.
Shuffle and remove three urn cards from the game.

Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign as part of the Weird Giraffe Games Promo Pack 2023.

Includes: 18 cards + 2 rules cards.
8 cards are for Fire in the Library, 4 cards are for Wicked & Wise, and 6 cards are for Dreams of Yesterday.

boardgame: Wir sind das Volk!: Combined Edition
description:
1949: Germany, still in ruins from WWII, is split in two, as the Iron Curtain of the Cold War descends. East Germany and West Germany become bitter rivals, each wanting to show its system is superior. Which will reconstruct quicker? Which will make its people happier?

This combined edition brings two games in one box: The award winning "Wir sind das Volk!" and the 4-player expansion "2+2".

In "Wir sind das Volk!" you take the role of East or West Germany. Use your action cards wisely. Build up your economy and increase the living standards of your people. Precise planning is the key to victory. But be careful! Even the best plans may be derailed by events like the 1973 Oil Crisis or the Monday demos. Mass protests, that may seal your fate, are always on the horizon.

With "2+2" the USA and the Soviet Union enter the game. Players form two partnerships — but there can only be ONE winner! Therefore every decision is a nail-biter: What is more important right now? Your side's interests or your own?

In "2+2", the range of strategic choices is significantly widened, turning it to a completely different game: While you are building up "your" Germany (as in the base game) you also seek to manipulate your partner's choices, or even to sabotage their plans. In addition, the super powers struggle for global dominance and compete in the arms and space race. But most importantly, always remember the need for world peace, otherwise there might be no tomorrow.

Game contents:

– 2 gameboards
– 87 wooden pieces
– 148 playing cards
– 3 sheets of die-cut markers
– 2 set off rules

description from the back of the box

Zany zoo-dwellers are parading around the pool in their bikinis. That's just plain weird. Some other animals are completely naked. That's less weird — or is it weirder? Not sure. Clearly, you want to be first to be free of these crazy animals. Ironically, or perhaps coincidentally, one thing that may help you do just that is a zucchini. What? You were expecting a cucumber?

Your goal in Zookini is to get rid of all cards in your hand first. On a turn, you draw one card from the deck, then play up to two cards from your hand on the discard pile; each card you play must match the top card of the discard pile either in animal type or in what the animal is wearing (or not wearing). For example, if the top card shows a flamingo wearing a zebra bikini, you may play:


     Any flamingo
     Any zebra
     Any other animal wearing a flamingo bikini
     Any other animal wearing a zebra bikini


Other cards like crazy gorillas, naked animals, and the unique zucchini will spice up this fast-paced card game.

boardgame: Битвы фэнтези: Герои меча (Fantasy Battles: Heroes of the Sword)
description:
Heroes of the Sword is part of a series of five games.  The other four are:


     Key Battle
     Combat in the Magic Ruins
     Musketeers of the Isle of Mages
     Mystery of the Forgotten Dungeon


Like the other games, this one has its own rules and victory conditions.

This particular game has the players trying to get from their starting point tom their base, but at the same time trying to prevent the other side from doing so.

boardgame: Битвы фэнтези: Падение Рима (Fantasy Battles: The Fall of Rome)
description:
Mages, archers, pirates, Roman legionnaires, elves, skeletons, zombies, dwarves, Vikings, barbarians... The world of Magic is engulfed in endless battles, in which literally everyone takes part! Squads face off against each other to prove their superiority over the rest. The noise of battles is accompanied not only by the grinding of blades and the whistling of arrows, but also by the loud shots of cannons, catapults and other ranged equipment. And there is no end in sight to these clashes...

All this is the combat system of the "Battle of Fantasy" from the Russian company "Technolog"! Assemble your squad from all classes and races, spice it up with guns, and face off in an epic battle with your friends! Each set of the series includes miniatures of units and armor sheets, which contain all the necessary information. You can buy a couple of boxes with armies of soldiers, and this will already be enough for heated clashes. But if you're looking for some truly epic battles, check out the Fortress Packs, which can be used to build any battlefield you want!

An exceptional feature of the combat system created by the Technologist company is the long-range weapons - they can literally shoot small shells and attack miniatures of the enemy army. This feature makes the gameplay even more fun!

—description from the publisher (translated)

This is a new game in the Fantasy Battles series.

This is a shooting game where the players try to be the first tom eliminate all their opponent's soldiers.

—user summary

Welcome to the world of Big Time Sports from Pungo Games. Each hard-hitting, fast-playing game is card driven, designed for two players, and will play in about 30-60 minutes. With uncomplicated rules, Big Time Sports games will have you suiting up and hitting the ice, field, pitch or ring in no time at all.

Big Time Hockey is the first game in our lineup; it's short on rules (an eight-page booklet) but long on depth and replayability.  Big Time Hockey has everything you'd expect in a real hockey game -- skating, passing, face-offs, great saves by your goalie, defense, off sides, penalties, fights -- and more!

—description from the publisher

To play with the Mantis Power Token, give it to the player on the right of the starting player, then follow these rules during the game:

- If you begin your turn with the Power Token, take one additional turn to Steal or Score from any player at the
end of your turn.

- If you successfully steal card(s) from a player with the Power Token, you steal the Power Token as well! When you take the Power Token, immediately take one additional turn to Steal or Score from any player.

- For a 2 player game, instead of using the “2 Player Game” rules in the Mantis instructions, use these rules and play
to 15 points instead of 10.

Let's save the bugs!

My goodness, how many bugs and fruits there are in the garden! With so much mess, someone can step on a bug and not even realize it. That can't happen!

At Busca Bichos our goal will be to keep all the bugs in the garden safe. In addition, we will have to feed them (so that they are strong and healthy!) but not all bugs eat the same... Give each bug the corresponding fruit according to its color.

Do you think you will be able to save all the bugs? Come on, the garden is not going to take care of itself!

---

¡Salvemos a los bichos!

Madre mía, ¡cuántos bichos y frutas hay en el jardín! Con tanto desorden, alguien puede pisar un bicho y no darse ni cuenta. ¡Eso no puede pasar!

En Busca Bichos nuestro objetivo será poner a salvo todos los bichos del jardín. Además, tendremos que darles de comer (¡para que estén fuertes y sanos!) pero no todos los bichos comen lo mismo… Dale a cada bicho la fruta que le corresponde según su color.

¿Crees que serás capaz de salvar a todos los bichos? Vamos, ¡que el jardín no se va a ordenar solo!

Three Moles in a Trenchcoat is a single player deck building skill check game, with a unique mole management system.

Pirates have kidnapped Great Aunt Hilda! Each round you will select an event to attempt, either unearthing more information about the occult Sisters of Delahaye or disrupting their operations.

The round starts by taking a hand or mole management action to increase your chances of succeeding, after which you combine a dice roll with bonus points from your hand and your moles to determine success. Passing unlocks a new part of the story. However, failing will increase your suspicion meter - can you navigate the human world and rescue Great Aunt Hilda, before they figure out that you're actually Three Moles in a Trenchcoat?

Created as part of the 2023 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.

—description from the designer

Each of the 10 animals has a different shaped dish.
In the search, each party card must have 9 different dishes, and one dish is missing. The goal of the game is to find the dish that is not drawn on the card faster than anyone else, and to guess the animal that belongs to that dish.

—description from the publisher

It is clear that they are coming for you, will you be able to survive?

In the Last Meiga you wake up in a dark cell. Your last memory is of being detained while you were going to the town market. You have been accused of witchcraft and now you have to deal with the consequences

Choose a name for your character with which from now on the Inquisition will address you, you will use the questions from the deck of cards to resolve the situations and try to survive.

Everything the cards say is true, so you'll have to use persuasion to save yourself.

Read the cards aloud.
The accused parties respond.
Vote and choose.

La Última is a narrative game system created by Roberto Alhambra.

An adventure with 30 cards, in which each of them will pose situations that you can only get rid of with your best arguments. The trial begins. Will you burn or survive?

---

Está claro que van a por ti, ¿serás capaz de sobrevivir?

En la Última Meiga despiertas en una celda oscura. Tu último recuerdo es el de ser detenida mientras ibas al mercado de la villa. Has sido acusada de brujería y ahora te toca lidiar con las consecuencias

Elige un nombre para tu personaje con el que a partir de ahora la Inquisición se dirigirá a ti, usarás las preguntas del mazo de cartas para resolver las situaciones e intentar sobrevivir.

Todo lo que plantean las cartas es cierto, así que tendrás que usar la persuasión para salvarte.

Lee las cartas en voz alta.
Las partes acusadas responden.
Votad y elegid.

La Última es un sistema de juego narrativo creado por Roberto Alhambra.

Una aventura con 30 cartas, en las que cada una de ellas te planteará situaciones de las que solo podrás librarte con tus mejores argumentos. El juicio comienza. ¿Arderás o sobrevivirás?

In Xalingópolis you work for the city council, erecting the best buildings for the city, using wooden building blocks from a traditional Brazilian children's toy, "O Pequeno Engenheiro" (The Little Engineer).

Each round the active player rolls a die and moves his pawn forward on a path around the city's ground plan. Your pawn position will dictate your action: receiving building blocks, receiving design cards, exchanging pieces. But not all actions are good and, if you're unlucky, you could lose a project or even lose your previously collected blocks.
Each project card shows a construction using specific blocks, and an amount of victory points corresponding to the difficulty of the construction. If a player has the card and the necessary blocks, instead of rolling the die, they can build on the city's ground plan.
Once there are 12 buildings in the city, the game is over and whoever has the most points wins.

Ravohria is a competitive game featuring limitless strategies, character-building dynamics, resource management, and direct player interactions such as stealing, duels, and even killing. The game incorporates hidden leaders and battle royale elements, with various factors enhancing its replayability.

As mercenaries in a dystopian future, players are hired by an infiltrator to disrupt the Ravohria family's control. After successfully capturing a capsule and its blueprints from their research lab, they must escape the restricted area, evade the Ravohria sentinels, utilize resources to survive, and deliver the capsule to their leader — all while advancing their secret personal agendas.

It’s a relentless mission where each mercenary fights to secure all the reputation and glory for their leader, ultimately aiming to bring about Ravohria's downfall.

In the end, history remembers only the winners.

—description from designer

CUBE IT GAME: The ultimate 2-player block-building puzzle game. Choose your level- easy, medium, or hard. Flip the card and race to match that shape. Face the challenge and be the best on the block!
     MATCH, MAKE, CREATE: Two cube sets included- each set forms one cube. If you are a fan of the original Rubik’s Cube 3D puzzle, prepare yourself for hours of sequence puzzle game fun with your opponent.
     Includes 2 Sets of Shapes (each set contains 8 different shapes), 54 Challenge Cards, Instructions


—description from publisher

Summer 1989: The Iron Curtain is not coming down. Tensions are high. Will war return to Europe?.... Blackhorse is a grand tactical game of furious mechanized command in the summer of 1989. Units are companies and platoons... Hexes are 1000 meters across. Turns are three hours each, using an innovative system emphasizing friction and the importance of command and control. Blackhorse puts the player in the shoes of the commanding generals of the Soviet 79th Guards Tank Division and an array of U.S. units drawn from the 11th Armored Cavalry Regiment and the 3rd Armored Division. The Cold War has become damn hot. Will you be up to the task of winning this small piece of it?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Struckle is a quadruple-player abstract strategy board game that takes players on a journey into the depths of creativity and critical thinking. It's a unique blend of tactics and strategy, where the engaging game's mechanics are matched by the depth of the gameplay.

The game board is an 8x8 grid reminiscent of a chessboard with colorful tiles each connecting with different elements of the game to create a uniquely challenging landscape. The number four plays a special role in Struckle, with four sides battling via a set of four pieces. Each set of four pieces available to a player is nudged to behave like a team by the game’s peculiar turn’s definition.

At its core, Struckle is a game of strategy and emergence. Players take turns mobilizing their entire teams at once and using their powers, when available. Progress toward the final objective of occupying two goal squares is intertwined by actions to disrupt the opponents’ plans.

The rules are simple, but the strategic depth is profound. It's easy to learn, yet it takes time to master. At every turn, players must consider not only their immediate actions but also anticipate their opponent's responses.

—description from the designer
This game uses the same basic rules as Pictionary (released as Imagem & Ação 1 in Brazil) but this one has a timer and whiteboard pilots.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Hockey Blast Express uses the same cards and concepts as the full-play version (Hockey Blast). The fun and excitement of hard-hitting hockey action, in about ten minutes of game time.

This is primarily meant as a solitaire game, but has options for 2 players.  5 stacks of players are lined up against each other and dice determine the interactions between the players.  Results are based on scoring charts and/or symbols on the player's card.  Goalies come into play with save ratings to possibly stop goal opportunities.

boardgame: 恐竜ボードゲーム ボルケーノ大噴火 (Dinosaur Boardgame: Great Volcano Eruption)
description:
This is a game that plays much like the Game of Life.  Players take on the roles of Dinosaurs as they try to make their way through the game, accumulating dinosaur cards, and make it to the goal with the most cards in their possession.

Along the way they may land on rewards and penalties, which can cost them cards. Battles may be fought between rivals, and at one point they will have to cross the volcano.  The volcano has working sounds, and the player must press the button on the back whenever they are on the eruption spot.  The volcano will then launch three fireballs unto the gameboard, and if a dinosaur is hit, there is a penalty.

The spinner also Grows like a dinosaur when it is spinned.  There are 30 different sounds.

—user summary

Wyverns of Wylemuir is an expansion for Catacombs Third Edition and Catacombs & Castles Second Edition. The set includes eight Wyverns enabling characters such as heroes and monsters to ride them and unlock their powers. New heroes, monsters, items, rooms and warriors complement a double-sided playmat featuring a wooded meadow on one side and a dangerous swamp on the other. Finally new area of effect templates add new strategic elements to both base games.

New in the second edition:
1. The double-sided game board was replaced by a double-sided neoprene playmat.
2. The manual was completely revised and updated.
3. There are more cards included with the expansion including dedicated cards for both Catacombs Third Edition and Catacombs & Castles Second Edition

Llépol is a card game that consists of opening a restaurant with typical dishes from the Terres de l'Ebre and using the coolest expressions of "Terreno".

It has very simple mechanics, ideal for playing with friends or the whole family.

The goal of the game is to get 4 customers (with a minimum of one regular customer) for your restaurant before the other players. Customers are obtained by adding up the points of each dish.


     A regular customer costs 23 points.
     A passing customer costs 12 points


—description from the publisher

Llépol és un joc de cartes que consisteix en obrir un restaurant amb plats típics de les Terres de l'Ebre i utilitzar les expressions més xaladores Del Terreno.

Té una mecànica molt senzilla, ideal per jugar amb amics o amb tota la família.

L’objectiu del joc és aconseguir 4 clients (amb un mínim d’un client habitual) per al teu restaurant abans que els altres jugadors. Els clients s’aconsegueixen sumant els punts de cada plat.

Un client habitual té un cost de 23 punts.

Un client de pas té un cost de 12 punts.

—description from the publisher (Catalan)

Gamble Rabbit is the game to either obtain the most money through 6 rounds of regular play, or obtain 3 carrot tokens at any time and win instantly.

One round consists of a “money phase” (first half) and a “gambling phase” (second half). 
During the “money phase”, players take actions to increase their amount of money. 
In the “gambling phase”, players place bets against each other and receive rewards or penalties based on their bets.
The game ends after 6 rounds. However, if one of the players owns 3 carrot tokens, it will be a special victory and that player wins. In this case, the game ends even before 6 rounds.
publishers: クードゥーラパン (Queue de Lapin)

description:
L'Esmolet will be in charge of sharpening the tools of the characters that we will have strategically saved until the end.

We will be playing action cards and stealing. In addition to keeping the pairs of cards of traditional professions until the Esmolet comes out, when it appears the money collected during the game will be counted and whoever has the most wins.

—description from the designer (translated)

L’esmolet serà l’encarregat d’afilar les eines dels personatges que haurem guardat estratègicament fins al final.
Anirem jugant cartes d'acció i robant. A més de guardar les parelles de cartes de professions tradicionals fins que surti l'Esmolet, quan aparegui es farà recompte dels diners recaptats durant la partida i qui tingui més guanya.

—description from the designer

This exclusive character pack for Lucca Comics & Games 2023, inspired by the famous Italian comic book "La Compagnia della Forca" by Magnus & Romanini, includes 13 miniatures and 28 cards to expand the fantasy Zombicide game.

Players step by step develop their insects and acquire new ones into their insect crews. They look for food, can attack the opponent's insects and do other special actions.

The game is shared deck building focused to 2 players which means that there is a one deck related to both players where each player contributes to the deck with his own cards. Each round reveals 3 cards and players mainly try to develop their insect cards and acquire new ones.

Another element is dice placement. Each round players roll the number of dice depending on currently revealed cards and alternatively place dice on designated places on cards which trigger actions like acquire, development and attack.

Final mechanic is development and is performed by rotating of squar cards by 90 degrees (all cards are squared). Rotating of cards represents 4 stages of insect metamorphosis which gives to players advantages in form of dice rolling number and victory points which are summarized from the whole deck on the end of the game.

-description from designer

Practice and try property investment with play money before you buy with real money. You get the opportunity as often as you want to practice getting it right for the rest of your life.

Property Millionaire is a simulation of the Australian property market where players develop investment strategies.  Whoever gains the most income and property wins.

Following a Monopoly like board design, players bid on properties and learn its' special rules  - like negative gearing - that influence the Australia property market while attempting to amass their property empire and win the game.

boardgame: War of the Ring: Kings and Lords of Middle-Earth – Limited Edition
description:
This new limited-edition expansion for War of the Ring comes in an “extended version” that will include a deluxe version of the content of both Kings of Middle-earth and Lords of Middle-earth, packaged together in a box matching the size of the previous Anniversary Release and Warriors of Middle-earth Limited Edition.

Also included are deluxe versions of the Treebeard promo and of the mini-expansion The Fate of Erebor, with overlay tiles suitable for use with the normal and Deluxe game board.
